name: Cohiba  Club (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand applying a third barrel fermentation to filler leaves — born in 1966 as a state secret, never sold publicly for 16 years.
description (EN Store):
Cohiba's origin is a deliberate act of statecraft: in 1966, Fidel Castro commissioned a private cigar rolled exclusively for diplomatic gifts, produced in secret at El Laguito — a French-colonial mansion in Havana's Cubanacán district — for nearly 16 years before any public sale was permitted. That founding secrecy shaped a production philosophy of uncompromising selectivity that persists today.

Tobacco is drawn exclusively from Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's most prized growing corridor, where red ferralitic soils and a sheltered microclimate concentrate oils in the leaf. In an average year, only the output of roughly five of the ten contracted fields clears Cohiba's quality threshold.

The structural signature is a mandatory third fermentation applied to the seco and ligero filler leaves: leaves are stacked in small wooden barrels in temperature-controlled closets at El Laguito, a process lasting up to 18 months that strips residual tars and nicotine while building layered aromatic complexity. No other Habanos brand employs this protocol. The three-leaf filler construction — Volado (combustion), Seco (aroma), Ligero (body) — is finished entirely by hand by skilled torcedores.

The Club vitola sits within the Línea Clásica, delivering Cohiba's characteristic cedar, dark chocolate, and roasted coffee profile at a shorter format suited to a 30-minute smoke — the same triple-fermented tobacco architecture as the Lancero, compressed into a more accessible ring gauge.

name: Cohiba  Club (20 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Cuba's only triple-fermented Habano — born as a state secret in 1966, rolled by hand at El Laguito from five hand-selected Vuelta Abajo fields.
description (EN Store):
Cohiba's origin is a deliberate act of concealment: production began in 1966 exclusively for Fidel Castro and diplomatic gifts, with no public sale for nearly 16 years — a provenance that shaped the brand's position at the apex of the Cuban cigar hierarchy before the world knew it existed. The tobacco is sourced from approximately ten Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's most prized growing corridor — where deep, well-drained red soils and consistent humidity produce leaf of exceptional oil content and combustion quality. In an average year, only the output of roughly five of those fields passes Cohiba's quality threshold.

The defining technical signature is a mandatory third barrel fermentation applied to the seco and ligero filler leaves inside temperature-controlled closets at the El Laguito factory — a process lasting up to 18 months and applied by no other Habanos brand. This additional fermentation cycle strips residual nicotine and tars while building layered aromatic complexity unavailable through the standard two-fermentation protocol used across the rest of the Cuban portfolio. The Club vitola is rolled entirely by hand (Totalmente a mano) by El Laguito's torcedores using a three-leaf filler construction: Volado for combustion, Seco for aroma, and Ligero for body and strength.

The Cohiba Club's compact format concentrates the house profile — cedar, dark chocolate, roasted coffee — into a shorter, accessible smoke that retains the structural distinction of the triple-fermentation process. Finished cigars undergo fumigation and freezing at Habanos S.A.'s Guanabacoa facility to eliminate tobacco beetle before release.

name: Cohiba  Mini (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Cuba's only triple-fermented Habano — born as a state secret in 1966, now in a compact Mini vitola from Vuelta Abajo's finest Vegas Finas de Primera.
description (EN Store): In 1966, Fidel Castro's personal torcedores began rolling what would remain a state secret for nearly 16 years — a cigar reserved exclusively for diplomatic gifts and the Cuban leadership, produced at El Laguito, a French-colonial mansion in Havana's Cubanacán district, named after the small pond in its grounds. That origin imposed a standard no commercial pressure could dilute. The tobacco is sourced from approximately ten Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's most prized growing corridor — and in a typical year only five of those fields pass Cohiba's quality threshold. The defining technical signature is a mandatory third barrel fermentation applied to the seco and ligero filler leaves in temperature-controlled closets within El Laguito, a process that can extend up to 18 months and is employed by no other Habanos brand. Standard Cuban production stops at two fermentations; this additional cycle strips residual harshness, reduces nicotine and tars, and builds a layered aromatic complexity structurally unavailable to peers such as Montecristo or Partagás. The Mini vitola delivers that same triple-fermented filler construction — Volado, Seco, and Ligero, all Vuelta Abajo — in a short-smoke format, making the house's signature cedar, dark chocolate, and roasted-coffee profile accessible without the time commitment of a Lancero or Esplendido. Finished cigars undergo fumigation and freezing at Habanos S.A.'s Guanabacoa facility; each stick is hand-rolled (Totalmente a mano) by skilled torcedores.

name: Cohiba  Siglo I (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand applying a third barrel fermentation to filler leaves — born as a state secret in 1966, never sold publicly for 16 years.
description (EN Store):
Cohiba's origin is a documented state secret: production began in 1966 exclusively for Fidel Castro and diplomatic gifts, with no public sale permitted until 1982 — a provenance that shaped every production decision that followed. That founding mandate demanded a structurally different cigar, which is why Cohiba remains the only Habanos brand to apply a third fermentation to its seco and ligero filler leaves, stacked in small wooden barrels in temperature-controlled closets at the El Laguito factory in Havana's Cubanacán district — a converted French-colonial mansion. This additional fermentation, lasting up to 18 months, strips residual nicotine and tars while building layered aromatic complexity unavailable through the standard two-fermentation process used by every other Cuban brand.

Tobacco is sourced exclusively from approximately 10 Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — in an average year, only five fields' output passes quality selection. The Siglo I is the smallest vitola in the Línea Clásica, a short, narrow format that concentrates the three-leaf filler blend (Volado, Seco, Ligero) into an accessible 20-minute smoke without sacrificing the house's signature cedar, dark chocolate, and roasted coffee character. Presented in an aluminum tube for humidity stability and travel resilience.

name: Cohiba  Siglo II (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): El Laguito's Línea Clásica Mareva: the only Cuban cigar brand applying a third barrel fermentation to seco and ligero filler leaves — up to 18 months.
description (EN Store): Born in 1966 as a state secret — rolled exclusively for Fidel Castro and diplomatic gifts for nearly 16 years before any public sale — Cohiba's founding mandate was to produce Cuba's most technically processed cigar, a standard enforced to this day. The Siglo II is a Mareva vitola (42 ring gauge, 5⅛ inches) within the Línea Clásica, hand-rolled by skilled torcedores at the El Laguito factory, a French-colonial mansion in Havana's Cubanacán district. Tobacco is drawn exclusively from approximately ten Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's most prized alluvial red-clay soils — with only the output of roughly five fields passing quality selection in a typical year. The defining production step is a mandatory third fermentation of the seco and ligero filler leaves, stacked in small wooden barrels in temperature-controlled closets within El Laguito for up to 18 months — a protocol applied by no other Habanos brand — which strips residual nicotine and tars while building layered aromatic complexity. The three-leaf filler construction (Volado for combustion, Seco for aroma, Ligero for body) is finished with a natural Cuban colorado wrapper. The Siglo II's shorter format concentrates the Línea Clásica profile into a cooler, slower burn, making the triple-fermentation character — cedar, dark chocolate, roasted coffee, and a distinctive barnyard earthiness — more immediately legible than in longer vitolas. The aluminum tube provides hermetic protection, preserving humidity integrity from factory to humidor.

name: Jose L. Piedra  Brevas (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand built entirely on Vuelta Arriba (Remedios) tobacco — short-filler, fully handmade, uncompromisingly rustic.
description (EN Store):
When virtually every premium Cuban marque was abandoning short-filler construction in favour of long-leaf prestige, José L. Piedra held its ground — Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Corta remains the brand's defining technical signature, a method where cut pieces of Remedios leaf are hand-bunched rather than whole leaves laid in parallel, yielding a draw character and combustion profile no long-filler vitola replicates.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from the Vuelta Arriba / Remedios sub-region of Villa Clara province, central Cuba, a growing zone whose soils lack the celebrated red colorado earth of Vuelta Abajo. That geological distinction is not a compromise: it is the source of the brand's pronounced leather, raw cocoa, dry wood, and black pepper character — bolder and more tannic than the rounder profiles Pinar del Río tobacco typically produces.

The Brevas vitola measures 42 ring gauge × 133 mm, a format that concentrates the Remedios blend into a medium-to-full strength smoke with no dilution from neutral filler. José L. Piedra is the sole Habanos brand to source 100% of its tobacco outside Pinar del Río — a distinction it held alone among Habanos export brands until Guantanamera's launch in 2002. No Edición Limitada or special release has ever been produced; the blend has never been softened for market trends.

name: Jose L. Piedra  Petit Cazadores (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand built entirely on Vuelta Arriba / Remedios tobacco — short-filler, fully handmade, unapologetically raw in a 105 mm petit format.
description (EN Store):
When virtually every premium Cuban marque abandoned short-filler construction, José L. Piedra held its ground — the brand remains one of the last Habanos labels built entirely on Tripa Corta (short-filler) technique, with cut leaf pieces bunched and wrapped fully by hand since the policy shift of c.2006. That deliberate refusal to modernise is the point.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from the Vuelta Arriba / Remedios sub-region of Villa Clara Province, central Cuba, a growing zone distinct from the celebrated red colorado soils of Pinar del Río's Vuelta Abajo. Remedios leaf carries a stronger, more rustic character: pronounced leather, raw cocoa, dry wood, and pepper rather than the rounder, creamier profile Vuelta Abajo commands.

The Petit Cazadores vitola measures 43 ring gauge × 105 mm — a compact format that concentrates the blend's medium-to-full strength into a 20–25 minute smoke. Construction is Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Corta: a method used by virtually no other current Habanos brand. No Edición Limitada, no special releases — this is the unadorned, uncompromised core expression.

The special feature here is categorical: José L. Piedra is the sole Habanos brand to source 100% of its tobacco outside Pinar del Río, a distinction it held alone among Habanos export brands until Guantanamera launched in September 2002.

The short format delivers the full Remedios character quickly — dry earth, leather, and pepper from the first third, with cocoa deepening toward the final third. Best smoked fresh or within two to three years of purchase.

name: Jose L. Piedra  Conservas (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand built entirely on Vuelta Arriba / Remedios tobacco — short-filler, fully handmade, uncompromisingly rustic.
description (EN Store):
When virtually every premium Cuban marque abandoned short-filler construction in favour of long-filler prestige, José L. Piedra held its ground — Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Corta remains the brand's defining technical signature, a method in which cut pieces of Remedios leaf are bunched and wrapped entirely by hand, a process that was machine-assisted until around 2002 before being brought fully into hand-rolling by approximately 2006.

The tobacco itself is the brand's most transgressive credential: 100% Vuelta Arriba / Remedios origin for filler, binder, and wrapper alike — sourced from Villa Clara Province in central Cuba rather than the celebrated red colorado soils of Vuelta Abajo in Pinar del Río. Remedios leaf, cultivated in a region with documented tobacco history stretching to the 16th century, produces a stronger, more tannic character than its western counterpart, driving the pronounced leather, cocoa, raw wood, and black pepper profile the brand has never softened.

The Conservas vitola (44 × 140 mm) sits at the fuller end of the range — the 44-ring gauge drawing the Remedios blend into a slow, even burn that amplifies the earthier, more resinous registers of the short-filler construction. José L. Piedra is the sole Habanos brand to have never produced an Edición Limitada, Edición Regional, or commemorative release, yet held 9.3% of total Habanos export share by 2001.

name: Jose L. Piedra  Mini (20 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand built entirely on Vuelta Arriba / Remedios tobacco — short-filler, fully handmade, uncompromisingly rustic since 1880.
description (EN Store): When Habanos S.A. reclassified José L. Piedra as a Global brand in 2007, it was a quiet acknowledgment of something the Cuban cigar world had long resisted admitting: a short-filler, Remedios-tobacco cigar had captured 9.3% of total Habanos export share by 2001 without producing a single Edición Limitada or special release. Every leaf — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from the Vuelta Arriba / Remedios sub-region of Villa Clara Province, central Cuba, making this the sole Habanos brand to never draw on the celebrated red colorado soils of Vuelta Abajo in Pinar del Río. Remedios leaf grows on less-celebrated terrain, producing tobacco with a stronger, more angular character: pronounced leather, raw cocoa, dry wood, and black pepper rather than the creamy sweetness associated with Vuelta Abajo. Construction is Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Corta — cut short-filler pieces bunched and wrapped entirely by hand, a method abandoned by virtually every other premium Cuban marque. The Mini vitola delivers that medium-to-full strength profile in a compact format, front-loading the blend's tannic punch with no long-filler dilution. What no peer does: José L. Piedra has actively resisted the industry-wide drift toward lighter, rounder profiles, keeping its raw Cuban character intact across decades of ownership changes.

name: Montecristo  Edmundo
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo puro, hand-rolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory — the Edmundo vitola brings Montecristo's colorado-wrapper house blend into a broader 52-ring gauge.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935, he built a brand around a single proposition: Vuelta Abajo tobacco, rolled entirely by hand, blended for consistency at scale. After the 1960 revolution, floor manager José Manuel González sustained that standard through nationalization — a rare case of artisan continuity inside a state-seized factory. The Edmundo is a product of that unbroken lineage, rolled at the H. Upmann factory in Havana in the 52 × 135 mm robusto extra vitola, a format introduced to the core Montecristo portfolio in 2004 to exploit the fuller combustion dynamics of a wider ring gauge.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — is sourced exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone. The region's red clay (colorado) soil, moderated by Atlantic breezes, produces leaves of concentrated mineral complexity and natural sweetness that define the Montecristo house profile. The Edmundo's broader gauge slows the burn rate, allowing the ligero-forward filler blend more time to develop, which pushes the body toward the fuller end of Montecristo's medium-to-full register. Construction is Totalmente a Mano — long-filler entubado technique, triple cap, natural colorado wrapper — with tobacco aged approximately one to two years before rolling. The Edmundo's 52-ring format is the widest in the standard Montecristo line, a deliberate departure from the brand's traditionally slender vitolas that delivers a cooler, slower smoke unavailable in the No. 2 or No. 4.

name: Montecristo  Edmundo (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Handmade Cuban puro from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — the Edmundo vitola in protective aluminum tube, rolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory.
description (EN Store): When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built a brand around torcedor craft rather than estate prestige — a philosophy that survived nationalization only because floor manager José Manuel González maintained rolling standards after the founding family fled Cuba in 1960. The Edmundo is a 21st-century vitola addition to that lineage: a 52-ring-gauge robusto extra that gives the classic Montecristo blend more filler volume and a slower, cooler burn than the No. 4 or No. 5. All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soil and ocean-moderated tropical conditions concentrate mineral complexity and natural sweetness in the leaf. Construction is Totalmente a Mano using long-filler entubado or accordion-fold technique by skilled torcedores, with tobacco undergoing natural fermentation in pilones before blending. The aluminum tube — a practical departure from the brand's traditional cedar-lined dress boxes — preserves humidity integrity across tropical shipping conditions without cedar interference on the wrapper. Within the core Montecristo portfolio, the Edmundo occupies an unusual middle ground: wider than a Robusto, shorter than a Churchill, it delivers the house profile of cocoa, cedar, and restrained earth at a pace and temperature that a thinner ring gauge cannot replicate.

name: Montecristo  Petit Edmundo (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Petit Edmundo: Vuelta Abajo colorado-wrapper puro, hand-rolled at H. Upmann factory, delivering Montecristo's cocoa-cedar house profile in a 52-ring robusto format.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935, he built the brand on a single conviction: that Vuelta Abajo leaf, rolled with precision, needed no embellishment. The Petit Edmundo — a 52-ring, 110mm robusto corto — is the compact expression of that philosophy, hand-rolled Totalmente a Mano by torcedores using the entubado or accordion-fold technique that ensures even airflow through a short, wide filler column.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate moderated by Atlantic breezes concentrate mineral complexity and aromatic oils in the leaf. The colorado wrapper, characteristic of the core Montecristo line, delivers the house's signature creaminess against a backdrop of cedar and cocoa.

The Petit Edmundo's special distinction within the portfolio is its 52-ring gauge on a short 110mm frame — a format that concentrates the blend's ligero-driven body into a 30–40 minute smoke, producing a denser, more immediate expression of the Montecristo profile than the longer No. 4 or No. 2 achieve in their opening third. Presented in an aluminum tube, the cigar is protected from humidity fluctuation during transit — a practical choice that also makes it the most travel-ready format in the standard line. The tobacco undergoes natural fermentation in pilones and approximately 1–2 years of aging before rolling.

name: Montecristo  No.2
short_description (EN Store): Torpedo-format Cuban puro from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — the No.2 is the format that defined the vitola globally since 1935.
description (EN Store): When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo at Havana's Particulares Factory in 1935, the brand's survival after the 1960 revolution rested on a single artisan: torcedor José Manuel González, promoted to floor manager, whose continuity of hand preserved the blend through nationalization. That institutional memory lives in every No.2 rolled today at the H. Upmann factory in Vedado. The tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate tempered by Atlantic breezes concentrate mineral complexity and aromatic richness unavailable elsewhere on the island. Filler, binder, and wrapper are all Vuelta Abajo leaf, making this a 100% Cuban puro. Leaves undergo natural fermentation in pilones before blending; the cigar itself is Totalmente a Mano, rolled by skilled torcedores using entubado or accordion-fold technique, then triple-capped and presented uncut. The No.2's torpedo (figurado) vitola is the format's defining expression — the tapered head concentrates smoke through a narrowing draw, amplifying the house profile of cedar, cocoa, and restrained earth at medium-to-full body, sitting deliberately between Bolívar's pepper intensity and Hoyo de Monterrey's lighter register. Smoke now through five years of box rest for peak integration.

name: Montecristo  No.4
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo puro, hand-rolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory since 1944 — the world's best-selling Cuban cigar, built on red-clay colorado soil complexity.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he created a brand that would eventually account for roughly one-quarter of all Habanos S.A. global sales — yet the post-revolutionary survival of its quality rests on a single artisan: José Manuel González, the torcedor promoted to floor manager after the Menéndez family fled Cuba in 1960, whose continuity preserved the house blend through nationalization.

All tobacco originates from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soil, a tropical microclimate tempered by Atlantic breezes, and consistent humidity produce filler, binder, and wrapper leaves of exceptional mineral depth and aromatic concentration. Every leaf in this 100% Cuban puro shares the same terroir.

The No. 4 is rolled Totalmente a Mano by skilled torcedores using the entubado or accordion-fold long-filler technique, ensuring even draw and combustion. Tobacco undergoes natural fermentation in pilones and a minimum of approximately 1–2 years of aging before rolling. The finished cigar is triple-capped and hand-finished, presented uncut in natural colorado wrapper.

At the Robusto vitola (48 ring gauge), the No. 4 is the entry point into the Montecristo house style — medium-to-full bodied, positioned deliberately between the peppery intensity of Bolívar and the lighter register of Hoyo de Monterrey, a calibrated middle register no peer in the Habanos portfolio occupies at this price and volume.

Smoke now or rest 3–5 years in a stable humidor at 65–70% RH to allow the colorado wrapper to deepen its cedar and cocoa register.

name: Montecristo  No.5
short_description (EN Store): Petite corona from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — handrolled 'Totalmente a Mano' at Havana's H. Upmann factory since 1944.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built a medium-to-full-bodied house style deliberately positioned between the peppery aggression of Bolívar and the lighter register of Hoyo de Monterrey — a calculated middle ground that would eventually make Montecristo the single best-selling Cuban brand globally, accounting for roughly one-quarter of all Habanos S.A. worldwide sales.

Every leaf in the No. 5 — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier tobacco zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate moderated by ocean breezes yield leaves of exceptional mineral complexity and aromatic density. The No. 5 is the smallest vitola in the core Montecristo line, a petite corona format that concentrates the house blend into a shorter, more immediate smoke.

Construction is Totalmente a Mano — entirely handmade by torcedores using traditional long-filler entubado or accordion-fold rolling — with tobacco undergoing natural fermentation in pilones before blending. The 100% Cuban puro blend uses proprietary seco, viso, and ligero ratios from Vuelta Abajo, triple-capped and presented uncut.

The No. 5's special distinction within the portfolio is its accessibility: the compact format delivers the full Montecristo cedar-cocoa-earth profile in under 30 minutes, making it the only vitola in the core line that fits a compressed timeframe without sacrificing the house character.

name: Montecristo  Open Eagle (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo colorado-wrapper puro in sealed aluminum tube — Montecristo's accessible Open line, handrolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory from 100% Pinar del Río leaf.
description (EN Store): When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, the brand's survival after the 1960 revolution rested on a single torcedor — José Manuel González — whose artisan continuity kept the house style intact through nationalization. That style endures in the Open Eagle: a 100% Cuban puro with filler, binder, and wrapper all sourced from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soil and a tropical microclimate moderated by ocean breezes produce leaf of exceptional mineral complexity. The tobacco undergoes natural fermentation in pilones and a minimum aging period before torcedores at the H. Upmann factory roll each cigar Totalmente a Mano using traditional long-filler entubado technique, then finish it with a triple cap. The Open line is Montecristo's deliberate transgression: a shorter, faster-burning format designed for the modern smoker's schedule, yet built on the same Vuelta Abajo blend architecture as the flagship No. 2. The aluminum tube provides hermetic protection, preserving humidity integrity from factory to first light. Expect the house signature of cedar, cocoa, and restrained earth — medium-to-full in body, positioned between the peppery intensity of Bolívar and the lighter register of Hoyo de Monterrey.

name: Montecristo  Open Junior (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made Open line cigarillo from Vuelta Abajo tobacco, triple-capped and aluminum-sealed — Montecristo's accessible format, same D.O.P. terroir.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, the brand was built on a single premise: consistency at scale. The Open Junior is the format that carries that mandate furthest — a machine-made cigarillo designed to deliver the house profile in under fifteen minutes without sacrificing the D.O.P. Cuban tobacco credential.

Tobacco originates from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's red clay (colorado) soil belt, where ocean breezes moderate a tropical microclimate and produce leaves of mineral complexity that no other growing zone replicates. That terroir is the Open line's primary claim to legitimacy within the broader Montecristo portfolio.

Unlike the handmade core line rolled Totalmente a Mano by torcedores at the H. Upmann factory, the Open Junior is machine-made, using short-filler construction and a homogenized binder — a deliberate departure from the entubado long-filler technique that defines the No. 2 or No. 4. The aluminum tube provides hermetic protection against humidity fluctuation, critical in tropical retail environments. The cigar is triple-capped and presented uncut.

What sets the Open Junior apart within the Montecristo range is precisely this transgression: it is the only format in the portfolio that abandons handmade construction entirely, trading artisan complexity for format convenience while retaining the Habanos D.O.P. designation. Expect the house signature of cedar, cocoa, and mild earth — compressed into a shorter, lighter register than the full handmade line.

name: Montecristo  Open Regata (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo puro in aluminum travel tube — handrolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory, Open line built for accessible draw and consistent burn.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, the brand was a relative newcomer in a city already crowded with century-old marques — yet within decades it would account for roughly one-quarter of all Habanos S.A. worldwide sales, a volume achievement that makes its consistency genuinely remarkable. The Open Regata belongs to the accessible tier of that portfolio, designed for a shorter, more approachable smoke without abandoning the house commitment to 100% Vuelta Abajo puro construction — filler, binder, and wrapper all sourced from Pinar del Río's red clay (colorado) soils, where tropical heat moderated by Atlantic breezes drives the mineral complexity and natural sweetness the Montecristo profile is built on.

Each cigar is Totalmente a Mano — entirely handmade by torcedores using long-filler entubado or accordion-fold technique — with tobacco undergoing natural fermentation in pilones before blending. The aluminum tube format is the defining practical feature here: hermetically sealed, it arrests humidity fluctuation during transit and storage, making the Regata the format of choice for travel or gifting where a cedar box is impractical. Within the Open line, the Regata vitola offers a medium ring gauge and moderate length, producing a smoke that sits in Montecristo's characteristic medium-to-full register — earthier and more cedar-driven than Hoyo de Monterrey, noticeably less peppery than Partagás — with the cocoa and subtle sweetness the house style is known for arriving early and holding through the final third.

name: Montecristo  Joyitas
short_description (EN Store): Handmade Cuban puro from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — Montecristo's petite format delivering the house's creamy cocoa-cedar signature in a short, accessible smoke.
description (EN Store): When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built the blend around a single ambition: consistency at scale — a standard that survived nationalisation in 1960 only because master torcedor José Manuel González remained on the factory floor to safeguard the rolling tradition. The Joyitas is the smallest handmade expression in the core portfolio, a petite corona format that concentrates the house profile into a brief, high-intensity smoke. All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — is sourced exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soil and a tropical microclimate moderated by ocean breezes produce leaves of exceptional mineral complexity. Leaves undergo natural fermentation in pilones before blending, and the cigars are rolled entirely by hand ('Totalmente a Mano') using the entubado or accordion-fold technique, then triple-capped and presented uncut. Standard production tobacco is aged approximately 1–2 years pre-rolling. What distinguishes the Joyitas within the Montecristo range is its format paradox: the reduced ring gauge concentrates draw resistance and amplifies the cedar and cocoa core while shortening the smoke time — delivering full house character without the commitment of a robusto or torpedo. Smoke young; the tight construction rewards a slow, deliberate pace.

name: Montecristo  Mini Cigarillos (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made cigarillo from Vuelta Abajo tobacco — the entry point to Montecristo's Havana house profile of cedar, cocoa, and red-clay minerality.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, the brand was built on a single proposition: consistent, medium-to-full-bodied Cuban character at scale. The Mini Cigarillos are the machine-made expression of that same house DNA — a deliberate departure from the Totalmente a Mano handmade portfolio, designed for format accessibility without abandoning Vuelta Abajo origin.

Tobacco is sourced from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soil and a tropical microclimate moderated by ocean breezes produce leaves of pronounced mineral complexity. That terroir underpins the characteristic cocoa, cedar, and earthy register that defines the Montecristo house style across all formats.

Unlike the core handmade line — where filler, binder, and wrapper undergo natural fermentation in pilones and minimum 1–2 years of aging before a torcedor rolls them by hand — the Mini is machine-made, using a shorter, more uniform construction. This is the brand's acknowledged transgression: volume and convenience over artisan process, yet still drawing on the same Vuelta Abajo leaf source that anchors the No. 2 Torpedo.

The result sits lighter in body than the handmade portfolio — cedar and cocoa forward, with the red-clay mineral thread intact — making it the most approachable expression of a house that accounts for roughly one-quarter of all Habanos S.A. global sales.

name: Partagas  Serie P No.2 (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Vuelta Abajo Habano in aluminium tube — long-filler, totally handmade at Havana's Francisco Pérez Germán factory since 1845.
description (EN Store): Don Jaime Partagás is credited as the inventor of the cigar factory lector — the reader who recites books aloud to rolling-room workers — a tradition still alive at the Francisco Pérez Germán factory in Central Havana, the casa matrice for five Habanos brands. That same factory is where every Serie P No. 2 is constructed by a single torcedor using the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method: long filler assembled, bound, and wrapped entirely by hand without mechanical aid. All leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's westernmost growing zone, where red clay soils and a persistently humid microclimate produce tobacco of exceptional oil content and combustion consistency. The Serie P No. 2 sits at the full-body end of the Partagás spectrum — richer and more structurally dense than the Serie D No. 4 — delivering the house's signature deep earth, leather, dark cocoa, and exotic spice profile in a torpedo (figurado) format that concentrates draw intensity at the tapered head. Finished cigars pass draw-testing machines and senior quality-control inspection, with a rejection rate of approximately 10–15%. The individual aluminium tube preserves humidity integrity without a humidor, making this the collector-grade travel format for a vitola that rewards at least 30 minutes of unhurried smoking time.

name: Partagas  Serie D No.4 (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo red-clay tobacco, long-filler hand-rolled at Havana's Francisco Pérez Germán factory — the robusto format that defined the Serie D since the 1930s.
description (EN Store):
Don Jaime Partagás y Ravell, who founded the brand in 1845, is credited as the inventor of the factory lector — a reader who recites books aloud to rolling-room workers — and, more consequentially, as the pioneer of systematic multi-stage tobacco fermentation in Cuba, the technique that locks in the house's characteristically dense, earthy profile across nearly 180 years of production. The Serie D line itself dates to the 1930s, making the No. 4 robusto format one of the longest-running vitola expressions in the Habano world.

All leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler — originates exclusively from the Vuelta Abajo zone of Pinar del Río, Cuba's westernmost province, where red clay soils and a persistently humid microclimate produce tobacco of unusual density and oil content. That terroir is the direct source of the Colorado (mid-brown, oily) wrapper characteristic of the Serie D line and the resinous, spice-forward core that separates Partagás from lighter Havana houses such as Romeo y Julieta or H. Upmann.

Construction follows the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method: each torcedor hand-assembles the entire cigar from long-filler assembly through wrapper application. Draw-testing machines screen every finished cigar; a senior quality-control team applies a further spot-check, with a rejection rate of approximately 10–15%. This individual tube (tubo) format provides an aluminium-sealed microenvironment that stabilises humidity during transit — an advantage for single-cigar retail in tropical climates. Body sits at medium-full, anchored by dark cocoa, leather, and Vuelta Abajo pepper — structured density over sheer strength.

name: Partagas  Mini (20 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Partagas Mini: Vuelta Abajo long-filler, hand-rolled at Francisco Pérez Germán factory — full-bodied Cuban intensity in a compact vitola.
description (EN Store): Don Jaime Partagás y Ravell, who founded the brand in 1845, is credited as the inventor of the factory lector — a reader who recites books aloud to rolling-room workers — a tradition still observed at the Francisco Pérez Germán factory in Central Havana, where every Partagás Mini is rolled today. The tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's westernmost growing zone, where red clay soils and a persistently humid microclimate produce leaf of exceptional oil content and combustion consistency — the direct physical reason Partagás maintains its dense, earthy core across nearly 180 years of production. Each Mini is constructed by a single torcedor using the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method — long filler assembled and wrapped entirely by hand — with all leaf (wrapper, binder, filler) sourced from the same Vuelta Abajo zone, 100% natural tobacco, zero casing agents. Finished cigars pass draw-testing machines and senior quality-control inspection, with a rejection rate of approximately 10–15%. What distinguishes the Mini within the Partagás portfolio is the delivery of the house's characteristically full-bodied, peppery, dark-cocoa profile — richer and more structurally dense than peers such as Romeo y Julieta — compressed into a short-smoke format without blending down to a milder character. For a cigar of this size, the intensity-to-format ratio is the defining transgression.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  Mini (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made mini format from Vuelta Abajo's colorado clay soils — the accessible face of a house that named the Churchill vitola.
description (EN Store): When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Havana factory in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, Romeo y Julieta permanently attached his name to a cigar format now used by virtually every house in the world — a piece of category history that underscores just how central this brand is to the Habano canon. Founded in 1875 by Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García, the marque is produced under Habanos S.A.'s Protected Appellation of Origin for Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — a zone whose red, clay-rich colorado soils, warm humid microclimate, and reliable trade winds yield leaves with a rare equilibrium of strength, aroma, and combustibility. Filler and binder leaves are sun-grown in Vuelta Abajo; the wrapper is shade-grown Criollo from the same zone, delivering a finer-textured draw. Post-harvest leaves are air-cured for 25–50 days in ventilated barns, then undergo bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and build aromatic complexity. The Mini format is machine-produced — a deliberate departure from the brand's 'totalmente a mano, tripa larga' standard-line mandate — making it the entry point into the Romeo y Julieta house style: medium-bodied, aromatic, built around citrus, almond, cedar, and a subtle chocolate finish. Ten sticks per box; short smoke, full provenance.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.1 (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Totalmente a mano, long-filler Habano from Vuelta Abajo's colorado clay soils — cedar-lined aluminum tube preserves the No.1 petit corona format.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1875 by Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García, Romeo y Julieta became the house that gave the world the Churchill format — when Sir Winston visited the Havana factory in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, every cigar brand on earth subsequently adopted the designation. The No.1 is the petit corona expression of that same house blend, sealed in a cedar-lined aluminum tube that functions as a portable humidor. Tobacco is sourced exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin — where red colorado clay soils, warm humidity, and consistent trade winds produce leaves with an exceptional balance of combustibility, aroma, and measured strength. Sun-grown leaves supply the filler and binder; shade-grown Criollo wrapper from the same zone delivers a finer-textured, silkier burn. Construction follows totalmente a mano, tripa larga protocol: long-filler leaves hand-rolled by torcedores, with post-harvest leaves having undergone bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and develop layered complexity. What distinguishes the No.1 within the Romeo y Julieta range is format economy: the shorter ring gauge concentrates the blend's cedar, almond, and citrus character into a tighter draw, amplifying aromatic intensity relative to the fuller Churchill. The aluminum tube format also makes this the most travel-stable expression in the standard line.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.2 (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): The cigar that named a format: Churchill's 1946 Havana visit immortalised this 7×47 Julieta No.2, rolled long-filler from Vuelta Abajo's colorado clay.
description (EN Store): When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Romeo y Julieta factory in Havana in 1946 and declared his preference for the Julieta No. 2 — 7 inches × 47 ring gauge — the brand named the size after him, and every cigar house on earth followed suit. That single human endorsement codified a vitola now universal across the industry. The tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin, where deep red colorado clay soils, warm humidity, and consistent trade winds produce leaves with an exceptional equilibrium of combustibility, aromatic density, and measured strength. Sun-grown leaves form the long filler and binder; a shade-grown Criollo wrapper from the same zone delivers a finer-textured, silkier draw. Post-harvest leaves undergo air-curing for 25–50 days in ventilated barns, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and build layered flavor complexity. The cigar is then hand-rolled by torcedores using the totalmente a mano, tripa larga method — no short filler, no compromise. The aluminum tube is cedar-lined, preserving humidity and imparting a subtle resinous character absent from box-stored examples. At medium body, this is the widest-ranging Habanos marque deliberately calibrated for aromatic complexity over raw power.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.2 (10 Sticks per Box) (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): The cigar that named a format: Vuelta Abajo's colorado soils, shade-grown Criollo wrapper, long-filler construction — 10 Churchill-format tubes.
description (EN Store):
When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Havana factory in 1946 and declared his preference for the Julieta No. 2 — 7 inches at 47 ring gauge — Romeo y Julieta did not merely gain a famous customer; it handed every cigar brand on earth a new standard vitola designation. The Churchill format as a universal category begins here.

The tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin — where red colorado clay soils, warm humidity, and consistent trade winds produce leaves with a precise equilibrium of combustibility, aroma, and measured strength. Sun-grown leaves supply filler and binder; shade-grown Criollo leaf from the same zone provides the finer-textured wrapper.

Post-harvest leaves undergo air-curing in ventilated barns for 25–50 days, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and develop aromatic complexity. Each cigar is constructed by a torcedor using the totalmente a mano, tripa larga method — long-filler, totally handmade — then sealed in a cedar-lined aluminum tube that continues passive humidification in transit.

What distinguishes this format within the Romeo y Julieta range is the ring gauge-to-length ratio of the Churchill: the 47-gauge draw slows combustion relative to the tobacco column, extending the aromatic development of cedar, almond, and citrus across a significantly longer smoke than any petit corona format allows. This box of 10 represents standard-line production — not Reserva or Gran Reserva — delivering the house's medium-bodied signature without the minimum 3-year post-roll aging of the tiered releases.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.3 (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo long-filler Habano in cedar-lined aluminum tube — medium-bodied, shade-grown Criollo wrapper, air-cured & pilón-fermented.
description (EN Store):
When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Romeo y Julieta factory in Havana in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, he inadvertently handed every cigar brand on earth a new format name — a measure of how deeply this house, founded in 1875 by Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García, has shaped the global cigar lexicon. The No. 3 is a shorter petit corona expression of that same house philosophy, rolled totalmente a mano, tripa larga (totally handmade, long filler) by torcedores in Havana.

Tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — the Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin — where red colorado clay soils, warm humidity, and reliable trade winds produce leaves with an optimal balance of combustibility, aroma, and measured strength. The wrapper is a shade-grown Criollo leaf from the same zone, delivering a finer texture and restrained oil content that keeps the draw clean throughout.

Post-harvest leaves undergo air-curing in ventilated barns for 25–50 days, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days — the process that strips ammonia and builds the layered aromatic complexity the house is known for. The cedar-lined aluminum tube preserves humidity integrity from factory to lighter, a practical luxury detail absent from standard dress-box formats.

The No. 3 format delivers the full Vuelta Abajo medium-bodied profile — citrus zest, toasted almond, cedar, and a trailing dark chocolate finish — in a shorter smoke that loses none of the blend's structural coherence.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.3 (3 Sticks per Box) (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo colorado-soil Habano, hand-rolled long-filler, cedar-lined aluminum tube — the No.3 petit corona in medium-bodied house style.
description (EN Store): When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Romeo y Julieta factory in Havana in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, the brand immortalised his name in a vitola format now adopted universally across the cigar world — a single visit that reshaped an entire industry's vocabulary. The No. 3, a petit corona expression, draws from the same Vuelta Abajo terroir: red colorado clay soils in Pinar del Río's Protected Appellation of Origin, where warm humidity and consistent trade winds produce filler leaves of exceptional combustibility and aromatic density. Shade-grown Criollo wrapper from the same zone delivers a finer-textured, more restrained exterior leaf. Production follows totalmente a mano, tripa larga — entirely handmade, long-filler construction by torcedores — with leaves air-cured in ventilated barns for 25–50 days, then bulk-fermented in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and develop layered complexity. The cedar-lined aluminum tube (Cedros de Luxe format) is not merely packaging: the cedar insert actively continues micro-humidification and imparts a secondary aromatic layer absent in standard box presentation. At the petit corona format, the No. 3 concentrates the house's citrus-almond-cedar profile into a shorter, more intense draw than the flagship Churchill — the same all-Cuban blend, compressed into a format that burns cooler and delivers more immediate complexity per centimetre.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  Short Churchills (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Short Churchills in cedar-lined aluminum tube — Vuelta Abajo colorado-soil Habano, totalmente a mano, long-filler, medium-bodied aromatic complexity.
description (EN Store): When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Romeo y Julieta factory in Havana in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, he inadvertently gave every cigar brand on earth a new format name — a measure of how deeply this house has shaped the global vocabulary of the cigar world. The Short Churchills distils that same DNA into a shorter ring-gauge format, drawing exclusively on Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río tobacco: the region's red colorado clay soils, warm humid microclimate, and consistent trade winds yield leaves with an unusually precise balance of combustibility, aromatic intensity, and restrained strength. Shade-grown Criollo wrapper leaves deliver a finer-textured, oil-rich surface, while sun-grown Vuelta Abajo leaves form both filler and binder in an all-Cuban blend. Every cigar is constructed totalmente a mano, tripa larga — totally handmade with long-filler — by torcedores in Havana, with leaves having undergone bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C to strip ammonia and build layered flavor before rolling. The cedar-lined aluminum tube is the special feature here: it actively humidifies the cigar in transit and storage while the cedar lining imparts a secondary aromatic conditioning unavailable in standard dress-box formats. The result is a medium-bodied smoke — citrus zest, roasted almond, dry cedar, and a trailing cocoa finish — that rewards both the initiated and the curious without demanding the time commitment of a full Churchill.

name: Montecristo  Petit Edmundo (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Petit Edmundo: Vuelta Abajo colorado-wrapper puro, 52-ring robusto corto, hand-rolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory by Montecristo torcedores since 1935.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935, he built the brand on a single conviction: that Vuelta Abajo leaf, rolled with sufficient skill, needed no blending from other Cuban zones. The Petit Edmundo — a 52-ring, 110mm robusto corto — is that conviction compressed into a shorter, wider format that concentrates the house profile without extending the smoking time.

All tobacco originates from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate moderated by Atlantic breezes produce filler, binder, and wrapper of exceptional mineral density. The wide 52-ring gauge forces the torcedor to pack a higher ratio of ligero-heavy filler into the blend, driving body and combustion heat upward relative to the slimmer No. 4.

Totalmente a Mano construction uses the entubado or accordion-fold long-filler technique; leaves undergo natural fermentation in pilones before blending. The colorado wrapper is triple-capped and presented uncut — no filtration, no machine intervention. Standard production tobacco is aged approximately 1–2 years pre-rolling.

What distinguishes the Petit Edmundo within the core Montecristo line is its ring gauge: at 52, it is the widest vitola in the standard portfolio, producing a draw resistance and smoke volume that pushes the house's characteristic cedar-cocoa-earth profile into decidedly fuller territory than the No. 2 or No. 4.

Best smoked after a minimum of 6–12 months of additional humidor rest; the wide gauge rewards patience as the blend integrates and the wrapper sweetness emerges fully.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  Short Churchills (15 Sticks per Box) (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo colorado-soil Habano, short Churchill format in cedar-lined aluminum tube — the vitola format Romeo y Julieta gave the world in 1946.
description (EN Store): When Sir Winston Churchill visited the Romeo y Julieta factory in Havana in 1946 and declared his preference for the 7×47 Julieta No. 2, the brand named the size after him — and every cigar house on earth subsequently adopted 'Churchill' as a standard vitola designation. The Short Churchills compress that legacy into a shorter ring, preserving the house blend's architecture in a more concentrated draw. Tobacco is sourced exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin — where red colorado clay soils, warm humid air, and reliable trade winds produce leaves with an exceptional balance of combustibility, strength, and aromatic complexity. Sun-grown leaves form the filler and binder; a shade-grown Criollo wrapper from the same zone delivers finer texture and controlled burn. Post-harvest, leaves are air-cured 25–50 days in ventilated barns, then undergo bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and develop layered flavor. Each cigar is totalmente a mano, tripa larga — long-filler, entirely hand-rolled by torcedores. The cedar-lined aluminum tube is not merely packaging: it actively regulates humidity during transit and aging, making this the format of choice for gifting and cellaring. The medium-bodied house profile — citrus zest, roasted almond, cedar, and a trailing dark chocolate finish — remains consistent across the 15-stick box.

name: Montecristo  Edmundo (25 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Handrolled Havana puro from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — the Edmundo vitola in Montecristo's medium-to-full house register.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built a brand around a single proposition: maximum consistency at medium-to-full body — a deliberate counterpoint to the peppery aggression of Bolívar and Partagás. That house philosophy survives intact in the Edmundo, a robusto extra vitola (52 ring gauge, 135mm) that sits at the fuller end of the core portfolio.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate moderated by Atlantic breezes concentrate mineral complexity and aromatic richness in the leaf. No other Cuban province contributes to this 100% puro blend.

Construction is Totalmente a Mano by trained torcedores using the traditional entubado or accordion-fold long-filler technique, which creates individual leaf channels that promote even airflow and combustion. Leaves undergo natural fermentation in pilones before blending; standard production tobacco is aged approximately 1–2 years pre-rolling. The cigar is hand-finished with a triple cap and presented uncut.

The Edmundo's wider ring gauge distinguishes it from the slender No. 1 and No. 2 formats, slowing combustion and amplifying the cedar-cocoa core that defines the Montecristo house style — a format transgression within a brand historically associated with thinner, longer vitolas.

As a non-vintage production cigar, the Edmundo rewards 2–5 years of cellaring at 65–70% RH, during which the colorado wrapper deepens and the characteristic subtle sweetness integrates further.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  No.1 (3 Sticks per Box) (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo colorado-soil Habano, hand-rolled long-filler, cedar-lined aluminum tube — the No.1 petit corona in its most travel-ready format.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1875 by Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García, Romeo y Julieta built its reputation on a single deliberate conviction: that Vuelta Abajo tobacco, handled with absolute fidelity to long-filler construction, could deliver complexity without demanding full-body from the smoker. The No.1 — a petit corona vitola — distils that philosophy into a shorter, more concentrated format. Tobacco is grown exclusively in Pinar del Río's Vuelta Abajo zone, a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin whose red colorado clay-rich soils, warm humid microclimate, and reliable trade winds produce leaves with an exceptional balance of combustibility, aroma, and measured strength. Filler and binder leaves are sun-grown; the wrapper is a finer-textured shade-grown Criollo leaf from the same zone. Post-harvest, leaves undergo air-curing for 25–50 days in ventilated barns, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to purge ammonia and develop layered flavor compounds. Each cigar is then hand-rolled by torcedores using the totalmente a mano, tripa larga method — no short-filler compromise. The aluminum tube is cedar-lined, preserving humidity and imparting a subtle resinous note during resting. What distinguishes this format is the cedar-tube packaging itself: it functions as a micro-aging vessel, allowing the No.1 to continue developing after leaving the factory in a way that standard box presentation does not replicate. The compact ring gauge concentrates the colorado-soil earthiness and almond character into a tighter, more immediate draw than the flagship Churchill.

name: Partagas  Aristocrats (25 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo long-filler Habano, hand-rolled at Partagás' Francisco Pérez Germán factory — full-bodied, peppery, dark cocoa-driven.
description (EN Store): Don Jaime Partagás y Ravell, who founded the house in 1845, is credited with pioneering systematic tobacco fermentation in Cuba — the multi-stage process that concentrates flavour and strips harshness from raw leaf, and the direct reason Partagás has maintained its distinctively dense, full-bodied profile across nearly 180 years of production. The Aristocrats are rolled at the Francisco Pérez Germán factory in Central Havana, the casa matrice that also produces Bolívar and Ramón Allones, using 100% Vuelta Abajo leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler sourced exclusively from Pinar del Río's westernmost zone, where red clay soils and a humid microclimate produce tobacco of exceptional oil content and structural density. Construction follows the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method: each torcedor assembles the entire cigar individually, from filler arrangement through wrapper application, with draw-testing on every finished stick and a senior quality-control team maintaining a 10–15% rejection rate. No additives or casing agents are used; the blend is 100% natural tobacco throughout. What distinguishes the Aristocrats within the Partagás portfolio is the vitola format itself — a longer, thinner gauge that draws the same full-bodied Vuelta Abajo blend through a narrower column, concentrating pepper and cedar while extending the smoke time relative to the robusto-format Serie D. Standard production; no stated post-rolling aging requirement, though boxed cigars rest before release.

name: Partagas  Serie D No.4 (3 Sticks per Box) (Aluminum Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Vuelta Abajo red-clay tobacco, long-filler hand-rolled at Havana's Francisco Pérez Germán factory — the robusto format that defined a category since the 1930s.
description (EN Store):
Don Jaime Partagás is credited with pioneering systematic tobacco fermentation in Cuba — the multi-stage post-harvest process that is the direct technical reason this house's full-bodied profile has held consistent across nearly 180 years of production. That same fermentation discipline underpins every Serie D No. 4 leaving the Francisco Pérez Germán factory today.

All leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler — originates exclusively from the Vuelta Abajo zone of Pinar del Río, Cuba's westernmost province, where red clay soils and a humid Atlantic microclimate produce tobacco of unusual density and oil content. The Colorado wrapper typical of the Serie D line reflects that terroir directly: mid-brown, visibly oily, structurally tight.

Construction follows the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method — long filler assembled and wrapped entirely by a single torcedor, draw-tested on completion, and subject to spot-check rejection rates of 10–15%. No additives or casing agents are used; 100% natural Cuban tobacco throughout. The aluminium tube (tubo) format preserves humidity without a humidor, making these three sticks travel-ready without compromise to construction.

What distinguishes the Serie D No. 4 within the Habano world is its position as the format that codified the robusto vitola as a global standard — a 52-ring-gauge, 124mm cigar that balances draw resistance, burn time, and flavour development in a way no peer has displaced since the 1930s.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  Mini (20 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made mini format from Vuelta Abajo's red colorado soils — the accessible face of Cuba's most format-diverse Habano house, founded 1875.
description (EN Store): When Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García registered Romeo y Julieta in 1875, they built the brand on a deliberate philosophy of accessibility without compromise — a principle that explains why this house now offers the widest vitola range of any Habanos marque, from the mini Puritos format to the 7×47 Churchill. The Mini (Puritos) sits at the machine-made end of that spectrum, distinct from the brand's flagship Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Larga handmade production, and is produced using processed short-filler tobacco rather than the long-filler leaves reserved for standard vitolas. Tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin — where red colorado clay-rich soils, warm humid microclimate, and reliable trade winds yield leaves with an optimal balance of strength, aroma, and combustibility. Post-harvest leaves undergo air-curing for 25–50 days in ventilated barns, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to reduce ammonia and develop aromatic complexity. The special feature here is format transgression: Romeo y Julieta deploys its all-Cuban Vuelta Abajo blend identity — cedar, almond, citrus, subtle chocolate — into a machine-made petit format that no other Habanos house matches in breadth of size offering. The medium-bodied profile remains consistent, making the Mini a genuine entry point into the house style.

name: Jose L. Piedra  Petit Caballeros (3 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Cuba's sole Habanos brand built entirely on Vuelta Arriba short-filler — a deliberate rejection of Vuelta Abajo orthodoxy, rolled Totalmente a Mano since c.2006.
description (EN Store):
When Habanos S.A. reclassified José L. Piedra as a Global brand in 2007, it was a quiet acknowledgement of something the brand had always known: that Remedios tobacco, grown in Villa Clara's central plains rather than Pinar del Río's celebrated red colorado soils, could build a loyal international following on its own uncompromising terms. The Petit Caballeros — a 48-ring-gauge, 120 mm vitola added to the portfolio only in 2019 — is the newest and widest format in the line, designed to open the blend's fuller character at a shorter smoke time.

All filler, binder, and wrapper originate exclusively from the Vuelta Arriba / Remedios sub-region, a growing zone whose less-celebrated soils yield leaf with a stronger, more tannic, and more rustic character than the silkier output of Vuelta Abajo. This is not a compromise — it is the brand's founding identity. Construction is Totalmente a Mano, Tripa Corta: short-filler pieces bunched and wrapped entirely by hand, a method abandoned by virtually every other premium Habanos marque. The result is a medium-to-full strength profile with pronounced leather, cocoa, raw wood, and pepper — texturally denser and more immediate than long-filler peers at any price point.

José L. Piedra has never produced a single Edición Limitada or Edición Regional, yet held 9.3% of total Habanos export share by 2001. The Petit Caballeros is presented in a display box of 15, untubed and cellophane-wrapped.

name: Montecristo  Puritos (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made Puritos from Vuelta Abajo leaf — Montecristo's house cedar-cocoa profile in a compact, approachable cigarillo format.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, the brand was built on a single conviction: that Vuelta Abajo leaf, handled with discipline, could deliver consistency at scale. The Puritos sit at the accessible edge of that legacy — machine-made cigarillo variants distinct from the handmade portfolio, yet drawing on the same tobacco origin.

Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, is Cuba's premier growing zone: red clay (colorado) soil moderated by Atlantic ocean breezes produces leaf of concentrated mineral complexity and natural sweetness that defines the Montecristo house profile — cedar, earth, and cocoa sitting between the peppery intensity of Bolívar and the lighter register of Hoyo de Monterrey.

Unlike the core handmade line — rolled Totalmente a Mano by torcedores using entubado or accordion-fold technique — the Puritos are machine-made, a deliberate format choice that trades the nuance of long-filler construction for accessibility and price efficiency. Filler, binder, and wrapper remain sourced from Vuelta Abajo, preserving the D.O.P. Cuban tobacco origin.

The defining transgression here: this is the only format in the Montecristo portfolio that openly steps outside the handmade Habano tradition, making the brand's signature flavor architecture available in a short, fast smoke. Five sticks per box; no aging designation applies.

name: Partagas  Chicos Cello (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Partagas Chicos: Vuelta Abajo long-filler, hand-rolled at Francisco Pérez Germán factory — full-bodied Cuban intensity in a short, sharp format.
description (EN Store):
Don Jaime Partagás y Ravell is credited with pioneering systematic tobacco fermentation in Cuba — the multi-stage post-harvest process that drives the deep, earthy complexity the house has maintained since 1845. The Chicos format delivers that same Partagás DNA in a compact vitola, built entirely from 100% Vuelta Abajo leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler all sourced from Pinar del Río's westernmost zone, where red clay soils and a persistently humid microclimate produce tobacco of unusual density and oil content.

Every Chicos is constructed by a single torcedor using the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method — long filler assembled and wrapped by hand in one continuous process, with no short-filler blending. Finished cigars pass draw-testing machines and senior quality-control inspection, with a rejection rate of approximately 10–15%. No casing agents or additives are used; the tobacco is 100% natural throughout.

What distinguishes the Chicos within the Partagas portfolio is format transgression: the house's characteristically full-bodied, peppery-earthy profile — typically expressed across longer, slower-burning vitolas — is compressed into a small cigar that delivers intensity without the time commitment. A direct entry point into one of Havana's most structurally dense blends.

name: Romeo Y Julieta  Puritos (5 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): Machine-made Puritos from Vuelta Abajo's colorado clay soils — Romeo y Julieta's accessible petit format, air-cured and pilón-fermented for aromatic complexity.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1875 by Inocencio Alvarez and Manin García, Romeo y Julieta became the house that literally named the Churchill format after Sir Winston visited the Havana factory in 1946 — yet the Puritos sits at the opposite end of the portfolio, a compact machine-made petit cigar designed for shorter smoking occasions. Tobacco originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, a Habanos Protected Appellation of Origin whose red, clay-rich colorado soils, warm humid microclimate, and consistent trade winds yield leaves with an unusually precise balance of combustibility, aroma, and measured strength. Sun-grown leaves form the filler and binder; a shade-grown Criollo wrapper from the same zone delivers a finer-textured draw. Post-harvest, leaves undergo air-curing in ventilated barns for 25–50 days, followed by bulk fermentation in pilones at 40–50°C for 15–90 days to strip ammonia and build layered flavor compounds. The critical distinction: unlike the brand's flagship handmade vitolas — rolled totalmente a mano, tripa larga — the Puritos is machine-produced, making it the one format in the Romeo y Julieta line that breaks the house's handmade rule. The result is a consistent, medium-bodied smoke with the house signature of cedar, almond, and citrus in a format that finishes in under 20 minutes.

name: Cohiba  Short (10 Sticks per Box)
short_description (EN Store): The only Habanos brand applying a third barrel fermentation to filler leaves — born as a state secret in 1966, reserved for Castro before any public sale.
description (EN Store):
Cohiba began in 1966 as a state secret: rolled at the converted French-colonial mansion of El Laguito, Cubanacán, Havana, exclusively for Fidel Castro and diplomatic gifts — no public sale for nearly 16 years. That origin shaped a production philosophy of uncompromising selectivity that persists today.

Tobacco is drawn exclusively from approximately 10 Vegas Finas de Primera in the San Luis and San Juan y Martínez zones of Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río — Cuba's most prized alluvial red-clay and sandy-loam soils. In an average year, only the output of roughly five of those fields clears Cohiba's quality threshold.

The defining technical signature is a mandatory third barrel fermentation applied to the seco and ligero filler leaves — stacked in small wooden barrels in temperature-controlled closets at El Laguito, lasting up to 18 months. No other Habanos brand employs this step. It strips residual nicotine and tars while building layered aromatic complexity unavailable through standard two-fermentation Cuban production. The three-leaf filler construction — Volado, Seco, Ligero — is finished by hand by skilled torcedores and fumigated at Habanos S.A.'s Guanabacoa facility post-rolling.

The Short vitola delivers the Línea Clásica profile — cedar, dark chocolate, roasted coffee — in a compact format that concentrates the third-fermentation character into a shorter, more intense smoke without sacrificing structural complexity.

name: Montecristo  Petit Edmundo (No Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Petit Edmundo: Vuelta Abajo colorado-wrapper robusto gordo, hand-rolled at Havana's H. Upmann factory by torcedores using entubado technique.
description (EN Store): When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built a brand on artisan continuity — a philosophy that survived nationalisation only because torcedor José Manuel González stayed on as floor manager after the Menéndez family fled in 1960, preserving the house's rolling standards through the revolution. The Petit Edmundo is a 52-ring robusto gordo vitola, short enough for a focused smoke yet wide enough to carry the full Montecristo blend architecture. All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate tempered by ocean breezes produce leaves of pronounced mineral complexity and natural sweetness. Construction is Totalmente a Mano, using the traditional entubado or accordion-fold long-filler technique that creates individual leaf tubes within the bunch, maximising draw consistency and even combustion across the wide ring gauge. Tobacco undergoes natural fermentation in pilones before blending, with standard production leaves aged approximately one to two years pre-rolling. What distinguishes the Petit Edmundo within the core portfolio is its format transgression: the 52-ring gauge forces the medium-to-full blend into a denser, slower burn that amplifies the cocoa and cedar registers the house is known for, while the shorter length keeps the smoke cooler than a full Edmundo. Drink now or rest up to five years in a calibrated humidor at 65–70% RH.

name: Montecristo  Open Master (No Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Handmade Cuban puro from Vuelta Abajo's red clay colorado soils — the Open line's accessible entry into Montecristo's creamy, cocoa-driven house profile.
description (EN Store):
When Alonso Menéndez launched Montecristo in 1935 at Havana's Particulares Factory, he built a brand around consistency at scale — a discipline so rigorous that Montecristo today accounts for roughly one-quarter of all Habanos S.A. worldwide sales without sacrificing the handmade standard. The Open Master is the brand's deliberate move toward a shorter, more accessible format while retaining the full Totalmente a Mano construction: long-filler leaves folded via entubado or accordion technique, triple-capped by torcedores, and finished uncut.

All tobacco — filler, binder, and wrapper — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's premier growing zone, where red clay (colorado) soils and a tropical microclimate tempered by ocean breezes yield leaves of pronounced mineral complexity and natural sweetness. Standard production tobacco undergoes natural fermentation in pilones and approximately one to two years of aging before rolling. The result sits squarely in Montecristo's medium-to-full-bodied house register: creamy cedar and cocoa up front, earth mid-palate, with the subtle sweetness that separates the marque from the peppery intensity of Bolívar or Partagás. The Open line's special feature is its format philosophy — shorter vitolas engineered for a complete smoke in under an hour, breaking the Cuban tradition that equates prestige exclusively with larger ring gauges and extended smoking times.

name: Partagas  Serie P No.2 (No Tube)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Cuban torpedo from Vuelta Abajo's red clay soils — hand-rolled at Partagás' Francisco Pérez Germán factory since 1845.
description (EN Store): Don Jaime Partagás y Ravell is credited with pioneering systematic tobacco fermentation in Cuba — the multi-stage process that unlocks the deep, earthy density the house has maintained across nearly 180 years of production. That innovation is the direct reason the Serie P No. 2 smokes the way it does. All leaf — wrapper, binder, and filler — originates exclusively from Vuelta Abajo, Pinar del Río, Cuba's westernmost growing zone, where red clay soils and a persistently humid microclimate produce tobacco of unusual structural density and aromatic concentration. The torpedo vitola is constructed by a single torcedor using the Tripa Larga, Totalmente a Mano method — long filler assembled and wrapped entirely by hand — with finished cigars passing draw-testing machines and senior quality-control inspection before release; rejection rates run 10–15%. What sets the Serie P No. 2 apart within the Partagás portfolio is the torpedo format itself: the tapered head concentrates smoke and amplifies the full-body profile — rated among the most powerful in the standard Habanos lineup — beyond what the robusto-format Serie D delivers. Presented without tube in natural cedar-lined format, this is a cigar built for extended smoking sessions where the full arc of the Partagás profile — pepper, dark cocoa, leather, cedar — can develop across its length.

name: St-Rémy  X.O (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Premium French X.O brandy with rich vanilla oak, dried fruit, and warming spice—elegant digestif.
description (EN Store): St-Rémy X.O represents a mature, well-aged French brandy with a full, warming body and low acidity characteristic of extended cask maturation. The spirit opens with prominent vanilla and oak influence from years in wood, followed by layers of dried fruit, caramel sweetness, and subtle spice. The finish is smooth and lingering, with gentle warmth and complexity that rewards sipping neat or in premium cocktails.

name: Remy Martin  Louis XIII (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): ~1,200 eaux-de-vie aged 40–100 years, Grande Champagne chalk only — distilled on lees in charentais copper pot stills, rested in century-old Limousin oak.
description (EN Store): Louis XIII is a perpetual accumulation of time: each decanter holds approximately 1,200 individual eaux-de-vie, the youngest aged 40 years, the oldest approaching a century — assembled across generations of cellar masters, no single person alive at blending having witnessed the first distillation in the blend. That continuity is the product's defining technical reality. Every component originates exclusively from Grande Champagne, Cognac's innermost and most prized cru, where deep chalky-calcareous subsoils retain moisture through dry summers, sustaining grape acidity and aromatic concentration in Ugni Blanc that shallower soils cannot. Rémy Martin distils on the lees — retaining residual yeast sediment through double distillation in small charentais alembic copper pot stills — a house-wide signature that adds measurable aromatic depth peers who rack before distillation cannot replicate. Maturation proceeds exclusively in French Limousin oak from trees at least 100 years old, wood seasoned outdoors 2–3 years before coopering; the large open grain drives substantial oxygen exchange, building the rancio character and layered spice that define the expression. The oldest parcels are transferred to glass demi-johns to arrest evaporation and preserve the most ancient eaux-de-vie intact. What no peer replicates at this scale: Louis XIII draws from Grande Champagne exclusively — not Fine Champagne blended with Petite — making it the purest single-cru expression in the house's range and among the most terroir-specific cognacs in commercial production.

name: Remy Martin  V.S.O.P (700 ml)
