description (EN Store): Matsui Shuzo, a Tottori sake and spirits house operating since 1910, acquired its whisky distillation license only in 2015 — meaning the Pure Malt tier predates any in-house distillate and is built on sourced Scotch single malt whisky blended with Japanese malt and matured in Japan, a provenance the early labelling did not make explicit. That controversy is the essential context for understanding what this bottle is and is not. The distillery sits at the foothills of Mt. Daisen (1,729 m), an extinct volcano in Tottori Prefecture whose volcanic rock filters the deep-aquifer groundwater used throughout production — a source the producer credits for the house style's characteristic softness. The San-in Coast's extreme seasonal swing — sub-zero winters to 38 °C summers — accelerates cask interaction in a way the producer explicitly compares to Islay maturation. This expression is finished in ex-sherry casks, layering dried-fruit concentration and oxidative spice over the bourbon-matured base; bottled at 43% ABV within the Pure Malt range. The special feature: unlike most sherry-finished Japanese whiskies from major houses, this is an NAS blended malt openly drawing on Scotch malt character, making the sherry cask the primary flavour architect rather than a polish on long-aged house spirit. Chill-filtration status is not disclosed by the producer.

name: Jack Daniel's  Old No.7 Cradle Limited Edition (1 L)
short_description (EN Store): Cave Spring Hollow limestone water + 10-foot sugar maple charcoal vats: the Lincoln County Process that legally separates Tennessee Whiskey from bourbon.
description (EN Store):
The technique at the core of this whiskey was originally taught to Jack Daniel by Nathan 'Nearest' Green — an enslaved man who served as the distillery's first Master Distiller, a contribution erased from official histories for over a century. That method, the Lincoln County Process, remains the defining act: new-make spirit distilled to 140 proof is gravity-fed drop-by-drop through 10-foot vats of handmade sugar maple charcoal over 3–5 days before any barrel contact, stripping harsh congeners in a step that legally and stylistically separates Tennessee Whiskey from bourbon under Tennessee HB 1084 (2013).

The distillery occupies Cave Spring Hollow, Lynchburg, Moore County, anchored by a limestone cave spring holding a constant 56°F (13°C) year-round. Limestone filtration removes iron — detrimental to whiskey flavor — while contributing calcium and magnesium. Multi-storey rickhouses expose barrels to Tennessee's full seasonal temperature swings, driving active wood interaction across floors.

The 80% corn / 12% malted barley / 8% rye mash bill ferments in open-top cypress vats using a sour mash process (up to 30% backset for pH control), then distilled through a 100-foot copper column still with doubler. Spirit enters 100% new charred American white oak at 125 proof. Old No. 7 is mingled from 175–200 barrels per batch — upper and lower rickhouse floors blended deliberately — then bottled at 40% ABV, chill-filtered. This Cradle Limited Edition presents the standard expression in a collector format without altering the production parameters.

name: The Glenturret  30 Years Single Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's oldest documented distillery, 30 years in dunnage on Loch Turret water — a rare long-aged release from Crieff's smallest still pair.
description (EN Store): When Lalique Group's Silvio Denz acquired Glenturret in 2019, he inherited a site documented to 1763 — and a cellar of long-sleeping casks that predate the distillery's entire identity pivot away from Famous Grouse blending stock. This 30 Year Old is drawn from that legacy inventory, aged in six on-site dunnage warehouses in the sheltered 'Hosh' glen on the banks of the Turret Burn, Crieff, Perthshire. Process water is piped exclusively from Loch Turret, a Grampian Mountain reservoir fed by Ben Chonzie runoff; the catchment geology yields exceptionally soft water that shapes the distillate's famously light, high-ester new-make character from the outset. That new-make originates from one of Scotland's smallest still pairs — a 12,500-litre wash still and a 9,000-litre spirit still fitted with a reflux bowl — with only the middle cut retained, producing a delicate, fruity spirit that three decades in American and European oak sherry-seasoned casks have slowly deepened without overwhelming. The special feature here is structural: a 30-year age statement from a distillery whose entire core range tops out publicly at 21 years, making this an outlier expression that tests the house's light-bodied style against extreme oak contact. No vintage character notes or critic scores are publicly documented for this release; drinking window is open now, with the soft-water distillate providing the acidity to sustain further cellaring.

name: Matsui  The Daisen Wine Cask Blended Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Mt. Daisen volcanic-aquifer water meets ex-wine cask finishing — Matsui Shuzo's blended whisky draws on Tottori's extreme seasonal swings for accelerated maturation.
description (EN Store):
Matsui Shuzo, operating from Kurayoshi City since 1910, acquired its whisky distillation license only in 2015 — meaning this blended expression draws on a deliberately compressed production history, with in-house distillation at Kurayoshi Distillery commencing in 2017 using two Japanese-made copper pot stills (wash still 5,000 L; spirit still 3,000 L) fitted with adjustable lyne arms that allow the distiller to dial between lighter and heavier spirit profiles run by run.

The distillery sits at the foothills of Mt. Daisen (1,729 m), an extinct volcano whose fractured basalt filters deep-aquifer groundwater to exceptional softness — that water character is the throughline of every Matsui expression, producing a mellow, low-mineral sweetness that distinguishes the house style from the harder-water profiles of Nikka or Suntory. Tottori's Sea of Japan climate compounds this: near-freezing winters against summers reaching 38 °C create thermal cycling that accelerates cask extraction in a manner the producer explicitly compares to Islay conditions.

The Daisen Wine Cask expression introduces ex-wine barrel finishing into the blended whisky tier — a deliberate departure from the bourbon-primary aging that anchors most of the range. This finishing layer adds stone-fruit and light tannic grip absent from the standard NAS blended expressions. As a blended whisky, this expression draws on malt and grain whiskies from undisclosed domestic and international partners rather than solely in-house distillate. Special feature: wine cask finishing on a sub-฿1,500 Japanese blended whisky is structurally rare in this price tier, where ex-bourbon dominates by default.

name: Wolfburn  Single Malt Whisky Small Batch No.204 (Limited Edition) (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's most northerly mainland distillery, fully manual grain-to-glass, non-chill filtered Small Batch No.204 from Caithness flagstone-filtered spring water.
description (EN Store):
When Aurora Brewing Ltd. revived Wolfburn in 2013 — siting the new distillery 350 metres from the ruins of William Smith's 1821 original at Henderson Park, Thurso — the deliberate choice was to build with zero automation, a commitment that holds through every Small Batch release today. Process water is drawn from the Wolf Burn itself, a spring-fed stream filtered through Caithness flagstone, arriving crystal-clear and cold, contributing to the clean, precise spirit character that defines the house style.

The extreme northerly coastal position of Caithness — the Scottish mainland's northernmost distillery — subjects dunnage-warehoused casks to a mild maritime climate year-round, threading a subtle saline edge through the fruit-forward spirit. Casks rest on clay floors, stacked no higher than three, in conditions that slow extraction and preserve delicacy.

The spirit still's reflux bowl delivers extended copper contact, producing a lighter, fruitier new-make than most Highland peers. Long fermentation in stainless steel washbacks — sourced from the closed Caperdonich distillery — builds the biscuity, orchard-fruit backbone. Small Batch No.204 is non-chill filtered and naturally coloured, bottled on-site at Wolfburn's own purpose-built facility, one of very few Scottish distilleries to distil, mature, and bottle entirely under one roof.

What sets No.204 apart within the Small Batch series is the specific cask selection driving this batch's profile — a direct expression of Wolfburn's cask-diversity philosophy rather than any single wood type dominating. The 2013 revival stock is now entering genuine maturity, and the complexity gap between early NAS releases and current batches is measurable.

name: Wolfburn  Single Malt Whisky Small Batch No.458 (Limited Edition) (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's most northerly mainland distillery, fully manual grain-to-glass, non-chill filtered Small Batch No.458 from Caithness flagstone-filtered spring water.
description (EN Store): When Aurora Brewing Ltd. revived Wolfburn in 2013 — on Henderson Park, Thurso, just 350 metres from the ruins of William Smith's original 1821 distillery — they made a deliberate choice to automate nothing. Every stage from milling through on-site bottling is entirely manual, a grain-to-glass integrity claim almost no Scottish distillery of any scale can match. Process water is drawn from the Wolf Burn itself, a spring-fed stream naturally filtered through Caithness flagstone, arriving crystal-clear and cold at the most northerly whisky distillery on the Scottish mainland. The north-coast proximity imparts a mild maritime salinity during maturation in traditional dunnage warehouses — clay floors, low ceilings, casks stacked no higher than three. Distillation runs through two Forsyths copper pot stills; the spirit still features a reflux bowl for additional copper contact, deliberately pulling the new make toward a lighter, fruitier profile. Long fermentation in stainless steel washbacks — repurposed from the closed Caperdonich distillery — builds the biscuity, fruity character that defines the house style. Small Batch No.458 is bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, with no artificial colouring, at Wolfburn's own on-site bottling facility. The special feature here is the batch-specific cask selection within a fully manual, zero-automation production chain — a level of hands-on traceability that larger Highland peers cannot replicate. Drink now through the mid-2030s.

name: Wolfburn  Single Malt Whisky Year of The Bull (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's most northerly mainland distillery, fully manual grain-to-glass, non-chill filtered — a Lunar New Year limited release from Caithness flagstone-filtered spring water.
description (EN Store): Wolfburn was revived on Burns Night 2013 at Henderson Park, Thurso — just 350 metres from the rubble of William Smith's original 1821 distillery — after more than 150 years of silence, making this Year of the Bull release part of the distillery's earliest maturing stock. Process water is drawn from the Wolf Burn itself, a spring-fed stream naturally filtered through Caithness flagstone, arriving at the stills crystal-clear and cold, contributing to the house's characteristically clean, light-bodied spirit. Two Forsyths copper pot stills — a 5,500-litre wash still and a 3,600–3,800-litre spirit still fitted with a reflux bowl — drive additional copper contact, deliberately engineering a fruity, biscuity profile over weight or peat. Long fermentation in four ex-Caperdonich stainless steel washbacks amplifies that fruit-forward character before distillation. Maturation takes place in traditional dunnage warehouses on-site (clay floors, maximum three casks stacked), where the proximity to the north Caithness coast imparts a mild maritime edge. Wolfburn is one of the only Scottish distilleries to distil, mature, and bottle entirely on-site with zero automation — the Year of the Bull is non-chill filtered and naturally coloured, bottled at 46% ABV. As an NV limited release, it draws on the distillery's cask diversity (ex-Bourbon, Oloroso Sherry, and Port) to frame a celebratory expression that no age-statement peer from this latitude can replicate.

name: Wolfburn  Cask Strength  Sherry & Bourbon Cask (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's most northerly mainland distillery, bottled on-site at cask strength — Sherry & Bourbon casks, zero automation, natural colour.
description (EN Store): Wolfburn was silent for over 150 years before Aurora Brewing Ltd. relit the stills on Burns Night, 25 January 2013 — a deliberate symbolic choice that anchored the revival to Scottish cultural identity while committing to a production philosophy with almost no modern parallel. Henderson Park, Thurso, Caithness sits closer to the Arctic Circle than to Edinburgh, and process water drawn from the Wolf Burn — a spring-fed stream filtered through ancient Caithness flagstone — arrives at the stills crystal-clear and cold, contributing to the distillery's characteristically clean, fruit-forward new make. The coastal proximity imparts a mild maritime salinity during maturation in traditional dunnage warehouses (clay floors, maximum three casks stacked). This expression draws from both ex-Oloroso Sherry hogsheads/butts and ex-Bourbon American oak casks, the interplay of dried-fruit density and vanilla-driven sweetness shaped by the reflux bowl on the spirit still — a design choice by Forsyths of Rothes that deliberately lightens the distillate, preventing wood from overwhelming the house's fruity, biscuity core. Bottled at natural cask strength, non-chill filtered, with no artificial colouring — all carried out on-site at Wolfburn's own bottling facility, one of the only Scottish distilleries to complete the entire grain-to-glass process without leaving the property. The zero-automation mandate (the sole computer governs mash tun temperatures) means every cask selection and vatting decision is a human one.

name: Wolfburn  Aged 10 Years Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Scotland's most northerly mainland distillery debuts its first age-statement: 10 years in 100% second-fill Oloroso Sherry cask, non-chill filtered at 46% ABV.
description (EN Store):
On Burns Night, 25 January 2013, Wolfburn lit its stills for the first time in over 150 years — and the spirit drawn that day, matured entirely in second-fill Oloroso Sherry casks, became the backbone of this 2023 debut age-statement release, marking the distillery's formal transition from NAS producer to a house with declared vintage stock.

Henderson Park, Thurso, sits at the extreme northern tip of the Scottish mainland, where Caithness flagstone filters the Wolf Burn spring water to near-crystalline clarity and cold coastal air slows maturation in traditional dunnage warehouses — clay floors, low ceilings, maximum three casks stacked — compressing the interaction between spirit and wood into something measured and deliberate rather than accelerated.

The spirit itself is shaped by a reflux bowl on the 3,600-litre spirit still (Forsyths of Rothes), which maximises copper contact and drives the house's characteristically light, fruity profile. Long fermentation in ex-Caperdonich stainless steel washbacks builds biscuity, orchard-fruit esters before distillation. The 10 Year Old is bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, natural colour — and uniquely, distilled, matured, and bottled entirely on-site with zero automation, a grain-to-glass integrity claim almost no Scottish distillery of comparable scale can match.

The exclusive use of 100% second-fill Oloroso Sherry casks — rather than the first-fill sherry or mixed-cask approach common among Highland peers — preserves the distillery's delicate spirit character while layering dried fruit and walnut depth without overwhelming oak extraction. Drink now through 2035+.

name: Royal Salute  21 years Richard Quinn Roses Edition II (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Strathisla-vaulted blend where every component — malt and grain alike — carries a minimum 21-year age statement, dressed in a hand-crafted porcelain flagon.
description (EN Store):
Royal Salute was born on 2 June 1953, created by Chivas Brothers to mark the coronation of Queen Elizabeth II — a founding purpose that locked in an uncompromising standard: no component whisky, grain or malt, may enter the blend younger than 21 years. That floor is not a marketing claim but a structural commitment that most luxury single malts do not match even at their entry tier.

The blend is married at the Royal Salute Vault, a dedicated bonded warehouse within Strathisla Distillery, Keith, Moray — the oldest continuously operating distillery in the Scottish Highlands (est. 1786), drawing process water from the Fons Bulliens Well, a peat-free natural spring prized for its softness. Speyside's mild, humid valley microclimate around the River Isla promotes slow, even oak extraction across the two-decade-plus maturation.

The whisky rests in a combination of ex-bourbon American oak and ex-sherry casks, the interplay building the house signature of ripe pear, citrus peel, dried fruit, and vanilla cream over a gently spiced finish. Strathisla's short, squat pot stills (wash stills 12,500 L; spirit stills 8,500 L) encourage copper contact and a characteristically rich, fruity new-make that sustains two decades of wood influence without drying out.

The Richard Quinn Roses Edition II is presented in a hand-crafted porcelain flagon — produced from Cornish clay with double-layer glazing over up to six days — making the vessel itself a collectible object distinct from any other age-statement Scotch at this price point. Drink now or hold; the 21-year floor means the whisky enters the bottle already fully integrated.

name: Jameson  Cold Brew Whiskey & Coffee (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Jameson Cold Brew: triple-distilled Midleton pot still whiskey cold-infused with coffee, bottled at 30% ABV for a clean, unpeated mocha-driven dram.
description (EN Store):
Jameson's unpeated house character — born from a closed gas-fired kiln at New Midleton Distillery in County Cork — makes it an unusually clean canvas for coffee integration. Where peated Scotch peers would clash with roast, Midleton's triple-distilled pot still spirit, drawing water from the Dungourney River and barley from within 50 miles of the distillery, yields a grain-forward sweetness that coffee amplifies rather than fights.

The base follows Jameson's signature architecture: Single Pot Still whiskey (malted and unmalted barley, triple-distilled in copper pot stills) married with maize-based grain whiskey (column-distilled), matured in a combination of ex-bourbon American oak casks and ex-sherry European oak butts. The Cold Brew expression is then cold-infused with Brazilian and Colombian coffee, bottled at 30% ABV — below the standard Irish Whiskey minimum, positioning it technically as a spirit drink rather than a regulated whiskey.

That lower ABV is the defining transgression here: by stepping outside the Irish Whiskey Technical File's 40% floor, the blending team could calibrate sweetness and coffee intensity without the heat that would otherwise mask the cold-brew aromatics. The result foregrounds dark chocolate, roasted arabica, and vanilla oak in a register no standard-strength expression could achieve.

name: The Legendary Silkie Blended Irish Whiskey (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Silkie Blended Irish Whiskey: smooth, approachable blend with vanilla oak and warming spice.
description (EN Store): Silkie Blended Irish Whiskey is a medium-bodied expression showcasing the hallmark smoothness of Irish whiskey tradition. Vanilla and oak dominate the palate, supported by hazelnut and cocoa notes from cask maturation. Warming spice and subtle smoke add complexity, with dark fruit undertones providing depth. Approachable yet layered, this blend suits both casual sipping and food pairing occasions.

name: Jameson  18 Years Irish Whiskey (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Single-distillery blend from Midleton, Cork — 18 years in ex-bourbon & ex-sherry casks, bottled at cask strength from the historic Bow Street warehouse.
description (EN Store): When John Jameson took control of the Bow Street Distillery in Dublin in 1786, the decision to include unmalted barley alongside malted grain in the pot still mash was driven by 18th-century Irish malt tax avoidance — a fiscal workaround that accidentally defined a style unique to Ireland. That same Single Pot Still character anchors the 18 Year expression today. All component whiskeys originate from a single site: New Midleton Distillery, County Cork, fed by the Dungourney River and supplied by over 200 local barley farmers within 50 miles — a single-distillery blend that breaks the multi-source convention of virtually every other blended whiskey house on the planet. Triple distillation through copper pot and column stills strips harsh congeners before the spirit enters a combination of ex-bourbon American oak casks and ex-sherry European oak butts for a minimum of 18 years. The final stage returns the whiskey to the original Bow Street maturation warehouse in Dublin's Smithfield for finishing, before cask-strength bottling — ABV varying by batch, with no dilution to a standard commercial proof. Barley is kiln-dried over natural gas rather than peat, preserving a clean, unsmoked malt backbone that lets the long oak maturation speak without interference. At 18 years, this is the deepest expression of Midleton's house character: the unmalted barley spice of pot still Irish whiskey amplified by two decades of cask dialogue.

name: The Glenlivet  12 years Old 200 Year Anniversary Edition (700 ml) (Limited Edition)
short_description (EN Store): The first legally licensed Highland distillery (1824), bottled to mark 200 years — lantern-still fruit purity over ex-bourbon and European oak.
description (EN Store): In 1824, George Smith accepted a legal licence under the newly enacted Excise Act — a decision that made him a target of illicit rivals but established the founding reference point for Speyside whisky. His deliberate choice to build a lighter, cleaner spirit broke from the heavy drams of the era and defined a regional style that endures today. The Minmore Farm distillery sits high above the River Livet, fed by Josie's Well, a natural mountain spring descending from the surrounding hills; the elevation and cool, damp dunnage conditions slow maturation and preserve the distillate's inherent delicacy across more than 65,000 casks held on site. Fermentation runs approximately 48 hours in wooden washbacks — retained despite higher maintenance cost specifically for the floral, estery lift they contribute. Double distillation through 14 lantern-shaped copper pot stills with pinched waists and long narrow necks maximises reflux; the heart cut is held to roughly 18–20% of total distillate, stripping heads and tails to yield a purer, lighter new-make. The 12-year maturation draws on ex-bourbon American oak and European oak casks, building vanilla and orchard-fruit character without sherry dominance. What distinguishes this anniversary release within the core range is its commemorative framing of the 200-year legal milestone — the expression that started the Speyside template, bottled at the bicentennial. Bottled at 40% ABV, chill-filtered; drink now through the near term.

name: Arran  Malt Sherry Cask Finish (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Lochranza granite-water new make finished in ex-sherry oak — no chill filtration, no colouring, 46% ABV from Scotland's 'new wave' island pioneer.
description (EN Store): When Harold Currie, former Managing Director of Chivas Brothers, revived legal distillation on Arran in 1995 after a 160-year island silence, he made a deliberate break from island convention: no peat, no maritime smoke — instead, a clean, fruit-forward spirit that would let cask work speak clearly. The Sherry Cask Finish is where that philosophy lands most expressively. Water drawn from Loch na Davie cascades through six waterfalls over granite bedrock above Lochranza's Glen Eason Biorach, arriving at the mash tun soft and mineral-clean — a foundation that preserves the distillery's signature citrus brightness through maturation. In the stillhouse, 60–75-hour fermentations in Oregon pine washbacks build fruity congeners before double distillation through tall-necked, long-lyne-arm copper pot stills; the broad wash stills and bulb-shaped spirit stills together produce a new make collected at ~68% ABV with exceptional delicacy. The spirit matures in primarily refill casks before a dedicated ex-sherry European oak finish layers dried fruit, cocoa, and warm spice over the house's characteristic apple-and-citrus core. Crucially, the whisky is bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, with zero caramel colouring — a standard Arran holds across virtually its entire range, rare discipline for a producer at this price point. What sets this expression apart within the Arran portfolio is the sherry finish's ability to amplify body without suppressing the granite-water minerality that defines Lochranza's terroir.

name: Arran  Quarter Cask (Bothy 4) (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Unpeated Islands malt matured in small quarter casks; granite-filtered Loch na Davie water, non-chill filtered at 56.2% natural cask strength.
description (EN Store): When Harold Currie, former Managing Director of Chivas Brothers, revived legal distillation on Arran in 1995 after a 160-year island silence, he made a deliberate transgression: no peat, no maritime smoke — just clean, fruit-driven spirit that defied every Islands convention. The Quarter Cask 'The Bothy' (Batch 4) is that philosophy taken to its most concentrated form. Water drawn from Loch na Davie, high in the granite mountains above Lochranza, cascades through six waterfalls of the Easan Biorach burn, emerging soft and mineral-clean — a direct contributor to the distillery's characteristically bright, citrus-forward new make. Fermentation runs 60–75 hours in Oregon pine washbacks using Kerry M yeast, deliberately extended to build fruity congeners before double distillation through tall-necked, long-lyne-arm copper pot stills. The resulting spirit, collected at ~68% ABV, is then matured in quarter casks — smaller vessels that dramatically increase the wood-to-spirit contact ratio, accelerating the extraction of vanilla and spice from American oak. Bottled at 56.2% ABV natural cask strength, non-chill filtered, with zero caramel colouring, The Bothy breaks from the Islands norm twice over: no peat, and no dilution. The result is a dense, orchard-rich dram where the small-cask oak integration is palpable but the house's signature citrus and cereal core remains legible.

name: Arran  25 Years old Single Malt (700 ml) (Limited)
short_description (EN Store): 25 years on granite-filtered Loch na Davie water — Arran's deepest age statement, non-chill filtered, no colouring, from Scotland's island that defied peat.
description (EN Store): When Harold Currie, former Managing Director of Chivas Brothers, revived legal distillation on Arran in 1995 after a 160-year absence, he made a deliberate transgression: no peat, no maritime smoke — just clean, fruit-forward spirit that would need time to prove itself. Twenty-five years is that proof. The Lochranza distillery sits in Glen Eason Biorach ('Valley of the Sharp Waterfalls'), where water descends through six falls from Loch na Davie, filtering through granite-dominant terrain on the northern side of the Highland Boundary Fault. That granite contact strips the water of hardness while the Gulf Stream microclimate accelerates maturation — conditions that allow a quarter-century of aging without the spirit turning austere. Double distillation through tall-necked wash stills (7,100 L) and bulb-shaped spirit stills (4,300–4,800 L) with long lyne arms produces a new make collected at ~68% ABV, engineered for maximum fruity congener development via 60–75 hour fermentations in Oregon pine washbacks. Maturation draws on ex-bourbon American oak and ex-sherry European oak over the full 25-year span, with no E150a colouring added and no chill filtration — a house-wide commitment that preserves every ester the long distillation built. What no peer at this age does: an unpeated Islands malt reaching 25 years without leaning on smoke or heavy sherry to carry the weight.

name: Bruichladdich  Black Art Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Bruichladdich's most secretive expression: Head Distiller Adam Hannett's undisclosed cask assemblage, bottled unchill-filtered on Islay with zero caramel addition.
description (EN Store): Black Art exists as the single expression in Bruichladdich's portfolio where Head Distiller Adam Hannett discloses nothing — no cask breakdown, no age statement, no recipe. In a house built on radical transparency (barley variety, farm, field, and harvest year printed on every other bottle), this deliberate silence is itself the statement. Matured entirely on Islay in the distillery's flat, brick-built dunnage warehouses on the western shore of Loch Indaal, where Atlantic-maritime air driven by the Gulf Stream and near-constant high humidity slow the spirit's development to a pace impossible to replicate inland. The Rhinns of Islay's coastal microclimate imparts a saline, mineral edge that threads through even the deepest wood influence. Reduction uses water from the Octomore Farm spring, flowing through ancient Lewisian gneiss — the island's oldest rock formation — delivering a high-silica softness unique to this site. Distilled in the distillery's famously tall, narrow-necked spirit stills (6-metre column, 0.9m neck diameter), which maximise copper reflux and produce a clean, high-ester new make capable of absorbing complex cask regimes over long maturation. Bottled without chill-filtration and without caramel colouring — non-negotiable across every Bruichladdich expression. The special feature: Black Art is the only release where the cask composition — rumoured to include first-growth Bordeaux and rare fortified wine wood — is withheld entirely, making each numbered edition a one-time, unrepeatable configuration.

name: Woodford Reserve  Kentucky Derby 148 (Limited Edition) Bourbon Whiskey (1 L)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Kentucky bourbon with vanilla oak, caramel spice, and dark fruit complexity. Limited Derby edition.
description (EN Store): Woodford Reserve Kentucky Derby 148 is a full-bodied bourbon whiskey showcasing the distillery's signature craftsmanship. The palate opens with prominent vanilla and oak character from careful barrel aging, followed by supporting notes of caramel sweetness and warming spice. Dark fruit and mocha undertones add depth, while subtle smoke and hazelnut notes emerge on the finish, reflecting extended wood contact and careful distillation. This limited-edition release balances approachability with complexity, making it suitable for both neat sipping and classic cocktails.

name: Jack Daniel's  Old No.7  McLaren X JD Edition 2024 (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Cave Spring Hollow limestone water, 10-ft sugar maple charcoal mellowing, McLaren F1 2024 livery bottle — Old No.7 in collector dress.
description (EN Store):
The single technical decision that defines this whiskey is one Jack Daniel made before a single barrel was filled: dripping new-make spirit gravity-fed through 10-foot vats of handmade sugar maple charcoal over 3–5 days — the Lincoln County Process — stripping harsh congeners before any wood contact begins. That process, originally taught to Jack Daniel by Nathan 'Nearest' Green, the distillery's first Master Distiller, was largely erased from official histories until the 2010s and now anchors the brand's identity.

The distillery sits in Cave Spring Hollow, Lynchburg, Moore County, Tennessee, fed by a limestone cave spring locked at a constant 56°F (13°C). Limestone filtration removes iron — detrimental to whiskey flavor — while contributing calcium and magnesium to the iron-free process water.

The mash bill runs 80% corn / 12% malted barley / 8% rye, fermented in open-top cypress vats via sour mash (up to 30% backset) for approximately six days, then distilled in a 100-foot copper column still with doubler to ~140 proof. Spirit enters new charred American white oak barrels at 125 proof; Old No.7 is mingled from 175–200 barrels drawn across multiple rickhouse floors to balance upper-floor wood intensity against lower-floor subtlety, then chill-filtered and bottled at 40% ABV.

The 2024 McLaren x JD Edition dresses this unchanged liquid in a Formula 1 collaboration bottle — the only visual departure from the standard Black Label release, making it a collector piece with no reformulation of the whiskey inside.

name: Arran  Barrel Reserve Single Malt Whisky  (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Unpeated island malt from granite-filtered Loch na Davie water, non-chill filtered at 46% ABV — Arran's fruit-forward house style without age compromise.
description (EN Store):
When Harold Currie, former Managing Director of Chivas Brothers, revived legal distillation on Arran in 1995 after a 160-year island silence, he made a deliberate transgression: no peat. On an island where maritime smoke was convention, Lochranza was built to produce a clean, fruit-driven malt — a decision that defines Barrel Reserve today.

The distillery sits in Glen Eason Biorach ('Valley of the Sharp Waterfalls') in Arran's granite-dominant north, drawing water from Loch na Davie via the Easan Biorach burn — six cascades through granite that strip minerals and soften the liquor before it ever reaches the mash tun. The Gulf Stream microclimate accelerates maturation in on-site dunnage and racked warehouses.

Fermentation runs 60–75 hours in six Oregon pine washbacks using Kerry M yeast — an extended window deliberately targeted to develop fruity congeners in the new make. Double distillation through tall-necked, long-lyne-arm copper pot stills (wash stills 7,100 L; spirit stills 4,300–4,800 L, bulb-shaped) with shell-and-tube condensers produces a new make collected at ~68% ABV, matured primarily in ex-bourbon American oak and ex-sherry European oak refill casks. Bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill filtered, no caramel colouring (E150a) — a standard Arran holds across virtually its entire core range.

The special feature: Barrel Reserve carries no age statement yet refuses the industry shortcut of chill filtration or colour correction, delivering full texture and natural hue at an accessible price point that most NAS peers at this tier do not match.

name: Johnnie Walker  Blue Label Ice Chalet (Limited Edition) (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Blue Label's 1-in-10,000 cask threshold meets alpine limited-edition packaging — rare ghost-distillery whiskies blended at Leven, Fife, 40% ABV.
description (EN Store):
When John Walker opened his Kilmarnock grocery in 1820, his tea-trade instinct — that blending across origins eliminates batch variation — became the founding logic of an entire category. Blue Label is where that logic reaches its most selective expression: only 1 in every 10,000 casks in Diageo's reserve of approximately 10 million barrels qualifies, a threshold that routinely pulls in whiskies from permanently closed 'ghost' distilleries such as Brora and Port Ellen, making each batch a historically unrepeatable document.

The blend draws from all four Scottish regions — Cardhu (Speyside) for honeyed fruit at the heart, Caol Ila (Islay) for clean maritime peat, Clynelish (Highland) for waxy coastal depth, and Lowland grain distilleries for structural lift — a pan-Scotland architecture that no single-region producer can replicate. Casks are predominantly ex-bourbon American oak and ex-Sherry European oak; exact split is undisclosed, but the selection protocol demands individual cask analysis and approval by the blending team before any parcel enters the vatting.

Bottled at 40% ABV, chill-filtered, and presented here in the Ice Chalet limited-edition livery — the special feature is the ghost-distillery access: Blue Label remains one of the only commercially available vehicles for tasting whisky from distilleries that no longer exist, a collector's proposition entirely separate from its luxury positioning. Non-vintage by design; drinking window is open-ended.

name: Compass Box  Crimson Cask (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Compass Box's red-wine-cask-finished blended malt — non-chill-filtered, no colouring, built on the house's custom French oak philosophy.
description (EN Store): John Glaser founded Compass Box in 2000 with a winemaker's instinct applied to Scotch — sourcing, selecting, and re-maturing casks rather than distilling, a deliberate structural transgression in an industry that prizes distillery provenance above all else. Crimson Cask extends that philosophy into red-wine-cask finishing, layering ex-red-wine wood influence over a blended malt foundation sourced from distilleries across Scotland's malt-producing regions. The result sits within Compass Box's signature framework: bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, and free of added colouring — parameters that preserve the textural density and natural hue that chill-filtration and E150a would otherwise flatten or falsify. As a blending house operating without its own still, Compass Box commissions bespoke casks with specified toast levels and holds maturing stocks in bonded Scottish warehouses, giving it a degree of cask-level control unusual for a non-distillery producer. The special feature here is the red-wine cask itself: within a category dominated by ex-bourbon and ex-sherry maturation, the use of wine-seasoned wood introduces anthocyanin-derived colour and a fruit-skin tannic grip that neither bourbon nor Oloroso delivers. Non-vintage by design, the blend is recipe-driven for consistency across batches rather than declared by year.

name: Compass Box  Nectarosity (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Grain-forward blended Scotch from Compass Box, bottled at 46% non-chill-filtered — built around silky Cameronbridge grain whisky and custom French oak cask work.
description (EN Store): John Glaser founded Compass Box in 2000 with a winemaker's instinct applied to Scotch blending — and Nectarosity sits squarely in that grain-forward tradition, drawing on the same Cameronbridge-anchored philosophy that produced Hedonism, the world's first commercial blended grain Scotch whisky. Compass Box holds no distillery of its own; instead, it selects specific cask parcels from across Scotland and re-matures them in custom-commissioned casks fitted with heavily toasted French oak heads, a technique that layers vanilla, coconut, and pastry-like sweetness onto the naturally silky texture of column-still grain spirit. The grain whiskies at the core of this expression mature primarily in first-fill ex-bourbon American oak hogsheads, which preserve the delicate, high-ester character of Cameronbridge before the French oak influence adds structural depth. Bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, and without added colouring — a deliberate rejection of mass-market blending norms — the whisky retains a viscosity and natural hue that most commercial grain blends sacrifice for shelf consistency. What sets Nectarosity apart categorically is its unapologetic celebration of grain whisky as a luxury object, not a filler component, at a time when the industry still treats grain as secondary to malt.

name: Compass Box  Orchard House (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Blended malt from Linkwood, Clynelish & Benrinnes — ex-bourbon, Oloroso sherry & French oak — 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, no colouring.
description (EN Store): When John Glaser founded Compass Box in 2000, he applied a winemaker's logic to Scotch blending — sourcing specific cask parcels from named distilleries rather than buying bulk spirit, then assembling them with the precision of a grand cru assemblage. Orchard House is the direct expression of that philosophy. Linkwood (Speyside, ~39%) anchors the blend with its characteristic waxy, orchard-fruit character; Clynelish (~29%) from the coastal Highlands contributes its signature beeswax and saline tension; Benrinnes (~20%), a meaty, sulphury Speyside distilled through partial triple distillation, adds structural depth. The three-distillery core is matured across ex-bourbon American oak, ex-Oloroso sherry casks, and French oak — the French oak component a deliberate Compass Box signature, delivering dried-fruit spice and pastry notes that no standard Scotch regime produces. Bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, with no added colouring, the whisky retains the natural texture and vibrancy that chill filtration strips away. What sets Orchard House apart within the blended malt category is the explicit, named-distillery transparency Compass Box fought the Scotch Whisky Association to publish — a recipe declaration most blenders still refuse to make. Non-vintage by design; drink now or hold 3–5 years.

name: Douglas Laing's Double Barrel Islay & Highland (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Islay smoke meets Highland malt: Douglas Laing's no-colouring, non-chill-filtered double-region blend bottled at 46%.
description (EN Store): Founded in Glasgow in 1948 by Fred Douglas Laing, the house built its reputation on long-standing distillery filling contracts — access that now extends to rare and closed-distillery stocks unavailable to most bottlers. Double Barrel Islay & Highland draws on that same network, marrying casks from Scotland's peatiest coastal region with the broader, orchard-fruited character of the Highland interior. No single production site defines this whisky; instead, the craft lies entirely in cask selection, conducted personally by third-generation custodians Fred Laing Jr. and Cara Laing from the company's Hillington Sample Room. Bottled at 46% ABV, without added colouring or chill-filtration, the natural oils and texture of both regional components are preserved intact — a house-wide commitment Douglas Laing adopted before it became industry fashion. The double-barrel concept is the structural transgression here: rather than blending finished whiskies, the expression marries two distinct regional cask streams, allowing Islay's iodine-laced peat smoke and Highland's heathered, malty weight to coexist without either dominating. As a non-vintage, small-batch release, character will shift subtly across batches, but the no-filtration policy ensures each iteration delivers full-texture spirit. Drink now or hold short-term.

name: Balblair  170 Commemorative Pack
short_description (EN Store): Balblair's 170th commemorative release: Northern Highlands single malt from Edderton's dunnage warehouses, bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, natural colour.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1790 by John Ross on the southern shore of the Dornoch Firth — with unofficial distilling records tracing to 1749 — Balblair relocated in 1872 to sit beside the Inverness–Ardgay railway, a site designed by distillery architect Charles C. Doig and still operating from its original 1894 dunnage warehouses with beaten-earth floors. Those warehouses are not incidental: slow, low-evaporation maturation in traditional dunnage is a structural commitment, not a heritage flourish.

Water is drawn from the Allt Dearg burn, piped five miles from the Edderton Hills — the same source retained when the distillery physically moved. Unpeated malted barley from Glen Ord Maltings is mashed for clear wort, fermented approximately 62 hours in Oregon pine washbacks, then distilled through squat, onion-shaped copper pot stills with wide necks and no reflux bowls. New make is filled to cask at ~68.5% ABV — notably above the Scottish industry norm of ~63.5% — meaning the spirit matures more slowly and retains more of its original fruity, ester-forward character over time.

The 170 Commemorative expression is bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered and at natural colour, consistent with the entire core range. What sets this apart: Balblair was one of Scotland's few distilleries to release whisky by distillation vintage rather than age statement from 2007 to 2019, and the house philosophy of bottling only casks judged at 'optimum maturation' — each hand-stencilled by the men who filled them — underpins every commemorative selection. Citrus peel, orchard fruit, honey, and sweet toffee define the mid-palate, with spice building on the finish.

name: Compass Box  Flaming Heart 25th Anniversary Edition Blended Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Laphroaig, Talisker & Caol Ila smoke meets custom French oak spice — Compass Box's 25th-anniversary Flaming Heart, non-chill-filtered blended malt.
description (EN Store): John Glaser founded Compass Box in 2000 with a winemaker's logic applied to Scotch — no owned stills, only obsessive cask selection — and Flaming Heart has become the house's most structurally ambitious expression of that philosophy. This 25th Anniversary Edition draws from a multi-distillery malt roster anchored by Laphroaig, Caol Ila, Talisker, Glen Elgin, and Balmenach, each contributing a distinct register: coastal peat smoke, maritime iodine, and a spiced Highland core. Rather than a single terroir, the architecture is deliberate contrast — heavily peated Islay malts colliding with a custom French oak-matured Highland malt blend, the French oak heads commissioned by Compass Box to specified toast levels after the SWA banned their inner-stave technique in 2006. Component casks span ex-bourbon hogsheads, refill barrels, and bespoke French oak, with no added colouring and non-chill-filtration preserving the full texture of the assembled parcels. The defining transgression here is the French oak integration: where most peated blended malts rely on American oak to soften smoke, Compass Box drives spice and tannin structure directly into the peat, producing a tension that no standard Islay bottling replicates. Released in numbered batches with varying component parcels, this anniversary edition is a non-vintage, recipe-driven limited release — no drinking window applies in the conventional sense, but the architecture rewards time in glass.

name: Compass Box  Hedonism 2025 Blended Grain Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): The world's first commercial blended grain Scotch — Cameronbridge-anchored, ~23yr avg age, 46% non-chill-filtered, no colouring.
description (EN Store): When John Glaser launched Hedonism in 2000 as Compass Box's debut release, it was the first whisky ever bottled and marketed as a commercial blended grain Scotch — a deliberate provocation aimed at an industry that treated grain whisky as filler rather than subject. That founding transgression remains the product's defining logic. Cameronbridge distillery anchors the blend, with parcels drawn from multiple grain distilleries — typically three to seven single-grain components per release — selected for cask quality rather than distillery prestige. Maturation averages approximately 23 years across components, drawing on first-fill American oak ex-bourbon hogsheads and antique sherry casks, the long contact time coaxing grain whisky's column-still character — coconut, vanilla, toffee — into something architecturally complex. Bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, with no added colouring, the texture retains the natural oiliness that chill filtration would strip. What no peer does: Compass Box treats grain whisky as a single-malt-grade blending ingredient, applying the same parcel-level cask selection discipline normally reserved for aged malts, then publishes the methodology openly — a transparency standard the Scotch Whisky Association formally resisted in 2015. Non-vintage by design; recipe evolves batch to batch as component parcels are exhausted and replaced.

name: Compass Box  Secrets of Smoke Blended Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Islay-dominant blended malt from London's activist blending house — non-chill-filtered, no colouring, built around Caol Ila and Laphroaig peat.
description (EN Store): When John Glaser founded Compass Box in 2000, he did so without a still — deliberately. The house operates as a precision blending atelier in Richmond, London, sourcing and re-maturing casks from named Scottish distilleries rather than distilling a drop itself. Secrets of Smoke is a blended malt built on the peated bedrock of Islay, drawing its structural weight from Caol Ila and Laphroaig — two distilleries whose coastal, peat-cut water sources and heavily kilned malts produce phenolic profiles of fundamentally different character: Caol Ila's medicinal, oily smoke versus Laphroaig's tar-and-seaweed intensity. These Islay components are matured in refill hogsheads and ex-bourbon American oak barrels, a deliberate choice that keeps wood influence subordinate and lets phenolic expression dominate. The blend is bottled at 46% ABV, non-chill-filtered, and without added colouring — a production standard Compass Box holds across its range, yielding a texture and natural hue that most commercial peated blends sacrifice for shelf consistency. What distinguishes this expression within the peated Scotch category is the blending-house model itself: no single-distillery constraint, no vintage pressure, and the freedom to balance Islay smoke against medium-peated Highland parcels for architectural precision no individual distillery can replicate.

name: Royal Salute 21 years Harris Reed Edition III - Black Gold (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Blended Scotch from Strathisla's Fons Bulliens Spring — every component a minimum 21 years in ex-bourbon & ex-sherry oak. Harris Reed Edition III.
description (EN Store): Royal Salute was born on 2 June 1953, created by Chivas Brothers to mark the coronation of Queen Elizabeth II — a founding purpose that locked in a non-negotiable standard: no component whisky, grain or malt, may enter the blend younger than 21 years. That floor, maintained across every expression in the portfolio, is a commitment no other Scotch whisky house matches at its entry tier. The brand home is Strathisla Distillery in Keith, Moray — the oldest continuously operating distillery in the Scottish Highlands, founded 1786, drawing process water from the Fons Bulliens Well, a peat-free natural spring prized for its softness, which shapes the characteristically smooth, creamy texture of the house style. Speyside's mild, humid valley microclimate along the River Isla promotes slow, even maturation, coaxing ripe pear, dried citrus, and vanilla from the combination of ex-bourbon American oak and ex-sherry casks held in the dedicated Royal Salute Vault within Strathisla. The Harris Reed Edition III — Black Gold — layers the collaborative creative vision of the designer onto this 21-year blended architecture, bottled at 40% ABV in a hand-crafted ceramic flagon requiring up to six days of production from Cornish clay with double-layer glazing. What no peer does: the entire Royal Salute range guarantees 21+ years on every component — a standard most luxury single malts cannot claim at their own entry level.

name: Royal Salute 21 years Harris Reed Edition III - Black Fuchsia (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Blended at Strathisla's Royal Salute Vault — every component, grain and malt, carries a minimum 21-year age statement. Harris Reed III in black fuchsia ceramic.
description (EN Store):
Royal Salute was born on 2 June 1953, created by Chivas Brothers to mark the coronation of Queen Elizabeth II — a founding purpose that locked in an absolute floor of 21 years' maturation on every component whisky, grain and malt alike, a standard the portfolio has never relaxed. The Harris Reed Edition III continues that lineage in a hand-crafted ceramic flagon produced from Cornish clay with double-layer glazing, taking up to six days to complete.

The whisky is married at the Royal Salute Vault, a dedicated bonded warehouse within Strathisla Distillery, Keith, Moray — founded 1786, the oldest continuously operating distillery in the Scottish Highlands. Process water is drawn from the Fons Bulliens Well, a peat-free natural spring prized for its softness, which shapes the distillate's notably clean, creamy texture. The Speyside valley microclimate — mild, humid, low-elevation — supports the slow, even maturation that 21-plus years demands.

The Signature Blend combines Strathisla single malt with grain whiskies from across Scotland's five regions, all rested a minimum of 21 years in a combination of ex-bourbon American oak and ex-sherry casks, bottled at 40% ABV. The result is a fruit-forward, vanilla-laced profile with dried fruit depth from the sherry wood and a long, gently spiced finish.

What no peer does: Royal Salute is the only Scotch whisky portfolio in the world where the youngest component across its entire range — including the grain whisky — is guaranteed at least 21 years old; most luxury blends guarantee only the youngest malt.

As a non-vintage blend, character is consistent across releases; the drinking window is open now and will hold comfortably for several years with proper storage.

name: Nikka YOICHI 10 Year Single Malt (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Yoichi's rare direct coal-fired pot stills yield a heavy, peated maritime malt aged 10 years — one of the last distilleries on earth using this technique.
description (EN Store):
When Masataka Taketsuru founded the Yoichi Distillery in 1934 at the base of Hokkaido's Shakotan Peninsula, he chose direct coal-fired distillation — the same method used at Longmorn in Scotland — deliberately preserving the heavier congeners and complex flavour compounds that steam-heating strips away. That decision, labour-intensive and difficult to control, has never been reversed, making Yoichi one of the last distilleries in the world still operating this way.

The site sits where mountains on three sides funnel cold Sea of Japan air and coastal fog across the distillery, with snowmelt from the Yoichi River providing the water source. That cool, damp maritime microclimate — combined with historically local peat and coal from the Ishikari Plain — drives the distillate toward weight, brine, and smoke from the outset.

Straight-head pot stills with downward lyne arms suppress reflux, pushing oils and heavier esters into the new-make spirit. After 10 years of maturation, the result carries the full structural density that Yoichi's production philosophy demands. The 10 Year is the entry point into Nikka's age-stated Yoichi range — a category where most Japanese producers have suspended age statements entirely due to stock constraints, making this expression a genuine rarity in the current market.

The maritime peat character is present but measured against a decade of cask integration; the drinking window is open now and will hold comfortably through the mid-2030s.

name: Nikka YOICHI Discovery - Non-Peated (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Yoichi's coal-fired stills stripped of peat — the Discovery series isolates the distillery's maritime weight and heavy congeners in their purest, unsmoked form.
description (EN Store): When Masataka Taketsuru established Yoichi in 1934 at the base of the Shakotan Peninsula — mountains on three sides, the Sea of Japan directly ahead — he was deliberately recreating Scottish Highland conditions on Hokkaido's northwest coast. The distillery draws snowmelt from the Yoichi River, and its cool, fog-laden maritime microclimate drives slow maturation and the retention of dense, waxy spirit character. The Discovery Non-Peated expression isolates a single variable: Yoichi's rare direct coal-fired pot still distillation with straight-head stills and downward lyne arms — a method retained from Longmorn, Scotland, and largely abandoned globally for its labour intensity — applied here to unpeated malt, removing smoke from the equation entirely to expose the raw textural weight and heavy congener profile the coal-fire technique produces. What remains is the distillery's structural signature: the oily, coastal density that coal-firing generates, without peat acting as a masking or amplifying agent. This is the special feature: virtually no other distillery in the world still coal-fires its stills, and no other Yoichi release foregrounds that technique so nakedly. As a non-vintage, no-age-statement release in the Discovery series, it is designed to communicate distillery character over cask influence, making oak a supporting rather than dominant voice.

name: Nikka  Yoichi Discovery Aromatic Yeast (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Yoichi's coal-fired pot stills meet a single aromatic yeast strain — a limited Discovery Series expression pushing fermentation science at Japan's most maritime distillery.
description (EN Store): The Nikka Discovery Series exists because Masataka Taketsuru built Yoichi in 1934 to be a laboratory as much as a distillery — and this expression takes that mandate literally, isolating a single aromatic yeast strain as the sole variable against Yoichi's otherwise fixed production template. The distillery sits at the base of the Shakotan Peninsula on Hokkaido's northwest coast, facing the Sea of Japan, with snowmelt from the Yoichi River as its water source and a cool, fog-laden maritime microclimate that slows maturation and concentrates ester development. Yoichi's straight-head pot stills with downward lyne arms are fired directly by coal — one of the last distilleries in the world to retain this method — suppressing reflux and retaining heavier congeners that would be stripped away by steam heating. Where the standard Yoichi expression relies on that coal-fire character and coastal peat as its primary identity, the Discovery Aromatic Yeast edition deliberately foregrounds fermentation-derived esters, making yeast — not cask or peat — the narrative protagonist. This is the transgression: in a category where oak and smoke dominate the conversation, Nikka turns the dial entirely to the washback. The result is a Yoichi that retains its maritime backbone while amplifying fruity, floral fermentation compounds that the distillery's heavy-congener distillation style would ordinarily subordinate.

name: Nikka MIYAGIKYO Discovery Aromatic Yeast (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Miyagikyo single malt fermented with aromatic yeast strains — a distillery-series experiment in ester-driven character from Sendai's forested river valley.
description (EN Store):
The Miyagikyo Discovery Series exists because Nikka's blenders operate under a structural constraint: Japanese regulations prohibit distillate exchange between competing groups, so Masataka Taketsuru built Miyagikyo in 1969 specifically to give his blending team a second, contrasting voice. The Discovery releases turn that in-house laboratory into a public experiment — isolating single production variables to expose what the distillery can do beyond its standard expression.

Miyagikyo sits in a forested valley west of Sendai, fed by the Hirose and Nikkawa rivers drawing snowmelt from the Zao Mountain Range. The water is among the softest of any Japanese distillery, and the humid, cool forest microclimate slows maturation, preserving delicate ester compounds that harder water or warmer warehouses would suppress.

This Aromatic Yeast expression isolates fermentation as the variable: specific aromatic yeast strains are selected to amplify ester production during fermentation in Miyagikyo's 22 stainless-steel washbacks, before distillation through the distillery's characteristic bulging-neck, upward-lyne-arm pot stills with steam heating at 130°C — a configuration engineered for maximum reflux and a lighter, fruit-forward spirit.

What is unusual here is the deliberate foregrounding of yeast as a flavour architect in a category that rarely names fermentation as the headline: this is a single-variable release designed to demonstrate that Miyagikyo's floral register is not solely a function of still geometry.

As a non-vintage expression, it reflects Nikka's house philosophy of consistency over year-to-year variation; drink now through the medium term.

name: Nikka The Grain Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Miyagikyo's twin Coffey stills — Glasgow-built, corn-dominant — deliver grain whisky of uncommon creaminess, bottled under Nikka's single-category label.
description (EN Store): When Masataka Taketsuru transferred a pair of Coffey continuous stills — originally manufactured by Blairs Ltd. of Glasgow and installed at Nikka's Nishinomiya plant — to the Miyagikyo distillery in Sendai's forested Hirose River valley, he was importing a 19th-century Scottish technology into one of Japan's most distinctive microclimates. Miyagikyo's water, drawn from the Zao Mountain Range via the Hirose and Nikkawa rivers, registers among the lowest hardness of any Japanese distillery, a geological fact that directly shapes the spirit's texture. The valley's humid, cool forest air slows maturation and preserves delicate congeners through the aging cycle. The grain bill is corn-dominant, processed through continuous Coffey distillation — a method that strips heavier oils while retaining the cereal sweetness and creamy mouthfeel that define this style. Maturation proceeds in ex-bourbon American oak casks (re-filled, remade, and re-charred), which layer vanilla and light caramel without overwhelming the spirit's inherent lightness. What sets this expression apart categorically: Nikka releases it as a standalone grain whisky under its own label — a transparency almost no Japanese producer extends to grain distillate, which is typically absorbed invisibly into blends. No vintage character data applies to this NV expression; drink now or hold short-term.

name: Kavalan Solist Port Single Cask Strength Single Malt Whiskey (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Single-cask, cask-strength Taiwanese malt finished in Portuguese port barriques — subtropical heat at Yuan Shan compresses decades of maturation into years.
description (EN Store):
In January 2010, a Kavalan expression defeated three Scotch whiskies in a blind tasting held in Leith on Burns Night — the moment that forced the whisky world to reckon with what subtropical heat can do to oak. That heat is the central fact of the Solist Port: Yuan Shan Distillery sits at the intersection of cold air descending from the Xueshan (Snow Mountain) Range and humid Pacific Ocean breezes, with upper-floor warehouse temperatures reaching 42°C in summer. Wood extraction that would take 15–25 years in Scotland is compressed into roughly 4–6 years, driving the port cask's tannin and dried-fruit character deep into the spirit at a pace no cooler climate can replicate.

The new-make itself is built for this: double-distilled in Forsyths copper pot stills (12,000-litre wash / 7,000-litre spirit) with a narrowed spirit cut of 65–55% ABV to maximise ester complexity, preceded by a three-stage fermentation — including a dedicated 'fruit producer' yeast round — totalling 60–72 hours. Water throughout is sourced from Snow Mountain springs, filtered through gravel and sand. The result is then filled into ex-port Portuguese barriques for single-cask maturation at natural strength.

What separates this expression from the Solist range's sherry variants is the port cask's contribution: dried fig, dark plum, and cocoa layered over the distillery's signature mango-and-cream tropical base — a combination no European port-finished malt can produce, because no European distillery operates in 42°C warehouses. Bottled at natural cask strength (typically 50–59.9% ABV), non-chill-filtered, single barrel — every bottle carries individual cask fill and dump dates.

name: Kavalan 15th Anniversary Vintage 2009 ex-bourbon Cask (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Distilled 2009 at Yuan Shan in Yilan's subtropical heat — ex-bourbon American oak, cask strength, non-chill-filtered, from Kavalan's founding production era.
description (EN Store): When T.T. Lee founded Kavalan in 2005, the received wisdom was that great single malt demanded cold, slow-maturing air. The spirit filled into this ex-bourbon cask in 2009 — drawn from Kavalan's earliest production years — became part of the evidence that dismantled that assumption entirely. Yuan Shan Distillery sits at the intersection of cold air descending from the Xueshan (Snow Mountain) Range and humid Pacific Ocean breezes, with warehouse temperatures swinging from 24°C on lower floors to 42°C on upper floors in summer, no climate control applied. Snow Mountain spring water, filtered through gravel and sand, feeds the mash. That thermal violence drives wood extraction at a pace unmatched in cooler climates: four Taiwanese years broadly equate to 10–15 Scottish ones. The mash bill is 100% unpeated malted barley; fermentation runs 60–72 hours across a multi-stage cycle including a dedicated 'fruit producer' yeast strain for ester development, followed by a lactic round; double distillation in Forsyths copper pot stills with a spirit cut narrowed to 65–55% ABV for added complexity. Maturation in ex-bourbon American oak channels the distillery's tropical-fruit and vanilla character without sherry-cask interference — the purest read of Kavalan's house DNA. Bottled at natural cask strength, non-chill-filtered, as a 15th Anniversary single-cask release from 2009 spirit, this expression captures the distillery's founding-era production before the 2016 capacity expansion.

name: Kavalan 15th Anniversary Vintage 2009 Oloroso Sherry Cask (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): 2009-distilled Oloroso sherry cask from Yilan's subtropical heat — Taiwan's accelerated maturation delivers sherry depth no Scottish peer achieves in equivalent years.
description (EN Store):
When T.T. Lee founded the Yuan Shan Distillery in Yuanshan Township in 2005, the founding premise was deliberate transgression: that subtropical heat could compress decades of Scottish maturation into years. Spirit distilled in 2009 — among Kavalan's earliest production runs — was filled into a single Oloroso sherry cask and left to the mercy of Yilan's warehouse floors, where summer temperatures climb to 42°C on upper tiers, driving wood extraction at a rate unmatched in any cool-climate distillery.

The Yuan Shan site sits at the convergence of cold air descending from the Xueshan (Snow Mountain) Range at 3,886 m and humid Pacific Ocean breezes, with distillation water drawn from Snow Mountain springs filtered through gravel and sand. That thermal swing — not a gradual Scottish chill — is what forces the new-make deep into the Oloroso oak, pulling dried fruit, oxidative nut, and dark chocolate from the sherry-seasoned wood while the tropical Pacific humidity preserves body and prevents over-extraction.

Production follows double distillation in Forsyths copper pot stills (12,000-litre wash / 7,000-litre spirit), with the spirit cut narrowed to 65–55% ABV specifically to increase ester complexity before cask entry. As a 15th Anniversary bottling, this is released at natural cask strength, non-chill-filtered — single barrel, no dilution, no filtration stripping the long-chain esters accumulated over a subtropical maturation equivalent to 25+ years in Scotland.

The special feature: a 2009 fill date places this among the distillery's foundational-era spirit, the same vintage cohort that underpins the inaugural Kavalan 15 Years Old (2025 Edition) — making this Oloroso single cask a rare single-barrel window into that historically significant early production.

name: Kavalan Triple Sherry Cask Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Three sherry cask types converge at Kavalan's subtropical Yilan distillery, where 42°C warehouse heat compresses years into months.
description (EN Store):
When T.T. Lee founded the Yuan Shan Distillery in Yuanshan Township, Yilan County in 2005, he made a deliberate wager: that Taiwan's subtropical extremes — not Scotland's cool patience — could forge world-class single malt. The Triple Sherry Cask is the direct expression of that wager, drawing on three distinct sherry cask types (Oloroso, PX, and a third sherry variant from Kavalan's portfolio) to layer oxidative, raisined depth over the distillery's tropical-fruit base.

The Yuan Shan site sits at the intersection of cold air descending from the Xueshan (Snow Mountain) Range and humid Pacific Ocean breezes, with maturation warehouse temperatures swinging from 24°C on lower floors to 42°C on upper floors — no air-conditioning, by design. That thermal amplitude drives wood extraction at a rate broadly equivalent to 10–15+ years in Scotland within a fraction of the time, concentrating sherry-cask sugars and tannins with unusual density.

New-make spirit is produced via double distillation in Forsyths copper pot stills (12,000-litre wash / 7,000-litre spirit), with the spirit cut narrowed to 65–55% ABV specifically to increase ester complexity. Fermentation runs a multi-stage 60–72-hour cycle using a dedicated 'fruit producer' yeast strain for ester development, building the tropical scaffold that sherry maturation then transforms. The distillery's Snow Mountain spring water, filtered through gravel and sand, underpins the signature creaminess that carries the sherry oak influence without heaviness.

What distinguishes this expression within Kavalan's own lineup is the deliberate stacking of three oxidative sherry registers — dry-nutty Oloroso, dense PX sweetness, and a third sherry dimension — onto a tropical-climate spirit that no Jerez or Scottish producer can replicate: the geography is the transgression.

name: Kavalan Lan Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Yuan Shan Distillery's subtropical heat (42°C warehouses) compresses maturation to a fraction of Scottish timelines — tropical fruit intensity, Snow Mountain spring water creaminess.
description (EN Store):
On Burns Night 2010, a Kavalan expression was poured blind in Leith, Scotland, and beat three Scotch whiskies outright — the moment that forced the whisky world to abandon the assumption that cold, slow climates hold a monopoly on great single malt. That result was built on a deliberate geographic gamble: T.T. Lee sited Yuan Shan Distillery at the intersection of cold air descending from the Xueshan (Snow Mountain) Range and humid Pacific Ocean breezes in Yilan County, northeastern Taiwan.

Warehouse upper floors reach 42°C in summer with no air-conditioning, driving wood extraction at a rate that makes four to six years in Taiwan broadly equivalent to 15–25 years in Scotland. Water is drawn from Snow Mountain springs, filtered through gravel and sand, and contributes the signature creamy texture that anchors the house style.

Production begins with unpeated malted barley imported from Scotland and Finland, mashed over eight hours, then fermented across a three-stage cycle — approximately 60–72 hours total — using a dedicated 'fruit producer' yeast strain to maximise ester development. Double distillation runs through Forsyths copper pot stills; the spirit cut was deliberately narrowed from 78–72% down to 65–55% ABV to increase complexity. Maturation draws on a portfolio of ex-bourbon, ex-sherry, ex-port, and proprietary S.T.R. (Shaved, Toasted, Re-charred) casks — a technique Kavalan pioneered with the late Dr. Jim Swan, now adopted globally.

The Lan expression blends multiple cask types into a core-range bottling that distils the full tropical-and-cream house character without the single-cask extremity of the Solist range — the entry point that won Distiller of the Year at the 2026 World Whiskies Awards.

name: Kurayoshi  Matsui Single Malt whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): 100% in-house distilled at Kurayoshi's Mt. Daisen foothills, this NAS single malt is the producer's legitimate answer to years of sourcing controversy — 48% ABV, fruit-forward.
description (EN Store):
When Matsui Shuzo acquired its whisky distillation licence in 2015 and fired up two Japanese-made copper pot stills in 2017, it was a deliberate act of legitimisation: the Matsui Single Malt sub-range, launched 2018, exists precisely to separate the distillery's genuine in-house output from the sourced-Scotch 'Pure Malt' tier that had drawn sustained industry scrutiny. This is not the same whisky as the age-stated Kurayoshi bottles — provenance matters here.

The distillery sits at the foothills of Mt. Daisen (1,729 m), an extinct volcano in Tottori Prefecture whose volcanic substrate filters deep-aquifer groundwater to exceptional softness — that water is the structural backbone of the house's characteristically mellow, fruit-led spirit. Tottori's Sea of Japan coastal climate delivers near-freezing winters against summers reaching 38 °C, compressing the maturation cycle through extreme seasonal cask expansion and contraction.

The wash still runs 5,000 L; the spirit still 3,000 L, both fitted with adjustable lyne arms — angled upward for the lighter, cleaner spirit profile this expression targets. Maturation draws on ex-bourbon American white oak alongside Mizunara (Japanese oak) and Sakura (cherry wood) casks, the latter two imparting sandalwood, coconut, and faint cherry-blossom character absent from any Scottish-influenced peer. Bottled at 48% ABV, non-chill-filtration status unconfirmed for this specific expression.

The special feature: Sakura (cherry wood) cask influence — heads-swapped bourbon barrels re-coopered in cherry wood — is a technique with virtually no parallel outside Japan, and it is what separates this NAS from the broader craft-Japanese crowd.

name: Kurayoshi  18 Year Old Pure Malt Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Sourced-Scotch blended malt aged 18+ years at Kurayoshi's Mt. Daisen foothills, bottled at a full 50% ABV — Double Gold SFWSC 2021.
description (EN Store):
Matsui Shuzo's flagship age-stated expression carries a provenance flag every serious buyer must understand: the Kurayoshi Pure Malt range blends sourced Scotch single malt with Japanese malt, matured in Japan — a composition the early labelling did not make explicit, and one that remains contested in the Japanese whisky trade. That transparency caveat aside, the 18-year minimum age and 50% ABV bottling strength — higher than any other tier in the Kurayoshi lineup — give this expression genuine structural weight that younger siblings lack.

Maturation occurs at the distillery's warehouses in Kurayoshi City, Tottori Prefecture, at the foothills of Mt. Daisen (1,729 m), an extinct volcano whose porous volcanic-rock aquifer filters the deep-groundwater used throughout production. The San-in Coast's extreme seasonal swing — sub-zero winters to 38 °C summers — drives accelerated cask interaction, compressing what might take longer in Scotland's temperate cellars. Primary maturation is in ex-bourbon American white oak; sherry casks and Mizunara (Japanese oak) are employed across the broader range, though the exact cask split for this expression is undisclosed.

What no peer in the blended-malt category routinely does: Matsui Shuzo bottles this at 50% ABV without reducing to the industry-standard 43–46%, preserving the honey, smoke, spice, vanilla, and mint character that accumulates over 18+ years rather than diluting it for palatability. The 18yr earned Double Gold at the San Francisco World Spirits Competition 2021 and Gold at the IWSC — the most decorated expression in the Kurayoshi portfolio. Drink now through the medium term; decanting 20 minutes opens the mid-palate considerably.

name: Loch Lomond  Original Single Malt Scotch Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Alexandria distillery on the Highland Boundary Fault — 92–160hr fermentation, three still types, one roof. Scotland's most technically plural single malt.
description (EN Store): When Duncan Thomas installed straight-neck pot stills fitted with 17 fixed copper rectification plates at Loch Lomond — a design he had pioneered at Littlemill in the 1950s — he created a still geometry no other distillery in the world has replicated. That engineering decision is the reason a single address in Alexandria, Dunbartonshire produces over thirteen distinct spirit styles simultaneously. The distillery occupies a former Victorian iron foundry on the southern shore of Loch Lomond, sitting precisely on the Highland Boundary Fault, and draws its process water from the loch itself. The site's mix of original brick warehouses and modern racked structures creates varied maturation microclimates across the same campus. The Original is built on an unpeated malt stream from traditional swan-neck pot stills, with fermentation running a minimum 92 hours and up to 160 hours — roughly double the Speyside norm — generating long-chain esters that push the spirit toward ripe orchard fruit before a single cask is filled. Maturation proceeds in ex-bourbon American oak hogsheads and barrels, adding honey and vanilla without obscuring the fermentation-derived fruitiness. What no peer does: this same campus also runs continuous column stills on 100% malted barley mash, making Loch Lomond the only site globally producing peated single grain whisky. The Original, bottled at 40% ABV, is the accessible entry point into that system — soft, fruit-forward, and shaped more by fermentation science than by oak.

name: Loch Lomond  12 Year Old Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Highland malt from Scotland's most technically plural distillery — three still types, 92–160hr fermentation, ex-bourbon oak, 12 years.
description (EN Store):
Duncan Thomas built Loch Lomond's distillery in Alexandria, Dunbartonshire in the mid-1960s around a set of straight-neck pot stills fitted with 17 fixed copper rectification plates — a design he had pioneered at Littlemill and that no other distillery in Scotland has ever replicated. That engineering decision is the reason the 12 Year Old exists as it does: the straight-neck stills drive spirit to roughly 90% ABV versus the ~68–70% of conventional swan-neck stills, stripping heavier congeners and producing a lighter, more floral new-make that absorbs oak differently over time.

The distillery sits on the Highland side of the Highland Boundary Fault, just 25 km north-west of Glasgow, on a former Victorian foundry site whose varied brick warehouses and modern racked structures create distinct maturation microclimates. Fermentation runs a minimum 92 hours and up to 160 hours — roughly double the Speyside norm — in 21 stainless steel washbacks, building long-chain esters that underpin the honey-sweet, fruit-forward house character. The 12 Year Old matures entirely in ex-bourbon American oak hogsheads and barrels, which the distillery credits for its vanilla and sweet-honey register. Bottled at 46% ABV, this expression sits above the standard 40% core releases, preserving texture without cask-strength intensity. The special feature: Loch Lomond is the only distillery globally producing both peated single grain and single malt under one roof — the 12 Year Old is the clearest window into what those straight-neck stills alone can do.

name: Loch Lomond  18 Year Old Single Malt Whisky (700 ml)
