description (EN Store): Granmonte's natural sweet wine from Khao Yai's Asoke Valley marries four complementary white varieties into a balanced dessert expression. Chenin Blanc and Sémillon provide honeyed structure and stone-fruit backbone, while Verdelho and Muscat introduce tropical aromatics and floral complexity. Medium body with bright acidity preserves freshness despite residual sweetness, making this 375 ml format ideal for intimate service or casual entertaining.

name: Graham's  10 Year Tawny Port
short_description (EN Store): Graham's 10 Year Tawny: rich dark fruit, caramel, and spice with silky tannins. A refined Portuguese classic.
description (EN Store): Graham's 10 Year Tawny Port represents a mature expression of Portugal's most celebrated fortified wine style. A blend of traditional Port varieties aged in oak for a decade, this wine develops a complex interplay of dark plum and black cherry fruit balanced by caramel, vanilla, and warming spice notes. The extended barrel aging imparts a velvety texture and subtle dried-fruit complexity, while maintaining the low acidity and medium tannin structure characteristic of fine Tawny. Ideal as an apéritif, dessert wine, or meditation glass.

name: Graham's  20 Year Tawny Port
short_description (EN Store): Graham's 20 Year Tawny: rich dark plum, caramel, and hazelnut complexity with silky tannins and lingering spice.
description (EN Store): Graham's 20 Year Tawny Port represents the house's mastery of extended barrel aging, delivering a wine of considerable depth and refinement. The palate opens with concentrated dark plum and raisin character, supported by layers of caramel, hazelnut, and subtle vanilla from decades in wood. Fine-grained tannins have softened to silk, while emerging tertiary notes of leather and tobacco add complexity and elegance. The finish is long and warming, with persistent spice and dried-fruit echoes. A sophisticated aperitif or dessert wine suited to contemplative moments and premium chocolate pairings.

name: Massolino  Moscato D'Asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Moscato d'Asti from Piedmont: lychee, peach, and delicate florals with a refreshing, gently sparkling finish.
description (EN Store): Massolino's Moscato d'Asti DOCG is a quintessential expression of Piedmont's most celebrated aromatic white. This lightly sparkling wine showcases the Moscato grape's signature floral and stone-fruit character—ripe peach and lychee dominate the nose, supported by honeyed undertones and a whisper of citrus zest. The palate is refreshingly light-bodied with bright acidity and a delicate mousse that enhances its aromatic profile. A versatile aperitif and dessert wine, it pairs beautifully with soft cheeses, fruit tarts, and Asian cuisine where its subtle sweetness and effervescence cut through spice and richness.

name: Monsoon Valley  Chenin Blanc late Harvest (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Luscious late-harvest Chenin Blanc from Thailand's Hua Hin region—honeyed apricot, tropical fruit, and silky sweetness.
description (EN Store): Monsoon Valley's late-harvest Chenin Blanc captures the essence of tropical ripeness in a 375 ml format ideal for intimate occasions. Dominant apricot and honey aromatics lead to a palate of candied peach and subtle tropical character, balanced by crisp medium-high acidity that prevents cloying sweetness. The silky medium body and supporting vanilla notes from careful winemaking create a dessert wine of elegance and restraint, finishing with mineral precision. A versatile choice for fruit tarts, creamy desserts, soft cheeses, or as a sophisticated aperitif.

name: Cantine Pellegrino  Marsala Superiore Oro (500 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Marsala Superiore Oro: honeyed, rich, and complex with apricot and caramel notes.
description (EN Store): Cantine Pellegrino's Marsala Superiore Oro is a traditional Sicilian fortified wine crafted from Grillo, Catarratto, and Inzolia grapes. This amber-hued dessert wine delivers a rich, honeyed palate with prominent apricot and caramel character, supported by subtle vanilla and toasted almond notes. The medium-full body and balanced acidity create a lush, warming finish ideal for sipping after dinner or pairing with chocolate-based desserts and aged cheeses.

name: Cantine Pellegrino  Marsala Fine Rubino (500 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Marsala Fine Rubino: dark fruit, honey, and caramel in a luscious 500 ml format.
description (EN Store): Cantine Pellegrino's Marsala Fine Rubino is a traditional Sicilian fortified dessert wine crafted from Nero d'Avola grapes. This ruby-hued expression balances dark plum and raisin primary notes with honeyed sweetness and caramel undertones, supported by subtle spice and almond complexity. Medium body and moderate acidity create a smooth, approachable texture suited to both standalone sipping and pairing with chocolate desserts, creamy pastries, and aged cheeses. The 500 ml format offers elegant portion control for intimate entertaining or personal enjoyment.

name: Bottega  Moscato Vino dell'Amore Spumante (200 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Moscato spumante with lychee, peach, and floral notes. Ideal aperitif or dessert sparkler.
description (EN Store): Bottega's Moscato Vino dell'Amore Spumante is a light, aromatic sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region. Moscato's signature lychee and peach character dominates the nose, supported by honeyed floral notes and a whisper of brioche from the secondary fermentation. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a delicate mouthfeel with bright acidity and natural sweetness. A versatile aperitif or dessert wine suited to casual entertaining and light cuisine.

name: La Spinetta   Bricco Quaglia Moscato D'Asti
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Moscato d'Asti with honeyed florals, stone fruit, and tropical notes. Lightly sparkling, off-dry, and refreshingly elegant.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Bricco Quaglia Moscato d'Asti is a lightly sparkling dessert wine from Piedmont's Asti region, crafted from 100% Moscato Bianco. The wine opens with pronounced floral aromatics—honeysuckle and jasmine—followed by stone fruit (peach, apricot) and exotic tropical notes (lychee, citrus). On the palate, medium body and bright acidity balance the wine's residual sweetness, creating a refreshing, elegant finish. Ideal as an apéritif, with light desserts, or alongside Asian cuisine where its sweetness and acidity complement spiced and sour flavors.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Moscato d'Asti DOCG Zagara
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Moscato d'Asti with honeyed stone fruit, floral jasmine, and gentle sweetness—ideal for dessert or aperitif.
description (EN Store):
Marchesi di Barolo's Moscato d'Asti Zagara is a lightly sparkling dessert wine from Piedmont's prestigious Barolo region, crafted from 100% Moscato grapes. The wine displays a pale golden hue with fine, persistent bubbles characteristic of the appellation's traditional production method.

On the nose, delicate floral aromatics—particularly jasmine and honeysuckle—mingle with ripe stone fruit and lychee. The palate reveals honeyed peach and apricot, balanced by medium-high acidity that prevents cloying sweetness. A subtle tropical undertone and light effervescence create an elegant, refreshing finish ideal for dessert courses or as an aperitif.

name: Taylor’s  Fine Tawny Port
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, full-bodied Tawny Port with dried fruit, caramel, and hazelnut—a refined digestif.
description (EN Store): Taylor's Fine Tawny Port is a smooth, full-bodied fortified wine blending Touriga Nacional, Touriga Franca, and Tinta Roriz from Portugal's Port region. The primary character showcases dried fruit and caramel notes, complemented by secondary vanilla and hazelnut complexity. Low acidity and soft tannins create a velvety mouthfeel ideal for sipping after dinner or pairing with chocolate and aged cheeses.

name: Castellare Di Castellina  Vin Santo S.Niccolò IGT  (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Vin Santo: honeyed dried fruit, almond, and caramel in a silky, elegant half-bottle dessert wine.
description (EN Store): Castellare Di Castellina's Vin Santo S.Niccolò is a traditional Tuscan dessert wine crafted from 60% Malvasia bianca and 40% Trebbiano toscano. The 2018 vintage expresses the classic profile of this category: honeyed aromatics, dried apricot, and subtle almond notes on the nose, with a silky medium body and bright acidity that prevents cloying sweetness. Secondary vanilla and caramel emerge from careful aging, while a whisper of citrus zest and hazelnut add complexity. The 375 ml format makes it ideal for intimate occasions or as a refined gift.

name: Maison Castel  Medium Sweet Muscat Pays d'Oc
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Muscat with honeyed tropical fruit, floral notes, and refreshing acidity—elegant dessert wine for fruit tarts and soft cheese.
description (EN Store): Maison Castel's Medium Sweet Muscat from Pays d'Oc delivers a classic expression of the Muscat grape: aromatic, fruit-forward, and gracefully balanced. The wine opens with honeyed tropical notes and stone-fruit character, supported by delicate floral aromatics and citrus zest. Medium body and medium-high acidity provide structure and refreshment, making this dessert wine versatile at table or as an apéritif. Ideal for fruit-based desserts, light pastries, and fresh soft cheeses.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Baronne Paulinne AOC Sauternes
short_description (EN Store): Sauternes blend of honeyed apricot, citrus, and botrytis complexity—elegant dessert wine with balanced sweetness.
description (EN Store): Baronne Paulinne is a classic Sauternes expression from the prestigious Baron Philippe de Rothschild portfolio. Crafted from Sauvignon Blanc, Sémillon, and Muscadelle, this dessert wine showcases the region's signature botrytis-influenced character: honeyed stone fruit, preserved apricot, and delicate floral notes on the nose. The palate reveals layered complexity with vanilla oak undertones and a caramel sweetness balanced by bright citrus zest and crisp acidity. The high acidity provides lift and prevents cloying, making it versatile with both desserts and savory pairings like blue cheese or foie gras.

name: Fontanafredda  Moscato D'Asti Briccotondo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Moscato d'Asti with lychee, peach, and honeyed florals—a gently sparkling dessert wine of elegance and charm.
description (EN Store):
Fontanafredda's Moscato d'Asti Briccotondo is a classic expression of Piedmont's most beloved dessert wine. Delicate lychee and stone fruit aromas dominate the nose, supported by honeyed floral notes and a whisper of citrus zest. On the palate, the wine reveals a medium body with bright, refreshing acidity and a subtle sparkle that lifts the sweetness without overwhelming. The finish is clean and aromatic, lingering with peach and honeysuckle.

This wine excels as a standalone dessert, paired with fruit tarts, light pastries, or soft cheeses. Its gentle effervescence and moderate alcohol (typically 5–6%) make it an ideal apéritif or casual celebration wine.

name: Fontanafredda  Moscato D'Asti Le Fronde DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Piedmont Moscato with honeyed stone fruit, floral notes & gentle fizz—perfect for dessert or aperitif.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda Moscato d'Asti Le Fronde is a lightly sparkling dessert wine from Piedmont's prestigious Barolo region, crafted from the aromatic Moscato grape. This DOCG-classified wine balances natural sweetness with bright acidity, delivering honeyed peach and apricot on the palate alongside delicate floral aromatics and citrus zest. The gentle effervescence and medium body create an elegant, refreshing finish ideal for dessert courses, soft cheeses, or as a sophisticated aperitif.

name: Primosic  Ribolla di Oslavia DOC (Orange wine)
short_description (EN Store): Ribolla Gialla orange wine from Collio with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral complexity—ideal for spicy Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Primosic's 2019 Ribolla di Oslavia DOC is a skin-contact (orange) wine crafted from 100% Ribolla Gialla, a white grape native to Friuli-Venezia Giulia. Extended maceration imparts a golden hue and textural depth, layering stone fruit and citrus zest with herbaceous undertones and subtle honey notes. The wine's bright acidity and mineral spine make it a versatile partner for spicy Asian dishes, grilled seafood, and Mediterranean small plates.

name: 2Naturkinder  Weinschwärmer
short_description (EN Store): Franken orange wine blending Grauburgunder & Riesling—stone fruit, citrus, and mineral complexity for adventurous palates.
description (EN Store):
2Naturkinder Weinschwärmer is a 2018 orange wine from Franken, Germany, crafted from 75% Grauburgunder and 25% Riesling. Extended skin contact imparts a distinctive amber hue and textural depth, while the blend balances the fuller body of Grauburgunder with Riesling's aromatic lift and acidity.

On the nose, stone fruit and citrus zest lead, followed by honeyed notes and herbaceous undertones. The palate is medium-bodied with bright acidity and mineral tension, finishing with white pepper spice and dried apricot complexity. This wine bridges the gap between white wine freshness and red wine texture, making it a versatile choice for adventurous food pairing.

name: Primosic  Pinot Grigio "Skin" (Orange wine)
short_description (EN Store): Friuli orange Pinot Grigio with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral complexity—versatile with seafood and spicy Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Primosic's 2020 Pinot Grigio 'Skin' is a skin-contact orange wine from Collio, Friuli-Venezia Giulia, showcasing the textural and aromatic depth that extended maceration brings to this typically crisp white varietal. The extended skin contact imparts golden hues and layered complexity: primary stone fruit and citrus zest meld with secondary honey and brioche notes, while tertiary minerality and white pepper add savory intrigue. Medium body and bracing acidity make this wine equally at home with delicate shellfish, grilled white fish, or the bold spice and acidity of Thai cuisine.

name: Shumi  Kisi Dry Amber
short_description (EN Store): Georgian orange wine with stone fruit, honey, and herbaceous complexity. Bright acidity and minerality for versatile food pairing.
description (EN Store): Shumi Kisi Dry Amber is a Georgian orange wine crafted from white grapes macerated on skins to develop distinctive amber hues and textural depth. The wine opens with stone fruit and honey aromatics, supported by herbaceous and citrus zest notes that reflect the extended skin contact. Medium body and elevated acidity provide freshness and food-friendliness, while subtle minerality and white pepper spice add complexity. Ideal for adventurous palates seeking natural, skin-contact whites with character and versatility at the table.

name: Santa Julia  Cuy Orange Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): 45-day skin-contact Chardonnay from Maipú's gravel-sandy soils; indigenous yeasts, zero sulfur, unfined — Argentina's largest organic estate goes fully natural.
description (EN Store): When José Alberto Zuccardi named this label after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, the family was already farming Maipú's gravel-and-sand flats — the same soils that now anchor Santa Julia's Natural line. The Cuy Orange is built on certified-organic Chardonnay grown at the Maipú estate (approx. 650 m a.s.l.), where well-drained gravel over sandy subsoil strips the vine of excess vigour, concentrating phenolics and preserving the high natural acidity that makes extended skin contact viable rather than astringent. Mendoza's arid continental climate — wide diurnal swings, minimal rainfall, intense solar radiation — amplifies that phenolic structure while keeping aromatic lift intact. In the cellar, indigenous ambient yeasts drive fermentation with 45 days of skin-contact maceration, extracting tannin, colour, and textural grip without any sulfur additions at any stage. The wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered, preserving every particle of yeast-derived texture and skin-derived bitterness. What sets this apart categorically: at this price point in Argentina, zero-addition, unfiltered orange wine from a Fair for Life–certified estate (the first in Argentina, 2013) is essentially without peer. Drink on release for maximum aromatic freshness; the absence of SO₂ means the window is intentionally short.

name: Don Luciano  pink moscato
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Spanish pink Moscato sparkler with lychee, peach, and floral notes—perfect for celebrations and light appetizers.
description (EN Store): Don Luciano's pink Moscato from La Mancha delivers a charming, approachable sparkling wine with a light touch and vibrant acidity. Lychee and peach dominate the nose, supported by subtle floral aromatics and a whisper of brioche from the winemaking process. The palate is refreshing and elegant, with fine bubbles and a touch of honey sweetness that balances the wine's natural crispness. Ideal as an apéritif, with light desserts, or alongside casual entertaining.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Gris Blanc Rose
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic rosé from Languedoc's Mediterranean coast — saignée method, no malolactic, lunar-calendar harvest.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand formally founded his wine company in 1992, but the family's roots in Languedoc stretch back to his grandmother Paule Bertrand in the 1920s — a lineage that now underpins the world's largest Demeter-certified biodynamic wine operation, spanning over 900 hectares across 17 estates. Gris Blanc draws on the warm Mediterranean terroir of Languedoc-Roussillon, where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming without chemical inputs, and limestone and clay soils lend the wine its pale, mineral-driven character. The rosé is produced by the saignée method, with fermentation in stainless steel and no malolactic fermentation — a deliberate choice to lock in the region's natural acidity and keep the palate taut rather than creamy. Every stage, from harvest timing to bottling, is governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar, a discipline Bertrand applies uniformly across all estates. The blend centres on Grenache, Cinsault, and Syrah — the classic Languedoc rosé trio — producing a pale copper-pink wine with dried garrigue herbs, white peach, and a saline coastal lift that distinguishes it from the rounder, fruit-forward Provence style. At this price point, the Demeter certification and estate-grown sourcing across biodynamically farmed parcels is genuinely unusual. Drink young to capture the freshness the no-malolactic regime preserves.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Cote des Roses Rose
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc rosé from the world's largest Demeter-certified biodynamic producer — saignée method, no malolactic, timed to the lunar calendar.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand built his empire on a deliberate provocation: proving that the South of France, long dismissed as a bulk-wine factory, could produce wines of genuine terroir precision at scale. The Côte des Roses rosé is the entry point to that argument, sourced from across Languedoc-Roussillon's Mediterranean corridor where warm coastal winds reduce disease pressure and limestone-dominant soils drive natural acidity in the fruit. Every estate feeding this blend is farmed to Demeter biodynamic standards — no synthetic inputs, all vineyard decisions governed by the lunar calendar — a commitment unmatched by any producer of comparable volume globally. In the cellar, the rosé is made by the saignée method, fermented in stainless steel, with malolactic fermentation deliberately blocked to lock in the natural tartaric freshness that the Mediterranean climate can otherwise erode. The blend draws on Grenache, Cinsault, and Syrah — the Grenache providing red-fruit weight, Cinsault lending pale colour and floral lift, Syrah adding spice and structural backbone. What separates this from the Provence template is its Languedoc origin: warmer, more expressive fruit character than Côtes de Provence, yet disciplined by biodynamic farming and cold fermentation. Drink young, within 1–2 years of vintage, to capture the saignée's vivid aromatic precision.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Art de Vivre Languedoc AOP (Grenache Syrah Cinsault)
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic rosé from Languedoc AOP — saignée method, no malolactic, lunar-calendar harvest across 17 biodynamic estates.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand formally founded his wine company in 1992, but the more consequential decision came in 2002 when he began converting all estates to Demeter-certified biodynamics — a commitment that now spans over 900 hectares across 17 Languedoc-Roussillon properties, making him the world's largest biodynamic wine producer by certified area. Art de Vivre draws on that network, with Grenache, Syrah, and Cinsault sourced from warm Mediterranean terroir where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and limestone-dominant soils drive mineral tension in the fruit. The rosé is produced by the saignée method, fermented in stainless steel at controlled temperatures, and deliberately held back from malolactic fermentation — a choice that preserves the natural tartaric acidity and keeps the palate taut rather than creamy. Every stage of production, from harvest date to bottling, is governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar. What sets this cuvée apart within the Bertrand portfolio is its accessibility: the same Demeter-certified farming philosophy applied to prestige cuvées like Clos du Temple is present here at a fraction of the price, with zero chemical inputs across the entire growing cycle. The current vintage reflects the warm, sun-driven character typical of southern Languedoc — expect concentrated Provençal fruit with the freshness that no-malolactic stainless-steel vinification delivers.

name: Chavost Rose de Saignee Brut Nature - Rose
short_description (EN Store): ~1,000-bottle saignée from Chavot-Courcourt clay-limestone: zero sulphites, zero dosage, 67% Meunier on 35–60yr vines — co-op Champagne's radical outlier.
description (EN Store):
In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of the Chavost cooperative — persuaded over 70 multi-generational grower families to abandon sulphur additions, dosage, and conventional inputs entirely, launching one of Champagne's most uncompromising natural portfolios from an unclassified village most grandes maisons overlook.

The Rosé de Saignée draws from 22 plots across 5–5.5 hectares in Chavot-Courcourt, at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne. The dual clay-limestone and chalky-limestone soil mosaic on north-facing slopes slows ripening, preserving the taut acid structure essential to a zero-dosage Champagne that carries no corrective sugar at disgorgement.

The blend is ~67% Pinot Meunier / ~33% Pinot Noir from vines aged 35–60 years, using 100% first-press cuvée juice only — taille excluded. Alcoholic and malolactic fermentations are both spontaneous with indigenous organisms; no enzymes, no chaptalization, no added sulphites at any stage (total SO₂ measured at 6–18 mg/L naturally occurring). Second fermentation proceeds in bottle without liqueur de tirage sugar addition, with a minimum of approximately 20 months on lees. Bottled unfiltered and unfined.

At roughly 1,000 bottles per disgorgement, this is among the rarest expressions in the Chavost range — and one of the only zero-sulphur saignée rosés produced by a cooperative anywhere in Champagne AOC.

name: Domaine Sylvain Pataille Marsannay "Fleur de Pinot" Rose
short_description (EN Store): Three-vintage Pinot field blend from Marsannay's organic parcels — 1935 Pinot Gris vines anchor a rosé that defies Burgundy convention.
description (EN Store):
When Sylvain Pataille founded his domaine in 1999 on entirely leased land, Marsannay rosé was an afterthought in the Côte de Nuits. Fleur de Pinot was his deliberate argument against that assumption — a field-blend rosé assembled from three distinct vine plantings across decades: 70% Pinot Noir (1949), 15% Pinot Gris (1935), and 15% Pinot Noir from Chamforey (1954). The oldest Pinot Gris vines, planted before the Second World War, contribute a textural density and aromatic complexity that straight Pinot Noir rosé cannot replicate.

The Marsannay parcels sit at 270–330 m elevation on east and southeast-facing slopes across Marsannay-la-Côte, Chenôve, and Couchey, where gravelly topsoils over Comblanchien limestone and iron-rich clay produce wines of pronounced mineral tension rather than soft fruit weight. All fruit is farmed under organic certification (Ecocert) with biodynamic methods integrated since 2007, including horse-ploughing to preserve soil structure.

Vinification follows Pataille's house philosophy: indigenous yeast fermentation, no sulphur added during fermentation, minimal SO₂ at bottling only. The 2021 vintage — marked by spring frost, mildew pressure, and sharply reduced yields — delivered exactly the conditions that suit this cuvée: cool-season precision, elevated natural acidity, and a mineral spine that amplifies the limestone-gravel terroir signal.

The inclusion of pre-war Pinot Gris vines in a Marsannay AOC rosé is without parallel among the appellation's producers — a transgression that is simultaneously a preservation of pre-appellation field-blend tradition.

name: Alto Los Romeros Rose
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua rosé from LFE's multi-valley estate network — coastal cool and mountain elevation driving the structure at this price point.
description (EN Store): Luis Felipe Edwards Sr. acquired the Puquillay Alto estate in 1976, planting into a horseshoe-shaped micro-valley sheltered by the San Fernando ridge — a deliberate topographic choice that would define the tension in every wine carrying the Alto Los Romeros name. That mountain influence, with vineyards reaching up to 900 m asl in Colchagua, is among the highest elevation sourcing in the valley, and it shows in the structural backbone of even this rosé tier. Soils at Puquillay Alto are a complex mix of granitic and volcanic rock, delivering low organic matter and forcing vines to work for water — a natural yield-limiter that concentrates juice before the winemaking team at the Puquillay facility even touches the fruit. The winery employs pneumatic pressing and temperature-controlled fermentation under chief winemaker Nicolás Bizzarri, preserving the aromatic lift that cool-site fruit generates. What separates this rosé from typical warm-valley Chilean pink wine is precisely that elevation and the multi-valley sourcing philosophy: where most Colchagua rosés lean into soft, sun-saturated fruit, the mountain and coastal-cool influence here produces a firmer acid line and more restrained fruit weight. Drink young to capture that freshness.

name: Apollonio Elfo Susumaniello Salento Rosato
short_description (EN Store): Susumaniello rosato from Salento's calcareous-clay soils — a rare varietal expression from one of Puglia's oldest family estates, founded 1870.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1870 and now in its fourth generation under brothers Marcello and Massimiliano Apollonio, this Lecce-based estate has long championed Salento's most obscure indigenous varieties — and Susumaniello, a thin-skinned red grape that nearly vanished from Puglian viticulture, is among its most deliberate bets. The Elfo draws fruit from sandy soils within the Salento IGT zone, a geological departure from the calcareous-clay that defines the estate's red wine corridor; sand drains aggressively, stresses the vine, and concentrates aromatics while naturally limiting colour extraction — a structural reason to vinify Susumaniello as rosato rather than red. Situated at roughly 100 m elevation near Monteroni di Lecce, the vineyards are cooled by simultaneous Adriatic and Ionian sea breezes, preserving acidity in a climate that routinely pushes 14–15% ABV in the estate's reds. Vinification follows a short, temperature-controlled maceration to capture Susumaniello's vivid pigment without extracting the grape's characteristically grippy tannins, with fermentation in stainless steel preserving primary fruit. The special feature here is categorical: while Apollonio ages approximately one million litres per year in French oak — a house-defining commitment — the Elfo deliberately bypasses oak entirely, making it the estate's most texturally transparent expression. Drink young, within 2–3 years of vintage, to capture the grape's fleeting floral lift.

name: Fratelli Ponte Brachetto Fiore Di Loto DOC
short_description (EN Store): 100% Brachetto from San Damiano d'Asti — 6–7 hr skin maceration, fermented to ~6.5% ABV, retaining natural residual sweetness in a delicate frizzante rosé.
description (EN Store):
Giuseppe Ponte established the Gorzano cellar in 1968 in San Damiano d'Asti, planting the family's identity firmly between Asti and Alba — a corridor where Brachetto, one of Piedmont's most ancient aromatic red varieties, has been cultivated for centuries. The Piemonte Brachetto DOC designation covers the broader regional expression of this grape, distinct from the fully sparkling Brachetto d'Acqui DOCG, and Fratelli Ponte's version leans into that distinction deliberately.

Sourced from chalky soils in the San Damiano d'Asti zone, the calcareous substrate tempers Brachetto's naturally exuberant perfume, lending a flinty undercurrent to what would otherwise be purely floral aromatics. Skin maceration is held to just 6–7 hours — long enough to extract the grape's characteristic rose-petal pigment and aromatic oils, short enough to avoid tannin accumulation. Fermentation is arrested at approximately 6–6.5% ABV in temperature-controlled stainless steel, preserving residual sugar and the grape's native muscat-adjacent fragrance without the full effervescence of Acqui-style production. What makes this expression unusual: at this ABV and sugar level, it occupies a frizzante-adjacent register that no dry rosé and no fully sparkling Brachetto quite replicates — a structural category of its own. Drink young, within 1–2 years of vintage, to capture peak aromatic lift.

name: One By Penfolds Australia Rose
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' Pinot Noir-dominant rosé from cool-climate Adelaide Hills — the house's blending philosophy applied to a lighter, site-expressive style.
description (EN Store): Penfolds was founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide — a house whose entire identity is built on winemaker-led blending rather than single-terroir orthodoxy. Applying that same multi-source discipline to a rosé is, within the Penfolds canon, a deliberate act of category extension rather than an afterthought. Adelaide Hills provides the primary fruit: high-altitude, cool-climate viticulture that preserves natural acidity and aromatic precision in Pinot Noir, the dominant variety in the Max's Rosé blend. The elevated sites and diurnal temperature swings of the Hills slow ripening, concentrating red-fruit character without sacrificing freshness. Winemaking follows the house's fruit-first approach: whole-bunch pressing of hand-picked Pinot Noir is standard practice for Penfolds whites and rosé-style wines, limiting phenolic extraction and delivering pale colour with structural delicacy. The One By Penfolds range is designed as the accessible entry point into the house style — the same blending rigour applied at a democratic price. What sets this apart within its category is provenance: this is rosé built on the same Adelaide Hills Pinot Noir supply chain that feeds Yattarna and the Reserve Bin whites, not a commodity fruit source. Drink young, on current release, to capture primary fruit lift.

name: Santa Julia  Cuy Malbec Rose Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): Malbec rosé from Argentina's largest certified-organic estate — natural-line, no added sulfites, indigenous yeast, unfined and unfiltered.
description (EN Store): José Alberto Zuccardi named this brand after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, and the Natural line it eventually spawned represents the family's most deliberate break from Mendoza convention: zero additions, ambient fermentation, and a refusal to fine or filter. The Cuy Malbec Rosé draws fruit from Familia Zuccardi's Maipú estate on Ruta Provincial 33 — gravel and sandy soils at approximately 650 m a.s.l. under an arid continental climate where wide diurnal swings lock acidity into the berry skins before harvest. Those free-draining soils suppress vigour and concentrate flavour without irrigation-driven dilution. Fermentation proceeds on indigenous yeasts with no sulfur additions, the hallmark of the Natural line; the wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered, preserving texture and a faint natural haze that signals zero processing aids. What sets this expression apart within the rosé category is the combination of certified-organic Malbec — sourced from Argentina's single largest organically certified vineyard holding (~400 ha) — with a completely additive-free protocol at a price point where such commitments are almost nonexistent. Drink young to capture the primary fruit and the lively, unmanipulated acidity that defines the current vintage.

name: Gran Sasso  Montepulciano D' Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly tannins—a versatile Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Gran Sasso's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a medium-full-bodied red wine that captures the essence of central Italy's most celebrated varietal. Emerging tertiary notes of tobacco and leather suggest modest aging potential. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or rustic Mediterranean fare.

name: Vigneti del Salento  I Muri` Negroamaro

name: Carta Vieja  Limited Release Cabernet Sauvignon

name: Palazzo Grimani by Allegrini  Valpolicella Ripasso DOC

Medium-full body and balanced tannins deliver a velvety mouthfeel that lingers with grace. This is a versatile, food-friendly expression ideal for braised meats, aged cheeses, and hearty Italian fare.

name: Castello Banfi  Chianti

short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and subtle vanilla—medium-full body, soft tannins, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Private Selection Vintage Merlot from Napa Valley's Central Coast delivers a classic American expression of the varietal. Ripe plum and berry medley dominate the nose, with hints of red fruit and vanilla oak integration. The palate is juicy and well-structured, balancing cranberry acidity with soft, integrated tannins and subtle herbaceous undertones. Medium-full body and moderate alcohol create an approachable, versatile wine suited to everyday dining and special occasions alike.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Napa Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Private Selection Pinot Noir from Oakville showcases the classic elegance of Napa Valley's premier Pinot terroir. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice. The wine's silky tannin structure and crisp acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile that evolves gracefully on the palate. A versatile expression suited to warm-weather entertaining and lighter protein pairings.

name: Robert Mondavi   Woodbridge Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, vanilla oak, and tobacco complexity. Age-worthy classic.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Woodbridge Cabernet Sauvignon from Oakville, Napa Valley, delivers a full-bodied expression of California's flagship varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by generous vanilla oak and warming spice from careful winemaking. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, suggesting structure and aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Montes  Alpha "M"
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Bordeaux blend from Chile's Casablanca Valley; blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Montes Alpha "M" is a premium Bordeaux-style red blend from Chile's Casablanca Valley, crafted from Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Merlot, and Petit Verdot. The 2020 vintage displays a full-bodied structure with high tannins and medium-high acidity, delivering dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit on the palate. Cedar and oak frame the primary fruit, while emerging tobacco and leather notes suggest complexity and aging potential. This wine pairs beautifully with grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes, and holds its own alongside aged hard cheeses.

name: Montes  Alpha Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Montes Alpha Merlot from Colchagua Valley's Apalta subregion delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of Chile's signature Merlot character. Ripe plum and mixed berry dominate the nose, with supporting notes of cranberry and subtle herbaceous undertones. The palate is juicy and well-integrated, with soft tannins that frame the fruit without demanding extended cellaring. Vanilla from oak aging adds gentle complexity. This wine balances approachability with structure, making it versatile at table.

name: Montes  Alpha Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—a classic expression of Maipo Valley power.
description (EN Store): Montes Alpha Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers the hallmark intensity and structure expected of a premium New World Cabernet. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cedar and oak from careful winemaking. The palate is full-bodied with high, well-integrated tannins and medium-high acidity that frames the wine's dark fruit core. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, suggesting complexity and aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meats, lamb, and game.

name: Montes  Purple Angel Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Ripe, fruit-forward Carmenère from Chile's Apalta Valley—dark plum and blackberry with smooth tannin and spiced oak.
description (EN Store): Montes Purple Angel Carmenère 2021 showcases the signature character of Chile's flagship red varietal. Sourced from Apalta in the Casablanca Valley, this medium-full-bodied wine delivers dominant dark plum and blackberry fruit on the nose and palate, supported by black pepper spice and vanilla-tinged oak from careful winemaking. The tannin structure is approachable yet structured, offering immediate pleasure without demanding extended cellaring. A food-friendly expression of Carmenère that bridges ripe fruit intensity with subtle earth and tobacco undertones on the finish.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva - Blend
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Bordeaux blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity from Puente Alto.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva is a structured Bordeaux-style blend from Chile's prestigious Puente Alto subregion in Maipo Valley. The wine combines Cabernet Sauvignon, Carmenere, Syrah, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot into a full-bodied expression marked by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and tobacco notes. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide backbone and aging potential, while secondary oak and spice notes add complexity. This is a serious, age-worthy red suited to substantial dishes and contemplative cellaring.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Puente Alto Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and spice—a food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Puente Alto showcases the signature depth and structure of Chile's premier Maipo Valley terroir. Ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the palate, supported by subtle cedar and cocoa from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet complex, with emerging tobacco and spice notes that reward cellaring. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Reserva Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Merlot from Puente Alto with ripe dark fruit, cocoa, and balanced tannins—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Reserva Merlot is a single-varietal expression from Chile's prestigious Puente Alto subregion in Maipo Valley. The wine showcases the ripeness and structure characteristic of quality Chilean Merlot: dominant black cherry and dark plum on the nose, supported by subtle cocoa and oak from careful winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or tomato-based preparations. A food-friendly Reserva-tier offering that reflects the brand's commitment to accessible premium quality.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Cabernet Sauvignon (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Elegant Maipo expression.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Cabernet Sauvignon from Puente Alto showcases the depth and structure characteristic of Chile's premier Maipo Valley terroir. This full-bodied red opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, complemented by pronounced cedar and oak spice from careful winemaking. High tannins provide a firm backbone, with subtle tobacco and earth notes emerging on the finish. The screw cap ensures freshness and accessibility, making this an excellent choice for immediate enjoyment or short-term cellaring.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's Gato Negro Cabernet Sauvignon from Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with ripe blackcurrant and black cherry fruit at its core. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary oak and cedar notes add complexity and aging potential. A straightforward, well-balanced red wine suited to everyday enjoyment and versatile table pairing.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, soft berry, and integrated tannins—ideal for weeknight dining.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's Gato Negro Merlot from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a fruit-forward, accessible profile anchored by ripe plum and a juicy berry medley. The palate shows cranberry and red-fruit character with a hint of vanilla from winemaking, while tannins remain soft and well-integrated. Medium-Full body and moderate acidity make this a versatile, food-friendly red suited to casual entertaining and everyday meals.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Graves Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Graves Bordeaux blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Cabernet Franc—elegant, food-friendly red with ripe fruit and balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mouton Cadet Reserve Graves Rouge is a classic Bordeaux blend anchored by 61% Merlot, with 31% Cabernet Sauvignon and 8% Cabernet Franc. This wine exemplifies the approachable elegance of the Graves appellation, delivering ripe red and dark fruit character balanced by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make it versatile at table—equally at home with grilled meats, roasted poultry, or Mediterranean-inspired dishes. A reliable, well-crafted expression of Bordeaux tradition.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Médoc blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot & Cabernet Franc with ripe dark fruit, cedar & herbaceous depth.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Bordeaux from the prestigious Médoc appellation, this Mouton Cadet Reserve showcases the signature blend of 50% Cabernet Sauvignon, 45% Merlot, and 5% Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and plum aromatics, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes that reflect the terroir's mineral character. Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide food-friendly structure without heaviness, making it equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses. A versatile expression of Left Bank Bordeaux tradition.

name: Concha Y Toro  Frontera Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly structure—ideal for weeknight dining.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Frontera Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a straightforward, fruit-forward profile suited to everyday enjoyment. Ripe blackcurrant and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by subtle bell pepper notes and gentle oak influence. Medium-full body and moderate tannin provide structure without demanding extended cellaring, making this wine an accessible companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. A reliable commercial offering for those seeking reliable quality at an approachable price point.

name: Carpineto  Brunello Di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with layered cherry, earth, and tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): Carpineto's Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a 100% Sangiovese expression from Tuscany's most prestigious red wine region. The 2020 vintage displays the characteristic medium-full body and structured tannins expected of the appellation, with primary notes of cherry and plum supported by earthy minerality. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge from careful aging, while tertiary complexity adds depth and food-pairing versatility. A classic Tuscan red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Talamonti  Moda' Montepulciano D'Abruzzo Doc
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure—a classic Italian red.
description (EN Store): Talamonti's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a quintessential expression of this central Italian varietal, delivering medium-full body with balanced tannins and vibrant acidity. On the nose, ripe red cherry and dark plum lead, followed by subtle earth and dried herb complexity. The palate shows good structure with black pepper spice and mineral grip on the finish, with hints of leather and oak integration. A versatile, food-friendly red that bridges casual weeknight dining and more considered table pairings.

name: Conte Placido  Placido Chianti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Chianti blend of Sangiovese, Canaiolo Nero & Cabernet—medium-full body, food-friendly red with cherry, herb & spice.
description (EN Store): Conte Placido's Chianti DOCG is a classically structured Tuscan red built on Sangiovese with supporting Canaiolo Nero and Cabernet Sauvignon. Medium-full body and balanced tannin deliver approachable yet age-worthy character, opening with red cherry and dark plum before revealing herbaceous and black pepper spice. Earthy undertones and hints of leather suggest modest bottle age potential, making this a versatile companion to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic Mediterranean fare.

name: Argento  Estate Bottle Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, dark plum, and cedary spice—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Argento Estate Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers a classic expression of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by subtle cedar and black pepper notes that speak to careful oak aging. The palate is medium-full and structured, with balanced tannins and a dry finish that lingers with earthy undertones and hints of tobacco complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Argento  Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit, cocoa spice, and supple tannins—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Argento's Malbec from Mendoza showcases the varietal's signature dark fruit profile—ripe plum and blackberry—layered with cocoa and black pepper spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured, ideal for grilled meats, braised beef, and rich mushroom dishes. A reliable, well-crafted expression of Argentina's flagship red.

name: Bodegas Palacios Remondo  La Vendimia Rioja
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja blend of Tempranillo & Garnacha with dark plum, balanced tannin & food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A classic Rioja expression from Bodegas Palacios Remondo, this 50/50 Tempranillo and Garnacha blend delivers a dry, approachable red with medium-full body and vibrant acidity. Deep plum and red fruit dominate the nose, supported by subtle spice and earthy undertones. The balanced tannin structure and long finish make this wine a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare. Excellent value for everyday enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Domaine L'Ostal Cazes  Estibals Minervois AOC
short_description (EN Store): Minervois blend of Syrah, Grenache & Carignan—dark fruit, spice & garrigue. Food-friendly Southern Rhône style.
description (EN Store): Domaine L'Ostal Cazes' Estibals is a classic Minervois blend anchored by equal parts Grenache and Carignan (35% each) with 30% Syrah, delivering a medium-full-bodied red rooted in dark cherry and blackberry fruit. Secondary spice and herbaceous notes—characteristic of the region's garrigue influence—provide complexity and food-friendly structure. Moderate tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable in youth while retaining enough backbone for short-term cellaring. A versatile expression of Southern Rhône tradition suited to grilled meats, Mediterranean fare, and rustic braised dishes.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Beyer Ranch Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Zinfandel-led blend from Wente's Beyer Ranch: dark berry, black pepper spice, and ripe tannins. Food-friendly California classic.
description (EN Store): Wente Vineyards' Beyer Ranch Zinfandel is a medium-full-bodied California red built on 80% Zinfandel and 20% Petite Sirah. The primary character centers on dark berry fruit—blackberry and plum—with a supporting note of black pepper spice that carries through to the finish. Oak and subtle vanilla from winemaking provide secondary complexity, while the structured, ripe tannins frame the wine without overwhelming. This is a food-friendly expression suited to grilled red meat, BBQ, and braised dishes, as well as cured meats and pizza.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Southern Hill Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Wente Southern Hill Cabernet Sauvignon: dark fruit-driven California blend with cedar and cocoa depth, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Wente Southern Hill Cabernet Sauvignon is a California proprietary red blend anchored by 80% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Petit Verdot, Petite Sirah, Malbec, and Tempranillo. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, supported by secondary cedar and cocoa notes that reflect careful oak integration. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin structure provide backbone for food pairing across grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes emerge with age, adding complexity to an approachable, well-balanced California red.

name: Talamonti  Kudos Colline Pescaresi Rosso IGP
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano-Merlot blend from Abruzzo; ripe red fruit, soft tannins, food-friendly Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Talamonti's Kudos Colline Pescaresi Rosso IGP is a 70% Montepulciano and 30% Merlot blend from Abruzzo's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo subregion. This medium-full-bodied red showcases ripe red cherry and dark plum on the nose, with supporting notes of spice and herbaceous character. The palate is approachable and well-balanced, with soft tannins that make it an ideal everyday wine for Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic Mediterranean fare. A reliable, food-friendly expression of the region's signature varietal.

name: Carpineto  Chianti Classico
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese-led blend with balanced red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure—a classic Chianti Classico.
description (EN Store): Carpineto's Chianti Classico exemplifies the traditional Tuscan red blend, anchored by 80% Sangiovese with supporting Canaiolo and regional varietals. The wine presents a medium-full body with measured tannin and acidity, delivering red cherry and plum on the nose alongside subtle dried herb and earthy undertones. A restrained oak influence provides structure without dominating the fruit-forward profile. This is a versatile, food-friendly expression suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Catena Zapata  Catena Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Catena Zapata's Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers a classic expression of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Dark plum and blackcurrant dominate the primary profile, supported by herbaceous undertones and mineral tension. Cedar and black pepper emerge on the mid-palate, with subtle tobacco and earth notes developing on the finish. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled beef, braised short ribs, or aged hard cheeses.

name: Concha Y Toro  Marques de Casa Concha Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic structure for red meat.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Marqués de Casa Concha Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Rapel Valley delivers a classically structured red wine with full body and assertive tannins. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Secondary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the palate, building complexity and age-worthiness. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled steaks, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Carpineto  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Riserva with ripe cherry, spice, and tertiary tobacco complexity. Food-friendly structure for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store):
Carpineto's Vino Nobile di Montepulciano Riserva is a classically structured Tuscan red built on Prugnola Gentile (90%) with Canaiolo Nero support. The wine opens with dominant cherry and plum fruit, supported by warming spice and subtle oak influence. With age, tertiary notes of tobacco, leather, and cedar emerge, adding savory depth and complexity.

Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes. The earthy secondary character also bridges well to tomato-based pasta and aged hard cheeses, making this a versatile food wine for the Tuscan table.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barolo Tortoniano
short_description (EN Store): Classic Barolo from Michele Chiarlo: elegant Nebbiolo with rose, tar, and spice; structured for cellaring and food.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Barolo Tortoniano is a benchmark expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious appellation. The 2018 vintage showcases the varietal's signature elegance: a primary bouquet of red cherry and rose, layered with secondary notes of tar and licorice that speak to the wine's structure and aging potential. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a palate of dried fruit and spice, with tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerging as the wine opens. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses; also rewarding alongside game and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Concha Y Toro  Don Melchor
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Valley Cabernet-led Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins—a food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro Don Melchor is a flagship Bordeaux-style red from Chile's prestigious Maipo Valley. Dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon (92%) with supporting Cabernet Franc, Merlot, and Petit Verdot, this wine delivers a structured, medium-full body with dark fruit intensity and elegant secondary complexity. Blackcurrant and dark plum lead the palate, followed by cedar, black pepper, and subtle tobacco notes that evolve with air. The wine's medium-high tannins and balanced acidity make it a versatile food companion, equally at home with grilled steaks, braised beef, or aged cheeses.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barolo Cerequio
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's Cerequio cru: cherry, tar, and rose with fine tannin structure.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Barolo Cerequio is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious terroirs. The 2018 vintage displays the classic architecture of fine Barolo: bright red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with tar, licorice, and delicate rose petal. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure provide elegance rather than power, with mineral undertones and hints of tobacco and leather emerging on the finish. A wine built for the table and for cellaring.

name: Castello Banfi  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG Poggio alle Mura
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with cherry, plum, and cedar; structured tannins and food-friendly profile.
description (EN Store): Castello Banfi's Brunello di Montalcino Poggio alle Mura is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 100% Sangiovese. The 2018 vintage displays ripe cherry and plum on the nose, supported by cedar and subtle spice from careful oak aging. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide framework for a wine that evolves gracefully across the palate, revealing tertiary notes of tobacco and earth. A food-friendly expression of Brunello suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Mastrojanni  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Classic Brunello di Montalcino: elegant Sangiovese with cherry, earth, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Mastrojanni's Brunello di Montalcino DOCG 2019 is a benchmark expression of Tuscany's most prestigious red wine appellation. Crafted from 100% Sangiovese Grosso (Brunello), this wine displays the hallmark structure and complexity expected from the region: ripe cherry and plum fruit balanced by earthy minerality, tobacco nuance, and fine-grained tannins. The wine's medium-full body and medium-high acidity provide food-friendly elegance suitable for both immediate enjoyment and medium-term cellaring.

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci  Monna Lisa Toscana Rosso IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese with vibrant cherry, dried herbs, and leather—a food-friendly classic with mineral grip.
description (EN Store): Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci's Monna Lisa Toscana Rosso IGT is a single-varietal Sangiovese that embodies the rustic elegance of Tuscan winemaking. The wine opens with bright sour cherry and red fruit on the nose, supported by dried herb and earthy undertones. On the palate, medium-full body and high acidity create a lively frame, while medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness. Secondary leather and tobacco notes add complexity, evolving toward mineral and dried-fruit nuances on the finish. This is a versatile food wine suited to tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and cured charcuterie.

name: San Polo  Brunello Di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): 100% Sangiovese Brunello from Montalcino. Medium-full body, elegant cherry and earth with tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): San Polo's Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a pure expression of Sangiovese from Tuscany's most prestigious red wine region. The 2018 vintage displays the classic profile of the appellation: ripe cherry and plum fruit balanced by earthy minerality, with secondary tobacco and cedar notes emerging from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and structured tannins frame a wine built for food pairing and cellaring potential.

name: Batasiolo  Barolo Vigneto Boscareto
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's Boscareto vineyard: structured red fruit, tar, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Batasiolo's Barolo Vigneto Boscareto is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious appellation. The 2009 vintage displays the classic architecture of mature Barolo: dominant red fruit and dark plum on the nose, layered with secondary tar and tobacco notes that speak to the wine's structure and age-worthiness. Tertiary leather and mineral complexity emerge on the palate, supported by medium-high tannins and balanced acidity. This is a food-friendly yet serious wine, suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Batasiolo  Barolo Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barolo Riserva from Piedmont's premier producer. Structured, age-worthy red with dark fruit, spice, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Batasiolo's 2013 Barolo Riserva represents the classical expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious appellation. This medium-full bodied red displays the characteristic structure and complexity expected of a riserva-level wine, with dominant black cherry and plum fruit supported by secondary tar and tobacco notes. The palate reveals medium-high tannins and balanced acidity, with tertiary leather and cedar complexity emerging from extended aging. A food-friendly yet serious wine suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Finca La Celia  La Celia Supremo
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec-led blend with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly Argentine red.
description (EN Store): Finca La Celia Supremo is a structured Mendoza red blend anchored by Malbec (35%), with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon (28%), Syrah (23%), Merlot (10%), and Cabernet Franc (3%). The 2013 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, with secondary cedar and spice notes that suggest careful oak aging. The palate is medium-full and balanced, with moderate tannins that frame the wine's dark fruit core and emerging tertiary tobacco and leather complexity. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Vigneti del Salento  `I Muri` Primitivo Puglia
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and pepper. Smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Vigneti del Salento's I Muri Primitivo is a bold, fruit-driven red from Italy's sun-soaked Salento peninsula. The wine opens with a generous dark fruit character—black cherry and raspberry dominate—followed by subtle smoke and black pepper on the palate. Fully rounded tannins and a smooth texture make this wine approachable and versatile at table. A touch spicier than typical Primitivo, it delivers complexity without pretension, offering excellent value for everyday enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Vigneti del Salento  "ZOLLA" Primitivo Merlot Puglia IGP
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, fruit-forward Primitivo-Merlot blend from Puglia with dark cherry, pepper spice, and rounded tannins—easy-drinking and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Vigneti del Salento's ZOLLA is a Primitivo-Merlot blend (70/30) from the warm Salento subregion of Puglia, crafted for immediate enjoyment and food compatibility. The wine opens with a bold, fruity nose dominated by dark cherry and raspberry, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper notes. On the palate, it delivers a full-bodied, well-rounded character with fully integrated tannins and a smooth, drinkable finish. The blend's spice-forward profile distinguishes it from sweeter Primitivos, making it versatile at the table—equally at home with pizza and pasta as with grilled meats and braised dishes.

name: Gran Sasso  Sangiovese Terre Di Chieti IGT
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Sangiovese from Abruzzo with ripe cherry, plum, and earthy minerality. Food-friendly, balanced, and excellent value.
description (EN Store): Gran Sasso Sangiovese Terre Di Chieti IGT is a classic expression of Abruzzo's red wine tradition. This 100% Sangiovese delivers ripe red cherry and dark plum on the nose, with subtle herbaceous and earthy undertones that reflect the region's terroir. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and approachable tannins, making it an ideal companion to everyday Italian cuisine and grilled meats. A food-friendly wine that offers genuine character and excellent value for the price point.

name: Lungarotti  Rubesco Torgiano Riserva Vigna Monticchio
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese-Canaiolo blend from Umbria's Torgiano zone; ripe cherry, tobacco, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Lungarotti's Rubesco Torgiano Riserva Vigna Monticchio is a classically structured red from Umbria's prestigious Torgiano subregion. The 70% Sangiovese and 30% Canaiolo blend delivers ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, with supporting notes of tobacco leaf and dried herb. On the palate, medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame a mineral-driven finish with hints of leather and earth—hallmarks of age-worthy Sangiovese. The 2017 vintage shows maturity and complexity suitable for food pairing or cellaring.

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci  Chianti Leonardo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese blend with ripe cherry, plum, and herbaceous notes—a food-friendly classic for Italian cuisine.

name: Mastrojanni  Rosso di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with bright cherry, plum, and mineral notes—a food-friendly Tuscan classic.

name: Pio Cesare  Dolcetto D'Alba

The bright acidity and earthy minerality make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, bridging casual weeknight dinners and more refined table settings.

name: Poggio Al Tesoro  Sondraia DOC

name: Santa Helena  Siglo de Oro Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon

name: Chateaux Cantemerle  Haut Medoc 2021
short_description (EN Store): Classic Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with food-friendly structure.

name: Velenosi  Ludi Offida Rosso DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Marche blend of Montepulciano, Cabernet & Merlot; dark fruit, structured tannins, food-friendly elegance.

name: Velenosi  Montepulciano D' Abruzzo The Year of the Rabbit
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, earthy spice, and food-friendly tannins—a classic Italian red at an excellent value.

name: Velenosi  Brecciarolo, Rosso Piceno Superiore The Year of the Rabbit
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano-Sangiovese blend from Marche; medium-full red with dark fruit, herbaceous spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Velenosi's Brecciarolo is a Rosso Piceno Superiore from Italy's Marche region, blending Montepulciano and Sangiovese to deliver a medium-full-bodied red wine with approachable complexity. The wine opens with ripe dark cherry and plum, supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. Moderate tannins and balanced acidity make it a versatile table wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes. Tertiary earth and dried-fruit notes add subtle depth for contemplative sipping.

name: Vigneti Del Vulture  Pippoli Aglianico Del Vulture DOC
short_description (EN Store): Volcanic Aglianico from Basilicata's Vulture zone: dark fruit, mineral grip, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Vulture's Pippoli Aglianico del Vulture DOC is a medium-full-bodied red from southern Italy's volcanic Vulture region. The wine opens with ripe dark plum and black cherry, supported by black pepper spice and earthy minerality. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge mid-palate, while a slate-driven minerality and firm tannin structure provide grip and aging potential. name: Vigneti Del Vulture  Piano Del Cerro Aglianico Del Vulture DOC
short_description (EN Store): Aglianico del Vulture from Basilicata's volcanic slopes—dark cherry, mineral grip, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Vulture's Piano Del Cerro is a single-varietal expression of Aglianico del Vulture, the signature red of Basilicata's Vulture zone. Grown on volcanic soils, this 2019 vintage delivers dark cherry and black plum on the nose, supported by black pepper spice and a mineral backbone that reflects the region's distinctive terroir. Medium-full body and firm tannins provide structure without heaviness, while secondary leather and smoke notes add complexity. A food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Vigneti Del Vulture  Tufarello Nero Di Troia IGT
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Nero di Troia from Puglia with dark fruit, peppery spice, and smoke—a food-friendly red for pizza, pasta, and grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Vulture's Tufarello Nero di Troia is a medium-full-bodied red from Puglia's volcanic terroir, built on the indigenous Nero di Troia grape. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark cherry and raspberry, followed by a palate marked by smooth, rounded tannins and a distinctive peppery spice. Subtle smoke and earth notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. Eminently drinkable and versatile at the table, this wine pairs beautifully with tomato-based pasta, pizza, grilled red meat, and braised beef dishes.

name: Vigneti Di Zabu  Nero D' Avola Sicilia IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with ripe plum, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly red for Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Di Zabu's Nero d'Avola Sicilia IGT is a medium-full-bodied red anchored by ripe black cherry and plum fruit. Secondary spice and herbaceous notes add textural interest, while tertiary earth and leather suggest modest age-worthiness. A versatile, food-friendly expression of Sicily's signature varietal, suited to grilled meats, lamb, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Little Yering  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Yarra Valley entry-tier Pinot Noir from Australia's oldest vineyard site — wild yeast, hand-plunged, screwcap-sealed for precision.
description (EN Store): Yering Station's home estate traces to 1838, yet every vine standing today dates from a 1988 replanting — meaning Little Yering is built entirely on young-vine fruit from volcanic loam slopes and clay-loam soils across five sites in Yarra Glen and Coldstream, not on the romance of age. Wild yeast fermentation and hand-plunging during primary fermentation define the house method at every tier: gentle tannin extraction without mechanical intervention, preserving the Yarra Valley's cool-climate acidity and site-driven minerality rather than engineering weight. French oak is used with deliberate restraint — new oak percentages kept low across the portfolio — so the volcanic loam's characteristic red-fruit tension reads clearly in the glass. What makes Little Yering structurally unusual is the annual blind allocation tasting: all parcels, including those that will become this wine, are assessed without identity before tier assignment, meaning no block is pre-destined for the entry label — it earns its place by merit each vintage. The result is a Pinot Noir sealed under screwcap (a policy held for over 20 years) that prioritises phenolic ripeness over sugar ripeness, landing at approximately 13.5% ABV with the kind of taut, cool-climate line that warmer Australian regions structurally cannot replicate.

name: Allegrini  Palazzo della Torre IGT (Magnum) (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Benchmark Valpolicella blend: dark cherry, chocolate, spice. Velvety, food-friendly, exceptional value.
description (EN Store): Palazzo della Torre represents Allegrini's masterful interpretation of Valpolicella Classico, blending 70% Corvina with Rondinella and Sangiovese. This medium-full-bodied red opens with a discrete but inviting nose of dark cherry compote and black fruit, evolving on the palate to reveal velvety tannins, judicious oak integration, and warming baking spices. The wine's structure and depth make it exceptionally versatile at table—equally at home with braised beef, aged cheeses, or tomato-driven pasta dishes. A benchmark expression of the region's potential, delivering luxury and complexity at remarkable value.

name: Batasiolo  Langhe Rosso

description (EN Store): Batasiolo's Langhe Rosso is a traditional Piedmont field blend uniting three classic regional varieties: Barbera, Nebbiolo, and Dolcetto. The wine opens with bright red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and earthy minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it an approachable yet structured expression, ideal for Italian cuisine and grilled meats. Subtle oak integration and emerging leather notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

name: Alois Lageder  Pinot Noir Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Alto Adige Pinot Noir with bright cherry, subtle oak, and mineral-driven finish. Food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Alois Lageder's Pinot Noir from the Adige Valley showcases the Alpine terroir of Alto Adige with a refined, medium-full frame. Bright red cherry and strawberry dominate the nose, supported by gentle oak spice and a whisper of earth. The palate is balanced and food-friendly, with silky tannins and a mineral-edged finish that speaks to the region's limestone-rich soils. A versatile red suited to both casual dining and more considered pairings.

name: Allegrini  Valpolicella Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Allegrini's benchmark Valpolicella Classico: dark cherry, velvety tannin, and warm spice—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Allegrini's Valpolicella Classico represents the benchmark expression of this celebrated Veneto region. Crafted from Corvina Veronese (65%), Rondinella (30%), and Molinara (5%), this medium-full-bodied red delivers a compelling primary profile of dark cherry and black fruit compote, supported by subtle oak and warm baking spices from careful winemaking. The palate is inviting and structured, with velvety tannins that frame dried plum and cocoa undertones, making it an exceptionally food-friendly choice for braised meats, aged cheeses, and rich tomato-based dishes.

name: Allegrini  Palazzo della Torre IGT
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella benchmark: dark cherry, cocoa, and baking spice in a velvety, food-friendly frame.
description (EN Store): Palazzo della Torre represents Allegrini's mastery of the Corvina-based blend, a wine that balances approachability with serious depth. The nose opens with dark cherry and black fruit compote, underpinned by subtle cocoa and warm baking spices from careful oak aging. On the palate, the wine delivers a velvety texture with medium-full body and balanced tannin, making it remarkably food-friendly across a wide range of dishes. The finish is warm and lingering, with echoes of dark chocolate and spice. This is a benchmark expression of modern Valpolicella—elegant, concentrated, and built for the table.

name: Allegrini  Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Concentrated Amarone from Veneto's benchmark producer—dark fruit, cocoa, and spice in a full-bodied, age-worthy expression.
description (EN Store): Allegrini's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico represents the pinnacle of the appassimento tradition, where hand-selected Corvina and Corvinone grapes are dried to concentrate their essence. The 2020 vintage delivers a full-bodied wine of considerable depth: dark cherry and blackcurrant dominate the nose, supported by cocoa and warming spice on the palate. Leather and tobacco notes emerge with air, signaling the wine's capacity to evolve gracefully over the next 15–20 years. A benchmark expression of Valpolicella's noble potential.

name: Allegrini  La Poja IGT
short_description (EN Store): Allegrini's benchmark Corvina: dark cherry, chocolate, and spice with velvety tannins. Exceptional value.
description (EN Store): Allegrini's La Poja is a single-varietal Corvina expression from Valpolicella Classico, showcasing the house's mastery of this noble grape. The wine opens with a dominant dark cherry and black fruit compote character, supported by subtle dark chocolate and warming baking spices from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and refined tannins deliver a velvety mouthfeel that lingers with elegance. A benchmark expression of Valpolicella's potential, offering remarkable depth and food-friendliness at its price point.

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Merlot del Veneto
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Merlot from Veneto's benchmark producer—dark fruit, cocoa, and velvety tannin. Exceptional value.
description (EN Store): Corte Giara represents Allegrini's mastery of Merlot in the Veneto region, delivering a wine of impressive depth and approachability. The nose opens with concentrated dark fruit—blackcurrant and black cherry—complemented by subtle oak and cocoa undertones. This is a food-friendly expression that bridges casual dining and more refined occasions, offering exceptional value for its quality and provenance.

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Bardolino DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Classico blend of dark cherry, subtle oak, and balanced tannin—excellent value for everyday red-meat and pasta pairing.
description (EN Store):
Corte Giara by Allegrini represents the benchmark quality of Valpolicella Classico, crafted from the traditional blend of Corvina Veronese, Rondinella, and Molinara. This medium-full-bodied red opens with a discrete but inviting nose of dark cherry compote and black fruit, followed by a palate marked by velvety tannin and judicious oak integration.
