Ideal for braised beef, hearty tomato-based pasta, and aged cheeses, Corte Giara exemplifies Allegrini's mastery of the Corvina grape and the region's winemaking tradition.

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Valpolicella DOC

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Amarone Della Valpolicella "La Groletta" DOCG

description (EN Store): Corte Giara's La Groletta represents the pinnacle of Amarone della Valpolicella craftsmanship. name: Torres  Coronas

name: Louis Latour  Pinot Noir Domaine de Valmoissine
short_description (EN Store): Var département Pinot Noir from Domaine de Valmoissine — Louis Latour's deliberate southward leap beyond Burgundy's Côte d'Or, flash-pasteurised, unfined.
description (EN Store): When Louis Latour planted Pinot Noir in the Var département of Provence — far south of any Burgundian appellation boundary — it was a calculated transgression: the house believed the high-altitude, cool-climate terroir of the Valmoissine valley could ripen Pinot Noir with the structure their Côte d'Or reds demanded, without the land costs of the Côte de Beaune. Domaine de Valmoissine sits at elevation in the Var, where cooler nights preserve acidity in a region better known for rosé and sun-driven reds. The resulting Pinot Noir carries a leaner, more tensile profile than its southern latitude suggests — red-fruited, with a savory, earthy undercurrent driven by the site's distinct soils rather than Burgundy's limestone and marl. Winemaking follows the house's Burgundy red protocol: 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed, fermented in open wooden vats with manual pigeage, then aged in French oak. As with all Louis Latour reds, the wine is flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling — the house's openly acknowledged and uniquely consistent practice across the range, eliminating fining while remaining a point of critical debate. What sets Valmoissine apart categorically: it is Burgundy's most prominent négociant-domaine producing estate-grown Pinot Noir entirely outside any Burgundy AOC, bottled under the Vin de Pays framework.

name: Lindeman's  Bin 45 Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a classic Australian red.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Bin 45 Cabernet Sauvignon from Australia's Hunter Valley delivers a classically structured red wine built on ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the palate with measured complexity, while medium-high tannins provide grip and aging potential. A straightforward, food-friendly expression of Cabernet suited to grilled meats, lamb, and hearty braised dishes.

name: Stonier  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and food-friendly versatility.
description (EN Store): Stonier's Pinot Noir from Victoria showcases the varietal's hallmark elegance and restraint. Bright red cherry and strawberry dominate the nose, supported by subtle plum and earthy minerality. The palate is medium-full bodied with refined, silky tannins and balanced acidity that invites food pairing. Understated oak integration and hints of forest floor complexity round out a wine built for the table rather than the cellar.

name: Masseria Altemura  Sasseo Primitivo
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, fruit-driven Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, raspberry, and peppery spice. Rounded tannins and full body make it perfect for pizza and pasta.
description (EN Store): This 100% Primitivo from Masseria del Fauno delivers the bold, fruit-forward character that defines southern Italian reds. Dark cherry and raspberry dominate the palate, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper notes that add complexity without overwhelming. The wine's rounded tannins and medium-full body create a smooth, approachable drinking experience—neither austere nor overly extracted. Ideal for casual entertaining, pizza nights, and hearty pasta dishes.

name: St. Hallett  Gamekeeper's Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and subtle oak—a food-friendly red for fine dining.
description (EN Store): St. Hallett's Gamekeeper's Shiraz from Eden Valley in the Barossa region delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of Australia's signature varietal. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by secondary notes of oak, vanilla, and cocoa that reflect careful winemaking. A subtle smoke character and black pepper spice add complexity and structure. The wine's balanced acidity and moderate tannin make it versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled red meat, lamb, or braised beef preparations.

name: E.Guigal  Gigondas AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône GSM blend: ripe red berries, garrigue herbs, and peppery spice with food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Gigondas AOC is a classically structured Rhône South blend (65% Grenache, 25% Mourvèdre, 10% Syrah) from the prestigious Gigondas appellation. The 2020 vintage expresses the region's signature character: ripe red and dark plum fruit layered with garrigue herbs, black pepper, and mineral earth. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining complexity for short-term cellaring. Ideal for Mediterranean-inspired cuisine and grilled meats.

name: E.Guigal  Cotes du Rhone Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône blend of Syrah, Grenache & Mourvèdre—ripe berries, spice & garrigue. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Côtes du Rhône Rouge exemplifies the house style: a balanced, medium-full-bodied blend of 50% Syrah, 40% Grenache, and 10% Mourvèdre. The wine opens with ripe red berries and dark plum, layered with white pepper and garrigue—the signature herbs of the southern Rhône. Medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this an ideal everyday red for grilled meats, Mediterranean fare, and rustic cuisine. A benchmark expression of the appellation's food-friendly character.

name: Chateau Cissac  Cru Bourgeois
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Cru Bourgeois with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured, age-worthy Bordeaux.
description (EN Store):
Château Cissac is a Cru Bourgeois estate in the Médoc, crafting a classic Bordeaux blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. This wine displays a full body with high tannins and medium-high acidity, anchored by dominant blackcurrant and cedar notes that define its primary character. Secondary oak and black pepper add complexity, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with bottle age, signaling its potential for cellaring.

The structure and intensity suit grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes; the evolved tertiary profile pairs beautifully with aged hard cheeses and game. A reliable, traditional Médoc expression.

name: Beringer  Founders Estate Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Beringer Founders Estate Merlot is a medium-full bodied red wine from Napa Valley that delivers accessible, fruit-forward character. Ripe plum and berry dominate the nose and palate, with supporting notes of cranberry and subtle herbaceous complexity. The wine's integrated tannin structure and balanced acidity make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and classic comfort-food preparations. A reliable, well-crafted expression of California Merlot suited to both casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Two Oceans  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied South African Shiraz with dark fruit, pepper spice, and smoky complexity.
description (EN Store): Two Oceans Shiraz is a full-bodied red wine from South Africa's Western Cape, showcasing the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity and peppery spice. The wine displays ripe blackberry and plum on the palate, supported by medium-high tannins and a smoky, earthy character that adds depth and structure. Ideal for grilled meats, braised dishes, and BBQ preparations.

name: Two Oceans  Cabernet Sauvignon/Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Bordeaux blend from South Africa's Western Cape—ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, versatile table wine.
description (EN Store): Two Oceans Cabernet Sauvignon/Merlot is a classically structured Bordeaux blend from South Africa's Western Cape, balancing the structure of Cabernet with the approachability of Merlot. The wine displays ripe dark-fruit character—blackcurrant and plum—with supporting notes of cedar and black pepper, finishing with soft, integrated tannins. Medium-full body and moderate acidity make this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Continuum 2019
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Bordeaux blend: dark fruit, structured tannins, elegant complexity. Premium Cabernet-led expression.
description (EN Store): Continuum 2019 is a Bordeaux-style red blend from Napa Valley, anchored by 50% Cabernet Sauvignon with supporting Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, and Merlot. The wine displays a full body with medium-high tannins and medium acidity, characteristic of premium Napa Valley reds. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—dominates the primary profile, complemented by cedar and black pepper notes from oak aging. Tertiary complexity emerges through tobacco and leather, suggesting structure and aging potential. This is a food-friendly yet sophisticated expression suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes.

name: Donnafugata  Mille e Una Notte Contessa Entellina DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with ripe dark fruit, spiced oak, and Mediterranean character. Food-friendly, elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata Mille e Una Notte Contessa Entellina DOC is a Sicilian red wine crafted from Nero d'Avola, the island's signature dark-skinned varietal. The 2019 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit with supporting notes of oak spice and vanilla, layered with subtle earth and tobacco complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured, ideal for pairing with grilled lamb, beef stews, and Mediterranean herb-forward dishes. A versatile expression of Sicilian terroir.

name: Donnafugata  Tancredi D&G (Cabernet Sauvignon, Nero  d'Avola, Tannat)
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Cabernet-Nero d'Avola-Tannat blend with dark fruit depth, cedar structure, and Mediterranean spice.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Tancredi D&G is a Sicilian red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon with Nero d'Avola and Tannat, crafted to express both New World power and Old World elegance. The 2020 vintage delivers a full-bodied wine with prominent blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and oak complexity. High tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide backbone and aging potential, while secondary spice and tertiary tobacco notes add sophistication. This is a wine for serious red-meat cuisine and extended cellaring.

name: Donnafugata  Sedàra Sicilia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian red blend of Nero d'Avola, Cabernet, Merlot & Syrah—dark fruit, pepper, and earthy depth.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Sedàra is a Sicilian red blend anchored by Nero d'Avola, the island's signature dark-skinned variety, complemented by Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Syrah. The wine delivers ripe black cherry and blackberry on the nose, with supporting notes of black pepper and herbaceous character typical of Mediterranean blends. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it food-friendly across grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. Earthy undertones add complexity and suggest modest aging potential.

name: Fairview  Pinotage
short_description (EN Store): South African Pinotage with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Fairview's Pinotage from Paarl delivers the signature character of South Africa's flagship varietal: ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, with supporting spice and subtle smoke from careful winemaking. The medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet structured enough for serious food pairing. Earthy undertones and a hint of leather emerge on the finish, adding complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile red that bridges casual entertaining and more formal dining.

name: Frescobaldi  Nipozzano Riserva Chianti Rufina DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti Rufina with cherry, herbs, and mineral complexity—a classic Tuscan expression.
description (EN Store): Frescobaldi's Nipozzano Riserva represents the traditional character of Chianti Rufina, a subregion celebrated for high-altitude vineyards and mineral-driven Sangiovese. This medium-full-bodied red opens with bright cherry and herbaceous notes, supported by earthy undertones and a distinctive mineral spine. The palate reveals layered complexity: leather and tobacco emerge as secondary and tertiary characteristics, while high acidity and medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential. A wine of elegance and restraint, suited to food-forward occasions.

name: Frescobaldi  Castiglioni Chianti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti DOCG with bright cherry, herbaceous herbs, and earthy leather—a versatile Tuscan red for Italian classics.
description (EN Store): Frescobaldi's Castiglioni Chianti DOCG is a classically structured Sangiovese from Tuscany's heartland. The wine opens with vibrant cherry and herbaceous notes, supported by earthy minerality and subtle spice. Medium-Full body and high acidity provide freshness and food-friendliness, while emerging leather and tobacco complexity suggest modest aging potential. A reliable expression of Chianti's traditional character—approachable yet layered enough for thoughtful pairing.

name: Frescobaldi  Remole Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon & Pecorino with cherry, herbaceous notes & structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Frescobaldi's Remole Toscana IGT is a Super Tuscan blend anchored by Sangiovese with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Pecorino. The wine displays a medium-full body with high acidity and medium-high tannins, delivering a structured, age-worthy profile. Primary aromas of cherry and herbaceous character lead to secondary leather and spice notes, with tertiary tobacco and earth complexity emerging on the palate. This blend is well-suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and lamb dishes, as well as tomato-based pasta and aged hard cheeses.

name: Altivo  Classic Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Altivo Classic Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers the signature dark-fruit intensity and structured tannins expected of Argentina's premier red-wine region. Blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the palate, supported by subtle cedar and vanilla oak from careful aging. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine an approachable yet serious companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Piccini  Chianti Arancio DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti with bright acidity, cherry fruit, and earthy complexity—a versatile food-friendly Tuscan red.
description (EN Store): Piccini's Chianti Arancio DOCG showcases the classic profile of Sangiovese from Tuscany's renowned region. Medium-Full bodied with vibrant acidity and structured tannins, this wine opens with cherry and red fruit aromatics, supported by dried herb and mineral notes. The palate reveals layered complexity: bright fruit acidity drives the mid-palate, while leather and tobacco undertones add depth and age-worthiness. A food-friendly expression that bridges everyday enjoyment with serious food pairing potential.

name: Penfolds  Bin 23 Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Adelaide Hills Pinot Noir from Penfolds — a house built on Barossa Shiraz deliberately stepping into cool-climate Burgundian territory.
description (EN Store): Penfolds was founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate to produce fortified wine for medicinal use — a house whose entire modern identity is anchored in Barossa Shiraz and American oak. Bin 23 Pinot Noir is the deliberate transgression: Penfolds applying its multi-regional selection discipline to a variety that demands the opposite of everything the house is famous for. Adelaide Hills supplies the fruit — a high-altitude, cool-climate zone where diurnal temperature swings preserve aromatic precision and natural acidity that the Barossa floor cannot offer. The region's red-brown clay loams over ironstone retain just enough moisture to moderate vine stress without sacrificing concentration. Winemaking follows cool-climate logic: whole-bunch pressing protocols informed by the same hand-selection rigour applied across the Penfolds range, with oak used in a supporting rather than structural role — a marked departure from the new American hogshead regime that defines Grange. The special feature here is institutional: no other wine in the Penfolds portfolio so completely inverts the house's oak-forward, warm-climate signature, making Bin 23 the clearest evidence that the blending philosophy is about fruit quality first, variety second. Drink on release through the near term for primary fruit lift, or cellar 3–6 years for secondary development.

name: Louis Latour  Marsannay Cote de Nuits
short_description (EN Store): Marsannay Pinot Noir from Louis Latour's négociant range: hand-harvested, open-vat fermented, flash-pasteurised before bottling — Côte de Nuits entry point from a house founded in 1797.
description (EN Store): When Jean Latour established the house in 1797 — his family already three generations into vine ownership on the Côte d'Or — the ambition was to bottle Burgundy's northern appellations with the same rigour applied to Grand Cru parcels. Marsannay sits at the northern gateway of the Côte de Nuits, where limestone-bedrock soils give way to lighter clay-limestone mixes at relatively modest elevations, producing Pinot Noir with a cooler aromatic register and firmer structural backbone than villages further south. Grapes are 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed, and fermented in large open wooden vats for approximately 14 days with manual pigeage — the same gravity-flow infrastructure used at the Corton Grancey Cuverie, built in 1832 into the hillside at Aloxe-Corton. Aging proceeds in French oak at 15–35% new, medium toast, for 10–12 months, using barrels coopered in-house from air-dried French oak. The house's most openly debated signature: all reds are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining while ensuring microbiological stability — a practice no other major Burgundy négociant publicly acknowledges. For 2023, the Côte de Nuits delivered bright, aromatic Pinot Noir with good natural acidity; drinking well on release with a 3–6 year window ahead.

name: Louis Latour  Bourgogne Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Entry-level Burgundy from an 11-generation house that flash-pasteurises every red — 100% Pinot Noir, fermented in open wooden vats, aged in French oak.
description (EN Store): When Jean Latour formalised the house in 1797, his family had already been growing vines in the Côte d'Or for over six decades — a continuity that now spans eleven generations and 28.63 hectares of Grand Cru land. The Bourgogne Pinot Noir sits at the base of that pyramid, but it is made under the same roof and the same philosophy as the Grand Crus above it. Grapes are 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed, and fermented in large open wooden vats on the first floor of the Corton Grancey Cuverie — the first purpose-built winery in France, completed in 1834 — with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days, gravity moving the wine at every stage. Aging follows in French oak at 15–35% new, medium toast, 10–12 months, with barrels coopered in-house from air-dried French oak staves. The defining house transgression: all red wines are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating the need for fining while remaining one of the most openly debated practices in Burgundy. At village level, this Bourgogne delivers the house's characteristic clean, fruit-forward Pinot profile — the flash-pasteurisation producing microbiological stability and textural clarity that few négociant peers achieve at this price point.

name: Luce Della Vite   Luce IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese and Merlot with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Luce Della Vite's 2019 Luce IGT is a Tuscan Super Tuscan crafted from Sangiovese and Merlot, representing the modern Montalcino style. The wine opens with dominant red and dark plum fruit, supported by secondary cedar and spice notes from careful oak aging. Tertiary leather and earthy undertones emerge with air, suggesting food-friendly structure and mid-term cellaring potential. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this a versatile pairing for grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Luce Della Vite  La Vite Lucente Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese and Merlot; medium-full body with red fruit, cedar, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Luce Della Vite's La Vite Lucente is a Sangiovese-Merlot blend from Montalcino, Tuscany, crafted in the Super Tuscan tradition. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe red and dark fruit on the nose—cherry, plum, and blackcurrant—complemented by cedar spice and herbaceous notes. On the palate, secondary oak and spice integrate smoothly, while tertiary leather and earthy undertones suggest structure and aging potential. A food-friendly expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, tomato-based pasta, and aged cheeses.

name: Pala  Essentija Isola Dei Nuraghi IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sardinian red with ripe fruit, herbaceous charm, and food-friendly structure—a versatile table wine for Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Pala's Essentija Isola Dei Nuraghi IGT is a Sardinian red wine that embodies the rustic character and mineral-driven terroir of the island. The 2019 vintage presents a medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, offering primary notes of red and dark plum fruit alongside herbaceous undertones. Subtle spice and earthy minerality in the secondary and tertiary layers add complexity without overwhelming the palate. This is a food-friendly wine suited to grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Pala  I Fiori Centosere  Cannonau Di Sardegna DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sardinian Grenache with ripe red fruit, spice, and mineral backbone—food-friendly Mediterranean classic.
description (EN Store): Pala's I Fiori Centosere is a single-varietal Grenache from Sardinia's Cannonau DOC, a region celebrated for producing structured, mineral-driven expressions of this Mediterranean grape. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by herbaceous notes and a subtle peppery spice that reflects the warm, rocky terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile food companion, equally at home with grilled lamb, rustic tomato-based pasta, or Mediterranean charcuterie. Earthy undertones and mineral finish suggest careful winemaking that respects the grape's natural character.

name: Pala  Silenzi Rosso Isola Dei Nuraghi IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese from Montalcino with bright cherry, herbaceous notes, and structured tannins—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A single-varietal Sangiovese from Montalcino in Tuscany, this wine expresses the classic profile of the region: bright cherry fruit balanced by herbaceous undertones and mineral-driven acidity. Medium-Full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, making it versatile at table. Subtle spice and earth in the mid-palate evolve toward leather and tobacco complexity on the finish, suggesting modest aging potential.

name: Tommasi  Amarone Della Valpolicella Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Amarone Classico from Veneto's Tommasi: concentrated black cherry, dried fruit, and spice with structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Tommasi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico 2020 exemplifies the appassimento tradition of northeastern Italy. Corvina and Pecorino grapes are dried before fermentation, concentrating sugars and flavors into a wine of considerable depth and power. The palate opens with ripe black cherry and dried fruit, supported by almond and cocoa notes from careful oak aging. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide structure; tertiary leather and earth notes emerge with air, suggesting complexity that will develop further in bottle. A wine suited to serious food pairing and contemplative enjoyment.

name: Tommasi  Ripasso Valpolicella Classico Superiore IGT
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Corvina-based Ripasso from Valpolicella Classico with ripe red fruit, herbaceous notes, and balanced oak—excellent value for everyday red.
description (EN Store): Tommasi's Ripasso Valpolicella Classico Superiore showcases the signature character of Corvina from the Veneto region. The wine opens with dominant red fruit and dark plum aromatics, complemented by herbaceous undertones and subtle oak influence from winemaking. Medium body and tannin structure provide approachable drinking without sacrificing complexity. The palate reveals layered dark fruit compote, spice, and hazelnut notes, with a clean, balanced finish. This expression exemplifies the traditional Ripasso technique—partial drying of grapes before fermentation—delivering depth and richness at an exceptional price point.

name: Umani Ronchi  San Lorenzo Rosso Conero DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano from Marche with red cherry, plum, and mineral complexity—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): San Lorenzo Rosso Conero DOC represents the classic expression of Montepulciano from Italy's Marche region. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure provide structure without heaviness, while subtle mineral and earthy notes emerge on the finish, suggesting careful winemaking and terroir expression. This is a versatile red suited to both casual dining and more considered food pairings.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Cigalus Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Domaine de Cigalus — Bertrand's first biodynamic estate (2002) — blends Bordeaux varieties with Syrah and Grenache under IGP Aude Hauterive, aged in French oak.
description (EN Store):
When Gérard Bertrand converted Domaine de Cigalus to biodynamics in 2002, it became the founding experiment of what would grow into the world's largest Demeter-certified wine operation — every harvest decision, from picking date to bottling, governed by the lunar calendar. That origin story matters technically: two decades of biodynamic soil management on this IGP Aude Hauterive estate has built microbial complexity in the ground that no chemical shortcut can replicate.

Cigalus sits within the Aude Hauterive IGP, a designation that grants Bertrand the latitude to plant Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, and Merlot alongside indigenous Syrah, Grenache, and Carignan — a transgression of Languedoc convention that no AOP framework would permit. The warm Mediterranean climate is tempered by the estate's inland position, and the 2021 vintage's cooler, later-ripening conditions delivered fresher acidity across the Languedoc, moderating what can otherwise be a sun-saturated, heavy-footed profile.

Vinification follows the house protocol of separate parcel fermentation in concrete vats, with aging in French oak barrels — the precise new-oak percentage undisclosed for this cuvée but consistent with Bertrand's graduated approach that reserves 100% new oak for Clos d'Ora alone. The Bordeaux-Languedoc assemblage is Cigalus Rouge's defining structural anomaly: Cabernet's cassis frame and Merlot's mid-palate flesh are anchored by Syrah's dark olive and pepper, producing a red that sits outside any single regional archetype. Drink 2024–2029.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve St.Emilion
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion blend of Merlot, Cabernet Franc & Cabernet Sauvignon; ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mouton Cadet Reserve Saint-Émilion is a classically proportioned Bordeaux red built on a foundation of 88% Merlot, with supporting Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine expresses ripe black cherry and dark plum on the nose, with subtle cassis undertones and a whisper of herbaceous character typical of the appellation. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannins create an approachable yet structured palate, while restrained oak integration and earthy minerality add complexity without dominating. This is a food-friendly expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, roasted poultry, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Gerard Bertrand  6eme Sens Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc GSM blend from the world's largest Demeter-certified biodynamic producer — lunar-calendar farming, parcel-separate vinification, Mediterranean terroir.
description (EN Store):
Gérard Bertrand formally founded his wine company in 1992, inheriting a family winemaking lineage stretching back to his grandmother Paule Bertrand in 1920 — but the defining decision came in 2002, when he began converting all estates to Demeter-certified biodynamics, a commitment now spanning over 900 hectares across 17 Languedoc-Roussillon properties, making him the world's largest biodynamic producer at scale.

The 6ème Sens Rouge draws on the warm Mediterranean basin of Languedoc-Roussillon, where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming without chemical intervention. The core Syrah, Grenache, and Mourvèdre blend is sourced across multiple terroirs — gravelly soils at Château de Villemajou in Corbières-Boutenac, limestone at La Clape, and clay-limestone at inland sites — each contributing structural contrast to the final assemblage.

Every parcel is vinified separately in concrete vats or stainless steel tanks before blending, with harvest timing and cellar decisions governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar. The result is a négociant-scale wine built with single-estate discipline. No chemical inputs are used across the farming program, consistent with Demeter certification requirements.

What sets this cuvée apart within its price tier is the Demeter-certified biodynamic provenance applied to an entry-level blend — a standard of farming more commonly reserved for prestige cuvées. The current vintage reflects the warm, concentrated character typical of southern Languedoc, with the multi-terroir blend providing structural breadth for near-term drinking.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturalys Carbernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Cabernet Sauvignon from Languedoc's largest biodynamic producer, vinified to lunar calendar timing across 900+ ha of southern French terroir.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand formally built his wine company in 1992 on a foundation his grandmother Paule Bertrand established in 1920 — and the Naturalys Cabernet Sauvignon is where that century of Languedoc rootedness meets his most radical commitment: converting every one of his 17 estates to Demeter-certified biodynamics, making him the world's largest producer operating under that standard. Every decision from harvest to bottling is governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar, a discipline that begins in the vineyard and runs through to the cellar. The warm Mediterranean climate of Languedoc-Roussillon, tempered by strong winds that reduce disease pressure without chemical intervention, provides the conditions that make zero-input farming viable at this scale. Vinification follows the Bertrand house method of separate-parcel processing, with fermentation in stainless steel or concrete to preserve varietal precision before assemblage. The Naturalys range carries certified organic status with no added sulfites — an unusual commitment for a Cabernet Sauvignon at this price point, where most producers rely on sulfur as a stabilising crutch. The result is a Cabernet that reads as genuinely site-expressive rather than corrected: dark-fruited, herb-edged, and structured by the grape's natural tannin frame rather than oak intervention.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturalys Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Syrah from Languedoc, vinified parcel-by-parcel under the lunar calendar by the world's largest biodynamic producer.
description (EN Store):
When Gérard Bertrand began converting his Languedoc estates to biodynamics from 2002 onward — ultimately certifying all properties to Demeter standard — he made a deliberate wager that the South of France could shed its bulk-production identity entirely. The Naturalys Syrah sits within that mission: a certified biodynamic expression built on the warm Mediterranean terroir of Languedoc-Roussillon, where strong seasonal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming without chemical intervention, and where sun-baked soils push Syrah toward dark fruit concentration and savory depth.

Vinification follows Bertrand's house discipline of separate parcel fermentation, with reds processed through destemming and maceration in concrete vats or stainless steel tanks before Gérard himself oversees the final assemblage. Every stage — from harvest timing to bottling — is governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar, a practice Bertrand applies uniformly across all 17 estates. The Naturalys range is produced with no added sulfites, certified organic, and vegan-suitable, placing it among the most technically constrained expressions in the portfolio.

What distinguishes Naturalys within the Bertrand lineup is the zero-sulfite commitment at an accessible price point — a technical discipline more commonly reserved for prestige cuvées. The warm, drought-prone character of recent southern vintages delivers concentrated dark fruit and a plush mid-palate, with the biodynamic farming lending a mineral clarity that lifts the finish. Drink now through the near term.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturalys Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Merlot from Languedoc, vinified to the lunar calendar by the world's largest biodynamic estate group.
description (EN Store): When Gérard Bertrand began converting his Languedoc estates to biodynamics from 2002 onward — ultimately certifying all holdings to Demeter standard — he made every harvest, fermentation, and bottling decision subject to the lunar calendar, a discipline that governs the Naturalys range as much as his prestige cuvées. Naturalys Merlot is produced under IGP Pays d'Oc, drawing from the warm Mediterranean basin of Languedoc-Roussillon where strong seasonal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming with zero chemical inputs. The warm, sun-drenched terroir drives Merlot toward plush, dark-fruited concentration, while biodynamic soil management — no synthetic fertilisers, compost preparations, herbal teas — preserves microbial life and mineral exchange in the vineyard. Vinification follows Bertrand's parcel-by-parcel approach, with fermentation in stainless steel or concrete vats and winemaking timed to biodynamic fruit and root days. The Naturalys range carries no added sulfites and is certified organic and vegan-suitable — a rare combination at this price point within the Languedoc IGP tier. What distinguishes this expression within its category is the Demeter biodynamic certification applied at industrial scale: the same rigorous lunar-calendar discipline used at Clos d'Ora (฿65k tier) is non-negotiable here. Drink young, where the fruit is at its most expressive.

name: Piccini  Chianti Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese Riserva from Chianti with bright cherry, herbaceous notes, and structured tannins—a classic Tuscan expression.
description (EN Store): Piccini's Chianti Riserva DOCG showcases the hallmark character of Sangiovese from Tuscany's premier region. Medium-Full bodied with vibrant acidity and firm tannin structure, this wine opens with primary cherry and herbaceous aromatics, followed by earthy undertones and subtle spice. Secondary leather notes emerge with air, while tertiary tobacco and minerality add complexity and age-worthiness. A food-friendly classic suited to grilled meats, tomato-based dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Shiraz Viognier
short_description (EN Store): Eden Valley Shiraz co-fermented with Viognier from Australia's pioneering Viognier house — planted the variety in 1980 when it was virtually unknown outside France.
description (EN Store): Yalumba planted Viognier in Eden Valley in 1980 — nearly two decades before the variety gained traction elsewhere in Australia — establishing the house as the country's foremost authority on the grape and making this co-fermentation a direct expression of that institutional knowledge. Eden Valley's shallow, rocky, well-drained soils at 400–550 m elevation slow ripening considerably against the Barossa floor, preserving the aromatic lift and natural acidity that define the region's Shiraz. The Viognier component is co-fermented with the Shiraz rather than blended post-vinification — a technique that bonds the aromatic compounds of the white grape irreversibly into the red's colour and structure, producing a violet-tinged hue and a floral-savoury tension no post-blend can replicate. Fermentation follows Yalumba's minimal-intervention philosophy, with oak treatment calibrated to preserve fruit transparency rather than impose wood character. All Yalumba wines have been 100% vegan since the 2012 vintage, with no animal-derived fining agents — an unusual commitment at this production scale. The Y Series sits at the entry point of a house whose Viognier expertise runs from this wine up to the benchmark single-vineyard Virgilius, meaning the technical DNA here is genuinely pedigreed. Drink now through 3–4 years for peak aromatic expression.

name: Yalumba  Organic Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Clare Valley organic Shiraz from Australia's oldest family winery, vegan-certified since 2012, with minimal-intervention winemaking at its core.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith and still held by the Hill-Smith family six generations later, Yalumba has built its identity around viticultural precision and winemaking restraint — commitments that extend to this certified organic Shiraz from Clare Valley. Clare Valley sits at elevations of roughly 400–500 m in South Australia's Mid-North, where a continental climate with cool nights preserves natural acidity in Shiraz, and the region's characteristic red-brown clay-loam and limestone-influenced soils lend a savoury, iron-tinged character distinct from the warmer Barossa floor. The organic farming protocol eliminates synthetic inputs, allowing soil biology to express itself directly in the fruit. Fermentation follows Yalumba's minimal-intervention philosophy, with wild/spontaneous fermentation used to initiate the process in open-top vessels, building textural complexity without manufactured aromatic shortcuts. All Yalumba wines have been 100% vegan since the 2012 vintage, with no animal-derived fining agents used at any stage. What sets this expression apart within the Yalumba range is its organic certification applied to a cool-climate Clare Valley site — a deliberate departure from the house's Barossa-centric red wine identity. Drink now through the near term for primary fruit character, or hold briefly for further integration.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Entry-tier Merlot from Australia's oldest family winery, vegan since 2012, built on Yalumba's minimal-intervention philosophy at Barossa/Eden Valley.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith and still held by the sixth-generation Hill-Smith family, Yalumba built its reputation on viticultural research and winemaking restraint long before either became fashionable — the Y Series exists to channel that institutional knowledge into an accessible format without abandoning the house philosophy. The Y Series Merlot draws on South Australian fruit, where warm days and cool nights preserve both ripeness and freshness, with Yalumba's minimal-intervention approach guiding the winemaking from vineyard to bottle. Fermentation follows Yalumba's house preference for controlled, low-intervention vinification, with the wine crafted to be 100% vegan — no animal-derived fining agents used since the 2012 vintage, a commitment applied uniformly across the entire portfolio regardless of price tier. What distinguishes the Y Series within the entry-level Australian Merlot category is precisely this: the same vegan certification and minimal-intervention philosophy that governs The Caley and The Octavius applies here without compromise. Drink now through the near term for plush, fruit-forward character.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Entry-tier Shiraz from Australia's oldest family winery, built on vegan-certified, minimal-intervention winemaking and the only Southern Hemisphere winery cooperage.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1849 by Samuel Smith and still held by the sixth-generation Hill-Smith family, Yalumba made a deliberate institutional commitment to oak control that no Australian peer has matched — operating the only fully functional winery-owned cooperage in the Southern Hemisphere, hand-crafting barrels from French, American, and Hungarian oak on-site. The Y Series Shiraz draws on South Australian fruit, where warm Barossa Valley floor sites and cooler Eden Valley elevations of 400–550 m over shallow, rocky, well-drained soils contribute contrasting registers: the valley floor delivering mid-palate density, the elevated sites lending tension and lift. Winemaking follows the house's minimal-intervention philosophy — wild/spontaneous fermentation initiated in open-top fermenters, some of which date to the 1800s, with oak sourced and crafted through the on-site cooperage. The wine has been 100% vegan since the 2012 vintage, with no animal-derived fining agents used across the entire Yalumba range. What distinguishes the Y Series within its entry-tier category is that it inherits the same winemaking infrastructure and cooperage philosophy as Yalumba's ฿9,000+ flagships — the oak regime simply scales down. Drink now through the near term for primary dark-fruited character, or hold briefly for integration.

name: Mascota Vineyards  La Mascota Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit intensity, structured tannin, and savory spice—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' La Mascota Malbec is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza, Argentina's premier red wine region. The wine displays the hallmark dark fruit character of quality Malbec: ripe plum and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by black cherry notes and a framework of medium tannin. Secondary spice and smoke add complexity, while subtle earth and leather undertones suggest careful winemaking and potential for short-term cellaring. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or cured charcuterie.

name: Mascota Vineyards  La Mascota Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' La Mascota Cabernet Sauvignon is a medium-full bodied red from Argentina's Mendoza region, built on a foundation of blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and vanilla notes from oak aging provide secondary complexity, while subtle earth and herbaceous undertones emerge on the finish. The wine's balanced tannin and acidity make it a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Mascota Vineyards  Unánime Gran Vino Tinto
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Bordeaux blend of dark fruit, cedar, and spice—food-friendly structure for grilled meats and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' Unánime Gran Vino Tinto is a Mendoza Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (60%), with supporting Malbec (25%) and Cabernet Franc (15%). The 2018 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with cedar and subtle spice from oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with secondary cocoa notes and emerging tertiary tobacco complexity. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes; also pairs well with aged hard cheeses and mushroom-forward preparations.

name: Caliterra  Cabernet Sauvignon Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet Reserva with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Caliterra's Cabernet Sauvignon Reserva from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic Bordeaux-style profile anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum. The blend of Cabernet Sauvignon with Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc introduces cedar and cocoa undertones, supported by balanced medium tannin and acidity. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Ste. Michelle  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet-led California blend with dark cherry, blackcurrant, and cedar—approachable medium-full structure for everyday red-meat pairing.
description (EN Store): Chateau Ste. Michelle's Cabernet Sauvignon is a food-friendly California blend anchored by 85% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Syrah, Merlot, and other varieties. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and black cherry fruit, layered with dark plum undertones. Oak aging introduces vanilla and cedar notes, while black pepper spice adds textural complexity. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannin make this an accessible choice for casual dining and everyday entertaining. The herbaceous undertones suggest careful winemaking restraint, allowing the fruit to remain the focal point.

name: Chateau Ste. Michelle  Syrah
short_description (EN Store): California Syrah with dark plum, black pepper, and smoke—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Chateau Ste. Michelle's Syrah is a California red wine built on a foundation of dark plum and blackberry fruit, with supporting notes of black pepper and smoke that add complexity and structure. The 5% Viognier co-ferment contributes subtle floral character and softens the tannin profile, making this wine approachable yet substantial enough for hearty fare. Medium-full body and balanced acidity position it as a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef preparations.

name: Planeta  Plumbago Nero d'Avola IGT Sicilia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark plum, blackberry, and spice—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Planeta's Plumbago Nero d'Avola is a medium-full-bodied Sicilian red built on the indigenous Nero d'Avola varietal. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, supported by blackberry notes and a mineral-driven spice character. Secondary earth and subtle oak frame the palate, while tertiary tobacco and leather emerge with air, lending complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and tomato-based Mediterranean dishes.

name: Millaman  Estate Reserve Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured for food.
description (EN Store): Millaman Estate Reserve Merlot from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a classic single-varietal expression of Merlot's fruit-forward character. The wine opens with dominant plum and berry aromatics, supported by hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes. On the palate, a juicy medley of red fruits unfolds with integrated tannins and a soft, approachable texture that belies the wine's medium-full body. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add subtle complexity without overwhelming the fruit. This is a versatile, well-balanced Merlot suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty pasta preparations.

name: Millaman  Limited Reserve Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Zinfandel with dark berry intensity, peppery spice, and oak complexity—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Millaman's Limited Reserve Zinfandel from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers the varietal's signature dark fruit character with medium-full body and structured tannins. Blackberry and plum dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper spice and subtle oak influence from winemaking. The wine shows enough complexity for casual aging and pairs excellently with grilled red meat, smoked preparations, and hearty braised dishes. A versatile, approachable red for the premium casual dining segment.

name: Catena Zapata  Nicasia Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec-led blend with dark fruit, spice, and structured tannin—elegant Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Catena Zapata's Nicasia Malbec is a Mendoza-based red blend anchored by 90% Malbec, with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure, offering dark plum and blackberry on the nose, layered with cocoa and spice on the palate. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, suggesting food-friendly complexity suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses. A reliable expression of Argentine Malbec craftsmanship.

name: Santa Carolina  Cabernet Sauvignon Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—a food-friendly Reserva for grilled meats and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Santa Carolina's Cabernet Sauvignon Reserva from Colchagua Valley delivers a classic Chilean expression of the varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by cedar and subtle oak from careful aging. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannin frame flavors of black cherry and spice, with emerging tobacco and leather notes that suggest complexity and structure. A versatile red suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged hard cheeses.

name: Lindeman's  Bin 40 Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Hunter Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for casual dining.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Bin 40 Merlot is a medium-full bodied red from Australia's Hunter Valley, showcasing the varietal's signature plum and berry-forward character. The wine delivers a juicy palate with integrated tannins and subtle herbaceous undertones, finishing with soft texture and approachable structure. A versatile everyday red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Lindeman's  Bin 50 Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, black pepper spice, and food-friendly structure—a classic Australian red.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Bin 50 Shiraz is a benchmark Hunter Valley expression showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit character and peppery spice. The wine displays ripe plum and blackberry on the nose, with a medium-full body and balanced tannin structure that makes it approachable yet substantial enough for grilled meats and hearty dishes. A touch of subtle oak adds complexity without overshadowing the fruit-forward profile, positioning this as a reliable, food-friendly red for everyday enjoyment and casual entertaining.

name: Lindeman's  Premier Selection Shiraz / Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Shiraz-Cabernet blend: dark fruit, spice, and approachable structure for everyday red wine enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Premier Selection Shiraz/Cabernet is a food-friendly red blend from Australia's Hunter Valley, combining the richness of Shiraz with the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by black pepper spice and subtle oak influence. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or casual pasta dinners. A reliable, approachable choice for everyday enjoyment.

name: Masi  Amarone "Costasera" Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Amarone Classico from Veneto's legendary Masi: ripe dark fruit, oak-aged complexity, and silky tannins for serious red-meat dining.
description (EN Store):
Masi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico exemplifies the appassimento tradition of northeastern Italy. Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara grapes are dried before fermentation, concentrating sugars and flavors into a wine of considerable depth and structure.

On the nose, dark fruit compote—black cherry and plum—dominates, with subtle oak and vanilla from careful aging. The palate is full and enveloping, with medium-high tannins that frame the wine's richness without overwhelming. Tobacco and leather notes emerge in the finish, adding tertiary complexity and food-friendly grip.

A benchmark expression of Amarone's power and elegance, suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, aged cheeses, and game.

name: Masi  Amarone Riserva "Costasera" Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Masi's flagship Amarone Riserva: concentrated dark fruit, oak-aged complexity, and tertiary tobacco depth. Premium Valpolicella.
description (EN Store): Masi's Amarone Riserva Costasera represents the pinnacle of appassimento winemaking from Veneto's Valpolicella Classico zone. This full-bodied red showcases the signature intensity of dried-grape fermentation: dominant dark plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with cocoa and warming spice from extended oak aging. The palate is rich and structured, with medium acidity balanced by medium-high tannins that frame the wine's complexity. Tertiary notes of tobacco, leather, and earth emerge with air, revealing the wine's capacity for further evolution. A benchmark expression of Amarone's power and elegance.

name: Masi  Valpolicella "Bonacosta" Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with cherry-forward fruit, herbaceous notes, and balanced oak—a versatile Italian red for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Masi's Bonacosta Classico exemplifies the Valpolicella Ripasso tradition: a medium-bodied red built on Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara grapes from Veneto's historic heartland. The wine opens with ripe cherry and dark fruit compote, supported by subtle herbaceous undertones and restrained oak integration. Medium acidity and tannin create a food-friendly structure that bridges casual dining and more refined table settings. Almond and dried-fruit notes emerge on the finish, suggesting modest aging potential.

name: Chateau Meyney  Cru Bourgeois Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Saint-Estèphe Cru Bourgeois with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—a structured, food-friendly Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Château Meyney's 2007 Cru Bourgeois Supérieur is a classically proportioned Saint-Estèphe blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (45%) and Merlot (41%), with Petit Verdot (14%) adding structure and spice. The wine displays a dominant primary of blackcurrant and dark plum, supported by secondary cedar and black pepper notes from oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerge, characteristic of mature Bordeaux. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a food-friendly profile suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Ricasoli  Brolio Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese-led blend with red fruit, herbs & subtle oak—food-friendly Chianti Classico.
description (EN Store): Ricasoli's Brolio Chianti Classico DOCG is a classically structured Tuscan red anchored by Sangiovese (80%), with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The 2022 vintage expresses bright red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with herbaceous notes and a whisper of oak from careful aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine a versatile table companion—equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or aged cheeses. The wine's earthy undertones and subtle black pepper spice reflect the terroir of Chianti Classico, while its food-friendly structure ensures broad appeal across Mediterranean and Italian cuisine.

name: Ricasoli  Chianti Classico Gran Selezione Castello di Brolio DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico Gran Selezione with ripe cherry, violet, and cedar—a food-friendly Tuscan red.
description (EN Store): Ricasoli's Castello di Brolio Gran Selezione represents the pinnacle of Chianti Classico tradition, built on a foundation of 90% Sangiovese with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied expression with bright red cherry and plum on the nose, framed by violet florality and herbaceous undertones. Secondary oak and spice notes add complexity, while tertiary leather and earthy minerality emerge on the palate, suggesting graceful aging potential. This is a wine of balance and restraint—neither overpowering nor austere—making it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Ricasoli  Rocca Guicciarda Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico Riserva with ripe cherry, plum, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store): Ricasoli's Rocca Guicciarda Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG is a refined expression of Sangiovese-led Tuscan winemaking. The 2019 vintage showcases the characteristic red-fruit profile of the region—ripe cherry and plum on the nose—layered with dried herb, leather, and subtle tobacco notes that develop with air. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while retaining the aging potential expected of a Riserva designation. Earthy minerality and spice on the finish reflect the terroir of Chianti Classico, positioning this wine as a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Fattoria dei Barbi  Brunello Di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with ripe cherry, plum, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store): Fattoria dei Barbi's Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a single-varietal expression of Sangiovese from one of Tuscany's most prestigious terroirs. The 2017 vintage displays the characteristic red-fruit purity and mineral structure expected of the appellation, with layers of cherry, plum, and subtle dried-herb notes. Oak aging contributes gentle spice and vanilla undertones, while tertiary development introduces earthy, tobacco, and leather complexity. Medium-high tannins and acidity provide structure and food-pairing versatility, making this wine equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Fattoria dei Barbi  Rosso Di Montalcino DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino: bright cherry, earthy minerality, and food-friendly tannin.
description (EN Store): Fattoria dei Barbi's Rosso di Montalcino is a pure expression of Sangiovese from Tuscany's prestigious Montalcino zone. This medium-full-bodied red opens with vibrant red cherry and plum, grounded by earthy undertones and subtle herbaceous notes. The palate is structured by medium tannin and bright acidity, delivering mineral complexity on the finish. A versatile, classically styled wine built for the table—equally at home with pasta, grilled meats, or aged cheese.

name: Tenuta San Guido  Le Difese
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet Sauvignon–Sangiovese from Bolgheri's calcareous marl and gabbro soils — the estate's entry point into coastal Tuscany's founding terroir.
description (EN Store):
Le Difese is the third wine of Tenuta San Guido, the Bolgheri estate where Marchese Mario Incisa della Rocchetta planted Cabernet Sauvignon in 1944 — two decades before the practice was fashionable in Tuscany — and where the Super Tuscan movement was effectively born. That founding ambition filters down into Le Difese, a Cabernet Sauvignon–Sangiovese blend carrying genuine estate DNA at an accessible tier.

The vines draw from Bolgheri's characteristically complex soils: calcareous marl, gabbro, alberese, and old alluvial pebbles across plots ranging from 60 to 400 m a.s.l., moderated by Tyrrhenian Sea breezes channelled through the Bibbona–Castagneto amphitheatre. The maritime influence suppresses heat accumulation, preserving the aromatic lift and structural acidity that define the house style.

Fermentation proceeds in temperature-controlled stainless steel at ≤28–29°C with estate-selected indigenous yeasts; malolactic fermentation is spontaneous. Oak aging follows in French barriques (Tronçais, Allier, Jupilles; light toast, long-seasoned staves), with a split across new, first-fill, and second-fill casks — a regime scaled from Sassicaia's own barrel program.

The special feature: blending Cabernet Sauvignon with Sangiovese rather than Merlot is a deliberate departure from Bolgheri convention, threading Tuscan identity — dried cherry, iron-tinged acidity — through a coastal-Bordeaux framework. The 2022 vintage has no estate-specific notes in the brand library; drink from release through 2028–2030.

name: Sandrone  Nebbiolo D'Alba "Valmaggiore" DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo d'Alba with cherry, licorice, and cedar—age-worthy Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Sandrone's Nebbiolo d'Alba 'Valmaggiore' is a structured, medium-full red from Piedmont's prestigious Langhe region. The 2021 vintage displays the varietal's signature cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes. Medium-high acidity and tannin provide backbone and aging potential, while the wine's complexity unfolds through dried fruit, tar, and subtle rose character. A refined expression of Nebbiolo suited to serious food pairing and cellar development.

name: Chateau Riviere "Cuvee Prestige"
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Grenache-Syrah blend with ripe red fruit, spice, and garrigue—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Château Rivière's Cuvée Prestige is a classic Languedoc-Roussillon red built on the Rhône South tradition of Grenache and Syrah. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and a medium acidity that keeps it fresh and versatile at table. Expect ripe red berries and dark plum on the nose, layered with white pepper spice and subtle garrigue—the hallmark of southern French terroir. The palate is round and approachable, with earthy undertones that suggest modest aging potential. A food-friendly expression ideal for casual entertaining and everyday dining.

name: Chateau Bonnet  "Reserve" Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Balanced Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar, and approachable tannins.
description (EN Store): Château Bonnet's Reserve Bordeaux pairs equal parts Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon in a classically structured red wine. The 2018 vintage expresses ripe black cherry and dark plum on the nose, with supporting notes of cedar and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or herb-roasted poultry. A food-friendly expression of traditional Bordeaux winemaking.

name: Agricola Querciabella  Mongrana Maremma Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Merlot & Cabernet Sauvignon with sour cherry, leather & dried herbs.
description (EN Store): Agricola Querciabella's Mongrana from Maremma showcases a classically structured Tuscan blend anchored by Sangiovese with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine displays a vibrant primary character of red cherry and red fruit, complemented by dried herb notes that speak to the region's Mediterranean terroir. Medium-Full body and high acidity provide excellent food-pairing versatility, while medium-high tannins add grip and aging potential. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge on the palate, with subtle earth and cedar complexity developing in the glass, suggesting this wine will evolve gracefully over several years.

name: Agricola Querciabella  Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti Classico with bright acidity, dried herbs, and leather—a versatile food wine.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Chianti Classico from Agricola Querciabella, this 2021 Sangiovese expresses the region's hallmark balance of bright acidity and refined tannin. The nose opens with red cherry and dried herbs, followed by subtle leather and mineral notes that evolve on the palate. Medium-full body and high acidity make this wine a natural companion to tomato-based dishes, grilled meats, and aged charcuterie. The wine's earthy undertones and tobacco leaf character suggest cellaring potential, though it drinks with immediate pleasure.

name: Chateau Laffitte Carcasset  Cru Bourgeois
short_description (EN Store): Saint-Estèphe Bordeaux blend of dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco—a food-friendly classic for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Château Laffitte Carcasset's 2017 Cru Bourgeois from Saint-Estèphe is a traditional Bordeaux red blend anchored by 70% Cabernet Sauvignon and 30% Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and plum fruit, supported by secondary cedar and tobacco notes that reflect careful oak aging. Tertiary leather and mineral complexity emerge with time, offering depth and structure. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Bel-Air  Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Pomerol blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): Château Bel-Air's 2019 Pomerol is a classically structured Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine displays a full body with high tannin grip and medium-high acidity, delivering a serious, age-worthy profile. Primary notes of blackcurrant and dark plum are complemented by secondary cedar and oak character, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest complexity and depth. This is a wine built for the table—pair with grilled red meat, lamb, or aged cheeses.

name: Chateau La Conseillante  Pomerol 2018
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol Merlot with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft tannins; silky texture with cocoa undertones.
description (EN Store): Château La Conseillante's 2018 Pomerol showcases the region's signature Merlot-driven elegance. A medium-full-bodied expression with dominant ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by juicy berry complexity and subtle herbaceous notes. The palate reveals integrated tannins and a silky texture, with secondary vanilla and cocoa notes from careful oak aging. Balanced acidity and a persistent finish make this a refined, approachable Bordeaux suitable for near-term enjoyment or modest cellaring.

name: Chateau L'Evangile  Pomerol 2015
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc with dark cherry, plum, and subtle cedar complexity.
description (EN Store): Château L'Évangile's 2015 Pomerol showcases the classic elegance of this Right Bank Bordeaux appellation. The blend of 84% Merlot and 16% Cabernet Franc delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, supported by subtle cedar and oak from careful aging. The palate reveals layered complexity, with tobacco and leather notes emerging as the wine opens, grounded by fine-grained tannins and balanced acidity. This is a food-friendly expression of Pomerol, approachable in its youth yet structured for medium-term cellaring.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Beaujolais Villages
short_description (EN Store): 100% Gamay from Beaujolais Villages AOC, vinified by Drouhin's finesse-first house under fourth-generation family stewardship since 1880.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1880 by Joseph Drouhin and now stewarded by the fourth generation — Frédéric, Véronique, Philippe, and Laurent — Maison Joseph Drouhin built its reputation on a singular conviction: terroir transparency over extraction. That philosophy, most visible in the Côte d'Or Grand Crus, extends without compromise to this Beaujolais Villages cuvée.

Beaujolais Villages draws from the granite and schist-dominant soils of the northern Beaujolais hills, where shallow topsoil over fractured bedrock forces Gamay roots deep, producing fruit with tighter structure and more mineral tension than the clay-heavy southern plains. The elevated terrain moderates ripening, preserving the natural acidity that defines the appellation's freshness.

Drouhin vinifies this 100% Gamay with the same low-intervention discipline applied across the portfolio: indigenous yeast fermentation, minimal extraction to protect the grape's inherent red-fruit transparency, and élevage decisions driven by tasting evaluation rather than fixed protocol. Oak is used sparingly at this level, preserving primary fruit character.

The transgression here is deliberate restraint: where most Beaujolais Villages is rushed to market for carbonic freshness, Drouhin's house style imposes a Burgundian patience — the result sits closer to a light Pinot Noir in structure than to a typical Gamay. Drink now through 2–3 years for peak vibrancy.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Brouilly
short_description (EN Store): Beaujolais Brouilly from Drouhin's Gamay-focused négociant work — biodynamic sourcing, indigenous yeasts, silky granite-driven red fruit.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin is one of Burgundy's rare négociant-domaines to hold full ECOCERT organic and biodynamic certification across its entire 100-hectare estate — a conversion completed under Philippe Drouhin from 1990, with certification awarded for the 2009 vintage. That same low-intervention philosophy extends to its Beaujolais sourcing. Brouilly, the largest of the ten Beaujolais crus, sits on the slopes of Mont Brouilly, where decomposed granite and blue volcanic schist soils deliver the cru's characteristic mineral lift and fine-grained texture to Gamay. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts in small open vats, consistent with Drouhin's house-wide native-yeast commitment, with maceration calibrated for freshness over extraction. Élevage follows Drouhin's minimal-intervention philosophy, preserving the site's granite-driven aromatic transparency rather than layering oak. What sets this cuvée apart within the Drouhin portfolio is its status as one of the house's rare departures from its strict Pinot Noir–Chardonnay focus — Gamay is the singular exception the house permits. The 2018 vintage delivered excellent phenolic maturity across the region; the Brouilly shows ripe, expressive fruit with structural freshness, drinking well now through 2026.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Fleurie
short_description (EN Store): Beaujolais Fleurie from Drouhin's négociant range: 100% Gamay, indigenous yeast fermentation, French oak élevage, house finesse philosophy applied to granite-terroir cru.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 by Joseph Drouhin and now stewarded by the fourth generation under winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin, the house built its reputation on a single conviction: terroir transparency over extraction. That philosophy extends to this Fleurie, one of the few Beaujolais crus in the Drouhin portfolio — a deliberate outlier in a range otherwise restricted to Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. Fleurie sits on decomposed granite soils in the northern Beaujolais crus, where shallow, sandy granite over bedrock forces Gamay roots deep, producing wines of notable lift and floral precision rather than the plush fruit weight of clay-dominant southern crus. Drouhin's vinification follows the house red protocol: fermentation with indigenous yeasts in small open vats with pigeage, followed by élevage in French high-forest oak at village-level proportions (15–25% new oak), integrating structure without masking the granite's characteristic mineral tension. The special feature here is categorical: Gamay is the sole exception to Drouhin's Pinot Noir/Chardonnay-only rule, making this Fleurie a genuine anomaly within the portfolio. The 2018 vintage delivered excellent phenolic maturity with increased whole-cluster vinification across the range; expect a wine of aromatic intensity and supple grip, drinking well now through 2026.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Laforet Bourgogne Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Drouhin's Bourgogne-wide Pinot Noir, vinified in 228–500L French oak with indigenous yeasts — house finesse philosophy at regional level.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation, Maison Joseph Drouhin built its reputation on a single conviction: that Burgundy's two noble varieties — Pinot Noir and Chardonnay — require no supplementation, only transparency. Winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin applies the same house philosophy to Laforet that governs the Grand Cru cellar: no overextraction, no shortcuts.

Laforet draws on Drouhin's négociant network across the Bourgogne AOC, sourcing from the clay-limestone and gravel-over-marl soils that define the Côte d'Or's regional tier — sites that deliver red-fruit precision rather than weight. The 2022 growing season was hot and dry, producing phenolically ripe fruit with firm structural backbone, a vintage where Drouhin's restraint-first approach prevents the heat from tipping into overripeness.

Vinification follows the house protocol: total destemming, fermentation in small open vats with indigenous yeasts, 2–3 weeks maceration with pigeage, followed by élevage in French high-forest oak (228L–500L) with 15–25% new wood. The special feature here is institutional: Drouhin restricts its entire red portfolio to Pinot Noir, meaning every sourcing and blending decision at the Laforet level is benchmarked against the same palate that produces Chambertin-Clos de Bèze. The 2022 drinks well now and will hold through 2027–2028.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Cotes De Beaune Villages
short_description (EN Store): 100% Pinot Noir from Côte de Beaune, biodynamic farming, indigenous-yeast fermentation, 12–18 months in French oak — 2021's frost-reduced yields deliver precision over volume.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation of the Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate — certified by ECOCERT for the 2009 vintage — an extraordinary undertaking for a négociant-domaine of this scale. The Côte de Beaune Villages appellation draws from the clay-limestone soils that define the southern Côte d'Or, where moderate elevation and easterly aspects temper ripening and preserve the taut acidity that distinguishes Beaune-side Pinot Noir from the more muscular expressions of the Côte de Nuits.

Vinification follows the house's low-intervention doctrine: total destemming for village-level reds, fermentation in small open vats with indigenous yeasts, pigeage and pumping-over across a 2–3 week maceration, then 12–18 months in French high-forest oak (228L–500L) at 15–25% new wood. Élevage decisions are made by tasting rather than fixed protocol — a deliberate rejection of formula winemaking. The 2021 vintage, marked by spring frost and mildew pressure, produced severely reduced yields; strict sorting delivered wines of notable freshness and structural precision rather than volume. Drink 2024–2029.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Santenay
short_description (EN Store): Santenay Pinot Noir from Drouhin's organically farmed Côte de Beaune holdings — 2021's frost pressure yielded small crops of striking freshness and precision.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full organic and biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate — earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage — a commitment rarely matched by a négociant-domaine of this scale. Santenay sits at the southern tip of the Côte de Beaune, where clay-limestone soils over Jurassic bedrock give Pinot Noir a firmer, more mineral-edged character than the silkier expressions found further north toward Volnay or Pommard. The 2021 growing season brought severe frost and mildew pressure, forcing strict sorting and yielding unusually small crops; where fruit was healthy, the result was wines of notable freshness and structural precision rather than the generous weight of warmer years. Vinification follows total destemming for village-level reds, with fermentation in small open vats using indigenous yeasts over a 2–3 week maceration with both pigeage and pumping-over. Élevage proceeds in French high-forest oak (228L–500L barrels) for 12–18 months at 15–25% new oak, with all decisions made by tasting evaluation rather than fixed protocol — a deliberate rejection of formulaic production. Winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin's house philosophy of finesse over extraction means this village-level Santenay is built on silky tannin architecture rather than extracted weight. The 2021 vintage rewards drinking from 2024 through 2030, its cool-year acidity providing genuine mid-term cellaring potential.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Volnay
short_description (EN Store): 2021 Volnay from Drouhin's biodynamic Côte de Beaune holdings — small-yield frost year delivering precision Pinot Noir over power.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full biodynamic conversion of its Burgundy estate under Philippe Drouhin — earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage, an extraordinary commitment for a 100-hectare négociant-domaine hybrid. Volnay sits on the southern Côte de Beaune, its clay-limestone soils over a compact limestone bedrock delivering the appellation's signature combination of floral lift and silky tannin grip that distinguishes it from the more muscular Pommard to its north.
