The 2021 vintage was defined by spring frost and persistent mildew pressure, forcing strict sorting and yielding one of the smallest crops in recent memory. Where fruit was healthy, the result was notable freshness and precision rather than generosity. Winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin vinified with total destemming for this village-level cuvée, fermenting in small open vats with indigenous yeasts over 2–3 weeks of maceration, using pigeage and pumping-over. Élevage followed in French high-forest oak (228L–300L barrels, 15–25% new oak) for 12–18 months, with oak seasoned approximately three years before coopering to minimise raw wood influence.

What sets this expression apart is Drouhin's house-wide refusal to chase extraction: the entire red portfolio is built on finesse, and the 2021 vintage's naturally lower yields and cool-season acidity align precisely with that philosophy — producing a Volnay where the appellation's violet and red-fruit character reads with uncommon clarity.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Pommard
short_description (EN Store): Pommard 2021: biodynamic Pinot Noir from a frost-tested vintage; indigenous-yeast fermentation, 15–25% new French oak, silky structure over concentration.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate under Philippe Drouhin — earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage, an extraordinary commitment for a négociant-domaine of this scale. Pommard sits on the Côte de Beaune's heaviest clay-limestone soils, a denser, iron-rich terroir that naturally pushes Pinot Noir toward darker fruit, firmer tannin architecture, and a more muscular frame than its neighbours in Volnay or Beaune. Vinification follows total destemming for this village-level cuvée, with fermentation and maceration in small open vats over 2–3 weeks using indigenous yeasts, pigeage and pumping-over throughout. Élevage runs 12–18 months in 228L or 300L French high-forest oak barrels at 15–25% new wood, seasoned approximately three years before coopering — a deliberate restraint that keeps oak as a structural tool rather than a flavour source. The house philosophy of finesse over extraction is the transgression here: where many Pommard producers lean into the appellation's power, Drouhin's winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin consistently pulls toward precision and silky tannin resolution. The 2021 vintage — marked by spring frost and mildew pressure, strict sorting, and small yields — delivered wines of notable freshness and precision; this Pommard rewards 3–8 years of cellaring.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Corton Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Corton Grand Cru 100% Pinot Noir from biodynamic vines; partial whole-cluster fermentation, indigenous yeasts, aged in French high-forest oak.
description (EN Store): When Maurice Drouhin began acquiring Premier and Grand Cru parcels in the early 20th century, the house's guiding conviction was that Burgundy's greatest sites should be farmed, not merely sourced — a principle that drove the full biodynamic conversion completed under Philippe Drouhin, with ECOCERT certification awarded for the 2009 vintage across 100 hectares. Corton, the Côte de Beaune's sole red Grand Cru, sits on a broad hill of iron-rich limestone and clay-marl soils at elevation, where the aspect and drainage concentrate Pinot Noir into a more structured, darker-fruited register than the silkier Côte de Nuits Grand Crus. Vinification employs partial whole-cluster inclusion (up to 40–50% in ripe vintages), fermentation in small open vats with pigeage and pumping-over over 2–3 weeks, driven entirely by indigenous yeasts. Élevage proceeds in 228L or 300L French high-forest oak barrels for 12–18 months, with new oak proportions elevated above the house's village-level norm for Grand Cru. What distinguishes Drouhin's Corton within the appellation is the deliberate restraint of extraction — the house philosophy of finesse over power holds even at Grand Cru level, where many producers push for maximum concentration. The 2018 vintage delivered excellent phenolic maturity in reds with increased whole-cluster vinification, producing wines of depth and structural grip that reward 5–12 years of cellaring.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Cote De Nuits Villages
short_description (EN Store): Drouhin's village-tier Côte de Nuits: 100% Pinot Noir, indigenous yeasts, French oak at 15–25% new, from a biodynamic estate certified since 2009.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880, Maison Joseph Drouhin has spent four generations building a house philosophy that prioritises terroir transparency over extraction — a discipline that shapes every tier of the range, including this Côte de Nuits Villages. The Côte de Nuits Villages appellation draws from the northern and southern fringes of the Côte de Nuits, where clay-limestone soils at moderate elevation produce Pinot Noir with firmer structure and darker fruit character than the lighter gravel-influenced soils further south in the Côte de Beaune. Vinification follows total destemming for village-level reds, with fermentation and maceration in small open vats over two to three weeks using indigenous yeasts, pigeage and pumping-over. Élevage proceeds in French high-forest oak barrels (228L or 300L) for 12–18 months at 15–25% new oak, with decisions made by tasting evaluation rather than fixed protocol. What sets Drouhin apart at this level is the biodynamic farming underpinning the fruit: the estate completed full ECOCERT-certified organic and biodynamic conversion by the 2009 vintage — an extraordinary commitment across 100 hectares. The 2022 vintage delivered phenolically ripe fruit from a hot, dry growing season, with good structural backbone; the wines are approachable now but will reward two to four years of additional cellaring.

name: Nederburg  Duet Shiraz Pinotage
short_description (EN Store): South African Shiraz-Pinotage blend with dark fruit, pepper spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied red from Paarl blending 60% Shiraz with 40% Pinotage. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper and subtle spice. The palate shows ripe, integrated tannins with earthy undertones and restrained oak influence. A versatile, approachable wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and charcuterie.

name: Nederburg  Winemaster's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): South African Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Food-friendly medium-full body.
description (EN Store):
Nederburg's Winemaster's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Paarl showcases the classic structure and depth expected of a premium South African expression. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the core, supported by cedar and subtle oak spice from careful winemaking. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge on the palate, adding savory complexity and age-worthiness.

Medium-full body and balanced medium tannin make this wine approachable yet structured enough for cellaring. The wine's food-friendly profile suits grilled and braised red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Louis Latour  Cote de Nuits-Villages (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Côte de Nuits-Villages Pinot Noir from Louis Latour's 11th-generation négociant house — hand-harvested, open-vat fermented, flash-pasteurised before bottling.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1797 by Jean Latour — a third-generation winegrower whose family had tended Côte d'Or vines since 1731 — Maison Louis Latour built its Corton Grancey cuverie in 1832 as France's first purpose-built gravity-flow winery, a philosophy that still governs every red wine produced today. Côte de Nuits-Villages occupies the northern and southern bookends of the Côte de Nuits, where clay-dominant soils over limestone bedrock at modest elevations produce Pinot Noir with firmer structure and darker fruit character than the lighter gravel-over-limestone of the Côte de Beaune villages to the south.

The wine is 100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, fully destemmed and crushed, then fermented in large open wooden vats for approximately 14 days with manual pigeage and gravity-only transport throughout — no pumping at any stage. Aging follows in French oak at 15–35% new, medium toast, 10–12 months, with barrels coopered in-house from air-dried French oak staves. The house's most openly debated signature: all reds are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining entirely while ensuring microbiological stability — a practice no other major Burgundy négociant openly acknowledges. In 2021, a cool, selective growing season across the Côte de Nuits delivered wines of genuine freshness and tensile structure, with drinking windows extending comfortably through the late 2020s.

name: Marques De Riscal  Reserva Rioja DOC (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja Reserva with deep plum, leather, and vanilla oak—elegant structure for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Marques De Riscal's Reserva from Rioja Alta showcases the classic elegance of aged Tempranillo. Deep red cherry and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by supple tannins and medium-high acidity that frames a long, dry finish. Vanilla oak and leather notes emerge on the mid-palate, with subtle dried herbs and cedar adding complexity. A wine of balance and restraint, suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged hard cheeses.

name: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite)  Aussieres  Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Cabernet-Syrah blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—elegant Rothschild craftsmanship.
description (EN Store): Domaines Barons de Rothschild's Aussières Rouge is a full-bodied Languedoc blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah, delivering the house's signature balance of fruit intensity and structural elegance. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility, with secondary notes of tobacco and leather emerging as the wine develops. A refined expression of southern French winemaking at an accessible price point.

name: Louis Jadot  Beaujolais Villages
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Beaujolais Villages with bright red fruit, earthy minerality, and silky tannins—a versatile food-friendly Pinot Noir.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Beaujolais Villages is a classic expression of Burgundy's lighter-styled Pinot Noir, showcasing the varietal's signature elegance and freshness. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry aromatics, supported by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the region's terroir. On the palate, high acidity and silky tannins create a refined texture that feels both approachable and food-friendly, making it an ideal companion for a wide range of dishes from roasted poultry to mushroom risotto.

name: E.Guigal  Crozes Hermitage Rouge AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Crozes Hermitage Syrah: dark fruit, peppery spice, and mineral structure. Food-friendly Rhône classic.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Crozes Hermitage Rouge is a benchmark expression of northern Rhône Syrah, sourced from the appellation's prime terroir. The 2020 vintage delivers a harmonious profile: ripe dark fruit (blackberry, plum) on the nose, layered with white pepper and subtle smoke from careful oak handling. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced tannins and mineral undertones, offering both immediate appeal and aging potential. A versatile food wine that bridges casual dining and fine dining tables.

name: E.Guigal  Chateauneuf du Pape Rouge AOC
short_description (EN Store): Classic Châteauneuf-du-Pape: ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and supple tannins. Food-friendly Rhône blend.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Châteauneuf-du-Pape Rouge is a benchmark expression of the southern Rhône's most celebrated appellation. The blend—dominated by Grenache with supporting Syrah and Mourvèdre—delivers ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with garrigue herbs, black pepper, and mineral earth. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannins create a wine that is both approachable and age-worthy, with secondary spice notes and tertiary leather complexity emerging with time. A versatile food wine suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and herb-forward preparations.

name: E.Guigal  Cote-Rotie Brune Et Blonde De Guigal
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Côte-Rôtie blending Syrah power with Viognier's floral grace—dark fruit, pepper, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Brune et Blonde de Guigal is a benchmark expression of Côte-Rôtie's dual-slope terroir. The 96% Syrah base delivers dark cherry, black pepper, and licorice notes with medium-full body and refined tannin structure, while the 4% Viognier contribution adds floral aromatics and textural elegance. Smoke and spice emerge on the mid-palate, with underlying minerality and earth that speak to the region's granite and schist soils. A food-friendly, age-worthy red suited to grilled meats, game, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Castello Colle Massari  Montecucco Riserva DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese with sour cherry, dried herbs, and leather—structured acidity and tannin for food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Castello Colle Massari's Montecucco Riserva is a classically structured Sangiovese from Tuscany's Montecucco subregion. The wine opens with dominant sour cherry and red fruit character, supported by dried herbs and earthy minerality. Secondary leather and spice notes emerge mid-palate, while tertiary tobacco complexity develops with age. High acidity and medium-high tannin provide excellent food compatibility and aging potential, making this a versatile choice for Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and cured preparations.

name: Castello Colle Massari  Rigoleto Montecucco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-Montepulciano blend from Montecucco with cherry, dried herbs, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Rigoleto represents Castello Colle Massari's expression of Montecucco's terroir through a Sangiovese and Montepulciano blend. The wine displays a medium-full body with vibrant acidity and structured tannins, offering cherry and red fruit on the nose alongside dried herbs and mineral notes. Secondary leather and tobacco character emerge on the palate, providing age-worthy complexity suitable for food-forward occasions.

name: St. Hallett  Blackwell Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and oak—a medium-full, food-friendly red for fine dining.
description (EN Store): St. Hallett's 2019 Blackwell Shiraz from Eden Valley, Barossa Valley, showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla oak and black pepper spice. Subtle smoke and cocoa notes add complexity, making this a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-roasted poultry. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure deliver approachable elegance suitable for both casual entertaining and fine-dining occasions.

name: Marques de Caceres  Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Rioja Reserva blend of Tempranillo, Garnacha, and Graciano with dark plum, spice, and balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Marqués de Cáceres Reserva is a classic Rioja blend anchored by Tempranillo with supporting Garnacha Tinta and Graciano. The wine displays a deep red color and opens with dominant dark plum and red fruit aromas, complemented by subtle spice and cedar from careful oak aging. On the palate, medium-full body and medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food compatibility. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, adding complexity and age-worthiness. A food-friendly expression of traditional Rioja craftsmanship.

name: Marques de Caceres  Crianza
short_description (EN Store): Rioja Crianza with ripe plum, leather, and vanilla oak—balanced structure for everyday red meat and roasted fare.
description (EN Store): Marqués de Cáceres Crianza is a classically styled Rioja red from the prestigious Rioja Alta subregion, blending Tempranillo with Garnacha Tinta and Graciano for depth and complexity. The wine displays a deep ruby color with aromas of dark plum and red cherry, supported by subtle leather and vanilla oak notes from careful barrel aging. On the palate, medium-full body and medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food-friendliness. Dried herb and black pepper notes add savory complexity, making this an excellent choice for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Clarendelle  Rouge Inspired by Haut-Brion AOC
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux blend from Saint-Émilion with ripe plum, cherry, and subtle cedar—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store):
Clarendelle Rouge Inspired by Haut-Brion is a Bordeaux red blend anchored by 78% Merlot, with 17% Cabernet Sauvignon and 5% Cabernet Franc. Sourced from Saint-Émilion, this wine delivers a medium-full body with balanced tannins and moderate acidity, making it an accessible yet structured expression of the region's classic style.

On the nose, ripe black cherry and dark plum dominate, supported by subtle cedar and oak undertones. The palate shows good concentration of dark fruit with a mineral backbone and soft tannin structure that encourages food pairing. A wine designed for everyday elegance and table versatility.

name: Chateau Latour  A Pomerol 2018
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot & Cabernet Franc with dark fruit, cedar, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Château Latour à Pomerol's 2018 vintage showcases the classic elegance of Pomerol's finest terroir. The 85% Merlot base provides a voluptuous core of dark plum and blackcurrant, while 15% Cabernet Franc adds structural definition and herbal nuance. Medium-full body and balanced tannins frame layers of cedar, tobacco, and earthy minerality that evolve with air. The wine displays both immediate fruit-forward appeal and the complexity to reward cellaring, with leather and dried fruit notes emerging on the finish.

name: Cristo Di Campobello  Rosso IGP
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola-led blend with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly, medium-full red.
description (EN Store): A Sicilian proprietary blend anchored by Nero d'Avola with supporting Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Syrah. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, layered with black cherry and subtle spice. Secondary herbaceous and oak notes provide structure and complexity, while tertiary earth and tobacco character emerge on the finish. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food wine suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Monchiero Carbone  Barbera d'Alba MonBirone DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Barbera with bright cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Monchiero Carbone's Barbera d'Alba MonBirone DOC represents the classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with vibrant red cherry and plum on the nose, supported by black pepper spice and subtle earthy minerality. Medium tannins and bright acidity provide structure without heaviness, making this wine exceptionally food-friendly across a range of Italian and Mediterranean cuisines. The wine shows restraint and elegance typical of quality Roero producers, with a dry finish that invites another sip.

name: Rocca Di Frassinello  Ornello DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Syrah, Cabernet & Merlot; medium-full body, red fruit & spice.
description (EN Store): Rocca Di Frassinello's Ornello DOC is a structured Super Tuscan blend anchored by Sangiovese (40%) with supporting Syrah, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot. The 2020 vintage delivers ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with black pepper and cedar from oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine food-friendly across grilled meats, braised beef, and tomato-based dishes. Subtle leather and earth notes suggest modest aging potential.

name: Bottega  Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with dark cherry, almond, and balanced tannin—versatile with red meat and pasta.
description (EN Store): Bottega's Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso DOC showcases the classic Veneto blend of Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara grapes. The wine opens with dark fruit and cherry aromatics, supported by subtle hazelnut and herbaceous notes from careful oak aging. On the palate, medium body and balanced tannin structure deliver a smooth, food-friendly profile ideal for braised meats, tomato-based dishes, and aged cheeses. The ripasso technique—partial re-fermentation on dried grape skins—adds depth and complexity while maintaining approachability. A reliable choice for everyday enjoyment and versatile table pairing.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Barbera d'Alba DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbera d'Alba with ripe cherry, plum, and balanced spice—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store):
Cordero Di Montezemolo's Barbera d'Alba DOC is a benchmark expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal. The 2022 vintage displays the characteristic medium-full body and moderate tannin structure that defines quality Barbera, with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit on the nose and palate. Supporting notes of spice and subtle oak frame the wine without overwhelming its primary fruit character, while emerging tertiary earth and tobacco hints suggest modest aging potential.

This is a versatile, food-friendly red suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and mushroom-forward dishes. The balance of acidity and tannin makes it approachable in youth while rewarding brief cellaring.

name: Nicolis  Seccal Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with dark cherry, plum, and subtle oak—food-friendly Veneto classic for red meat and rich sauces.
description (EN Store): Nicolis Seccal Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso DOC is a refined expression of Veneto's signature red blend, anchored by Corvina with supporting Rondinella, Molinara, and Croatina. The ripasso technique—extended contact with dried-grape skins—imparts depth and complexity without heaviness. Dark cherry and ripe plum dominate the nose, with subtle oak and spice from careful aging. The palate is smooth and approachable, with medium tannin and balanced acidity that invites food pairing. Tertiary notes of dried fruit and earth emerge with aeration, revealing the wine's classical structure. A versatile choice for braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Nebbiolo with red cherry, rose, and tarry complexity—a food-friendly classic for grilled meats and aged cheeses.
description (EN Store): Cordero Di Montezemolo's Langhe Nebbiolo is a benchmark expression of Piedmont's most noble red varietal. The 2023 vintage showcases the characteristic elegance and structural complexity that define quality Nebbiolo: a dominant primary of red cherry and rose, layered with secondary tar and licorice notes that emerge mid-palate. Medium-high tannins and acidity provide the backbone for extended cellaring, while tertiary leather and tobacco hints suggest the wine's potential for graceful evolution. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Feudi Del Pisciotto  Versace Nero d'Avola
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark plum, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly, medium-full red.
description (EN Store): A single-varietal expression of Nero d'Avola from Sicily's renowned subregion. The 2020 vintage delivers dominant dark plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by subtle spice and mineral earth. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet layered, with emerging tobacco and leather notes that suggest modest aging potential. Versatile at table across Mediterranean and grilled preparations.

name: Castello Di Bolgheri  Varvara DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of power and elegance: blackcurrant and plum with cedar, tobacco, and structured tannin.
description (EN Store): Castello Di Bolgheri's Varvara is a classically structured Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (55%), with supporting Merlot (28%), Cabernet Franc (12%), and Petit Verdot (5%). The 2021 vintage expresses ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, layered with cedar and tobacco from oak aging. Medium-high tannins and medium acidity provide a framework suited to extended cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial food. The wine's tertiary notes of leather and slate suggest complexity developing with time.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barbera d'Alba Peiragal DOC (Single Vineyard)
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Barbera from Piedmont's Barolo zone—bright cherry, peppery spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Marchesi di Barolo's single-vineyard Barbera d'Alba Peiragal represents the classic expression of Piedmont's most versatile red grape. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full frame with vibrant acidity and moderate tannin, balancing ripe red fruit with peppery spice and subtle earthy minerality. Barbera's natural brightness and moderate tannin structure make this wine exceptionally food-friendly across a wide range of Italian and Mediterranean cuisines.

name: Rocca Di Frassinello  Le Sughere DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Merlot & Cabernet. Dark fruit, cedar spice & earthy complexity. Food-friendly, age-worthy.
description (EN Store):
Rocca Di Frassinello Le Sughere is a Tuscan Super Tuscan crafted from 50% Sangiovese, 25% Merlot, and 25% Cabernet Sauvignon. The 2019 vintage displays a harmonious blend of primary dark cherry and plum fruit, supported by secondary cedar and spice notes from careful oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerges, reflecting the wine's structure and potential for further development.

Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and moderate tannins, this wine bridges traditional Sangiovese character with international varietals, creating a versatile food companion suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Cristo Di Campobello  Lu Patri DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark plum, spiced earth, and mineral grip—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store):
Cristo Di Campobello Lu Patri DOC is a 100% Nero d'Avola red from Sicily's sun-drenched terroir. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full frame with ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with earthy spice and subtle smoke. The palate is structured yet approachable, with medium tannin and acidity that frame the wine's mineral backbone. Emerging leather and tobacco notes suggest modest aging potential and complexity.

A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and Mediterranean-inflected dishes. Serve at 16–18°C.

name: Cristo Di Campobello  Lusira DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Shiraz with dark plum, spice, and smoky depth. Full-bodied, medium-high tannins.
description (EN Store): Cristo Di Campobello's Lusira DOC is a full-bodied Sicilian Shiraz showcasing the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity and peppery spice. Dominant primary notes of dark plum and blackberry are complemented by secondary smoke and licorice character, with emerging leather and earth complexity. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity support food-friendly structure suitable for grilled meats, braised dishes, and smoky preparations.

name: Edi Simcic  Duet
short_description (EN Store): Slovenian Bordeaux blend of ripe plum, soft berries, and integrated tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Edi Simcic's Duet is a Bordeaux-style red blend from Slovenia's Goriška Brda region, combining Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full body with dominant dark plum and berry fruit character, supported by subtle vanilla and herbaceous notes. Tannins are soft and well-integrated, creating a juicy, approachable palate that balances structure with early drinkability. This wine suits grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, roasted poultry, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Castellare Di Castellina  Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese-Canaiolo blend with ripe cherry, leather, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly Riserva from Chianti Classico.
description (EN Store): Castellare Di Castellina Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG (2020) is a traditional Tuscan red built on Sangiovese and Canaiolo, anchored in the prestigious Chianti Classico zone. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure, offering ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with dried herb and subtle oak from aging. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge mid-palate, while tertiary earthy minerality and spice provide complexity and food-pairing versatility. A classic expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, aged cheeses, and tomato-based Italian cuisine.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barbaresco Serragrilli DOCG (Single Vineyard)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Piedmont's Serragrilli vineyard: red cherry, rose, and tarry complexity with fine tannins.
description (EN Store): Marchesi di Barolo's single-vineyard Barbaresco Serragrilli DOCG showcases the classical elegance of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barolo region. The 2019 vintage expresses the varietal's signature red cherry and rose aromatics, layered with tar, licorice, and spice on the mid-palate. Fine-grained tannins and bracing acidity provide structure and aging potential, while tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the finish, reflecting the wine's complexity and terroir expression.

name: Castello Di Bolgheri  Bolgheri Superiore DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of power and elegance: dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Castello Di Bolgheri Bolgheri Superiore DOC is a Tuscan Bordeaux-style red built on a foundation of Cabernet Sauvignon (85%) with Cabernet Franc (15%) for aromatic lift and finesse. The 2020 vintage expresses the region's signature dark fruit intensity—blackcurrant and dark plum—layered with cedar, black pepper, and herbaceous notes that speak to the Bolgheri terroir. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, while secondary oak influence adds subtle leather and tobacco complexity. This is a wine of balance and food-friendliness, equally at home with grilled steak, braised beef, or aged hard cheeses.

name: Bottega  Amarone della Valpolicella DOC
short_description (EN Store): Amarone della Valpolicella: ripe dark fruit, structured tannins, oak-aged complexity. Premium Veneto red for meat & cheese.
description (EN Store):
Bottega's Amarone della Valpolicella DOC (2020) is a full-bodied Veneto red built on the traditional blend of Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara grapes. The wine displays a rich core of dark cherry and plum fruit, layered with oak-derived spice and vanilla warmth. Medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, while tertiary notes of leather and tobacco emerge on the finish, reflecting the wine's complexity and maturity.

This is a wine for serious red-meat cuisine: grilled steaks, braised beef, and game meats all find natural partners in its fruit-forward primary and structured tannin profile. Aged hard cheeses and rich pâtés complement the secondary oak character. A classic expression of Amarone's power and elegance.

name: Monchiero Carbone  Roero Printi Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo Riserva from Roero with red cherry, tar, and mineral complexity. 2017 vintage.
description (EN Store): Monchiero Carbone's Roero Printi Riserva DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's Roero subregion. The 2017 vintage expresses the varietal's signature red cherry and floral primary character, layered with secondary tar and leather notes from careful oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and slate minerality emerge with bottle age, offering complexity and food-friendly structure. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine approachable in youth while rewarding cellaring.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  "Monfalletto" Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Classic Barolo from Piedmont: elegant Nebbiolo with rose, tar, and structured tannins. Age-worthy and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Cordero Di Montezemolo's Monfalletto Barolo DOCG is a benchmark expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious appellation. The 2021 vintage delivers the varietal's signature elegance: delicate red cherry and rose on the nose, layered with tar, licorice, and spice on the palate. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this wine approachable in youth yet capable of graceful aging. A food-friendly classic suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Terrazas  Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure—classic Argentine elegance.
description (EN Store): Terrazas Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers the hallmark richness of Argentina's premier red-wine region. Blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous undertones and subtle cedar oak. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannin create a wine that is approachable yet structured, with vanilla and black pepper notes adding complexity. The finish shows emerging earth and tobacco character, suggesting modest aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled beef, braised lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Edi Simcic  Duet Lex
short_description (EN Store): Slovenian Bordeaux blend of elegance and restraint—ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Edi Simcic's Duet Lex is a Bordeaux-style red blend from Slovenia's Goriška Brda region, built on a foundation of Merlot (80%) with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc. The 2019 vintage expresses a medium-full body with balanced acidity and approachable tannins, offering ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with subtle herbaceous notes and soft oak influence. The palate is structured yet elegant, with earthy undertones that suggest careful winemaking and regional terroir expression. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Rocca Di Frassinello DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Merlot & Cabernet. Medium-full body, red fruit, spice & cedar.
description (EN Store): Rocca Di Frassinello's 2019 DOC red represents a classic Tuscan approach to international varietals. The blend of 60% Sangiovese with 20% each Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon creates a medium-full-bodied wine with balanced tannin and food-friendly structure. Primary notes of red cherry and dark plum are supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice, while secondary oak aging introduces cedar and subtle vanilla complexity. The wine's medium acidity and integrated tannin make it versatile at table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Nicolis  Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Amarone Classico with dark cherry, plum, and spice; structured tannins and dried-fruit complexity.
description (EN Store): Nicolis Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG 2019 is a full-bodied red wine from Veneto's prestigious Amarone region, crafted from a traditional blend of Corvina, Rondinella, Molinara, and Croatina grapes. The wine displays dominant dark cherry and black plum on the nose, supported by dried-fruit notes and subtle cocoa undertones. On the palate, structured medium-high tannins frame a rich, warming profile with spice and oak complexity, evolving toward tobacco and leather in the finish. This is a wine built for serious food pairing—particularly suited to grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, aged cheeses, and game preparations.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barolo Sarmassa DOCG (Single Vineyard)
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Nebbiolo from Barolo's prestigious Sarmassa cru; elegant red fruit, rose, and mineral complexity with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Marchesi di Barolo's Barolo Sarmassa DOCG is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most celebrated terroirs. The 2019 vintage showcases the varietal's signature elegance: bright red cherry and rose on the nose, layered with tar and licorice notes from the wine's natural phenolic richness. The palate is structured and mineral-driven, with medium-high tannins and acidity that promise graceful evolution over the next 15–20 years. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, reflecting both the vineyard's character and the wine's aging potential. A food-friendly yet serious Barolo suited to special occasions and cellar investment.

name: Nicolis  Ambrosan Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Amarone from Veneto's Valpolicella Classico zone: dark cherry, plum, and tobacco complexity with firm tannins.
description (EN Store): Nicolis Ambrosan Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOC 2015 is a full-bodied red wine from Italy's prestigious Veneto region, crafted from a traditional blend of Corvina (70%), Rondinella (20%), and Croatina (10%). The wine displays dominant dark cherry and black plum on the nose, supported by cocoa and oak from careful aging. On the palate, firm tannins and medium acidity frame layers of dried fruit, tobacco, and earthy minerality that develop with air. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, and aged cheeses. The tertiary complexity of leather and tobacco suggests cellaring potential and pairs beautifully with game and rich terrines.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Barolo Enrico VI DOCG (Single Vineyard)
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Barolo of elegance and structure: dark fruit, rose florality, and mineral complexity from 100% Nebbiolo.
description (EN Store):
Cordero Di Montezemolo's Barolo Enrico VI represents the pinnacle of single-vineyard Piedmontese winemaking. Crafted from 100% Nebbiolo, this wine displays the classic architecture of great Barolo: a full-bodied frame supported by medium-high tannins and vibrant acidity.

On the nose, dark plum and black cherry dominate, layered with floral rose petal notes and hints of tar and licorice. The palate reveals leather, tobacco, and cedar complexity, grounded by slate minerality and earthy undertones. This is a wine built for cellaring and food, with the structure and elegance to age gracefully while complementing the finest red meats and game.

name: Edi Simcic  Kolos
short_description (EN Store): Slovenian Bordeaux blend of ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Edi Simcic's Kolos is a Bordeaux-style red blend from Slovenia's Goriška Brda region, built on Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The 2015 vintage displays a medium-full body with dominant dark plum and berry character on the nose, supported by hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes. On the palate, the wine delivers a juicy, well-integrated texture with soft tannins and a clean finish. The secondary vanilla undertones add roundness without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile, making this a versatile food wine suitable for grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty pasta preparations.

name: Rocca Di Frassinello  Baffonero DOC
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Tuscany: ripe plum, soft tannins, and balanced oak. Food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Rocca Di Frassinello's Baffonero is a pure expression of Merlot from Tuscany's DOC region. The 2018 vintage delivers ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, with a medium-full body and supple tannin structure that makes it immediately approachable. Oak aging contributes subtle vanilla and spice notes, while the wine's balanced acidity and earthy undertones reflect its Tuscan terroir. A versatile red suited to casual dining and everyday occasions.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic Curicó Valley expression.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with full body and commanding tannin structure. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Black pepper spice and subtle tobacco complexity emerge on the palate, providing depth and aging potential. The wine's medium-high acidity and firm tannins frame a long, satisfying finish suited to substantial dishes.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Carmenère with dark plum, black cherry, and signature green pepper notes. Medium-full body, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store):
Echeverria's Reserva Carmenère from Curicó Valley showcases the varietal's hallmark character: ripe dark plum and black cherry fruit anchored by the distinctive green bell pepper note that defines Carmenère's identity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure support a palate that bridges primary fruit with subtle oak and spice complexity, finishing with earthy undertones.

A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and herb-forward preparations. The green pepper secondary note pairs naturally with roasted vegetables and Mediterranean-influenced dishes.

name: Echeverria  Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Curicó Valley Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a confident expression of the varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the nose, supported by cedar and subtle black pepper. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannins frame layers of dark plum and vanilla oak, with emerging tobacco and leather notes that suggest modest aging potential. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Echeverria  Gran Reserva Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured for everyday red-wine enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Gran Reserva Merlot from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by dominant plum and berry-fruit character. The palate shows juicy red-fruit medley with cranberry notes and subtle herbaceous undertones, supported by integrated tannins and a clean finish. Vanilla accents from winemaking add softness and approachability, making this wine a versatile choice for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Chateau Labarthe Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc with ripe plum, cherry, and subtle cocoa—soft tannins, approachable structure.
description (EN Store): Château Labarthe presents a classic Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot with Cabernet Franc support. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry aromatics, layered with supporting berry complexity and hints of red fruit. On the palate, cocoa and vanilla notes emerge from careful winemaking, complemented by subtle herbaceous undertones that speak to the Cabernet Franc component. Medium-Full body and integrated medium tannins deliver a soft, approachable texture that rewards both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Chateau Ducru-Beaucaillou  Le Petit Caillou, St Julien
short_description (EN Store): Second-label from Saint-Julien's 'Super Second,' grown on 6–8m Günz gravel beds and aged in French oak — precision Bordeaux at entry price.
description (EN Store):
When Bruno Borie slashed Grand Vin production from over 20,000 to just 6,000–8,000 cases annually — selecting only 27% of vineyard output — the displaced fruit had to go somewhere. Le Petit Caillou is that fruit: declassified from the same 75-hectare Saint-Julien estate whose gravel beds are among the deepest in the Médoc.

Those beds — 6–8 metres of Günz-era quartz pebbles, lydian jasper, flint, and agatoids deposited by the ancient Garonne two million years ago over Oligocene mud and Eocene limestone — drain aggressively, stress the vine roots downward, and concentrate flavour. The Gironde estuary's four daily tidal movements buffer frost and summer heat, preserving the linear freshness that defines Saint-Julien's house style.

Vinification follows the Grand Vin protocol: manual harvest with multiple sorting passes, parcel-by-parcel fermentation in variable-volume stainless steel tanks, and malolactic fermentation completed in concrete or stainless vats. The second-label oak regime is lighter than the Grand Vin's 100% new French barriques, allowing the Günz gravel's graphite and pencil-lead character to speak more directly.

What is unusual here: this is second-label fruit subject to the same obsessive terroir selectivity as one of Bordeaux's most demanding Grand Vin programmes — a level of raw-material quality rarely found at this price point in the appellation.

The 2017 vintage delivered a cool, even growing season with good freshness and moderate concentration; the wine is approachable now but carries the structural backbone of its Saint-Julien origins.

name: S De Siran  2nd label of Chateau Siran Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Margaux second label: dark fruit, cedar spice, and refined tannin structure. Food-friendly Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): S de Siran is the second label of Château Siran, a Margaux estate known for balanced, age-worthy Bordeaux blends. This 2019 vintage combines 46% Merlot, 44% Cabernet Sauvignon, 9% Petit Verdot, and 1% Cabernet Franc into a medium-full-bodied wine with elegant dark plum and blackcurrant fruit. Cedar and spice from careful oak aging provide secondary complexity, while emerging tertiary notes of tobacco and leather suggest graceful evolution. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable now yet capable of cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Torres  Ibericos Crianza La Rioja DOC
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja Tempranillo with ripe plum, cherry, and oak—a structured, food-friendly crianza with long finish.
description (EN Store): Torres Ibéricos Crianza is a classically styled Rioja built on Tempranillo's dark fruit foundation. The wine displays a deep red hue with aromas of plum and cherry, supported by vanilla oak and subtle spice from careful aging. Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and moderate tannin, it offers a dry, structured palate with good length. This is a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses—a reliable expression of Rioja tradition at an accessible price point.

name: Chateau Tournefeuille  Lalande-de-Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Lalande-de-Pomerol blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc with ripe plum, cherry, and subtle chocolate—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Château Tournefeuille's Lalande-de-Pomerol showcases the classic marriage of Merlot and Cabernet Franc from this prestigious Right Bank appellation. The wine opens with ripe plum and dark cherry on the nose, complemented by hints of vanilla and herbaceous nuance. On the palate, a juicy berry medley unfolds with cranberry notes and integrated tannins that deliver a soft, elegant finish. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this a versatile expression suitable for both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Casanova di Neri  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Seven-vineyard Brunello from Montalcino's galestro heartland — spontaneous fermentation, gravity-fed cellar, proprietary Cerretalto clone across all sites.
description (EN Store): Giovanni Neri founded this estate in 1971, but the defining act came decades later: selecting a proprietary Sangiovese Grosso clone from the iron- and magnesium-rich galestro of Cerretalto and propagating it across all seven vineyards — a deliberate move to build coherent house DNA while letting radically different soils speak. The White Label Brunello draws from that full seven-vineyard mosaic, spanning north-east Montalcino's cooler galestro marl sites (Fiesole at 350–380 m, Podernuovo at 450–480 m) down to the warmer southern slopes near Castelnuovo dell'Abate, where Tenuta Nuova first planted Sangiovese in that zone. Fermentation is spontaneous — no added yeasts — in open-topped truncated conical vats of stainless steel and wood, with punch-downs over approximately 23–24 days. The three-story hillside cellar operates entirely by gravity; no pumps touch the wine. Aging runs in large botte and tonneaux for approximately 43 months total in oak. What sets this expression apart: in the 2020 vintage, Cerretalto fruit was folded directly into this cuvée when Cerretalto was not produced as a standalone — a rare window into that amphitheatre's iron-laden galestro at the White Label price. The 2019 growing season delivered exceptional concentration and aromatic generosity, producing a wine more approachable in youth than the austere 2016, with a drinking window extending to 2050.

name: Casanova di Neri  Irrosso Toscana Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-dominant IGT from Montalcino's galestro hillsides — the entry point into Casanova di Neri's estate-clone DNA, built for early pleasure.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1971 by Giovanni Neri, Casanova di Neri built its reputation on a single obsessive decision: propagating a proprietary Sangiovese Grosso clone selected from the Cerretalto vineyard and planting it across all seven estate sites. IrROSSO is where that clone DNA appears in its most accessible form — a Toscana IGT blending Sangiovese with small additions of other estate varieties, bypassing the DOCG aging minimums entirely to deliver the fruit in its most immediate state.

The fruit draws from galestro-dominant soils across multiple Montalcino sites — the same iron- and magnesium-rich marl that underpins the estate's grand crus — at elevations ranging from 250 m to over 450 m a.s.l. Galestro's low water retention forces deep root development, concentrating aromatics while preserving the natural acidity that keeps the wine taut rather than heavy.

Winemaking follows the full estate protocol: manual bunch selection, individual berry sorting via optical sorter, spontaneous fermentation with no added yeasts in open-topped truncated conical vats, and gravity-fed transfers throughout — no pumps at any stage. The IGT classification is the deliberate transgression here: Casanova di Neri uses it not to escape quality, but to escape the mandatory five-year Brunello release clock, bottling the wine while its dark fruit is still vivid and the tannins are pliant.

The 2021 Tuscan growing season delivered even ripening and good aromatic definition. Drink now through 2027.

name: Casanova di Neri  Rosso di Montalcino DOC
short_description (EN Store): Casanova di Neri's Rosso draws from galestro-rich Montalcino sites, aged 12 months in wood from the estate that put single-vineyard Brunello on the global map.
description (EN Store): Giovanni Neri founded this estate in 1971, and it was his son Giacomo who made the deliberate choice to propagate a proprietary Sangiovese Grosso clone bred from the Cerretalto vineyard across all seven estate sites — meaning even the Rosso di Montalcino carries the same genetic backbone as the wine that earned Italy's first perfect 100-point score from Wine Spectator. The Rosso draws from multiple Montalcino sites spanning galestro marl at 270–480 m a.s.l., soils rich in iron, magnesium, and eroded rock that compress the berry and sharpen the fruit's definition without sacrificing flesh. In the cellar, manual bunch selection, optical berry sorting, and spontaneous fermentation in open-topped truncated conical vats proceed without added yeasts; punch-downs at controlled temperature extract colour and structure without brutality. Twelve months in wood — shorter than any Brunello tier — preserves the primary fruit energy that the longer-aged cuvées deliberately sacrifice for complexity. The 2020 vintage is notable for a specific reason: Cerretalto was not produced, and its fruit was redirected into the White Label Brunello, concentrating the estate's top-site material upward in the hierarchy and leaving the Rosso to express the broader multi-vineyard blend with unusual purity. Drink 2023–2028.

name: Casanova di Neri  Brunello di Montalcino Cerretalto DOCG 2016
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Cerretalto: galestro-iron-magnesium soils in a natural amphitheatre, fermented in oak, aged 30 months in tonneaux (20% new) — the cru that earned Brunello its first-ever 100-point score.
description (EN Store): In 2007, Wine Spectator awarded the 2001 Cerretalto a perfect 100 points — the first Italian wine ever to receive that score — anchoring the reputation of a single plot that Giacomo Neri had long treated as the estate's most demanding and most revealing site. The Cerretalto vineyard sits at 270–300 m a.s.l. in a natural amphitheatre along the Asso torrent in Torrenieri, east-facing, with galestro marl laced with alluvial stone, eroded rock, and deposits rich in iron, magnesium, and plinthites. That mineral-dense subsoil, combined with the widest diurnal temperature range of any Casanova di Neri site, drives phenolic precision and structural tension rather than sheer volume. White truffles grow on-site — a marker of the soil's complexity. Fermentation is spontaneous, in oak, with no added yeasts; maceration runs approximately 23–24 days in open-topped conical vats via a fully gravity-fed, pump-free cellar. Aging: 30 months in tonneaux at 20% new oak, followed by 30 months in bottle — Cerretalto is released a full year after its stablemates. The 100% Sangiovese Grosso planted here is the estate's own proprietary clone, bred from this very vineyard and propagated across all seven sites. The 2016 vintage is widely regarded as one of Montalcino's finest modern harvests, delivering concentration and structural precision in equal measure; drinking window extends comfortably to 2045+.

name: Conti Zecca  Donna Marzia Primitivo IGP Salento Primitivo Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Bold Salento Primitivo with dark fruit, smoke, and pepper—smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Conti Zecca's Donna Marzia Primitivo from Salento showcases the warmth and generosity of southern Italian winemaking. A blend of 85% Primitivo with complementary regional varieties, this wine delivers a bold nose followed by a palate rich in dark cherry, raspberry, and dark fruit, accented by subtle smoke and black pepper. Fully rounded tannins and a smooth finish make it an accessible yet satisfying red—equally at home with pizza and pasta as with grilled meats and hearty braises.

name: Conti Zecca  Primitivo Zinfandel Rifugio IGT
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Conti Zecca's Primitivo Zinfandel Rifugio IGT is a bold, full-bodied red from southern Italy's sun-drenched Puglia region. The wine opens with a generous dark fruit character—ripe black cherry and raspberry—complemented by subtle smoke and black pepper notes that add complexity without overwhelming the palate. Fully rounded tannins and smooth texture make this an exceptionally approachable expression of Primitivo, delivering richness and depth at an accessible price point. A versatile table wine equally at home with pizza, pasta, grilled meats, or casual entertaining.

name: Corte Rugolin  Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Ripasso with dark cherry, almond, and herbaceous character. Medium-bodied, food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Corte Rugolin's Valpolicella Classico Superiore Ripasso DOC (2020) exemplifies the traditional Veneto style: a medium-bodied red built on Corvina, Corvinone, and Rondinella with supporting local varieties. The wine opens with dark cherry and black plum, supported by subtle almond and herbaceous notes from the blend's complexity. Mid-palate shows balanced tannin and acidity, with dried fruit and spice emerging in the finish. The ripasso technique—partial re-fermentation on dried grape skins—adds depth and structure without overwhelming the wine's elegant restraint. A versatile food wine suited to braised meats, tomato-based pasta, and aged cheeses.

name: Corte Rugolin  Amarone Valpolicella Classico "Crosara de le Strie" DOC
short_description (EN Store): Amarone Classico from Veneto: concentrated dark fruit, cocoa, and tobacco complexity. Structured, age-worthy red for serious collectors.
description (EN Store): Corte Rugolin's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico exemplifies the appassimento tradition of northeastern Italy. This 2016 vintage blends Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, and local varieties into a wine of concentrated dark plum and black cherry, layered with cocoa, almond, and warming spice on the mid-palate. Secondary oak integration is measured and refined. Tertiary notes of tobacco, leather, and cedar emerge with bottle age, signaling a wine built for the cellar. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity support food-friendly structure despite the wine's richness.

name: Feudo Maccari  Saia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark plum, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly red for Mediterranean tables.
description (EN Store): Feudo Maccari's Saia is a 100% Nero d'Avola from Sicily's warm volcanic terroir. The 2021 vintage delivers ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by subtle spice and mineral earth. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured, with tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerging on the palate. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Feudo Maccari  Maharis IGT
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Sicilian blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Nero d'Avola & Syrah with dark fruit, cedar & structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Feudo Maccari's Maharis IGT is a structured Sicilian red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon, Nero d'Avola, and Syrah. The 2019 vintage delivers a full-bodied profile with prominent blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar oak and black pepper spice. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide backbone and aging potential, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes add complexity. A serious food wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and game.

name: Massolino  Dolcetto D’Alba DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Dolcetto with ripe cherry, plum, and mineral-driven spice. Medium-full body, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Massolino's Dolcetto d'Alba DOC is a classically structured red from Piedmont's Barolo subregion, showcasing the varietal's signature balance of ripe red fruit and earthy minerality. The 2021 vintage displays dominant cherry and plum on the nose, supported by subtle spice and dried herb notes. Medium-full body and moderate tannin make this wine approachable in youth while retaining enough structure for modest cellaring. The finish carries mineral precision and hints of leather, characteristic of quality Dolcetto from this terroir.

name: Massolino  Nebbiolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo with ripe cherry, licorice, and cedar—age-worthy with firm tannins and high acidity.
description (EN Store): Massolino's 2020 Nebbiolo DOC from Barolo showcases the classic elegance and structure of Piedmont's most prestigious red varietal. The wine opens with dominant cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the region's terroir. Full-bodied with high acidity and firm tannins, this wine demands proper food pairing and rewards cellaring. Tertiary complexity—tar, leather, and dried fruit—emerges with age, making it an excellent investment for serious collectors.

name: Massolino  Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's heartland: ripe cherry, licorice, and cedar with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Massolino's 2020 Barolo DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious region. The wine opens with dominant black cherry and licorice aromatics, supported by herbaceous undertones characteristic of the variety. Cedar and tar emerge on the mid-palate, framed by the wine's signature high acidity and firm tannin architecture. With bottle age, tertiary notes of tobacco and leather develop, adding complexity and savory depth. This is a wine built for the cellar—a benchmark expression of Barolo's power and elegance.

name: Orma by Tenuta Sette Ponti  Bolgheri IGT
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Super Tuscan blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Franc—dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannin.
description (EN Store): Orma by Tenuta Sette Ponti is a Bolgheri IGT red wine crafted from a Bordeaux-inspired blend: 50% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, and 20% Cabernet Franc. This medium-full-bodied wine delivers ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, with supporting cedar and spice from careful oak aging. The palate shows structured tannins and a dry finish with hints of tobacco and leather—hallmarks of Tuscan Super Tuscan style. Ideal for grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Pietradolce  Archineri Etna Rosso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Etna Rosso from pure Nerello Mascalese: bright cherry, volcanic minerality, silky tannins.
description (EN Store): Pietradolce's Archineri Etna Rosso DOC is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from Sicily's volcanic slopes. The 2019 vintage delivers a refined profile: bright red cherry and stone fruit on the nose, underpinned by distinctive volcanic minerality and white pepper spice. The palate is medium-full bodied with supple, food-friendly tannins and vibrant acidity that lifts the wine's earthy undertones. A classic representation of Etna's terroir—elegant, mineral-driven, and versatile at table.

name: Podere Sapaio  Sapaio Toscana Rosso DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of power and elegance—dark fruit, structured tannins, and savory complexity.
description (EN Store): Podere Sapaio's Sapaio Toscana Rosso DOCG is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (70%), with Petit Verdot (20%) and Cabernet Franc (10%) adding depth and aromatic complexity. The 2017 vintage expresses the ripe, structured character typical of Montalcino reds: dominant dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, supported by cedar and black pepper spice. Mid-palate tannins are firm and well-integrated, with a savory tobacco and leather finish that suggests age-worthiness. A food-friendly yet serious red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Tedeschi  Amarone Della Valpolicella Classico Riserva Capitel Monte Olmi
short_description (EN Store): Riserva-aged Amarone from Tedeschi: dark cherry, plum, and spice with structured tannins. Premium Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Tedeschi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico Riserva Capitel Monte Olmi is a full-bodied, age-worthy expression of Veneto's most prestigious appellation. The 2018 vintage showcases the signature dark cherry and plum character of the Corvina-led blend, layered with cocoa, spice, and subtle oak influence from extended aging. Medium acidity and medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the palate, reflecting the wine's complexity and cellaring potential. This is a wine built for the table—ideal with grilled red meat, braised beef, aged cheeses, and game preparations.

name: Tenuta Sette Ponti  Crognolo
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Tuscan blend with ripe cherry, spice, and mineral depth—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Sette Ponti's Crognolo is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 90% Sangiovese with 10% Merlot. The 2022 vintage expresses the primary character of ripe red cherry and plum, layered with herbaceous notes typical of Chianti terroir. Secondary spice and black pepper emerge from careful winemaking, while tertiary earth, tobacco, and mineral complexity suggest a wine built for both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it a versatile table wine.

name: Tenuta Sette Ponti  Oreno
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Petit Verdot with dark fruit, cedar & spice.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Sette Ponti's Oreno is a classically structured Super Tuscan blend combining 50% Merlot with 40% Cabernet Sauvignon and 10% Petit Verdot. The 2021 vintage expresses dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, supported by cedar and subtle spice from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins frame a palate with layers of black cherry, tobacco, and mineral complexity. This is a food-friendly Tuscan red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenuta Sette Ponti  Chianti Vigna di Pallino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Petit Verdot—structured, food-friendly red with dark fruit and cedar.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Sette Ponti's Chianti Vigna di Pallino DOCG is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Merlot (50%), Cabernet Sauvignon (40%), and Petit Verdot (10%), crafted in Tuscany's prestigious Chianti region. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and structured tannins, offering dark cherry, plum, and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with cedar and subtle spice. Earthy undertones and herbaceous notes add complexity, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Cantina Terlano  Monticol Riserva DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir from Alto Adige's Terlano zone; ripe red fruit, mineral poise, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Cantina Terlano's Monticol Riserva is a single-varietal Pinot Noir from the prestigious Terlano subregion of Trentino-Alto Adige, Italy's cool alpine terroir. The 2021 vintage expresses the variety's hallmark elegance: ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, with subtle oak integration and a mineral undertone that reflects the region's limestone-rich soils. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or light meat preparations. A refined expression of northern Italian Pinot Noir craftsmanship.

name: Zenato  Amarone Riserva Sergio DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Amarone Riserva with dark fruit, oak, and earthy complexity. Premium Veneto expression.
description (EN Store): Zenato Amarone Riserva Sergio DOCG (2018) is a prestigious expression from Veneto's Amarone della Valpolicella region, crafted from 80% Corvina with supporting Rondinella, Oseleta, and Croatina. This full-bodied red displays dominant dark cherry and black plum on the nose, complemented by cocoa and oak from careful aging. The palate reveals layered complexity: ripe dark fruit and spice in the primary tier, vanilla and toasted oak in the secondary, and emerging tobacco and leather notes in the tertiary. Medium-high tannins provide structure without harshness, while balanced acidity ensures food-friendliness across rich meat dishes, aged cheeses, and game preparations.

name: Zenato  Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Amarone with dark cherry, cocoa, and tobacco complexity. Elegant structure for premium red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store):
Zenato Amarone della Valpolicella Classico 2018 is a full-bodied red wine from Veneto's prestigious Amarone region, crafted from 80% Corvina with supporting Rondinella, Oseleta, and Croatina. The wine displays dominant dark cherry and blackberry fruit with supporting cocoa and spice notes from careful oak aging, evolving toward tobacco and leather complexity on the finish.

This is a wine of substance and elegance—structured for serious food pairing and cellar aging. The medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide framework for rich, savory dishes and aged cheeses.

name: Zenato  Ripassa Valpolicella Superiore DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with ripe dark fruit, subtle oak, and food-friendly structure. Excellent value.
description (EN Store): Zenato's Ripassa Valpolicella Superiore is a classically styled red from Veneto's renowned Valpolicella Ripasso zone. Blended from 80% Corvina, 10% Rondinella, and 10% Oseleta, this wine displays the hallmark character of the region: ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, with a palate that balances fruit-forward appeal against measured oak influence and fine-grained tannin. The medium-full body and moderate acidity make it a versatile companion to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based dishes. A benchmark example of the Ripasso style—approachable yet structured.

name: Fontodi  Flaccianello della Pieve
short_description (EN Store): Pure Sangiovese from Chianti Classico with elegant red fruit, violet florals, and mineral-driven structure.
description (EN Store): Fontodi Flaccianello della Pieve is a single-varietal Sangiovese expression from Chianti Classico, Tuscany. The 2020 vintage showcases the classic profile of this noble Italian grape: bright red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with violet florals and herbaceous notes. The palate reveals medium-full body with medium-high tannins and acidity, delivering a food-friendly structure that balances ripe fruit with mineral precision and subtle spice. Earthy undertones and emerging leather complexity suggest aging potential, positioning this wine as both an approachable current release and a candidate for medium-term cellaring.

name: Fontodi  Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti Classico: bright red fruit, herbaceous spice, and mineral backbone. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Fontodi's Chianti Classico DOCG is a benchmark expression of Sangiovese from Tuscany's most prestigious subregion. The 2021 vintage delivers a vibrant core of red cherry and plum, framed by herbaceous notes and subtle spice that reflect the varietal's classic profile. Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide structure without heaviness, while bright acidity ensures versatility at the table. Mineral undertones and hints of dried fruit complexity suggest modest aging potential, though the wine is approachable in its youth.

name: Framingham  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—elegant summer drinking.
description (EN Store): Framingham's 2018 Pinot Noir from Marlborough's Wairau Valley delivers the region's signature elegance: a light-bodied red with vibrant red fruit and raspberry character, underpinned by earthy complexity and a whisper of spice. High acidity and silky tannins create a refreshing, food-friendly profile that evolves gracefully on the palate. A versatile choice for warm-weather entertaining and lighter cuisine.

name: Salisbury 1848  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Murray Darling Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Salisbury's 1848 Cabernet Sauvignon from Murray Darling delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the core, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice from winemaking. Structured tannins and medium-high acidity provide backbone for cellaring potential. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest complexity developing with age.

name: Altivo  Vineyard Selection Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa. Medium-full body, smooth tannins, French oak aging.
description (EN Store): Altivo Vineyard Selection Malbec from Mendoza showcases the signature depth and elegance of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Aromas of blackberry, plum, and dark fruit lead to a palate marked by cocoa, violet, and subtle spice notes. Medium-full body with well-integrated, smooth tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable yet structured. French oak aging contributes vanilla and toast complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. A versatile expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Altivo  Barrel Selection Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with dark plum, cocoa, and leather—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Altivo's Barrel Selection Malbec from Mendoza showcases the signature depth and structure of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cocoa and warming spice from barrel aging. The medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet complex, with emerging leather and tobacco notes that suggest modest cellaring potential. A versatile choice for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes.

name: Altivo  Secret Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body, smooth tannins, French oak aged.
description (EN Store): Altivo's Secret Malbec from Mendoza showcases the region's signature dark fruit character: dominant blackberry and plum on the nose, supported by cocoa and vanilla from 15 months in French oak. Medium-full bodied with round, smooth tannins and mineral undertones, this wine balances fruit-forward appeal with structural elegance. Earth and subtle spice notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity to an approachable yet age-worthy expression.

name: Carta Vieja  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Loncomilla Valley Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Carta Vieja's Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Loncomilla Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit on the nose, supported by cedar and black pepper from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured enough for serious food pairing. Subtle herbaceous and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and age-worthiness.

name: Carta Vieja  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for weeknight red-meat dinners.
description (EN Store): Carta Vieja's Merlot from Chile's Loncomilla Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine with a juicy, fruit-forward character. Ripe plum and berry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle vanilla and herbaceous undertones. The tannins are well-integrated and soft, creating a smooth, approachable finish that suits casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Vina El Principal  Calicanto
short_description (EN Store): Alto Maipo Cabernet-led blend with Carménère spice and Syrah depth—structured, food-friendly Chilean red.
description (EN Store):
Viña El Principal's Calicanto is a Cabernet Sauvignon–dominant blend from Alto Maipo, Chile's premier high-altitude red wine subregion. The composition—57% Cabernet Sauvignon anchored by Carménère (18%), Syrah (9%), Petit Verdot (9%), and Cabernet Franc (7%)—builds a structured, medium-full-bodied wine with dark fruit intensity and layered complexity.

On the palate, blackcurrant and dark plum lead, supported by black pepper spice and cedar notes from oak aging. The wine develops leather and tobacco undertones with air, suggesting modest tertiary development. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Vina El Principal  Memorias
short_description (EN Store): Alto Maipo Cabernet-led blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins—elegant Chilean red for the table.
description (EN Store): Vina El Principal's Memorias is a structured Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (73%), with supporting Petit Verdot, Syrah, Carménère, and Cabernet Franc. Alto Maipo's cool-climate terroir yields ripe dark plum and blackcurrant fruit balanced by mineral acidity and medium-high tannins. Cedar and black pepper notes from the blend's complexity suggest careful oak aging, while subtle tobacco and leather undertones hint at mid-term cellaring potential. A food-friendly, elegant expression suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Vina El Principal  El Principal
short_description (EN Store): Alto Maipo Bordeaux blend: dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Food-friendly structure for grilled meats and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): A structured Bordeaux-style blend from Chile's Alto Maipo subregion, anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (45%) with supporting Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, and Syrah. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum, layered with cedar and black pepper from oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with air, suggesting modest bottle age potential. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a food-friendly profile suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-crusted lamb.

name: Chateau Fontenil  Fronsac
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Fronsac Merlot with ripe plum, soft berry, and integrated tannins—a refined, approachable Bordeaux.
