description (EN Store): Château Fontenil's 2016 Fronsac Merlot delivers a harmonious expression of the Right Bank's signature varietal. On the nose, ripe plum and dark berry fruit dominate, with subtle hints of vanilla and cocoa from careful oak aging. The palate is juicy and medium-full, with well-integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. A whisper of herbaceous character and earthy undertones add complexity, while the finish remains smooth and lingering. This wine exemplifies the approachable elegance of Fronsac—neither austere nor overly extracted—making it an ideal companion for grilled meats, braised beef, and rustic comfort dishes.

name: Chateau Pey la Tour  Reserve Bordeaux Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Bordeaux Supérieur with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy structure for serious red meat.
description (EN Store): Château Pey la Tour Reserve Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend from the Right Bank's prestigious appellation. The wine displays a full body with firm, high tannins and medium-high acidity, anchored by dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, supported by subtle spice, while tertiary leather and earth complexity suggest age-worthiness. This is a serious, food-friendly red suited to extended cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial dishes.

name: Chateau Timberlay  Prestige Red
short_description (EN Store): Balanced Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Chateau Timberlay's Prestige Red is a classic 50/50 Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon blend from Bordeaux, crafted in the traditional style. The wine displays a medium-full body with approachable tannins and a harmonious balance of ripe dark fruit, subtle oak influence, and earthy undertones. Designed for immediate enjoyment and food pairing versatility, this expression reflects the house's commitment to accessible, well-structured Bordeaux.

name: Chateau Tourtirac  Castillon Cotes de Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux red blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Tourtirac's Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux represents the classic Right Bank Bordeaux style—a balanced blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with ripe plum and black cherry, supported by subtle cedar and tobacco leaf notes from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and approachable tannins make this a versatile table wine, equally at home with grilled meats, braised beef, or herb-forward Mediterranean dishes. The earthy minerality on the finish reflects the region's clay-limestone terroir.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Classic Médoc blend of power and elegance—blackcurrant and cedar with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): A traditional Bordeaux expression from the Médoc appellation, this Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend showcases the region's hallmark balance of ripe dark fruit and structural complexity. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedar and tobacco notes that speak to careful oak aging. The wine's full body and high tannin profile promise evolution in the cellar, developing tertiary earth and graphite character over time. A food-friendly yet serious red suited to special occasions and classic French cuisine.

name: Chateau Belgrave  Haut-Medoc Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Left Bank Bordeaux classic: blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with structured tannins. Age-worthy 2016 from Haut-Médoc.
description (EN Store): Château Belgrave's 2016 Haut-Médoc Grand Cru is a classically structured Left Bank Bordeaux built on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and tobacco complexity. High, well-integrated tannins provide backbone and aging potential, while secondary oak and spice notes add refinement. Tertiary leather and graphite minerality emerge on the palate, suggesting this wine will develop further over the next decade. A serious, traditional expression of Médoc terroir.

name: Chateau Le Boscq  Cru Bourgeois - AOC Saint-Estèphe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe Cru Bourgeois: blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Elegant Bordeaux blend for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Château Le Boscq represents the traditional character of Saint-Estèphe, a Left Bank appellation renowned for structured, age-worthy reds. This Cru Bourgeois blend—55% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot, 5% Petit Verdot—delivers a medium-full frame with ripe blackcurrant and plum fruit, complemented by cedar and oak complexity. The palate shows balanced acidity and medium tannins that suggest food pairing versatility and modest cellaring potential. name: Barton&Guestier  Saint-Emilion

name: Cakebread  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cab from a food-first house: 50+ years, 4 winemakers, night-harvested fruit built for the table, not the trophy shelf.
description (EN Store): Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded this Rutherford estate in 1973 with a culinary philosophy so literal they installed a resident chef and launched the American Harvest Workshop by 1985 — embedding table compatibility as a winemaking parameter, not an afterthought. That ethos has been held by only four winemakers across five decades, an extraordinary continuity at 200,000-case scale. The 2019 growing season in Napa Valley was cool and extended, producing layered, structured reds with natural freshness rather than heat-driven concentration. Fruit is drawn from estate sites spanning 11 Napa sub-appellations, including the rocky, well-drained hillside soils of Suscol Springs Ranch and the original Winery Ranch in Rutherford, where proximity to the Napa River and Mayacamas Mountains moderates diurnal swings and preserves acidity. All fruit is hand-harvested at night — a practice in place since 2004 — then passed through an optical berry sorter, hand-sorted, destemmed, and given a pre-fermentation cold soak to soften skins and calibrate tannin texture before fermentation. The 2021 vintage tech sheet (the closest confirmed reference) documents 17 months in French oak, 48% new for the Napa Valley Cabernet; the 2019 follows the same house regime. What separates Cakebread from extracted Napa peers is deliberate restraint: pump-over selection is used as a precision tannin-management tool, and the blend — Cabernet Sauvignon with Merlot, Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc, Malbec, and Syrah — is calibrated for structure that serves food rather than scores. The 2019's cool-season character delivers drinking pleasure now with genuine cellaring potential through the late 2020s.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2018
short_description (EN Store): Fifth Growth Pauillac that trades at Second Growth prices — Bages plateau gravel, 75% new French oak, Winegrid-monitored fermentation, 2018's heat fully realized.
description (EN Store):
In 1985, a half-bottle of Lynch-Bages 1975 flew aboard NASA's Discovery shuttle — a footnote that captures the estate's outsized ambition relative to its official Fifth Growth rank. That classification, frozen in 1855, has never reflected market reality: Liv-Ex consistently places Lynch-Bages among Bordeaux's Super Seconds, a status earned through Jean-Michel Cazes's 15-year modernisation programme from 1975 and sustained by fourth-generation Jean-Charles Cazes IV since 2006.

The Bages plateau, south-southwest of Pauillac town, sits on deep Garonne gravel over limestone sub-strata — sediment carried by millennia of Pyrenean erosion. Free-draining and heat-retentive, this geology amplifies the 2018 growing season's hot, dry conditions into full phenolic ripeness, producing concentrated blackcurrant and dark berry with cushioned, generous tannins rather than the austere architecture of plateau neighbours Latour or Pichon-Baron.

Vinification proceeds parcel-by-parcel across 80 temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats, with malolactic fermentation split: one-third in vat, two-thirds in barrel. Aging runs 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new oak. Lynch-Bages was the first Bordeaux estate to deploy the Winegrid in-barrel monitoring system (from 2015), tracking alcohol, sugar, colour, and temperature in real time — a precision tool that underpins the house's signature ripe-yet-structured consistency. The 2018 is best approached from 2026, with a long runway beyond.

description (EN Store):
Château Certan de May is a distinguished Right Bank Bordeaux estate producing refined Pomerol wines. This 2018 vintage showcases the classic blend of 70% Merlot, 25% Cabernet Franc, and 5% Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a harmonious expression of the appellation's terroir.

name: Tenuta dell'Ornellaia  Le Volte dell’Ornellaia
short_description (EN Store): Coastal Bolgheri second-label from Ornellaia's 99 ha estate — Merlot-forward blend, Tyrrhenian-moderated clay and alluvial soils, 2023 vintage.
description (EN Store):
Le Volte dell'Ornellaia is the entry point into a portfolio conceived in direct rivalry with Sassicaia and Solaia — founder Marchese Lodovico Antinori planted his 99 ha Bolgheri estate from 1981 precisely to challenge his own family's Super Tuscan dominance. That competitive DNA runs through every tier of production, including this blend.

The fruit draws from two adjacent blocks: the original 41 ha estate surrounding the winery, where denser clay soils push concentration and structure, and the 58 ha Bellaria property to the north, where stonier, more alluvial ground delivers fresher, more vibrant character. Elevations of 50–120 m asl and persistent Tyrrhenian Sea breezes moderate summer heat, preserving acidity and coastal salinity in the finished wine.

Vinification follows the estate's block-by-block protocol, with each variety fermented separately in stainless steel and concrete. Malolactic fermentation takes place in French oak barriques, integrating texture from the outset rather than as an afterthought. The blend — typically Merlot-led at this tier, supported by Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc — is assembled to reflect the vintage's character before bottling.

What sets Le Volte apart within its price tier is direct access to the same composite soils — marine, alluvial, volcanic, clay, marl, and calcareous rock — that underpin the flagship Bolgheri Superiore, without the 18-month barrique regime. The 2023 vintage, shaped by the same Tyrrhenian microclimate, is expected to deliver the estate's characteristic Mediterranean generosity with early approachability.

description (EN Store):
Kanonkop Kadette is a Stellenbosch proprietary blend anchored by Pinotage (57%), with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon (26%), Merlot (14%), and Cabernet Franc (3%). The wine displays a dark-fruit-forward profile with ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, complemented by subtle oak spice from winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannin create a food-friendly structure suitable for immediate enjoyment or short-term cellaring.

The blend's approachability and versatility make it an ideal companion for grilled red meats, braised beef dishes, and herb-roasted poultry. Winesensed tasting feedback suggests the wine benefits from three years of additional aging to develop tertiary complexity, though it drinks well upon release.

name: Kanonkop  Paul Sauer
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Bordeaux blend: blackcurrant & cedar with structured tannin. Elegant now, cellaring potential.
description (EN Store): Kanonkop's Paul Sauer is a classically structured Bordeaux blend from Simonsberg-Stellenbosch, anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (68%) with supporting Cabernet Franc and Merlot. The 2018 vintage displays ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, layered with cedary oak and black pepper from careful winemaking. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame a wine built for both near-term enjoyment and graceful evolution. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with bottle age, rewarding patience. Food-friendly across grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb preparations.

name: Kanonkop  Pinotage
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Pinotage with dark plum, smoke, and spice—a food-friendly South African red with structure and character.
description (EN Store): Kanonkop's 2020 Pinotage from Simonsberg-Stellenbosch delivers the signature character of South Africa's flagship varietal. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by smoke and spice from careful winemaking. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide structure suitable for grilled meats, braised beef, and game. Earthy undertones and subtle leather suggest modest aging potential, though the wine is approachable now.

name: Kanonkop  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Age-worthy, structured tannins.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Cabernet Sauvignon from Simonsberg-Stellenbosch, this 2014 vintage showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and mineral backbone. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose give way to cedar and black pepper on the mid-palate, with emerging tobacco and leather notes reflecting the wine's maturity and cellaring potential. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity support food-friendly versatility across grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenuta dell'Ornellaia  Le Serre Nuove dell’Ornellaia
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri's second wine from the 99-ha Ornellaia estate — coastal clay-and-schist terroir, Bordeaux-variety blend, barrique-aged with Mediterranean saline lift.
description (EN Store):
Le Serre Nuove is the direct descendant of fruit declassified from the Ornellaia Bolgheri Superiore selection — a deliberate quality gate that feeds the estate's second wine with parcels from the same two vineyard blocks that produce one of Tuscany's most scrutinised reds. Marchese Lodovico Antinori conceived the estate in 1981 in direct rivalry with Sassicaia and Solaia, and that competitive DNA runs through every tier of production.

The 99 ha split between the original estate (denser clay-dominant soils) and the Bellaria property north of Bolgheri (stonier, alluvial, better-drained ground) at 50–120 m asl produces a structural contrast: clay retains heat and builds mid-palate weight; Bellaria's stones shed water and deliver the fresher, more vibrant register. Tyrrhenian Sea breezes moderate summer temperatures across both sites, preserving acidity and the estate's signature saline coastal character.

Each variety and block is vinified separately, with fermentation in stainless steel and concrete below 25 °C, followed by 10–15 days maceration. Malolactic fermentation occurs predominantly in French oak barriques (70% new, 30% once-used) — the same regime as the grand vin, scaled to the wine's weight. The 2022 vintage note is not yet fully documented, but the Bolgheri growing season consistently benefits from the coastal microclimate that moderates the heat stress seen further inland.

What distinguishes Le Serre Nuove within its peer group is access to the full Ornellaia winemaking infrastructure — same block-by-block vinification, same barrique regime — rather than the simplified production typical of second wines at comparable estates.

name: Castello di Fonterutoli  Siepi Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-Merlot Super Tuscan blend with dark fruit, spice, and elegant structure—food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store): Castello di Fonterutoli's Siepi represents a refined interpretation of Tuscan winemaking, blending 50% Sangiovese with 50% Merlot to create a harmonious Super Tuscan. The wine opens with ripe dark cherry and plum, supported by blackcurrant depth and subtle herbaceous notes. Mid-palate spice and cedar emerge, while leather and tobacco undertones develop with air, revealing the wine's tertiary complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile food companion, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Castello di Fonterutoli  Castello Fonterutoli Chianti Classico Gran Selezione DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Chianti Classico Gran Selezione with red cherry, violet, and earthy complexity—elegant Tuscan structure.
description (EN Store): Castello di Fonterutoli's Chianti Classico Gran Selezione is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 92% Sangiovese, with supporting Malvasia Nera and Colorino. The 2018 vintage expresses the primary character of ripe red cherry and plum, framed by violet florality and herbaceous undertones. Secondary oak integration is restrained, allowing the wine's natural spice and herb profile to emerge. Tertiary development brings earthy minerality and tobacco notes, typical of well-aged Sangiovese in the Classico zone. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine food-friendly across a range of Italian and Mediterranean dishes.

name: Umani Ronchi  Cùmaro – Conero Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano Riserva from Marche with dark plum, spice, and tertiary tobacco complexity. Food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Umani Ronchi's Cùmaro Conero Riserva DOCG is a structured Montepulciano from Italy's Marche region, built on a foundation of dark plum and blackberry fruit with supporting spice and cedar notes. The medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone for extended cellaring, while tertiary tobacco and leather character emerges with age. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Cornerstone  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon from Colchagua Valley with dark fruit, spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Cornerstone Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with medium-full body and balanced tannin. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—dominates the palate, supported by cedar and black pepper notes that evolve toward subtle tobacco and earth on the finish. A versatile red wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Mt Monster  Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Mount Benson Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Mt Monster Cabernet Sauvignon from Mount Benson, Australia, delivers a full-bodied expression of the varietal with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food-pairing versatility. Secondary oak influence adds complexity, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with bottle age, creating a wine suited to grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Mt Monster  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Mount Benson Shiraz with dark plum, spice, and smoky complexity—built for grilled red meat and BBQ.
description (EN Store):
Mt Monster Shiraz from Mount Benson delivers a full-bodied expression of Australia's signature dark-skinned varietal. Dominant dark plum and blackberry fruit form the core, supported by black pepper spice and subtle smoke that reflect both fruit ripeness and winemaking craft. Secondary licorice and spice notes add textural depth, while tertiary leather and earth suggest structure built for cellaring and food pairing.

This wine excels alongside grilled and charred red meats, slow-cooked beef stews, and smoky BBQ preparations. The medium-high tannin frame and full body command rich, savory dishes and aged hard cheeses.

name: Jip Jip Rocks  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with dark plum, blackberry, and peppery spice—a bold, structured wine for red meat and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Jip Jip Rocks Shiraz from Padthaway, Australia, is a full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of dark plum and blackberry fruit. Black pepper and spice dominate the mid-palate, supported by smoky secondary notes that add depth and complexity. The wine's medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, with hints of leather and earth emerging on the finish. This is a wine designed for hearty fare—grilled steaks, lamb, and slow-cooked braises all find natural partners in its bold fruit and peppery character.

name: Jip Jip Rocks  Shiraz Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Shiraz-Cabernet blend from Padthaway with dark fruit, spice, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Jip Jip Rocks Shiraz Cabernet Sauvignon is a full-bodied red blend from Australia's Padthaway region, combining the fruit-forward intensity of Shiraz with Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, layered with black pepper and licorice spice. Medium-high tannins provide grip and aging potential, while secondary leather and earthy undertones add complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and smoky barbecue.

name: Brotte  La Fiole Cote Du Rhone
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Côtes du Rhône with ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Brotte's La Fiole Côtes du Rhône exemplifies the Southern Rhône's signature GSM blend—a harmonious marriage of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre. Medium-full bodied with balanced tannins, this wine delivers ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with garrigue herbs and black pepper spice. The palate shows good structure and approachability, with earthy undertones that suggest modest aging potential. A versatile, food-friendly expression ideal for casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Chateau La Petite Borie  Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Classic Bordeaux blend with ripe plum, blackcurrant, and cedar—food-friendly structure for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Château La Petite Borie presents a traditional Bordeaux red blend anchored by blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle cedar and tobacco notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet layered, with earthy minerality emerging on the finish. A versatile expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Mcguigan  Black Label Red
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian red blend with ripe red fruit, soft tannin, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): McGuigan Black Label Red is a medium-full bodied Australian red blend from Hunter Valley, crafted for everyday enjoyment and versatile food pairing. The wine opens with dominant ripe red fruit and dark plum character, supported by subtle oak and vanilla notes from winemaking. Medium tannin and balanced acidity create a smooth, approachable palate that complements grilled meats, braised dishes, and classic comfort food without demanding attention. A reliable, well-made commercial offering suited to casual dining and entertaining.

name: Mcguigan  Black Label Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Merlot with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly structure—a reliable everyday red.
description (EN Store): McGuigan Black Label Merlot is a medium-bodied red wine from Australia's Hunter Valley, crafted from 100% Merlot. The wine displays dominant notes of black cherry and dark plum on the nose, with supporting blackberry character and subtle oak influence. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable and versatile at the table, suitable for casual dining and everyday enjoyment. The soft, fruit-forward profile reflects the commercial positioning of the McGuigan brand within the accessible red wine segment.

name: Mcguigan  Private Bin Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Hunter Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for weeknight red-meat dinners.
description (EN Store): McGuigan's Private Bin Merlot from Australia's Hunter Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red with generous plum and berry-fruit character. The palate shows juicy red-fruit layers with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a soft, approachable finish. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness without overwhelming the fruit. A versatile, food-friendly Merlot suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and casual dining.

name: Mcguigan  Private Bin Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Hunter Valley Shiraz with dark fruit, pepper spice, and smoky complexity.
description (EN Store): McGuigan's Private Bin Shiraz from Hunter Valley delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Full-bodied and structured with medium-high tannins, this wine showcases ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit on the palate, complemented by black pepper and licorice spice. Subtle smoke and leather notes add depth and age-worthiness, making it an excellent match for grilled red meat, braised beef, and smoky barbecue.

name: Monsoon Valley  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Thai Shiraz with dark fruit, pepper spice, and food-friendly structure—a versatile red for grilled meats and bold Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Monsoon Valley's Shiraz from Hua Hin showcases the varietal's signature dark-fruit profile with ripe plum and blackberry at the fore. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure deliver approachability without sacrificing complexity. Black pepper and spice notes add vibrancy, while subtle oak integration and earthy undertones provide depth. A food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Southeast Asian preparations.

name: Monsoon Valley  Cuvee de Siam Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Shiraz-Sangiovese blend from Thailand's Hua Hin region; dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity in a food-friendly medium-full frame.
description (EN Store): Monsoon Valley's Cuvée de Siam Rouge marries Shiraz (70%) and Sangiovese (30%) in a distinctive Thai red that balances Southern Rhône power with Italian structure. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by blackberry depth and subtle spice. Secondary oak and herbaceous notes add complexity, while tertiary earth and tobacco suggest modest aging potential. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine versatile at table—equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or Mediterranean-inflected cuisine.

name: Berri Estates  BIN 999 (Shiraz) (Fruit wine blend)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable South Australian Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, black pepper spice, and food-friendly tannin structure.
description (EN Store): Berri Estates BIN 999 is a 100% Shiraz from South Australia, crafted in a commercial, fruit-forward style. The wine displays dominant dark plum and blackberry on the nose, with supporting black pepper and spice notes that add complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile table wine suited to casual dining and everyday enjoyment. The approachable profile emphasizes ripe fruit character over oak or tertiary development, positioning it as an accessible entry point to Australian Shiraz.

name: Vigneti del Salento  "ZOLLA" Primitivo Di Manduria DOC
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Manduria with bold dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): A characterful expression of Primitivo from Vigneti Del Salento's Manduria holdings, this wine delivers the warm-climate generosity the region is known for. Dark cherry and ripe berry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper that add complexity without overwhelming the fruit. The tannins are fully rounded and integrated, creating a smooth mouthfeel that belies the wine's full body. This is a no-fuss, food-friendly red that performs equally well at the table or as an aperitif, with enough structure to stand up to grilled meats and bold flavors, yet enough approachability to pair with casual pizza and pasta.

name: Vigneti del Salento  Vigne Vecchie Leggenda Primitivo Di Manduria
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Manduria with ripe dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Salento's Vigne Vecchie Leggenda Primitivo di Manduria is a bold, fruit-driven red from Puglia's sun-drenched Manduria region. The 2021 vintage delivers a full-bodied expression of Primitivo with lashings of dark cherry and raspberry, complemented by subtle smoke and black pepper notes. Fully rounded tannins and smooth palate integration make this wine remarkably approachable, yet with enough structure and complexity to reward food pairing. A classic warm-climate red that balances richness with drinkability—ideal for pizza, pasta, and grilled meats.

name: Domaine Laroche  Pinot Noir de la Chevaliere IGT
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundian Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, subtle oak, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A classically styled Pinot Noir from Burgundy's esteemed Domaine Laroche, this wine expresses the varietal's hallmark elegance through bright red cherry and raspberry aromatics balanced by earthy minerality. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure make it an approachable yet sophisticated choice for the table, pairing seamlessly with roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, and lighter meat preparations. The subtle oak integration and black pepper spice add complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural fruit-forward character.

name: Signus  Fruit Red Blend (Merlot) (Fruit wine blend)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for casual dining and grilled meats.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied Chilean Merlot from the Central Valley, this fruit-forward blend delivers immediate appeal with dominant plum and berry character on the nose. The palate unfolds with juicy red fruit, cranberry, and subtle vanilla notes from winemaking, supported by well-integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. Herbaceous undertones add complexity. The soft texture and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled steaks, braised beef, roasted chicken, or tomato-based pasta dishes.

name: Signus  Classic Red Blend (Cabernet Sauvignon) (Fruit wine blend)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a classic red for grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Signus Classic Red Blend presents a full-bodied Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Central Valley, delivering the varietal's signature blackcurrant and dark plum fruit character. Cedar and oak frame the palate, supported by firm, well-integrated tannins and medium-high acidity that provide structure and aging potential. Subtle tobacco and leather notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and depth. This wine suits grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes, where its tannin grip and secondary oak complexity enhance savory, umami-rich flavors.

name: Chateau Cheval Blanc 1er Grand Cru Classe A 2008
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet Franc-dominant Right Bank anomaly: 56 plots on Isle river alluvium, each vinified separately in gravity-fed concrete vats, aged 16–18 months in 100% new Tronçais oak.
description (EN Store): When Jean Laussac-Fourcaud replanted the estate in the 1860s with Cabernet Franc as the structural backbone — locally called 'Bouchet' — the decision was considered audacious on a Right Bank where Merlot was already king. That single choice still defines every bottle made here. The 42-hectare vineyard sits on quaternary alluvium deposited by the Isle river, non-calcareous soils that divide into three identities: roughly 40% gravel over deep blue clay (home to Cabernet Franc vines planted in 1920, now over a century old), 40% deep gravel, and 20% sandy clay. This flat alluvial terrain sits at the north-western edge of Saint-Émilion, physically bordering Pomerol — a borderline position that produces a wine neither appellation can fully claim. All 56 plots are vinified separately in temperature-controlled, gravity-fed concrete vats sized to individual plot tonnage, with malolactic fermentation completed in tank; the Grand Vin is assembled by tasting alone, with no prescriptive varietal formula. Aging runs 16–18 months in 100% new French oak — Tronçais and Bercé forest barrels from 180–350-year-old trees, replaced entirely each vintage, held at a constant 14°C, racked manually through the bung hole using nitrogen. Cheval Blanc is the only First Growth-equivalent on the Right Bank where Cabernet Franc, not Merlot, anchors the blend — a structural transgression that has no peer in Bordeaux. The 2008 vintage is not among the library's scored benchmarks, but sits between the concentrated 2009 and the earlier decade; at this stage it is entering a generous drinking window with the Cabernet Franc's silky tannin architecture fully resolved.

name: Monteverro  Tinata IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Syrah-Grenache blend with dark plum, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): A Maremma red from the Monteverro portfolio, this 2017 Tinata IGT blends 70% Syrah with 30% Grenache to deliver a medium-full-bodied wine with ripe dark plum and blackberry at its core. Black pepper and spice notes from the Syrah component add textural interest, while subtle earth and herbaceous undertones suggest modest aging potential. Approachable tannins and balanced acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: “MLV” Masseria Li Veli Salento IGT
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Primitivo-Cabernet blend from Salento with dark fruit, cherry, and subtle smoke—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): A well-balanced red from Masseria Li Veli in Puglia's Salento region, this 2020 blend marries Primitivo's ripe dark fruit character with Cabernet Sauvignon's structure. On the nose, bold dark cherry and raspberry lead, followed by hints of smoke and black pepper. The palate is full and rounded, with smooth tannins and a mix of red and dark fruit that fills the mouth without heaviness. Spice notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. Eminently drinkable and versatile at the table.

name: Luccarelli  Campo Marina Primitivo di Manduria DOP
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Manduria with bold dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, rounded tannins; perfect for pizza and pasta.
description (EN Store): Luccarelli's Campo Marina Primitivo di Manduria DOP is a full-bodied red wine from Puglia's renowned Manduria subregion. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark fruit, black cherry, and raspberry, followed by a palate-filling mix of ripe plum, smoke, and black pepper. Fully rounded tannins and smooth texture make this wine eminently drinkable and food-friendly. A touch of spice adds complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. Ideal for pizza, pasta, grilled meats, and hearty braised dishes.

name: Vesevo  Aglianico del Beneventano IGT
short_description (EN Store): Campanian Aglianico with dark fruit, spice, and mineral depth—a food-friendly Southern Italian red.
description (EN Store): Vesevo's Aglianico del Beneventano IGT is a single-varietal expression from Campania's volcanic terroir. The wine displays the characteristic dark plum and black cherry fruit of Aglianico, layered with spice, leather, and earthy undertones. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: La Piuma  Montepulciano D'Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with red fruit drive, earthy spice, and food-friendly tannin—a versatile Italian red.
description (EN Store):
La Piuma's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a classically structured Italian red from the Abruzzo region, built on a foundation of ripe red and dark plum fruit. The palate shows medium-full body with balanced acidity and approachable tannin, making it an ideal companion to everyday Italian cuisine and grilled meats.

Earthy undertones and subtle spice notes add complexity without overshadowing the wine's primary fruit character. The rustic, food-friendly profile reflects the Montepulciano varietal's natural affinity for regional dishes—from tomato-based pasta to cured meats and roasted vegetables.

name: Chateau Caruel  (Cotes de Bourg AOC)
short_description (EN Store): Balanced Bordeaux blend from Côtes de Bourg with dark fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Caruel represents the approachable side of Bordeaux winemaking from the Côtes de Bourg appellation. This Cabernet Franc–led blend combines ripe blackcurrant and dark plum with subtle cedar and black pepper notes, delivering a well-structured wine with medium-full body and moderate tannin. The balance between primary fruit and secondary oak complexity makes it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-forward dishes. Drink now or cellar for 3–5 years for additional complexity.

name: Casanova di Neri  Brunello di Montalcino Tenuta Nuova DOCG 2017
short_description (EN Store): Southern Montalcino's Tenuta Nuova site — first Sangiovese planted near Castelnuovo dell'Abate — aged ~30 months in tonneaux from a proprietary Cerretalto clone.
description (EN Store): When the 2001 Tenuta Nuova was named Wine Spectator Wine of the Year in 2006, it was the first time a single-vineyard Brunello had claimed that title — a moment that forced the global market to reckon with southern Montalcino as a serious terroir address. The Tenuta Nuova / Le Cetine vineyard sits near Castelnuovo dell'Abate in the warmer southern sector of the appellation, where Giacomo Neri planted Sangiovese before any other producer had committed to that zone. The warmer aspect and lower elevation relative to the estate's north-eastern sites drive fuller physiological ripeness, producing a denser, more voluminous fruit profile than the mineral austerity of Cerretalto. Vines are planted with the estate's proprietary Sangiovese Grosso clone, bred from the Cerretalto mother vineyard and propagated across all seven sites — a deliberate genetic coherence that anchors house DNA regardless of terroir. In the cellar, individual berries pass an optical sorter before gravity-fed transfer (no pumps) into open-topped conical vats; spontaneous fermentation proceeds with punch-downs over approximately 23–24 days, followed by ~30 months in tonneaux. What distinguishes Tenuta Nuova within the Casanova di Neri range is its position as the power expression — richer and more immediately generous than the White Label, yet released a full year ahead of Cerretalto. The 2017 vintage in Montalcino was a warm, dry year producing concentrated, structured wines with deep fruit and firm backbone; drinking window broadly estimated 2024–2042.

name: Anakena  Birdman Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, juicy berries, and soft tannins—approachable and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Birdman Merlot from Chile's Rapel Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of the varietal with immediate appeal. The nose opens with ripe plum and a medley of berry fruits, supported by subtle vanilla notes and a whisper of herbaceous character. On the palate, juicy red and dark fruit flavors dominate, with cranberry acidity providing freshness and tannins that are already well-integrated, creating a smooth, approachable finish. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and hearty pasta preparations.

name: Viu Manent  Rayuela Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure—a reliable Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Viu Manent's Rayuela Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the nose, supported by cedar and subtle spice from oak aging. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced tannins and moderate acidity, making it an approachable yet structured wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses. A food-friendly Chilean red with enough complexity for casual collectors.

name: Silverado Vineyards  Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cabernet-led blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar oak, and structured tannins—classic food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Silverado Vineyards' 2019 Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley is a classically proportioned Bordeaux-style blend anchored by 89% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Merlot, Petit Verdot, and Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and black cherry fruit, layered with cedar and vanilla oak from careful aging. Mid-palate tannins are structured and medium-high, providing grip without harshness, while secondary black pepper spice and tertiary tobacco notes add complexity. Medium acidity and medium-full body make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Angove  The Medhyk Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied McLaren Vale Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and mocha—organic-certified, elegantly structured.
description (EN Store): Angove's The Medhyk Shiraz represents the pinnacle of organic McLaren Vale winemaking, sourced from the country's largest certified organic vineyard estate. This full-bodied expression showcases the coastal Mediterranean climate through ripe dark berries, plum, and mocha complexity, underpinned by soft, well-integrated tannins and subtle earthiness. The wine balances power with elegance, delivering a vibrant, food-friendly profile that reflects Angove's heritage of sustainable viticulture and premium craftsmanship.

name: Angove  Organic Shiraz Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Organic Shiraz-Cabernet blend from South Australia's pioneering winemaker—ripe dark fruit, elegant structure, Mediterranean warmth.
description (EN Store): Angove's Organic Shiraz Cabernet represents the winery's commitment to sustainable viticulture and premium South Australian winemaking. This Riverland blend marries the richness of Shiraz with the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a full-bodied red with vibrant dark berry fruit, subtle earthiness, and elegantly balanced tannins. The wine reflects the coastal Mediterranean climate of its region, offering both immediate fruit appeal and layered complexity suitable for food pairing or contemplative drinking.

name: Angove  Organic Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Certified organic Merlot from South Australia's Riverland, delivering ripe dark berries and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Angove's Organic Merlot represents the winery's commitment to sustainable viticulture and premium quality. This single-varietal expression showcases the warmth and generosity of the Riverland region, with a palate dominated by ripe black cherry and plum fruit. Subtle earthiness and mineral undertones provide complexity, while the medium tannin structure ensures food-friendly elegance. A classic Australian Merlot suited to everyday dining and special occasions alike.

name: Angove  Studio Series Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Organic Cabernet from South Australia's heritage winemaker—ripe dark berries, elegant structure, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Angove's Studio Series Cabernet Sauvignon represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture and premium South Australian winemaking. This medium-full bodied expression showcases ripe dark berries and blackcurrant on the nose, with subtle earthiness and soft tannin structure on the palate. The wine reflects the coastal Mediterranean climate of the Riverland region, delivering a vibrant, food-friendly profile that complements a wide range of dishes from grilled meats to pasta.

name: Chateau La Rose Gadis  Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Bordeaux Merlot with ripe plum, soft tannins, and subtle chocolate—elegant everyday red.
description (EN Store): Château La Rose Gadis presents a classic Bordeaux Merlot with medium-full body and well-integrated tannins. The nose opens with ripe plum and red berry fruit, complemented by hints of vanilla and cocoa from careful winemaking. On the palate, a juicy medley of berry flavours unfolds with subtle herbaceous notes, leading to a smooth, satisfying finish. The wine's approachable structure and balanced acidity make it versatile at table—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or roasted poultry.

name: Chateau Côtes De Chambeau  Montagne Saint-Emilion
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Montagne Saint-Émilion with ripe plum, cassis, and subtle oak—elegant, food-friendly Bordeaux.
description (EN Store): Château Côtes de Chambeau's Montagne Saint-Émilion is a classically structured Bordeaux blend anchored by 90% Merlot and 10% Cabernet Franc. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and approachable tannins, offering ripe red plum and black cherry on the nose, complemented by subtle herbaceous notes and earthy minerality. Soft oak integration and a clean finish make this a versatile food wine suitable for everyday occasions and special meals alike.

name: Chateau Plantey  Pauillac Cru Bourgeois
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac Cru Bourgeois: balanced Merlot-Cabernet blend with dark fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Plantey's Pauillac Cru Bourgeois represents the approachable side of Bordeaux's Left Bank. This 50/50 Merlot–Cabernet Sauvignon blend delivers a medium-full frame with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and graphite minerality. The 2018 vintage shows classic Pauillac structure: firm but integrated tannins, balanced acidity, and a dry finish that invites food pairing. Ideal for grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Pounamu  Special Selection Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, subtle oak, and food-friendly structure—elegant New Zealand expression.
description (EN Store): Pounamu's 2014 Special Selection Pinot Noir from Marlborough showcases the region's signature red-fruit purity and elegant restraint. Ripe cherry and plum dominate the nose, supported by whispers of oak and spice from careful winemaking. The palate is medium-full and supple, with fine-grained tannins and bright acidity that frame the fruit without dominating. Earthy undertones emerge on the finish, suggesting modest bottle age and terroir influence. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, duck, and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Castello di Bossi  Corbaia IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-Cabernet blend from Chianti with ripe red fruit, cedar, and herbaceous complexity. Food-friendly, structured for pasta and grilled meats.
description (EN Store):
Castello di Bossi's Corbaia IGT is a Super Tuscan blend of 70% Sangiovese and 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, crafted in the heart of Chianti. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by blackcurrant depth. Cedar and herbaceous notes from the Cabernet component add secondary complexity, while subtle spice and earthy undertones suggest careful oak integration and aging potential.

Medium-full bodied with balanced tannin and acidity, Corbaia is a versatile food wine suited to tomato-based pasta, grilled red meat, braised beef dishes, and mushroom preparations. The structure and flavor profile make it equally at home with pizza, Mediterranean cuisine, and aged hard cheeses.

name: Renieri  Brunello di Montalcino Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello Riserva with structured tannins, red cherry, and dried herbs—a refined Tuscan expression of Sangiovese.
description (EN Store): Renieri's Brunello di Montalcino Riserva DOCG 2013 represents the classical expression of Sangiovese from Tuscany's most prestigious appellation. The wine displays a medium-full body with high acidity and medium-high tannins, delivering a structured, age-worthy profile. Red cherry and dried herb notes form the primary character, complemented by leather and tobacco in the secondary layer, with mineral and earthy complexity emerging on the finish. This Riserva designation reflects extended aging in oak and bottle, building tertiary depth suitable for cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial food.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Poggio Valente Rosso IGT
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with red cherry, plum, and earthy minerality—food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store): Fattoria Le Pupille's Poggio Valente Rosso is a pure expression of Sangiovese from the Rosso di Montalcino subregion of Tuscany. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with bright red cherry and plum on the nose, supported by subtle herbaceous and earthy undertones. The palate shows balanced acidity and medium tannin, with mineral precision and hints of dried herb and spice. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes add complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile food wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic preparations.

name: Napa Cellars  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins. Classic California elegance.
description (EN Store): Napa Cellars' 2021 Cabernet Sauvignon exemplifies the region's signature style: a full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the palate with classic structure, while tobacco notes add tertiary complexity. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food-pairing versatility. A benchmark expression of Napa Valley Cabernet for the discerning collector.

name: Napa Cellars  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Napa Valley Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, raspberry, and earthy complexity. Fresh, silky, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): This 2019 Napa Valley Pinot Noir from Napa Cellars delivers the classic elegance and approachability the region is known for. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a silky texture that speaks to careful winemaking. The wine's fresh acidity and medium tannin structure make it a versatile companion to a wide range of dishes—from roasted poultry to mushroom risotto to lighter seafood preparations. A summer-friendly expression that rewards both casual sipping and thoughtful pairing.

name: Luce Della Vite  Luce Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Pure Sangiovese from Montalcino: elegant red cherry, earthy minerality, and structured tannins for serious food pairing.
description (EN Store): Luce Della Vite Brunello di Montalcino DOCG 2020 is a single-varietal expression of Sangiovese from one of Tuscany's most prestigious appellations. This wine showcases the classic profile of Brunello: ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with earthy minerality and dried herb complexity. The palate is structured and elegant, with medium-high tannins and acidity that frame the fruit and suggest considerable aging potential. Secondary oak and tertiary leather and tobacco notes add depth and sophistication, making this a wine suited to serious food pairing and contemplative cellaring.

name: Chateau Timberlay  Bordeaux Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Bordeaux Supérieur blend of Merlot, Cabernet, and Franc—soft tannins, dark fruit, and herbaceous charm.
description (EN Store): Château Timberlay's Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured red blend anchored by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc. The wine displays ripe black cherry and plum on the nose, with subtle cedar and herbaceous notes that speak to its Bordeaux terroir. Medium-full body and approachable tannins make it food-friendly across a range of occasions—from casual weeknight pasta to grilled meats and charcuterie boards. A reliable, well-balanced expression of the appellation.

name: Chateau Haut Fourat  Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Pure Merlot from Bordeaux with ripe plum, cherry, and soft tannins—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Château Haut Fourat presents a 100% Merlot expression from Bordeaux's prestigious terroir. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and approachable tannin structure, offering ripe red and dark fruit character with subtle earthy undertones and gentle oak influence. A versatile, well-crafted Bordeaux suitable for both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Speri  Valpolicella DOC Classico
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Ripasso from Speri: dark fruit-forward Corvina blend with balanced oak and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Speri's Valpolicella Classico Ripasso is a traditional Veneto red built on Corvina Veronese (60%), Rondinella (30%), and Molinara (10%). The ripasso technique—partial re-fermentation on dried-grape skins—imparts depth and complexity without excess weight. Dark berry compote and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by subtle oak integration and a whisper of spice. The palate is inviting and structured, with medium tannin and balanced acidity that frame savory earth notes. A versatile food wine that shines alongside braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Masseria Li Veli  Orion Primitivo IGT
short_description (EN Store): Bold Primitivo from Salento with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Masseria Li Veli's Orion Primitivo is a full-bodied red from Puglia's Salento region, showcasing the varietal's signature dark-fruit intensity and warming spice. The wine opens with a bold nose of cherry and raspberry, followed by a palate marked by smoke, black pepper, and a touch of earthiness. Fully rounded tannins and smooth texture make this an eminently drinkable expression—neither overly complex nor austere, but rather a confident, well-balanced wine that delivers immediate pleasure. Ideal for pizza, pasta, grilled meats, and casual entertaining.

name: Delas  Cotes Du Rhone Saint Esprit Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Southern Rhône blend of Syrah and Grenache with ripe red fruit, peppery spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Delas Côtes du Rhône Saint Esprit Rouge is a classic expression of Southern Rhône winemaking, blending Syrah and Grenache to deliver a medium-full-bodied red with immediate appeal and versatility at table. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and subtle herbaceous notes that reflect the garrigue character of the region. Smooth, medium tannins and balanced acidity make this a natural partner for everyday dining—from grilled meats and tomato-based pasta to Mediterranean fare and cured meats.

name: Delas  Vacqueyras Domain des Genets
short_description (EN Store): Rhône South blend of dark cherry, spice, and earth—food-friendly medium-full red for lamb and grilled meats.
description (EN Store): A classic Rhône South blend from Delas, combining Syrah, Grenache, and Mourvèdre in a medium-full frame. The wine opens with dominant dark cherry and blackberry fruit, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous notes from the winemaking. Tertiary earth and leather complexity emerge on the palate, offering structure and food compatibility across grilled meats, lamb, and Mediterranean herb-forward dishes.

name: Delas  Crozes Hermitage "Domaine des Grands Chemins"
short_description (EN Store): Pure Syrah from Crozes-Hermitage: dark fruit, peppery spice, and structured tannins in a food-friendly Northern Rhône classic.
description (EN Store): Delas's Crozes-Hermitage 'Domaine des Grands Chemins' is a single-varietal Syrah from the Northern Rhône's most approachable appellation. The 2020 vintage delivers ripe black cherry and blackcurrant on the nose, underpinned by white pepper and subtle smoke—hallmarks of cool-climate Syrah. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or herb-roasted lamb. A touch of earth and leather on the finish hints at the wine's aging potential, though it drinks beautifully young.

name: Oxford Landing  Cabernet Sauvignon & Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Cabernet-Syrah blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Oxford Landing's Cabernet Sauvignon and Syrah blend from the Riverland region delivers a full-bodied red wine with pronounced dark fruit character and well-integrated oak influence. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedar and black pepper notes that add complexity and structure. The wine exhibits medium-high tannins and a spiced, leathery finish that suggests careful aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Oxford Landing  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for weeknight red-meat dinners.
description (EN Store): Oxford Landing Merlot from Australia's Riverland region delivers a medium-full-bodied red built on ripe plum and berry fruit. The palate shows juicy red-fruit character with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a soft, approachable finish. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. A versatile, food-friendly Merlot suited to grilled beef, braised dishes, roasted poultry, and tomato-based pastas.

name: Oxford Landing  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with ripe blackberry, pepper spice, and smoky depth. Structured tannins and dark fruit intensity.
description (EN Store): Oxford Landing Shiraz from Australia's Riverland delivers a full-bodied expression of the varietal with dominant blackberry and dark plum fruit, layered with black pepper and spice. Medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary smoke and licorice notes add complexity. The wine shows leather and dried fruit character on the finish, suggesting modest age-worthiness. A food-friendly, approachable premium red suited to grilled meats, lamb, and hearty braised dishes.

name: Wairau River  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Wairau River's Pinot Noir from Marlborough's Wairau Valley delivers the region's signature red-fruit character with restraint and balance. Bright cherry and plum dominate the palate, supported by subtle oak integration and a whisper of black pepper. The wine's medium-full body and approachable tannin structure make it a versatile table companion, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or lighter red-meat preparations. A food-friendly expression of New Zealand Pinot Noir.

name: Chateau Lynch-Moussas  Grand Cru Classé AOC Pauilac 2021
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac Grand Cru Classé with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—a structured, elegant Bordeaux for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Château Lynch-Moussas 2021 is a classic Pauillac expression from Bordeaux's Left Bank, built on a traditional blend of 72% Cabernet Sauvignon and 28% Merlot. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and medium acidity, offering ripe blackcurrant and plum on the nose, complemented by secondary cedar and tobacco notes that speak to careful oak aging. Tertiary leather and mineral complexity emerge on the palate, suggesting a wine with structure and aging potential. Ideal for grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Morgon
short_description (EN Store): Beaujolais Gamay from Morgon's volcanic granite, vinified by Drouhin's finesse-first house under fourth-generation family stewardship.
description (EN Store): Joseph Drouhin, founded in 1880 and now stewarded by four siblings of the fourth generation, built its reputation on a singular conviction: terroir transparency over extraction. That philosophy, honed across Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, is applied here to Morgon — one of Beaujolais' ten crus — where decomposed granite and schist soils on the slopes of Mont du Py produce Gamay of unusual depth and structural grip, distinct from the lighter sandy soils of neighbouring crus. The volcanic subsoil drives the appellation's characteristic iron-tinged, almost Burgundian density. Drouhin's approach mirrors its Côte d'Or reds: fermentation in small open vats with indigenous yeasts, pigeage and pumping-over during maceration, and élevage in French high-forest oak barrels with restrained new oak — a regime that preserves fruit clarity without imposing wood. What sets this bottling apart is the producer's choice to apply a Grand Cru Burgundy winemaking framework — native yeast fermentation, tasting-led élevage decisions — to a cru Beaujolais priced at village level. The current vintage delivers the generous, charming fruit character Morgon produces in warm years, with the granite-driven structure that rewards 2–4 years of cellaring.

name: Kendall Jackson  Vintner's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Cabernet blend with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and food-friendly structure—a reliable everyday premium red.
description (EN Store): Kendall Jackson's Vintner's Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by 85% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Malbec, and Merlot from Sonoma County. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and medium acidity, offering approachable dark fruit character—blackcurrant and black cherry—layered with vanilla oak and subtle cocoa from barrel aging. Cedar and black pepper notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. This is a versatile, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and casual entertaining.

name: Kendall Jackson  Vintner's Reserve Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Sonoma Merlot blend with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and vanilla oak—food-friendly everyday red.
description (EN Store): Kendall Jackson's Vintner's Reserve Merlot is a Sonoma County blend anchored by 93% Merlot with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, Malbec, and Petit Verdot. The wine delivers a medium-full body with ripe black cherry and dark plum on the nose, complemented by subtle cocoa and vanilla oak from careful winemaking. Soft, approachable tannins and balanced acidity make this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. A reliable, commercially polished expression of California Merlot.

name: Kendall Jackson  Grand Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Cabernet blend with dark cherry, blackcurrant, and cedar—structured, food-friendly, classic California style.
description (EN Store): Kendall Jackson's Grand Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Sonoma County is a classically structured Bordeaux-style blend anchored by 97% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, Merlot, and Malbec. The 2018 vintage delivers dominant blackcurrant and black cherry on the nose, with secondary cedar and vanilla oak from careful aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with tertiary tobacco and cocoa notes emerging on the finish. A versatile pairing for grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Montes Winemaker's Choice  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, soft tannins, and herbaceous depth—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Montes Winemaker's Choice Merlot from Chile's Casablanca Valley presents a medium-full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe plum and layered berry fruit. The palate delivers juicy red fruit and cranberry notes with integrated tannins and a soft, approachable texture. Subtle vanilla and oak provide secondary complexity, while herbaceous undertones add freshness and structure. A versatile wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-forward cuisine.

name: Montes Winemaker's Choice  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—a classic expression of Maipo Valley power.
description (EN Store): Montes Winemaker's Choice Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while high tannins provide structure and aging potential. Black pepper spice and subtle tobacco notes add complexity, making this wine a versatile partner for grilled red meats, lamb, and braised beef dishes. The wine's balance of fruit intensity and structural grip reflects careful winemaking in one of South America's premier Cabernet regions.

name: Batasiolo  Barolo Brunate DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Classic Barolo from Brunate vineyard: elegant Nebbiolo with rose, cherry, and mineral complexity. 2012 vintage.
description (EN Store): Batasiolo's Barolo Brunate DOCG is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious terroirs. The 2012 vintage showcases the characteristic elegance and structure of this noble varietal: a dominant core of red cherry and rose petal aromatics, supported by tarry and leathery secondary notes from extended aging in traditional oak. With bottle age, tertiary tobacco and earthy minerality emerge, adding complexity and refinement. Medium-full body and firm tannins frame a wine built for the table and the cellar alike.

name: Chateau L'Ecuyer
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot & Cabernet Franc with ripe red fruit, soft tannins & mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Château L'Ecuyer is a classically structured Pomerol red, built on a foundation of 90% Merlot with 10% Cabernet Franc. The 2019 vintage expresses the region's signature softness and depth, offering ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by subtle oak and earthy mineral notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining the structure for modest cellaring. A food-friendly expression of Right Bank Bordeaux character.

name: Chateau Teyssier  St.Emillion Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Saint-Émilion Grand Cru with dark plum, black cherry, and cedary complexity. Food-friendly, elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Château Teyssier's 2019 Saint-Émilion Grand Cru is a classically proportioned Bordeaux blend anchored by 70% Merlot and 30% Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, supported by subtle cassis and herbaceous undertones characteristic of the appellation's terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this a versatile table wine suited to both casual dining and more formal occasions. Cedar and mineral notes emerge on the mid-palate, suggesting modest aging potential and food-pairing versatility across red meat, poultry, and aged cheese.

name: Chateau Laforge  St.Emillion Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Saint-Émilion Grand Cru with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and silky tannins. Merlot-led Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Château Laforge Saint-Émilion Grand Cru is a classically structured Bordeaux blend anchored by 92% Merlot with 8% Cabernet Franc. The 2019 vintage expresses the region's signature dark fruit character—plum and blackcurrant—layered with cedar, tobacco, and subtle earth. Medium-full body and approachable tannins make this wine food-friendly across a range of red meat and poultry dishes. The wine reflects Saint-Émilion's limestone-rich terroir and traditional winemaking approach.

name: Granducato  Chianti Classico
short_description (EN Store): Classic Chianti Classico: bright cherry, herbaceous spice, and mineral-driven acidity with elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Granducato's Chianti Classico exemplifies the region's signature Sangiovese character: vibrant cherry fruit balanced by high acidity and medium-high tannins that frame a mineral core. Herbaceous notes and subtle spice emerge on the mid-palate, with leather and dried-fruit complexity developing through the finish. A food-friendly, age-worthy expression suited to Italian cuisine and grilled preparations.

name: Morambro Creek  Cabernet sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): South Australian Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit depth and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Morambro Creek's Cabernet Sauvignon is a classically styled South Australian red offering dark cherry and blackcurrant fruit with subtle cedar and herbaceous notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it an approachable yet serious food wine, suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Morambro Creek  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Ripe, peppery South Australian Shiraz with dark fruit and savory spice—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Morambro Creek Shiraz is a classic South Australian expression of the varietal, delivering ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit with a distinctive peppery spice character. Medium-full bodied with balanced tannins, the wine shows secondary notes of black pepper and subtle oak that add complexity without overshadowing the primary fruit. A savory, food-friendly red suited to grilled and braised beef, lamb, and smoky preparations.

name: Stefano Antonucci  Moschio Da Monte DOC
short_description (EN Store): Marche blend of Montepulciano & Sangiovese; medium-full red with cherry, plum & herbaceous character. Food-friendly Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Stefano Antonucci's Moschio Da Monte is a Montepulciano–Sangiovese blend from Italy's Marche region, representing the food-friendly tradition of central Italian red winemaking. The 2019 vintage displays a medium-full body with balanced tannin and medium acidity, opening with ripe red cherry and dark plum fruit supported by herbaceous and black pepper notes. Secondary earth and mineral complexity emerge on the palate, with subtle tobacco and dried-fruit undertones suggesting modest aging potential. A versatile table wine suited to tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and rustic Italian fare.

name: Marchesi Di Gresy  Camp Gros Barbaresco Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo Riserva from Barbaresco with silky tannins, rose florals, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Marchesi Di Gresy's Camp Gros Barbaresco Riserva DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone. The 2015 vintage displays the varietal's signature red cherry and rose aromatics, layered with tar and licorice notes from extended aging. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure make this wine food-friendly yet age-worthy, with tertiary leather and mineral complexity emerging on the palate. A benchmark expression of traditional Barbaresco craftsmanship.

name: Marchesi Di Gresy  Gaiun Barbaresco DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco with rose, cherry, and mineral complexity; structured for cellaring and food.
description (EN Store): Marchesi Di Gresy's Gaiun Barbaresco DOCG is a classically styled expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone. The 2016 vintage displays the varietal's signature aromatic profile: delicate rose and red cherry on the nose, with underlying minerality and subtle tar notes. Medium-full body and firm, refined tannins provide structure and aging potential, while secondary tobacco and leather notes emerge on the palate. This wine rewards patient cellaring and pairs beautifully with grilled lamb, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenuta delle Terre Nere  Etna Rosso Feudo Di Mezzo Il Quadro delle Rose DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Etna Nerello Mascalese with volcanic minerality, red cherry, and silky tannins—a food-friendly Sicilian classic.
description (EN Store): Tenuta delle Terre Nere's Etna Rosso Feudo Di Mezzo Il Quadro delle Rose is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from Sicily's volcanic slopes. The 2021 vintage showcases the grape's signature elegance: bright red cherry and stone fruit on the nose, layered with black pepper spice and mineral-driven acidity that reflects the terroir's volcanic character. Medium-full body and silky tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining structure for food pairing. A versatile red that bridges casual dining and more refined table settings.

name: Bibi Graetz  Testamatta Toscana Rosso Igt
short_description (EN Store): Self-taught artist, 100% Sangiovese from north-facing galestro slopes — Burgundian transparency over Tuscan muscle, unfiltered, ~10% new oak.
description (EN Store): Bibi Graetz arrived at winemaking in 2000 with no formal oenology training, a 2-hectare family plot at Castello di Vincigliata in the Fiesole hills northeast of Florence, and a deliberate refusal to pursue the Super Tuscan power template. That founding instinct has since been codified into a sourcing rule: Testamatta draws exclusively from north-facing or cooler-mesoclimate sites — Vincigliata, Olmo, Londa, Lamole, and Montefili — where stony galestro (schist) and clay soils at high-altitude hillside elevations restrict vigour, sharpen acidity, and drive mineral precision over fruit weight. The 100% Sangiovese blend, from vines 35–50 years old, undergoes spontaneous fermentation on indigenous yeasts in open-top 225L French oak barriques with manual pigeage and zero concentration techniques. Aging runs 20 months in French oak, approximately 10% new, 75% second-to-fourth-fill, followed by a minimum 6 months in bottle before release; the wine is bottled unfiltered. The rule broken here is categorical: despite sourcing from Chianti Classico DOCG sites including Lamole and Montefili, Graetz deliberately declassifies every bottle to Toscana IGT, rejecting the appellation hierarchy entirely. The 2019 vintage delivers the fresh, precise character the north-facing parcel strategy is designed to produce — taut galestro-driven structure with the lifted acidity that rewards 3–8 years of further cellaring.

name: Castello Di Ama  Vigneto Bellavista DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Tuscan blend with bright cherry, plum, and herbaceous character—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Castello Di Ama's Vigneto Bellavista DOCG is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 80% Sangiovese with 20% Malvasia Nera. The 2018 vintage expresses the primary fruit-forward character typical of Chianti expressions: ripe red cherry and plum dominate the nose and palate, supported by herbaceous undertones and a mineral spine. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable in youth while retaining enough structure for modest cellaring. Earthy secondary notes and hints of dried fruit complexity suggest food-pairing versatility across Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Castello Di Ama  Vigneto La Casuccia DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-Merlot blend from Chianti's Vigneto La Casuccia: elegant red fruit, herbaceous spice, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Castello Di Ama's Vigneto La Casuccia is a Super Tuscan expression of Chianti terroir, blending 80% Sangiovese with 20% Merlot to create a wine of medium-full body and refined structure. The 2019 vintage reveals primary notes of red cherry and plum, supported by herbaceous and spice-driven secondary characteristics. name: Pio Cesare  Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's heartland: structured tannins, rose florals, and tarry complexity.

name: Pio Cesare  Barbaresco DOCG
