Medium-full body and structured tannins frame a mineral-driven palate, with tertiary leather and tobacco complexity emerging as the wine evolves. A food-friendly classic suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Pio Cesare  Barbaresco il Bricco DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Il Bricco cru: red cherry, rose, and mineral complexity with fine tannins.
description (EN Store): Pio Cesare's Barbaresco il Bricco DOCG is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious terroirs. The 2020 vintage displays the classic Barbaresco profile: delicate red-fruit aromatics with floral notes and mineral undertones on the nose, followed by a palate of cherry, tar, and licorice with silky, well-integrated tannins. Medium-full body and bright acidity provide structure and food-friendliness. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with bottle age, adding complexity and elegance.

name: Domaine Philippe Colin  Santenay Clos Saint Jean
short_description (EN Store): Santenay Clos Saint Jean 2022: full-destemmed Pinot Noir from Côte de Beaune's southern edge, aged in 500 L barrels for energy over extraction.
description (EN Store):
When Philippe Colin split from Domaine Michel Colin-Déléger in 2004 — ending a family winemaking lineage traceable to the 1850s — he made a deliberate stylistic declaration: Pinot Noir built on tension, not timber. The Santenay Clos Saint Jean sits at the southern reach of the Côte de Beaune, where soils shift toward heavier limestone-laced clay, lending the wine a slightly firmer structural backbone than the silkier Chassagne parcels to the north, while the appellation's cooler microclimate preserves the aromatic lift that defines Colin's red wine philosophy.

Full destemming is non-negotiable here, followed by cold pre-fermentation maceration and moderate extraction — a deliberate counter to the extracted, oak-forward Santenay style some négociants favour. Aging proceeds exclusively in 500 L barrels (larger format than the standard 228 L Burgundy barrique) for 10–12 months, then a further 3–6 months in stainless steel vat before light filtration — the larger wood volume reducing oak imprint while maintaining textural development. The 500 L barrel is the technical signature across all Colin reds, a format choice that keeps the fruit primary and the tannin grain fine.

In 2022, village-level reds from the domaine averaged 89/100 across aggregated critics — a warm year that amplifies the red-fruit register without sacrificing the site's characteristic mineral edge.

name: Domaine Lafarge-Vial  Fleurie
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Gamay from Fleurie with silky red fruit, floral notes, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A classic expression of Fleurie, this 100% Gamay wine showcases the elegance and finesse characteristic of the appellation. The 2019 vintage reveals bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, complemented by delicate floral aromatics and subtle spice. On the palate, silky tannins and vibrant acidity create a balanced, approachable profile that emphasizes fruit purity over extraction. The wine's medium body and restrained oak treatment allow the varietal character to shine, making it an ideal companion for a wide range of cuisine.

name: Tenuta Lunelli  Carapace Sagrantino
short_description (EN Store): Sagrantino 100% from Umbria: structured, age-worthy red with dark fruit, spice, and mineral grip.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Lunelli Carapace Sagrantino 2017 is a single-varietal expression of Sagrantino, Umbria's signature red grape. This medium-full bodied wine displays dominant dark plum and black cherry on the nose, with supporting spice and tobacco notes from extended aging. The palate reveals firm, medium-high tannins and mineral-driven acidity, with tertiary leather and earth complexity emerging. A structured, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Domaine Jan Marc & Hugues  Pavelot Savigny-Les-Beaune 1er Cru "aux Guettes"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and vibrant acidity—a refined expression of Savigny-les-Beaune terroir.
description (EN Store): This 2022 Savigny-les-Beaune 1er Cru from Domaine Jan Marc & Hugues Pavelot showcases the classic elegance of Burgundy Pinot Noir. The wine opens with bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, supported by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the vineyard's limestone-rich soils. On the palate, silky tannins and high acidity create a refined structure, with stone fruit and mineral notes adding complexity. The medium body allows the wine's delicate flavors to shine without overwhelming the senses, making it an ideal companion to lighter proteins and vegetable-forward dishes. A wine that rewards contemplation and pairs beautifully with the seasons.

name: Philippe Pacalet  Gevrey-Chambertin
short_description (EN Store): Whole-cluster, zero-sulphur Gevrey from Jules Chauvet's direct disciple — clay-limestone terroir, unfined, unfiltered, 2022 warmth held in check by native ferment.
description (EN Store): Philippe Pacalet reportedly declined the winemaker role at Domaine de la Romanée-Conti to build his own négociant label in 2001 — a structural bet that whole-cluster transparency and zero-sulphur vinification could outperform prestige ownership. That conviction is the technical foundation of every wine he makes. Gevrey-Chambertin's clay-limestone soils deliver the appellation's characteristic iron-edged grip and dark-fruited depth; Pacalet's plots, farmed organically with yields held to roughly 31–35 hL/ha and vine density at 10,000 vines/ha, push that mineral tension further than the village norm. Whole-cluster fermentation in open wooden vats, indigenous yeasts only, pigeage twice daily for 2.5–3 weeks, and malolactic completed in 228 L French oak pièces at below 25% new oak keep extraction subtle and aromatics lifted. Aging runs 15–18 months on lees without racking, using a barrel-rolling system to keep lees in suspension — the reductive 'en réduction' method that builds saline, tensile texture. No fining, no filtration, and no SO₂ added until a micro-dose at bottling: the house's defining transgression in a village where interventionist élevage is standard. The 2022 vintage brought unusual weight to Pacalet's typically fine-boned style — riper fruit and more structure than 2021 — but the whole-cluster approach and no-sulphur fermentation preserve the freshness that distinguishes this address. Drink from 2026 through 2033.

name: Delas  Cotes Du Ventoux

name: Delas  Chateauneuf du pape "Haute Pierre"

name: Delas  Hermitage "Domaine des Tourettes"

short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Nautilus Estate's 2020 Pinot Noir from Marlborough showcases the region's signature elegance. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice. The wine's high acidity and medium tannin structure deliver a silky mouthfeel, making it versatile at the table and refreshing as an aperitif. A summer-friendly expression of New Zealand's premier cool-climate Pinot Noir terroir.

name: Casarena Single Vineyard Owen Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and structured tannins—elegant Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Casa Casarena's Single Vineyard Owen Cabernet Sauvignon from Luján de Cuyo showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and mineral backbone. Blackcurrant and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by cedar and cocoa notes from careful oak aging. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide structure suitable for cellaring or immediate enjoyment with hearty cuisine. A food-friendly expression of Argentine Cabernet craftsmanship.

name: Robert Mondavi  The Estate Oakville  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Oakville Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, vanilla oak, and structured tannins—classic Napa elegance.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Estate Oakville Cabernet Sauvignon showcases the hallmark richness and structure of Napa Valley's premier terroir. Ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the palate, supported by generous vanilla oak and cedar complexity. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine displays the aging potential and food-friendliness expected of a serious Cabernet. Cocoa and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, suggesting cellar-worthiness and versatility at the table.

name: Howard Park  Miamup Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Margaret River Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—elegant Australian classic.
description (EN Store): Howard Park's Miamup Cabernet Sauvignon from Margaret River's Wilyabrup subregion delivers the region's signature power and refinement. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and subtle mint notes. High tannin structure and medium acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility. A full-bodied expression of Australian Cabernet suited to premium red-meat cuisine and cellar investment.

name: Little Yering  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Yarra Valley cool-climate Shiraz from volcanic loam and gravel sites, wild-yeast fermented, screwcap-sealed — entry-tier wine earning its place via blind allocation.
description (EN Store): Yering Station's annual blind allocation tasting is the structural engine behind every bottle in the portfolio: all parcels are randomised and assessed without identity before tier assignment, meaning Little Yering earns its designation on merit each vintage rather than by formula. Sourced from Yering's five sites across Yarra Glen and Coldstream — volcanic loam slopes and well-drained gravels — the cool maritime climate at elevations up to 1,250 m across the GI slows ripening dramatically, preserving natural acidity and building phenolic structure before sugar accumulation catches up. Wild yeast fermentation drives the primary conversion, with hand-plunging during maceration for gentle tannin extraction rather than aggressive colour stripping. French oak aging at restrained new-oak percentages (consistent with the estate's 14–29% new-oak philosophy across tiers) keeps the fruit profile transparent rather than overlaid. Sealed under screwcap — a policy in place for over two decades — the wine retains the Yarra Valley's hallmark cool-climate lift through to the glass. What distinguishes Little Yering within its entry-level peer group is precisely that blind-allocation discipline: a parcel good enough for Reserve in a generous vintage may appear here in a leaner year, making the tier a moving target rather than a fixed address. The 2024 season's mid-March rains and extended cool finish allowed Shiraz tannin development well beyond typical early-ripening years — drink from release through 3–5 years.

name: Cono Sur  Tocornal Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Valley Cabernet from Chile's Pinot Noir specialist — temperature-controlled vinification, French oak aging at an entry price that punches above its tier.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1993 as an independent subsidiary of Concha y Toro, Cono Sur built its reputation on Burgundian Pinot Noir discipline — yet its Colchagua Valley Cabernet Sauvignon draws on the same estate infrastructure anchored by the Santa Elisa home winery in Chimbarongo, where the house manages 1,246 hectares across ten owned estates. Colchagua's continental interior climate — warm days moderated by Andean drainage winds, with alluvial clay and gravel soils retaining just enough moisture to sustain even ripening — drives the variety's characteristic dark-fruit density and firm tannic architecture. Vinification proceeds in temperature-controlled stainless steel tanks (total winery capacity 12 million litres), preserving primary fruit character before the wine moves to French oak barrels for structured aging, consistent with the house's entry-red protocol. The Tocornal tier sits below the 20 Barrels and Reserva lines, making it the broadest-access expression in a portfolio that otherwise dedicates its most technical resources to Pinot Noir — an unusual inversion where the flagship variety is not the one on this label. Colchagua's warm-season consistency delivers a reliably approachable current-vintage expression; drink within 2–3 years of release for peak primary fruit.

name: Cono Sur  Bicicleta Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Valley Cabernet from Chile's sustainability-first house — stainless-fermented, screwcap-sealed since 2002, built for immediate drinking.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1993 as an independent subsidiary of Concha y Toro, Cono Sur made its name by breaking conventions — most visibly in 2002, when it became the first Chilean winery to seal quality wines under screwcap, a decision that protected aromatic freshness at a time when cork was still considered non-negotiable for serious bottlings. The Bicicleta Cabernet Sauvignon carries that same pragmatic philosophy: no oak aging, no pretension, just clean varietal expression from Colchagua Valley fruit. Colchagua's valley floor delivers warm days moderated by Andean drainage, with alluvial soils — gravel over clay — that concentrate berry character while retaining enough structure to keep the wine from going flat. Vinification is entirely in temperature-controlled stainless steel tanks, preserving primary cassis and dried herb aromatics without the overlay of wood. The screwcap closure, still the house standard for early-drinking reds, eliminates cork taint risk and locks in freshness from bottling to glass. What sets this apart within the Bicicleta tier is its position inside a portfolio where every winemaking decision — from geese-managed organic blocks to Burgundian pigeage on Pinot Noir — is made with deliberate intent. Drink within 2–3 years of vintage for peak primary fruit expression.

name: Cono Sur  Single Vineyard Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Old-vine Casablanca Pinot Noir from Fundo El Triángulo (planted 1989), cold-macerated and hand-pigeaged in open tanks on granitic coastal soils.
description (EN Store):
Cono Sur was founded in 1993 with a single audacious mandate: make Chile's first world-class Pinot Noir. The Single Vineyard expression draws from Fundo El Triángulo in Casablanca Valley, where vines planted in 1989 root into red clay, sand, and granitic soils shaped by Humboldt Current fog and cool coastal breezes — conditions that compress the ripening window and preserve natural acidity in a way warmer Chilean valleys cannot replicate.

Vinification follows Burgundian discipline: cold maceration for colour and aromatic extraction, followed by foot-treading and manual pigeage in small open tanks — human energy deliberately chosen over mechanical pump-overs to avoid over-extraction of tannin from Pinot's thin skins. Aging proceeds in French oak at a duration and new-oak percentage calibrated to the single-vineyard tier, preserving site transparency over wood imprint.

What separates this from peers is the vine age: El Triángulo's 1989 plantings are among the oldest Pinot Noir blocks in Casablanca, predating the valley's commercial reputation, delivering concentration and root depth that younger sites in the appellation cannot match. The current vintage delivers taut red-fruit character and saline mineral tension characteristic of cool Casablanca seasons, drinking well now through four to six years.

name: Cono Sur  Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Maipo Cabernet aged ~17 months in French oak — from Chile's sustainability-first, Pinot Noir-obsessed house that rarely spotlights this variety.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1993 as an independent subsidiary of Concha y Toro, Cono Sur built its reputation almost entirely on Pinot Noir — which makes this Single Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon from Maipo Valley a deliberate departure, a house that knows Burgundy intimately turning its attention to Chile's most storied Cabernet appellation. Maipo's upper reaches deliver the conditions that define the valley's reputation: deep alluvial gravel over clay subsoils, Andean snowmelt irrigation, and a wide diurnal temperature swing that preserves acidity while allowing full phenolic development in Cabernet Sauvignon. Vinification follows the 20 Barrels tier protocol: French oak barrels for approximately 17 months, a regime long enough to integrate tannin and add structural complexity without overwhelming the site's natural cassis and dried herb character. Cono Sur's 1,246 hectares across ten owned estates are managed under certified organic and sustainability programs — geese for pest management, drip irrigation, solar energy — giving the fruit a cleaner, less-intervened baseline before oak contact. The special feature here is the producer's identity itself: a house whose entire premium infrastructure was engineered for Pinot Noir applying that same precision to Maipo Cabernet, with no commercial pressure to conform to a house red style. Drink now through 4–6 years for primary fruit expression; structured enough to reward short cellaring.

name: Cono Sur  Single Vineyard Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Carmenère from Cachapoal Valley — Chile's adopted red grape at its most site-specific, from Cono Sur's El Encanto estate.
description (EN Store): Cono Sur was founded in 1993 as an independent subsidiary of Concha y Toro with a mandate to push Chilean varieties beyond convention — and while the house built its reputation on Pinot Noir, its commitment to Carmenère at the single-vineyard level reflects the same terroir-first logic applied to Chile's most distinctive red grape. El Encanto estate (101 ha), dedicated to red varieties including Carmenère, Cabernet Franc, Grenache, and Carignan, sits within the Cachapoal Valley, where Andean-influenced soils and diurnal temperature swings slow ripening and preserve the variety's characteristic green-herb tension alongside dark fruit. The valley's position — closer to the Andes than coastal appellations — delivers well-drained alluvial soils and reliable sun exposure, conditions that push Carmenère toward full phenolic maturity without sacrificing freshness. Vinification follows temperature-controlled fermentation with French oak aging consistent with the Single Vineyard tier; the house's broader winemaking philosophy favors minimal intervention and screwcap closures (adopted 2002, a Chilean first) for aromatic preservation on select lines. What distinguishes this expression within the portfolio is its single-estate provenance — a deliberate break from the multi-valley blending that defines most Chilean Carmenère at this price tier. Drink now through 4–6 years for primary fruit and savory herb character at their most expressive.

name: Buronga Hill Estate  Cabernet sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Barossa Valley Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Buronga Hill Estate's Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley delivers a classic full-bodied expression of the varietal. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit on the nose are complemented by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The palate shows excellent structure with high tannins and medium-high acidity, providing grip and aging potential. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge, adding complexity and sophistication. This wine rewards pairing with grilled red meats, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Rapel Valley Cabernet-Syrah blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and food-friendly medium-full structure.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Rapel Valley marries 85% Cabernet Sauvignon with 15% Syrah to create a medium-full-bodied red with classical structure. Dominant blackcurrant and plum fruit on the nose give way to secondary cedar and black pepper notes, with subtle cocoa undertones from oak aging. The palate shows balanced medium tannin and acidity, supporting a food-friendly profile suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb dishes. Tertiary tobacco and earth notes suggest modest aging potential.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Morellino Di Scansano Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Tuscan Riserva with dark cherry, plum, and herbaceous spice; structured tannin and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Fattoria Le Pupille's Morellino di Scansano Riserva is a classically structured Tuscan red anchored by 90% Sangiovese with 10% Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine opens with dominant red and dark fruit—cherry and plum—supported by herbaceous and spice notes from both grape and oak aging. Tertiary earth, leather, and tobacco complexity emerge with bottle age, reflecting the Riserva designation and Maremma terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a food-friendly yet age-worthy expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Morellino di Scansano DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Tuscan blend with dark fruit, mineral edge, and food-friendly structure from Maremma.
description (EN Store): Fattoria Le Pupille's Morellino di Scansano DOCG is a Sangiovese-dominant blend anchored by 70% of the estate's core varietal, with 15% Alicante and 15% Malvasia Nera adding depth and complexity. This medium-full-bodied red displays ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, supported by earthy minerality and dried herb notes characteristic of Maremma's coastal terroir. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make it an approachable yet structured wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Le Coste  Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Lazio blend with red cherry, plum, and herbaceous charm—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Le Coste Rosso is a Sangiovese-dominant blend from Lazio, Italy, anchored by 90% Sangiovese with supporting local varieties (Ciliegiolo, Cannaiolo, Vaiano, Colorino). The 2020 vintage expresses classic red-fruit character—cherry and plum—with herbaceous undertones and subtle earthy complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine a natural companion to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes. A food-friendly expression of central Italian winemaking tradition.

name: Amuse Bouche  2019
short_description (EN Store): Napa Merlot-Cabernet Franc blend with ripe plum, soft tannins, and approachable elegance.
description (EN Store): A Napa Valley red blend anchored by Merlot and Cabernet Franc, this 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full frame with integrated tannins and balanced acidity. The nose opens with ripe plum and dark berry, supported by subtle vanilla and herbaceous undertones. On the palate, juicy red fruit and cranberry notes combine with a soft texture that makes the wine accessible young while retaining enough structure for food pairing versatility. Ideal for casual entertaining or weeknight dining alongside grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Au Bon Climat  Santa Barbara County
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Santa Barbara Pinot Noir blend with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and refreshing acidity—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Au Bon Climat's Santa Barbara County blend showcases the region's signature elegance through ripe red fruit and raspberry notes balanced by crisp acidity and silky tannin structure. The wine displays fresh, approachable character with subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice on the finish. Ideal for warm-weather entertaining, this 2023 vintage captures the essence of California's cooler coastal terroir—a wine that invites contemplation with each sip.

name: Freemark Abbey  Napa Valley Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Freemark Abbey's 2016 Napa Valley Merlot delivers a classic expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe plum and dark berry fruit on the nose. The palate is juicy and medium-full bodied, with integrated tannins and a hint of vanilla from oak aging. Herbaceous undertones add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Layer Cake  Cabernet Sauvignon - Alexander Valley
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, vanilla oak, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Layer Cake Cabernet Sauvignon from Alexander Valley delivers a straightforward, fruit-forward expression of California's signature varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by subtle vanilla oak and cedar notes that add complexity without overwhelming the wine's core character. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine an accessible choice for everyday enjoyment and casual entertaining, with enough structure to pair confidently with grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Lillian  Syrah
short_description (EN Store): California Shiraz with dark plum, spice, and smoky depth—a full-bodied red for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Lillian's 2016 Syrah from California delivers a full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit with pronounced black pepper spice. Secondary smoke and cocoa notes add complexity and structure, while tertiary leather and earth tones suggest careful aging potential. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame the wine for hearty red meat preparations, from grilled steaks to slow-braised beef and game.

name: Opus One  'Overture' Napa Valley Red Wine
short_description (EN Store): Opus One's second-label NV blend: Oakville gravelly clay loam, gravity-flow vinification, 100% new French oak — Pauillac discipline in a Napa address.
description (EN Store):
When Baron Philippe de Rothschild and Robert Mondavi formalized their joint venture in 1980, they transferred a 35-acre block of the historic To Kalon vineyard — the so-called 'Q Block' — into the new estate, anchoring Opus One to one of Napa's most storied alluvial fans. Overture is the non-vintage second selection, assembled from lots that did not enter the grand vin, giving it access to the same Oakville estate fruit without the vintage-specific constraints of the flagship.

The four estate parcels sit on the western bench of Oakville AVA, where alluvial material washed from the Mayacamas Mountains produces gravelly clay loam with volcanic components. Cool afternoon air drawn through from San Pablo Bay creates a sharp diurnal shift that preserves acidity in fruit that would otherwise over-ripen at this latitude. Vines are farmed organically on low Guyot trellising with tight spacing, and grapes are hand-harvested before dawn.

Vinification follows the flagship protocol exactly: each parcel ferments separately in one of approximately 50 dedicated stainless-steel tanks, with all movement by gravity — no pumps at any stage. Native yeasts isolated from each vineyard site drive fermentation. Aging proceeds in 100% new French oak in the semi-circular underground Grand Chai, followed by additional bottle aging before release. Fining is by fresh egg whites; filtration status is undisclosed.

What distinguishes Overture categorically is its NV construction: blending across multiple harvests allows winemaker Michael Silacci to target a consistent structural profile — Bordeaux Left Bank restraint, graphite-edged tannin architecture — rather than chasing any single year's character. The result is a Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant Bordeaux blend that drinks closer to a Pauillac than to the Oakville blockbuster norm.

name: Protest  Sonoma County Red Bland
short_description (EN Store): Syrah-led Sonoma blend with dark plum, black pepper, and smoky spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Protest's Sonoma County Red Blend is a Syrah-dominant cuvée (89%) anchored by Malbec (7%) and Zinfandel (4%), crafted to deliver approachable complexity and broad food compatibility. The primary character centers on dark plum and blackberry fruit with assertive black pepper, while secondary smoke and spice notes add textural depth. Tertiary leather and earth tones emerge on the palate, suggesting modest aging potential. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a reliable choice for grilled meats, braised beef, and smoked preparations.

name: Realm Cellars  'The Bard' Napa Valley
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley red with balanced red fruit, dark plum, and subtle oak—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Realm Cellars' 'The Bard' is a medium-full bodied red wine from Napa Valley, crafted in a food-friendly style. The wine opens with dominant red fruit and dark plum character, supported by black cherry notes and a balanced mid-palate. Secondary oak and vanilla add subtle complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. Medium tannin and acidity make this wine versatile at the table, pairing well with grilled meats, braised dishes, roasted poultry, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Reference Book  Napa Valley Red Wine
short_description (EN Store): 2011 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon: structured, age-worthy red with dark fruit, cedar, and elegant tannin.
description (EN Store): A classic Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon from the 2011 vintage, this wine exhibits the hallmark structure and depth expected from the region's premier terroir. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—dominates the primary aromatics, supported by subtle cedar and black pepper notes. The medium-full body and balanced tannin provide a framework for food pairing, while emerging tertiary characteristics of tobacco and leather suggest complexity gained through bottle age. Suitable for cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial dishes.

name: Starmont  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic California elegance.
description (EN Store): Starmont's 2021 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon is a classically structured Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon with supporting Petit Verdot, Merlot, and Malbec. The wine displays a full body with high tannin grip and medium-high acidity, delivering ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, complemented by cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Secondary notes of cocoa and black pepper add complexity, while tertiary tobacco and leather suggest age-worthiness. A food-friendly expression of Napa Valley's signature style.

name: Z88.88 Napa Valley Red Wine
short_description (EN Store): 2016 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): A classic Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon from the 2016 vintage, this wine displays the hallmark richness and structure expected from the region's premier terroir. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The wine's full body and high tannin structure suggest careful aging potential, with emerging tobacco and leather notes hinting at tertiary complexity. Ideal for cellaring or pairing with substantial red meat dishes and aged cheeses.

name: Layer Cake  Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with dark fruit, cocoa spice, and supple tannins—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Layer Cake Malbec from Mendoza delivers the signature dark-fruit intensity and approachable structure that define Argentina's flagship varietal. Ripe plum and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by cocoa and warm spice from careful oak aging. Supple, medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and rich, savory fare. A versatile, well-crafted expression suited to both casual dining and table-friendly entertaining.

name: Aldridge  'Rams Head' Shiraz Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Shiraz-Cabernet blend from South Australia with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Aldridge's Rams Head Shiraz Cabernet is a full-bodied red wine from South Australia that showcases the classic partnership of Cabernet Sauvignon's structure with Shiraz's ripe fruit character. The wine opens with prominent blackcurrant and dark plum aromatics, complemented by cedary oak and black pepper spice. A firm, high-tannin backbone provides excellent aging potential and food-pairing versatility, with secondary notes of oak and vanilla adding complexity. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes emerge with bottle age, creating a wine suited to premium red-meat cuisine and substantial cheese boards.

name: Layer Cake  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant McLaren Vale Shiraz with dark fruit intensity, peppery spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Layer Cake Shiraz from McLaren Vale delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by black pepper spice and subtle licorice notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured, with enough complexity for serious food pairing. Leather and earthy undertones emerge on the finish, adding depth to an otherwise fruit-forward profile.

name: Corte Viola  Cabernet Sauvignon IGT
short_description (EN Store): Veneto Cabernet Sauvignon with dark berry depth, oak structure, and food-friendly tannins—excellent value for red meat and cheese.
description (EN Store): Corte Viola's Cabernet Sauvignon IGT from Veneto delivers a compelling balance of ripe dark fruit and measured oak influence. The wine opens with aromas of blackcurrant and black cherry compote, supported by subtle herbaceous undertones. On the palate, medium-full body and structured tannins frame the fruit, with oak providing vanilla and toast complexity without overwhelming the varietal character. The finish is savory and persistent, making this an approachable yet serious expression suited to classic red-wine cuisine—grilled meats, braised dishes, aged cheeses, and rich sauces.

name: Corte Viola  Merlot IGT
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Veneto Merlot with dark berry fruit, balanced oak, and food-friendly structure. Excellent value for everyday red wine enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Corte Viola's Merlot IGT represents a well-crafted expression of this versatile varietal from Italy's Veneto region. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark fruit character—blackberry and plum compote dominate the nose and palate—complemented by subtle oak influence and balanced tannin structure. The palate is inviting and approachable, with enough substance to pair confidently with meat-based dishes, braised preparations, and aged cheeses, yet retains the elegance expected of quality Italian reds. A food-friendly wine that delivers genuine pleasure at its price point.

name: Corte Viola  Rubicone Sangiovese IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese from Veneto with dark berry compote, balanced oak, and food-friendly structure. Excellent value.
description (EN Store): Corte Viola's Rubicone Sangiovese IGT is a medium-full bodied red from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from 100% Sangiovese. The wine opens with a discrete but inviting nose of dark berry compote and black cherry, leading to an enveloping palate marked by plum, subtle oak integration, and well-balanced tannins. Earthy undertones emerge on the finish, adding complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. This is a classically food-friendly expression, equally at home with braised meats, tomato-based pasta, or aged cheeses.

name: San Filippo  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino with dark fruit, cedar, and earthy complexity—a classic Tuscan expression of power and refinement.
description (EN Store): San Filippo's 2016 Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a structured, age-worthy Tuscan red built on the foundation of Brunello's signature dark plum and black cherry fruit. Cedar and leather notes emerge on the mid-palate, supported by medium-high tannins and a mineral backbone. Tertiary tobacco and dried-fruit complexity suggest this wine will develop further with bottle age, rewarding patient cellaring with additional layers of earth and spice.

name: San Filippo  Brunello di Montalcino Le Lucere
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello with dark cherry, structured tannins, and earthy complexity. A classic Montalcino expression.
description (EN Store): San Filippo Brunello di Montalcino Le Lucere 2016 is a single-varietal expression of Sangiovese Grosso from Tuscany's most prestigious red wine region. The wine displays a medium-full body with medium-high tannins and balanced acidity, offering dark cherry and plum on the nose with supporting cedar and spice from careful oak aging. Tertiary notes of earth and subtle tobacco emerge on the palate, reflecting the wine's structure and aging potential. A food-friendly Brunello suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Bodegas Muga  Reserva Rioja DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tempranillo-led Rioja Reserva with dark plum, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Food-friendly, structured, elegant.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Muga's Reserva from Rioja Alta showcases the classic elegance of a Tempranillo-dominant blend (70% Tempranillo, 20% Garnacha, 10% Mazuelo and Graciano). Deep plum and blackcurrant fruit dominate the nose, supported by cedar and subtle oak spice from careful aging. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and refined tannins, revealing layers of tobacco and leather that speak to the wine's maturity and complexity. A dry, bold expression with a long finish, this Reserva pairs beautifully with grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Bodegas Muga  Selección Especial Rioja DOC
short_description (EN Store): Rioja Alta blend of Tempranillo, Grenache & Mazuelo with dark plum, cedar & spice—food-friendly, structured red.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Muga's Selección Especial is a classically structured Rioja Alta blend anchored by 70% Tempranillo, with supporting Grenache, Mazuelo, and Graciano. The wine displays a deep red hue and opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry aromatics, complemented by subtle cedar and spice from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and medium tannin provide backbone without heaviness, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food compatibility. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, suggesting modest age-worthiness. A versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Chateau Julien  Haut-Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc blend with ripe plum, cherry, and soft tannins—a refined yet approachable Bordeaux.
description (EN Store): Château Julien's Haut-Médoc is a classic Bordeaux blend anchored by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot. The 2016 vintage delivers a medium-full body with ripe dark-fruit character—plum and black cherry dominate the nose, complemented by subtle herbaceous notes and a whisper of vanilla oak. The palate is juicy and well-balanced, with integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. A wine of approachable elegance, suited to both everyday dining and special occasions.

name: Chateau Les Cabannes  St.Emilion Grand Cru AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion Grand Cru blending Merlot and Cabernet Franc with ripe plum, cherry, and subtle cocoa complexity.
description (EN Store): Château Les Cabannes Saint-Émilion Grand Cru presents a harmonious Bordeaux blend anchored by Merlot with Cabernet Franc support. The 2021 vintage delivers a medium-full frame with integrated tannins and approachable texture. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the primary aromatics, supported by subtle berry notes and a hint of cranberry acidity. Vanilla and cocoa from careful oak aging provide secondary complexity, while herbaceous undertones add freshness and structure. name: Domaine Thibault Liger-Belair  Bourgogne Rouge “les Grands Chaillots”
short_description (EN Store): Nine-generation négociant family turned vigneron; biodynamic Côte de Nuits Pinot, horse-plowed limestone-flint soils, unfined/unfiltered, 2020.
description (EN Store): Thibault Liger-Belair became the first vigneron in nine generations of a family that once owned La Tâche Grand Cru — sold to DRC in 1929 — and was listed on the Lyon Stock Exchange as a négociant house tracing to 1720. When he founded the domaine in 2001, he chose to make wine rather than trade it, and every technical decision since has been a deliberate break from the extraction-driven Nuits-Saint-Georges archetype his ancestors profited from. Les Grands Chaillots is sourced from Côte de Nuits soils of chaille — limestone-flint mix with clay, the same geological backbone that gives the appellation its structural tension even at the regional Bourgogne level. Vineyards are plowed by horse under full biodynamic management, with grapes triple-sorted (at vine, before and after destemmer) and fermented with native yeast in open-top vessels using minimal pumpovers — infusion over extraction. Aging runs in oak barrels with village-level one-third new oak, lightly charred over 5–7 minutes, with a mandatory 3-year wood-drying period Thibault imposes to soften tannin integration; bottled unfined and unfiltered. The special feature here: a regional Bourgogne Rouge built on the same biodynamic protocols and cellar restraint as the domaine's Grand Cru parcels — no concessions for appellation level. In the warm 2020 vintage, Thibault deliberately avoided over-extraction to preserve freshness and energy, yielding a Pinot of fine promise per Winehog.

description (EN Store): Eugenio Collavini's Forresco Rosso is a Friuli-Venezia Giulia blend anchored by two Refosco clones (80%) and Pignolo (20%), crafted in the Colli Orientali del Friuli subregion. The 2013 vintage expresses the region's characteristic red-fruit intensity and peppery spice, with a medium-full body and balanced tannin structure that rewards both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring. Dark cherry and plum dominate the palate, supported by herbaceous and black-pepper notes; mineral and earthy undertones emerge on the finish, reflecting the terroir's limestone-rich soils.

name: Ca’Dei Frati  Ronchedone Vino Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Lombardy red with balanced red fruit, plum, and cherry—a food-friendly Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Ca Dei Frati's Ronchedone Vino Rosso is a medium-full-bodied red from Lombardy, Italy, built on a foundation of ripe red fruit, dark plum, and cherry character. The wine carries medium tannin and acidity, making it an approachable yet structured expression suited to a broad range of Italian and Mediterranean cuisine. Subtle oak and spice notes provide secondary complexity without overwhelming the primary fruit profile.

name: Louis Jadot  Pommard Clos de la Commaraine Premier Cru
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and vibrant acidity—a refined expression of Pommard's terroir.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Pommard Clos de la Commaraine Premier Cru is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir that showcases the region's hallmark balance of fruit, earth, and mineral complexity. The 2016 vintage reveals bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, supported by subtle mushroom and earthy undertones that speak to the vineyard's limestone-rich soils. Medium body and silky tannins frame a palate of fresh acidity, making this wine both approachable in its youth and capable of graceful evolution. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, mushroom-forward dishes, and lighter red meat preparations.

name: Louis Jadot  Volnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and bright acidity—a refined expression of Volnay's terroir.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's 2015 Volnay is a classic expression of Burgundy's most elegant Pinot Noir appellation. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry, supported by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the region's limestone-rich soils. A silky mid-palate and bright acidity define the structure, while mineral complexity and forest-floor notes emerge on the finish. This is a wine of restraint and finesse—ideal for those seeking Pinot Noir's delicate balance rather than power.

name: 9 Lives Reserve Delirious Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins—a bold, age-worthy expression.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's 9 Lives Reserve Delirious Cabernet Sauvignon from Curicó Valley presents a full-bodied, classically structured red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary aromatics, while black pepper and subtle vanilla add complexity on the mid-palate. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity suggest careful oak aging and potential for further development. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the finish, lending savory depth and minerality characteristic of well-made Chilean Cabernet.

name: Allegrini  La Grola IGT
short_description (EN Store): Allegrini's benchmark Valpolicella blend: dark cherry, cocoa, and spice with velvety tannins. Food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Allegrini's La Grola represents the producer's mastery of Corvina-based blending in Valpolicella Classico. This 80% Corvina and 20% Syrah expression delivers a medium-full frame anchored by dominant dark cherry and black fruit compote on the nose, with supporting cocoa and warm baking spice from thoughtful oak aging. The palate is inviting and structured, with velvety tannins that frame rich dark fruit without heaviness. A benchmark wine for the region, La Grola pairs beautifully with braised beef, aged cheeses, and hearty Mediterranean fare.

name: Cono Sur  20 Barrels Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Casablanca Pinot Noir aged 14 months across 70% French oak barrels + 30% large foudres — foot-trodden, hand-punched, small open tanks.
description (EN Store): When Cono Sur inaugurated a dedicated Pinot Noir annex winery in 1999 — then expanded it with a 530,000-litre premium cellar in 2009 — the investment signalled a structural commitment to Burgundian discipline inside a New World operation. The 20 Barrels expression draws on Fundo El Triángulo in Casablanca Valley, where Pinot Noir vines planted in 1989 grow in red clay and granitic sand over a coastal subsoil cooled daily by Humboldt Current fog, slowing ripening and preserving natural acidity. Fermentation proceeds in small open tanks via foot-treading and manual pigeage, prioritising human extraction over mechanical intervention — a deliberate choice that limits tannin aggression in a thin-skinned variety. Aging runs 14 months across a split regime: 70% French oak barrels plus 30% large French oak foudres, the foudre fraction moderating new-wood imprint while sustaining slow micro-oxygenation. What separates 20 Barrels from peers at this price tier is precisely that foudre component — most Chilean Pinot Noir at this level defaults to 100% barrique, compressing fruit behind oak; here the larger vessel preserves Casablanca's cool-site transparency. The 2017 vintage, drawn from a season of measured coastal temperatures, delivers taut red-fruit character and saline mineral lift with a drinking window extending comfortably through the mid-2020s.

name: Cono Sur  20 Barrels Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Cab aged ~17 months in French oak — the rule-breaking Pinot house turns its discipline to Chile's Andean red heartland.
description (EN Store): Cono Sur was founded in 1993 as a Pinot Noir specialist, yet its 20 Barrels tier proves the house applies the same exacting standards to Cabernet Sauvignon — a deliberate statement that Burgundian rigour belongs in Maipo as much as Casablanca. Maipo Valley, Chile's Andean benchmark for Cabernet, delivers alluvial soils over clay and gravel, with diurnal temperature swings driven by cold air descending from the Andes at night, preserving acidity and slowing phenolic development through the growing season. In the cellar, the wine undergoes approximately 17 months of aging in French oak barrels — a notably extended regime for this tier, and longer than the 14-month program applied to the flagship 20 Barrels Pinot Noir. New-oak percentage is not publicly disclosed, but the barrel-only format (no foudre component, unlike the Pinot Noir sibling) signals a deliberate push toward structural density and oak integration. The 20 Barrels name itself signals strict selection: only the finest lots from the vintage are retained, a production philosophy that runs counter to the house's high-volume Bicicleta identity. What makes this expression unusual is the producer context — a winery whose entire identity and consultant investment (Martin Prieur of Domaine Jacques Prieur) was built around Pinot Noir, here channelling that same precision into Chile's most competitive red variety. The 2019 vintage in Maipo delivered a warm, dry growing season with good concentration and firm tannic architecture, best approached from 2024 onward with a drinking window extending comfortably to 2030.

name: Cono Sur  Ocio Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Old-vine Casablanca Pinot Noir from Fundo El Triángulo (planted 1989), foot-trodden and pigeage in open tanks, 14 months in 70% new French oak.
description (EN Store):
When Burgundy consultant Martin Prieur of Domaine Jacques Prieur was brought to Chile — a first for any New World producer — the brief was to build a Pinot Noir program capable of competing at the highest international level. Ocio is the result of that ambition, sourced from Fundo El Triángulo in Casablanca Valley, where old vines planted in 1989 grow in red clay and granitic soils cooled by Humboldt Current fog and morning marine breezes that slow ripening and preserve natural acidity.

Vinification follows a Burgundian discipline rare at this scale: cold maceration for colour and aromatic extraction, followed by foot-treading and manual pigeage in small open-top tanks — human energy deliberately chosen over mechanical extraction to protect the grape's delicate cell structure. The wine then ages 14 months in French oak, 70% new barrique, a regime that builds textural density without masking the site's cool-climate transparency.

What separates Ocio from its New World peers is the combination of Burgundian grand cru methodology applied to vines now over 35 years old in a valley that was still considered experimental Pinot Noir territory when they were planted. The 2018 vintage in Casablanca delivered measured ripeness with sustained acidity, producing a wine that is already expressive but carries the structural architecture for continued development through 2028–2032.

name: Cono Sur  Silencio Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Cab from Chile's Pinot Noir house — aged ~17 months in French oak, natural cork, icon-tier ambition from an unexpected source.
description (EN Store): Cono Sur was founded in 1993 as a Pinot Noir specialist, yet its Silencio Cabernet Sauvignon represents the house's deliberate push to prove that the same Burgundian discipline applied to its flagship variety translates to Chile's most celebrated Cabernet appellation. Maipo Valley's Andean-facing sites deliver the alluvial gravel-over-clay soils and diurnal temperature swings that concentrate cassis character while preserving structural acidity — conditions that make the valley Chile's canonical address for the variety. Aging runs approximately 17 months in French oak barrels, a duration that exceeds most Chilean Cabernet Reserva programs and aligns Silencio closer to the house's icon-tier 20 Barrels range in ambition. The wine is sealed under natural cork — a deliberate departure from Cono Sur's own 2002 screwcap revolution, signalling that this expression is built for bottle evolution rather than early drinking. What sets Silencio apart within the portfolio is its position as a premium Cabernet Sauvignon from a producer whose entire identity and technical infrastructure was engineered around Pinot Noir — the French oak program, the premium cellar capacity, and the winemaking precision are all borrowed from that tradition and redirected here. The 2017 vintage in Maipo delivered a warm, even growing season with good phenolic ripeness; the wine is approachable now but carries the oak integration and fruit density to develop further over the next several years.

name: Rawson's Retreat  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Barossa Merlot with ripe blackberry, plum, and vanilla oak—a versatile red for everyday dining.
description (EN Store):
Rawson's Retreat Merlot from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a fruit-forward, medium-full-bodied red wine with immediate appeal. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla and oak from winemaking, with subtle black pepper spice and cocoa undertones. The wine's balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure make it an accessible choice for both casual meals and more structured dining occasions.

This is a classic food-friendly Merlot—versatile enough for grilled meats, braised beef, roasted chicken, and tomato-based pasta dishes. The wine's approachable profile and reliable quality position it as a dependable everyday red.

name: Rawson's Retreat  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and subtle oak—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and hearty fare.
description (EN Store): Rawson's Retreat Shiraz from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackberry and plum fruit. Secondary oak and vanilla notes add structure and warmth, while a subtle smoke and pepper character emerges on the finish. Approachable tannins and balanced acidity make this a versatile choice for casual dining and grilled preparations.

name: Gallo Family Vineyards  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, smooth tannins, and food-friendly character.
description (EN Store): A classic California Cabernet Sauvignon offering ripe black cherry and blackcurrant fruit with subtle oak influence. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled steaks, braised beef, or casual roasted chicken. The wine's straightforward fruit-forward profile and accessible structure position it as a reliable everyday red for both seasoned collectors and newcomers to Cabernet.

name: Gallo Family Vineyards  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Merlot with ripe plum and cherry, soft tannins, and food-friendly character.
description (EN Store): Gallo Family Vineyards Merlot is a classic California red wine built on ripe red and dark fruit character with a smooth, medium-full body. The wine delivers straightforward plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by subtle oak and vanilla notes from winemaking. Soft, integrated tannins and balanced acidity make this wine an accessible choice for everyday enjoyment and versatile food pairing.

name: Masseria Li Veli  Askos Susumaniello
short_description (EN Store): Susumaniello from Salento: dark fruit, smoke & pepper. Smooth, food-friendly Puglian red.
description (EN Store): Masseria Li Veli's Askos Susumaniello is a single-varietal expression of Puglia's distinctive Susumaniello grape, sourced from the Salento subregion. The wine displays a bold, fruit-forward character anchored by dark cherry and raspberry, with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper that add complexity and spice. Medium-full body and rounded, approachable tannins make this wine eminently drinkable across a range of occasions—from casual pizza and pasta nights to grilled meats and roasted poultry. The wine's smooth texture and lack of aggressive tannins position it as an accessible yet characterful red from southern Italy.

name: Masseria Li Veli  Passamante Negroamaro IGT
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Negroamaro from Puglia with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Food-friendly, full-bodied, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Masseria Li Veli's Passamante Negroamaro is a characterful Puglian red built on dark fruit intensity and subtle complexity. The palate opens with ripe black cherry and raspberry, supported by a distinctive peppery spice and smoke undertone that adds dimension without overshadowing the fruit. Medium tannins are fully rounded and approachable, making this wine equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or casual pizza nights. A rustic, food-friendly expression of Negroamaro that delivers genuine pleasure at its price point.

name: Ricasoli  Casalferro IGT (Merlot)
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Merlot with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and herbaceous charm—a food-friendly red for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Ricasoli's Casalferro IGT showcases the approachable side of Tuscan Merlot. The intense ruby color signals ripe fruit concentration, with primary notes of black cherry and dark plum leading to subtle herbaceous and spice undertones. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine an ideal companion to braised beef, roasted poultry, and tomato-based pasta dishes. A versatile, well-crafted expression suited to both casual dining and modest special occasions.

name: Chateau Meyre  Cru Bourgeois Haut-Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc blend of Merlot and Cabernet with ripe plum, cedar, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Château Meyre's Cru Bourgeois Haut-Médoc is a classically structured Bordeaux blend anchored by Merlot (44%) and Cabernet Sauvignon (40%), with supporting Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc. The 2019 vintage expresses the region's signature dark-fruit character—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar and subtle tobacco notes from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine food-friendly across a range of preparations, from grilled meats to braised dishes. The wine's mineral undertones and emerging tertiary complexity suggest modest cellaring potential.

name: Angove  Long Row Cabernet Sauvignon (187 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Cabernet from South Australia's Riverland: ripe dark berries, cedar, and structured tannins in a food-friendly 187 ml format.
description (EN Store): Angove's Long Row Cabernet Sauvignon represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture and authentic regional expression. This 187 ml single-serve delivers the signature profile of South Australian Cabernet: ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, underpinned by subtle cedar and earthy minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare. A versatile, food-friendly expression from one of Australia's pioneering organic producers.

name: Caricature  Red Wine
short_description (EN Store): Bold California blend of Cabernet, Zinfandel & Petit Sirah with dark fruit, cedar & high tannins.
description (EN Store): Caricature's 2017 red blend showcases the power of California's diverse terroir through a carefully composed mix of Cabernet Sauvignon, Zinfandel, Petit Sirah, Sangiovese, and Tannat. The wine opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum, layered with bright black cherry notes that define the primary impression. Cedar and oak emerge on the mid-palate, supported by structured black pepper spice, while the wine's full body and high tannin structure demand substantial food pairings. With age, tobacco and leather complexity develop, adding depth and sophistication to this serious, food-friendly red.

name: Henschke  Hill Of Grace Shiraz, Eden Valley
short_description (EN Store): Own-rooted 'Grandfather' vines planted c.1860 on Parrot Hill schist-laced loam; 2019's brutal 44.3°C heat and frost slashed yields to 10–15% of normal, forging a wine of surgical purity.
description (EN Store): When Cyril Henschke bottled the first Hill of Grace in 1958, Australia's fine-wine trade was still dominated by blends and fortifieds — a single-vineyard Shiraz from ungrafted, pre-phylloxera vines was an act of quiet transgression that effectively invented a category. The 'Grandfather' block at the heart of this wine has grown on its original rootstock since approximately 1860, surviving on Parrot Hill, 4 km northwest of Henschke Cellars, under a sharecropping agreement that remains one of Australian viticulture's most unusual arrangements. The 8-hectare site sits at 400 m elevation on alluvial sandy loam grading to deep silty loam with patches of limestone and schist, dry-grown without irrigation; the schist fraction drives the wine's tensile mineral spine while the loam's water-retention moderates the old vines' already minuscule yields. In 2019, record heat of 44.3°C, combined with frost and hail, reduced the crop to 10–15% of a normal harvest — the resulting fruit is concentrated to the point of severity, yet retains the cool-climate Eden Valley precision that separates this address from floor-Barossa Shiraz. Fermentation proceeds in traditional open-top concrete fermenters with full malolactic conversion; no tannin additions are made, and natural grape acidity from the old vines is relied upon structurally. Aging runs 18 months in French and American oak hogsheads, with recent releases weighted approximately 86% French / 14% American, ~33% new oak. What no peer replicates: six discrete blocks spanning plantings from c.1860 through 1965, blended as a single vineyard expression from own-rooted, ungrafted vines that predate phylloxera's arrival in Australia. The 2019 is rated 97+ points (Vintage Journal); drink 2025–2038+.

name: Henschke  Mount Edelstone Shiraz, Eden Valley
short_description (EN Store): Own-rooted 1912 vines on Eden Valley's 400 m plateau — Cyril Henschke's 1952 transgression that invented Australian single-vineyard wine, now a 97+ pt 2019 of extreme purity.
description (EN Store):
In 1952, when Australian fine wine meant blends and fortifieds, Cyril Henschke bottled a single vineyard under its own name — Mount Edelstone — an act of deliberate transgression that effectively invented a category. The vines he drew from, planted in 1912 from James Busby stock, are own-rooted and pre-phylloxera, still growing on their original rootstock at 400 m elevation on an easterly-facing slope in the Eden Valley GI.

That elevation and aspect are the technical argument for everything that follows: cooler nights than floor Barossa preserve natural acidity and produce the five-spice and dark-fruit precision that defines Eden Valley Shiraz. The 16-ha vineyard is farmed organically and biodynamically under viticulturist Prue Henschke, dry-grown, trained on Scott Henry trellis to manage canopy in the cool-climate site.

In the winery, hand-harvested fruit is fermented in open-top vessels with submerged-cap technique, undergoes full malolactic fermentation, and is aged in French and American oak hogsheads (similar regime to Hill of Grace: predominantly French, mix of new and seasoned) with no added tannins — natural grape acidity from old vines does the structural work. Separate block parcels are blended prior to bottling.

2019 saw yields of just 10–15% of normal crop following frosts, hail, and a record 44.3°C heat event, producing a wine of concentrated purity and precision — awarded 97+ points by Vintage Journal. Drinking window: 2025–2038+.

name: Henschke  Cyril Henschke Cabernet Sauvignon, Eden Valley
short_description (EN Store): 100% Cabernet Sauvignon from Eden Valley's ~500 m Cranes Range Road site — sandy loam over sandstone/schist, biodynamically farmed, named for the pioneer of Australia's single-vineyard movement.
description (EN Store):
Cyril Henschke — the fourth-generation family member who in 1952 and 1958 produced Australia's first single-vineyard wines at a time when the country ran almost exclusively on blends and fortifieds — lends his name to this 100% Cabernet Sauvignon from the Eden Valley Vineyard on Cranes Range Road. The tribute is deliberate: this wine exists because Cyril proved that a single site could carry a wine's entire identity.

The Eden Valley Vineyard sits at approximately 500 m elevation on sandy loam over sandstone and schist — a cool, high-altitude pocket of the Barossa Zone where diurnal temperature swings preserve natural acidity and extend hang time, producing Cabernet of structural precision rather than Barossa-floor opulence. Oldest vines date to 1968. All fruit is farmed organically and biodynamically under viticulturist Prue Henschke.

Grapes are hand-harvested and fermented in open-top vessels with full malolactic fermentation; no tannin additions are made, with Stephen Henschke relying entirely on natural grape tannin and acidity. Aging proceeds in French and American oak hogsheads (seasoned and new), consistent with the house's broader hogshead regime across single-vineyard expressions.

What distinguishes Cyril Henschke within the Australian Cabernet landscape is its Eden Valley provenance: schist-and-sandstone soils at altitude that no floor-Barossa Cabernet can replicate, bottled under Stelvin screwcap from the 2021 vintage onward. The 2018 vintage falls within a period Henschke describes as delivering structurally dense, precisely integrated wines across the Eden Valley range.

name: Yalumba  Rare & Fine The Octavius Old Vine Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Shiraz from vines planted 1854–1908, aged 22 months in 100-litre octave barrels hand-crafted at the only winery cooperage in the Southern Hemisphere.
description (EN Store):
Yalumba's in-house cooperage — the only fully operational winery-owned barrel workshop in the Southern Hemisphere — exists largely because of this wine. The 100-litre octave barrel was invented on-site specifically for The Octavius, its reduced volume accelerating oak integration while preserving the identity of fruit sourced from Barossa Shiraz vines dating to 1854 and 1908: dry-grown, low-yielding bush vines rooted in the warm, alluvial clay-loam and ironstone soils of the Barossa floor, where heat accumulation drives phenolic ripeness and mid-palate density that no cooler site can replicate.

Vinification begins with wild/spontaneous fermentation in open-top fermenters, some in use since the 1800s, before the wine moves into bespoke octave barrels for approximately 22 months — 21% new French and American oak, the remainder older French — a regime that delivers spice integration without obscuring the iron-rich, sun-baked character of the old vines. The 2016 vintage is drawn from a stellar Barossa season that produced wines of uncommon structural poise and slow-ageing profile. The wine is 100% vegan, with no animal-derived fining agents used since 2012. The octave format itself is the transgression: no other Barossa producer controls oak at this level of bespoke precision, crafting roughly 300 barrels per year from raw stave to finished cask on the estate. Drink 2024–2038.

name: Little Things  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied South Australian Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Little Things Cabernet Sauvignon from South Australia delivers a classic expression of the varietal with full body and commanding tannin structure. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cedary oak and black pepper on the palate. The wine's backbone of high tannins and medium-high acidity provides excellent aging potential and food compatibility, particularly with grilled red meats, lamb, and braised beef dishes. Subtle tobacco and earth notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and depth.

name: Yellow Tail  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Merlot with ripe plum and cherry fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly character.
description (EN Store): Yellow Tail Merlot from Australia's Riverina region delivers a straightforward, fruit-forward red wine built for everyday enjoyment. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by soft tannins and a smooth, accessible texture. Subtle oak integration adds gentle complexity without overshadowing the wine's primary fruit character. This is a versatile, unpretentious Merlot suited to casual dining, grilled meats, and pasta dishes.

name: Yellow Tail  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Cabernet with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly balance—ideal for weeknight dining.
description (EN Store): Yellow Tail Cabernet Sauvignon from Australia's Riverina region delivers a straightforward, fruit-forward expression of the varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and plum dominate the palate, supported by subtle herbaceous notes and a whisper of cedar oak. Medium-full body and soft, integrated tannins make this wine accessible without sacrificing structure, positioning it as a reliable everyday red for casual entertaining and home dining.

name: Yellow Tail  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, spice, and smooth tannin—ideal for grilled meats and casual dining.
description (EN Store): Yellow Tail Shiraz is a medium-full bodied red wine from Australia's Riverina region, crafted from 100% Shiraz. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and blackberry fruit, complemented by black pepper spice and subtle oak-derived vanilla notes. The palate is smooth and food-friendly, with balanced acidity and approachable tannin structure that makes it versatile across grilled meats, braised beef, and casual roasted dishes. A reliable, everyday red with enough character to pair confidently at the table.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Parinacota
short_description (EN Store): Maule Valley Syrah-Carignan blend with dark fruit, pepper spice, and mineral grip. Food-friendly Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Parinacota is a 70% Syrah and 30% Carignan blend from Chile's Maule Valley, a region known for structured, fruit-forward reds. The 2021 vintage delivers dark plum and blackberry on the nose, with black pepper and spice on the palate, supported by medium-full body and balanced tannin. Earthy undertones and mineral finish provide complexity without heaviness, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Tectonia Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, fresh acidity, and silky tannins—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Tectonia Pinot Noir from Maule Valley presents a light-bodied expression of this classic varietal, marked by vibrant red fruit and raspberry character. High acidity and medium tannins create a fresh, food-friendly profile with earthy undertones and mineral complexity. The silky texture and bright fruit make this wine approachable yet nuanced, suitable for warm-weather dining and a range of lighter proteins and vegetable-forward dishes.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Tectonia Grenache-Petite Syrah Mourvedre
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-Petite Syrah-Mourvèdre blend from Chile's Rapel Valley with ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Tectonia is a Rhône-inspired blend anchored by Grenache (45%), with Petite Syrah (38%) and Mourvèdre (17%) adding structure and depth. From Chile's Rapel Valley, this medium-full-bodied red delivers ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with black pepper and herbaceous notes. Mid-palate tannins are firm yet approachable, with secondary smoke and spice complexity evolving toward earthy, leathery tertiary notes. Food-friendly and versatile, it suits grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Chateau Barreyres  AOC Haut-Medoc Cru Bourgeois
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Cru Bourgeois with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring and classic red meat.
description (EN Store):
Château Barreyres is a Cru Bourgeois estate in the Haut-Médoc appellation, crafting a traditional Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine displays a full body with high tannins and medium-high acidity, characteristic of Left Bank Médoc terroir.

On the nose, ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate, complemented by cedary oak and black pepper from careful winemaking. With air, tobacco and leather notes emerge, signaling age-worthiness and complexity. The palate is structured and demanding, rewarding patient cellaring and pairing with substantial dishes.

This is a wine for the classic Bordeaux enthusiast—food-friendly yet serious, with the backbone to age gracefully over a decade or more.

name: Terre del Vulcano  Lava Aglianico Beneventano IGT
short_description (EN Store): Campanian Aglianico with dark cherry, mineral grip, and structured tannin—a food-friendly red from volcanic terroir.
description (EN Store): Terre del Vulcano's Lava Aglianico Beneventano IGT is a single-varietal expression of Campania's signature red grape, grown on volcanic soils that impart distinctive mineral character. The wine opens with ripe dark cherry and black plum, supported by black pepper spice and a leathery secondary layer. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide structure suitable for grilled meats, braised beef, and game. Earthy undertones and subtle tobacco notes emerge on the finish, reflecting both the grape's natural phenolic ripeness and the region's geothermal terroir.

name: Terre del Vulcano  Lava Taurasi DOC
short_description (EN Store): Pure Aglianico from Taurasi's volcanic soils—dark fruit, mineral grip, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Terre del Vulcano Lava Taurasi DOC is a 100% Aglianico expression rooted in Campania's volcanic terroir. The wine delivers dark plum and black cherry on the nose, underpinned by mineral tension and black pepper spice. On the palate, medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame leather and tobacco notes, with earthy undertones reflecting the region's distinctive volcanic character. A food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Penfolds  Bin 8 Shiraz Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Kalimna-anchored Shiraz/Cabernet from phylloxera-free Barossa sandy loam — aged in barrels previously used for Grange, transferring house DNA at entry level.
description (EN Store):
Bin 8 occupies a deliberate position in the Penfolds hierarchy: it draws on the same Kalimna property in the Barossa Valley — 290 hectares of ancient, phylloxera-free sandy loam and deep alluvial soils — that supplies fruit for wines many times its price, then matures in oak previously seasoned by Grange, the house's flagship. This is not a coincidence of logistics; it is a conscious decision to transmit winemaking DNA downward through the range.

Barossa's deep, low-vigour sandy loam forces vine roots deep, concentrating flavour and producing the mid-palate density that defines the house style. The Shiraz/Cabernet Sauvignon blend follows the same structural logic as Bin 389 — Shiraz supplying dark fruit weight and spice, Cabernet lending backbone and cassis definition — though Bin 8 sits at a more approachable extraction point. Maturation in seasoned American oak hogsheads (larger than standard Bordeaux barriques) delivers the characteristic vanilla and cedar integration without overwhelming primary fruit.

What separates Bin 8 from peers at this price is provenance of vessel: the oak has already shaped Grange, meaning the tannin structure is softer and the integration faster, yet the aromatic imprint of the house — ironstone, dark plum, warm spice — remains unmistakable. Drink now through the near term.

name: Penfolds  Bin 128
short_description (EN Store): Coonawarra terra rossa Shiraz aged in seasoned American oak hogsheads — Penfolds' single-appellation statement from Australia's most celebrated limestone ridge.
description (EN Store):
Bin 128 is Penfolds' deliberate exception to its own house doctrine: where Grange draws power from multi-regional blending, Bin 128 commits entirely to Coonawarra — a single, narrow strip of weathered limestone capped by iron-rich terra rossa clay, a soil profile that delivers structural precision and cooler-climate restraint rare in the Penfolds red portfolio. The cool Coonawarra microclimate extends hang time, concentrating dark fruit while preserving the natural acidity that underpins the wine's cellaring architecture.

Vinification follows the Penfolds red-wine template: acclimatised pure yeast cultures, temperature-controlled fermentation, and maturation in seasoned American oak hogsheads — the same vessel format used for Grange, but here in previously-used wood rather than new, allowing the limestone-driven fruit to speak without oak domination. The hogshead format (larger than a standard Bordeaux barrique) promotes slower, more even oxidative development through the aging cycle.

What distinguishes Bin 128 categorically is its position as Penfolds' only 100% Coonawarra Shiraz in the Bin range — a single-appellation wine from a house philosophically opposed to single-terroir orthodoxy, making it the most terroir-transparent expression in the entire Penfolds red lineup. The 2021 vintage in Coonawarra benefited from a mild, even growing season with well-distributed rainfall, producing fruit of natural balance and medium-full structure suited to drinking from 2026 through the mid-2030s.

name: Penfolds  Bin 2
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Shiraz/Mataro from Penfolds' Kalimna estate — aged in barrels previously used for Grange, delivering house DNA at an accessible tier.
description (EN Store): Bin 2 sits at an instructive point in the Penfolds hierarchy: it draws on Kalimna, the 290-hectare Barossa Valley property where Cabernet and Mataro vines share ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils that have never been touched by phylloxera. That ungrafted continuity is not incidental — it produces fruit of unusual concentration and structural density from vines that have never been reset. The Barossa floor's low-vigour soils stress the vine naturally, compressing berry size and intensifying pigment and tannin without irrigation intervention.
