The blend centres on Shiraz and Mataro (Mourvèdre), a Southern Rhône-adjacent pairing that Penfolds has long championed in the Barossa, and the wine is matured in seasoned American oak hogsheads — the same vessel format used for Grange, here in previously-used form, which moderates oak extraction while preserving the house's characteristic mid-palate richness. What distinguishes Bin 2 within its tier is precisely this oak lineage: barrels that have already carried a senior wine impart structural memory without dominating the fruit. Penfolds' multi-vineyard blending philosophy, which deliberately subordinates single-site purity to winemaker-driven consistency, means Bin 2 delivers a recognisable stylistic signature — generous dark fruit, earthy Mataro spice, and the firm but resolved tannin architecture that defines the house — at a price point that makes the Penfolds winemaking method genuinely accessible.

name: Penfolds  Bin 28 Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Kalimna's ancient sandy loam Shiraz, matured in ex-Grange American oak hogsheads — Penfolds' multi-regional blending philosophy distilled into a single Bin.
description (EN Store): Bin 28 carries a lineage directly tied to Penfolds' Kalimna property in the Barossa Valley — a 290-hectare estate where Shiraz roots push through ancient, phylloxera-free deep sandy loam and alluvial soils, a geological circumstance that has kept these vines ungrafted and continuously producing for generations. That soil profile, low in nutrients and moisture-retentive at depth, drives the concentrated, generously fruited character that defines Bin 28's mid-palate weight. Sourced across multiple South Australian districts under Penfolds' deliberate house philosophy — fruit quality over single-terroir purity — the blend is assembled to hit a consistent structural target vintage after vintage, a discipline enforced by one of the longest winemaking lineages in the New World. Maturation takes place in American oak hogsheads previously used for Grange, meaning the wood has already surrendered its primary tannin load to one of Australia's most celebrated reds; what remains imparts subtle vanilla integration and spice without overwhelming the fruit. No new oak is introduced, a deliberate restraint that separates Bin 28 from the more oak-assertive Bin 707 or Grange itself. The special feature here is provenance by proxy: every barrel in this wine has already shaped Grange, making Bin 28 the only widely accessible Penfolds red whose oak regime is a direct downstream consequence of the flagship. The 2021 vintage from Barossa delivers the house's signature dark-fruited density with the structural composure to reward 8–12 years of cellaring.

name: Penfolds  Bin 407
short_description (EN Store): Bin 407: Penfolds' multi-regional Cabernet Sauvignon aged in seasoned American oak hogsheads, built for structure without the Bin 707 price.
description (EN Store): Bin 407 occupies a deliberate position in the Penfolds hierarchy — the house's entry point into 100% Cabernet Sauvignon, drawing fruit from the same South Australian regions (Barossa Valley, Coonawarra, McLaren Vale) that feed the flagship Bin 707, but matured in seasoned rather than new oak, a calculated decision that lets the fruit architecture carry the wine rather than new-wood extraction. Penfolds' multi-regional blending philosophy — the antithesis of single-terroir orthodoxy — is fully at work here: Barossa Valley contributes deep alluvial and ancient sandy loam fruit from phylloxera-free soils, while Coonawarra's terra rossa over weathered limestone adds structural precision and cool-climate cassis definition. The winemaking follows house protocol: temperature-controlled fermentation using acclimatised pure yeast cultures, a technique refined under Ray Beckwith's pH-meter innovations, followed by maturation in American oak hogsheads (the slightly larger-than-barrique format that is Penfolds' signature vessel). What distinguishes Bin 407 within its category is the frank transparency of the blending decision: where most producers at this tier hide multi-regional sourcing, Penfolds codifies it as craft. The 2022 vintage reflects a South Australian season of moderate temperatures and even ripening, delivering characteristic mid-palate density with tannin grip suited to 5–10 years of further development.

name: Penfolds  Bin 389 Cabernet Sauvignon/Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet/Shiraz aged in ex-Grange barrels — Penfolds' deliberate hand-me-down oak strategy gives Bin 389 its structural backbone without new-oak weight.
description (EN Store):
Penfolds calls Bin 389 the 'Baby Grange' not for sentimentality but for a precise technical reason: the wine is matured in the same American oak hogsheads that previously held Grange, absorbing residual tannin structure and spice from barrels already seasoned by Australia's most scrutinised red. It is a deliberate cascade in the Penfolds hierarchy — second use of the house's most prized cooperage, not a compromise but a calculated stylistic position.

Fruit is drawn from Penfolds' multi-regional South Australian network, anchored by Barossa Valley sources including the Kalimna property, where ancient deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free since first planting — yield Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz of unusual concentration and mid-palate density. The warm Barossa floor amplifies fruit generosity while the blending philosophy allows the winemaking team to select across districts for structural balance.

The Cabernet Sauvignon/Shiraz blend is vinified under the house's acclimatised pure yeast culture protocol, with the ex-Grange hogsheads providing a secondary tannin scaffold rather than primary oak flavour — the result is a wine with more structural grip than its price tier typically delivers. What makes Bin 389 categorically unusual is this inherited-oak logic: no peer at this price point systematically sources its aging vessels from a wine valued at multiples above it. The 2022 vintage reflects a more moderate South Australian growing season following the compressed heat of 2019–2021, pointing toward earlier accessibility while retaining the mid-palate density the Barossa floor reliably provides.

name: Penfolds  Bin 707 Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Bin 707: 100% Cabernet Sauvignon aged in 100% new oak, Kalimna's pre-phylloxera Barossa soils delivering the structural backbone of Penfolds' Cabernet flagship.
description (EN Store): When Max Schubert's successors needed a Cabernet Sauvignon counterpart to Grange, they turned to Kalimna — Penfolds' 290-hectare Barossa Valley property where Cabernet vines have grown in ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils since the 1880s, on ground that phylloxera never reached. That geological continuity is not incidental: ungrafted old vines in low-vigour sandy loam produce naturally concentrated, small-berried fruit with tightly-knit tannin architecture that no replanted vineyard can replicate. Bin 707 is 100% Cabernet Sauvignon, sourced multi-regionally across South Australia, and matured in 100% new oak — both American and French hogsheads — a regime more aggressive than any other wine in the Penfolds portfolio outside Grange itself. Where Bin 389 inherits second-fill barrels previously used for Grange, Bin 707 demands virgin wood, driving a deeper integration of cedar, graphite, and dark cassis into the wine's frame. The special feature here is the deliberate inversion of Old World logic: no single château, no appellation boundary, yet the winemaking team's multi-regional selection achieves a structural consistency across decades that single-site Cabernets rarely match. The 2022 vintage is drawn from a growing season that followed several years of La Niña-influenced rainfall recovery in South Australia, delivering fruit with natural freshness alongside the density the Barossa floor provides; drink 2030–2045+.

name: Penfolds  St.Henri Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): St Henri's defining transgression: 100% old large-vat oak aging — zero new oak — where every Penfolds peer leans hard on American hogsheads.
description (EN Store): While Max Schubert was building Grange on new American oak hogsheads in the 1950s, St Henri took the opposite path — matured entirely in large old oak vats, a deliberate refusal of new-oak influence that has defined this wine across every vintage since its inception. The result is a Shiraz whose structure comes from fruit and time, not timber. Sourced from Penfolds' multi-regional South Australian network — anchored by Barossa Valley fruit grown in ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils on phylloxera-free rootstock, including parcels from the 290-hectare Kalimna property — the warm valley floor delivers concentrated dark fruit, while cooler-climate contributions from McLaren Vale and beyond add tension to the mid-palate. Fermentation follows Penfolds' house protocol using acclimatised pure yeast cultures in temperature-controlled stainless steel fermenters, with basket pressing preserving textural integrity. The wine then enters large old oak vats — no new oak at any stage — allowing slow, oxidative development without the vanilla or coconut signatures that mark the Bin range. This is the one expression in the Penfolds red portfolio where the winemaker's hand is most deliberately hidden: no new-oak scaffolding, no Grange-level extraction, just multi-regional fruit resolved over time. The 2020 South Australian growing season delivered warm, even ripening with good diurnal range; expect the characteristic St Henri profile of dark plum, ironstone minerality, and dried herb complexity, with a drinking window extending well into the 2030s.

name: Penfolds  Grange Bin 95
short_description (EN Store): Multi-district Shiraz fermented and finished in 100% new American oak hogsheads — the deliberate anti-terroir that cellars 40–50+ years.
description (EN Store):
In 1951, Max Schubert returned from Bordeaux determined to build Australia's first age-worthy red — not from a single château plot, but by blending the best fruit across South Australia's districts and completing fermentation inside new American oak hogsheads, a technique he borrowed from the Médoc and made entirely his own. That founding transgression remains the technical core of every Grange made since.

The 2020 blend draws predominantly from Barossa Valley (typically 77–82% of the final wine), where ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free since settlement — force Shiraz vines into low-yield concentration. McLaren Vale's sea-breeze-moderated Mediterranean warmth and Magill Estate's urban hillside block at the foot of the Mount Lofty Ranges contribute structural counterpoint in select proportions.

Fermentation proceeds in small open-top stainless steel fermenters, with the final stages completed inside the aging vessel itself — 100% new American oak hogsheads (slightly larger than Bordeaux barriques), where the wine then matures for 18–20 months. Acclimatised pure yeast cultures and temperature-controlled fermentation, a Penfolds innovation pioneered by Ray Beckwith, underpin vintage-to-vintage consistency. The blend is typically 93–98% Shiraz with 2–7% Cabernet Sauvignon, sealed under natural cork at approximately 14.5% ABV.

No other commercially produced wine in the New World is heritage-listed by a national trust — Grange holds that status in South Australia — and no peer has matched its documented 40–50+ year cellaring trajectory outside Bordeaux's First Growths.

name: Penfolds  Koonunga Hill Shiraz/Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry-level expression of its multi-regional blending philosophy — Barossa Shiraz muscle anchored by Cabernet structure, aged in seasoned oak.
description (EN Store): When Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold planted his first vines at Magill Estate in 1844, the ambition was medicinal — fortified wine as tonic. Nearly 180 years later, Koonunga Hill distils that same house logic into its most accessible form: a deliberate, winemaker-led blend that prioritises fruit consistency over single-terroir declaration, the philosophical inverse of Burgundy. Sourced from Barossa Valley and broader South Australia, the Shiraz component draws on the region's ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free ground that yields old-vine concentration without the need for irrigation stress. The Cabernet Sauvignon component adds structural backbone and dark-fruit definition to the warmer-climate Shiraz core. Vinification follows the Penfolds house protocol: temperature-controlled fermentation using acclimatised yeast cultures, with maturation in seasoned oak that integrates without dominating — the same philosophy that governs St Henri's large old-vat regime, scaled to this tier. What distinguishes Koonunga Hill within its price category is precisely this: it is made under the same multi-regional blending discipline and winemaking continuity — only four Chief Winemakers in nearly 180 years — that governs wines fifty times its price. The result is a Barossa-weighted red with the structural coherence of a house that has never outsourced its stylistic DNA.

name: Penfolds  Koonunga Hill Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry point into Barossa Shiraz: multi-vineyard blending philosophy, ancient phylloxera-free sandy loam, American oak maturation.
description (EN Store): Koonunga Hill is the expression through which Penfolds has, since the 1970s, applied its signature multi-vineyard blending discipline — the same philosophy that governs Grange — to an accessible tier, treating fruit sourcing and winemaker judgment as the primary quality levers rather than any single plot. Barossa Valley anchors the blend, drawing on ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils that are notably phylloxera-free, allowing old-vine material to persist on its own rootstock — a geological advantage that delivers concentrated, dark-fruited Shiraz with characteristic mid-palate density. Penfolds' winemaking here follows the house red protocol: temperature-controlled fermentation using acclimatised yeast cultures (a technique Ray Beckwith pioneered at Penfolds using pH meters), followed by maturation in American oak, the hogshead-driven signature that defines the house's structural identity across every tier from Koonunga Hill to Grange itself. What distinguishes this expression within its price category is precisely that continuity of method: the same blending logic, the same oak grammar, the same Chief Winemaker lineage — only four in nearly 180 years — applied without compromise at entry level. Current vintage delivers the archetypal Penfolds Barossa profile: dark plum, ironstone earth, and vanilla-laced oak framed by firm but approachable tannin structure.

name: Penfolds  Koonunga Hill Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry point into the Kalimna-anchored Barossa blending philosophy — multi-vineyard Cabernet built for immediate accessibility without sacrificing house structure.
description (EN Store): Koonunga Hill takes its name from a hill on Penfolds' Kalimna property in the Barossa Valley, the same 290-hectare estate whose Block 42 Cabernet Sauvignon vines date to the 1880s and are among the oldest continuously-producing Cabernet plantings on earth. That lineage matters: even at this tier, the fruit draws on Barossa's ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free ground that produces low-stressed vines with naturally concentrated berry character. The wine is assembled via Penfolds' signature multi-vineyard blending protocol, prioritising fruit quality and house consistency over single-site expression — a deliberate inversion of Old World terroir orthodoxy that has defined the house since Max Schubert's era. Maturation follows the Penfolds red-wine template in seasoned American oak, delivering the mid-palate richness and approachable structure that is the house's commercial calling card without the extended new-oak regimes reserved for Bin 707 or Grange. What separates Koonunga Hill from peers at this price point is institutional: it is made under the same Chief Winemaker lineage — only four in nearly 180 years — that governs Grange, meaning quality discipline flows top-down through every tier. Drink now through near-term cellaring.

name: Penfolds  Max's Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Named for Max Schubert, Penfolds' founding winemaker — Barossa Shiraz matured in seasoned American oak, delivering the house's signature mid-palate density at an accessible tier.
description (EN Store): Max's Shiraz is named for Max Schubert, the winemaker who in the early 1950s pioneered barrel fermentation in new American oak hogsheads at Penfolds — a technique borrowed from Bordeaux and applied to Barossa Shiraz with results that redefined Australian red wine. This bottling carries that lineage forward at an entry-level price point, drawing on Barossa Valley fruit grown in the valley's ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free ground that allows ungrafted old vines to push roots deep, concentrating flavour without irrigation stress. Maturation takes place in seasoned American oak, delivering the house's characteristic vanilla-framed mid-palate richness without the structural weight of new-oak tannin. The multi-vineyard blending philosophy — Penfolds' deliberate departure from single-terroir orthodoxy — allows the winemaking team to select parcels for consistency of fruit quality across vintages rather than geographic purity. What distinguishes Max's within the Penfolds portfolio is its role as the most direct expression of the house style at an accessible price: the same Barossa Shiraz DNA, the same American oak signature, stripped of the extended aging and selection intensity of Bin 28 or RWT. Drink on release through 5–7 years for primary dark fruit and oak-spice character.

name: Penfolds  Max's Shiraz / Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry-level red named for Max Schubert's successor, blending Barossa Shiraz and Cabernet from ancient phylloxera-free sandy loam soils.
description (EN Store): Max Schubert spent 18 years as Penfolds' Chief Winemaker building the house's blending doctrine — the philosophy that winemaker judgment, not a fixed plot, defines quality. This wine carries his successor's name as a deliberate nod to that lineage. Sourced from Barossa Valley's ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free ground that allows ungrafted old vines to push roots deep, concentrating fruit without irrigation stress. The Kalimna property, 290 ha with 153 ha under vine, anchors the Shiraz component alongside Cabernet Sauvignon, the same two varieties that define Bin 389 higher in the range. Winemaking follows Penfolds' multi-vineyard blending protocol, with the Cabernet Sauvignon component adding structural backbone to the Barossa Shiraz's characteristic mid-palate density. Oak maturation uses barrels previously seasoned within the Penfolds system — the house's signature method of imparting integration without overwhelming primary fruit. Special feature: unlike single-appellation Australian reds at this price, Max's is an explicit exercise in the same multi-regional, winemaker-led blending philosophy that governs Grange itself — house consistency over terroir declaration. Approachable on release, with the structural architecture to reward 3–5 years of cellaring.

name: Penfolds  Max's Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Named for Max Schubert, Penfolds' founding winemaker — Barossa Cabernet aged in seasoned hogsheads, delivering Kalimna-rooted depth at an accessible price.
description (EN Store): Max Schubert, the winemaker who returned from Bordeaux in 1950 and pioneered barrel fermentation in Australian red wine, lends his name to this Cabernet Sauvignon — a deliberate nod to the technical lineage that defines Penfolds' house DNA. Sourced from Barossa Valley, where ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free and among the oldest continuously-farmed Cabernet ground on earth — produce fruit of concentrated, sun-saturated character. The Kalimna property, home to Cabernet vines planted in the 1880s, anchors the fruit profile with structural density and dark-berry depth that younger plantings rarely match. Maturation follows the Penfolds hogshead tradition, using seasoned American oak barrels that deliver the house's signature mid-palate richness without overwhelming the fruit with new-wood tannin — a deliberate calibration for approachability. The multi-vineyard blending philosophy, the antithesis of single-terroir orthodoxy, allows the winemaking team to select across Barossa sub-zones for consistency vintage to vintage. What distinguishes Max's within the Penfolds range is its position as the entry point into the house's red winemaking philosophy — the same Barossa fruit sources, the same hogshead regime, the same blending discipline — compressed into an everyday format without compromise on provenance.

name: Beringer  Main & Vine Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Napa Merlot with ripe plum, cherry, and subtle oak—a versatile red for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Beringer Main & Vine Merlot is a classic expression of Napa Valley Merlot, crafted to deliver immediate appeal and food-friendly versatility. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum, layered with black cherry and subtle cocoa notes from oak aging. Medium-full body and soft, integrated tannins create a smooth, approachable palate that pairs effortlessly with grilled meats, braised dishes, roasted poultry, and tomato-based preparations. A reliable choice for both casual weeknight meals and informal entertaining.

name: The Old Town  South African Dry Red (Fruit wine blend)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable South African dry red fruit wine blend with bright berry character and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): The Old Town South African Dry Red is a fruit wine blend from Western Cape offering straightforward red fruit and berry character. Dry on the palate with medium body and balanced tannin, this wine delivers accessible flavor without complexity, making it an ideal everyday pour for casual dining and informal gatherings.

name: 19 Crimes  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and subtle oak—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied Shiraz from Australia's Barossa Valley, this wine showcases dominant blackberry and plum fruit with supporting notes of vanilla and cocoa from oak aging. Black pepper spice and smoke add complexity and structure. The balanced acidity and medium tannins make it approachable yet substantial enough for hearty dishes. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and smoky preparations.

name: 19 Crimes  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Cabernet with ripe blackberry, plum, and vanilla oak—a food-friendly, approachable red.
description (EN Store):
19 Crimes Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackberry and plum fruit. Secondary oak and vanilla notes add structure and warmth, while subtle black pepper and cocoa round out the mid-palate. A touch of smoke and tobacco complexity emerges on the finish, making this wine versatile at the dinner table.

Ideal for grilled steaks, braised beef, lamb, and roasted comfort dishes. The wine's balanced tannin and acidity make it equally at home with cured meats and charcuterie.

name: 19 Crimes  Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Barossa blend of dark fruit, spice, and oak—a bold red for grilled meats and hearty fare.
description (EN Store): 19 Crimes Red Blend is a full-bodied Barossa Valley blend anchored by Shiraz, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Grenache. The wine opens with dominant blackberry and dark plum fruit, supported by black pepper and clove spice. Oak and vanilla from winemaking add richness and structure, while smoke and chocolate notes provide complexity. High tannins and medium acidity frame a wine built for grilled red meat, braised beef, and smoky barbecue.

name: Angove  Long Row Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Organic Cabernet from South Australia's heritage producer. Ripe dark berries, structured tannins, elegant earthiness.
description (EN Store): Angove's Long Row Cabernet Sauvignon represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture and premium South Australian winemaking. This medium-full bodied expression showcases ripe dark berries and blackcurrant on the nose, with subtle earthiness and cedar undertones. The palate is structured by elegant, medium tannins that frame the wine's dark fruit core without overwhelming it, making it an approachable yet serious food wine. A signature Angove profile: vibrant, balanced, and reflective of the Riverland's Mediterranean climate.

name: Angove  Long Row Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Organic Merlot from South Australia's Riverland: ripe dark berries, soft tannins, food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Angove's Long Row Merlot represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture and sustainable winemaking in South Australia's Riverland region. This single-varietal expression showcases the classic Merlot profile: ripe dark cherry and plum fruit with subtle earthiness and approachable, medium-weight tannins. The wine's soft structure and balanced acidity make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. A food-friendly, everyday-premium red that reflects the Mediterranean warmth of its terroir.

name: Angove  Long Row Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Organic Riverland Shiraz with ripe dark berries, black pepper spice, and elegant structure—a food-friendly Australian classic.
description (EN Store): Angove's Long Row Shiraz represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture and authentic expression of South Australia's Riverland terroir. This medium-full bodied red showcases the varietal's signature ripe dark plum and blackberry character, layered with black pepper spice and subtle licorice notes. The palate is structured by medium tannins and balanced acidity, with emerging earthy and leather undertones that suggest complexity. A versatile, well-crafted Shiraz suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Mount Riley  Merlot Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Merlot-Malbec blend: ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Mount Riley's Merlot Malbec from Marlborough showcases the region's signature ripe dark fruit character. The blend is dominated by Merlot (93%), contributing plush black cherry and blackcurrant notes, while the 7% Malbec adds subtle spice and structural depth. Medium-full body with approachable tannins makes this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled steak, braised beef, or roasted poultry. A commercial-friendly expression of New Zealand red wine craftsmanship.

name: Penfolds  Grange 2013 Aevum Limited Edition Decanter
short_description (EN Store): 2013 Grange in Aevum decanter: 100-pt Lisa Perrotti-Brown, Barossa-dominant Shiraz finished in 100% new American oak hogsheads, 18–20 months.
description (EN Store):
When Lisa Perrotti-Brown awarded the 2013 Grange a perfect 100 points for Wine Advocate, she was scoring a wine built on a deliberate act of winemaking transgression: Max Schubert's original decision to complete fermentation inside new American oak hogsheads — a technique borrowed from Bordeaux but executed in a vessel slightly larger than a barrique, producing a textural integration no post-fermentation racking can replicate. That founding logic remains unchanged in 2013.

The fruit draws primarily from Barossa Valley (typically 77–82% of the blend), where ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free, ungrafted — force vines to root deep for water, concentrating flavour into small, thick-skinned berries. McLaren Vale, Coonawarra, and Clare Valley fill the remaining parcels, each selected by Chief Winemaker Peter Gago for fruit quality over geographic consistency — the deliberate anti-terroir philosophy that defines the house.

The blend runs ~93–98% Shiraz with up to 7% Cabernet Sauvignon, fermented in small open-top stainless steel tanks before barrel completion, then aged 18–20 months in 100% new American oak hogsheads, followed by extended bottle maturation under natural cork at 14.5% ABV. The Aevum decanter format is a limited-release presentation of the same wine, not a separate cuvée.

The 2013 is described as concentrated and precise — a structured, cellar-demanding expression drinking well from now through the 2040s and beyond.

name: Chateau Palmer  Margaux 2018
short_description (EN Store): Margaux's rule-breaker: 47% Merlot on prime gravel croupes, biodynamic since 2017–18, unfined/unfiltered. 2018 scores 97/100 — drink 2028–2060.
description (EN Store):
When the Mähler-Besse and Sichel families consolidated ownership in 1938, they inherited an estate already defying Médoc logic: Merlot planted deliberately on the finest Gunzian Garonne alluvial gravel outcrops of the Cantenac-Margaux plateau — the very parcels where Médoc orthodoxy demands Cabernet Sauvignon. That inversion remains Palmer's defining technical signature today.

The 122 plots span 18 distinct soil types across Cantenac, Issan, and Margaux communes. The 30-hectare plateau behind the château delivers gravel-clay that tempers Merlot's exuberance into structure; the higher-gravel Issan sector to the south contributes the Cabernet Sauvignon backbone. The Gironde estuary moderates diurnal swings, extending hang time and phenolic precision. Full biodynamic certification (2017–2018) has sharpened terroir expression across all 122 plots, a commitment almost unmatched among Médoc classified growths.

Vinification proceeds plot-by-plot in 42 temperature-controlled stainless vats, with indigenous yeasts and a three-week fermentation. Élevage runs 20–22 months in two stages: first in 225-litre French oak barriques at under 50% new wood, then transferred to 30-hectolitre oak vats for the second year — a deliberate step that adds patina without layering excess oak over the fruit. The Grand Vin is bottled unfined and unfiltered.

The 2018 is widely cited as the finest Palmer of the recent era — sumptuous, silky, and concentrated, described as 'ethereal' by critics, with a 97/100 consensus score. Drinking window: 2028–2060.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barolo DOCG Cannubi (Single Vineyard)
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Barolo from Cannubi: elegant Nebbiolo with rose, cherry, and mineral structure. Serious, age-worthy Piedmont.
description (EN Store): Marchesi di Barolo's Barolo DOCG Cannubi is a single-vineyard expression of Nebbiolo from one of Barolo's most prestigious crus. The 2018 vintage displays the classic architecture of fine Barolo: bright red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with floral rose and mineral notes. The palate is structured and refined, with medium-high tannins and acidity that frame dark fruit, tobacco, and subtle tar complexity. This is a wine built for the cellar and the table—elegant enough for immediate enjoyment, yet with the backbone to develop further over the next 10–15 years.

name: Hopes End  Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz-led blend with dark fruit, spice, and oak—a food-friendly red for fine dining.
description (EN Store): Hopes End Red Blend is a Barossa Valley expression anchored by Shiraz (42%), with supporting Grenache (38%), Malbec (13%), and Petit Verdot (7%). The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe blackberry and plum fruit, layered with vanilla oak and black pepper spice. Smoke and chocolate notes add complexity, making it a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and roasted poultry. Approachable yet structured, it bridges casual entertaining and fine dining occasions.

name: Ferraton Pere & Fils  Cornas Lieu-dit Patou
short_description (EN Store): Structured Cornas Syrah with dark plum, blackberry, and peppery spice—a serious, mineral-driven Northern Rhône expression.
description (EN Store): Ferraton Père & Fils's Cornas Lieu-dit Patou is a single-vineyard Syrah from one of the Northern Rhône's most prestigious terroirs. The 2021 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with pronounced dark fruit character—ripe plum and blackberry—layered with black pepper spice and mineral earth. Secondary notes of smoke and licorice add complexity, while the wine's medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential. A classic expression of Cornas's volcanic slate soils and continental climate, this wine rewards patient cellaring and pairs beautifully with grilled red meat, braised beef, and game.

name: Ferraton Pere & Fils  Cote-Rotie Lieu-dit “Montmain”
short_description (EN Store): Northern Rhône Syrah from Montmain lieu-dit: dark plum, black pepper, smoke & mineral complexity. Food-friendly, age-worthy.
description (EN Store): Ferraton Père & Fils' Côte-Rôtie Montmain is a single-varietal Syrah from one of the Northern Rhône's most prestigious appellations. The 2020 vintage expresses the terroir of the Montmain lieu-dit through ripe dark plum and black pepper on the nose, with supporting notes of licorice and violet. On the palate, medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide structure; smoke and mineral notes emerge mid-palate, evolving toward leather and subtle tobacco in the finish. This is a classically styled Côte-Rôtie: elegant, food-friendly, and built for cellaring.

name: Les Allées de Cantemerle Haut-Médoc
short_description (EN Store): Structured Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy with firm tannins.
description (EN Store): Les Allées de Cantemerle represents the classic Haut-Médoc style: a Cabernet Sauvignon–led blend with Merlot and Cabernet Franc, built for cellaring. The 2018 vintage displays a full-bodied frame with high tannins and medium-high acidity, anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, with hints of graphite minerality and developing leather complexity. This is a wine of structure and age-worthiness, best paired with grilled red meat, lamb, or braised beef dishes.

name: Chateau Les Reuilles  Bordeaux rouge AOC
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Bordeaux blend with ripe red fruit, cedar, and balanced structure—ideal for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Château Les Reuilles presents a classic Bordeaux rouge with approachable medium-full body and balanced tannin. Ripe red and dark plum fruit dominate the palate, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes from careful oak aging. A whisper of earth on the finish adds complexity without overwhelming the wine's primary fruit-forward character. This is a versatile, well-structured wine suited to casual weeknight meals and more formal table settings alike.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Reserva Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Carmenere with dark plum, black cherry, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Rapel Valley classic.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Reserva Carmenere is a medium-full bodied red from Chile's Rapel Valley, showcasing the signature character of this distinctive varietal. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose and palate, complemented by bell pepper and warm spice notes that reflect both fruit ripeness and winemaking craft. Subtle oak and tobacco undertones add depth, while fine-grained tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable yet structured enough for food pairing. A versatile choice for grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Reserva Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and refreshing acidity—a versatile table wine.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Reserva Pinot Noir from Casablanca Valley delivers the varietal's signature elegance in a light-bodied, food-friendly expression. Bright raspberry and cherry fruit dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice. The wine's high acidity and silky tannin structure create a fresh, lively mouthfeel that pairs well with roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, and delicate seafood. A reliable, approachable choice for everyday enjoyment or casual entertaining.

name: Famille Perrin  La Vieille Ferme Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Rhône blend with ripe red fruit, spice, and mineral backbone—food-friendly Southern Rhône classic.
description (EN Store): Famille Perrin's La Vieille Ferme Rouge is a traditional Rhône South blend anchored by Grenache, with supporting Syrah, Carignan, and Cinsault. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, layered with black pepper and herbaceous spice from the Syrah component. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it approachable yet structured enough for food pairing. Mineral undertones and subtle earth notes add complexity without overshadowing the fruit-forward character. A versatile, well-made expression of Southern Rhône terroir at an accessible price point.

name: Argiano  Non Confunditur
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Super Tuscan blend of Cabernet, Sangiovese, Merlot & Syrah from Montalcino with dark fruit, cedar & structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Argiano Non Confunditur is a premium Super Tuscan red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Sangiovese, with supporting Merlot and Syrah from the prestigious Montalcino subregion of Tuscany. This full-bodied wine displays dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit character, layered with cedar and oak complexity from careful winemaking. High, structured tannins and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility. The wine's tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with bottle age, adding sophistication to an already complex profile.

name: Argiano  Rosso di Montalcino
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with bright cherry, plum, and mineral notes—a food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store): Argiano's Rosso di Montalcino is a pure expression of Sangiovese from one of Tuscany's most prestigious terroirs. This medium-full-bodied red opens with vibrant red cherry and plum, supported by earthy minerality and subtle dried-herb complexity. The wine's balanced tannin structure and bright acidity make it an ideal companion to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and aged cheeses. A versatile, food-friendly Tuscan that delivers classic regional character at an accessible price point.

name: La Belle Angele   Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): La Belle Angele Cabernet Sauvignon is a medium-full-bodied red from Languedoc-Roussillon, built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and black pepper notes from oak aging add complexity and structure, while herbaceous undertones reflect the varietal's classic profile. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable yet capable of pairing with grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-forward dishes. A food-friendly expression of Cabernet suited to casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Igneo
short_description (EN Store): Petite Sirah–Petit Verdot blend from Chile's Rapel Valley. Dark fruit, spice, and structured tannins for red meat.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Igneo pairs Petite Sirah (55%) with Petit Verdot (45%) to craft a structured, food-friendly red from Chile's Rapel Valley. The blend marries the bold dark-fruit character and peppery spice of Petite Sirah with the refined tannin architecture and cedar notes of Petit Verdot, yielding a medium-full-bodied wine with medium-high tannins and balanced acidity. Expect layers of dark plum, blackberry, and cocoa on the palate, with secondary cedar and leather complexity. A versatile pairing for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes.

name: Antigal  Estimulo Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit depth, structured tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Antigal's Estimulo Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza delivers a classic expression of Argentina's flagship red varietal. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and black cherry—dominates the palate, supported by herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the wine's structure and aging potential. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or aged cheeses. The emerging tobacco and earthy undertones suggest complexity that will develop with bottle age.

name: Antigal  Aduentus Mediterraneo
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza blend of Malbec, Tempranillo & Syrah—dark fruit, spice & herbaceous depth for food-friendly red wine.
description (EN Store): Antigal's Aduentus Mediterraneo is a Mendoza red blend anchored by 40% Malbec, with 30% Tempranillo and 30% Syrah. The combination yields a medium-full bodied wine with dark plum and black cherry primary notes, supported by peppery spice and herbaceous undertones characteristic of Syrah. Medium tannin and acidity make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or tomato-based pasta dishes.

name: Vigneti del Salento  "ZOLLA" Malvasia Nera Solento IGP
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, spiced Malvasia Nera from Puglia with dark cherry, raspberry, and smoke—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Vigneti del Salento's ZOLLA Malvasia Nera showcases the dark-fruit character and warm-climate generosity of southern Italy's Salento peninsula. This 100% Malvasia Nera red opens with ripe black cherry and raspberry, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper spice. The medium-full body and rounded tannins deliver a smooth, drinkable profile that avoids heaviness, making it equally at home with pizza and pasta as with grilled meats and roasted pork. A versatile, unpretentious red that rewards casual enjoyment and everyday entertaining.

name: Vigneti del Salento  "ZOLLA" Salice Salentino DOP
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Negroamaro-led Salentino with dark fruit, pepper spice, and rounded tannins—a food-friendly Puglian red.
description (EN Store): Vigneti del Salento's ZOLLA Salice Salentino DOP blends 80% Negroamaro with 20% Malvasia Nera, capturing the warmth and generosity of southern Puglia. The wine opens with bold dark fruit—cherry and raspberry—layered with black pepper and subtle smoke, leading to a palate that is full yet approachable, with fully rounded tannins and excellent drinkability. A versatile table wine that bridges casual dining and more structured meals.

name: La Spinetta   Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Nebbiolo with rose, cherry, and tarry complexity—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Langhe Nebbiolo is a benchmark expression of this noble Piedmont varietal. The wine opens with bright red cherry and floral rose notes, supported by licorice and spice on the mid-palate. Structured tannins and bracing acidity frame the wine's characteristic tar and leather undertones, which deepen with air. A versatile food wine suited to grilled lamb, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: La Spinetta   Monferrato Rosso " Pin "DOC
short_description (EN Store): Nebbiolo-Barbera blend from Monferrato with ripe red fruit, spice, and mineral complexity—food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Pin is a Monferrato Rosso DOC blending 65% Nebbiolo with 35% Barbera d'Asti Bionzo, capturing the essence of Piedmont's dual-varietal tradition. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it approachable yet age-worthy, while earthy minerality and dried-fruit complexity emerge on the finish. Versatile at table, it bridges casual weeknight pasta and more formal braised meat courses.

name: La Spinetta   Barolo "Garretti" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's Garretti vineyard; cherry, tar, and rose with structured tannins.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Barolo Garretti DOCG is a single-varietal Nebbiolo expression from one of Piedmont's most prestigious appellations. The 2021 vintage showcases the characteristic elegance and complexity of the Garretti site, with dominant red cherry and plum fruit balanced by secondary tar and licorice notes. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, while tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerges with bottle age. A food-friendly Barolo suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: La Spinetta   Barbaresco "Gallina" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbaresco from La Spinetta: 100% Barbera with ripe cherry, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Barbaresco "Gallina" is a single-varietal expression of Barbera from the prestigious Barbaresco DOCG in Piedmont. The 2014 vintage showcases the grape's characteristic balance of ripe red fruit, structured tannins, and aromatic spice. On the palate, cherry and plum lead, followed by layers of leather, earth, and subtle tobacco that develop with air. The wine's medium-full body and medium-high acidity make it a versatile food companion, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or rich pasta preparations.

name: La Spinetta   Barolo "Campe" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barolo from Piedmont's Campe vineyard: dark fruit, cedar, and mineral complexity in a structured, age-worthy Nebbiolo.
description (EN Store): La Spinetta's Barolo "Campe" DOCG is a classically structured Piedmont red built on 100% Nebbiolo from the renowned Barolo zone. The 2014 vintage displays the varietal's signature dark plum and black cherry fruit, layered with cedar, tobacco, and subtle rose florals. Medium-high acidity and tannin provide backbone and aging potential, while tertiary notes of leather and mineral earth emerge on the palate. A food-friendly yet serious expression suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Livon  Collio Merlot DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Friuli Merlot with ripe plum, cherry, and herbaceous notes—a versatile, food-friendly red from Italy's Collio.
description (EN Store): Livon's Collio Merlot is a classically styled single-varietal red from northeastern Italy's prestigious Collio appellation. The wine displays the hallmark softness and approachability of well-made Merlot, with ripe red and dark fruit character balanced by subtle herbaceous notes and a mineral undertone. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make this wine an ideal companion to a broad range of dishes—from pasta and risotto to roasted meats and Mediterranean fare. The wine reflects the cool-climate influence of Friuli-Venezia Giulia, where Merlot achieves elegant balance and food-friendly versatility.

name: Livon  Collio Cabernet Sauvignon DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Collio Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, herbaceous notes, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Livon's Collio Cabernet Sauvignon is a classic expression of northeastern Italy's premier red wine region. The wine opens with dominant black cherry and blackcurrant aromatics, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous undertones characteristic of the Collio terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised beef, or tomato-based pasta dishes. The wine's moderate acidity and restrained oak treatment allow the varietal character to shine, with emerging tertiary tobacco and earth notes suggesting modest aging potential.

name: Livon  Tiareblu Grand Cru IGT
short_description (EN Store): Friuli Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe fruit, herbaceous depth, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Livon's Tiareblu Grand Cru IGT represents a classic Friuli interpretation of Bordeaux varietals. This medium-full bodied red blend marries the soft, plum-forward character of Merlot with the structured blackcurrant and cedar complexity of Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine displays balanced tannins and herbaceous undertones typical of the Collio terroir, making it a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. The blend's moderate acidity and approachable profile position it as an everyday premium red suitable for both casual dining and more formal table settings.

name: Hopes End  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Barossa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—classic Australian power.
description (EN Store): Hopes End Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley delivers the hallmark richness and structure expected of premium Australian Cabernet. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the core, supported by pronounced cedar and oak influence that speaks to careful winemaking. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide the backbone for extended cellaring and food pairing versatility. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with bottle age, adding complexity to an already layered profile.

name: Santa Carolina  Cellar Selection Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, juicy berries, and soft tannins—a versatile table wine for everyday enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Santa Carolina's Cellar Selection Merlot from Colchagua Valley delivers a fruit-forward profile anchored by ripe plum and a juicy medley of red berries. The palate shows good integration of medium tannins with a hint of vanilla from winemaking, creating a soft, approachable texture. Herbaceous notes add subtle complexity without overwhelming the wine's primary fruit character. At 14% ABV, this medium-full-bodied wine balances richness with drinkability, making it an excellent choice for casual dining and food pairing across a range of preparations.

name: Ronan by Clinet  Bordeaux AOC Red
short_description (EN Store): Second label from Pomerol's Günz-gravel plateau — Ronan Laborde's approachable entry into Clinet's graphite-and-iron house signature.
description (EN Store): Ronan Laborde — who took the reins at Château Clinet in 2004 and promptly cut new oak from 100% to 60% to chase delineation over extraction — lends his own name to this Bordeaux AOC cuvée, positioning it as the accessible expression of the same philosophy that drives one of Pomerol's most structurally distinctive estates. The wine draws on the broader Bordeaux appellation rather than the Pomerol plateau's 35-metre peak, yet the house's commitment to precision over plushness carries through. Fermentation in temperature-controlled stainless steel preserves primary fruit clarity, while aging in French oak adds spice and frame without overwhelming the mid-weight core. Where the Grand Vin is defined by Günz gravel and crasse de fer iron subsoil driving graphite minerality, Ronan by Clinet trades that site-specific intensity for broader Right Bank accessibility — more Merlot-forward generosity, softer tannin architecture, and an earlier drinking window. The special feature here is provenance transparency: the Laborde name on the label is a deliberate signal that this is a producer-driven, not négociant-anonymous, second label. Drink on release through five to seven years.

name: Chateau Brisson  Castillon Cotes de Bordeaux AOC
short_description (EN Store): Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Château Brisson's Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux presents a classic right-bank expression, built on 85% Merlot with 15% Cabernet Sauvignon. The blend delivers ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, supported by subtle herbaceous notes and a mineral undertone characteristic of the Castillon terroir. On the palate, soft, well-integrated tannins frame a medium-full body with balanced acidity, making this wine approachable in youth while retaining enough structure for modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Livio Felluga  Refosco dal Peduncolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Friuli red from native Refosco: dark cherry, peppery spice, mineral grip. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Livio Felluga's Refosco dal Peduncolo from Colli Orientali del Friuli showcases the distinctive character of this indigenous northeast Italian varietal. Medium-full bodied with balanced tannin and acidity, the wine opens with dark cherry and plum fruit, layered with herbaceous and black pepper notes that speak to the region's mineral terroir. A dry, structured red with earthy undertones and a clean mineral finish, it bridges traditional Italian winemaking with food-friendly versatility.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Syrah with ripe blackberry, black pepper, and smoky depth—a bold Gran Reserva from Puente Alto.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Syrah is a full-bodied expression from Chile's prestigious Puente Alto subregion in Maipo Valley. Dominated by ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit with assertive black pepper spice, the wine develops secondary layers of smoke and oak complexity. Tertiary notes of leather and earth emerge with air, reflecting careful aging and the terroir's mineral character. Medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, making this wine an excellent match for grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-cooked game.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Valley Cabernet with dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco complexity—food-friendly Gran Reserva from Chile's premier region.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Puente Alto, Maipo Valley, delivers the signature depth and structure expected from Chile's premier red-wine terroir. Dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—dominates the primary profile, supported by cedar and black pepper notes that speak to careful oak aging. Secondary tobacco and leather complexity emerge on the mid-palate, with mineral undertones that reflect the region's volcanic soils. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine an approachable yet serious expression, suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2015
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, structured tannins. Elegant Bordeaux for the table.
description (EN Store):
Château Clerc Milon 2015 is a Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (58%), with supporting Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, and Carmenère. The wine displays a medium-full body with medium-high tannins and balanced acidity, characteristic of Pauillac terroir.

On the nose, dominant blackcurrant and plum fruit lead, with secondary cedar and black pepper notes from oak aging. The palate shows structure and depth, with tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerging. Minerality rounds the finish.

This is a food-friendly Bordeaux suited to grilled red meats, lamb, braised beef, aged cheeses, and earthy mushroom preparations. Decant 30–45 minutes before service to open the aromatics.

name: Robert Mondavi   Woodbridge Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, vanilla oak, and tobacco—a classic food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Woodbridge Red Blend showcases the hallmarks of a well-crafted Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla oak and subtle cedar notes that speak to careful barrel aging. The wine carries medium-high tannins and a full body, with emerging tobacco and leather complexity that rewards pairing with grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses. A reliable, approachable expression suited to both casual dining and special occasions.

name: Chateau Deyrem Valentin  Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Margaux blend of Merlot & Cabernet Sauvignon with cassis, cedar, and refined structure.
description (EN Store): Château Deyrem Valentin's 2017 Margaux is a classically proportioned Bordeaux red blend anchored by 50% Merlot and 48% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Petit Verdot and Carmenere. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and structured tannins characteristic of the appellation. Primary aromas of cassis and dark plum give way to secondary cedar and tobacco notes, with tertiary graphite minerality emerging on the palate. Food-friendly and age-worthy, this wine pairs naturally with grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Ogier  Lou Camine Lirac
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Rhône blend with soft red berries, cherry, and balanced spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Ogier's Lou Camine Lirac is a Rhône South blend (Syrah, Grenache, Mourvèdre, Cinsault) offering ripe red-fruit character with cherry and blackberry notes. Medium-full body and soft tannins make this wine accessible and food-friendly, with subtle spice complexity. Ideal for casual dining, pizza, pasta, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Allegrini  Amarone Classico Reserva Fieramonte DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Allegrini's benchmark Amarone: concentrated dark cherry, velvety chocolate, and warming spice from appassimento-dried Corvina.
description (EN Store): Allegrini's Amarone Classico Reserva Fieramonte represents the pinnacle of Valpolicella craftsmanship. This full-bodied expression showcases the traditional appassimento method—drying grapes to concentrate their essence—yielding a wine of remarkable depth and complexity. Dark cherry and plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by rich dark chocolate and baking spice on the palate. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, revealing the wine's age-worthiness and structure. The medium-high tannins provide elegant grip without harshness, making this a versatile companion to grilled red meats, braised beef, aged cheeses, and game preparations.

name: Poggio Al Tesoro - (Bolgheri)  IL Seggio DOC
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Bordeaux blend: dark fruit, cedar, structured tannins. Elegant Tuscan expression of Merlot-led complexity.
description (EN Store):
Poggio Al Tesoro – IL Seggio DOC is a Bordeaux-style red blend from Bolgheri, Tuscany's premier terroir for international varietals. The blend—40% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, 20% Cabernet Franc, 10% Petit Verdot—delivers a medium-full bodied wine with medium-high tannins and balanced acidity.


On the nose, dark plum and blackcurrant dominate, supported by cedar and cassis notes. The palate shows structured tannins, black cherry, and a subtle tobacco undertone that evolves toward graphite minerality. This is a food-friendly yet age-worthy expression, built for both near-term enjoyment and cellar potential.


Ideal with grilled red meat, braised beef, lamb, and aged hard cheeses. A classic Tuscan statement wine.

name: Alois Lageder  Cabernet Riserva DOC
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Cabernet Riserva from Trentino-Alto Adige with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Alois Lageder's Cabernet Riserva DOC represents the producer's commitment to structured, age-worthy red wines from Italy's northern Alpine region. This full-bodied Cabernet Sauvignon displays dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, complemented by pronounced cedar and oak character from careful barrel aging. The wine's high tannin structure and tobacco-leather tertiary complexity suggest several years of cellaring potential, making it an excellent choice for serious collectors and those seeking a wine to accompany substantial meat dishes or aged cheeses.

name: Alois Lageder  Merlot MMXIII  DOC
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Trentino-Alto Adige; medium-full body with ripe red fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly balance.
description (EN Store): Alois Lageder's 100% Merlot from the Trentino-Alto Adige region presents a classic expression of the varietal in northern Italy's cool-climate terroir. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by subtle herbaceous notes and a soft, approachable tannin structure. The palate reveals balanced acidity and earthy undertones that suggest careful winemaking and modest aging potential. This is a versatile red suited to casual dining and food pairing across red meat, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Damilano  Barbera D'Asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Piedmont Barbera with bright acidity, red cherry, and peppery spice—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Damilano's Barbera d'Asti DOCG is a benchmark expression of Piedmont's most versatile red varietal. The wine displays the characteristic bright acidity and medium-full body that define quality Barbera, with primary notes of red cherry and plum complemented by black pepper and subtle herbaceous undertones. The palate shows good structure and food-friendly tannins, making it an ideal companion to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes. A reliable, well-crafted example of the appellation.

name: Damilano  Dolcetto D' Alba DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Dolcetto with ripe cherry, plum, and mineral earth—approachable yet structured for food.
description (EN Store): Damilano's Dolcetto d'Alba is a classic expression of Piedmont's most food-friendly red varietal. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and plum fruit, supported by subtle spice and earthy minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make it versatile at table—equally at home with pasta, roasted chicken, or cured meats. A wine that prioritizes drinkability without sacrificing complexity.

name: Damilano  Barbera D'Alba "LABLU" DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Barbera with bright cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Damilano's Barbera d'Alba LABLU is a classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal, showcasing the region's tradition of producing vibrant, food-friendly wines. The wine displays a medium-full body with prominent red cherry and plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and subtle earthy undertones. name: Damilano  Barolo "Lecinquevigne" DOCG

name: Damilano  Barolo "Liste" DOCG

name: Damilano  Barolo "Cannubi" DOCG

name: Damilano  Barolo "Cannubi" Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo Riserva from Barolo's Cannubi vineyard; structured, age-worthy, with rose and tar complexity.

name: Jermann  Red Angel Pinot Nero Venezia DOC

name: Mastroberardino  Radici Taurasi DOCG

name: Monteverro  Verruzzo IGT

name: Monteverro  Terra Di Monteverro IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend: dark fruit, cedar, and peppery spice. Structured, food-friendly, elegant.

This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged cheeses.

name: Rapitala by Giv  "Hugonis" Cabernet Sauvignon & Nero D' Avola IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian blend of Cabernet Sauvignon & Nero d'Avola with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity.

name: Goulee by Cos d'Estournel Medoc AOC
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Bordeaux blend led by Merlot; medium-full body, ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, food-friendly structure.

name: Chateau Chasse-Spleen
short_description (EN Store): Classic Médoc blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly structure for everyday elegance.

name: Chateau De Camensac  Haut Medoc Grand Cru Classe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend: ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and structured tannins for elegant cellaring.

A food-friendly Bordeaux for serious collectors and fine-dining tables.

name: Chateau Bel-Orme Tronquoy De Lalande "The Second Estate Of Château Rauzan-Gassies"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc second wine with ripe dark fruit, cedar structure, and food-friendly balance.

name: Les Pensees De La Tour Carnet "Les 2nd Vin De Chateau La Tour Carnet"

The wine opens with ripe dark plum and black cherry, layered with cassis and subtle cedar. Mid-palate tannins are refined and integrated, with tobacco leaf and graphite minerality adding tertiary complexity. The structure is classic Bordeaux—medium-full bodied with balanced acidity and a persistent finish.


A food-friendly expression of the appellation, suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb dishes.

name: Chateau Fombrauge  Grand Cru Classe
short_description (EN Store): Saint-Émilion Grand Cru Classé with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and silky tannins. Merlot-led Bordeaux blend.

name: Chateau D'Arcole  Grand Cru

name: Talamonti  Tre Saggi Montepulciano

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2013
short_description (EN Store): Fifth Growth Pauillac trading at Second Growth level — deep Garonne gravel, 75% new French oak, ripe-harvest house doctrine on the Bages plateau.
description (EN Store): When Jean-Michel Cazes launched a 15-year modernisation programme in 1975, his stated ambition was full phenolic ripeness — Lynch-Bages was famously among the last estates in Pauillac to harvest, a deliberate gamble on concentration over caution that redefined the château's trajectory. That doctrine is written into every bottle. The Bages plateau sits south-southwest of Pauillac town, its soils a deep, homogeneous bed of Garonne gravel — slow Pyrenean erosion deposited over limestone sub-strata — delivering the free drainage that forces vine roots deep and concentrates flavour while the Gironde estuary moderates temperature extremes. Parcel-by-parcel fermentation across 80 temperature-controlled stainless vats is followed by malolactic fermentation split one-third in vat, two-thirds in barrel, then 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new oak — a regime that builds structural density without sacrificing the estate's signature mid-palate generosity. The 2013 vintage in Bordeaux was cool and uneven, demanding rigorous selection; the result at Lynch-Bages is a leaner, more Cabernet-driven expression than the opulent 2015 or 2019, with graphite, cedar, and dark cassis in a tighter frame and notably fresh acidity for the appellation. A Fifth Growth by 1855 decree, Lynch-Bages has long traded at Second Growth prices — the 2013 offers a rare entry point into that quality tier at a fraction of the cost of its warmer-vintage siblings; drink 2024–2035.

name: Bodegas Salentein  Portillo Malbec Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Malbec from Mendoza with blackberry, plum & cocoa. Medium-full body, smooth tannins. Elegant Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Salentein's Portillo Malbec Organic is a single-varietal expression from Argentina's premier Mendoza region, crafted under organic viticulture principles. The wine displays a compelling aromatic profile dominated by blackberry and plum, layered with violet florality and cocoa undertones. This is a food-friendly, well-structured red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Shafer  TD-9

name: G.D. Vajra  Barolo Albe DOCG

name: G.D. Vajra  Barolo Bricco delle Viole DOCG

name: G.D. Vajra  Dolcett D'Alba Coste & Fossati DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Dolcetto from Barolo's Coste & Fossati vineyard: ripe cherry, plum, and spice with silky tannins.

name: Castello di Albola Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti Classico; red cherry, plum, and herbaceous notes with food-friendly acidity.

name: Chateau Lagrezette  Chevalier du Chateau Lagrezette

name: Famille Perrin  Réserve Côtes-du-Rhône Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône blend from Châteauneuf-du-Pape: ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and mineral depth. Food-friendly classic.

name: Dalrymple  Pinot Noir

name: Vasse Felix  Premier Range Cabernet Suavignon
short_description (EN Store): Margaret River Cabernet-led blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins. Elegant, age-worthy Australian classic.

name: De Stefani  Cabernet Sauvignon

De Stefani's Cabernet Sauvignon from Veneto delivers a compelling expression of the varietal at an accessible price point. name: De Stefani  Solèr

name: De Stefani  Kreda (Single Vineyard)
