name: Chateau Petrus  2011
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Pomerol's 40M-year-old blue smectite clay plateau — unclassified, unblended, unfiltered. 2011 drinks now through 2040+.
description (EN Store):
Pomerol carries no official classification, yet Château Pétrus commands prices that exceed every 1855 Médoc First Growth — a status built entirely on a geological accident: a 40-million-year-old blue smectite clay boutonnière rising to 40 metres at the plateau's apex, covering roughly 7.8 of the estate's 11.4 hectares. No neighbouring estate sits atop the same continuous clay lens. That iron-rich, water-retentive subsoil — overlaid by 60–80 cm of dark organic topsoil — is the sole reason Merlot achieves the structural depth here that Cabernet Sauvignon commands elsewhere in Bordeaux.

Harvest is compressed into 2–3 afternoons only, avoiding morning dew that would dilute must concentration. Fruit is 100% destemmed, optically sorted, and fermented in temperature-controlled concrete vats on native yeasts — no saignée, no inoculation. The wine ages 18–20 months in French oak, approximately 50% new (barrels steamed before use), then bottled unfiltered at natural ABV. No second wine exists; declassified fruit is sold anonymously as generic Pomerol.

The 2011 Bordeaux growing season was marked by a warm, dry summer interrupted by September rain — a vintage that rewarded estates with exceptional drainage. Pétrus's elevated clay plateau, which sheds excess water precisely because of its topographic position, was structurally advantaged. The 2011 drinks earlier than the monolithic 2010 or 2009, with a drinking window extending through 2040+. No vintage score is confirmed in available sources for this year.

name: Giuseppe Cortese  Barbaresco Rabaja
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Rabaja cru; silky tannins, floral complexity, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Giuseppe Cortese's 2017 Barbaresco Rabaja is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious single vineyards. The wine displays the hallmark elegance of the appellation: delicate red cherry and rose on the nose, layered with tar, licorice, and mineral notes. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure support a long, evolving palate that reveals leather and dried fruit in the finish. Ideal for cellaring or enjoying with aged cheeses and braised meats.

name: Giuseppe Cortese  Barbaresco Riserva Rabaja
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo Riserva from Barbaresco's Rabaja vineyard; structured, mineral-driven, age-worthy.
description (EN Store): Giuseppe Cortese's Barbaresco Riserva Rabaja is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious terroirs. The 2013 vintage displays the hallmark elegance of Barbaresco: refined red fruit aromatics layered with rose petal and mineral tension, supported by firm, silky tannins and bright acidity. Secondary notes of tar and licorice emerge mid-palate, while tertiary leather and tobacco complexity develops with bottle age. This is a wine built for the table—pair with grilled lamb, game, or aged cheeses—and for the cellar, where it will continue to evolve gracefully over the next 15–20 years.

name: Travaglini Gattinara  Nebbiolo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Gattinara with silky tannins, red cherry, and earthy complexity—a refined Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Travaglini Gattinara is a single-varietal Nebbiolo from the prestigious Gattinara subregion of Piedmont, Italy. The 2017 vintage expresses the grape's signature elegance: a core of red cherry and dried rose on the nose, supported by subtle tar and licorice notes from careful aging. The palate is medium-full bodied with refined, silky tannins and bright acidity that carries flavors of cherry, leather, and black pepper into a long, mineral-driven finish. This wine reflects the terroir's volcanic soils and continental climate, developing tertiary tobacco and earthy complexity that rewards patient cellaring. A food-friendly expression of Nebbiolo suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Travaglini Gattinara  Tre Vigne
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Gattinara with structured tannins, red cherry, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Travaglini Gattinara Tre Vigne is a single-varietal Nebbiolo from Italy's prestigious Gattinara subregion in Piedmont. The 2018 vintage displays the hallmark structure and elegance of northern Italian Nebbiolo: a medium-full body supported by medium-high acidity and tannins that frame red cherry, tar, and leather notes. Secondary tobacco and tertiary earth minerality emerge with air, revealing the wine's layered complexity. This is a food-friendly expression suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Travaglini Gattinara  IL Sogno
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Gattinara with silky tannins, dried rose, and earthy complexity—a refined Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Travaglini Gattinara IL Sogno is a 100% Nebbiolo expression from the prestigious Gattinara subregion of Piedmont, Italy. The 2016 vintage showcases the varietal's signature elegance: a refined palate of red cherry and dried rose on entry, supported by medium-high acidity and silky, well-integrated tannins. Secondary notes of tar and leather emerge mid-palate, while tertiary earth and tobacco complexity develops with air, reflecting the wine's structure and aging potential. A food-friendly, sophisticated red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Torres  Sangre de Toro Red
short_description (EN Store): Garnacha-led Spanish blend from Toro with ripe red fruit, spice, and earthy depth—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Torres Sangre de Toro is a Toro-region red blend anchored by Garnacha, with supporting Cariñena, Syrah, and Tempranillo. The wine delivers a medium-full frame with ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, complemented by subtle spice and earthy undertones. The palate shows balanced tannin and acidity, with emerging tobacco and leather complexity that reflects the blend's maturity. A classic Spanish food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and cured charcuterie.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection  Vint Bourbon Barrel-Aged Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, vanilla oak, and tobacco complexity—age-worthy and structured.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Private Selection Bourbon Barrel-Aged Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley delivers a classic expression of California Cabernet with depth and refinement. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by generous vanilla oak and cedar from barrel aging. The wine's full body and high tannin structure provide excellent aging potential, while secondary cocoa and tertiary tobacco notes add layers of complexity. Ideal for serious red-meat dishes and cellar-worthy collection.

name: Robert Mondavi  Private Selection Vint Rum Barrel-Aged Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Napa Merlot aged in rum barrels; plum-forward with soft tannins and vanilla oak complexity.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Private Selection Merlot from Oakville showcases the signature ripe plum and berry character of Napa Valley fruit, enhanced by rum barrel aging that imparts subtle vanilla and oak complexity. Medium-full bodied with integrated tannins and a juicy, approachable palate, this wine balances fruit-forward appeal with textural refinement. The herbaceous undertone adds subtle complexity without overwhelming the primary fruit expression.

name: Shafer  Hillside Select Cabernet Sauvignon 2018
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cabernet of power and structure: blackcurrant and cedar with firm tannins and aging potential.
description (EN Store): Shafer's Hillside Select Cabernet Sauvignon 2018 is a full-bodied expression of Napa Valley terroir, built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary character, while black pepper and vanilla add textural complexity. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity promise graceful evolution over the next 15–20 years, with tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerging as it matures. This is a serious, age-worthy Cabernet suited to the finest red-meat cuisine and cellar investment.

name: Conti Zecca  Negroamaro Leverano Liranu Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Puglia's Negroamaro shines in this riserva: dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice in a smooth, food-friendly frame.
description (EN Store): Conti Zecca's Negroamaro Leverano Liranu Riserva is a medium-full-bodied red from Puglia's sun-drenched vineyards, built on a foundation of dark cherry and ripe berry fruit. The palate reveals layers of smoke and black pepper spice, balanced by rounded tannins and a clean, dry finish. This is a wine of approachable complexity—bold enough for grilled meats and hearty pasta, yet refined enough to reward contemplation. The riserva designation signals extended aging, adding subtle earth notes and structural polish to the primary fruit expression.

name: Brancaia  Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese-Merlot blend with ripe cherry, leather, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly Riserva.
description (EN Store): Brancaia's Chianti Classico Riserva is a structured Tuscan red built on 80% Sangiovese with 20% Merlot, delivering the classic elegance of the Chianti Classico zone. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, supported by dried herb and subtle oak from careful aging. Mid-palate reveals leather, tobacco, and earthy minerality—hallmarks of Sangiovese's tertiary complexity. The medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Brancaia  ILatraia IGT Rosso Toscana
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of power and elegance—dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins for serious red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Brancaia's ILatraia IGT Rosso Toscana is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Petit Verdot, with Cabernet Franc adding aromatic complexity. The 2020 vintage expresses the ripe, structured character typical of Tuscan Super Tuscan expressions: dominant dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with cedar and black pepper from the Petit Verdot component. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins demand food pairing—this is a wine built for the table, not the glass alone. Graphite minerality and dried herb notes suggest age-worthiness and complexity beyond the immediate vintage.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Saffredi Maremma Toscana
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Cabernet, Merlot & Petit Verdot. Dark fruit, cedar, structured tannins. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store):
Fattoria Le Pupille Saffredi is a Maremma-based Super Tuscan red that blends 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 35% Merlot, and 5% Petit Verdot into a medium-full-bodied wine of classical structure and depth. The primary character opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum, supported by black cherry notes that give the wine immediate appeal. Cedar and vanilla oak from winemaking provide secondary complexity, while subtle tobacco and earth undertones suggest age-worthiness and tertiary development.


The wine's medium tannin and balanced acidity make it a versatile food companion—equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef dishes, or aged cheeses. A food-friendly expression of Tuscan winemaking tradition.

name: Alta Mora Etna Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Etna Rosso from Cusumano: mineral-driven Nerello Mascalese with red cherry, black pepper, and volcanic complexity.
description (EN Store): Alta Mora Etna Rosso is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from Sicily's volcanic Etna region. The wine displays a distinctive mineral-forward profile characteristic of high-altitude, lava-soil viticulture, with dominant red cherry and black fruit supported by peppery spice and herbaceous undertones. Medium-full body and bracing acidity frame a structured palate; tertiary leather and earthy notes emerge with air, suggesting food-friendly versatility across grilled meats, tomato-based dishes, and Mediterranean preparations.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Barolo di Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's heartland: dark fruit, structured tannins, and mineral complexity for serious collectors.
description (EN Store):
Marchesi di Barolo's Barolo di Barolo DOCG (2019) is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Italy's most prestigious red wine region. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, layered with tar and smoke, followed by secondary cedar and tobacco notes that speak to careful oak aging. Tertiary leather and earthy minerality emerge on the palate, revealing the wine's potential for further evolution in bottle.

Full-bodied with medium-high acidity and tannin, this is a wine built for contemplation and food pairing—a benchmark expression of Barolo's power and elegance.

name: Sutil  Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet with dark fruit depth, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure—a versatile Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Sutil Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley presents a classically structured red wine built on ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Medium-full body and balanced tannin provide approachable yet serious drinking, while secondary oak-derived cedar and vanilla notes add complexity. Black pepper spice and subtle cocoa undertones round the palate, making this wine a reliable partner for grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Sutil  Reserve Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Carmenere with dark plum, black cherry, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Reserve from Colchagua Valley.
description (EN Store): Sutil's Reserve Carmenere is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Colchagua Valley, showcasing the signature character of this distinctive grape. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary aromatics, complemented by herbaceous bell pepper notes that define Carmenere's varietal identity. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannins frame layers of cocoa and subtle oak, with emerging leather and earthy complexity in the finish. A versatile, well-structured red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Sutil  Reserve Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Merlot with ripe dark fruit, cocoa, and balanced tannins—food-friendly Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Sutil Reserve Merlot from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: dominant ripe black cherry and dark plum on the nose, supported by subtle cocoa and vanilla oak on the palate. Medium-full body and approachable medium tannins make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or rich pasta preparations. A reliable, well-crafted red for everyday enjoyment and entertaining.

name: Ceretto  Langhe Rosso Monsordo BIO DOC
short_description (EN Store): Organic Piedmont blend of Cabernet, Syrah & Merlot with dark fruit, spice & earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Ceretto's Langhe Rosso Monsordo BIO DOC is an organic Piedmont blend combining Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, and Merlot in a food-friendly medium-full structure. Dark plum and blackcurrant dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous undertones. Cedar and subtle oak emerge on the secondary layer, while tertiary earth and leather notes add complexity and age-worthiness. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Ceretto  Nebbiolo d' Alba "Bernardina" BIO DOC
short_description (EN Store): Organic Nebbiolo from Alba with elegant red cherry, licorice, and mineral complexity—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store):
Ceretto's Nebbiolo d'Alba Bernardina is an organically farmed expression of one of Italy's most noble red varieties. The 2020 vintage showcases the characteristic elegance of Alba-grown Nebbiolo: bright red cherry and licorice on the nose, supported by fine-grained tannins and vibrant acidity. Secondary notes of tar and black pepper emerge mid-palate, while earthy minerality and subtle leather undertones suggest age-worthiness and complexity.

Medium-full bodied with a dry, structured finish, this wine bridges the gap between approachable and serious—ideal for grilled meats, braised beef, mushroom dishes, and aged cheeses. The organic certification reflects Ceretto's commitment to sustainable viticulture in Piedmont.

name: Ceretto  Barbaresco BIO DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco: silky tannins, rose and cherry, mineral complexity. Organic farming.
description (EN Store): Ceretto's Barbaresco BIO DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone, produced under organic certification. The 2018 vintage expresses the varietal's signature elegance: a perfumed nose of red cherry and rose, supported by mineral undertones and subtle tar. Medium-full body and refined tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining the structure for 10+ years of cellaring. Ideal for lamb, mushroom risotto, and aged cheeses.

name: Ceretto  Barbaresco "Bernadot" BIO DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Bernadot vineyard; organic-farmed, structured with rose, tar, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Ceretto's Barbaresco "Bernadot" BIO DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone, farmed organically. The 2017 vintage expresses the varietal's signature aromatics—rose petals, red cherry, and licorice—layered with mineral earth and dried herbs on the nose. On the palate, medium-high tannins and acidity frame dark fruit, tar, and tobacco leaf, building toward a long, elegant finish. This is a wine built for cellaring and food pairing, particularly with braised beef, mushroom dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Ceretto  Barolo "Brunate" BIO DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Organic Nebbiolo from Barolo's Brunate cru: elegant red cherry, rose, and mineral complexity with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Ceretto's Barolo Brunate represents the classical elegance of Piedmont's most prestigious red wine appellation. This 100% Nebbiolo from the celebrated Brunate vineyard site expresses the variety's signature combination of delicate floral aromatics and structured tannins. The wine opens with red cherry and rose, evolving through layers of tar, licorice, and smoke on the mid-palate. With bottle age, tertiary notes of tobacco, leather, and mineral complexity emerge, rewarding patient cellaring. Produced under organic protocols, this 2017 vintage demonstrates the balance and finesse that define top-tier Barolo.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Pelofino Rosso IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Cabernet, and Merlot with ripe red fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Fattoria Le Pupille's Pelofino Rosso IGT is a classically proportioned Super Tuscan blend uniting Sangiovese, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot in equal measure. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle blackcurrant depth and herbaceous cedar notes. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannin make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and tomato-based pasta dishes. The blend's structure reflects Tuscan tradition while the Cabernet and Merlot components add roundness and approachability.

name: Arnaldo Caprai  Montefalco Rosso Riserva DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sagrantino-led Umbrian blend with dark plum, spice, and tertiary tobacco complexity. Food-friendly riserva for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Arnaldo Caprai's Montefalco Rosso Riserva is a structured Umbrian red built on Sagrantino's dark fruit foundation, supported by Merlot and Barbera. The primary layer delivers ripe dark plum and blackcurrant with bright black cherry undertones. Oak aging introduces spice and cedar notes that frame the mid-palate. Extended cellaring develops tertiary tobacco, leather, and earthy minerality, lending the wine complexity and food-pairing versatility. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this an approachable yet age-worthy expression of the Montefalco terroir.

name: Tenuta di Campriano Buccia Nera  Chianti Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese-led Riserva with dark fruit, tobacco, and mineral complexity—elegant food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Tenuta di Campriano Buccia Nera Chianti Riserva DOCG is a traditionally styled Tuscan red anchored by 85% Sangiovese, with Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon adding depth and structure. The 2018 vintage expresses the hallmark character of Chianti Riserva: ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, layered with dried herb and subtle oak spice from careful aging. Mid-palate reveals tobacco leaf and cedar complexity, while the finish carries earthy minerality and soft leather notes—markers of a wine built for the table and for evolution in bottle. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile partner to Tuscan cuisine, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Tenein  Bordeaux Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux Supérieur with ripe plum, cassis, and cedar—elegant, food-friendly, and approachable.
description (EN Store): Château Tenein's Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically proportioned blend anchored by 55% Merlot, with 25% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Cabernet Franc providing structure and aromatic complexity. The wine expresses ripe red and dark fruit—plum and black cherry—with supporting notes of cassis and cedar. A soft tannin profile and balanced acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to everyday entertaining and casual fine dining. The herbaceous undertone and subtle minerality reflect the terroir of the Bordeaux Supérieur appellation, positioning this as a reliable, well-made expression of the region's signature style.

name: San Polo  Brunello di Montalcino Podernovi DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino's prestigious DOCG zone; structured tannins, dark fruit, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): San Polo Brunello di Montalcino Podernovi DOCG 2015 is a single-varietal expression of Sangiovese from one of Tuscany's most revered terroirs. The 2015 vintage delivers a classic profile: ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, supported by secondary notes of tobacco leaf and cedar oak. The palate shows medium-full body with firm, well-integrated tannins and mineral-driven acidity that frames the wine's structure. Tertiary layers of leather and earth suggest the wine's capacity for further evolution in bottle. A food-friendly Tuscan red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Castello Di Ama  Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico with ripe cherry, plum, and herbaceous notes; medium-full body and balanced tannins for food-friendly enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Castello Di Ama's Chianti Classico DOCG 2022 is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 96% Sangiovese with 4% Merlot. The wine displays a harmonious balance of ripe red fruit—cherry and plum—layered with herbaceous minerality and subtle earth. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make this a versatile table wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes. The finish carries mineral precision and gentle spice, reflecting the terroir of Chianti Classico.

name: Agricola San Felice  Il Grigio di San Felice Chianti Classico Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese Riserva from Chianti Classico with ripe cherry, earthy minerality, and refined structure.
description (EN Store): A single-varietal Sangiovese expression from Agricola San Felice's Chianti Classico holdings, this 2021 Riserva demonstrates the classic profile of the region: bright red cherry and plum fruit balanced by herbaceous undertones and mineral-driven acidity. Oak aging contributes subtle vanilla and spice complexity, while tertiary development hints at leather and tobacco character typical of well-structured Tuscan reds. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin make this wine a versatile food companion, equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or aged cheeses.

name: Agricola San Felice  Bell'Aja Bolgheri DOC
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Merlot-Cabernet blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins—elegant Tuscan expression.
description (EN Store): Agricola San Felice's Bell'Aja represents the Bolgheri terroir through a classical 60% Merlot, 40% Cabernet Sauvignon blend. The 2022 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied red with dark plum and blackcurrant primary notes, supported by cedar and herbaceous secondary character. Structured medium tannins and a mineral backbone provide food-friendly complexity without excessive extraction. This is a food-centric Tuscan red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Agricola San Felice  Vigorello Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Pugnitello, Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Petit Verdot. Dark fruit, cedar spice, structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Agricola San Felice's Vigorello is a classically structured Super Tuscan that marries Tuscany's indigenous Pugnitello with international Bordeaux varieties. The 2019 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by cedary oak and warming spice from the blend's Cabernet and Petit Verdot components. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone for food-forward dining, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest modest aging potential. A food-friendly expression of modern Tuscan winemaking.

name: Tenute Del Cerro  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montepulciano with bright cherry, herbaceous notes, and structured tannins—a classic Tuscan expression.
description (EN Store): Tenute Del Cerro's Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG showcases the signature character of Sangiovese from one of Tuscany's most prestigious appellations. The wine opens with vibrant cherry and red fruit aromatics, complemented by herbaceous undertones and subtle spice. Medium-Full body and high acidity provide excellent structure and food-friendliness, while medium-high tannins add grip and aging potential. Tertiary notes of leather and tobacco emerge on the palate, reflecting the wine's complexity and terroir-driven minerality. A versatile pairing for grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenute Del Cerro  Vino Nobile di Montepulciano Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese Riserva from Montepulciano with layered red fruit, dried herbs, and leather complexity.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Sangiovese Riserva from Vino Nobile di Montepulciano, this 2018 vintage showcases the region's hallmark balance of bright acidity and refined tannin. The palate opens with red cherry and herbaceous notes, evolving toward earthy undertones and subtle spice on the mid-palate. Tertiary leather and tobacco character emerge with air, reflecting the wine's aging potential and complexity. Medium-Full body and high acidity make this a versatile food wine, particularly suited to tomato-based dishes, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenuta Lunelli  Ziggurat Montefalco Rosso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Umbrian blend with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly structure for meat and pasta.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Lunelli's Ziggurat Montefalco Rosso is a structured red blend anchored by Sangiovese (70%), with Sagrantino, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot adding depth and tannin grip. The wine displays ripe red and dark fruit on the nose, layered with spice, earth, and subtle cedar notes. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone for pairing with grilled meats, braised beef, and tomato-based pasta dishes. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with air, suggesting modest aging potential and versatility across Mediterranean and Italian cuisine.

name: Monte Antico  Rosso IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese blend with cherry, herbs, and earthy complexity. Medium-full body, high acidity, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Monte Antico Rosso IGT is a Tuscan red blend anchored by Sangiovese with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine presents a medium-full body with high acidity and medium-high tannin, delivering a structured, food-friendly profile. Primary notes of cherry and red fruit are complemented by herbaceous character; secondary spice and earth notes add complexity, while tertiary leather and tobacco emerge with bottle age. The high acidity and firm tannin structure make this wine particularly versatile at the table, pairing well with tomato-based dishes, grilled meats, and mushroom preparations.

name: Pala  Cannonau Riserva Sardegna DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sardinian Grenache Riserva with dark fruit, spice, and mineral complexity—elegant, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Pala's Cannonau Riserva from Sardinia showcases the depth and structure of aged Grenache in its Mediterranean homeland. This medium-full-bodied red displays ripe dark plum and cherry fruit balanced by herbaceous spice and mineral undertones, with leather and dried-fruit complexity from extended aging. A versatile, elegant expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and rustic Mediterranean fare.

name: San Marzano  Talo Primitivo di Manduria DOP
short_description (EN Store): Bold Primitivo from Manduria with dark cherry, raspberry, and smoke. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): San Marzano's Talo Primitivo di Manduria DOP is a characterful red from Puglia's warm southern terroir. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark fruit, cherry, and raspberry, followed by a palate of lashings of red and dark fruit with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper. Medium-full bodied with fully rounded, smooth tannins, it delivers complexity without pretension—eminently drinkable and versatile at the table. A touch spicier than typical Primitivo, with good structure and balance.

name: Tommasi  Graticco Appassionato IGT
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Corvina-Merlot blend with dark fruit, cherry, and herbaceous notes. Balanced, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Tommasi's Graticco Appassionato is a Valpolicella-based red from Veneto, blending Corvina and Merlot into a medium-bodied wine of approachable character. The primary expression centers on dark fruit compote and cherry, with herbaceous undertones typical of the region. Subtle oak integration and balanced tannin structure support versatile food pairing, from braised meats and tomato-based dishes to aged cheeses and charcuterie. A reliable, well-proportioned expression suited to casual dining and everyday occasions.

name: Altos de Rioja  Altos R Tempranillo
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja Tempranillo with ripe plum, cherry, and leather—structured, food-friendly, excellent value.
description (EN Store): Altos de Rioja's Tempranillo from Rioja Alavesa delivers a classic expression of Spain's signature red varietal. Deep ruby in color with pronounced aromas of dark plum and red cherry, the wine shows medium-full body and bracing acidity that frames its core fruit character. Vanilla oak and black pepper notes from aging provide structure and complexity, while subtle leather and tobacco undertones suggest careful winemaking and bottle maturity. A dry, bold wine with a long finish—ideal for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. Excellent value at this price point.

name: Altos de Rioja  Altos R Crianza
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja Tempranillo with ripe plum, leather, and vanilla oak—a structured, food-friendly crianza with excellent value.
description (EN Store): Altos de Rioja's Crianza is a classically styled Tempranillo from Rioja Alavesa, delivering the region's signature balance of fruit intensity and savory complexity. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum aromas, supported by subtle vanilla oak and dried herb notes from its time in barrel. On the palate, medium-full body and medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, while medium-high acidity keeps the wine fresh and food-friendly. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes add depth and sophistication. A versatile bottle suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and cured charcuterie.

name: Cuatro Mill  Discolo Cinco de Copas
short_description (EN Store): Spanish Toro red with ripe red fruit, plum, and spice—food-friendly medium-full body for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Cuatro Mill's Discolo Cinco de Copas is a Spanish red from the Toro region, crafted in a medium-full bodied style that balances approachable fruit with subtle structure. Ripe red fruit and dark plum dominate the primary profile, supported by gentle spice notes and earthy undertones. The wine's moderate tannin and acidity make it versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or Mediterranean fare.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  Clemente VII Riserva Chianti Classico DOCG Riserva

short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Panzano with ripe cherry, tobacco, and mineral depth—a classic Chianti Classico Gran Selezione.

Crafted from 100% Sangiovese, this 2018 vintage showcases the varietal's signature balance of bright red fruit, earthy minerality, and structured tannins. The wine opens with aromas of red cherry and plum, evolving toward dried herbs and subtle oak influence. On the palate, medium-full body and medium-high acidity provide excellent food compatibility, while tertiary notes of tobacco and leather suggest complexity from careful aging. A food-friendly yet sophisticated expression of Tuscan winemaking tradition.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  Clemente VII Gran Selezione Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione
short_description (EN Store): 100% Sangiovese Gran Selezione from Chianti Classico; structured red with cherry, earth, and tobacco complexity.

Structured and age-worthy, this Gran Selezione designation reflects rigorous selection and extended aging, positioning it as a serious expression of Chianti Classico terroir.

name: Maison de Grand Esprit  L'Etre Magique Bourgogne
short_description (EN Store): Burgundy blend of Pinot Noir and Gamay with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure.

name: Maison de Grand Esprit  La Mysteriale Chateauneuf du Pape
short_description (EN Store): Châteauneuf-du-Pape blend of dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity; food-friendly Rhône classic.

name: Sterling  Vintner S Collection Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Calistoga Cabernet with dark fruit, cedar, and balanced tannin—a versatile table wine.
description (EN Store): Sterling Vineyard's Vintner S Collection Cabernet Sauvignon from Calistoga delivers a classic California expression of the varietal. Medium-full bodied with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, supported by subtle cedar and black pepper notes. The palate shows balanced medium tannin and vanilla-oak influence, making this wine approachable yet structured enough for food pairing. name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci  Villa Da Vinci S.to Ippolito
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Syrah & Merlot—elegant red fruit, spice & earth for food-friendly dining.

Expect red cherry and dark plum on the nose, with herbaceous and earthy undertones that evolve on the palate. The wine's food-friendly profile suits everything from pasta and grilled meats to roasted poultry and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci   1502 Da Vinci in Romagna Sangiovese Appassimento
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Appassimento from Tuscany: ripe dark fruit, leather, and mineral complexity at a premium price point.
description (EN Store): Cantine Leonardo Da Vinci's 1502 Da Vinci in Romagna is a Sangiovese-dominant blend (85% Sangiovese, 15% other red grapes) crafted in the Appassimento style—a traditional Italian technique of partial drying grapes before fermentation to concentrate flavour and structure. The result is a medium-full bodied red with layered complexity: ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, supported by dried fruit and subtle spice, with emerging leather and tobacco notes that suggest careful aging potential. Mineral-driven finish and balanced tannins make this a versatile food wine suited to classic Italian cuisine and beyond.

name: Brancaia  Rosso "Tre" IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Merlot & Cabernet. Medium-full body, balanced structure, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Brancaia's Rosso "Tre" is a Tuscan Super Tuscan blend anchored by 80% Sangiovese with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The blend's composition—traditional Sangiovese backbone reinforced by international varieties—positions it as a food-friendly expression suited to grilled meats, braised preparations, and tomato-driven pasta dishes.

name: Brancaia  Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Chianti Classico: red cherry, plum, and herbaceous notes with mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): A classic expression of Chianti Classico, this 100% Sangiovese wine showcases the region's signature red-fruit character and food-friendly structure. Bright cherry and plum dominate the palate, supported by herbaceous undertones and subtle earth, while a mineral finish provides elegant complexity. name: Bodegas LAN  Rioja Crianza

Tertiary layers of tobacco and leather emerge on the finish, suggesting careful oak aging and complexity suited to cellaring or immediate enjoyment. A versatile pairing wine for grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Bodegas LAN  Rioja Gran Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Rioja Alta Gran Reserva with dark plum depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure. Elegant, age-worthy Tempranillo.

name: La Spinetta  Barbaresco Valeirano DOCG

name: Velenosi  Montepulciano D' Abruzzo DOC Circum
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano from Abruzzo with ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, and mineral backbone—a versatile food-friendly red.

name: Silver Oak  Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley 2018
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cabernet-led Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic food-friendly California red.

A food-friendly California red with depth and aging potential, suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Tenuta di Biserno  Biserno di Biserno IGT Toscana
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet Franc-led flagship from Bibbona's calcareous conglomerate soils — 70% new French oak, 15 months, outside Bolgheri DOC by design.
description (EN Store):
When Lodovico Antinori — architect of Ornellaia and Masseto — sold those estates and turned his attention to the stonier hills of Bibbona, he made a deliberate break: where Bolgheri's template demands Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot at the fore, the Biserno flagship inverts that hierarchy, placing Cabernet Franc at roughly one-third of the blend alongside equal parts Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, with a small Petit Verdot component (~6%).

The estate sits at approximately 90 m elevation in Alta Maremma, on soils of calcareous clay rich in calcium carbonate interlaced with the mineral-dense 'Bolgheri Conglomerate' — a stony, gravelly matrix that stresses the vine and concentrates aromatic intensity. Constant Tyrrhenian Sea breezes moderate summer heat and suppress humidity, preserving the Mediterranean herb register that defines the house style.

Grapes are hand-harvested, sorted on conveyor, and vinified plot-by-plot in temperature-controlled stainless steel with 3–4 weeks maceration at max 28°C. Malolactic fermentation runs spontaneously, split ~65% in barrique / 35% in steel. The wine then ages 15 months in French oak barriques, approximately 70% new, followed by a minimum 12 months in bottle before release.

The estate deliberately operates outside the Bolgheri DOC boundary — a structural choice, not an oversight — allowing the varietal freedom that makes Cabernet Franc dominance possible under Toscana IGT.

The 2022 vintage has no dedicated note in the brand library, but the blend mirrors the 2021 composition; expect the characteristic Mediterranean herb intensity and dense concentration typical of the estate's recent releases, with a drinking window extending well into the 2030s.

name: Tenuta di Biserno  IL Pino di Biserno DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Cabernet Franc-led Super Tuscan from Bibbona's calcareous conglomerate soils, aged 12 months in French oak — the second wine that breaks the Bolgheri mould.
description (EN Store): When Lodovico Antinori — architect of Ornellaia and Masseto — sold those estates and turned his attention to the stonier, hillier hills of Bibbona, he made a deliberate varietal bet: Cabernet Franc, not Cabernet Sauvignon, would anchor the house. Il Pino di Biserno is the estate's second wine, built on that same conviction. The ~49-hectare estate sits at roughly 90 metres above sea level on soils of calcareous clay dense in calcium carbonate and the mineral-rich 'Bolgheri Conglomerate,' with gravelly upper slopes reserved for Cabernet Franc; constant Tyrrhenian Sea breezes suppress summer humidity and preserve aromatic lift. Grapes are hand-harvested, sorted on conveyor, and vinified plot-by-plot in temperature-controlled stainless steel with 3–4 weeks maceration at a maximum of 28°C; 75% of the volume then ages 12 months in a mix of new and used French oak barriques, while 25% remains in steel — a split that preserves the Franc's herbal precision against oak weight. The blend of Cabernet Franc, Merlot, and Petit Verdot deliberately excludes Cabernet Sauvignon, making it one of the few coastal Tuscan reds at this level built entirely without the region's dominant variety. The 2022 vintage has no specific estate notes on record, but the wine's structure and the estate's track record on calcareous conglomerate suggest drinking well from 2026 through 2034.

name: Tenuta San Guido  Sassicaia 2019
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC's sole estate appellation: calcareous marl and gabbro soils, 24-month Tronçais barriques, 2019's textbook-balanced season.
description (EN Store):
Mario Incisa della Rocchetta planted Cabernet Sauvignon on the Castiglioncello hill in 1944 — decades before French varieties were accepted in Tuscany — and aged the wine in French barriques at a time when the practice was virtually unknown in Italy. The wine remained a private family project until the commercial debut of the 1968 vintage, making Sassicaia the origin point of the entire Super Tuscan movement and the only wine in Italy to hold a single-estate DOC appellation.

The estate's plots — Castiglioncello, Aia Nuova, Cerreta, and others — sit between 100 and 400 m a.s.l. on soils of calcareous marl, gabbro, alberese, and the stony pebble deposits that give the wine its name (sasso = stone). The Tyrrhenian Sea to the west and the Bibbona–Castagneto hill amphitheatre channel cooling summer breezes that preserve acidity and aromatic precision.

The 2019 was fermented in temperature-controlled stainless steel at ≤28–29°C using estate-selected indigenous yeasts; malolactic fermentation completed spontaneously in steel. The wine then aged 24 months in French oak barriques (Tronçais, Allier, Jupilles; extra-fine grain, light toast, long seasoning) in a split of approximately 40% new, 40% first-fill, 20% second-fill. The blend is 85% Cabernet Sauvignon, 15% Cabernet Franc, harvested just before full phenolic ripeness to preserve fragrance and restrain alcohol.

Unlike Bolgheri peers who soften structure with Merlot, Sassicaia holds a Graves-like austerity — graphite and Mediterranean garrigue over cassis, framed by fine-grained tannins and bracing acidity rather than fruit weight. The estate describes 2019 as a near-ideal growing season of great phenolic and acidic balance; drinking window 2026–2052.

name: Hardys  Brave New world Shiraz Black

description (EN Store): Hardys Brave New World Shiraz Black is a full-bodied red wine from McLaren Vale, Australia's premier Syrah region. Secondary smoke and licorice notes add complexity, while tertiary leather and earth characters emerge with bottle age. A classic Australian Shiraz built for hearty meat dishes and bold flavors.

name: Pian dell'Orino  Brunello di Montalcino Vigneti del Versante DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino: ripe red fruit, tobacco, and mineral earth. Food-friendly Sangiovese for grilled meats and aged cheeses.

name: Pian dell'Orino  Brunello di Montalcino Bassolino di Sopra DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino with ripe cherry, plum, and tobacco complexity; structured tannins and earthy depth.

name: Rocca di Montegrossi  Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Pure Sangiovese from Chianti Classico: elegant red fruit, herbaceous spice, and mineral structure for food-friendly dining.
description (EN Store): Rocca di Montegrossi's Chianti Classico DOCG is a 100% Sangiovese expression rooted in Tuscany's most prestigious red-wine region. name: Rocca di Montegrossi  Geremia IGT

name: Rocca di Montegrossi  Chianti Classico Gran Selezione DOCG Vigneto San Marcellino
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Chianti Classico Gran Selezione with cherry, leather, and mineral complexity; elegant structure for food.

name: Bret Brothers  Glou de Bret

name: Bret Brothers  Brouilly Climat "la Piloniere"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Beaujolais Cru from Brouilly's premier climat. Bright red fruit, silky tannins, food-friendly finesse.
description (EN Store):
Bret Brothers' Brouilly Climat "la Pilonière" is a refined expression of Beaujolais Cru, sourced from one of the appellation's most prestigious vineyard sites. The 2018 vintage showcases the elegance and approachability that define quality Gamay from this terroir.

The palate is medium-bodied with silky, well-integrated tannins and vibrant acidity that lifts the wine's natural fruit expression. Subtle earthy notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity without heaviness.


This is a wine built for the table—versatile across lighter meat dishes, pasta, and cheese courses. Drink now through 2026 for optimal freshness and charm.

name: Chateau Clinet  2017
short_description (EN Store): Pomerol's rare Cab Sauv holdout: Günz gravel & crasse de fer iron clay, 28–30-day maceration, unfined/unfiltered, 75–80% new French oak.
description (EN Store): When Ronan Laborde inherited stewardship of Clinet in 2004, one of his most deliberate acts was retaining Cabernet Sauvignon — a pre-1956-frost survivor — in a Pomerol landscape that had largely abandoned it. That decision is the structural spine of every bottle. Clinet sits at the highest point of the Pomerol plateau (35 m elevation), its 21 parcels rooted in a mosaic of Günz gravel over deep clay with iron-rich crasse de fer subsoil: the gravel drains and concentrates, the iron clay retains moisture and imparts a graphite-mineral precision that separates Clinet from the purely plush Merlot register of its neighbours. Since 2014, 17 double-walled stainless vats (15–45 hl) plus one concrete vat allow strict plot-by-plot fermentation; grapes arrive by gravity via cone-shaped vessels. Maceration runs 28–30 days, malolactic fermentation completes in barrel, and the wine ages 16–22 months in approximately 75–80% new French oak, with press wine held separately and incorporated only if additional structure is warranted. Bottled neither fined nor filtered, preserving the mineral imprint of the terroir intact. The 2017 vintage — absent from the estate's own benchmark notes — represents a leaner, more tensile expression of Clinet's iron-clay core, where the Cabernet Sauvignon component (12–20% depending on selection) sharpens the mid-palate and extends the finish. Drink from 2025 through 2038+.

name: Castello di Fonterutoli  Badiola
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend with ripe red fruit, cedar spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and age-worthy.
description (EN Store): Badiola from Castello di Fonterutoli is a Tuscan red blend that marries Sangiovese's classic cherry and earth character with Cabernet Sauvignon's structure and dark fruit depth. The wine opens with vibrant red fruit and blackcurrant, supported by cedar and subtle oak from careful aging. Secondary spice notes add complexity, while tertiary tobacco and leather emerge on the palate, reflecting the wine's potential for further development. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food companion—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Roccolo Grassi  Valpolicella Superiore
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Ripasso with dark cherry, plum, and subtle oak—a food-friendly Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Roccolo Grassi's Valpolicella Superiore Ripasso is a structured red from Veneto's celebrated appellation, built on a traditional blend of Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, and regional varieties. The 2017 vintage displays a dominant primary of dark cherry and black fruit compote, supported by measured oak and spice from winemaking. Earthy undertones emerge on the finish, lending complexity and age-worthiness. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine a natural partner to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based preparations—a benchmark expression of Ripasso's food-friendly character.

name: Tenuta Belguardo  Maremma Toscana DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Cabernet-Franc blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Belguardo's Maremma Toscana blend marries 85% Cabernet Sauvignon with 15% Cabernet Franc in a classically structured red wine. The 2018 vintage delivers ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, supported by cedar and herbaceous notes that speak to the Tuscan terroir. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone without heaviness, while secondary leather and tobacco notes add complexity and age-worthiness. A food-friendly expression of Bordeaux varieties in Tuscany's maritime climate.

name: Domaine Robert Groffier Pere & Fils  Chambertin Clos de Beze Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): 100-year-old vines, 100% whole-cluster, 100% new oak — Groffier's Clos de Bèze is the domaine's most technically extreme cuvée.
description (EN Store): Nicolas Groffier, who took the reins in 2005, made a deliberate choice to vinify Chambertin Clos de Bèze with 100% whole-cluster inclusion — the only cuvée in the cellar to receive this treatment in full — anchoring stem tannin and reductive lift as structural counterweights to the parcel's natural density. The ~0.42–0.5 ha parcel sits within Gevrey-Chambertin's Grand Cru belt on shallow calcaire brun (brown limestone) over bedrock, forcing century-old roots deep into fractured rock and producing must of extraordinary mineral concentration with minimal intervention required. After cold-soak at 10–12°C for 4–5 days and indigenous-yeast fermentation with frequent pigeage over 17–20 days total maceration, the wine is transferred to 100% new French oak — the sole cuvée in the domaine to receive this regime — before a single racking at ~12 months and return to stainless-steel vat until bottling. The wine is released unfined and unfiltered. What no peer does: Groffier ages grand cru Burgundy for only ~12 months in barrel, an unusually compressed élevage that preserves primary fruit and terroir signal over oak patina. The 2022 shows deep color, iron-edged cherry, and rose petal aromatics with supple structure; given the 100-year-old vine concentration and whole-cluster architecture, a minimum of ten years in bottle is warranted before the wine begins to reveal its full register.

name: Domaine Gros Frère et Soeur  Echezeaux Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Échezeaux Grand Cru from ~0.9 ha of Bajocian limestone-clay; 100% new Bertranges oak, unfiltered — Burgundy's most unapologetically opulent school.
description (EN Store):
When Gustave and Colette Gros divided Domaine Louis Gros in 1963, they inherited a philosophy as much as a parcel: concentration over restraint, new oak over neutrality. That conviction is nowhere more legible than in the Échezeaux, drawn from roughly 0.9 ha of mid-slope Côte de Nuits terroir where Bajocian and Bathonian limestone underpins iron-rich clay — a mosaic that delivers both structural grip and the ferrous depth that distinguishes Vosne-adjacent grands crus from their more northerly neighbours.

In the cellar, 100% destemmed Pinot Noir ferments in temperature-controlled concrete tanks with remontage before alcoholic fermentation and twice-daily pigeage once underway. Maturation proceeds in 228-litre Bertranges Forest pièces — 100% new oak, the wood air-dried for two years before coopering. Wines rest on lees without racking through the first year, then are bottled unfiltered as standard practice.

The special feature here is the deliberate transgression: in a region where 30–50% new oak is already considered assertive for grand cru Pinot Noir, Gros Frère et Soeur applies a full 100% — a house signature that produces a richer, darker-fruited Échezeaux than virtually any peer in the appellation. The 2022 vintage, not documented in the domaine's own vintage notes, follows a warm, even growing season across the Côte de Nuits; expect generous fruit density with the structural limestone backbone to reward 5–12 years of cellaring.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection  Vint Rye Barrel-Aged Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Rye barrel-aged Napa red blend with dark fruit, vanilla oak, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Private Selection Rye Barrel-Aged Red Blend from Oakville showcases the winery's commitment to innovative oak treatment. The rye barrel aging imparts distinctive vanilla, toasted oak, and subtle spice notes that layer over a core of dark plum and red fruit. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured enough for grilled meats, braised beef, and hearty comfort dishes. A versatile, well-crafted expression suited to everyday entertaining and casual dining.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Tectonia Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Carmenere from Chile's Rapel Valley: dark fruit, spice, and herbaceous depth in a food-friendly medium-full frame.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Tectonia Carmenere is a 100% varietal expression from the Rapel Valley, showcasing the signature character of Chile's flagship red grape. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary aromatics, supported by herbaceous notes that reflect the region's terroir. On the palate, black pepper and cocoa spice emerge alongside structured tannins, with subtle earthy undertones adding complexity. A versatile, well-balanced red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb preparations.

name: Rochester  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied South African Shiraz with dark fruit, spice, and smoky complexity.
description (EN Store): Rochester Shiraz from Western Cape delivers a full-bodied expression of South Africa's signature varietal. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, complemented by black pepper and licorice spice. Secondary smoke and leather notes add textural depth, while medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and smoky preparations.

name: Rhanleigh  Pinotage
short_description (EN Store): South African Pinotage with dark fruit, smoke, and spice—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and hearty dishes.
description (EN Store):
Rhanleigh's Pinotage from Western Cape delivers the signature character of South Africa's native varietal: ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, with supporting notes of smoke and subtle oak. The palate shows medium-full body with balanced tannins and a dry finish, making it versatile at the table. Earthy undertones and hints of leather suggest modest aging potential, while the spice profile adds complexity without overwhelming the fruit.

A food-friendly red suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes; equally at home with cured meats and mushroom-forward preparations.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Carmenere from Puente Alto: dark fruit, spice, and structured tannin. Food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Carmenere is a single-varietal expression from Puente Alto in Maipo Valley, Chile's premier red wine region. The wine showcases the signature character of Carmenere: ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with herbaceous undertones and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with secondary cocoa and black pepper notes adding complexity. Tertiary leather and earth notes emerge with bottle age, suggesting a wine built for both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and rich preparations.

name: Mascota Vineyards  Grand Mascota Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Argentine Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins. Elegant complexity for premium red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' Grand Cabernet Sauvignon from Mendoza showcases the depth and structure characteristic of Argentina's premier Cabernet terroir. Dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit form the aromatic foundation, complemented by pronounced cedar and oak-derived spice from careful winemaking. The wine's full body and high tannin structure provide a framework for extended cellaring, while tertiary notes of leather and mineral earth add complexity and sophistication. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meats, lamb preparations, and aged cheeses that echo its own maturity and refinement.

name: Mascota Vineyards  Grand Mascota Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec blend aged 15 months in French oak; blackberry, plum, cocoa, and mineral earth. Smooth, structured, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Grand Mascota Malbec is a premium Argentine red from Mendoza, blending 85% Malbec with 15% Cabernet Sauvignon sourced from high-altitude vineyards. Aged for 15 months in new French oak, the wine develops a complex aromatic profile dominated by blackberry and plum fruit, layered with cocoa, vanilla, and subtle spice notes. The palate is medium-full bodied with well-integrated tannins and mineral undertones, delivering a smooth, round mouthfeel that avoids heaviness despite its structure. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Folonari  Amarone della Valpolicella "Folonari" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Amarone with dark cherry, dried plum, and subtle oak—a structured, food-friendly Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Folonari's Amarone della Valpolicella DOCG is a full-bodied red wine crafted from the traditional Valpolicella blend: Corvina (65%), Rondinella (25%), and Molinara (10%). The 2017 vintage displays a complex profile of dark cherry, dried plum, and black fruit compote on the nose, with measured oak integration and spice notes on the palate. Medium acidity and medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, while subtle leather and tobacco undertones emerge on the finish. A versatile food wine suited to braised meats, game, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2018
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Elegant Left Bank expression.
description (EN Store): Château Clerc Milon 2018 is a Bordeaux Pauillac blend anchored by 60% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Merlot, Cabernet Franc, Petit Verdot, and Carmenère. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with secondary cedar and black pepper from oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge, reflecting the structure and aging potential characteristic of Left Bank Pauillac. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame a food-friendly profile suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Donnafugata  Sherazade Sicilia DOC Nero d'Avola
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Sherazade is a single-varietal expression of Nero d'Avola from Sicily's premier growing region. The wine opens with dark plum and blackberry fruit, supported by black cherry and subtle spice notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure provide a framework for earthy undertones and emerging leather character, with hints of tobacco complexity. Versatile at table and approachable in youth, this wine bridges everyday enjoyment with serious food pairing potential.

name: Domaine Du Pegau  Cotes du Rhone Cuvee Setier Villages Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Rhône blend from Châteauneuf-du-Pape with dark plum, spice, and mineral depth. Food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): A classic southern Rhône expression from Domaine Du Pegau, this Côtes du Rhône Villages Rouge showcases the signature blend of Grenache (60%), Syrah (20%), and Mourvèdre (20%) that defines the region's character. Dark plum and ripe red fruit dominate the primary impression, supported by black pepper spice and garrigue-tinged herbaceous notes from the winemaking process. With medium-full body and balanced tannin, the wine reveals earthy minerality and subtle leather complexity on the finish, suggesting modest age-worthiness. A versatile food wine suited to grilled lamb, braised beef, and Mediterranean-inflected cuisine.

name: Bosquet Des Papes  Chateauneuf-du-Pape Chante Le Merle
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Châteauneuf-du-Pape with ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and mineral depth—a classic southern Rhône expression.
description (EN Store): Bosquet Des Papes' Chante Le Merle is a traditional Châteauneuf-du-Pape built on 85% Grenache, with supporting Mourvèdre and Syrah. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full frame anchored by ripe red and dark cherry fruit, layered with garrigue, black pepper, and earthy minerality. Secondary spice and herbal notes reflect the southern Rhône terroir; tertiary leather and tobacco complexity suggest age-worthiness. Food-friendly and approachable, yet structured enough for cellaring.

name: Casisano  Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Classic Brunello di Montalcino: elegant Sangiovese with ripe red fruit, earthy complexity, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Casisano's Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a traditional expression of Tuscany's most prestigious red wine appellation. Crafted from 100% Sangiovese, this medium-full bodied wine showcases the varietal's signature balance of ripe red cherry and plum fruit with layered earthy, leather, and tobacco notes. The wine's medium-high tannin structure and acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility, making it equally suited to immediate enjoyment or cellaring. A benchmark Brunello that reflects the terroir and winemaking heritage of Montalcino.

name: Casisano  Colombaiolo Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino: pure Sangiovese with structured tannin, dark fruit, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Casisano's Colombaiolo Brunello di Montalcino DOCG is a classically structured Tuscan red built entirely from Sangiovese. The 2017 vintage expresses the varietal's signature red-to-dark fruit profile—cherry and plum—layered with dried herbs, minerality, and subtle leather. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide backbone without heaviness, while acidity lifts the palate and encourages food pairing. Earthy undertones and emerging tobacco notes suggest the wine's capacity to evolve gracefully over the next decade.

name: Messenez Family  Chateau M Gran Reserva Cabernet Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Rapel Valley Cabernet-Merlot blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Messenez Family's Gran Reserva Cabernet Merlot from Chile's Rapel Valley is a classically proportioned Bordeaux-style blend (60% Cabernet Sauvignon, 40% Merlot) built for the dinner table. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle cedar and spice notes from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable in youth while offering enough structure to pair with grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-roasted poultry. A reliable, well-made expression of New World Cabernet-Merlot craftsmanship.

name: Messenez Family  Chateau M Gran Vin Gold Label
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet-Merlot-Syrah blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): Messenez Family's Chateau M Gran Vin Gold Label is a structured red blend from Chile's Rapel Valley, built on Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Syrah. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Secondary layers of black pepper and spice lead to tertiary tobacco and leather notes, suggesting modest aging potential. Full body and medium-high tannins make this wine a natural partner for grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Oddero  Barolo Bussia Vigna Mondaca Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Nebbiolo Riserva from Barolo's Bussia cru—dark fruit, tar, and rose with structured tannins.
description (EN Store):
Oddero Barolo Bussia Vigna Mondaca Riserva DOCG 2017 is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious vineyard sites. The 2017 vintage delivers a full-bodied expression marked by dark plum and black cherry fruit, layered with tar, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Fine-grained tannins and bracing acidity provide the backbone for extended cellaring and food pairing versatility.

Bussia is celebrated for its mineral-rich soils and exposure, yielding wines of power and elegance. This Riserva designation reflects extended aging in barrel and bottle before release, deepening the wine's leather and earthy tertiary character. A benchmark Barolo for collectors and serious red-wine enthusiasts.

name: Oddero  Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's heartland: structured tannins, rose florals, and tarry complexity.
description (EN Store): Oddero's Barolo DOCG is a classic expression of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious region. The wine displays the hallmark characteristics of the appellation: a medium-full body supported by medium-high acidity and firm tannins that promise cellaring potential. On the nose, red cherry and rose florals dominate, with secondary notes of tar and licorice adding depth. The palate reveals leather and tobacco undertones that evolve with air, grounding the wine's elegant structure. This is a food-friendly Barolo suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Pinical Estate  Cabernet Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Cabernet-Merlot blend from South Eastern Australia with ripe dark fruit, subtle oak, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Pinical Estate's Cabernet Merlot is a classic Bordeaux-style red blend from South Eastern Australia, crafted to deliver immediate appeal and versatility at the table. The wine opens with ripe plum and blackcurrant fruit, supported by gentle cedar and oak notes that add complexity without overwhelming the palate. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this an ideal companion for grilled meats, braised beef, and tomato-based dishes, while its approachability extends to roasted poultry and casual comfort-food pairings.

name: Sartori di Verona  “Corte Bra” Amarone Delle Valpolicella DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Amarone with dark fruit, oak spice, and earthy complexity. A structured, age-worthy Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Sartori di Verona's Corte Bra Amarone della Valpolicella is a full-bodied red wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from a traditional blend of Corvina Veronese, Corvinone, Rondinella, and Oseleta grapes. The 2016 vintage displays the hallmark richness of Amarone: concentrated dark cherry and blackcurrant fruit on the nose, layered with cocoa, spice, and oak from careful winemaking. The palate is structured and enveloping, with medium-high tannins and a mineral, earthy backbone that evolves toward tobacco and leather notes—markers of the wine's depth and aging potential. A food-friendly yet serious expression of the appellation, suited to red meat, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Poggio Al Tufo  Rompicollo Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe red fruit, cedar, and herbaceous complexity.
description (EN Store): Poggio Al Tufo's Rompicollo is a classically structured Super Tuscan that marries Sangiovese's bright cherry and earthy character with Cabernet Sauvignon's darker fruit and structural backbone. The blend delivers medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, opening with dominant red fruit and blackcurrant on the nose, followed by cedar and herbaceous notes from careful oak integration. Subtle leather and mineral undertones emerge on the palate, suggesting modest age-worthiness. A food-friendly Tuscan red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Victoria Park  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Margaret River Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Victoria Park's Cabernet Sauvignon from Margaret River delivers the region's signature depth and structure. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. High tannins and medium acidity provide a firm backbone, with tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerging on the finish. A classic Australian Cabernet suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  Col Silente Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montepulciano with red fruit, earthy minerality, and refined tannin structure.
description (EN Store): Castelli del Grevepesa's Col Silente Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG is a classically styled Sangiovese from Tuscany's prestigious Montepulciano region. The 2019 vintage expresses the varietal's signature red cherry and plum fruit with layered earthy minerality and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this wine versatile at the table. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather suggest modest aging potential and complexity. A food-friendly expression of Tuscan winemaking tradition.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  Castello Di Bibbione Riserva Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico Riserva with ripe cherry, leather, and mineral complexity—a food-friendly Tuscan classic.
description (EN Store):
Castello di Bibbione Riserva represents the traditional heart of Chianti Classico: a Sangiovese-led blend (95% Sangiovese, 5% Merlot) from the Castelli del Grevepesa estate in Tuscany's most prestigious subregion. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied red with bright acidity and structured tannins characteristic of quality Riserva-level production.


On the palate, ripe red cherry and plum fruit form the primary impression, supported by dried herb and subtle oak from careful aging. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge with air, while earthy minerality and spice provide tertiary complexity. The wine's balance and food-friendly structure make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  Castello Di Bibbione Gran Selezione Chianti classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico Gran Selezione with ripe cherry, plum, and earthy complexity; food-friendly Tuscan red.
description (EN Store): Castello di Bibbione Gran Selezione represents the pinnacle of Chianti Classico tradition, crafted from 95% Sangiovese with 5% Merlot. The 2018 vintage displays a harmonious balance of ripe red fruit—cherry and plum—layered with dried herb and mineral undertones. Oak aging imparts subtle vanilla and spice, while tertiary development reveals tobacco, leather, and earthy complexity. Medium-full body and medium tannin structure make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, pairing beautifully with tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Villa di Corlo  Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro Corleto DOC
short_description (EN Store): Lively Lambrusco Grasparossa with red fruit, brioche, and fine bubbles—a versatile Italian sparkling for Asian cuisine and aperitifs.
description (EN Store): Villa di Corlo's Lambrusco Grasparossa di Castelvetro Corleto DOC is a vibrant, lightly sparkling red wine from Emilia-Romagna, Italy. Bright red fruit—raspberry and strawberry—dominates the nose, complemented by delicate floral and citrus notes. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with fine bubbles, subtle brioche complexity, and a clean, dry finish. High acidity and low tannin make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, ideal for Asian cuisine, charcuterie, and casual entertaining.

name: Moulin de Gassac  Syrah IGP
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Languedoc Syrah with blackberry, pepper, and smoky spice—a classic southern French red for grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Moulin de Gassac's Syrah from Languedoc-Roussillon delivers the hallmark power and structure of the region's finest reds. Full-bodied with medium-high tannins, this wine opens with ripe blackberry and dark plum, followed by black pepper and herbaceous spice. Subtle smoke and earthy undertones add complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and slow-cooked braises.

name: Chateau Duhart Milon Rothschild 2018
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac icon: structured Cabernet-Merlot blend with blackcurrant depth, cedar complexity, and age-worthy tannin architecture.
description (EN Store): Château Duhart Milon Rothschild 2018 is a quintessential Left Bank Bordeaux from Pauillac, built on a foundation of Cabernet Sauvignon with Merlot support. This vintage expresses the appellation's signature blackcurrant and cassis character, layered with cedar, graphite, and tobacco complexity. Full-bodied with structured, high tannins and medium-high acidity, the wine demonstrates the architectural precision expected of a fourth-growth estate. The secondary oak influence adds subtle leather and spice; tertiary development promises mineral and tobacco evolution over 15–25 years of cellaring.

name: Anakena  Birdman Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—classic structure for red meat.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Birdman Cabernet Sauvignon from Cachapoal Valley delivers the hallmark profile of a well-made New World Cabernet: dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide structure and aging potential, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes add complexity. A full-bodied wine suited to grilled steak, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: San Filippo  Brunello di Montalcino Lo Scorno
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino with dark plum, cedar, and tobacco complexity; structured for cellaring and food.
description (EN Store): San Filippo's Lo Scorno is a classically styled Brunello di Montalcino from the 2019 vintage, built on the foundation of Sangiovese Grosso (Brunello). The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, supported by secondary cedar and oak spice from careful aging in traditional Tuscan oak. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the palate, grounding the wine in earthy minerality and structural complexity. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame a wine suited to extended cellaring and elegant pairing with grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac-based Bordeaux blend: ripe plum, cassis, cedar. Food-friendly, structured, classic Left Bank style.
description (EN Store): A classic Pauillac expression from the prestigious Baron Philippe de Rothschild house, this Bordeaux blend marries 58% Merlot with 32% Cabernet Sauvignon and 10% Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with ripe dark plum and blackcurrant, supported by subtle cedar and graphite minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it approachable in youth while retaining aging potential. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef dishes, and herb-forward lamb preparations.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Saint-Estephe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe blend: blackcurrant, plum, and cedar with structured tannins. Food-friendly Bordeaux.
description (EN Store): A traditional Bordeaux blend from Saint-Estèphe, anchored by 55% Cabernet Sauvignon with 40% Merlot and 5% Petit Verdot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and plum fruit, supported by cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses. Subtle tobacco and earth notes emerge on the finish, adding classic Bordeaux complexity.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Margaux-sourced Bordeaux blend of Cabernet, Merlot & Petit Verdot; medium-full body, blackcurrant primary, cedar & tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux red from the Margaux appellation, this blend of 65% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, and 5% Petit Verdot expresses the region's signature dark-fruit character with elegant secondary complexity.
