Blackcurrant and plum dominate the nose and palate, supported by cedar and black pepper notes from careful oak aging. Tertiary earth and tobacco notes emerge with air, reflecting the wine's structure and potential for food pairing. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile choice for grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Chateau Montrose  2017
short_description (EN Store): 1855 Second Growth from a 95-ha undivided Saint-Estèphe croupe; Pyrenean deep gravel over clay-marl, 60% new French oak, 18 months — built for decades.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1815 by Étienne Théodore Dumoulin, Montrose achieved Second Growth status within just 40 years — a speed unmatched across the entire 1855 classification — and the Charmolüe family made it among the first Bordeaux châteaux to bottle at the estate, documented from 1904. That early commitment to provenance integrity persists today under the Bouygues brothers, who invested a reported €20 million between 2007 and 2013 in a gravity-flow winery and a 1,000 m² barrel hall without softening the estate's structural DNA.

The vineyard is a rare 95-hectare single block on a gravelly croupe in the south-eastern corner of Saint-Estèphe, roughly 800 metres from the Gironde estuary. Soils are deep coarse gravel — sourced geologically from the Pyrenees and Massif Central, exceeding two metres in depth — over a clay-marl subsoil, with a south-easterly aspect. The estuary's thermal mass suppresses both spring frost and summer heat spikes, preserving phenolic precision in the Cabernet Sauvignon, some vines dating to 1932.

Parcel-by-parcel fermentation feeds into 18 months aging in French oak barriques at 60% new wood, a regime that frames rather than overwhelms the wine's mineral-graphite core. The Grand Vin represents only 35–40% of total estate production, ensuring rigorous selection. The 2017 vintage blend runs approximately 60–65% Cabernet Sauvignon with Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot completing the assemblage.

Where peers have migrated toward earlier-drinking profiles, Montrose deliberately retains tannic density and a graphite-mineral backbone that demands extended cellaring — the defining transgression of the modern Médoc era. No vintage notes are confirmed for 2017 in the brand library; the wine's structural architecture places it firmly in the estate's long-aging tradition.

name: Chateau Grand-Puy-Lacoste  2016
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: ripe dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannin. Elegant Left Bank expression.
description (EN Store): Château Grand-Puy-Lacoste 2016 is a Left Bank Bordeaux from Pauillac, built on a foundation of 79% Cabernet Sauvignon and 21% Merlot. The wine displays the region's hallmark dark-fruit intensity—blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate—balanced by secondary cedar, tobacco, and black pepper notes that reflect careful oak aging. Tertiary earth and leather emerge with time, adding complexity and food-friendliness. Medium-high tannins and medium acidity frame a medium-full body suited to extended cellaring or near-term enjoyment with substantial fare.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Beaujolais Villages
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Beaujolais Villages with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and food-friendly charm—a classic French everyday red.
description (EN Store): Georges Duboeuf's Beaujolais Villages exemplifies the region's signature style: a medium-bodied red wine built on 100% Gamay with vibrant red cherry and raspberry fruit at its core. The wine displays the characteristic floral lift and subtle spice that define quality Beaujolais, supported by silky, approachable tannins and bright acidity that make it remarkably food-friendly. Ideal for casual dining and everyday enjoyment, this wine bridges the gap between serious structure and approachable pleasure.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Pinot Noir (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir from Beaujolais: bright red fruit, silky tannins, food-friendly versatility.
description (EN Store): A classic expression of Pinot Noir from the Beaujolais region, this wine showcases the varietal's hallmark elegance and approachability. Bright red cherry and strawberry dominate the palate, supported by subtle mineral and earthy undertones that add complexity without heaviness. Medium body and silky tannins make this an ideal companion for a wide range of cuisines—from roasted poultry and mushroom risotto to light pasta and charcuterie boards. The wine's balanced acidity ensures freshness and food-friendliness across the table.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Merlot (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Merlot from Beaujolais: ripe plum, black cherry, soft tannins. Food-friendly everyday red.
description (EN Store): A straightforward, fruit-forward Merlot from the Beaujolais region, crafted by the established Georges Duboeuf house. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by soft, integrated tannins and a clean, approachable finish. Medium-full body and moderate acidity make this wine a reliable companion to grilled meats, braised beef, roasted poultry, and tomato-based dishes. Ideal for casual dining and everyday enjoyment.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Cabernet Sauvignon (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Cabernet Sauvignon from Beaujolais with ripe dark fruit, cedar, and balanced tannin.
description (EN Store): A classic Cabernet Sauvignon from the Beaujolais region, this wine delivers approachable dark-fruit character with layers of blackcurrant, black cherry, and subtle herbaceous notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, braised beef, and Mediterranean fare. Subtle oak integration and black pepper spice add complexity without overshadowing the wine's food-friendly nature.

name: Argiano  Brunello di Montalcino
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with ripe cherry, tobacco, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Argiano's Brunello di Montalcino is a 100% Sangiovese expression from one of Tuscany's most prestigious appellations. The 2020 vintage displays the classic profile of the region: dominant cherry and plum fruit on the nose, supported by secondary notes of tobacco, cedar, and warm spice from careful oak aging. The palate reveals medium-full body with firm, well-integrated tannins and bright acidity that carries the wine through a long finish marked by earthy minerality and subtle leather notes. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged Tuscan cheeses.

name: Tasca D'Almerita  Tascante Contrada Sciaranuova VV
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Etna Nerello Mascalese with volcanic minerality, red cherry, and silky tannins—a food-friendly Sicilian classic.
description (EN Store): Tasca d'Almerita's Tascante Contrada Sciaranuova is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from the volcanic slopes of Mount Etna. The 2017 vintage showcases the variety's signature elegance: bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, layered with black pepper, dried herbs, and the distinctive mineral signature of Etna's volcanic terroir. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while retaining complexity for short-term cellaring. A versatile food wine that bridges Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Carmenere (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Carmenere from Puente Alto: dark fruit, spice, and mineral complexity in a food-friendly medium-full frame.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Carmenere represents the signature character of Chile's flagship red varietal from the prestigious Puente Alto subregion of Maipo Valley. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by herbaceous undertones that reflect the vineyard's terroir. Secondary notes of spice and cocoa emerge from careful winemaking, while tertiary leather and mineral complexity develop with bottle age. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine a versatile companion to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb dishes, as well as earthy preparations like mushroom risotto and aged charcuterie.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Merlot (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Carmenere from Puente Alto: dark fruit, spice, and herbaceous depth. Food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Merlot—a Carmenere-based expression from Chile's Puente Alto subregion—delivers the signature dark fruit intensity and herbal character that define the varietal. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet complex, with primary notes of dark plum and black cherry complemented by subtle spice and cocoa from careful winemaking. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerge with air, suggesting modest aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and rich preparations.

name: Masi  Campofiorin Rosso Veronese
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella blend with ripe cherry, dark fruit, and subtle oak—excellent value for everyday red meat and pasta.
description (EN Store): Masi's Campofiorin Rosso Veronese is a Valpolicella-based blend of Corvina, Rondinella, and Molinara grapes, delivering a medium-bodied red with approachable fruit-forward character. The nose opens with red cherry and dark fruit compote, supported by herbaceous undertones and a whisper of almond. On the palate, balanced tannins and moderate acidity frame the wine's core of ripe dark fruit, with subtle oak integration and a hint of spice on the finish. A versatile, food-friendly expression that punches above its price point.

name: Finca Museum  Real DO Cigales Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Cigales Reserva from Museum Wine: structured red with dark fruit, leather, and spice—a food-friendly Spanish classic.
description (EN Store): Finca Museum Real DO Cigales Reserva is a medium-full-bodied red wine from Spain's Cigales region, crafted in the traditional Tempranillo-based style. The wine displays ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, complemented by leather, tobacco, and subtle oak spice. Medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses. The Reserva designation indicates extended aging, contributing dried-fruit complexity and earthy undertones that evolve on the palate.

name: El Coto Rioja  Crianza
short_description (EN Store): Classic Rioja Crianza: bold plum and cherry with supple oak, leather, and dried herbs. Excellent value.
description (EN Store): El Coto's Rioja Crianza is a well-balanced, medium-full-bodied red built on ripe plum and red cherry fruit. Oak aging contributes vanilla and subtle cedar notes, while tertiary leather and earthy undertones add complexity. Medium tannins and medium-high acidity provide structure without heaviness, making this wine approachable yet food-friendly. A classic expression of Tempranillo's versatility at an accessible price point.

name: Benjamin Le-roux  Gevrey Chambertin
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Gevrey-Chambertin Pinot Noir with silky tannins, red fruit, and Burgundian minerality.
description (EN Store): Benjamin Le-roux's 2022 Gevrey-Chambertin is a classically structured Burgundy red built on 100% Pinot Noir from one of France's most prestigious terroirs. The wine displays the hallmark elegance of the appellation: bright red cherry and blackberry fruit on the nose, layered with earthy minerality and subtle oak influence. On the palate, medium-full body and refined tannin structure provide food-friendly balance, while dried herb and spice notes add complexity. A versatile expression suited to both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: The Long Little Dog  Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Grenache-Syrah blend with ripe red fruit, peppery spice, and earthy minerality—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): The Long Little Dog Rouge is a Languedoc-Roussillon red blend anchored by Grenache and Syrah, two hallmark varieties of southern Rhône tradition. The wine presents a medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, offering ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with black pepper spice and herbaceous undertones. Earthy minerality and subtle herb-driven complexity round the palate, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, Mediterranean fare, and rustic cuisine.

name: 19 Crimes  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Pinot Noir with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and food-friendly structure—a versatile red for fine dining and casual tables.
description (EN Store): 19 Crimes Pinot Noir from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by dominant black cherry and blackberry fruit. Secondary oak and vanilla notes, layered with black pepper and subtle smoke, create a polished, approachable profile suited to both restaurant and home settings. The wine's balanced tannin and medium acidity make it a natural companion to grilled meats, roasted poultry, and rich comfort dishes.

name: 19 Crimes  Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with ripe dark fruit, violet florals, and spiced cocoa—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): A classic Mendoza Malbec showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit character. Ripe plum and blackberry dominate the nose, supported by violet florals and a hint of spice. On the palate, cocoa and vanilla from careful oak aging add depth, while medium tannins and balanced acidity provide structure without heaviness. Subtle leather and earthy notes emerge on the finish, suggesting modest aging potential. A versatile, approachable red suited to grilled beef, lamb, and hearty braised dishes.

name: Penfolds  802.A Superblend Cabernet Sauvignon Shiraz 2018
short_description (EN Store): 802.A Superblend: Kalimna Barossa Cabernet-Shiraz aged in Grange-seasoned hogsheads — eight perfect critic scores in 2018.
description (EN Store):
The designation 802.A places this Superblend within Penfolds' internal coding architecture — a deliberate signal that this is not a Bin wine but a tier conceived to sit between the commercial range and Grange itself, drawing on Kalimna's ancient, phylloxera-free alluvial and sandy loam soils in the Barossa Valley, where Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz vines have been cultivated since the 1880s. Those deep, low-vigour soils force root systems downward, concentrating flavour and producing the mid-palate density that defines Barossa's most serious reds.

The Cabernet Sauvignon / Shiraz blend follows Penfolds' multi-vineyard blending doctrine — winemaker expertise over single-terroir purity — with maturation in barrels previously used for Grange (American oak hogsheads), a seasoning regime that imparts structural tannin and subtle vanilla integration without overwhelming the fruit with new-oak character. This is the same vessel lineage that underpins Bin 389, but the Superblend tier selects at a higher threshold.

What separates 802.A categorically is its position as a named Superblend expression — a classification Penfolds reserves for blends of exceptional vintage material that do not conform to the fixed Bin identity, making it one of the rarest recurring releases in the portfolio. The 2018 vintage earned eight perfect scores from leading critics including Andrew Caillard MW, Lisa Perrotti-Brown, Ken Gargett, and Nick Stock, alongside 98 pts from Wine Spectator; the drinking window is projected at 2030–2060+.

name: Hardys  The Chronicles Reserve Cebernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): McLaren Vale Cabernet with dark fruit depth, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure—a versatile red for grilled meats and hearty fare.
description (EN Store): Hardys The Chronicles Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from McLaren Vale delivers a classically structured red wine built on dark plum and blackcurrant fruit with subtle cedar and black pepper complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a reliable choice for grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheese pairings. The wine shows restraint in oak treatment, allowing varietal character to lead while maintaining food-friendly approachability across casual and formal dining contexts.

name: Lindeman's  Gentleman Collection Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—classic Australian power.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Gentleman Collection Cabernet Sauvignon from Hunter Valley delivers the hallmark richness of Australian Cabernet: dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. Full-bodied with high tannin structure, this wine shows secondary oak influence and emerging tertiary tobacco and leather complexity. Ideal for grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: J. Lebegue  Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Classic Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring and elegant red meat.
description (EN Store): J. Lebegue Bordeaux Rouge is a traditional Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend rooted in the Left Bank style. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedar and tobacco notes that suggest careful oak aging. A full body and high tannin structure promise evolution in bottle, while leather and spice undertones add complexity. This is a wine built for contemplation alongside grilled steaks, braised beef, or aged cheeses.

name: J. Lebegue  Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Classic Médoc Bordeaux blend: blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with age-worthy structure and elegant tannins.
description (EN Store): A traditional Médoc expression built on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot, this wine delivers the hallmark profile of the Left Bank: ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit anchored by cedary oak and subtle tobacco notes. Full-bodied with high, refined tannins and medium-high acidity, it shows the structure and complexity expected of a serious Bordeaux. Leather and graphite minerality emerge on the palate, suggesting age-worthiness and food-friendly versatility.

name: J. Lebegue  Lussac St-Emilion
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend from Lussac St-Émilion: blackcurrant and cedar with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux red from the Lussac St-Émilion appellation, this blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot delivers the hallmark elegance of the Right Bank. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedar and black pepper notes that speak to careful oak aging. The medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure suitable for cellaring, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest complexity to come. An age-worthy expression that rewards patient collectors and pairs naturally with grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: La Marchesana  Primitivo IGT
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, fruit-forward Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and pepper—an easy-drinking red for pizza and grilled fare.
description (EN Store): La Marchesana Primitivo IGT is a medium-full-bodied red from Puglia that delivers ripe dark fruit and black cherry on the nose, followed by a palate of raspberry and plum with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper. The tannins are fully rounded and approachable, making this wine eminently drinkable without demanding cellaring. A versatile food wine that pairs naturally with tomato-based pasta, pizza, grilled meats, and casual BBQ fare.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pessac-Léognan Bordeaux blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Dourthe's Grands Terroirs Bordeaux Rouge from Pessac-Léognan showcases the classic marriage of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot in one of Bordeaux's most prestigious left-bank appellations. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedary oak and subtle herbaceous notes. A medium-full body and medium-high tannin structure provide backbone and aging potential, while tertiary tobacco and graphite notes emerge on the finish, suggesting complexity that will develop further in the cellar. This is a food-friendly yet serious Bordeaux suited to both near-term enjoyment and medium-term cellaring.

name: Dourthe  Grands Terroirs Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring and classic red meat.
description (EN Store): Dourthe's Grands Terroirs Margaux is a classically proportioned Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and graphite minerality. Secondary oak and tobacco notes add complexity and structure, while the high tannin backbone and medium-high acidity promise graceful evolution. A wine built for the table and the cellar alike.

name: Montes  Twins Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Chilean blend of Cabernet, Syrah, Tempranillo & Carmenère with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Montes Twins Red Blend is a full-bodied Chilean wine from Apalta in Colchagua Valley, blending Cabernet Sauvignon, Syrah, Tempranillo, and Carmenère. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Medium-high tannins and tobacco-leather complexity develop on the palate, offering structure and aging potential. This is a serious, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Louis Latour  Mercurey Cote Chalonnaise Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Côte Chalonnaise Pinot Noir from Louis Latour's négociant range — fermented in open wooden vats, flash-pasteurised before bottling, no fining required.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1797 by Jean Latour — a third-generation winegrower whose family had tended Burgundian vines since 1731 — Maison Louis Latour built its négociant reach outward from Aloxe-Corton to span every major Burgundy appellation, including the limestone-and-clay rolling hills of the Côte Chalonnaise. Mercurey, the appellation's most serious red cru, sits on Jurassic limestone soils interlaced with clay and marl, producing Pinot Noir with more structural grip and darker fruit character than the lighter sandy terroirs to its south.

In the cellar, the house protocol applies: 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed Pinot Noir fermented in large open wooden vats with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days, then aged in French oak at 15–35% new, medium toast, for 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from air-dried French oak. The wine then undergoes the house's openly acknowledged and category-transgressive step: flash pasteurisation at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating the need for fining entirely as the wine falls clear naturally over 2–3 months.

That pasteurisation practice — unique among major Burgundy négociants and a persistent point of critical debate — is what separates every Latour red from its peers at this price point. The 2020 vintage in Burgundy delivered concentrated, ripe fruit with good natural structure, translating in the Côte Chalonnaise to dark cherry, iron-tinged earth, and firm but resolved tannins; drink now through 2027.

name: Louis Latour  Corton Grand Cru "Domaine Latour" 2017
short_description (EN Store): 17+ ha on Corton's red chalk-clay-marl face; 100% new in-house oak, flash-pasteurised — Burgundy's most openly transgressive Grand Cru red.
description (EN Store):
When Jean Latour established the Corton Grancey Cuverie in 1832–1834 — the first purpose-built winery in France — he carved five levels into the Corton hillside so that gravity alone would move every litre of wine, a structural commitment that still governs production today. That same infrastructure now vinifies Pinot Noir from over 17 hectares of Corton Grand Cru, the largest single-domaine red Grand Cru holding in the Côte d'Or.

The fruit comes from the south-east face of the Hill of Corton, where red chalk, clay, and marl soils sit at mid-slope elevation — heavier and more iron-rich than the limestone summit reserved for Corton-Charlemagne — producing Pinot Noir with pronounced structure and dark-fruited density. Vines average 35–40 years, concentrating extract without sacrificing site transparency.

After 100% hand-harvest and full destemming, grapes ferment in 45 large open wooden vats over approximately 14 days with manual pigeage; gravity transport is used throughout. Aging proceeds in 100% new French oak pièces (228 L), medium toast, 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from oak air-dried 24–36 months. The defining transgression: all red wines are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining entirely while remaining the most openly debated technique in serious Burgundy. The 2017 vintage delivered ripe, structured reds with good concentration and natural freshness; no critic scores are confirmed for this specific red cuvée in the brand library.

name: Cable Bay  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, earthy complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Cable Bay's 2018 Pinot Noir from Marlborough showcases the region's signature elegance and restraint. Bright red cherry and strawberry dominate the nose, supported by subtle plum and earthy mineral notes. The palate is silky and well-balanced, with medium tannins that frame rather than dominate, and a refreshing acidity that carries the wine through the finish. This is a versatile, food-friendly expression of New Zealand Pinot Noir—approachable now, with enough structure to age gracefully.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Dark Red
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean red blend with dark fruit, cocoa, and soft tannins—ideal for everyday meals and grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Casillero del Diablo Dark Red is a well-crafted Chilean red blend from the Maipo Valley, showcasing ripe dark plum, black cherry, and blackcurrant on the nose. The palate reveals cocoa and subtle spice notes, supported by medium tannins that are soft and integrated rather than austere. This is a food-friendly, approachable wine designed for everyday enjoyment—versatile enough for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based cuisine.

name: Montgras  Day One Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet with dark fruit depth, cedar structure, and food-friendly tannins—a versatile Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Montgras Day One Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal in a medium-full frame. Black cherry and blackcurrant dominate the primary profile, supported by herbaceous and mineral undertones characteristic of the region. Cedar and cocoa emerge on the mid-palate, with fine-grained tannins providing structure without heaviness. The wine's balance and approachability make it an excellent choice for everyday entertaining and food pairing across red meat, poultry, and aged cheese.

name: Piccini  Frapasso Primitivo di Manduria
short_description (EN Store): Bold Primitivo from Manduria with dark cherry, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A characterful Primitivo from Puglia's Manduria subregion, this wine delivers a bold nose and a palate brimming with dark fruit, cherry, and raspberry. Smoke and black pepper notes add complexity and spice, while fully rounded tannins provide structure without harshness. Smooth and approachable, it balances richness with drinkability—ideal for pizza, pasta, and grilled meats.

name: Chateau De Pez  2nd Pez Saint-Estèphe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe blend: blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Food-friendly Bordeaux for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store):
Château de Pez's 2nd Pez Saint-Estèphe is a traditional Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (58%) with supporting Merlot (38%) and Petit Verdot (4%). The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and medium tannin structure characteristic of the appellation.

On the nose and palate, blackcurrant and plum dominate the primary layer, supported by black cherry notes. Secondary winemaking character emerges through cedar and tobacco, with subtle oak integration. Tertiary earth and leather notes suggest classical Bordeaux aging potential and complexity.

This is a food-friendly expression suited to grilled red meats, lamb dishes, and braised beef preparations, as well as aged hard cheeses and cured charcuterie.

name: Dominio de Punctum  Oak Aged Tempranillo / Petit Verdot
short_description (EN Store): Oak-aged Tempranillo-Petit Verdot blend from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and spice—elegant structure for red meat and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Dominio de Punctum's oak-aged Tempranillo and Petit Verdot blend represents La Mancha's approachable yet structured red wine tradition. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, supported by vanilla and cedar from careful oak aging. Mid-palate tannins are refined and integrated, with secondary notes of leather and tobacco emerging on the finish. This medium-full-bodied wine balances fruit-forward character with subtle complexity, making it versatile at table and rewarding for short-term cellaring.

name: Dominio de Punctum  Finca Fabian Tempranillo
short_description (EN Store): Tempranillo from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and oak—a food-friendly Spanish red with medium tannin.
description (EN Store): Dominio de Punctum's Finca Fabian Tempranillo represents the approachable side of La Mancha's signature varietal. Ripe red cherry and plum dominate the nose, supported by toasted oak and subtle leather notes that speak to careful winemaking. The palate shows medium body with balanced tannin and acidity, making it versatile at table. Earthy undertones and a whisper of tobacco emerge on the finish, adding complexity without demanding attention. A reliable, well-crafted expression suited to everyday enjoyment and casual entertaining.

name: Dominio de Punctum  Norte Sur Tempranillo / Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): La Mancha Tempranillo-Cabernet blend with ripe cherry, leather, and oak—a food-friendly, medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Dominio de Punctum's Norte Sur is a Tempranillo and Cabernet Sauvignon blend from La Mancha, Spain. This medium-full-bodied red opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, supported by oak and subtle spice on the palate. Leather and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and cured charcuterie.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Haut Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe plum, berry, and subtle vanilla—balanced structure for food-friendly enjoyment.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mouton Cadet Reserve Haut-Médoc is a classically proportioned Bordeaux blend anchored by Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine opens with dominant ripe plum and berry aromatics, supported by hints of black cherry and red fruit. On the palate, vanilla and cocoa notes from careful oak aging complement the fruit-forward character, while integrated tannins and medium acidity create a soft, juicy texture. The herbaceous undertone adds complexity without dominating. Medium-Full body and balanced structure make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or roasted poultry.

name: Pinical Estate  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with dark plum, blackberry, and peppery spice—a bold, structured red for grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Pinical Estate Shiraz is a full-bodied red wine from South Eastern Australia, built on a foundation of dark plum and blackberry fruit with pronounced black pepper and spice. The palate shows medium-high tannin structure and a secondary layer of smoke and licorice, evolving toward leather and earthy notes on the finish. This is a wine designed for hearty fare: grilled steaks, braised beef, game, and smoky barbecue all find natural partners in its weight and complexity.

name: Judith Beck  Bambule Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Austrian Pinot Noir from Neusiedlersee with elegant red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Judith Beck's Bambule Pinot Noir is a single-varietal expression from Austria's Neusiedlersee region in Burgenland. The 2019 vintage showcases the classic elegance of Pinot Noir: bright red cherry and strawberry on the nose, layered with subtle plum and earthy mineral notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or tomato-based pasta dishes. The silky mouthfeel and clean finish reflect careful winemaking in a region known for quality red wine production.

name: Zenato  Podere Prospero Bolgheri Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannin—elegant Tuscan red for the table.
description (EN Store): Zenato's Podere Prospero Bolgheri Rosso is a Bordeaux-style blend from Tuscany's prestigious Bolgheri subregion, built on Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Merlot. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure, offering dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, layered with cedar and herbaceous notes. Secondary oak influence adds subtle spice and tobacco complexity. This is a food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and Mediterranean cuisine, with enough structure to age gracefully.

name: Zenato  Alanera Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Veneto blend of Corvina, Rondinella & Corvinone with Cabernet & Merlot. Dark fruit, balanced oak, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Zenato's Alanera Rosso is a Veneto-based proprietary blend anchored by Corvina (55%), Rondinella (25%), and Corvinone (10%), with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine presents a medium-full body with restrained acidity and moderate tannin, delivering dark berry compote and black cherry on the nose, followed by plum and subtle oak on the palate. Tertiary earth and leather notes emerge with air, adding complexity. A food-friendly red suited to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based preparations.

name: 2Naturkinder  Kleine Wanderlust
short_description (EN Store): German Regent-Grauburgunder blend with dark plum, cherry, and herbaceous notes. Food-friendly, medium-full body.
description (EN Store): A distinctive German red from Franken, this 2018 blend marries 85% Regent with 15% Grauburgunder to create a medium-full-bodied wine with approachable structure. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by subtle herbaceous and spice notes from the winemaking. Soft tannins and earthy undertones emerge on the finish, offering versatility at the table. Ideal for roasted poultry, tomato-based pasta, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: 2Naturkinder  Black Betty
short_description (EN Store): German Franken blend of Domina, Regent & Pinot Meunier. Dark fruit, spice & herbaceous notes; food-friendly medium-full body.
description (EN Store): A distinctive Franken red from 2Naturkinder, this 2020 blend marries three complementary varieties: Domina (57%) provides dark cherry and plum depth, Regent (42%) adds blackberry structure and soft tannins, and Pinot Meunier (1%) contributes subtle complexity. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and approachable tannin, making it versatile at the table. Herbaceous and spice notes weave through the dark fruit profile, suggesting careful winemaking that respects the varietal character of each component.

name: 2Naturkinder  Spatburgunder
short_description (EN Store): Elegant German Pinot Noir with ripe cherry, plum, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly medium-full body.
description (EN Store): 2Naturkinder's 2018 Spätburgunder represents the classical German expression of Pinot Noir from the Franken region. The wine displays a harmonious balance of ripe red fruit—cherry and plum—with subtle earthy undertones and mineral precision. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make this a versatile food companion, equally at home with roasted poultry, pork preparations, or mushroom-driven dishes. The wine's restraint and elegance reflect the cool-climate terroir of Franken, offering complexity without heaviness.

name: 2Naturkinder  Grosse Wanderlust
short_description (EN Store): German Regent red with dark fruit, herbaceous charm, and food-friendly tannins. Versatile Franken expression.
description (EN Store): 2Naturkinder's Grosse Wanderlust is a 100% Regent expression from Franken, Germany's distinctive red-wine region. Regent—a crossing of Chambourcin and Diana—delivers a medium-full frame with approachable tannin structure and layered red-to-dark fruit character. The 2018 vintage shows ripe plum and black cherry on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and subtle spice that reflect the variety's cool-climate origin. Soft, integrated tannins and balanced acidity make this wine a natural companion to roasted meats, herb-driven pasta, and earthy vegetable preparations.

name: Domaine Jean Fery & Fils  Pernand-Vergelesses 1er Cru "les Vergelesses"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir from a premier cru vineyard; red cherry, earth, and refined minerality.
description (EN Store): Domaine Jean Fery & Fils' Pernand-Vergelesses 1er Cru "les Vergelesses" is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir from the Beaune subregion. The wine displays a medium-full body with bright red fruit on the nose—cherry and red berry—complemented by herbaceous undertones and subtle oak influence from careful winemaking. Mid-palate minerality and earthy notes suggest terroir-driven complexity, while moderate tannins provide structure without heaviness. A food-friendly expression suited to roasted poultry, mushroom-based dishes, and lighter red meat preparations.

name: Domaine Jean Fery & Fils  Gevrey-Chambertin "Les Crais"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir from Gevrey-Chambertin with red cherry, minerality, and silky tannins. Food-friendly Burgundy classic.
description (EN Store): Domaine Jean Fery & Fils' Gevrey-Chambertin "Les Crais" is a classically structured Pinot Noir from one of Burgundy's most prestigious communes. The 2021 vintage expresses the appellation's hallmark elegance: bright red cherry and black cherry fruit on the nose, underpinned by mineral precision and subtle herbaceous notes. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, while restrained oak integration supports rather than dominates. Tertiary earth and tobacco notes suggest graceful aging potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, roasted poultry, and earthy vegetable preparations.

name: Domaine Jean Fery & Fils  Vougeot 1er Cru "Les Cras"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with red cherry, earthy minerality, and silky tannins—a food-friendly 1er Cru from Vougeot.
description (EN Store): Domaine Jean Fery & Fils' Vougeot 1er Cru 'Les Cras' is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir that exemplifies the region's hallmark balance of fruit, earth, and refinement. The 2021 vintage delivers bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, underpinned by subtle oak and mineral undertones. On the palate, medium-full body and silky tannins frame a food-friendly profile with earthy complexity and a lingering spiced finish. This wine reflects the terroir of the Côte de Nuits and rewards both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Les Solstices  Cuvee Tradition Rouge De France (Fruit Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Provence red with red fruit, plum, and herbaceous notes; medium-full body and balanced tannin.
description (EN Store): Les Solstices Cuvée Tradition Rouge de France is a medium-full-bodied red wine from Provence's Les Baux-de-Provence subregion. The wine opens with dominant red fruit and dark plum character, supported by herbaceous and spice notes that reflect the region's Mediterranean terroir. Balanced acidity and medium tannin structure make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Chateau Puy Favereau Bordeaux Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Bordeaux Supérieur with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy classic for red meat.
description (EN Store): Château Puy Favereau Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine displays a compelling primary profile of blackcurrant and dark plum, complemented by cedar and tobacco notes that deepen on the palate. Secondary oak and leather characteristics add complexity, while tertiary mineral and graphite nuances suggest age-worthiness. Ideal for grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Chateau De Parenchere  Bordeaux Superieur
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux Supérieur with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring and classic French cuisine.
description (EN Store): Château de Parenchère's Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically proportioned red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedar and spice notes that reflect careful oak aging. A mineral backbone and emerging tobacco character suggest age-worthiness and complexity. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this wine equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged charcuterie. The tertiary leather and earth notes develop with bottle age, rewarding patient collectors.

name: Torrebruna  Sangiovese Di Toscana IGP
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese with bright cherry, dried herbs, and structured tannins—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Torrebruna's Sangiovese di Toscana IGP delivers the hallmark character of central Italian red wine: vibrant red cherry and herbaceous notes on the nose, supported by earthy undertones and a mineral spine. The palate shows medium-full body with high acidity and medium-high tannin structure, creating a dry, refreshing finish ideal for pairing with Italian cuisine and grilled meats. A versatile, well-balanced expression of Sangiovese suited to both casual dining and food-focused occasions.

name: Birchgrove  Bird'S Block Cuvee "Inspiring Red" (Fruit Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian red with dark berry fruit and food-friendly structure. Smooth, versatile, and ready to drink.
description (EN Store):
Birchgrove's Bird's Block Cuvée 'Inspiring Red' is a medium-full bodied red wine from South Eastern Australia, crafted to deliver accessible, fruit-forward character with balanced tannin structure. Dark red and berry fruit dominate the palate, supported by a smooth, integrated tannin profile that makes this wine naturally food-friendly across a wide range of casual dining occasions.

The wine's approachable nature and medium acidity make it an ideal companion to grilled meats, tomato-based pasta dishes, roasted poultry, and comfort-food classics. Best enjoyed slightly cool or at room temperature.

name: Castello Romitorio  Brunello Di Montalcino Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello Riserva from Montalcino: dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco complexity in a structured, age-worthy Sangiovese.
description (EN Store): Castello Romitorio's Brunello di Montalcino Riserva DOCG is a classically structured Tuscan red built on 100% Sangiovese from the prestigious Montalcino terroir. The 2015 vintage displays the hallmark dark fruit intensity and mineral-driven tannin profile characteristic of the appellation's finest expressions. Riserva designation indicates extended aging in oak and bottle, developing secondary cedar and spice notes alongside tertiary leather and tobacco complexity. This is a wine of considerable depth and food compatibility, suited to serious cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial dishes.

name: Argiano  Solengo IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Cabernet, Merlot, Petit Verdot & Sangiovese. Full-bodied, structured, dark fruit & cedar.
description (EN Store): Argiano's Solengo IGT is a classically structured Super Tuscan that marries Tuscan Sangiovese with Bordeaux varietals—Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot—to create a wine of considerable depth and aging potential. The 2021 vintage displays a full body with high tannin architecture, anchored by blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar oak and black pepper spice. Secondary oak influence adds vanilla and toast complexity, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest a wine built for the cellar. Ideal for serious red-meat cuisine and aged cheese boards.

name: Passi di Orma Bolgheri DOC
short_description (EN Store): Bolgheri Bordeaux blend of dark plum, black cherry & cedar—medium-full, food-friendly Tuscan red.
description (EN Store): Passi di Orma Bolgheri DOC is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by Merlot (40%), Cabernet Sauvignon (35%), and Cabernet Franc (25%), sourced from Tenuta Sette Ponti's vineyard holdings in the prestigious Bolgheri subregion of Tuscany. The 2023 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, with supporting blackcurrant depth and subtle cedar complexity. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while retaining food-pairing versatility across grilled red meats, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb preparations. A classic expression of Tuscan terroir and Bordeaux varietal tradition.

name: Rosso di Orma IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Sangiovese. Dark fruit, cedar, herbaceous complexity.
description (EN Store): Rosso di Orma is a Super Tuscan red from Tenuta Sette Ponti in Bolgheri, blending 60% Merlot, 30% Cabernet Sauvignon, and 10% Sangiovese. The 2021 vintage delivers a medium-full bodied wine with ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by cedary oak and herbaceous undertones. The palate shows balanced tannins and medium acidity, with layered dark fruit, spice, and mineral notes that evolve toward tobacco and earth on the finish. A food-friendly Tuscan expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Domaine Lafage  Authentique Cotes Du Roussillon
short_description (EN Store): Food-friendly Côtes du Roussillon red with ripe fruit, spice, and earthy minerality—ideal for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Domaine Lafage's Authentique Côtes du Roussillon is a medium-full-bodied red wine from France's warm southern region. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle spice and herbaceous notes that reflect the terroir. Balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure make this an approachable, versatile bottle suited to casual meals and food pairing across Mediterranean and grilled preparations.

name: Feudi di San Gregorio  Primitivo di Manduria DOC
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo from Puglia with dark cherry, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Feudi di San Gregorio's Primitivo di Manduria is a bold, fruit-forward red from southern Italy's warm Puglia region. The wine opens with a generous nose of dark cherry and ripe dark fruit, followed by a palate filled with black cherry, raspberry, and a distinctive peppery spice. Smoke and subtle oak complexity add depth without overwhelming the primary fruit character. Fully rounded tannins and smooth texture make this wine approachable and versatile at the table, yet substantial enough to pair with grilled meats, hearty pasta, and pizza. A classic expression of Primitivo's warm-climate generosity—delicious, easy-drinking, and excellent value.

name: Tasca D'Almerita  Tascante Ghiaia Nera
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Etna Nerello Mascalese with volcanic minerality, red cherry, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Sicilian classic.
description (EN Store): Tasca d'Almerita's Tascante Ghiaia Nera is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from Mount Etna's volcanic slopes. This medium-full-bodied red showcases the distinctive mineral-driven character of high-altitude Sicilian viticulture, with bright red cherry and black pepper on the nose, layered by subtle smoke and dried herb complexity. The wine's medium-high tannins and acidity provide structure and food-friendliness, making it equally at home with grilled lamb, tomato-based pasta, or cured meats. A versatile, terroir-expressive bottle that reflects Etna's volcanic heritage.

name: Masseria Borgo Dei Trulli Negroamaro Salento IGP (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, dark-fruited Negroamaro from Salento with ripe cherry, smoke, and peppery spice—approachable and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): This 100% Negroamaro from Masseria Borgo Dei Trulli showcases the warm-climate character of Salento's red wines. Dark fruit dominates the nose and palate—ripe black cherry and raspberry—with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper that add complexity without overwhelming. The wine is medium-full bodied with smooth, rounded tannins and medium acidity, making it eminently drinkable and versatile at the table. A classic expression of southern Italian red wine craftsmanship, ideal for grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, and casual entertaining.

name: Masseria Borgo Dei Trulli Primitivo Salento IGP (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Smooth, fruit-forward Primitivo from Salento with dark cherry, smoke, and peppery spice—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied Primitivo from Masseria Borgo Dei Trulli in Salento, Puglia, delivering ripe dark fruit and red fruit character with supporting notes of smoke and black pepper. The palate is rounded and smooth with well-integrated tannins, making this wine eminently drinkable and versatile at the table. Ideal for pizza, pasta, and casual grilled fare.

name: Ricossa  Barbera D' Asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Piedmont Barbera with bright acidity, red fruit, and peppery spice—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Ricossa's Barbera d'Asti DOCG is a quintessential expression of Piedmont's most versatile red varietal. The wine opens with vivid red cherry and plum aromatics, supported by black pepper and subtle herbaceous notes. On the palate, medium-full body and lively acidity create a dynamic frame for dark fruit and mineral undertones, finishing with a dry, peppery persistence. A benchmark food wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Anakena  Nuna Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins—a classic expression of Cachapoal Valley terroir.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Nuna Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Cachapoal Valley delivers a classic full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while black pepper spice adds complexity on the mid-palate. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food-pairing versatility. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with bottle age, adding savory depth to an already compelling profile.

name: Anakena  Nuna Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Carmenère with ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, and elegant structure—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and rich dishes.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Nuna Carmenère from Chile's Cachapoal Valley delivers a classic expression of this distinctive varietal. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, complemented by characteristic bell pepper notes that define Carmenère's signature profile. Medium-full body and balanced tannins provide structure without heaviness, while subtle spice and smoke add complexity. Earthy undertones and hints of tobacco emerge on the finish, suggesting modest aging potential. A versatile, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Anakena  Nuna Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Malbec with ripe plum, blackberry, and chocolate; structured tannins ideal for grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Nuna Malbec from Cachapoal Valley presents a full-bodied expression of Chile's signature red varietal. Ripe dark fruit—blackberry and plum—dominates the nose, supported by cocoa and vanilla notes from oak maturation. On the palate, medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while earth and spice add complexity. The wine's roundness and smooth texture make it an approachable yet serious food companion, particularly suited to grilled red meat and braised beef dishes.

name: Cabriz  Cohleita Dao (Organic)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Dão red with red fruit, spice, and mineral character—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Cabriz Colheita Dão is an organic red wine from Portugal's Dão region, crafted with sustainable viticulture principles. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and a mineral-driven profile. Red and dark fruit dominate the palate, complemented by subtle spice and earthy undertones that reflect the region's granite-rich terroir. Approachable yet structured, it pairs well with grilled meats, tomato-based dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite) Saga R Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Prestigious Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend of power and elegance—blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Domaines Barons de Rothschild's Saga R is a classic Haut-Médoc expression built on Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and cassis fruit, supported by cedary oak and subtle tobacco complexity. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, it demands substantial food pairing and rewards cellaring. Tertiary minerality and slate notes emerge with age, adding gravitas to an already structured, age-worthy Bordeaux.

name: Maison Castel  Syrah Pays d'Oc
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Syrah with ripe blackberry, pepper spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly red for grilled meats and game.
description (EN Store): A full-bodied Syrah from Pays d'Oc, this wine showcases the classic dark-fruit intensity and peppery spice characteristic of southern French Syrah. Ripe blackberry and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by black pepper and subtle smoke notes that reflect both fruit ripeness and careful winemaking. Medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, with earthy undertones emerging on the finish. The wine's balance of fruit-forward character and savory complexity makes it a versatile partner for grilled red meats, lamb, and game preparations.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Cote des Roses Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Languedoc rouge from the world's largest biodynamic producer — Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre vinified parcel-by-parcel under the lunar calendar.
description (EN Store): When Gérard Bertrand began converting his Languedoc estates to biodynamics from 2002 onward — ultimately certifying all holdings to Demeter standard — he was making a deliberate argument against the region's bulk-production identity, insisting that the South of France could deliver mineral precision at scale. Côte des Roses Rouge draws on the warm Mediterranean basin of Languedoc-Roussillon, where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming without chemical inputs, and where the core Languedoc blend of Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre ripens fully under intense southern sun. Each parcel is vinified separately in concrete vats or stainless steel before assemblage — a discipline Bertrand applies across all 17 estates — with every winemaking decision timed to the biodynamic lunar calendar. The result is a wine that carries the aromatic lift and garrigue character typical of the appellation, but with a structural clarity that separates it from conventionally farmed peers. What is unusual here is the combination of Demeter certification at genuine commercial scale: no other producer in the world applies this level of biodynamic rigour across a portfolio of this size, meaning even this entry-level rouge is farmed to the same standard as Bertrand's ฿65,000 Clos d'Ora. Drink now through 2–3 years for primary fruit expression.

name: Grand Napa Vineyards  "Stone Crib" Rutherford Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Rutherford Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—classic Napa elegance.
description (EN Store): Grand Napa Vineyards' Stone Crib Rutherford Estate Cabernet Sauvignon exemplifies the power and refinement of Napa Valley's premier subregion. The 2018 vintage delivers a full-bodied expression anchored by dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, layered with pronounced cedar and oak complexity from careful winemaking. High, well-integrated tannins provide structure and aging potential, while subtle earth and leather notes hint at tertiary development. This is a wine built for the table—pair with grilled steaks, herb-roasted lamb, or slow-braised beef to unlock its full potential.

name: Grand Napa Vineyards  Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with blackcurrant depth, cedar structure, and refined tannins for premium red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Grand Napa Vineyards Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley presents a classic expression of California's flagship varietal. The wine displays a full body with prominent blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit on the nose, complemented by cedary oak and subtle tobacco notes. High, well-integrated tannins provide structure and aging potential, while secondary oak influence adds complexity and refinement. Tertiary leather and earth notes suggest maturity and depth. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Pure Bred  Old Vine Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Rustic California Zinfandel with dark berry intensity, peppery spice, and food-friendly tannin structure.
description (EN Store): Pure Bred Old Vine Zinfandel is a medium-full bodied red wine showcasing the characteristic dark fruit and spice profile of mature Zinfandel vines. The wine displays ripe blackberry and plum on the nose, layered with black pepper and subtle oak influence. On the palate, briary tannins and a rustic texture provide structure and grip, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, barbecue, and hearty braised dishes. The old vine designation suggests concentration and depth typical of established vineyard sites in California.

name: Noble Hill  Estate Reserve
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a classic pairing for grilled red meat.
description (EN Store):
Noble Hill Estate Reserve is a Bordeaux-style red blend from South Africa's Stellenbosch region, built on Cabernet Sauvignon with supporting Cabernet Franc, Merlot, and Petit Verdot. The wine displays a full body and high tannin structure, anchored by dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit on the primary palate. Cedar and oak from careful winemaking provide secondary complexity, while tertiary notes of tobacco and leather suggest aging potential and food-friendly depth.

This is a wine for serious red-meat occasions: grilled steaks, braised beef, lamb, and game all find natural partners in its structured tannins and savory secondary character. The wine's acidity and tannin grip make it equally suited to aged hard cheeses and rich, herb-forward preparations.

name: Noble Hill  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Cabernet-led blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure. Approachable now; gains complexity with age.
description (EN Store): Noble Hill Cabernet Sauvignon is a Bordeaux-style blend anchored by 93% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Franc from Stellenbosch's prime terroir. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, layered with cedar and black pepper spice. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it immediately approachable, yet the emerging tertiary tobacco and leather notes suggest rewarding cellaring potential. Well-suited to grilled and braised red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Noble Hill  Field Atlas Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Stellenbosch Syrah-led blend with dark fruit, pepper spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly, balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Noble Hill's Field Atlas Syrah is a Rhône-inspired blend anchored by 89% Syrah with supporting Mourvèdre, Grenache Noir, and Viognier from Stellenbosch. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and blackberry fruit, layered with black pepper and subtle smoke from measured oak aging. Secondary spice and licorice notes add textural interest, while tertiary leather and earth tones suggest modest cellaring potential. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and charcuterie boards.

name: Penfolds  Bin 600 California Collection Cabernet Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds transplants its cross-regional blending doctrine to Napa Valley and Paso Robles — Cabernet Sauvignon married to Shiraz, aged in American oak, bottled as a deliberate California provocation.
description (EN Store): When Penfolds — founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide — extended its multi-regional blending philosophy to California, it did so without apology: the same winemaker-over-terroir doctrine that built Grange now operates in Napa Valley and Paso Robles. Bin 600 is a Cabernet Sauvignon–Shiraz blend, a pairing that is orthodox in South Australia yet transgressive in Napa, where varietal purity and appellation singularity are the commercial religion. The California sites contribute warm-climate fruit density and structural weight, while Penfolds' house technique — American oak aging, acclimatised yeast cultures, and temperature-controlled fermentation pioneered by Ray Beckwith — imposes the recognisable mid-palate richness and structured tannin architecture that defines the Bin lineage. The oak regime follows the same hogshead-influenced thinking that underpins Bin 389, calibrated to frame fruit rather than dominate it. What is genuinely unusual here is the blend itself: no serious Napa producer co-ferments or blends Shiraz into a Cabernet-dominant red at this price tier — Penfolds does it because the house has never accepted the premise that a single variety or a single site is inherently superior to the winemaker's editorial hand. The 2020 vintage in California delivered concentrated, ripe fruit from a warm, low-yield growing season, producing a wine built for medium-term cellaring rather than immediate consumption.

name: Penfolds  II Dourthe Cabernet Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds crosses hemispheres: Haut-Médoc Cabernet meets South Australian Shiraz, vinified in Bordeaux with Penfolds' multi-regional blending doctrine.
description (EN Store): When Penfolds — founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide — extended its blending philosophy to Bordeaux, it did so not as homage but as provocation: sourcing Cabernet Sauvignon from Haut-Médoc soils and marrying it with South Australian Shiraz, a pairing that no Bordeaux château would sanction and no Australian producer had executed at this level. The collaboration with Dourthe places the wine in the Haut-Médoc, where gravel-over-clay terraces above the Gironde moderate ripening and deliver the structural backbone Cabernet Sauvignon requires. Vinification follows Penfolds' house protocol, with the winemaking team applying the same acclimatised yeast cultures and temperature-controlled fermentation that anchor the Grange lineage, then aging in oak — the precise regime informed by Chief Winemaker Peter Gago's cross-hemisphere brief. The 2019 vintage across Bordeaux delivered a warm, dry growing season that compressed yields and concentrated dark fruit, mirroring what Peter Gago described as a 'sleeper vintage' character in the Australian portfolio that year. What no peer does: this is a Bordeaux-appellation wine built on Penfolds' deliberate anti-terroir doctrine — winemaker intelligence over single-plot orthodoxy — producing a Cabernet-Shiraz that is structurally Médoc yet unmistakably Penfolds in its mid-palate generosity. Drink 2024–2034.

name: Domaine De Rochebin  Vieilles Vignes Bourgogne Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, earthy minerality, and silky tannins—a versatile classic.
description (EN Store): Domaine De Rochebin's Vieilles Vignes Bourgogne Pinot Noir from the Mâconnais subregion exemplifies the refined character of traditional Burgundian winemaking. This medium-bodied red showcases vibrant red cherry and raspberry on the nose, complemented by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the terroir. High acidity and medium tannins create a silky, elegant mouthfeel that evolves gracefully on the palate, revealing layers of stone fruit and forest-floor complexity. The wine's structure and balance make it an ideal companion to a wide range of cuisines, from classic French preparations to contemporary dishes.

name: Domaine De Rochebin  Bourgogne Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with bright cherry, raspberry, and earthy minerality. Silky texture, high acidity, versatile food companion.
description (EN Store):
Domaine De Rochebin's Bourgogne Pinot Noir from the Mâconnais subregion exemplifies the refined, food-friendly character of Burgundian Pinot. This wine balances bright red fruit—cherry and raspberry—with subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of mushroom complexity. The high acidity and silky medium tannin structure create a wine that feels both fresh and elegant on the palate, inviting rather than demanding.

A versatile table wine suited to lighter proteins, vegetable-forward dishes, and classic French preparations. The wine's transparency and restraint make it an ideal aperitif or casual weeknight companion.

name: La Cour Des Dames  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Languedoc Pinot Noir with bright raspberry, fresh acidity, and silky tannins—a versatile summer red.
description (EN Store): La Cour Des Dames Pinot Noir from Languedoc-Roussillon delivers the hallmark elegance of the varietal in a light-bodied, high-acidity expression. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle earthy and herbaceous undertones that speak to the region's terroir. The silky tannin structure and mineral backbone make this wine refreshing and food-friendly, ideal for warm-weather dining and a range of lighter proteins and vegetable-forward dishes.

name: La Cour Des Dames  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Languedoc Cabernet Sauvignon with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): La Cour Des Dames Cabernet Sauvignon from Languedoc-Roussillon is a classically structured red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary aromatics, while high tannins and medium-high acidity provide the backbone for extended cellaring or immediate enjoyment with hearty fare. Secondary oak and vanilla notes complement the fruit, while emerging tertiary tobacco and leather complexity suggest this wine has been thoughtfully aged before release. A serious, food-friendly expression of Cabernet from southern France.

name: La Cour Des Dames  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Merlot with ripe plum, soft berry, and integrated tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): La Cour Des Dames Merlot from Languedoc-Roussillon is a medium-full bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe plum and berry fruit. The palate delivers juicy red fruit character with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by soft, well-integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness and approachability. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Le Clos du Serres  Sainte-Pauline Terrasses du Larzac
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Rhône blend of Syrah, Cinsault & Carignan with dark fruit, spice & mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Le Clos du Serres' Sainte-Pauline from Terrasses du Larzac blends 60% Syrah, 20% Cinsault, and 20% Carignan in the classic Rhône South tradition. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous notes. Mineral undertones and subtle earth emerge on the palate, offering food-friendly structure and medium-full body. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, Mediterranean cuisine, and rustic French fare.

name: Le Clos du Serres  Saint-Jean Terrasses du Larzac
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc field blend of Grenache, Syrah & Mourvèdre with red fruit, spice & earthy minerality. Food-friendly & versatile.
description (EN Store): Le Clos du Serres Saint-Jean Terrasses du Larzac is a Languedoc field blend anchored by Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre, Cinsault, Carignan, and Oeillade—a traditional southern Rhône-inspired composition. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe red and dark plum fruit on the primary palate, supported by herbaceous and black pepper spice notes from the winemaking. Earthy minerality emerges on the finish, reflecting the limestone terroir of Terrasses du Larzac. A food-friendly, versatile red suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic charcuterie.

name: Notte Rossa  Negroamaro Di Terra D'otranto DOP
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Negroamaro from Puglia with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Full-bodied, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Notte Rossa's Negroamaro di Terra d'Otranto DOP is a medium-full bodied red from southern Italy's sun-drenched Puglia region. The wine opens with bold dark fruit aromas—black cherry and raspberry—layered with smoke and black pepper, followed by a palate of ripe, warm-climate fruit balanced by smooth, fully rounded tannins. The spice-forward character sets it apart from typical regional offerings, delivering complexity without pretension. A versatile, easy-drinking wine that shines with pizza, pasta, grilled meats, and casual entertaining.

name: Vivaldi Amarone della Valpolicella Classico D.O.C.G
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Amarone with dark cherry, dried plum, and subtle oak—a structured, food-friendly Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Vivaldi's Amarone della Valpolicella Classico D.O.C.G (2019) is a full-bodied red wine crafted from the traditional Valpolicella blend: 70% Corvina, 15% Corvinone, and 15% Rondinella. The wine displays a rich palate of dark cherry, dried plum, and black fruit compote, with supporting notes of oak and subtle spice. Earthy undertones and hints of tobacco and leather emerge on the finish, reflecting the wine's structure and aging potential. A versatile pairing partner for braised meats, game, and aged cheeses.

name: Vivaldi Amarone della Valpolicella D.O.C.G
short_description (EN Store): Amarone della Valpolicella: concentrated dark plum, black cherry, and dried fruit with cocoa spice and leather complexity.
description (EN Store): Vivaldi's 2019 Amarone della Valpolicella D.O.C.G. is a full-bodied Veneto red built from the classic Corvina, Corvinone, and Rondinella blend. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, supported by dried fruit notes and a subtle almond character typical of the region's appassimento method. Oak and spice provide secondary structure, while tertiary leather and herbaceous notes emerge with air, suggesting complexity suited to extended cellaring. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity frame a wine designed for rich meat dishes and aged cheeses.

name: Vivaldi Valpolicella Ripasso Classico
short_description (EN Store): Ripasso-style Valpolicella with dark cherry, plum, and balanced oak—a food-friendly Veneto classic.
description (EN Store):
Vivaldi's Valpolicella Ripasso Classico showcases the signature character of this Veneto tradition: a blend of Corvina, Corvinone, and Rondinella grapes unified by the ripasso technique, which adds depth and structure through extended contact with dried-grape skins.

The wine opens with dominant black cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle oak and spice from winemaking. A medium-full body and balanced tannin frame the palate, while emerging earth and leather notes hint at complexity. This is a versatile, food-friendly red suited to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot with ripe plum, soft berry, and integrated tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Reserva Merlot from Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with medium-full body and well-integrated tannins. The nose opens with ripe plum and red fruit, complemented by subtle vanilla notes from careful oak aging. On the palate, a juicy medley of berry and cranberry unfolds with herbaceous undertones, leading to a clean, balanced finish. The wine's soft texture and moderate acidity make it an accessible yet serious choice for everyday dining and special occasions alike.

name: 9 Lives Reserve Intriguing Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Carmenère with dark plum, spice, and tobacco complexity—food-friendly and structured.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied Carmenère from Chile's Curicó Valley, 9 Lives Reserve showcases the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity and herbaceous character. Primary notes of dark plum and black cherry are complemented by secondary spice and cocoa from careful winemaking, while tertiary tobacco and leather notes add sophistication and age-worthiness. The balanced tannin structure and medium acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled red meat, braised beef, or herb-crusted lamb.

name: 9 Lives Reserve Apasionado Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Chilean red blend with ripe dark fruit, subtle spice, and food-friendly structure from Curicó Valley.
description (EN Store):
9 Lives Reserve Apasionado is a medium-full bodied red blend from San Pedro's Curicó Valley portfolio. Ripe dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by gentle spice and subtle oak influence on the palate. The wine's balanced tannin and moderate acidity make it an approachable yet structured choice for everyday dining and special occasions alike.

This blend showcases the warmth and fruit-forward character typical of Chilean reds, with enough complexity to complement grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. A versatile, food-friendly expression suited to casual entertaining and table service.

name: Babich Family  Reserve Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Marlborough Syrah with blackberry, pepper, and smoky complexity—a bold red for grilled meats and game.
description (EN Store): Babich Family's Reserve Syrah from Marlborough showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and peppery spice. This full-bodied single varietal displays dominant blackberry and dark plum on the nose, supported by black pepper and subtle smoke on the palate. Secondary minerality and leather notes add complexity and structure, making it an excellent match for grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-cooked game dishes.

name: Bertani  Amarone della Valpolicella Valpantena DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Amarone with concentrated dark fruit, almond, and spice—a structured, age-worthy Veneto classic.
description (EN Store): Bertani's 2017 Amarone della Valpolicella Valpantena showcases the hallmark intensity of appassimento-dried Corvina grapes. The wine opens with concentrated black cherry and dried plum, supported by almond and subtle herbaceous notes. Mid-palate reveals cocoa and oak integration, while the finish develops leather and spice complexity. Medium-full body and balanced acidity frame firm tannins, creating a wine suited to extended cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial fare.

name: Bertani  Amarone della Valpolicella Classico
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Amarone Classico with dark cherry, plum, and tobacco complexity. Full-bodied, age-worthy Veneto icon.
description (EN Store): Bertani's 2015 Amarone della Valpolicella Classico is a benchmark expression of Veneto's most prestigious dried-grape red. Built on Corvina's naturally high acidity and tannin structure, this wine displays dominant dark plum and black cherry on the nose, supported by cocoa and subtle oak from careful aging. The palate is broad and structured, with leather and tobacco tertiary notes emerging as the wine opens, revealing the complexity that defines mature Amarone. Ideal for cellaring or enjoying now with rich meat dishes and aged cheeses.

name: Bertani  Catullo Valpolicella Ripasso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with dark fruit, almond, and herbaceous notes. Medium-bodied with balanced oak and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Bertani's Catullo Valpolicella Ripasso DOC 2018 exemplifies the refined character of this Veneto classic. Corvina-based, the wine displays a compelling nose of dark fruit compote and cherry, underpinned by subtle almond and herbaceous undertones. On the palate, the medium body and balanced tannin structure deliver a wine that is both approachable and layered, with restrained oak integration and a lingering spice finish. The Ripasso technique—partial drying of grapes before fermentation—adds depth without heaviness, making this a versatile food companion.

name: Bertani  Ripasso della Valpolicella Valpantena  DOC
