short_description (EN Store): Elegant Valpolicella Ripasso with dark fruit, almond, and herbaceous character. Medium-bodied with balanced tannin.
description (EN Store): Bertani's 2018 Ripasso della Valpolicella Valpantena is a refined expression of Corvina from the Veneto region. The wine displays a restrained nose marked by dark fruit compote, cherry, and subtle almond notes, leading to an inviting palate with herbaceous undertones and measured oak influence. Medium body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile food wine, particularly suited to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-driven pasta dishes. Excellent value proposition for a traditional Valpolicella Ripasso.

name: Chateau Clou Du Pin Superieur Premium
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy structure for serious red meat.
description (EN Store): Château Clou du Pin Supérieur Premium is a classically structured Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine displays a full body with high tannins and medium-high acidity, delivering a powerful first impression of blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, supported by black pepper spice and subtle oak influence. With bottle age, tertiary layers of leather and mineral complexity develop, marking this as a wine built for the cellar. The firm tannin structure and balanced acidity promise evolution over years, making it an excellent choice for serious collectors and those seeking traditional Bordeaux character.

name: Le Carillon de Rouget  Pomerol 2019
short_description (EN Store): Pomerol blend of ripe plum, dark cherry, and cocoa with silky tannins and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Le Carillon de Rouget is a classic Pomerol expression from the 2019 vintage, blending Merlot and Cabernet Franc to create a wine of medium-full body and refined character. The nose opens with dominant ripe plum and dark cherry, supported by subtle cocoa and vanilla notes from careful oak aging. On the palate, the wine delivers a juicy medley of berry fruits with well-integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. Herbaceous undertones add complexity, while the finish is smooth and persistent. This is a wine built for both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring, offering the soft texture and approachability Pomerol is known for.

name: Naudin Père & Fils  Pommard Premier Cru
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with silky tannins, bright red fruit, and earthy minerality—a classic expression of Pommard terroir.
description (EN Store): Naudin Père & Fils' 2016 Pommard Premier Cru is a refined expression of Burgundy's most structured Pinot Noir appellation. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry aromatics, supported by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the region's limestone-rich soils. Medium body and high acidity create a graceful frame, while silky tannins provide structure without heaviness. The palate evolves with minerality and hints of forest floor, characteristic of mature Pommard. This vintage rewards both near-term enjoyment and careful cellaring, offering versatility across elegant dining occasions.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Limited Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile table wine for poultry and lighter fare.
description (EN Store): Duckhorn's Decoy Limited Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir (2019) delivers the hallmark elegance of the region in a medium-bodied frame. Bright raspberry and cherry fruit dominate the nose, supported by subtle earthy minerality and stone fruit undertones. The palate is silky and refined, with medium tannins and vibrant acidity that keeps the wine fresh and food-friendly. A whisper of spice and subtle oak integration round out the profile, making this a versatile choice for roasted poultry, duck, and mushroom-based dishes.

name: Allegrini  La Bragia IGT
short_description (EN Store): Allegrini's benchmark Valpolicella blend: dark cherry, cocoa, and baking spice with velvety tannin and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A signature expression from Valpolicella's leading producer, this Corvina-based blend showcases the region's mastery of dark fruit concentration and elegant structure. The nose opens with ripe black cherry and dark plum compote, supported by subtle cocoa and warm baking spice from thoughtful oak aging. On the palate, the wine delivers a velvety mid-weight frame with balanced tannin and medium acidity, finishing with lingering spice and dark chocolate notes. This is a versatile, food-friendly red that bridges casual weeknight enjoyment and more formal table settings.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Nizza La Court Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Structured Barbera Riserva from Piedmont with ripe red fruit, oak spice, and earthy complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Nizza La Court Riserva DOCG is a single-varietal Barbera from Piedmont's prestigious Nizza zone, crafted to showcase the grape's natural structure and aging potential. The 2019 vintage displays a medium-full body with medium-high tannins and balanced acidity, offering ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, complemented by oak-derived spice and vanilla notes. Tertiary layers of earth, tobacco, and subtle leather emerge on the palate, reflecting careful oak maturation and the wine's capacity for further development. This is a serious, food-friendly expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barbaresco Reyna DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco with cherry, licorice, and cedar—structured, age-worthy, and classically Piedmontese.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Barbaresco Reyna DOCG is a refined expression of Nebbiolo from one of Italy's most prestigious red-wine regions. The 2018 vintage displays the varietal's signature combination of bright cherry and licorice on the nose, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the terroir's complexity. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide structure and grip, while balanced acidity ensures freshness and aging potential. Subtle tar and dried-fruit undertones emerge on the palate, hinting at the wine's capacity to evolve gracefully over the next decade. A classic food wine for serious collectors and those seeking authentic Piedmontese character.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barbaresco Asili DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Asili cru: cherry, licorice, and cedar with age-worthy structure and refined tannin.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's 2019 Barbaresco Asili DOCG showcases the pedigree of one of Piedmont's most prestigious single vineyards. This medium-full-bodied Nebbiolo opens with bright cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the site's mineral character. Medium-high acidity and tannin provide backbone and aging potential, while secondary spice and tertiary tar complexity emerge on the palate. A wine of classical elegance suited to contemplative cellaring or pairing with refined cuisine.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barbaresco Faset DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco with cherry, licorice, and cedar—structured tannins and age-worthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's 2019 Barbaresco Faset DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone. The wine opens with primary notes of cherry and licorice, layered with herbaceous undertones and cedar minerality. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide backbone, while secondary spice and oak integration support the fruit. Tertiary tar and dried-fruit character emerge, signaling age-worthiness and complexity. A refined expression suited to fine dining and cellar investment.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Barolo Cannubi DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's prestigious Cannubi cru; structured cherry, licorice, and cedar with age-worthy tannin.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Barolo Cannubi DOCG 2020 is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most revered vineyard sites. The wine opens with dominant cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous undertones characteristic of the variety. Cedar and tobacco emerge on the mid-palate, supported by firm, well-integrated tannins and bright acidity. Tertiary notes of tar and dried fruit suggest considerable aging potential, positioning this wine as a serious food companion for grilled red meats, lamb, and game. The Cannubi designation signals vineyard-specific quality and terroir expression.

name: Opus One  Vintage 2018
short_description (EN Store): Oakville's To Kalon Q Block alluvial gravel, gravity-flow vinification, 100% new French oak — Napa's original Franco-American grand vin, built closer to Pauillac than Oakville blockbuster.
description (EN Store):
When Baron Philippe de Rothschild and Robert Mondavi formalized their joint venture in 1980, they made a deliberate decision that still defines this wine: pick earlier than any Napa peer, target Bordeaux Left Bank structure, and never compromise on 100% new French oak — a commitment that runs counter to the valley's riper, more exuberant mainstream. The 2018 vintage produced double the volume of the fire-affected 2017, yet the estate maintained deep fruit concentration, a testament to the discipline of early-morning hand-harvesting and parcel-by-parcel vinification across four Oakville bench blocks.

The anchor site is the historic To Kalon Q Block, 35 acres on an alluvial fan washed down from the Mayacamas Mountains. Gravelly clay loam over alluvial and volcanic subsoil, moderated by cool afternoon air drawn from San Pablo Bay, delivers the pronounced diurnal shift that locks in acidity — the structural backbone that separates Opus One from warmer-floor Napa peers.

Each parcel ferments separately in one of approximately 50 dedicated stainless-steel tanks, with native yeasts isolated from each vineyard site. The slope-built winery moves every litre by gravity alone — no pumps at any stage. After blending, the wine ages 18–20 months in 100% new French oak in the semi-circular underground Grand Chai, then receives an additional 18 months of bottle age before release — roughly three years from harvest to market. Fining is by fresh egg whites; filtration status is undisclosed.

The 2018's higher yields did not dilute character: expect dark fruit density framed by graphite and cedar, with the fine-grained tannin architecture that defines this address. Drink 2026–2042+.

name: Jeff Carrel  Les Darons Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-based Languedoc red with ripe red fruit, spice, and mineral backbone—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Jeff Carrel's Les Darons Rouge is a single-varietal Grenache from Languedoc-Roussillon, delivering the classic warmth and spice character of the southern Rhône tradition. Ripe red fruit and plum dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper and herbaceous notes on the mid-palate. Earthy minerality in the finish provides structure and food-pairing versatility. A medium-full-bodied, medium-tannin expression suited to grilled meats, Mediterranean herb-forward dishes, and rustic tapas.

name: Jeff Carrel  La Bette
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Rhône blend of Syrah, Grenache & Carignan—dark fruit, spice & garrigue. Food-friendly, medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Jeff Carrel's La Bette is a Languedoc-Roussillon red blend anchored by Syrah (50%), Grenache (35%), and Carignan (15%)—a classic southern Rhône structure. The wine opens with dominant dark cherry and blackberry fruit, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous garrigue notes typical of the region. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it approachable yet structured, with earthy undertones emerging on the finish. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, Mediterranean dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Jeff Carrel  Grenache Under My Skin
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-Syrah blend from Languedoc with ripe red fruit, spice, and garrigue—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Jeff Carrel's Grenache Under My Skin is a Languedoc-Roussillon red built on 80% Grenache with 20% Syrah, delivering the warm, fruit-forward character typical of southern Rhône-style blends. Ripe cherry and blackberry dominate the palate, supported by white pepper spice and subtle garrigue notes that speak to the region's wild herbs. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured enough for grilled meats, Mediterranean fare, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Jeff Carrel  Un Jour Je Serai Une Icone Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Syrah with dark plum, black pepper, and smoke—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): A 100% Syrah from Languedoc-Roussillon, this wine expresses the classic character of the southern Rhône varietal: ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit anchored by peppery spice and subtle smoke. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it approachable yet structured enough for serious food pairing. Herbaceous undertones and emerging leather suggest modest aging potential, positioning it as a versatile table wine for grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef preparations.

name: F. Jeantet  Cotes du Rhone Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Rhône South blend with ripe red fruit, black pepper spice, and herbaceous charm—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): F. Jeantet's Côtes du Rhône Rouge is a classic expression of the southern Rhône Valley's signature GSM blend. Grenache anchors the wine with ripe red and dark plum fruit, while Syrah and Mourvèdre contribute black pepper spice, herbaceous complexity, and structural tannin. The medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled meats, Mediterranean herbs, and tomato-based dishes as it is with casual entertaining.

name: Belleville  Bordeaux AOC Red
short_description (EN Store): Classic Bordeaux blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy and structured.
description (EN Store): A traditional Bordeaux AOC red combining Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot in a full-bodied, high-tannin structure. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedar and tobacco notes that deepen on the palate. Leather and graphite minerality emerge in the finish, suggesting complexity and aging potential. Ideal for serious red-meat cuisine and contemplative cellaring.

name: Cartlidge & Browne  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): California Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Cartlidge & Browne's Merlot is a medium-full-bodied California red built on a foundation of ripe plum and mixed berry fruit. The palate delivers juicy red-fruit character with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a soft, approachable texture. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness. This is a versatile, everyday-drinking Merlot suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based pastas.

name: Rib Shack  Vintage Blend Red
short_description (EN Store): South African Pinotage-Shiraz blend with dark fruit, smoke, and spice—built for grilled meat and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Rib Shack Vintage Blend Red is a South African red wine from Western Cape, blending 60% Pinotage with 40% Shiraz. The combination delivers a medium-full bodied wine with dark plum and blackberry primary notes, complemented by smoke and spice from the winemaking. Earthy undertones and leather complexity develop on the palate, making this a food-friendly choice for grilled meats, BBQ, and cured charcuterie.

name: Strofilia  Mountain Fish Agiorgitiko
short_description (EN Store): Greek Agiorgitiko from Attica's mountains: medium-full red with cherry, plum, and peppery spice. Food-friendly and mineral-driven.
description (EN Store): Strofilia's Mountain Fish Agiorgitiko is a single-varietal red from Greece's Attica region, showcasing the native Agiorgitiko grape in its classic medium-full expression. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, supported by herbaceous notes and a mineral spine characteristic of mountain terroir. Black pepper and subtle spice emerge on the mid-palate, with earthy undertones adding complexity. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled lamb, Mediterranean herb-forward dishes, or tomato-based preparations.

name: Kaiken  Aventura Los Chacayes Norte
short_description (EN Store): Argentine Malbec-Cabernet blend from Mendoza with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body, smooth tannins.
description (EN Store): Kaiken's Aventura Los Chacayes Norte is a Malbec-dominant blend from Mendoza, Argentina, aged 15 months in new French oak. The wine opens with dominant blackberry and plum aromatics, supported by violet florality and dark fruit depth. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced medium acidity frame smooth, well-integrated tannins. Secondary oak aging imparts vanilla, cocoa, and subtle spice notes that enhance the fruit-forward profile without overwhelming it. Tertiary earth character adds complexity. A versatile, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and rich preparations.

name: Kaiken  Aventura Los Chacayes Sur
short_description (EN Store): Argentine Malbec-Cabernet blend from Mendoza with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body, smooth tannins.
description (EN Store): Kaiken's Aventura Los Chacayes Sur is a Malbec-dominant blend from Mendoza, Argentina, showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit character and structural elegance. Aged 15 months in new French oak, the wine displays dominant blackberry and plum aromatics layered with violet florality, supported by vanilla and cocoa notes from oak maturation. On the palate, medium-full body and smooth, medium-high tannins create a round, approachable profile without excessive weight. Mineral undertones and subtle earth complexity emerge on the finish, suggesting food-friendly versatility across grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged charcuterie.

name: Granmonte  Durif

name: Granmonte  The Orient Syrah

name: Granmonte  Asoke Cabernet Sauvignon Syrah

name: Colina  Cabernet Sauvignon

name: Concha Y Toro  Marques de Casa Concha Merlot

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2019

description (EN Store): Château Clerc Milon's 2019 vintage exemplifies the power and refinement of Pauillac. name: Aromo  Barrel Selection The Blend

A wine built for pleasure and versatility.

name: Les Vignerons du Sommierois  Les Arcades Languedoc
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-Syrah blend from Languedoc with ripe red fruit, peppery spice, and Mediterranean herbs—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Les Arcades is a classic Languedoc red built on the Rhône South tradition of Grenache and Syrah. The blend showcases ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, with supporting notes of black pepper and garrigue that speak to the region's wild herbs and limestone soils. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with grilled lamb, rustic Mediterranean dishes, or tomato-based pasta. A food-friendly expression of southern French winemaking.

name: Penfolds  Bin 149 Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cabernet from Penfolds' California program — house blending philosophy applied to Rutherford and Oakville fruit, aged in American and French oak.
description (EN Store): When Penfolds extended its multi-regional blending doctrine beyond South Australia into Napa Valley, it made a deliberate institutional statement: that winemaker intelligence, not appellation orthodoxy, defines the house. Bin 149 is the California Cabernet expression of that conviction, sourced from Napa Valley's benchland and valley-floor sites where deep alluvial gravels and well-drained loam concentrate fruit intensity while retaining structural tension through cool overnight temperatures. Fermentation follows Penfolds' established red-wine protocol — temperature-controlled in stainless steel with acclimatised yeast cultures, preserving primary fruit definition before the wine moves to oak. Maturation employs a combination of American and French oak barrels, consistent with the house's approach to Cabernet across its portfolio, integrating the cedar and dark-spice register that has defined Penfolds reds since Max Schubert's era. What distinguishes Bin 149 within its category is the explicit rejection of single-vineyard Napa fetishism: this is a blended-site wine in a region where single-block provenance commands premium positioning, applying a philosophy refined over 180 years of Australian multi-district winemaking to one of the world's most terroir-obsessed appellations. The 2020 vintage in Napa was shaped by the Glass Fire, with surviving blocks delivering concentrated, lower-yield fruit; drinking window is anticipated through the late 2030s.

name: Robert Mondavi   Woodbridge Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Napa Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for casual dining and food-friendly occasions.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Woodbridge Merlot is a medium-full bodied red wine from Napa Valley's Oakville subregion, crafted to deliver immediate appeal and versatility at the table. The wine opens with dominant plum and berry aromatics, supported by subtle vanilla notes from oak aging. On the palate, juicy red fruit and cherry flavors combine with well-integrated tannins and a soft texture, making this Merlot an excellent match for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. The herbaceous undertone adds complexity without demanding attention, positioning this wine as a reliable choice for both casual weeknight dinners and relaxed entertaining.

name: Chateau Magnol Haut Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc blend of ripe plum, cherry, and soft cocoa; medium-full body with integrated tannins.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux blend from Haut-Médoc, this wine showcases the signature elegance of Merlot-led compositions. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by subtle herbaceous undertones and hints of vanilla from careful oak aging. The palate is generous and well-structured, with soft, integrated tannins that provide backbone without harshness. Cocoa and subtle spice notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and warmth. The medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine approachable in its youth while retaining enough structure for modest cellaring.

name: Yellow Tail  Whiskey Barrel Aged Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Cabernet with ripe dark fruit and vanilla oak—food-friendly everyday red.
description (EN Store): Yellow Tail's Whiskey Barrel Aged Cabernet Sauvignon from Riverina delivers a straightforward, fruit-forward profile anchored by ripe dark plum and black cherry. Whiskey barrel aging imparts subtle vanilla and cedar notes that soften the tannin structure, making this wine accessible and versatile at the table. Medium-full body and balanced acidity position it as a reliable choice for casual entertaining and everyday meals.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Art de Vivre Languedoc AOP (Syrah, Grenache, Mourvèdre)
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Languedoc AOP — Syrah, Grenache, Mourvèdre farmed across 900+ ha of Mediterranean terroir, vinified parcel-by-parcel to the lunar calendar.
description (EN Store):
When Gérard Bertrand converted his first estate, Domaine de Cigalus, to biodynamics in 2002, he set in motion what would become the world's largest Demeter-certified biodynamic wine operation — a direct rebuke to the Languedoc's bulk-production legacy. Art de Vivre draws on that same philosophy, applied across warm Mediterranean terroir where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and allow farming with zero chemical inputs.

The Languedoc AOP designation pulls from the house's broader Mediterranean holdings, where soils shift between gravelly alluvium (Corbières-Boutenac), limestone and marl, and clay-rich subsoils — each contributing textural weight and sun-baked fruit concentration to the Syrah-Grenache-Mourvèdre core. Warm days and the region's characteristic dry heat drive phenolic ripeness, while biodynamic viticulture preserves natural soil microbiome and aromatic precision.

True to house method, each parcel is vinified separately in concrete or stainless steel vats before Gérard Bertrand personally oversees the final assemblage. Maceration in concrete builds structure without imposing oak, and all winemaking decisions — harvest timing, racking, bottling — are governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar. The result is a Mediterranean red that carries the mineral clarity of biodynamic farming at a scale no other Languedoc producer has attempted. In warmer vintages, expect concentrated dark fruit and a denser mid-palate; drink within 3–5 years for primary fruit expression.

name: Pico Maccario  Barbera d'Asti Lavignone (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Piedmont Barbera with red cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Pico Maccario's Barbera d'Asti Lavignone is a classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal, showcasing the region's tradition of bright, mineral-driven wines. The wine displays a medium-full body with lively acidity and moderate tannins, delivering red cherry and plum on the nose with subtle black pepper and herbaceous notes. A food-friendly profile with earthy undertones and licorice complexity, this Barbera excels alongside Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Pico Maccario  Tre Roveri Nizza DOCG (Barbera d'Asti Superiore) (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbera d'Asti Superiore with ripe red fruit, peppery spice, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Pico Maccario's Tre Roveri Nizza DOCG is a single-varietal Barbera d'Asti Superiore from Piedmont's prestigious Nizza subregion. This medium-full-bodied red showcases the grape's signature red cherry and plum fruit, layered with black pepper spice and subtle earthy minerality. The palate balances ripe tannins with bright acidity, revealing dried herb and licorice notes that evolve with air. A food-friendly expression of Barbera's versatility, equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or aged charcuterie.

name: Pico Maccario  Barbaresco DOCG (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco with silky tannins, rose florals, and mineral complexity—a refined Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store):

This wine displays the characteristic elegance and structure for which the region is renowned: a medium-full body supported by refined, medium-high tannins and bright acidity that frames the wine's aromatic profile.

On the nose, red cherry and rose lead, with supporting notes of licorice and tar that hint at the wine's aging potential. The palate reveals mineral tension and spice, while tertiary leather and tobacco notes suggest complexity that will develop further in the cellar. A food-friendly Barbaresco suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Pico Maccario  Barolo (750 ml)

description (EN Store): Pico Maccario's Barolo showcases the classic elegance of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious region. The wine opens with vibrant cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the grape's complexity. A medium-full body and high tannin structure provide the framework for extended cellaring, while medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food-friendliness. Tertiary notes of tar and leather emerge with age, adding depth and sophistication. name: Fattoria Mantellassi  Querciolaia Maremma Alicante

name: Casisano  Rosso di Montalcino DOC

name: Reserve St Martin  Cabernet Sauvignon
