name: Satellite  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, fresh raspberry, and silky tannins—elegant summer drinking.
description (EN Store): Satellite's Marlborough Pinot Noir delivers the region's signature elegance: bright red fruit and raspberry on the nose, with a fresh, mineral-driven palate. High acidity and silky medium tannins create a refined structure that feels weightless yet complex, with subtle earthy undertones and stone fruit depth. A versatile food wine that shines with lighter proteins, mushroom dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Cakebread  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Merlot from Cakebread's food-first house, built on the 2019 cool-season's extended hang time — structure without extraction.
description (EN Store): Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded their Rutherford estate in 1973 with a culinary philosophy baked into the winemaking itself — by 1985 a resident chef was shaping how the reds were built, not just how they were served. That food-first mandate is the reason this Merlot is calibrated for the table rather than the tasting room. Fruit is sourced from estate sites including Winery Ranch in Rutherford and Suscol Springs Ranch, where rocky, well-drained soils and Pacific-influenced airflow slow ripening and preserve natural acidity — the same conditions that keep the wine from tipping into the plush, over-extracted register common in warmer Napa Merlot. Grapes are hand-harvested at night (a practice in place since 2004) and pass through an optical berry sorter before hand-sorting, destemming, and a pre-fermentation cold soak designed to soften skins and build tannin texture without aggression; pump-over device selection is used as a precision tool to manage that texture lot by lot. What separates this from most Napa Merlot at this price is the house's deliberate restraint on extraction — four winemakers in 50+ years have held that line at 200,000-case scale, a consistency almost without parallel in California. The 2019 cool, extended growing season delivered layered complexity and structural lift; the wine is approachable now but has the architecture to develop further over the next four to six years.

name: Almaviva  2019
short_description (EN Store): Puente Alto's rocky Andean alluvium, gravity-only vinification, ~23% Carmenère, 20 months in 70–73% new French oak — the 2019 is balsamic, camphor-laced, and built for the long haul.
description (EN Store):
When Baroness Philippine de Rothschild and Eduardo Guilisasti Tagle formalized their joint venture in 1997, their founding premise was radical for Chile: one estate, one bodega, one technical team, one wine — the French Château model transplanted to the foot of the Andes. The 2019 vintage is the fullest expression of that ambition to date.

The single estate in Puente Alto sits at 620–650 m, where Andean alluvial fan deposits — loamy clay to 50 cm, then sandy loam, gravel, and rock — deliver both exceptional drainage and a naturally low-pH mineral backbone. Diurnal swings from the Cordillera preserve acidity even at 15% ABV, the warmth of 2019's dry growing season fully expressed without sacrificing tension.

Vinification is pump-free: hand-harvested fruit moves by gravity through cold maceration, then alcoholic fermentation in parcel-specific stainless steel tanks, then spontaneous malolactic directly into barrel. Aging runs 20 months in 70–73% new French oak, with basket-press wine integrated in small proportions. The 2019 blend leans into Carmenère at ~23% — the highest inclusion in recent memory — alongside approximately 68–74% Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot.

That elevated Carmenère share is the 2019's defining transgression: no Bordeaux or Napa peer can legally or historically replicate it, and it drives the vintage's signature balsamic depth and camphor lift. Only ~50% of total vineyard production was selected for this wine. Drink 2025–2042.

name: Domaine de Pellehaut  Harmonie De Gascogne Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Southwest French blend of Tannat, Syrah, Merlot & Cabernet—dark fruit, balanced tannins, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Harmonie de Gascogne Rouge is a carefully balanced Southwestern French blend that showcases the regional character of Gascony. The combination of Tannat, Syrah, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon creates a wine of medium-full body with approachable tannins and layered dark-fruit complexity. Dark plum and blackcurrant dominate the palate, supported by subtle herbaceous and spice notes that reflect the blend's diversity. The structure is elegant rather than imposing, making this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Cakebread  Vine Hill Ranch Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Vine Hill Ranch 2017 Napa Cab aged in French oak; structured vintage built for the cellar from a 50-year food-first house.
description (EN Store): Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded this Rutherford estate in 1973, and in over five decades the winery has employed only four winemakers — a continuity of house philosophy that shapes every blending and oak decision made on the 2017 Vine Hill Ranch Cabernet Sauvignon. The 2017 Napa Valley growing season delivered structured, age-worthy reds: warm days concentrated fruit while the estate's range of sub-AVA sites contributed layered complexity across the Bordeaux-dominant blend. Cakebread's food-first doctrine — embedded into winemaking since the founding generation, not retrofitted as marketing — drives a deliberate restraint in extraction: night harvesting (practiced since 2004) preserves natural acidity, and pre-fermentation cold soak softens skins to manage tannin texture before fermentation begins. Pump-over device selection is used as a precision tool to calibrate tannin grain lot-by-lot. Aging follows the Benchland Select protocol of 22 months in French oak at 60% new, building density without overwhelming the wine's structural backbone. What sets this expression apart within the Cakebread portfolio is its single-vineyard identity — Vine Hill Ranch — at a house that otherwise blends across 11 sub-appellations, making this a rare site-specific statement from a 200,000-case producer. The 2017 vintage character delivers concentrated dark fruit framed by firm tannins; drink 2024–2032.

name: Cakebread  Benchland Select Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): 22 months in 60% new French oak — Cakebread's Benchland Select pushes structure and age-worthiness beyond the house's food-first restraint.
description (EN Store):
When Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded in Rutherford in 1973, they embedded a resident chef and the American Harvest Workshop into the winery by 1985 — a culinary philosophy that has governed every winemaking decision across only four winemakers in over five decades. The Benchland Select is where that philosophy meets deliberate ambition: a Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant Bordeaux blend drawn from Napa Valley's benchland sites, where alluvial gravel-over-loam soils drain freely and moderate vine stress, concentrating fruit without sacrificing the house's signature freshness.

Grapes were hand-harvested at night (a practice in place since 2004), passed through an optical berry sorter, hand-sorted, destemmed, and given a pre-fermentation cold soak to soften skins and build tannin texture before fermentation. The wine then aged 22 months in French oak at 60% new — a notably higher new-oak commitment than the estate Napa Valley Cabernet (48% new, 17 months), signaling a deliberate step up in structural ambition within the same house style.

What separates this expression is that extended barrel regime applied to a producer philosophically opposed to extraction for its own sake — the result is oak-derived architecture in service of food compatibility, not power. The 2017 vintage delivered structured, age-worthy reds across Napa Valley, and this bottling carries a drinking window that rewards patience through the late 2020s.

name: Cakebread  Dancing Bear Ranch Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): 28 acres of volcanic Howell Mountain at 1,450–1,600 ft — Dancing Bear Ranch pushes Cakebread's food-first ethos to its most site-driven extreme.
description (EN Store): When Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded in 1973, they embedded a culinary philosophy so literally into production that a resident chef and the American Harvest Workshop followed by 1985 — winemaking decisions shaped by the table, not the trophy case. Dancing Bear Ranch is where that philosophy meets its most uncompromising terroir. Situated at 1,450–1,600 ft on Howell Mountain, the 28 planted acres sit on shallow, rocky volcanic soils with near 360-degree exposure — elevation that compresses the diurnal swing, volcanic substrate that starves vines into low-yield intensity, and aspect that delivers even sun distribution across the canopy. The result is Cabernet Sauvignon with structural density that no valley-floor site in Napa can replicate. Grapes are hand-harvested at night (a house practice since 2004), passed through an optical berry sorter, hand-sorted, destemmed, and given a pre-fermentation cold soak to calibrate tannin texture before fermentation. Aging proceeds in French oak, with pump-over selection used as a precision tannin-management tool rather than a default protocol. The special feature here is the site itself: Howell Mountain's AVA status rests on its position above the fog line, meaning Dancing Bear Ranch Cabernet develops without the maritime moderation that softens most Napa reds. The 2018 growing season delivered even ripening and firm structural backbone — expect dark-fruited density now, with the volcanic-soil grip to develop further through the late 2020s.

name: Cakebread  Bakestone Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Cab built on a food-first philosophy — night-harvested, cold-soaked, French oak aged — from a winery with only four winemakers in 50+ years.
description (EN Store): When Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded their Rutherford estate in 1973, they embedded a resident chef into the winery by 1985 — a deliberate structural choice that made culinary compatibility a winemaking constraint, not an afterthought. That philosophy still governs every red in the portfolio. Bakestone Cellars Cabernet Sauvignon draws on Cakebread's multi-site Napa Valley estate network, including hillside blocks at Suscol Springs Ranch — 110 acres of rocky, well-drained soils on a southeast-facing Napa hillside with cool Pacific-influenced airflow — conditions that slow ripening and preserve the natural acidity the house prizes above extraction. Grapes are hand-harvested at night (a practice in place since 2004), passed through an optical berry sorter, hand-sorted, destemmed, and subjected to a pre-fermentation cold soak to soften skins and build color without aggressive tannin. Pump-over device selection is used as a precision tannin-management tool lot by lot. The Napa Valley Cabernet program ages in French oak, with the house style deliberately pulling back from the maximum extraction and new-oak saturation common among Napa peers — an intentional transgression in a region where power is currency. The 2018 vintage delivered a warm, even growing season across Napa Valley, producing Cabernet with ripe dark fruit and firm but approachable structure; drinking well now with capacity to develop further through the mid-2020s.

name: Maison Castel  Grande Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon IGP Pays d'Oc
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Cabernet Sauvignon from Pays d'Oc with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Maison Castel's Grande Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Pays d'Oc delivers the classic structure and depth expected of this varietal in southern France's warm continental climate. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The wine's high tannin profile and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility, with tertiary notes of tobacco and slate emerging as the wine evolves. A versatile expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: The Noble Tread  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Structured Coonawarra Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, mint freshness, and elegant cedar complexity.
description (EN Store): The Noble Tread Cabernet Sauvignon from Coonawarra showcases the region's signature depth and structure. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the wine's core, complemented by distinctive mint and eucalyptus notes that reflect the cool Australian terroir. Secondary oak and cedar add refinement, while emerging leather and slate complexity suggest aging potential. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine demands substantial food pairing—grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses are ideal companions.

name: Trinity Reserve  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Structured Coonawarra Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, mint freshness, and firm tannins—a classic Australian expression.
description (EN Store): Trinity Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Coonawarra presents a full-bodied, classically structured red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Distinctive mint and eucalyptus notes—hallmarks of the region's cool-climate terroir—provide aromatic lift and freshness. Secondary cedar and black pepper spice complement the wine's firm, high tannin backbone, while emerging tertiary leather and slate complexity suggest cellaring potential. This is a serious, food-friendly Cabernet suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: The Mathematician  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): The Mathematician Cabernet Sauvignon from South Australia's Barossa Valley presents a classic expression of the varietal. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the core, supported by cedar and vanilla notes from oak maturation. The wine carries medium-full body with balanced acidity and medium tannin structure, making it versatile at the table. Subtle leather and earth undertones suggest modest bottle age complexity. A straightforward, well-crafted red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: The Blind Pig  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Merlot with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft tannins—food-friendly everyday red.
description (EN Store): The Blind Pig Merlot is a medium-full bodied red wine from South Eastern Australia, crafted to deliver immediate appeal and versatility at the table. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by subtle oak and vanilla notes that add depth without overwhelming the fruit. Soft, integrated tannins and balanced acidity make this wine an ideal companion for grilled meats, braised beef, roasted poultry, and rich cheese boards. A reliable, food-friendly choice for everyday enjoyment and entertaining.

name: Chapel Point  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, fresh raspberry, and silky tannins—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Chapel Point's Pinot Noir from South Eastern Australia delivers the varietal's signature elegance in a light-bodied, high-acidity frame. Bright red fruit and raspberry dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice. The silky tannin structure and fresh character make this wine approachable young, with enough complexity to reward food pairing. A versatile choice for warm-weather entertaining and lighter cuisine.

name: Of Stone + Gold  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, fresh raspberry, and earthy minerality. Silky, food-friendly, and perfect for summer.
description (EN Store):
Of Stone + Gold Pinot Noir is a refined expression of South Australia's cool-climate potential. This wine opens with vibrant red fruit and raspberry aromatics, supported by a whisper of earthy minerality that grounds the palate. The structure is characteristically silky, with high acidity and medium tannin that invite food pairing and encourage leisurely sipping.

Fresh and approachable, this Pinot Noir delivers the elegance expected of the varietal without pretension—a summer wine that tells its story with each sip.

name: From The Valley  Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, fresh acidity, and silky texture—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): From The Valley's 2023 Pinot Noir presents a classic expression of this elegant varietal, balancing bright red fruit character with fresh acidity and refined tannin structure. The wine opens with vibrant raspberry and stone fruit aromatics, supported by subtle earthy undertones that add complexity and food-friendliness. On the palate, silky tannins and crisp acidity create a refreshing mouthfeel ideal for warm-weather enjoyment and diverse table pairings. This is a wine that rewards both immediate pleasure and contemplative sipping.

name: The Saddler  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and chocolate; structured tannins and oak-driven spice.
description (EN Store): The Saddler 2018 Shiraz from Barossa Valley is a classically structured, full-bodied red wine anchored by dominant blackberry and plum fruit. Vanilla and oak from winemaking provide a creamy secondary layer, complemented by notes of chocolate and smoke. The wine's high tannin structure and medium-high acidity deliver grip and aging potential, with emerging black pepper and clove spice adding complexity. A wine suited to fine dining occasions and substantial red-meat cuisine.

name: The Bucks  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Barossa Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and chocolate; structured tannins and oak complexity.
description (EN Store): The Bucks Shiraz from Barossa Valley is a full-bodied red wine showcasing the region's signature dark fruit intensity. Dominant blackberry and dark plum notes define the primary character, supported by oak-derived vanilla and cocoa complexity. Structured tannins and medium-high acidity provide backbone, while tertiary smoke and pepper notes add savory depth. A wine built for serious food pairing and cellaring potential.

name: Distinction Point  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe dark fruit, oak spice, and smoke—a food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Distinction Point's 2018 Barossa Valley Shiraz delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by vanilla oak and cocoa notes from careful winemaking. Black pepper and smoke add complexity and structure, with medium tannins and balanced acidity that frame the wine without overwhelming. A versatile, well-crafted red suited to fine dining and everyday occasions alike.

name: Red Beast  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and spice—a food-friendly, medium-full red for fine dining.
description (EN Store): Red Beast Shiraz from Barossa Valley presents a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by oak-derived vanilla and black pepper notes. A subtle smoke character and cocoa undertone add complexity and depth. Medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity makes this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or roasted poultry.

name: Calix  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied McLaren Vale Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and mocha—soft tannins and warming spice.
description (EN Store): Calix Shiraz from McLaren Vale delivers a classic Australian expression of the varietal. Ripe blackberry and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by mocha and vanilla notes from oak aging. Black pepper spice and a soft tannin structure provide balance and approachability, making this wine equally suited to casual enjoyment or pairing with hearty cuisine.

name: The Wily Fox  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with dark fruit, spice, and smoky complexity. Bold tannins and ripe plum character.
description (EN Store): The Wily Fox Shiraz is a full-bodied red wine from South Eastern Australia, crafted from 100% Shiraz. This wine showcases the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity—ripe plum and blackberry—layered with black pepper spice and subtle smoke. Medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary notes of licorice and leather add depth and complexity. Ideal for grilled red meats, slow-cooked braises, and smoky barbecue.

name: Piccini  Collezione Oro Chianti Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Sangiovese with bright acidity, sour cherry, and dried herbs—a food-friendly Chianti Riserva for Italian classics.
description (EN Store): Piccini's Collezione Oro Chianti Riserva is a single-varietal Sangiovese from Tuscany's historic Chianti region. Medium-Full bodied with high acidity and medium-high tannins, this wine opens with dominant sour cherry and red fruit notes, supported by dried herbs and earthy undertones. Secondary tobacco and leather complexity emerge on the palate, lending structure and age-worthiness typical of Riserva classification. The mineral finish and brisk acidity make this an excellent match for tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and cured Italian charcuterie.

name: Corte Rugolin  Amarone Monte Danieli DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Amarone from Veneto's Valpolicella region: concentrated dark cherry, dried fruit, and herbaceous complexity with elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Corte Rugolin's Amarone Monte Danieli DOCG represents the traditional appassimento method of Veneto's Valpolicella region. This 2016 vintage blends Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, and local varieties into a wine of medium body and balanced tannin structure. The palate opens with concentrated black cherry and dried fruit, supported by subtle almond and herbaceous notes. Secondary spice and earth tones evolve on the mid-palate, while tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerge with air. A food-friendly Amarone suited to braised meats, aged cheeses, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Tedeschi  Marne 180 Amarone della Valpolicella
short_description (EN Store): Amarone della Valpolicella from Tedeschi: full-bodied dried-fruit intensity with cocoa and tobacco complexity. Elegant, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Tedeschi's Marne 180 Amarone della Valpolicella is a full-bodied expression of Veneto's most prestigious appellation, built on a blend of Corvina, Corvinone, Rondinella, and supporting varieties. The wine opens with concentrated dark plum and blackberry, layered with cocoa and subtle oak from careful aging. Mid-palate reveals spice and mineral structure; the finish carries tobacco and leather notes that speak to the wine's complexity and cellaring potential. A versatile pairing partner for grilled red meat, braised beef, aged cheeses, and game preparations.

name: Miguel Torres  Cordillera Vigno Carignan Red
short_description (EN Store): 100% Carignan from Chile's Curicó Valley—rustic red fruit, spice, and mineral edge. Food-friendly, medium-full body.
description (EN Store): Miguel Torres' Cordillera Vigno Carignan is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Curicó Valley, showcasing the grape's characteristic red-fruit core and earthy minerality. The wine displays medium-full body with balanced tannin and a dry, food-friendly profile. Primary notes of red cherry and blackberry are complemented by subtle spice and herbaceous undertones, with a mineral finish that reflects the vineyard terroir. Suitable for casual dining and rustic cuisine.

name: Pares Balta  Mas Elena
short_description (EN Store): Biodynamic Penedès red with red fruit, earth, and herbaceous character—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Pares Balta's Mas Elena is a biodynamically farmed red wine from Penedès, Catalunya, offering a medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity. The primary character centers on red and dark plum fruit with herbaceous undertones, complemented by earthy and spice-driven secondary notes. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Black Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic structure for red meat.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Black Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a full-bodied expression of Cabernet Sauvignon with confident structure. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cedar and oak from winemaking. The palate shows black cherry, black pepper, and tobacco notes with firm, well-integrated tannins. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Crono  Chianti Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-based Chianti Riserva with bright cherry, herbaceous notes, and structured tannins—elegant Tuscan tradition.
description (EN Store): Crono's Chianti Riserva DOCG showcases the classic profile of Sangiovese from Tuscany's heartland. Bright cherry and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by earthy undertones and fine-grained tannins. The wine's high acidity and medium-full body provide structure and food-friendliness, while tertiary leather and tobacco notes emerge with air, reflecting the complexity expected of a Riserva designation. A versatile expression suited to grilled meats, tomato-based dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Santa Helena  Gran Vino (Red Blend)
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean red blend with ripe dark fruit and balanced structure—ideal for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Santa Helena's Gran Vino is a food-friendly red blend from Chile's Colchagua Valley, crafted to deliver accessible pleasure without pretension. Ripe red and dark plum fruit dominate the palate, supported by subtle oak influence and moderate tannin that frames rather than dominates. The wine's medium-full body and balanced acidity make it a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Toro De Piedra  Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with dark fruit, cedar, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Reserve from Colchagua Valley.
description (EN Store): Toro De Piedra Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon is a medium-full bodied red wine from Chile's Colchagua Valley, built on the classic structure of 100% Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedar and black pepper spice. Oak aging contributes cocoa and subtle vanilla undertones, while bottle maturity introduces leather and tobacco complexity. Balanced acidity and medium tannin make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Toro De Piedra  Grand Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure—a classic Chilean expression.
description (EN Store):
Toro De Piedra's Grand Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from Colchagua Valley presents a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and black pepper notes emerge on the mid-palate, supported by subtle cocoa undertones from careful oak aging. Tertiary tobacco and leather character suggest modest bottle age potential and complexity beyond the primary fruit.

The wine's moderate tannin structure and balanced acidity make it versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or aged hard cheeses. A food-friendly Chilean expression suitable for both casual entertaining and more formal dining occasions.

name: Toro De Piedra  Grand Reserve Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Merlot from Colchagua Valley with ripe dark fruit, cocoa, and approachable tannins—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Toro De Piedra's Grand Reserve Merlot is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Colchagua Valley, crafted to showcase the region's capacity for structured yet approachable red wine. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark fruit character—plum and black cherry—complemented by subtle cocoa and oak notes that add depth without overwhelming the primary fruit. Moderate tannin structure and balanced acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, roasted poultry, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Sichel  AOC Bordeaux Red
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Franc with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with ripe plum and mixed berry aromatics, evolving to reveal cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes. On the palate, juicy red fruit and plum dominate, with vanilla undertones and well-integrated tannins that deliver a smooth, approachable finish. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this a versatile food wine suitable for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Clémentin de Pape  Clément Pessac-Léognan
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pessac-Léognan blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with dark fruit, cedar, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Clémentin de Pape from Château Pape Clément represents the classic Pessac-Léognan style: a balanced Bordeaux blend anchored by 60% Cabernet Sauvignon and 40% Merlot. The wine displays a medium-full body with refined tannin structure, offering primary notes of blackcurrant and dark plum alongside secondary cedar and tobacco complexity. Tertiary mineral and leather notes emerge with bottle age, reflecting the gravelly terroir of this historic Graves appellation. Food-friendly and age-worthy, this vintage suits grilled red meats, lamb preparations, and aged cheeses.

name: Bertinga  Sassi Chiusi Rosso Tuscany IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Merlot & Pecorino with cherry, herbs & leather—elegant, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Bertinga's Sassi Chiusi Rosso represents a modern Tuscan approach to the Super Tuscan category, blending Sangiovese's structural backbone with Merlot's softness and Pecorino's textural complexity. The wine displays a medium-full body with high acidity and medium-high tannins, delivering a dry, mineral-driven profile with layers of cherry, dried herbs, and leather. Suitable for both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring, this IGT offers versatility across Italian and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: KSG Life  Limited Edition Late Harvest Saperavi
short_description (EN Store): Georgian Saperavi Late Harvest: dark fruit intensity, spiced tannins, mineral complexity. Food-friendly premium red.
description (EN Store): KSG Life's Limited Edition Late Harvest Saperavi represents the signature depth of Georgia's most celebrated indigenous red varietal. Extended hang time concentrates dark plum and black cherry aromatics, layered with warming spice and structured tannins characteristic of Kakheti terroir. Secondary leather and tobacco notes emerge mid-palate, grounded by earthy minerality that speaks to the region's volcanic soils. A wine of substance and food-friendliness, suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and game.

name: Enzo Bartoli  Barolo Bussia DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Nebbiolo from Barolo's Bussia cru: dark cherry, licorice, and cedar with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Enzo Bartoli's 2016 Barolo Bussia DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious vineyard sites. The wine opens with dominant black cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous notes characteristic of the variety. Cedar and rose emerge on the palate, supported by firm, high-toned tannins and bracing acidity that define the vintage's backbone. Tertiary leather and tobacco complexity suggest this wine will evolve gracefully over the next 10–15 years, developing the mineral depth and dried-fruit character typical of age-worthy Barolo.

name: Enzo Bartoli  Barbaresco Rio Sordo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Rio Sordo cru: cherry, cedar, and licorice with age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Enzo Bartoli's 2017 Barbaresco Rio Sordo DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone. The wine opens with bright cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous notes and a distinctive cedar character. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide the framework for a wine built to evolve; tertiary tobacco and dried-fruit complexity emerge with bottle age. Ideal for grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses, or cellared for further development.

name: Vintae  La Garnacha Salvaje del Moncayo
short_description (EN Store): Wild Grenache from Aragón's Moncayo mountains—spiced red fruit with mineral grip and rustic charm.
description (EN Store): Vintae's La Garnacha Salvaje del Moncayo is a single-varietal Grenache from Spain's Aragón region, sourced from the Moncayo mountain terroir. The wine displays a medium-full body with red fruit primary notes, white pepper spice, and herbaceous undertones characteristic of high-altitude Grenache. Mineral and earthy secondary layers provide structure and food-friendly versatility. Best served with grilled lamb, Mediterranean herb-forward dishes, or Spanish charcuterie.

name: Vintae  Le Naturel D.O. Navarra
short_description (EN Store): Spanish natural red from Navarra with ripe dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Vintae Le Naturel D.O. Navarra is a natural red wine from Spain's Aragón region, crafted in the Navarra appellation. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and a medium acidity, offering ripe dark plum and red fruit on the nose, complemented by subtle spice and herbaceous notes. Earthy undertones add complexity and food-friendly versatility, making it an excellent choice for casual dining, grilled meats, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Seghesio  Sonoma Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Zinfandel with ripe dark berries, spice, and oak—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Seghesio's 2021 Sonoma Zinfandel is a classic expression of California's signature varietal, delivering ripe dark cherry and blackberry fruit with a backbone of medium-high tannin and balanced acidity. Oak aging contributes vanilla and subtle smoke, while black pepper spice and cocoa undertones add complexity. The medium-full body and approachable structure make this wine a natural partner for grilled red meat, smoked BBQ, and rustic charcuterie.

name: Seven Hills  Merlot Walla Walla Valley
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Walla Walla Merlot with ripe plum, soft cherry, and integrated tannins—elegant everyday red.
description (EN Store): Seven Hills Merlot from Walla Walla Valley presents a medium-full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe dark plum and juicy berry fruit on the nose. The palate delivers a harmonious medley of red and dark fruits—cherry, cranberry, and plum—with subtle vanilla undertones from measured oak influence. Tannins are well-integrated and soft, creating a smooth, approachable texture that finishes with gentle herbaceous notes. This 2021 vintage balances fruit-forward character with structural elegance, making it a versatile table wine suited to casual dining and everyday occasions.

name: Monfort  Pinot Nero Trentino DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir blend from Trentino with bright red fruit, mineral precision, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Monfort's Pinot Nero Trentino DOC is a refined red blend anchored by Pinot Noir, sourced from the Alpine foothills of northern Italy. The wine displays a classic profile of bright red cherry and strawberry on the nose, with subtle mineral undertones and a whisper of spice. Medium body and balanced tannin make it an approachable yet sophisticated choice for everyday dining and special occasions alike. The wine's natural acidity and clean finish complement a wide range of cuisines, from Italian pasta to roasted poultry.

name: Chateau Pichon  Longueville Baron 2017
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannin. Elegant Left Bank expression built for cellaring.
description (EN Store): Château Pichon Longueville Baron 2017 is a benchmark Pauillac expression from the Left Bank's second-tier hierarchy. The blend—78% Cabernet Sauvignon, 22% Merlot—delivers a medium-full frame anchored by blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar, black pepper, and mineral tension. Secondary oak integration introduces tobacco and leather complexity; tertiary age-worthiness suggests this vintage will evolve gracefully over 15–20 years. Food-friendly structure and classical Bordeaux architecture make it equally suited to immediate enjoyment or patient cellaring.

name: Angove  Naturalis Organic Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Certified organic Shiraz from South Australia's pioneering Angove estate—ripe dark berries, black pepper, and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Angove's Naturalis Organic Shiraz represents the winery's commitment to sustainable viticulture and premium quality. Sourced from the Riverland region and crafted under strict organic certification, this wine delivers a vibrant profile of ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit, layered with black pepper spice and subtle earthiness. The full body and medium-high tannin structure provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility, while the elegant finish reflects Angove's signature winemaking philosophy.

name: Angove  Naturalis Organic Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Organic Cabernet from Australia's Riverland: ripe dark berries, cedar, structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Angove's Naturalis Organic Cabernet Sauvignon represents the winery's commitment to certified organic viticulture and premium expression. Sourced from the Riverland region, this full-bodied red showcases the signature dark-fruit intensity and elegant structure characteristic of Angove's sustainable approach. On the palate, blackcurrant and dark plum dominate, supported by cedar and black pepper notes, with a foundation of subtle earthiness and well-integrated oak. The high tannin structure provides excellent aging potential and food-pairing versatility, making it an ideal companion to grilled red meat, lamb, and braised dishes.

name: Headline Acts  Shiraz (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Shiraz with dark plum, black pepper, and smoky spice—a bold, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Headline Acts Shiraz from South Eastern Australia delivers a full-bodied expression of this classic varietal. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper and licorice spice. Secondary smoke and leather notes add complexity and structure, while medium-high tannins provide grip and aging potential. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and smoky barbecue.

name: Bell' Agio  Chianti DOCG Fiasco
short_description (EN Store): Classic Chianti DOCG with bright cherry, plum, and earthy notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it supremely food-friendly.
description (EN Store):
Bell' Agio's Chianti DOCG exemplifies the traditional Tuscan red wine style. Crafted from Sangiovese grapes in the historic Chianti region, this wine displays a medium-full body with balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure characteristic of quality Chianti production.

On the nose, red cherry and plum dominate, supported by subtle dried herb and earthy undertones. The palate reveals a harmonious blend of fruit-forward character with gentle oak influence and mineral complexity. The wine's food-friendly profile makes it an ideal companion to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

The fiasco bottle—a traditional straw-wrapped vessel—adds authentic Tuscan charm to this approachable yet serious expression of Chianti DOCG.

name: Castello di Albola  Acciaola Toscana Red
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Sangiovese with dark fruit, cedar, and herbaceous complexity.
description (EN Store): Castello di Albola's Acciaola Toscana Red is a Super Tuscan blend anchored by 80% Cabernet Sauvignon and 20% Sangiovese, representing the modern Tuscan approach to international varietals within a traditional region. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full bodied profile with ripe blackcurrant and plum fruit, layered with cedar and herbaceous notes that reflect the Chianti Classico terroir. Medium tannins and balanced acidity support food-friendly versatility across grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Beau Reve De Tradition Bordeaux AOC
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe plum, cherry, and soft tannins.
description (EN Store): A classic Médoc expression from Maison Bouey, this Bordeaux AOC blend marries Merlot's plush dark fruit character with Cabernet Sauvignon's structure. The nose opens with ripe plum and cherry, supported by subtle vanilla notes from careful oak aging. On the palate, juicy berry medley and cranberry flavors unfold with nicely integrated tannins and herbaceous undertones, delivering a medium-full body with approachable texture. The 14% ABV provides a solid, balanced finish suitable for both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Elmfield  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable McLaren Vale Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for casual dining and grilled meats.
description (EN Store): Elmfield's McLaren Vale Merlot delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe plum and mixed berry character on the nose. The palate unfolds with juicy red fruit, cranberry, and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a clean finish. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. This is an approachable, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, roasted poultry, and tomato-based pasta.

name: MR.RIGGS  "The Gaffer" Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied McLaren Vale Shiraz with ripe blackberry, plum, and mocha—soft tannins, food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store):
The Gaffer is a full-bodied Shiraz from McLaren Vale that showcases the region's signature ripe dark-fruit character. Blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by mocha and subtle spice notes that emerge from careful winemaking. The soft tannin structure makes this wine approachable now while retaining enough depth for short-term cellaring.

A versatile choice for grilled red meat, braised beef, and smoky barbecue, this Shiraz delivers the richness and warmth expected of a premium Australian red without demanding extended aging.

name: Escorihuela Gascón Familia Gascón Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Argentine Malbec with blackberry, plum, and cocoa—elegant structure for premium red meat.
description (EN Store): Escorihuela Gascon's Familia Gascon Malbec from Mendoza's Uco Valley presents a polished expression of Argentina's signature varietal. Medium-full bodied with medium-high tannins, the wine balances ripe dark fruit with secondary oak complexity and tertiary mineral development. Blackberry and plum dominate the nose, supported by cocoa, vanilla, and subtle spice from oak aging. On the palate, the wine is round and smooth, with well-integrated structure and a mineral backbone. This is a food-friendly red suited to grilled steaks, braised beef, and lamb dishes.

name: Escorihuela Gascón Gran Reserva Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Mendoza Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body and smooth tannins.
description (EN Store): Escorihuela Gascon's Gran Reserva Malbec from Mendoza's Uco Valley showcases the depth and refinement characteristic of premium Argentine Malbec. Aged in French oak, this wine displays a harmonious balance of ripe dark fruit, subtle floral notes, and toasted oak complexity. The medium-full body and well-integrated tannins create a smooth, approachable palate suitable for both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring. Mineral undertones and earthy nuances add structural interest, making this a versatile companion to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Escorihuela Gascón DON
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Mendoza Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa; medium-full body and smooth tannins.
description (EN Store): Escorihuela Gascon's DON Malbec from Mendoza's Uco Valley showcases the region's signature dark-fruit character and structural elegance. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the nose, complemented by violet florality and cocoa notes from French oak aging. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced medium acidity support smooth, well-integrated tannins. Earthy undertones and subtle spice add complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Chateau Grand Lartigues St.Emilion Gran Cru AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar, and silky tannins.
description (EN Store): Château Grand Lartigues Saint-Émilion Grand Cru presents a classic Bordeaux expression anchored by 80% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Sauvignon. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with dominant black cherry and dark plum on the nose, supported by subtle cedar and tobacco notes from careful oak aging. The palate is structured yet approachable, with soft tannins and a mineral-tinged finish that speaks to the terroir of the Right Bank. This is a food-friendly wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and herb-forward preparations.

name: Chateau Du Mont Haut-médoc AOC
short_description (EN Store): Classic Haut-Médoc blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot; medium-full body with blackcurrant, cedar, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): A traditional Bordeaux blend from Haut-Médoc, this 2018 vintage combines 60% Cabernet Sauvignon with 40% Merlot to deliver a food-friendly red of medium-full body and balanced structure. The primary character opens with ripe blackcurrant and plum, supported by secondary cedar and tobacco notes from oak aging. Tertiary earth and mineral layers suggest modest bottle age potential. Tannins are refined and integrated, making this wine approachable in youth while offering enough structure for pairing with grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Moulin de la Bridane Saint-Julien AOC
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Julien blend: blackcurrant-driven Cabernet with plush Merlot, structured for cellaring and elegant dining.
description (EN Store):
A traditional Bordeaux blend from Saint-Julien's Left Bank terroir, this 2012 vintage showcases the region's hallmark structure and elegance. The 50% Cabernet Sauvignon foundation delivers ripe blackcurrant and dark plum, supported by 40% Merlot's plush mid-palate and 10% Petit Verdot's grip and complexity.

On the nose, cedar and oak frame the dark fruit primary; on the palate, medium tannins provide backbone without overwhelming, while earthy minerality and subtle tobacco notes emerge in the finish. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine food-friendly across a range of classic pairings.

Suitable for near-term enjoyment or modest cellaring (5–10 years), this cuvée represents solid Saint-Julien craftsmanship at an accessible price point.

name: Chateau Passe Craby Bordeaux Superieur AOC
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux Supérieur with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured.
description (EN Store): Château Passe Craby's Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically proportioned blend anchored by 90% Merlot, with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark-fruit character—plum and black cherry dominate the palate—balanced by herbaceous undertones and soft, integrated tannins. A subtle earthiness emerges on the finish, suggesting modest aging potential. This is a food-friendly expression of the appellation, suited to everyday entertaining and casual fine dining.

name: Chateau Saint-Yzans Médoc AOC
short_description (EN Store): Médoc classic: ripe plum & cherry with cedar structure. Food-friendly Merlot-Cabernet blend.
description (EN Store): Château Saint-Yzans Médoc AOC is a traditional Bordeaux red blend anchored by 60% Merlot and 40% Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering the hallmark balance and elegance of the Left Bank. Dark cherry and plum dominate the nose, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes that speak to the region's terroir. Medium-full body and approachable tannins make this wine an ideal companion to grilled beef, braised meats, and classic French cuisine. The structure suggests modest aging potential while remaining immediately approachable.

name: Chateau Franc-Maillet Le Secret de Franc Maillet Pomerol AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot & Cabernet Franc with ripe dark fruit, earthy depth, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Franc-Maillet's Le Secret de Franc Maillet is a classically proportioned Pomerol red, built on an 80% Merlot and 20% Cabernet Franc foundation. The 2020 vintage expresses the appellation's signature softness and dark-fruit generosity, with plum and black cherry at the fore, supported by subtle earth and herbaceous undertones. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine an approachable yet serious expression of the Right Bank, suited to both casual entertaining and thoughtful cellaring.

name: Memoire de Maison Bouey Pauillac AOC
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac blend of Cabernet Sauvignon & Merlot with blackcurrant, cedar & tobacco—classic Left Bank structure.
description (EN Store): A traditional Pauillac expression from Maison Bouey, this 50/50 Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend showcases the Left Bank's signature dark-fruit intensity and structural elegance. Blackcurrant and plum dominate the primary profile, supported by cedar and tobacco notes from careful oak aging. The palate reveals medium-full body with balanced tannins and a mineral-driven finish, developing leather and earthy complexity with bottle age. A food-friendly Bordeaux suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged charcuterie.

name: Bosquet des Boiron Cotes du Rhone
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Côtes du Rhône with ripe red fruit, garrigue herbs, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A classic southern Rhône blend anchored by Grenache Noir (80%), with supporting Syrah and Cinsault. The wine displays ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with garrigue, dried herbs, and black pepper spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make it an approachable yet structured expression, ideal for Mediterranean and rustic cuisine. Subtle oak integration and earthy minerality add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

name: Bosquet des Papes Chateauneuf-du-Pape Tradition Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Châteauneuf-du-Pape with ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and mineral depth. Food-friendly Rhône classic.
description (EN Store): Bosquet des Papes Châteauneuf-du-Pape Tradition Rouge is a classically structured southern Rhône blend anchored by Grenache Noir (75%), with supporting Mourvèdre, Syrah, and traditional field-blend varieties. The 2020 vintage expresses the appellation's signature ripe red and dark fruit character—cherry, raspberry, and plum—layered with garrigue herbs, black pepper, and earthy minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable in youth while retaining the structure for modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to grilled lamb, Mediterranean herb-forward dishes, and rustic braised meats.

name: Caleo Nero d'Avola Sicilia DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Caleo's Nero d'Avola from Sicily's Nero d'Avola subregion delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by dark plum and blackberry fruit. Black pepper and spice notes provide structure and aromatic lift, while earth and tobacco undertones add complexity and age-worthiness. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or tomato-based pasta.

name: Caleo Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo: smooth red fruit, earthy character, food-friendly Italian classic at accessible price.
description (EN Store): Caleo's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC is a quintessential expression of central Italy's most celebrated red varietal. The wine opens with dominant red fruit and dark plum aromatics, supported by subtle earthy undertones and gentle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this a versatile table wine suited to everyday dining and special occasions alike. The structure suggests modest aging potential, with hints of tobacco and leather emerging in the glass. A reliable, food-friendly choice for those seeking authentic Italian character without premium pricing.

name: Sartori di Verona Regolo Valpolicella Ripasso Superiore DOC
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Ripasso Superiore with dark berry depth, balanced oak, and food-friendly structure—excellent value for red-meat and cheese pairings.
description (EN Store): Sartori di Verona's Regolo Valpolicella Ripasso Superiore is a classically structured Veneto red built on Corvina Veronese, Corvinone, and Rondinella. The wine opens with a discrete but inviting nose of dark berry compote and black cherry, supported by subtle oak integration. On the palate, the wine is enveloping and well-balanced, with medium-full body and moderate tannin that frames plum and dried-fruit notes without heaviness. The ripasso technique—partial drying of grapes before fermentation—adds complexity and a savory earthiness that lingers on the finish. This is a versatile, food-friendly expression suited to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Sartori di Verona Reius Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Powerful Amarone Classico with dark cherry, plum, and oak; structured tannins and dried-fruit complexity.
description (EN Store): Sartori di Verona's Reius Amarone della Valpolicella Classico DOCG is a full-bodied red wine built on the traditional appassimento method, where grapes are dried before fermentation to concentrate flavour and alcohol. The blend of Corvina Veronese, Corvinone, Rondinella, and Cabernet yields a wine of considerable depth: dark cherry and plum dominate the nose, supported by oak and warming spice from careful barrel ageing. On the palate, medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary vanilla and oak notes frame the fruit. Tertiary tobacco, leather, and earthy undertones emerge with time, adding complexity and savouriness. This is a wine built for the table—rich enough for grilled red meat and braised dishes, yet refined enough to stand alongside aged cheeses and game.

name: Oddero  Barbera D'Alba Superiore DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Barbera with ripe cherry, plum, and balanced spice—a versatile, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Oddero's Barbera d'Alba Superiore represents the classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal. The 2020 vintage displays the characteristic red-fruit intensity and soft tannin structure that define quality Barbera, with layers of cherry and plum supported by subtle spice and earthy undertones. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine an ideal companion to a broad range of Italian and Mediterranean cuisine, from pasta dishes to grilled meats and aged cheeses.

name: Oddero  Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Langhe with cherry, licorice, and cedar—age-worthy structure for serious red meat.
description (EN Store): Oddero's 2019 Langhe Nebbiolo is a classically structured Piedmont red that showcases the varietal's signature aromatics and tannin architecture. Cherry and licorice dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the wine's terroir and careful winemaking. Medium-full body and medium-high acidity and tannin create a wine built for the table—one that demands rich, savory food and rewards patience in the glass. Dried fruit and tobacco emerge on the finish, hinting at the complexity that will develop with bottle age.

name: Chateau Graves de Pez  Saint-Estephe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe Bordeaux blend: dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins for elegant red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Château Graves de Pez is a traditional Saint-Estèphe estate producing a Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (65%) with supporting Merlot (33%) and Petit Verdot (2%). The 2020 vintage expresses the appellation's signature dark-fruit character—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar and tobacco complexity. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure suitable for cellaring and food pairing. A food-friendly classic for grilled red meat, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Pian delle Querce Rosso di Montalcino DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sangiovese from Montalcino with bright acidity, structured tannins, and earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Pian delle Querce's Rosso di Montalcino is a classically styled Sangiovese expressing the terroir of Tuscany's renowned subregion. The 2020 vintage displays vibrant red cherry and dried herb character on the nose, supported by a mineral-driven palate with firm, well-integrated tannins. High acidity provides freshness and food-friendliness, while subtle leather and earthy notes suggest modest aging potential. A versatile, food-focused red suited to Italian cuisine and Mediterranean fare.

name: Pian delle Querci Brunello di Montalcino Riserva DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello Riserva with layered cherry, herbs, and tobacco complexity. High acidity and structured tannins demand food.
description (EN Store): Pian delle Querce's 2017 Brunello di Montalcino Riserva exemplifies the precision and age-worthiness of Tuscany's most prestigious Sangiovese designation. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and dried herb aromatics, supported by secondary notes of tobacco leaf and cedar oak integration. A medium-full body carries high acidity and medium-high tannins that frame the palate with mineral tension and earthy undertones, suggesting considerable cellaring potential. The structure demands pairing with substantial dishes—grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged hard cheeses unlock the wine's layered complexity.

name: Vite Colte Dolcetto d'Alba "Roccabella" DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Dolcetto with ripe cherry, plum, and mineral-driven spice. Medium-full body, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Vite Colte's Dolcetto d'Alba "Roccabella" is a classically styled expression of Piedmont's most approachable red varietal. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle spice and earthy minerality. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with tomato-based pasta, roasted chicken, or aged cured meats. The emerging tertiary leather and tobacco notes suggest modest aging potential and reward patient cellaring.

name: Vite Colte Barbera d'Asti "San Nicolao" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbera d'Asti DOCG with bright cherry, plum, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Vite Colte's San Nicolao represents the classic expression of Barbera d'Asti, a cornerstone red from Piedmont's rolling hills. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and subtle earthy undertones that speak to the region's terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine remarkably versatile at the table, equally at home with tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, or cured charcuterie. The bright acidity cuts through rich dishes while the wine's inherent elegance prevents it from overwhelming lighter preparations.

name: Vite Colte Barolo del Comune di Serralunga d'Alba "Essenze" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Age-worthy Barolo from Serralunga d'Alba with structured tannins, cherry, licorice, and cedar complexity.
description (EN Store): Vite Colte's Barolo del Comune di Serralunga d'Alba "Essenze" is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Barolo's most prestigious communes. The 2015 vintage displays the hallmark power and elegance of the region: dark cherry and licorice on the nose, layered with cedar and herbaceous notes, supported by high acidity and firm tannins characteristic of age-worthy Barolo. Tertiary notes of tar, leather, and dried fruit emerge, signaling the wine's capacity for graceful evolution in bottle. A serious, traditional expression suited to patient collectors and those seeking Piedmontese complexity.

name: Clay Creek Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied California Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—classic American elegance.
description (EN Store): Clay Creek Cabernet Sauvignon is a full-bodied California red showcasing the varietal's signature blackcurrant and dark plum character. Cedar and oak frame the primary fruit, while high tannins provide structure and aging potential. Secondary notes of black pepper and vanilla complement the wine's complexity, with tertiary tobacco and leather emerging on the finish. A versatile pairing for grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Clay Creek Old Vine Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Ripe, spicy California Zinfandel with dark berry intensity and rustic charm—built for grilled meat and bold flavors.
description (EN Store): Clay Creek Old Vine Zinfandel is a classic California expression of the varietal, delivering ripe blackberry and plum fruit with a signature black pepper spice. Medium-full bodied with firm, ripe tannins, the wine shows subtle oak influence and earthy undertones that speak to mature vineyard character. A food-friendly, unpretentious red suited to grilled steaks, BBQ, and hearty braises.

name: Bindi Surgadi Sur Gardo Chianti Classico DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chianti Classico with bright cherry, dried herbs, and structured tannins—a versatile Tuscan red for food.
description (EN Store): A classically styled Sangiovese from Chianti Classico, this 2021 vintage expresses the region's hallmark balance of ripe cherry fruit, herbaceous minerality, and refined tannin structure. The wine opens with bright red-fruit aromatics and a core of dried cherry, supported by earthy undertones and subtle leather complexity. High acidity and medium-high tannins provide excellent food compatibility and aging potential, making this a versatile choice for Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and aged charcuterie.

name: Castello Monaci  Piluna Primitivo Salento IGT
short_description (EN Store): Bold Primitivo from Salento with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Castello Monaci's Piluna Primitivo showcases the ripe, sun-driven character of Salento's Primitivo grape. A bold nose leads to a palate filled with dark cherry, raspberry, and subtle smoke, framed by fully rounded tannins and a touch of black pepper. Medium-full bodied and smooth on the finish, this wine delivers approachable complexity without pretension—ideal for pizza, pasta, and grilled fare.

name: Blason d'Issan  Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Margaux blend of Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon & Petit Verdot with red fruit, cedar, and refined structure.
description (EN Store): Blason d'Issan is the second wine of Château d'Issan, a historic Margaux estate. This 2021 blend of 52% Merlot, 46% Cabernet Sauvignon, and 2% Petit Verdot expresses the elegance and balance characteristic of the appellation. Medium-full body with measured tannins frames red currant, plum, and blackcurrant on the palate, complemented by subtle cedar and graphite minerality. The wine shows food-friendly structure suitable for classic Bordeaux pairings—grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses—while remaining approachable in its youth.

name: Tarapaca  Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Maipo Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a classic Chilean expression.
description (EN Store): Tarapaca's Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers the hallmark profile of a serious, age-worthy red. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by pronounced cedar and oak spice from careful barrel aging. name: Chateau La Dame de Montrose  Saint-Estephe
short_description (EN Store): Second label of a 2nd Growth Saint-Estèphe: deep-gravel croupe soils, 12 months in 30% new French oak, Cabernet-driven structure at an accessible entry point.
description (EN Store): Étienne Théodore Dumoulin planted this gravelly croupe in 1815 — the youngest estate to earn Second Growth status in the 1855 classification, achieving it just four decades after its first vintage. La Dame de Montrose is the second selection from that same 95-hectare, undivided single-block vineyard in the south-eastern corner of Saint-Estèphe, roughly 800 metres from the Gironde estuary. Soils here are deep coarse gravel — Pyrenean and Massif Central origin, exceeding two metres in depth — over clay-marl subsoil, with a south-easterly aspect; the estuary's thermal mass suppresses both spring frost and summer heat spikes, preserving acidity and extending hang time. Parcel-by-parcel vinification in temperature-controlled vats feeds a gravity-flow winery installed during the Bouygues family's €20 million renovation (2007–2013). Aging runs 12 months in French oak barriques at 30% new wood — half the new-oak exposure of the Grand Vin's 60% — deliberately preserving fruit definition over toasty overlay. The 2017 vintage in Saint-Estèphe delivered moderate yields after a dry summer, producing wines with firm tannic architecture and good natural acidity. What distinguishes La Dame within its peer set is the source material itself: this is not a declassified négociant blend but a parcel selection from one of Bordeaux's most structurally serious estates, carrying the estate's graphite-mineral backbone at a fraction of the Grand Vin's price.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2015 (Premiers Crus)
short_description (EN Store): Plateau de Mouton's 5m-deep Günzian gravel, 44 oak vats, 100% new French oak — the only First Growth ever reclassified in 1855.
description (EN Store):
In 1924, Baron Philippe de Rothschild made a decision that would reshape all of Bordeaux: he began château-bottling his own wine, a practice then considered radical and now universal across the Médoc. That same instinct for deliberate transgression culminated in 1973 when Mouton became the sole estate ever reclassified under the 1855 system — promoted from Second to First Growth by ministerial decree, prompting the motto: Premier je suis, second je fus, Mouton ne change.

The Plateau de Mouton sits at 27 m elevation in northern Pauillac, its soils composed of Günzian gravel descending over 5 metres onto clay-limestone and iron-rich marl. That extraordinary depth drains aggressively while the gravel mass retains heat, accelerating phenolic ripeness in Cabernet Sauvignon and concentrating the dark-fruit density that defines Mouton's hedonistic register.

Grapes are hand-harvested and gravity-fed into 44 oak fermentation vats — one of the last such regimes in the Médoc — enabling parcel-by-parcel vinification with minimal mechanical stress. Malolactic fermentation occurs in tank; the wine then ages 19–22 months in 100% new French oak barriques (medium toast), first in the 100-metre Grand Chai designed by Charles Siclis in 1926. Fined with egg white before bottling. Only the finest parcels — at most 50% of estate production — qualify as Grand Vin.

The 2015 vintage is not individually documented in the château's published notes, but falls between the structured 2014 and the opulent 2016; Petit Verdot was absent from blends through 2015, making this a Cabernet Sauvignon–Merlot–Cabernet Franc construction. Approach with patience; the 2016 drinks from 2026, suggesting 2015 rewards cellaring through the late 2020s at minimum.

name: Duckhorn  Canvasback "Red Mountain" Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Washington Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins. Classic Red Mountain power.
description (EN Store): Duckhorn's Canvasback Cabernet Sauvignon from Red Mountain showcases the region's signature depth and structure. Dominant blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit form the primary character, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. Tobacco and leather notes emerge on the palate, adding tertiary complexity and age-worthiness. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine demands substantial food pairings—grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged hard cheeses. The 2019 vintage captures Red Mountain's ability to produce powerful, well-integrated Cabernet Sauvignon with both immediate appeal and cellaring potential.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Napa Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Duckhorn's Decoy Pinot Noir from Napa Valley presents a classic expression of the varietal: bright red fruit and raspberry on the nose, with a silky palate supported by medium tannins and vibrant acidity. Subtle earthy undertones add complexity without overwhelming the wine's primary fruit-forward character. The 2022 vintage captures the freshness and elegance Pinot Noir is prized for, making it an approachable yet refined choice for both casual enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Tenuta dell'Ornellaia  Ornellaia Bolgheri DOC 2021
short_description (EN Store): Lodovico Antinori's coastal rival to Sassicaia: 99 ha across marine-alluvial Bolgheri soils, 18 months in 70% new French oak barriques.
description (EN Store): Ornellaia was born from a family rivalry — Marchese Lodovico Antinori founded the estate in 1981 in deliberate competition with his cousin's Sassicaia and his brother Piero's Solaia, making Bolgheri's Super Tuscan triumvirate a single extended family's argument about greatness. That founding ambition is still encoded in every production decision. The estate's 99 ha divide between two distinct blocks: the original 41 ha surrounding the winery on denser clay soils, and the 58 ha Bellaria property to the north on stonier, more alluvial ground — the latter delivering the saline freshness and vibrant lift that separates Ornellaia's profile from more inland Bordeaux-style blends. Tyrrhenian Sea breezes moderate summer heat across both sites at 50–120 m elevation, preserving acidity through full phenolic ripeness. Each variety and individual block is vinified separately, fermented in stainless steel and concrete below 25°C, then macerated 10–15 days total. Malolactic fermentation occurs in barrel — 70% new French oak barriques, 30% once-used — where the wine ages 18 months (12 as individual lots, 6 post-assemblage), followed by a minimum 12 months bottle age before release. What no peer in Bolgheri replicates: the annual Vendemmia d'Artista project, where a commissioned international artist interprets a single winemaker-chosen word for each vintage, with proceeds to the Guggenheim Foundation. The 2021 vintage note is not yet confirmed in the brand library; the wine's structure — built on marine-alluvial complexity and extended oak integration — rewards cellaring of 10–15 years from vintage.

name: Sena  2020
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Aconcagua blend of Cabernet, Malbec, Carmenere & Petit Verdot with dark fruit, spice & structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Seña 2020 is a sophisticated Aconcagua Valley blend that marries the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon with the richness of Malbec, the distinctive character of Carmenere, and the fine-grained tannins of Petit Verdot. The wine displays a compelling core of dark plum and blackcurrant fruit, layered with cedar, black pepper, and cocoa notes that speak to careful winemaking. Structured tannins and balanced acidity provide the framework for a wine built for both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring potential. Food-friendly and expressive of its terroir.

name: Juris  Golser St.Laurent
short_description (EN Store): Austrian St. Laurent with ripe red fruit, black pepper spice, and food-friendly medium tannin—elegant Central European red.
description (EN Store): Juris St. Laurent from Neusiedlersee showcases the elegant, food-friendly character of Austria's signature red varietal. Ripe red cherry and dark plum dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous undertones that reflect the region's continental climate. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised beef, or roasted poultry. A mineral earthiness emerges on the finish, adding complexity and length.

name: Brendel  "Cooper's Reed" Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins. Classic California elegance.
description (EN Store): Brendel's 2019 Cooper's Reed Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley exemplifies the region's signature style: a full-bodied red wine with commanding presence and refined structure. Blackcurrant and dark cherry dominate the primary aromatics, supported by cedar and oak-derived spice from careful winemaking. The palate reveals layered complexity—tobacco and leather notes emerge as the wine opens, grounded by high, well-integrated tannins that promise cellaring potential. Ideal for serious red-wine enthusiasts seeking a classic Napa expression with depth and aging grace.

name: Dana Estates  Vaso Cabernet Sauvignon Napa Valley
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins; classic California elegance.
description (EN Store): Dana Estates' 2019 Vaso Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley exemplifies the region's signature style: a full-bodied red wine built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while high tannins provide structure and aging potential. Secondary notes of black pepper and spice add complexity, with tertiary tobacco and leather developing as the wine matures. This is a wine for serious red-meat cuisine and contemplative cellaring.

name: Durant & Booth  Bourbon Barrell Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Rutherford Cabernet with bourbon barrel complexity: blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins for serious collectors.
description (EN Store): This 2020 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon from Durant & Booth showcases the power and elegance of Rutherford terroir, enhanced by bourbon barrel aging. The wine opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry, layered with cedary oak and subtle vanilla notes that speak to careful wood management. A full body and high tannin structure provide the backbone for extended cellaring, while secondary tobacco and leather notes add complexity and sophistication. Ideal for those seeking a classically styled Cabernet with depth and aging potential.

name: Napa Cut  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): St. Helena Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins—classic Napa power.
description (EN Store): Napa Cut's 2019 Cabernet Sauvignon from St. Helena showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and oak-driven complexity. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by cedar and graphite minerality. On the palate, firm tannins and balanced acidity frame layers of cassis, vanilla oak, and subtle tobacco, building toward a long, structured finish. A full-bodied expression suited to cellar aging or immediate enjoyment with substantial fare.

name: 1881 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied 2019 Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): The 1881 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon 2019 is a classically structured red wine from California's premier growing region. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The wine's full body and high tannin structure suggest careful aging potential and food-friendly versatility with grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses. Cocoa and tobacco notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and length.

name: Black Stallion Estate  Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic California elegance.
description (EN Store): Black Stallion Estate's 2021 Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon delivers the hallmark richness and structure expected from the region's premier growing conditions. Secondary tobacco and leather notes emerge on the finish, suggesting cellaring potential and food-friendly versatility. A classic expression of Napa Cabernet craftsmanship.

name: Joseph Phelps  "Freestone Vineyard" Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Napa Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile table wine for poultry and lighter fare.
description (EN Store): Joseph Phelps' Freestone Vineyard Pinot Noir is a refined expression of California's premier cool-climate terroir. The 2019 vintage captures the freshness and balance characteristic of quality Napa Pinot Noir, making it equally at home at the dinner table or as an aperitif.

name: Joel Gott  815 California Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—classic California elegance.
