Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with time, adding complexity and sophistication to an already compelling profile.

name: Joel Gott  Willamette Valley Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Willamette Valley Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Joel Gott's Willamette Valley Pinot Noir delivers the hallmark elegance of Oregon's premier region. Bright red fruit and raspberry lead the palate, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a silky, refined texture. The wine's medium body and vibrant acidity make it approachable yet complex enough for food pairing, with a fresh character that evolves gracefully from the first sip. Ideal for warm-weather entertaining and a range of lighter proteins.

name: Far Niente  "Post & Beam" Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Napa Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar structure, and polished tannins—elegant power for the cellar.
description (EN Store): Far Niente's Post & Beam Cabernet Sauvignon from Napa Valley showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and architectural tannin structure. The palate is full-bodied and well-defined, with high tannins that frame the wine's complexity without overwhelming its fruit-forward character. This is a classically styled Napa Cabernet built for both near-term enjoyment and medium-term cellaring.

name: Belle Glos " Eulenloch Vineyard" Napa Valley Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Napa Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, silky tannins, and fresh acidity—a versatile summer wine.
description (EN Store): Belle Glos's Eulenloch Vineyard Pinot Noir showcases the classic elegance of Napa Valley terroir. name: Belle Glos  "Las Alturas Vineyard" Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant California Pinot Noir with vibrant red fruit, silky tannins, and refreshing acidity—a versatile summer wine.

name: Banrock Station  Cabernet Merlot

description (EN Store): Banrock Station's Cabernet Merlot from Australia's Riverland region delivers a medium-full bodied red with immediate appeal. name: Richland  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—built for grilled red meat.

name: Richland  Shiraz

description (EN Store): Richland Shiraz from Riverina showcases the hallmark richness of Australian Syrah. A full-bodied red with medium-high tannins, it delivers dominant blackberry and dark plum fruit on the palate, layered with black pepper spice and subtle smoke. Earthy undertones and emerging leather notes add complexity and structure, making this wine a natural partner for grilled red meat, lamb, and slow-cooked braises. Approachable yet age-worthy, it represents solid commercial quality from a consistent Australian producer.

name: Richland  Merlot
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Merlot with ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—ideal for casual red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Richland Merlot from Australia's Riverina region delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine centred on ripe plum and berry fruit. The palate shows juicy red-fruit character with hints of cranberry and subtle herbaceous notes, supported by well-integrated tannins and a soft, approachable finish. Vanilla undertones from winemaking add roundness without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. A versatile everyday red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Belena  Shiraz

description (EN Store): Belena Shiraz from South Australia delivers a full-bodied expression of the varietal, built on a foundation of ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit. Black pepper and spice notes provide textural complexity, while secondary smoke and leather undertones add savory dimension. Medium-high tannins frame the wine with structure suitable for grilled meats, braised beef, and lamb preparations. A versatile, food-friendly red with approachable ripeness and enough complexity for casual entertaining.

name: Abbotts & Delaunay  Cabernet Sauvignon Pay D'Oc
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Languedoc Cabernet with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic French red for serious meat dishes.
description (EN Store): Abbotts & Delaunay's Cabernet Sauvignon from Languedoc-Roussillon delivers a classic expression of the varietal in southern France. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by pronounced cedar and oak spice from careful winemaking. A full body and high tannin structure provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility. Subtle tobacco and earth notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and tertiary depth. This is a serious, well-structured red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Louis Jadot  Morey Saint Denis 1er CRU Clos
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and bright acidity—a refined expression of Morey-Saint-Denis terroir.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's 2018 Morey-Saint-Denis 1er Cru Clos is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir that showcases the elegance and complexity of the Côte de Nuits. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry, supported by subtle earthy undertones and mushroom-driven secondary characteristics. A silky tannin structure and bright acidity create a refined mouthfeel, while mineral notes and hints of forest floor add depth and sophistication. This is a wine built for food pairing and medium-term cellaring, offering both immediate pleasure and the potential to evolve gracefully over the next 5–8 years.

name: Louis Jadot  Bourgogne Couvent Des Jacobins Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with bright cherry, raspberry, and earthy minerality. Silky, food-friendly, and quintessentially Burgundian.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Bourgogne Couvent des Jacobins Rouge is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir that exemplifies the region's hallmark elegance. The 2022 vintage displays vibrant red fruit—cherry and raspberry—layered with subtle earthy and mushroom undertones characteristic of fine Pinot Noir. High acidity and silky medium tannins create a refined, balanced palate that invites food pairing across a broad spectrum: from roasted poultry and creamy risottos to grilled fish and mushroom-forward dishes. The wine's freshness and mineral complexity suggest careful winemaking that respects the fruit's natural expression.

name: Louis Jadot  Coteaux Bourguignons Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with bright cherry, raspberry, and earthy minerality. Silky, food-friendly, and refreshingly crisp.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Coteaux Bourguignons Rouge is a classic expression of Burgundian Pinot Noir, showcasing the region's hallmark elegance and restraint. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry aromatics, supported by subtle earthy and mushroom undertones that speak to the terroir. On the palate, medium body and silky tannins create a refined texture, while high acidity provides freshness and structure. The minerality adds complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character, making this wine exceptionally versatile at the table.

name: Louis Jadot  Vosne Romanee
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with bright cherry, raspberry, and earthy minerality; silky tannins and vibrant acidity.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's 2018 Vosne Romanée is a refined expression of Burgundy's Côte de Nuits, showcasing the classic elegance of Pinot Noir from this prestigious subregion. The wine opens with bright red fruit—cherry and raspberry—layered with subtle earthy and mushroom notes that speak to the terroir's complexity. Medium body and silky tannins provide structure without heaviness, while high acidity ensures freshness and food-friendliness. Minerality and hints of forest floor emerge on the finish, adding depth and sophistication. This is a wine that rewards both immediate enjoyment and careful contemplation.

name: Chateau Meyre  M De Haut Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Haut-Médoc blend with ripe plum, cherry, and cedar—food-friendly structure for red meat and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Château Meyre's Haut-Médoc blend marries 55% Merlot with 45% Cabernet Sauvignon to deliver a medium-full-bodied red with classic Bordeaux character. Ripe black cherry and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous undertones. The palate shows balanced tannin and acidity, making this wine an approachable yet structured choice for everyday entertaining and food pairing. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb preparations.

name: Castelli del Grevepesa  UN-IO BIO Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Organic Sangiovese-Merlot blend from Montepulciano with elegant red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store):
Castelli del Grevepesa UN-IO BIO is an organically farmed Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG from Tuscany's prestigious Montepulciano subregion. The 2017 vintage blends 95% Sangiovese with 5% Merlot, yielding a medium-full-bodied red with balanced tannin and medium acidity.


On the nose, ripe red cherry and dark plum lead, followed by dried herb and earthy minerality. The palate reveals subtle spice and leather undertones, with tertiary tobacco and slate complexity emerging. This is a classically structured Tuscan red suited to both casual dining and cellar aging.


Ideal with tomato-based pasta, grilled steak, braised beef, aged Tuscan cheeses, and mushroom risotto.

name: Chateau de G  Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon D.O.
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—a food-friendly Gran Reserva for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Chateau de G Gran Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley presents a classic expression of the varietal. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by cedar and black pepper notes that suggest careful oak aging. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannin structure deliver a wine suited to grilled steaks, braised beef, and lamb preparations. Tertiary tobacco and earth notes add complexity and food-pairing versatility, making this a reliable choice for both casual dining and cheese boards featuring aged hard varieties.

name: Maturana Wines  Reserva Privada Carmenere
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Carmenere from Colchagua Valley with dark fruit, spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Maturana Wines Reserva Privada Carmenere is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Colchagua Valley, showcasing the signature character of this distinctive grape. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose, complemented by herbaceous undertones and subtle spice. A versatile red suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Agua Santa  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure—a versatile Chilean red.
description (EN Store): Agua Santa's Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and herbaceous notes provide secondary complexity, while balanced medium tannins and acidity create a food-friendly profile suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses. A reliable, well-structured expression of the varietal.

name: Massolino  Barbaresco DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco with ripe cherry, licorice, and mineral structure. Age-worthy with fine tannins.
description (EN Store): Massolino's Barbaresco DOCG showcases the classic elegance of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's prestigious Barbaresco zone. The 2021 vintage displays a refined profile of dark cherry and licorice on the nose, complemented by herbaceous notes and subtle cedar complexity. The palate reveals the hallmark high acidity and structured tannins characteristic of quality Barbaresco, with layers of tar and smoke that evolve toward dried fruit and tobacco in the finish. This is a wine built for cellaring, with the structure and depth to develop tertiary complexity over the next decade.

name: Framingham  Nobody's Hero Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): Marlborough Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Framingham's Nobody's Hero Pinot Noir from Marlborough's Wairau Valley presents a classic expression of New Zealand's signature varietal. Red cherry and plum dominate the palate, supported by subtle strawberry notes and a mineral earthiness that grounds the wine's mid-weight frame. The silky tannin structure and balanced acidity make this wine an approachable yet sophisticated choice for everyday dining and special occasions alike.

name: Chateau Pericou  AOP Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft tannins. Medium-full body with cocoa undertones and integrated oak.
description (EN Store): Château Pericou's AOP Bordeaux Rouge is a classically structured blend of Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a harmonious expression of the Right Bank tradition. The 2022 vintage opens with dominant ripe plum and black cherry aromatics, supported by a juicy berry medley on the palate. Soft, well-integrated tannins provide structure without harshness, while secondary notes of vanilla and cocoa add depth and roundness. A subtle herbaceous character and hints of cedar suggest careful oak aging, with emerging earth and tobacco notes pointing toward graceful evolution. The medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine approachable now while retaining cellaring potential.

name: Chateau de la Vieille Tour  Bordeaux Supérieur AOP
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux Supérieur blend of ripe plum, black cherry, and cedar—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Château de la Vieille Tour's 2020 Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured red blend anchored by Merlot (60%), with supporting Cabernet Sauvignon (30%) and Cabernet Franc (10%). The wine opens with dominant dark cherry and plum fruit, layered with subtle cassis and herbaceous notes that speak to the Cabernet components. Mid-palate tannins are soft and integrated, supporting a medium-full body without heaviness. Cedar and spice emerge on the finish, with earthy undertones suggesting modest aging potential. A food-friendly expression of the appellation, suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Vinum Terra  Bordeaux AOP (No Sulfites)
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux blend with ripe plum, cassis, and herbaceous notes—food-friendly, no added sulfites.
description (EN Store): A sulfite-free Bordeaux AOP from Vinum Terra, built on a Merlot foundation (60%) with Cabernet Sauvignon (30%) and Cabernet Franc (10%). The 2021 vintage expresses ripe red and dark plum fruit with cassis undertones, framed by herbaceous minerality and subtle oak influence. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or tomato-based preparations. The absence of added sulfites appeals to natural-wine enthusiasts seeking a classically styled Bordeaux blend.

name: Reserve Tradition du Chateau de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP
short_description (EN Store): Classic Bordeaux Supérieur blend of dark cherry, plum, and cedar—food-friendly structure for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Chateau de la Vieille Tour's Reserve Tradition du Chateau de la Vieille Tour is a traditional Bordeaux Supérieur blend anchored by Merlot (55%), with Cabernet Sauvignon (30%) and Cabernet Franc (15%) adding structure and complexity. The 2019 vintage expresses ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, with supporting notes of cedar and subtle oak from careful aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining enough backbone for short-term cellaring. Earthy undertones emerge on the finish, suggesting food-friendly versatility across grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Olek Bondonio  Barbaresco Roncagliette DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barbaresco's Roncagliette vineyard; silky tannins, rose and cherry, mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Olek Bondonio's Barbaresco Roncagliette DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious appellations. The 2020 vintage expresses the varietal's signature elegance: bright red cherry and floral rose on the nose, layered with subtle tar and licorice notes from the wine's natural phenolic ripeness. Medium-full body and refined tannin structure support a mineral, slightly austere palate that rewards patient cellaring. The wine's tertiary complexity—leather, tobacco, dried fruit—emerges with age, making it an ideal companion to grilled lamb, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Olek Bondonio  Barbera D’alba DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbera d'Alba from Piedmont: bright cherry, soft tannin, and mineral depth. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Olek Bondonio's Barbera d'Alba DOC 2022 is a classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle spice and mineral undertones. Medium-full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, or mushroom risotto. The soft tannin structure and food-friendly profile reflect Barbera's reputation as one of Italy's most approachable fine reds.

name: Olek Bondonio  Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Langhe Nebbiolo with rose, tar, and licorice—a classic Piedmont expression of structure and finesse.
description (EN Store): Olek Bondonio's Langhe Nebbiolo DOC is a single-varietal expression rooted in Piedmont's prestigious terroir. The 2022 vintage showcases the hallmark character of Nebbiolo: a medium-full frame built on medium-high tannins and acidity, delivering red cherry and rose on the nose, with underlying tar and licorice complexity. Secondary notes of leather and tobacco emerge as the wine opens, reflecting both the grape's natural phenolic richness and careful winemaking. Food-friendly yet age-worthy, this wine bridges casual dining and serious cellaring.

name: Olek Bondonio  Barbera D’asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Piedmont Barbera with ripe red fruit, balanced tannin, and food-friendly structure. Elegant, approachable Italian classic.
description (EN Store):
Olek Bondonio's Barbera d'Asti DOCG represents the classic expression of Piedmont's most versatile red varietal. The 2022 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied wine with prominent red fruit character—cherry and plum—supported by subtle spice and a whisper of earth. The tannin structure is refined and balanced, neither aggressive nor soft, making this wine an ideal companion to a broad range of Italian and Mediterranean cuisine.


Barbera's natural acidity and food-friendly profile shine in this bottling, which carries the DOCG designation reflecting strict regional production standards. The wine shows immediate appeal on release while possessing enough structure to develop gracefully over the next 3–5 years.

name: Olek Bondonio  Vino Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Barbaresco Nebbiolo with elegant red fruit, tar, and tobacco—a food-friendly Piedmont classic.
description (EN Store): Olek Bondonio's Vino Rosso from Barbaresco showcases the hallmark character of Nebbiolo: ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, layered with tar and licorice complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerging on the palate. A versatile expression suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Chateau D'Armailhac  2021
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with structured tannins. Age-worthy Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Château d'Armailhac 2021 is a quintessential Pauillac expression, built on a foundation of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by pronounced cedar and tobacco notes that speak to careful oak aging. A mineral, graphite-tinged palate and firm, well-integrated tannins provide structure and aging potential. The tertiary leather and slate complexity suggest this wine will evolve gracefully over the next 15–20 years, developing additional nuance and refinement.

name: Chateau D'Armailhac  2020
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: Cabernet-led blend with blackcurrant depth, cedar structure, and food-friendly tannin.
description (EN Store): Château d'Armailhac is a fifth-growth Pauillac estate producing a traditional Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (59%), with supporting Merlot (30%), Cabernet Franc (8%), and Petit Verdot (3%). The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied red with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, framed by secondary cedar and tobacco notes. Medium-high tannins provide structure without harshness, making this wine approachable in youth while retaining aging potential. Minerality and subtle leather emerge on the finish, characteristic of Left Bank Pauillac terroir.

name: Daou  Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Cabernet from Adelaida with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Elegant California power.
description (EN Store): Daou Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon from California's Adelaida District presents a classically structured red wine with full body and commanding tannin presence. The primary expression centers on ripe blackcurrant and dark cherry fruit, complemented by secondary cedar and oak spice notes that reflect careful winemaking. As the wine evolves, subtle tobacco and leather undertones emerge, adding complexity and age-worthiness. The high acidity and firm tannin backbone suggest this wine will develop gracefully over time, rewarding patient cellaring while remaining approachable in its youth for those who prefer bold, fruit-forward California Cabernet.

name: Daou Cabernet Sauvignon Paso Robles
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Paso Robles Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Classic California power.
description (EN Store): Daou's Cabernet Sauvignon from Paso Robles' Adelaida District delivers the hallmark richness and structure expected from California's premier red wine region. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by pronounced cedar and oak spice from careful winemaking. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility. A full-bodied expression suited to grilled red meats, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Pingorote  Tempranillo (Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Vegan Tempranillo from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and oak—a food-friendly red for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Pingorote's Tempranillo from La Mancha delivers a classic Spanish red wine profile: ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, supported by subtle oak and leather notes that speak to careful winemaking. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured enough for grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cured meats. Vegan-certified, it offers versatility for conscientious wine lovers seeking quality without compromise.

name: Granduc  Tempranillo Crianza
short_description (EN Store): Tempranillo Crianza from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and oak—a food-friendly Spanish red.
description (EN Store): Granduc's Tempranillo Crianza represents the approachable, well-balanced character of La Mancha reds. Aged in oak to develop secondary complexity, this wine opens with ripe red cherry and plum, supported by leather and subtle spice notes. The medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make it versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or cured charcuterie. A reliable, everyday Spanish red with enough depth for casual entertaining.

name: Pingorote  Tempranillo Crianza
short_description (EN Store): Tempranillo Crianza from La Mancha with ripe cherry, oak aging, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Spanish red.
description (EN Store): Pingorote's Tempranillo Crianza represents the approachable side of La Mancha winemaking. Aged in oak to develop secondary complexity, this medium-full-bodied red opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, followed by cedar and spice notes from barrel maturation. A subtle leather and tobacco character emerges on the finish, grounding the wine in classic Spanish terroir. The balanced tannin structure and moderate acidity make it versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Granduc  Tempranillo Reserva
short_description (EN Store): La Mancha Tempranillo Reserva with ripe cherry, oak structure, and leather complexity—a food-friendly Spanish red.
description (EN Store): Granduc's Tempranillo Reserva from La Mancha presents a classic expression of Spain's signature red varietal. Ripe red cherry and plum dominate the nose, supported by oak aging that adds vanilla and subtle spice. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannin frame a dry, structured finish with emerging leather and tobacco notes—hallmarks of Tempranillo's tertiary evolution. This is a versatile, well-made wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Pingorote  Tempranillo Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Tempranillo Reserva from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and oak complexity—a food-friendly Spanish red.
description (EN Store): Pingorote's Tempranillo Reserva from La Mancha delivers a classic expression of Spain's signature red varietal. Ripe red cherry and plum dominate the palate, supported by well-integrated oak and cedar notes that speak to careful aging. A subtle leather and tobacco undertone emerges on the finish, adding tertiary complexity and food-pairing versatility. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable yet structured enough for grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Clearly Organic  Tempranillo (Organic & Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Tempranillo from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and oak—a vegan-friendly Spanish red for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Clearly Organic's Tempranillo represents a thoughtful approach to sustainable winemaking in Spain's La Mancha region. This organic and vegan-certified red showcases the classic character of the Tempranillo grape: ripe red cherry and plum on the nose, supported by subtle oak and spice from careful winemaking. The palate reveals medium-full body with balanced tannins and a lingering finish touched by leather and earth—hallmarks of Tempranillo's age-worthiness. Ideal for pairing with grilled meats, Spanish tapas, or tomato-based dishes.

name: Bohem  Tempranillo & Garnacha Blend (Organic & Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Balanced Tempranillo-Garnacha blend from La Mancha; organic, vegan, food-friendly red with cherry, plum & spice.
description (EN Store): A well-structured red blend from Spain's La Mancha region, combining equal parts Tempranillo and Garnacha in an organic and vegan format. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by subtle herbaceous notes and warming spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make it approachable yet complex enough for food pairing. Earthy undertones add depth, while the finish remains clean and dry. Ideal for casual dining and Mediterranean-inspired cuisine.

name: Latue  Tempranillo (Organic & Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Tempranillo from La Mancha with ripe cherry, leather, and spice—a vegan-friendly red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Latue's Tempranillo from La Mancha delivers a medium-full-bodied expression of Spain's signature red varietal, certified organic and vegan. The wine opens with ripe red cherry and plum fruit, supported by toasted oak and warming spice on the mid-palate. Tertiary notes of leather and tobacco add complexity and structure, making this a versatile partner for grilled lamb, beef stew, and cured charcuterie. A food-friendly, sustainably produced red at an accessible price point.

name: Latue  Cabernet Sauvignon & Syrah (Organic & Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Spanish blend of Cabernet Sauvignon & Syrah from La Mancha—dark fruit, spice, and earthy depth in a food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store):
Latue's Cabernet Sauvignon & Syrah blend from La Mancha marries the structure and dark-fruit intensity of Cabernet with the peppery spice and herbal character of Syrah. This organic, vegan-certified red delivers a medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, making it a versatile table wine for everyday enjoyment and special occasions alike.

On the palate, expect dark plum, blackcurrant, and black pepper notes with subtle herbaceous undertones and earthy complexity. The wine's approachable profile suits grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine without demanding premium aging.

name: R.A.W. Really Awesome Wine  Cabernet Tempranillo Merlot (Organic & Vegan)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Spanish blend of Cabernet, Tempranillo & Merlot—smooth, fruit-forward, and vegan-friendly for everyday red wine enjoyment.
description (EN Store): R.A.W. Really Awesome Wine's Cabernet Tempranillo Merlot is an organic, vegan-certified red from La Mancha, Spain. The blend marries the structure of Cabernet Sauvignon (60%) with the spiced elegance of Tempranillo (35%) and the softness of Merlot (5%), creating a medium-full-bodied wine with approachable tannins and vibrant red-fruit character. Primary notes of red fruit and dark plum are complemented by subtle oak and spice, with a whisper of earth on the finish. Ideal for casual dining, grilled meats, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Craggy Range  Sophia Gimblett Gravels, Hawke’s Bay
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux blend from Hawke's Bay's premium Gimblett Gravels; ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Craggy Range's Sophia Gimblett Gravels is a classically proportioned Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot (77%) with Cabernet Franc (23%), sourced from one of New Zealand's most celebrated terroirs. The wine displays a generous core of black cherry and dark plum, underpinned by subtle cassis and a mineral earthiness characteristic of the region's distinctive gravel soils. Soft, integrated tannins and a herbaceous secondary layer—typical of Cabernet Franc—create a wine that is both approachable and food-friendly, with enough structure to age gracefully.

name: Craggy Range  Syrah Gimblett Gravels Vineyard, Hawke’s Bay
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Hawke's Bay Syrah with blackberry, pepper, and smoky spice—a structured wine for red meat.
description (EN Store): Craggy Range's Syrah from the Gimblett Gravels vineyard in Hawke's Bay delivers a full-bodied expression of the varietal, anchored by ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit. Medium-high tannins and acidity provide structure and grip, while black pepper and spice notes add complexity. Subtle smoke and earth undertones suggest careful oak integration and terroir influence. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and slow-cooked game dishes.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2018 (Premiers Crus)
short_description (EN Store): Plateau de Mouton's deep Günzian gravel, 100% new French oak, 19–22 months aging — the only First Growth ever reclassified in 1855.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild pioneered château-bottling in 1924 — a practice now universal across Bordeaux — and in 1973 achieved the impossible: a ministerial reclassification from Second to First Growth, the sole amendment ever made to the 1855 system. Both decisions were acts of deliberate transgression, and they define how Mouton is made today. The Plateau de Mouton rises to 27 m above sea level in northern Pauillac, its soils composed of deep Günzian gravel descending over 5 m over iron-rich clay-limestone marl — exceptional drainage that stresses the vine into concentration, while heat retention in the gravel accelerates phenolic ripeness. The oceanic microclimate, moderated by both the Gironde Estuary and the Atlantic, tempers extremes and preserves aromatic precision. Vinification proceeds in 44 individual oak vats (plus 20 stainless-steel tanks) via gravity-feed, enabling parcel-by-parcel fermentation — Mouton is among the last Médoc châteaux to retain oak as a primary fermentation vessel. Aging runs 19–22 months in 100% new French oak, first in the 100-metre Grand Chai built in 1926, then transferred for the second year; barrels are egg-white fined before bottling. Only the finest parcels — at most 50% of estate production — qualify for the Grand Vin. The 2018 vintage delivered a warm, generous growing season: lush black fruit, velvety tannins, and a structural equilibrium between power and finesse that the library describes as exceptional. Drinking window: 2025–2055.

name: Chateau Clerc Milon  2021
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: blackcurrant-driven Bordeaux blend with cedar spice and structured tannins. Elegant, age-worthy.
description (EN Store): Château Clerc Milon's 2021 vintage is a classically structured Pauillac blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (59%), with supporting Merlot (28%), Cabernet Franc (10%), and trace Carmenere and Petit Verdot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with secondary cedar and black pepper from oak aging. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide the framework for a wine built for the table and the cellar alike. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with time, suggesting a 10–20 year evolution potential.

name: Pastourelle De Clerc Milon  "Le 2nd Vin De Chateau Clerc Milon" 2016
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac second wine: Cabernet-Merlot blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Pastourelle de Clerc Milon is the second wine of Château Clerc Milon, a Pauillac estate known for structured, age-worthy Bordeaux. This 2016 blend of 52% Cabernet Sauvignon and 48% Merlot delivers a medium-full body with ripe blackcurrant and plum fruit, supported by cedar spice and black pepper from oak aging. Tertiary notes of tobacco and earth emerge, offering complexity suitable for cellaring or near-term enjoyment with hearty fare.

name: Opus One  Vintage 2012
short_description (EN Store): Oakville's To Kalon Q Block alluvial gravel, 100% new French oak, gravity-flow vinification — Napa's original Franco-American grand vin.
description (EN Store):
When Baron Philippe de Rothschild and Robert Mondavi shook hands in 1979, their explicit brief was to build a Pauillac in Oakville — not a California blockbuster. That founding mandate still governs every decision: earlier picking windows than any comparable Napa estate, preserving acidity at the cost of maximum ripeness, and a winemaking protocol borrowed wholesale from the Left Bank.

The estate's anchor block is the historic To Kalon 'Q Block', sitting on an alluvial fan of gravel-over-clay loam washed down from the Mayacamas Mountains. Volcanic and alluvial components in the subsoil, combined with cool afternoon air drawn north from San Pablo Bay, create a pronounced diurnal shift that locks in structural acidity rare at this latitude.

Winemaker Michael Silacci vinifies each of the ~50 parcels separately in dedicated stainless-steel tanks, moving all fruit and must by gravity only — zero pumping at any stage. Fermentation proceeds on native yeasts isolated from each vineyard site. The wine then ages 18–20 months in 100% new French oak in the semi-circular underground Grand Chai, followed by a further 18 months of bottle age before release — roughly three years from harvest to market. Fining is by fresh egg whites only.

The 2012 vintage is cited alongside 2013, 2016, and 2017 as a benchmark Oakville year; expect the graphite-and-cedar architecture to continue resolving through the late 2030s.

name: Chateau Latour  Les Forts de Latour Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Second wine of Latour, aged in 50% new French oak, released 6–8 years post-vintage from biodynamically farmed L'Enclos gravel croupes.
description (EN Store): In 2012, Château Latour made the most disruptive commercial decision in modern Bordeaux: withdrawing entirely from the en primeur system — the first First Growth to do so — and committing to release wines only when deemed ready to drink. The bottle in hand is the direct product of that philosophy. Les Forts de Latour draws from parcels outside the walled L'Enclos but is vinified under the same parcel-by-parcel discipline across 80 temperature-controlled stainless steel vats, with fruit sorted at both bunch and berry level before full de-stemming and approximately three weeks of skin contact. Malolactic fermentation completes in tank; aging runs in 50% new French oak barriques — half the new-wood intensity of the Grand Vin, preserving more primary fruit definition. The estate's deep gravel croupes over polychrome clay-slate subsoil, just 300 metres from the Gironde Estuary, deliver the slow, even ripening that insulates Latour's second wine from vintage inconsistency. The 2015 vintage produced chalky, well-integrated tannins alongside violet and sweet black fruit, with intensity sufficient for decades of development — drinking window 2025–2055. What no peer replicates: a second wine released on a First Growth's own readiness schedule, not the market's.

name: Chateau Pichon  Longueville Baron 2019
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: Cabernet-led Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins for cellaring.
description (EN Store): Château Pichon Longueville Baron 2019 is a prestigious Pauillac expression anchored by 87% Cabernet Sauvignon and 13% Merlot. This medium-full-bodied blend delivers dominant blackcurrant and black cherry on the nose, supported by cedar and graphite minerality. The palate shows firm, medium-high tannins and balanced acidity, with secondary tobacco and spice notes emerging mid-palate. Tertiary leather and earth complexity suggest this wine will develop gracefully over 10–20 years in bottle. A food-friendly yet age-worthy Bordeaux classic.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2011
short_description (EN Store): Fifth Growth Pauillac trading at Second Growth level — deep Garonne gravel, 75% new French oak, ripe cassis and graphite from the Bages plateau.
description (EN Store): In 1985, a half-bottle of Lynch-Bages 1975 travelled aboard the NASA Discovery shuttle — a footnote that speaks to the estate's outsized reputation relative to its official Fifth Growth rank. That gap between classification and reality defines the château: on the Bages plateau south-southwest of Pauillac, deep homogeneous Garonne gravel over limestone sub-strata — deposited by millennia of Pyrenean erosion — drains freely and forces vines into deep root systems, concentrating fruit while the Gironde estuary moderates temperature extremes. The 2011 vintage, a cooler, structurally driven year in the Médoc, yielded a wine shaped by parcel-by-parcel fermentation across 80 temperature-controlled stainless vats, with malolactic split one-third in vat and two-thirds in barrel, then 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new. That oak regime — high even by Left Bank standards — is calibrated to absorb the estate's characteristically late-harvested, fully phenolic fruit without overwhelming it. The blend leans on Cabernet Sauvignon's structural backbone, supported by Merlot's mid-palate generosity, producing the house's signature opulence rather than the austere austerity of plateau neighbours. The 2011, cooler and more linear than the blockbuster years flanking it, rewards patience: firm tannins have softened with bottle age, and the wine is entering a composed, graphite-edged drinking window now through 2030.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2019
short_description (EN Store): Bages plateau deep Garonne gravel, 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 75% new French oak, 14.5% abv — a Fifth Growth that trades at Second Growth prices.
description (EN Store):
In 1985, astronaut Patrick Baudry carried a half-bottle of the 1975 vintage aboard NASA's Discovery shuttle — a detail that captures Lynch-Bages's peculiar position: officially a Fifth Growth since 1855, yet consistently priced, traded, and cellared alongside the Seconds. The Cazes family's four-generation stewardship, and Jean-Michel Cazes's deliberate policy of harvesting last in Pauillac to maximise phenolic ripeness, built this reputation from the ground up.

The Bages plateau sits south-southwest of Pauillac town, its soils a deep, homogeneous bed of Garonne gravel — slowly eroded from the Pyrenees — over limestone sub-strata. That gravel drains aggressively, stressing the vine and concentrating fruit, while the Gironde estuary moderates temperature extremes and the Landes forest blocks Atlantic frost.

The 2019 grand vin is 70% Cabernet Sauvignon, 24% Merlot, 3% Cabernet Franc, 3% Petit Verdot, fermented parcel-by-parcel in temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats, with malolactic split one-third in vat and two-thirds in barrel. Aging runs 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new. Lynch-Bages was also the first Bordeaux estate to deploy the Winegrid in-barrel monitoring system — tracking alcohol, sugar, colour, and temperature in real time — from 2015 onward.

The 2019 season delivered a mild winter, intensely hot summer, and timely autumn rainfall: the result is bright ripe red fruits, ultra-refined tannins, and 14.5% abv with the structural backbone for long ageing. Drink 2027–2050.

name: Le Merle De Peby Faugeres  Saint Emilion Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion Grand Cru with dark fruit, cedar, and earthy complexity—a classic Bordeaux for serious collectors.
description (EN Store): Le Merle De Peby Faugeres is a distinguished Saint-Émilion Grand Cru from Château Faugères, representing the refined tradition of Right Bank Bordeaux. This 2016 vintage showcases a harmonious Bordeaux blend with medium-full body and balanced tannins, delivering layers of dark plum and black cherry on the palate, complemented by cedar and subtle oak influence. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with age, adding complexity and structure. A food-friendly yet serious wine suited to cellar aging or immediate enjoyment with premium red meat dishes.

name: Chateau Bourgneuf  Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Right Bank Pomerol classic: ripe dark fruit, silky tannins, elegant structure. Merlot-led blend for immediate pleasure.
description (EN Store): Château Bourgneuf is a respected Pomerol producer crafting wines that exemplify the appellation's signature elegance and richness. This 2020 vintage blends 90% Merlot with 10% Cabernet Franc, delivering a medium-full-bodied red with dark plum, black cherry, and subtle earthy complexity. Soft, integrated tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable now while retaining structure for modest cellaring. A food-friendly Right Bank expression suited to classic French cuisine and premium dining.

name: Chateau Palmer  Margaux 2015
short_description (EN Store): Palmer's 2015 plants Merlot on its finest Cantenac gravel — Left Bank heresy yielding Right Bank richness; unfined, 20-month élevage, 96/100.
description (EN Store): When Major General Charles Palmer renamed Domaine de Gascq in 1814, he could not have anticipated that two centuries later his estate would deliberately invert Médoc orthodoxy by planting 47% Merlot on its finest Gunzian Garonne alluvial gravel croupes — the very parcels where Médoc convention demands Cabernet Sauvignon. That transgression defines every bottle. The Cantenac-Margaux plateau's mosaic of 18 distinct gravel, sand, and clay soil types — gravel-clay behind the château, pure-gravel Issan sector to the south — combined with Gironde estuary temperature moderation, delivers the long, even phenolic ripening that makes the 2015's density possible without coarseness. The 2015 growing season brought a hot, dry summer with textbook phenolic maturity; the result is described as 'extravagant and perfectly structured,' with high alcohol anchored by dense tannin architecture. Vinification proceeded plot-by-plot across 42 temperature-controlled stainless vats, with indigenous yeasts, no sulfur at harvest, and a three-week fermentation. Élevage ran 20–22 months: the first year in 225-litre French oak barriques at under 50% new wood, the second in 30-hectolitre oak vats to add patina without oak dominance — then bottled unfined and unfiltered. No peer classified growth in the Médoc combines full biodynamic trajectory, this Merlot proportion on gravel, and a two-vessel aging protocol simultaneously. Critic consensus: 96/100; drinking window 2025–2050.

name: Chateau Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande 2018
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: Cabernet-led blend with blackcurrant depth, cedar structure, and food-friendly tannins.
description (EN Store): Château Pichon Longueville Comtesse de Lalande 2018 is a Bordeaux Pauillac blend anchored by 71% Cabernet Sauvignon, with supporting Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe blackcurrant and plum on the nose, complemented by secondary cedar and tobacco notes. Moderate tannins and balanced acidity provide structure without heaviness, making this a versatile food wine. Tertiary leather and mineral complexity suggest modest aging potential. A classic right-bank Pauillac expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Mr.Riggs  'The Truant' Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): McLaren Vale Shiraz with dark plum, black cherry, and peppery spice—a food-friendly, medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Mr.Riggs 'The Truant' Shiraz is a McLaren Vale expression showcasing the region's signature dark-fruit character and peppery spice. The wine displays dominant primary notes of dark plum and black cherry, supported by secondary smoke and licorice complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it an approachable yet structured red, suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and smoked preparations.

name: Mr.Riggs  'Burnt Block' Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): McLaren Vale Shiraz with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice—a food-friendly, structured red.
description (EN Store): Mr.Riggs 'Burnt Block' Shiraz is a medium-full bodied red from McLaren Vale, Australia's premier Shiraz region. The 2020 vintage showcases the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity—plum and blackberry—layered with peppery spice, smoke, and licorice notes. Medium-high tannins and balanced acidity provide structure and aging potential, while secondary cocoa and leather notes add complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and smoky barbecue.

name: Costera  Red Blend (Cabernet Sauvignon) (Fruit Wine Blend) (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Cabernet-led blend with ripe blackcurrant and plum; approachable medium body and balanced tannin.
description (EN Store): Costera's Red Blend from Chile's Central Valley showcases the varietal character of Cabernet Sauvignon with a fruit-forward profile. Ripe blackcurrant and plum dominate the palate, supported by subtle herbaceous and spice notes that add complexity. Medium body and balanced tannin structure make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or casual pizza nights.

name: Chateau Maris  Merlot Syrah (Organic)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Merlot-Syrah blend from Languedoc with ripe red fruit, pepper spice, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Château Maris crafts this organic Merlot-Syrah blend in the sun-drenched Languedoc-Roussillon region, balancing the soft, plush character of Merlot with the peppery structure and dark fruit intensity of Syrah. Medium-full bodied with moderate tannin, the wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, evolving through herbaceous and black pepper notes that speak to the blend's Syrah component. Mineral undertones and earthy complexity provide food-friendly versatility, making this a natural choice for grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Lucien Crochet  Sancerre La Croix Du Roy
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Loire Pinot Noir with bright raspberry, mineral minerality, and silky tannins—a refined summer red.
description (EN Store): Lucien Crochet's Sancerre La Croix Du Roy is a refined expression of Pinot Noir from the Loire Valley's prestigious Sancerre appellation. The 2020 vintage showcases the varietal's signature elegance: bright raspberry and red fruit on the nose, underpinned by mineral and earthy undertones that reflect the region's distinctive terroir. On the palate, high acidity and silky medium tannins create a fresh, food-friendly profile that evolves gracefully from the first sip. This is a wine built for versatility—equally at home with summer fare as it is with autumn preparations.

name: Heim  Pinot Noir Imperial
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Alsatian Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and mineral freshness—a versatile summer red.
description (EN Store): Heim's Pinot Noir Imperial from Alsace delivers the region's signature elegance: a light-bodied red wine with vibrant acidity and refined tannin structure. On the nose, bright raspberry and red fruit dominate, supported by subtle earthy and mineral undertones. The palate is fresh and silky, with stone fruit notes and a whisper of spice that lingers gracefully. This wine's versatility makes it equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, or lighter seafood preparations.

name: Bindi Surgadi La Boncia Chianti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Tuscan Sangiovese with red cherry, plum, and herbaceous notes—a food-friendly classic for pasta, grilled meats, and rustic Italian fare.
description (EN Store): Bindi Surgadi's La Boncia Chianti DOCG is a pure expression of Sangiovese from Tuscany's historic heartland. The 2022 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied red with bright acidity and refined tannin structure, opening with red cherry and plum fruit supported by herbaceous and mineral undertones. Subtle spice and dried-fruit complexity emerge on the mid-palate, leading to a clean, mineral-driven finish. A versatile food wine suited to tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Pian Delle Querci  Brunello Di Montalcino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brunello di Montalcino with red cherry, dried herbs, and leather complexity. Structured, age-worthy Sangiovese.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Brunello di Montalcino from Pian delle Querce, this 2018 Sangiovese expresses the hallmark elegance of the Montalcino terroir. Red cherry and herbaceous notes dominate the primary impression, supported by leather and spice from careful oak aging. Tertiary earth and tobacco character emerge on the palate, revealing the wine's potential for further evolution. High acidity and medium-high tannin provide the backbone for food pairing versatility and cellaring potential.

name: Vite Colte Barolo "Paesi Tuoi" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barolo Nebbiolo with cherry, licorice, and cedar—age-worthy structure for serious collectors.
description (EN Store): Vite Colte's Paesi Tuoi is a classically structured Barolo DOCG from Piedmont's Nebbiolo heartland. The 2018 vintage displays the varietal's signature cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the region's terroir. Medium-full body and high tannin structure promise graceful evolution over the next decade, while the wine's current balance of acidity and fruit makes it approachable now with proper decanting. A serious expression suited to contemplative cellaring or pairing with substantial cuisine.

name: Orapiena  Montepulciano D'Abruzzo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, herbaceous notes, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Orapiena's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a classic expression of this central Italian varietal, delivering a medium-full bodied red with balanced tannins and vibrant acidity. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. Subtle earth and dried fruit notes emerge on the finish, adding complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile food wine with natural affinity for tomato-based dishes, grilled meats, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Fontana Di Papa Vino Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-led Lazio blend with bright cherry, herbaceous depth, and savoury grip. Food-friendly Italian red.
description (EN Store): Fontana di Papa's Vino Rosso is a Sangiovese-based blend from Lazio, combining the region's signature red-fruit character with supporting Merlot and Montepulciano. The wine opens with vibrant cherry and plum, underpinned by herbaceous and peppery notes that give it savoury complexity. High acidity and medium-full body make it a versatile table wine, equally at home with tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, or cured charcuterie. A straightforward, well-balanced Italian red offering genuine value.

name: Chateau Pape Clement  2018
short_description (EN Store): Pessac-Léognan Bordeaux blend of power and elegance—dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): Château Pape Clément is a historic Pessac-Léognan estate producing a classic Bordeaux red blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (66%), with Merlot (30%) and Cabernet Franc providing structure and finesse. The 2018 vintage expresses the region's signature dark fruit character—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar, tobacco, and mineral notes that evolve with air. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a wine built for both near-term enjoyment and graceful aging, equally at home with grilled red meat, lamb, or aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Cos D'Estournel  Saint-Estephe 2018
short_description (EN Store): Left Bank Bordeaux classic: structured Cabernet-led blend with dark fruit, cedar, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Château Cos d'Estournel 2018 is a prestigious Left Bank Bordeaux from Saint-Estèphe, anchored by 74% Cabernet Sauvignon with supporting Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot. The blend delivers a medium-full body with medium-high tannins and medium acidity, characteristic of the appellation's gravelly terroir. Primary notes of blackcurrant and dark plum give way to secondary cedar and tobacco, with tertiary minerality and leather complexity emerging with age. This is a food-friendly yet serious wine suited to grilled and braised red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Tierra Sagrada Vigno Carignan
short_description (EN Store): 100% Carignan from Chile's Maule Valley—rustic, food-friendly red with cherry, plum, and mineral spine.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Tierra Sagrada Vigno Carignan is a single-varietal expression from Chile's Maule Valley, showcasing the textural and spice-driven character of old-vine Carignan. Medium-full bodied with moderate tannin structure, this wine delivers primary notes of red cherry and plum, supported by dried herb and mineral undertones. The rustic profile and balanced acidity make it an approachable yet complex food companion, equally at home with grilled meats, braised preparations, or Mediterranean-inflected cuisine.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo 'Origine' Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Cabernet Sauvignon from Puente Alto with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo 'Origine' is a 100% Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's prestigious Puente Alto subregion in Maipo Valley. The 2020 vintage delivers a medium-full bodied expression with dominant blackcurrant and black cherry fruit, supported by cedar and cocoa notes from careful oak aging. Moderate tannins and balanced acidity make this wine approachable yet structured, ideal for pairing with grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses. The wine shows herbaceous undertones and mineral complexity that suggest careful winemaking and terroir expression typical of the Rothschild portfolio.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Baronesa P.
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Valley Cabernet-led blend with dark fruit, cedar, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Chilean classic.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Baronesa P. is a Cabernet Sauvignon–dominant blend from Puente Alto in Chile's Maipo Valley, anchored by 81% Cabernet with supporting Petit Verdot, Cabernet Franc, Syrah, and Carmenere. The 2021 vintage delivers a medium-full bodied wine with ripe dark fruit, cassis, and cedar on the nose, followed by black pepper spice and structured tannins on the palate. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather add complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and aged cheeses.

name: Angove  Brightlands Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Organic Shiraz from South Australia's Riverland: ripe dark berries, spice, and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Angove's Brightlands Shiraz represents the winery's commitment to certified organic viticulture and premium South Australian winemaking. This 2020 vintage delivers a full-bodied expression of Shiraz with ripe dark berry fruit, black pepper spice, and subtle earthiness characteristic of the region's Mediterranean climate. Medium-high tannins provide elegant structure and aging potential, while secondary notes of cocoa and leather add complexity. A vibrant, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Velenosi  Solesta Rosso Piceno DOC Superiore
short_description (EN Store): Marche's elegant Montepulciano-Sangiovese blend: ripe red fruit, mineral backbone, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Solesta Rosso Piceno DOC Superiore represents the Marche region's mastery of Montepulciano and Sangiovese in harmony. This medium-full-bodied blend opens with ripe cherry and plum, layered with herbaceous undertones and black pepper spice. The palate shows balanced tannin and mineral tension, with dried fruit complexity emerging on the finish. A versatile, age-worthy red suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and earthy preparations.

name: Velenosi  Verso Sera Montepulciano d’Abruzzo DOCG Colline Teramane
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, herbaceous notes, and mineral structure—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Velenosi's Verso Sera Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOCG Colline Teramane is a medium-full-bodied red wine showcasing the classic character of Abruzzo's signature varietal. The 2021 vintage opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by herbaceous and black pepper notes that reflect the wine's terroir. A subtle mineral backbone and earthy undertones provide complexity and structure, making this wine both approachable and food-friendly. The balanced tannin profile and medium acidity ensure versatility at the table, from grilled meats to tomato-based pasta dishes.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Devil's Carnaval Spectacular Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—a classic expression of Maule Valley power.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Devil's Carnaval Spectacular Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while black pepper spice adds complexity on the mid-palate. The wine's high tannin structure and medium acidity create a dry, gripping finish with emerging tertiary notes of tobacco and leather—hallmarks of a wine built for cellaring or pairing with substantial dishes.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Devil's Carnaval Fabulous Red
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Chilean red with dark fruit and smooth tannins; food-friendly everyday wine.
description (EN Store): Casillero Del Diablo Devil's Carnaval Fabulous Red is a medium-full bodied Chilean red from Maule Valley, crafted as an accessible, food-friendly expression. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary character, supported by subtle spice and oak influence. Moderate tannins and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, pairing naturally with grilled meats, hearty pasta, and roasted poultry.

name: Ferraton Pere & Fils  Hermitage “les Miaux” Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Hermitage Syrah with blackberry, pepper, and structured tannins—a classic Northern Rhône expression.
description (EN Store): Ferraton Père & Fils's Hermitage "les Miaux" Rouge is a single-varietal Syrah from France's Northern Rhône Valley, representing the region's benchmark for powerful, age-worthy red wine. The 2020 vintage delivers a full-bodied profile with medium-high acidity and tannin structure, anchored by dominant blackberry and dark fruit character with supporting black pepper and smoke. Secondary oak and spice notes reflect careful winemaking, while emerging leather and earth complexity hint at tertiary development. This is a wine built for serious food pairing and cellaring potential.

name: Maison 1982  'Atlantique' Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Classic Bordeaux blend with ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and food-friendly structure. NV release from Maison 1982.
description (EN Store): Maison 1982's 'Atlantique' is a traditional Bordeaux red blend balancing primary dark cherry and plum with secondary cedar and oak complexity. Medium-full body and moderate tannin structure make it approachable yet age-worthy, suitable for immediate enjoyment or modest cellaring. The earthy undertones and subtle herbaceous notes suggest careful winemaking in the classic Left Bank style.

name: Domaine Jaume  Cotes-Du-Rhone Rouge AOP
short_description (EN Store): Southern Rhône GSM blend: ripe red fruit, spice, and garrigue. Food-friendly, approachable, classic.
description (EN Store): A classic Southern Rhône expression from Domaine Jaume, this Côtes-du-Rhône Rouge blends Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvèdre in the traditional GSM style. Ripe red and dark plum fruit dominates the palate, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous garrigue notes characteristic of the region. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured enough for food pairing. Subtle earth and mineral undertones add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

name: Domaine Jaume  Vinsobres Altitude 420 AOP
short_description (EN Store): Southern Rhône GSM blend from Domaine Jaume: ripe red fruit, spice, and garrigue minerality. Food-friendly, elegant.
description (EN Store): Domaine Jaume's Vinsobres Altitude 420 is a classic Southern Rhône Grenache-Syrah-Mourvèdre blend from the elevated Vinsobres terroir. Ripe red and dark plum fruit dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper spice and subtle herbaceous notes. The palate shows medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, allowing the wine's garrigue character and mineral undertones to emerge. A food-friendly expression of the region's sun-ripened fruit and rustic elegance.

name: Domaine Jaume  Vinsobres Reference Rouge AOP
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Rhône South blend of Syrah, Grenache & Mourvèdre with dark fruit, pepper & mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Domaine Jaume's Vinsobres Reference Rouge represents the classic Southern Rhône GSM blend, anchored by Syrah, Grenache, and Mourvèdre. This full-bodied wine opens with dominant blackberry and dark fruit character, supported by black pepper spice and subtle smoke from winemaking. Medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary earth and minerality notes add complexity and age-worthiness. Ideal for grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Famille Jaume  Gigondas AOP
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Gigondas with dark plum, garrigue, and peppery spice—a food-friendly Rhône South blend.
description (EN Store): Famille Jaume's Gigondas AOP is a classic Rhône South blend anchored by Grenache with supporting Syrah and Mourvèdre. The wine opens with dark plum and blackberry fruit, layered with garrigue herbs and black pepper spice characteristic of the appellation. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare. Earthy minerality and subtle leather notes emerge on the finish, reflecting the terroir of Vinsobres.

name: Adulation  Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Ripe California Zinfandel with dark berry, spice, and grippy tannins—a bold, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): A classic California Zinfandel expressing the varietal's signature dark fruit intensity and peppery spice. Blackberry and plum dominate the palate, supported by black pepper and subtle oak influence. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, making this wine an excellent match for grilled meats, BBQ, and hearty braised dishes. Approachable now, with enough backbone for short-term cellaring.

name: Chateau Tour Bellevue Haut-Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Classic Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend: full-bodied, high-tannin, blackcurrant-forward with cedar complexity and age-worthy structure.
description (EN Store): Château Tour Bellevue's Haut-Médoc represents the traditional Bordeaux style: a Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, supported by firm, well-integrated tannins characteristic of quality Médoc terroir. The wine displays leather and earthy undertones that suggest graceful aging potential. Full-bodied and structured, it rewards decanting and pairs naturally with grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Du Fort Pontus  Fronsac
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Fronsac with ripe red fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Château du Fort Pontus presents a classic Fronsac Merlot with medium-full body and approachable structure. Ripe plum and black cherry dominate the palate, supported by subtle oak and earthy mineral notes. Soft, well-integrated tannins and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled beef, braised dishes, or herb-roasted chicken. A reliable, well-crafted expression of the Right Bank terroir.

name: Apollonio Terragnolo Negroamaro
short_description (EN Store): Salento Negroamaro from calcareous-clay soils near Lecce, aged in Slavonian oak by a family winery operating since 1870.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1870 and now designated an Impresa Storica d'Italia, Apollonio has spent four generations resisting the Puglian industry's pivot toward stainless-steel freshness — aging approximately one million litres per year in oak as a deliberate house doctrine. The Terragnolo draws its Negroamaro from the flat-to-karst-depressed terrain of the Valle della Cupa near Monteroni di Lecce, roughly 100 m above sea level, where calcareous soils laced with clay slow water uptake and concentrate phenolics, while dual sea breezes from the Adriatic and Ionian coasts temper the intense Mediterranean sun and preserve natural acidity. Grapes are harvested manually in late September, destemmed, and fermented at 27–28 °C in large Slavonian oak vats with floating-cap management and pump-overs; skin maceration extends to build polyphenolic structure before the wine moves to Slavonian oak barrels for entry-level aging. What distinguishes Terragnolo within Apollonio's range is its focus on Negroamaro as a near-varietal expression — the grape's characteristic bitter-cherry skin and tobacco-earth register left largely unmasked by new oak. The current vintage delivers the dark fruit density and Mediterranean herb character typical of Salento's sun-saturated growing season, approachable now yet structured for 3–5 years of further development.

name: Chateau du Bergey  Fronsac
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Fronsac: ripe plum and cherry with balanced tannins and earthy depth. Food-friendly Bordeaux classic.
description (EN Store): Château du Bergey's Fronsac is a pure expression of Merlot from the Right Bank's celebrated Fronsac appellation. Ripe dark plum and black cherry dominate the nose, supported by subtle oak and earthy minerality. The palate is medium-full and approachable, with soft tannins and a balanced structure that rewards both immediate enjoyment and modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and rich pasta preparations.

name: Chateau La Douce  Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Merlot with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft tannins—elegant Bordeaux for red meat and roasted fare.
description (EN Store): Château La Douce's Médoc Merlot delivers a classic Bordeaux expression centred on dark stone fruit and berry character. The palate shows ripe plum and black cherry with supporting vanilla notes and integrated tannins, creating a medium-full frame suited to grilled beef, braised dishes, and roasted poultry. Herbaceous undertones add complexity, while the soft tannin structure ensures approachability without sacrificing structure.

name: Chateau Du Gazin Cannon Fronsac
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Fronsac with ripe plum, cherry, and earthy complexity. Food-friendly medium-full body, soft tannins.
description (EN Store): Château du Gazin's Cannon Fronsac is a classic Right Bank Bordeaux blend anchored by 85% Merlot, with supporting Cabernet Franc and Malbec. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, layered with subtle herbaceous notes and a whisper of spice. Soft, approachable tannins and medium-full body make this a versatile table wine, while earthy undertones suggest modest age-worthiness. Ideal for casual entertaining or weeknight dining with grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Chateau Marjosse  Grand Vin de Bordeaux Entre deux-Mers
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Bordeaux blend from Entre-Deux-Mers with ripe red fruit, soft tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Château Marjosse's Grand Vin de Bordeaux is a classically proportioned blend anchored by Merlot (80%), with supporting Cabernet Franc, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Malbec. The wine expresses the approachable, fruit-forward character typical of Entre-Deux-Mers, balancing ripe red plum and black cherry with subtle herbaceous notes and a supple tannin structure. Medium-full body and moderate acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to casual dining and everyday entertaining.

name: Chateau Lafaurie Puisseguin-Saint-Emilion
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Right Bank Bordeaux: ripe dark fruit, cedar spice, and balanced tannins. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store):
Château Lafaurie's Puisseguin-Saint-Émilion is a classically structured Right Bank Bordeaux built on an 80% Merlot foundation, with Cabernet Sauvignon and Cabernet Franc adding depth and complexity. The wine opens with dominant black cherry and dark plum aromatics, supported by subtle cassis undertones that speak to the region's terroir.

On the palate, medium-full body and balanced medium tannins create a food-friendly profile without heaviness. Cedar and tobacco notes emerge in the mid-palate, suggesting careful oak aging, while earthy minerality in the finish hints at the limestone-clay soils of Puisseguin. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with grilled meats, braised dishes, or aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Premeaux  Cote de Nuits Villages
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Côte de Nuits Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and earthy minerality—a food-friendly Burgundy classic.
description (EN Store): Château Premeaux's Côte de Nuits Villages is a refined expression of Burgundy's signature varietal, Pinot Noir. The 2022 vintage showcases the region's hallmark elegance: bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, supported by subtle oak and a mineral backbone that speaks to the limestone-rich terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine approachable in youth while retaining structure for modest cellaring. A versatile food wine suited to roasted poultry, earthy mushroom preparations, and lighter red-meat dishes.

name: Chateau Premeaux  Nuits Saint Georges
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir from Nuits-Saint-Georges with red cherry, minerality, and subtle oak—a food-friendly Burgundy classic.
description (EN Store): Château Premeaux's Nuits-Saint-Georges is a refined expression of Burgundy's signature Pinot Noir, sourced from one of the region's most prestigious appellations. The wine opens with bright red cherry and black cherry fruit, grounded by the distinctive minerality characteristic of the terroir. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure provide elegance without heaviness, while subtle oak and black pepper notes add complexity and spice. Earthy undertones and herbaceous whispers emerge on the finish, suggesting modest age-worthiness and versatility at table. A classic food-friendly Burgundy suited to roasted poultry, mushroom dishes, and cured meats.

name: Arnaud Pellertier  Morey Saint Denis
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Morey-Saint-Denis Pinot Noir with silky red fruit, earthy minerality, and Burgundian finesse.
description (EN Store): Arnaud Pellertier's 2022 Morey-Saint-Denis is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir that exemplifies the region's hallmark balance of fruit purity and mineral complexity. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry, supported by subtle oak integration and a distinctive slate-driven minerality. Mid-palate reveals earthy undertones and fine-grained tannins that suggest food-friendly versatility. A wine of understated elegance suited to intimate dinners and thoughtful pairings.

name: Domaine Marchand -Grillot  Gevrey-Chambertin 'Enjouise'
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Gevrey-Chambertin Pinot Noir with silky tannins, red fruit, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Domaine Marchand-Grillot's Gevrey-Chambertin 'Enjouise' is a classically structured Burgundy Pinot Noir showcasing the region's hallmark elegance and terroir-driven character. The 2018 vintage delivers ripe red cherry and blackberry on the nose, layered with earthy minerality and subtle oak influence. On the palate, medium-full body and refined tannin structure provide backbone without heaviness, while secondary notes of dried herb and forest floor add complexity. This wine balances fruit-forward appeal with the mineral precision expected of Gevrey-Chambertin, making it versatile at table and rewarding with food.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Baron Carl AOC Saint-Emilion
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux blend from Saint-Émilion with dark fruit, cedar, and balanced structure—food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux red blend from the prestigious Saint-Émilion appellation, crafted by the storied Baron Philippe de Rothschild house. This wine showcases the hallmark character of the region: ripe dark plum and blackcurrant fruit on the nose, complemented by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes. The palate is well-balanced with medium tannins and good acidity, offering both immediate appeal and modest aging potential. Ideal for pairing with grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Baron Nathaniel AOC Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: blackcurrant-driven Bordeaux blend with cedar complexity and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A traditional Pauillac expression from the esteemed Baron Philippe de Rothschild portfolio, this Bordeaux blend anchors its character in ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. The medium-full body and balanced tannin structure reflect classic Left Bank winemaking, with emerging tertiary tobacco and mineral notes suggesting graceful aging potential. Food-friendly and versatile, it suits grilled red meats, braised beef, and aged charcuterie with equal ease.

name: Louis Latour  Moulin-A-Vent Les Michelons
short_description (EN Store): Moulin-à-Vent single-climat Gamay from Les Michelons; Louis Latour's négociant reach into Beaujolais's most age-worthy cru, flash-pasteurised before bottling.
description (EN Store):
Louis Latour — a house whose vine ownership traces to 1731 and whose Corton Grancey cuverie was France's first purpose-built winery — extends its négociant reach south into Beaujolais with this single-climat bottling from Les Michelons, one of Moulin-à-Vent's most structured lieux-dits. The climat sits on the appellation's characteristic manganese-rich pink granite soils (locally called gore), whose mineral poverty and exceptional drainage force Gamay roots deep, suppressing yields and concentrating phenolic structure in a way that distinguishes Moulin-à-Vent from every other Beaujolais cru.

Vinification follows the Louis Latour red-wine template: 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed Gamay, fermented in open wooden vats with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days, then aged in French oak (15–35% new) — a regime that adds spinal structure without overwhelming the granite-driven mineral tension. Most critically, the wine undergoes Louis Latour's openly acknowledged flash pasteurisation (approx. 72°C for 2–3 seconds) before bottling, eliminating fining and allowing the wine to fall clear naturally over two to three months.

The special feature here is categorical: flash pasteurisation on a Beaujolais cru is virtually unheard of among peers, and the deliberate use of oak aging on Gamay from a granite site is a direct argument that Les Michelons can sustain the same élevage philosophy applied to Louis Latour's Côte de Nuits reds. The 2021 vintage in Beaujolais delivered freshness and lifted aromatics after a cool, late-harvested season — expect taut red-fruit concentration with the appellation's signature iron-mineral grip.

name: Louis Latour  Les Chaillots Aloxe-Corton 1er Cru
