short_description (EN Store): Premier Cru Pinot Noir from Aloxe-Corton's Hill of Corton, fermented in open wooden vats, aged in 15–35% new French oak — and flash-pasteurised before bottling.
description (EN Store): The Latour family has vinified red Burgundy at the foot of the Hill of Corton since vine ownership traceable to 1731, and their Corton Grancey Cuverie — built 1832–1834 as France's first purpose-built winery — remains the operational heart of this Premier Cru. Les Chaillots sits on the south-east face of Corton on red chalk, clay and marl soils; the iron-rich clay fraction drives the darker-fruited, more structured character that distinguishes Aloxe-Corton Premiers Crus from the lighter soils of Beaune. 100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, fully destemmed and crushed, undergoes approximately 14 days of fermentation in one of 45 large open wooden vats on the first floor of the Grancey Cuverie, with manual pigeage and gravity-only transport throughout. Aging proceeds in 15–35% new French oak barrique, medium toast, 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from French oak air-dried 24–36 months. The defining house transgression: all reds are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining while ensuring microbiological stability — openly acknowledged, critically debated, categorically unique among major Burgundy houses. The 2022 vintage delivered warm, generous fruit with sufficient structure for medium-term development; drink 2026–2034.

name: Louis Latour  Chassagne-Montrachet Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Chassagne-Montrachet Rouge from Burgundy's most controversial red-wine technique: 100% Pinot Noir, flash-pasteurised, unfined, 2020 vintage.
description (EN Store): Louis Latour's house practice of flash-pasteurising all red wines at approximately 72°C for two to three seconds before bottling remains the most openly debated technical decision in the Côte de Beaune — eliminating fining entirely by allowing the wine to fall clear naturally over two to three months post-treatment. Chassagne-Montrachet Rouge sits on the southern flank of the Côte de Beaune, where the limestone-and-clay soils shift from the white-wine-dominant marl of Puligny toward heavier clay content, giving Pinot Noir firmer structure and darker fruit character than its northern neighbours. Grapes are 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed, and fermented in large open wooden vats for approximately 14 days with manual pigeage throughout; gravity-only transport is used at every stage inside the Corton Grancey Cuverie, the first purpose-built winery in France (constructed 1832–1834). Aging proceeds in French oak — 15–35% new, medium toast, 228-litre barrels air-dried 24–36 months in-house at the Louis Latour cooperage — for 10–12 months. The special feature here is structural: no other major Burgundy house openly applies flash-pasteurisation to village-level reds, making this wine microbiologically stable without a single fining agent. The 2020 vintage in Burgundy delivered concentrated, structured reds with deep colour and taut acidity — expect black cherry, blueberry, and earthy undertones with a drinking window extending comfortably through the late 2020s.

name: Louis Latour  Chambolle-Musigny
short_description (EN Store): Côte de Nuits Pinot Noir from the limestone-clay soils of Chambolle, fermented in open wooden vats and flash-pasteurised — Latour's openly controversial bottling signature.
description (EN Store):
When Jean Latour established the house in 1797 — his family already three generations into vine ownership on the Côte d'Or — the ambition was to vinify Burgundy's most fragrant appellation without compromise on stability. Chambolle-Musigny sits on a narrow band of Jurassic limestone and fine clay at the northern edge of the Côte de Nuits, its shallow, well-drained soils producing Pinot Noir of pronounced floral lift and tensile structure that distinguishes it from the broader, more muscular communes to the south.

Louis Latour's red winemaking begins with 100% hand-harvesting and full destemming, followed by fermentation in one of 45 large open wooden vats at the Corton Grancey Cuverie — a gravity-flow facility built into the hillside in 1832. Approximately 14 days of vatting with manual pigeage extracts colour and structure without over-extraction. Aging proceeds in French oak barrels (15–35% new, medium toast, 10–12 months), the staves air-dried for up to 36 months at the house's own cooperage. Before bottling, the wine undergoes flash pasteurisation at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds — a practice Latour openly acknowledges and defends for microbiological stability, eliminating fining entirely, though it remains a point of critical debate regarding long-term aromatic development. No other major Burgundy house applies this technique so consistently across its red range. The 2018 vintage delivered outstanding freshness across the Côte d'Or, the fifth consecutive fine Burgundy harvest, producing wines of approachable volume and good structural tension.

name: Louis Latour  Les Epenots Pommard 1er Cru
short_description (EN Store): Pommard 1er Cru Les Epenots: clay-iron soils, open-vat pigeage, 35% new French oak — then flash-pasteurised, Latour's openly controversial house signature.
description (EN Store): When the Latour family built France's first purpose-built winery into the Corton hillside in 1832, they engineered gravity-flow vinification across five subterranean levels — the same infrastructure that processes Les Epenots today. Les Epenots, one of Pommard's most celebrated Premier Cru lieux-dits, sits on the appellation's characteristic iron-rich clay and marl soils at mid-slope on the Côte de Beaune, conditions that drive deep colour extraction and the firm, structured tannin architecture Pommard is known for. In the cellar, 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed Pinot Noir is fermented in large open wooden vats with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days, then aged in French oak barrique (15–35% new, medium toast, 10–12 months) — staves air-dried up to 36 months at the house's own cooperage in Aloxe-Corton. The defining technical transgression: all Latour reds are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining while ensuring microbiological stability — a practice no other major Burgundy house openly employs. The 2018 vintage delivered outstanding ripeness with preserved freshness across the Côte d'Or, producing Pinot Noir of generous fruit concentration and supple structure; drink 2024–2032.

name: Louis Latour  Nuits Saint Georges Damodes 1er Cru
short_description (EN Store): Nuits-Saint-Georges 1er Cru Les Damodes: iron-rich clay-limestone on the Vosne border, 14-day open-vat fermentation, flash-pasteurised before bottling.
description (EN Store):
Louis Latour — a house whose vine ownership traces to 1731 and whose Corton Grancey cuverie, built 1832–1834, was the first purpose-built winery in France — applies its gravity-flow, open-vat philosophy to one of Nuits-Saint-Georges' most northerly Premiers Crus. Les Damodes sits at the appellation's boundary with Vosne-Romanée, where the limestone-bedrock soils carry a higher proportion of iron-rich clay than the more gravel-dominant southern Nuits lieux-dits, lending the wine a firmer, more structured tannic architecture and a darker, earthier fruit register than its neighbours to the south.

100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, fully destemmed and crushed, ferments for approximately 14 days in large open wooden vats with manual pigeage throughout, with gravity-only transport at every stage. Aging proceeds in French oak (15–35% new), barrels coopered in-house from air-dried French oak staves, for 10–12 months at medium toast. The house's openly acknowledged and categorically unique practice of flash-pasteurisation (approx. 72°C, 2–3 seconds) before bottling eliminates fining entirely — the wine falls clear naturally over 2–3 months post-treatment, a transgression no other major Burgundy négociant replicates or admits to.

The 2019 vintage in the Côte de Nuits delivered ripe, generous fruit with natural structural depth; Les Damodes' clay-limestone terroir channels that ripeness into a wine with grip and mid-palate density that rewards 3–6 years of further cellaring.

name: Louis Latour  Vosne-Romanee Chaumes 1er Cru
short_description (EN Store): Vosne-Romanée 1er Cru Les Chaumes: Pinot Noir from clay-limestone soils, fermented in open wooden vats, flash-pasteurised — Latour's most debated house signature.
description (EN Store): When the Latour family built the Corton Grancey Cuverie in 1832–1834 — the first purpose-built winery in France — they engineered five hillside levels so that gravity, not pumps, would move every litre of wine. That same infrastructure handles this Vosne-Romanée Premier Cru today. Les Chaumes sits on the southern flank of Vosne-Romanée on clay-limestone soils; the site's moderate elevation and easterly exposure temper ripening, preserving the taut, iron-edged character that separates Vosne's southern Premiers Crus from the more opulent northern cluster around La Croix Rameau. 100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, fully destemmed and crushed, ferments for approximately 14 days in large open wooden vats with manual pigeage; gravity-only transport throughout the Corton Grancey Cuverie. Aging proceeds in French oak barrique (15–35% new, medium toast, 10–12 months), staves air-dried 24–36 months at the house's own cooperage. The defining — and openly contested — house signature: flash pasteurisation at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining while ensuring microbiological stability, a practice no other major Burgundy house applies consistently across its red range. The 2017 vintage in the Côte de Nuits delivered precise, mid-weight Pinot with firm acidity and restrained fruit concentration; drinking well now through the late 2020s.

name: Louis Latour  Corton Les Perrieres Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Corton Les Perrières Grand Cru 2016: red chalk-clay soils, 100% new in-house oak, flash-pasteurised — Burgundy's most openly transgressive Grand Cru red.
description (EN Store): The cellars of the Corton Grancey Cuverie — built 1832–1834 as France's first purpose-built winery — are dug 20 metres into the bedrock of Corton Perrières itself, making the connection between site and cellar unusually literal. Les Perrières sits on the south-east face of the Hill of Corton on red chalk, clay and marl soils, a heavier, iron-rich profile that drives deeper colour and firmer structure than the limestone-dominant summit parcels above. Vines average 35–40 years of age, concentrating yield into fruit of measurable density. In the cellar, 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed Pinot Noir is fermented in 45 large open wooden vats over approximately 14 days with manual pigeage, transported entirely by gravity through the five-level Grancey infrastructure. Aging proceeds in 100% new French oak pièces (228 L), medium toast, 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from French oak air-dried 24–36 months. The defining transgression: every red wine Louis Latour bottles undergoes flash pasteurisation at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds, eliminating fining entirely while remaining a persistent point of critical debate on long-term aromatic evolution. The 2016 vintage delivered concentrated, structured Côte de Beaune reds with excellent cellaring potential; drinking window extends through the late 2020s.

name: Louis Latour  Chateau Corton Grancey Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Four Corton Grand Cru climats blended at the Hill of Corton's gravity-flow 1832 cuverie, flash-pasteurised — no fining, 40+-year vines.
description (EN Store): When Jean Latour's descendants built the Corton Grancey Cuverie into the hillside between 1832 and 1834, they created the first purpose-built winery in France — five levels of gravity-flow infrastructure dug 20 metres into the bedrock of Corton Grand Cru Perrières, a design that still moves every litre of this wine without a pump. Château Corton Grancey Grand Cru is produced only in declared vintages from a multi-climat blend of four named Corton Grand Cru parcels — Les Bressandes, Les Perrières, Les Grèves, and Le Clos du Roi — with proportions adjusted vintage to vintage, drawn from vines averaging 40+ years on the south-east face of the Hill of Corton, where red chalk, clay, and marl soils deliver structure and pigment density that pure limestone cannot. Grapes are 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed, and fermented for approximately 14 days in large open wooden vats on the cuverie's first floor with manual pigeage, then aged in French oak for 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from oak air-dried 24–36 months. The house's most openly transgressive decision: all reds are flash-pasteurised at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling, eliminating fining entirely while remaining a point of critical debate on long-term aromatic development. The 2019 vintage shows ripe, complex fruit drinking well on release, with the multi-climat architecture providing layered textural depth no single-parcel Corton can replicate.

name: Louis Latour  Echezeaux Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Échezeaux Grand Cru from Louis Latour's négociant range — 100% Pinot Noir, flash-pasteurised before bottling, aged in 100% new French oak from the house's own cooperage.
description (EN Store):
When Jean Latour established the house in 1797 — his family already three generations into vine ownership on the Côte d'Or — the ambition was to vinify Burgundy's greatest terroirs with the same rigour applied to their own Corton holdings. Échezeaux Grand Cru sits on the southern edge of the Vosne-Romanée Grand Cru belt, its soils a shallow mix of brown limestone and iron-rich clay over Bajocian bedrock, the elevation and easterly aspect delivering a longer, cooler ripening arc than the more sheltered Vougeot floor below.

The fruit is 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed and crushed, then fermented in Louis Latour's large open wooden vats at the Corton Grancey Cuverie with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days; gravity alone moves the wine throughout the five-level hillside facility built in 1832. Aging proceeds in 100% new French oak pièces (228 L), medium toast, 10–12 months — barrels coopered in-house from French oak air-dried 24–36 months. Before bottling, the wine undergoes flash pasteurisation (approx. 72°C, 2–3 seconds), the house's openly acknowledged and categorically unique practice among major Burgundy producers, which achieves microbiological stability and eliminates fining without extended cellar intervention.

The 2017 vintage across the Côte de Nuits delivered concentrated, structurally precise reds from a warm, early harvest — wines with taut fruit and firm backbone that reward patience. Drink 2024–2032.

name: Louis Latour  Romanée St Vivant Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Latour's 0.8-ha 'Les Quatre Journaux' parcel in RSV — 100% new French oak, flash-pasteurised, Pinot Noir from one of Burgundy's most storied lieux-dits.
description (EN Store):
When the Latour family registered their 0.8-hectare holding in Romanée-Saint-Vivant under the name Les Quatre Journaux — 'the four journals,' a medieval unit of land measure — they were staking a claim in one of the Côte de Nuits' most geologically privileged corridors. The parcel sits on the thin, well-drained limestone and marl soils of Vosne-Romanée's eastern slope, where shallow topsoil forces vine roots deep into fractured bedrock, concentrating extract and sharpening mineral tension across a growing season.

100% hand-harvested Pinot Noir, fully destemmed and crushed, ferments in large open wooden vats at the Corton Grancey Cuverie over approximately 14 days with manual pigeage, transported entirely by gravity throughout. Aging proceeds in 100% new French oak pièces (228 L), medium toast, built at the house's own cooperage from oak air-dried 24–36 months, for 10–12 months.

The defining transgression: Louis Latour flash-pasteurises all red wines at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling — the only major Burgundy house to do so openly and consistently — eliminating fining entirely as the wine falls clear naturally over 2–3 months post-treatment. In 2012, a vintage of genuine Côte de Nuits tension and age-worthiness, this wine rewards patience; the combination of a warm, even growing season and naturally high-acid Vosne terroir produced structured, concentrated reds with a long drinking arc.

name: Chavin Zero Non-Alcoholic Syrah (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Syrah from Languedoc with dark fruit, spice, and smoky complexity—a non-alcoholic red for serious food pairing.
description (EN Store): Chavin's Zero Non-Alcoholic Syrah delivers the structure and depth of a traditional Languedoc red without alcohol. Dark plum and blackberry dominate the primary profile, supported by black pepper and spice notes characteristic of Syrah. Secondary smoke and leather complexity add intrigue, while subtle earth and tobacco undertones provide tertiary development. Full-bodied with medium-high tannin, this wine suits grilled red meat, braised beef, game, and smoked preparations—ideal for those seeking serious wine character in a non-alcoholic format.

name: Giustini  Vecchio Sogno Negroamaro Salento IGP
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Negroamaro from Salento with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Full-bodied, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Giustini's Vecchio Sogno Negroamaro showcases the bold, fruit-forward character of Salento's signature red varietal. Dark cherry and ripe berry dominate the nose, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper notes that add complexity and structure. The palate is full and rounded, with well-integrated tannins and a smooth, lingering finish. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with pizza and pasta as with grilled meats and hearty casseroles. A reliable, unpretentious expression of southern Italian winemaking.

name: Xige Estate  Jade Dove Single Varietal Cabernet Gernischt
short_description (EN Store): Ningxia Cabernet Gernischt with dark plum, black cherry, and herbaceous cedar—food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's 2019 Jade Dove Single Varietal Cabernet Gernischt represents the emerging quality potential of Ningxia's Qingtongxia subregion. This medium-full-bodied red opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry, supported by blackcurrant depth. Cedar and black pepper notes from winemaking complement the primary fruit, while subtle herbaceous and earthy undertones suggest careful extraction and aging. The medium tannin structure and balanced acidity make this wine approachable in youth yet capable of modest cellaring. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: Xige Estate  N50 (Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Gernischt) (With package)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Ningxia Cabernet blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Premium Chinese red.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's N50 is a full-bodied red blend from Ningxia's Qingtongxia subregion, built on Cabernet Sauvignon with Cabernet Gernischt. The 2020 vintage displays dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedary oak and black pepper spice. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with air, reflecting the wine's structure and aging potential. High tannins and medium-high acidity frame a wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Xige Estate  N28 Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Ningxia Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar oak, and structured tannins—a serious food wine.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's N28 Cabernet Sauvignon from Ningxia's Qingtongxia subregion delivers the hallmark structure and depth expected of premium Chinese Cabernet. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit anchor the primary profile, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide the backbone for extended cellaring and pairing with grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses. Tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge with time, adding complexity and savory dimension.

name: Xige Estate  Jade Dove Red (Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Ningxia Cabernet-Merlot blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's Jade Dove Red is a structured Bordeaux-style blend from Ningxia's Helan Mountain region, showcasing the terroir potential of China's premier wine-growing area. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedary oak and black pepper spice. Secondary oak and tobacco notes add complexity and aging potential, while tertiary leather and mineral character emerge on the palate. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine demands pairing with substantial dishes—grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged hard cheeses.

name: Xige Estate  N609 (Cabernet Sauvignon, Cabernet Gernischt and Malbec) (Wooden Case)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Ningxia Cabernet blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins. Premium wooden case presentation.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's N609 represents the Helan Mountain terroir through a structured blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Malbec. The 2020 vintage delivers a full-bodied profile anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, complemented by cedar and oak complexity from careful winemaking. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential and food compatibility. Presented in a wooden case, this wine reflects the producer's commitment to premium positioning within China's emerging fine wine landscape.

name: Xige Estate  First Label, Helan Mountain Ningxia (Wooden Case)
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Ningxia Cabernet-Carmenère blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's First Label from Helan Mountain showcases the potential of Ningxia's continental terroir. This 2017 blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Carmenère presents a full-bodied profile with prominent blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and oak complexity. High tannins provide structure and aging potential, while secondary tobacco and leather notes add sophistication. A serious, food-friendly red suited to premium dining.

name: Rocca di Frassinello  Poggio alla Guardia
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Super Tuscan blend of Sangiovese, Cabernet, and Merlot with balanced red fruit, oak complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Rocca di Frassinello's Poggio alla Guardia is a classically structured Super Tuscan that marries Sangiovese's regional character with the depth of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The blend delivers a medium-full body with ripe red and dark fruit on the nose—black cherry and plum—supported by oak-derived vanilla and subtle spice. The palate shows balanced tannin and medium acidity, with emerging earth and tobacco notes that suggest Tuscan terroir complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Velenosi  SOLESTÀ Limited Edition Rosso Piceno DOC Superiore
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano-Sangiovese blend from Rosso di Montalcino with dark fruit, spice, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Velenosi's SOLESTÀ Limited Edition Rosso Piceno DOC Superiore is a structured Montepulciano-Sangiovese blend from the Rosso di Montalcino subregion. The 2019 vintage expresses ripe dark cherry and plum on the nose, supported by earthy minerality and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannin provide food-friendly structure suitable for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations. A versatile Italian red with regional character and aging potential.

name: Segno Librandi Cirò Rosso Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Calabrian Gaglioppo with dark cherry, pepper spice, and mineral earth—a food-friendly classic from Cirò.
description (EN Store): Librandi's Segno Cirò Rosso Classico is a single-varietal expression of Gaglioppo, the indigenous red grape of Calabria's Cirò region. The 2019 vintage displays a medium-full frame with ripe dark plum and black cherry on the nose, layered with black pepper and herbaceous notes that speak to the grape's natural character. On the palate, moderate tannins and balanced acidity support flavors of red and dark fruit, with earthy minerality and subtle spice that evolve toward leather and dried-fruit complexity. A versatile food wine suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Marques De Riscal  Proximo Rioja
short_description (EN Store): Bold Tempranillo from Rioja Alavesa with ripe plum, leather, and vanilla oak—a structured, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Marqués de Riscal's Próximo Rioja showcases the classic character of Tempranillo from Spain's prestigious Rioja Alavesa subregion. This medium-full bodied red opens with dominant dark plum and red cherry fruit, supported by vanilla oak and subtle black pepper spice. The palate reveals good structure and medium tannins, with a dry finish and herbaceous undertones that reflect the region's terroir. Leather and tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, adding complexity and age-worthiness. A versatile food wine with broad appeal.

name: Donnafugata  Dea Vulcano Etna Rosso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Etna Rosso blend of Nerello Mascalese, Pinot Noir & Pecorino with volcanic minerality, red fruit, and elegant structure.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Dea Vulcano Etna Rosso DOC is a distinctive Sicilian red from the volcanic slopes of Mount Etna, blending Nerello Mascalese (the region's signature variety) with Pinot Noir, Nerello Cappuccio, and Pecorino. The wine expresses the terroir's mineral-rich volcanic soils through a layered profile: bright red cherry and dark plum on the nose, supported by black pepper spice and earthy undertones on the palate. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make it food-friendly across Mediterranean and grilled preparations. A wine of elegance and regional authenticity.

name: Luca & La Posta by Laura Catena  Luca Old Vine Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Old Vine Malbec from Mendoza with ripe blackberry, plum, and violet; smooth, structured, and elegant.
description (EN Store): Luca Old Vine Malbec represents the pinnacle of Argentine Malbec craftsmanship from Mendoza's prestigious vineyards. This single-varietal expression showcases the depth and elegance that mature vines impart, delivering a wine of medium-full body with refined tannin structure. The nose opens with ripe blackberry and dark plum, layered with violet florality and hints of cocoa. On the palate, the wine is smooth and well-integrated, with earth and spice notes emerging alongside vanilla oak influence. The balance between fruit intensity and structural sophistication makes this wine a compelling choice for serious collectors and food-pairing enthusiasts alike.

name: Bodega Aleanna El Enemigo  Cabernet Franc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Argentine Cabernet Franc with red cherry, herbaceous notes, and mineral structure—food-friendly and age-worthy.
description (EN Store): Bodega Aleanna's 2021 Cabernet Franc from Mendoza showcases the varietal's signature elegance and food-friendly character. Red cherry and plum dominate the nose, complemented by herbaceous and black pepper notes that speak to the region's terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannins frame a mineral-driven palate with emerging earthy complexity. This wine rewards near-term enjoyment but possesses the structure to develop further over the next 3–5 years, making it a versatile partner for grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Bodega Aleanna El Enemigo  Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec-led blend with dark fruit intensity, spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Bodega Aleanna's El Enemigo Malbec is a Mendoza-based red blend anchored by 90% Malbec, with supporting Cabernet Franc and Petit Verdot. The wine displays a medium-full body with dark plum and blackberry on the nose, complemented by cocoa and cedar from careful winemaking. Medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, while a mineral acidity keeps the palate fresh. Tertiary leather and tobacco notes suggest complexity and balance. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb dishes.

name: Bodega Aleanna  Gran Enemigo Gualtallary
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Franc–Malbec blend with dark fruit, herbaceous spice, and mineral structure.
description (EN Store): Bodega Aleanna's Gran Enemigo Gualtallary is a Mendoza red blend anchored by Cabernet Franc (85%) with Malbec support (15%), crafted in a medium-full bodied style with medium-high tannin and balanced acidity. The wine opens with primary notes of black cherry and red plum, layered with herbaceous and bell pepper character typical of cool-climate Cabernet Franc. Secondary mineral and cedar notes provide structure and complexity, while the blend's Malbec component adds dark fruit depth and spice. Food-friendly and age-worthy, this wine suits grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef preparations.

name: Bodega Aleanna El Enemigo As Bravas Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Malbec with dark plum, blackberry, and spice—a food-friendly, medium-full red for grilled meats and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): Bodega Aleanna's El Enemigo As Bravas Malbec is a classically structured Mendoza red built on a foundation of dark plum and blackberry fruit. The 2016 vintage shows the ripeness and depth characteristic of Argentine Malbec, with supporting cocoa and spice notes from careful winemaking. Tertiary tobacco and leather complexity suggest modest aging potential and versatility at the table. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine an approachable yet serious pairing for grilled red meat, braised beef, and herb-forward lamb dishes.

name: Tikal  Natural Organic Red Blend
short_description (EN Store): Smooth Argentine Malbec-Syrah blend with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa—elegant structure for premium red-meat dining.
description (EN Store): Tikal Natural Organic Red Blend from Ernesto Catena (Mendoza, 2018) marries Malbec and Syrah in a medium-full-bodied expression of Argentine terroir. Ripe blackberry and plum dominate the nose, supported by cocoa, spice, and subtle oak complexity. On the palate, medium-high tannins frame a smooth, round mouthfeel with layered dark-fruit character and mineral undertones. The wine's natural and organic production methods preserve varietal purity while the blend's structure suits grilled meats, braised beef, and aged charcuterie with equal grace.

name: Domaine Thibault Liger-Belair  Nuits Saint Georges La Charmotte
short_description (EN Store): Village NSG from 30–40 cm clay-over-limestone north of Nuits; biodynamic, horse-plowed, unfined — infusion philosophy over extraction.
description (EN Store): Thibault Liger-Belair is the first vigneron in nine generations of a family that once owned La Tâche Grand Cru — sold to DRC in 1929 — and operated exclusively as a négociant since 1720. When he founded the domaine in 2001, he made a deliberate break: farming the land himself, biodynamically, with horses pulling the plows. La Charmotte sits north of the Nuits-Saint-Georges village on soils of 30–40 cm clay over solid limestone bedrock, a profile that retains just enough moisture to buffer the warm season while the limestone drives the wine's mineral tension. In 2020 — a warm, early vintage — Thibault consciously pulled back on extraction, prioritising freshness and energy over concentration; the result is a village wine that punches above its classification in precision. Hand-harvested with triple sorting, fermented with native yeast in open-top vessels, and aged 14–18 months in barrel with a maximum one-third new oak (lightly charred, 5–7 minutes, from wood dried a minimum three years), the wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered. What sets La Charmotte apart at village level is the same cellar discipline applied to the domaine's Grand Crus: no racking, no aeration, sulfites sourced from a natural Polish mine. Winehog notes the 2020 shows fine promise, with the vintage's warmth held in check by the domaine's infusion-over-extraction philosophy.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard  Heritage Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Zinfandel blend with ripe dark fruit, black pepper spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Dry Creek Vineyard's Heritage Zinfandel is a Sonoma classic built on 86% Zinfandel with supporting Petite Sirah and Carignane. The 2020 vintage delivers ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, layered with black pepper and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured—ideal for grilled meats, BBQ, and hearty pasta dishes. A food-friendly expression of California's signature varietal.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard  Heritage Cabernet
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Sonoma Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—classic California elegance.
description (EN Store):
Dry Creek Vineyard's Heritage Cabernet Sauvignon is a full-bodied expression of Sonoma terroir, built on a foundation of ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while high tannins provide structure and aging potential. Secondary notes of black pepper and spice add complexity; tertiary tobacco and leather emerge with bottle age, rewarding patient cellaring.

This wine pairs beautifully with grilled red meat, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes, as well as aged hard cheeses.

name: Dry Creek Vineyard  Old Vine Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Zinfandel blend with ripe dark berries, spice, and food-friendly structure—ideal for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Dry Creek Vineyard's Old Vine Zinfandel showcases the classic Sonoma expression of this varietal: rich dark berry fruit, plum, and black cherry on the nose, with supporting notes of black pepper and warming spice. The 80% Zinfandel base is anchored by Petite Sirah, Carignane, and Alicante Bouschet, which add depth and tannin structure without overwhelming the wine's approachable medium-full body. Medium acidity and balanced tannins make this a versatile table wine, equally at home with grilled steaks, smoked meats, or hearty braised dishes.

name: Angeline Vineyards  California Pinot Noir
short_description (EN Store): California Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and food-friendly elegance.
description (EN Store): Angeline's 2022 California Pinot Noir delivers the varietal's signature balance of ripe red fruit and refined structure. Bright cherry and strawberry notes dominate the palate, supported by subtle oak influence and earthy undertones that add complexity. Medium-full body and approachable tannins make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or light pasta dishes. A well-crafted expression of California's Pinot Noir tradition.

name: Martin Ray  Pinot Noir Sonoma Coast
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir with red cherry, strawberry, and coastal minerality—food-friendly and refined.
description (EN Store): Martin Ray's 2021 Pinot Noir from Sonoma Coast showcases the elegance and balance characteristic of California's cooler coastal regions. Red cherry and strawberry dominate the primary profile, supported by subtle oak and earthy undertones that speak to the wine's terroir. The silky tannin structure and medium-full body make this a versatile food companion, equally at home with roasted poultry, mushroom risotto, or grilled preparations.

name: Chateau Palmer  Margaux 2017
short_description (EN Store): Biodynamic-certified 2017: Palmer's Merlot-on-gravel inversion of Médoc orthodoxy, unfined/unfiltered, aged in 225L barriques then 30hL foudres.
description (EN Store): When the Mähler-Besse and Sichel families' syndicate acquired Palmer in 1938, they inherited an estate already defying Médoc convention — 47% of the vineyard planted to Merlot on the Cantenac-Margaux plateau's finest Gunzian Garonne alluvial gravel croupes, the very parcels where orthodoxy demands Cabernet Sauvignon. That deliberate inversion is the engine of Palmer's identity. The 122 plots across Cantenac, Issan, and Margaux sit on 18 distinct gravel, sand, and clay soil types, with the 30-hectare plateau behind the château offering gravel-over-clay and the Issan sector delivering higher pure-gravel content; the Gironde estuary moderates temperature swings, extending phenolic ripening across vines averaging 38 years old, with select blocks exceeding 70. The 2017 vintage marks Palmer's first year of full biodynamic certification — a watershed that sharpened terroir transparency across the estate. Hand-harvested fruit was fermented plot-by-plot in 42 temperature-controlled stainless vats using indigenous yeasts, with no sulfur additions at harvest; the Grand Vin then aged 20–22 months total: the first year in 225-litre French oak barriques at under 50% new wood, the second in 30-hectolitre oak vats to integrate without over-oaking. The wine was bottled unfined and unfiltered. No other Third Growth in the Médoc plants Merlot at this proportion on premier gravel, then certifies biodynamically — 2017 is the precise vintage where both commitments converged for the first time.

name: Francis Coppola  'Diamond Collection' Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Sonoma Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—a classic American expression.
description (EN Store): Francis Coppola's Diamond Collection Cabernet Sauvignon from Alexander Valley showcases the hallmark richness and structure of Sonoma County fruit. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide backbone and aging potential, while tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge on the finish. A full-bodied, food-friendly red suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: Domaine de la Mordorée  "Lirac La Reine des Bois" Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-led Rhône South blend with ripe red fruit, garrigue spice, and mineral earth—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Domaine de la Mordoree's Lirac La Reine des Bois is a classic Rhône South blend anchored by Grenache with supporting Syrah and Mourvèdre. The 2018 vintage expresses the appellation's signature red and dark fruit character—ripe plum and blackberry—layered with garrigue herbs and black pepper spice from the warm southern Rhône terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine approachable yet structured, with subtle oak integration and emerging earthy minerality that speaks to the region's limestone-rich soils. A versatile food wine suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and herb-forward preparations.

name: Domaine de la Mordorée  Chateauneuf-du-Pape Cuvee de "La Reine des Bois"
short_description (EN Store): Châteauneuf-du-Pape from Domaine de la Mordorée: structured Rhône blend of dark fruit, garrigue, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Domaine de la Mordorée's Cuvée de La Reine des Bois is a classic Châteauneuf-du-Pape expression built on the traditional Rhône South blend of Grenache, Syrah, Mourvèdre, Counoise, and Vaccarèse. The 2021 vintage delivers a full-bodied wine with medium acidity and medium-high tannins, anchored by dark cherry and blackberry fruit with supporting notes of garrigue, black pepper, and mineral earth. Secondary oak and spice complexity emerge mid-palate, while tertiary leather and dried-fruit notes suggest aging potential. A food-friendly, structured wine suited to grilled meats, lamb, and slow-braised dishes.

name: Domaine de la Mordorée  Vin de France "La Remise" Rouge "Sans Sulfites"
short_description (EN Store): Grenache-Marselan blend from Rhône Valley; natural winemaking, no added sulfites. Rustic red fruit, spice, and mineral depth.
description (EN Store): Domaine de la Mordoree's La Remise is a natural red wine crafted from Grenache and Marselan grapes in the Rhône Valley. Produced without added sulfites, this wine reflects a commitment to minimal intervention winemaking. The blend delivers dark plum and red fruit on the nose, with herbaceous and spicy undertones that evolve toward earthy minerality on the palate. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it versatile at table, particularly suited to Mediterranean cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic preparations.

name: L'Archetipo  Primitivo IGP Salento
short_description (EN Store): Bold Salento Primitivo with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): L'Archetipo's Primitivo from Salento delivers a bold, fruit-forward expression of this southern Italian classic. Dark cherry and ripe raspberry dominate the palate, supported by subtle smoke and black pepper notes that add complexity and structure. The wine's medium-full body and rounded tannins create a smooth, approachable mouthfeel—eminently drinkable yet with enough character for food pairing. A versatile red suited to pizza, pasta, grilled meats, and casual entertaining.

name: Platinetti Guido  Ghemme DOCG Vigna Ronco Maso
short_description (EN Store): Piedmont blend of Nebbiolo, Barbera, Merlot & Bonarda with dark plum, berry depth & soft integrated tannins.
description (EN Store): Platinetti Guido's Ghemme DOCG Vigna Ronco Maso is a carefully composed Piedmont red blend anchored by Nebbiolo's structure and Barbera's acidity, with Merlot and Bonarda adding plum-forward fruit and textural softness. The 2018 vintage delivers a medium-full body with dominant dark plum and berry character on the nose, supported by subtle vanilla and herbaceous undertones. Tannins are well-integrated, creating a smooth palate that balances richness with approachability. This is a wine suited to classic Italian cuisine and grilled meats, where its fruit-forward profile and moderate tannin grip enhance rather than dominate the table.

name: Platinetti Guido  Colline Novaresi Barbera Pieleo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Barbera with bright cherry, peppery spice, and food-friendly structure—a versatile red for Italian classics.
description (EN Store): Platinetti Guido's Colline Novaresi Barbera Pieleo is a classically styled Piedmont red built on the varietal's signature combination of bright acidity and supple tannins. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and subtle oak influence. Earthy undertones and dried herb complexity emerge on the mid-palate, lending depth and regional character. Medium-full body and medium tannin structure make this wine food-friendly across a wide range of Italian and Mediterranean dishes.

name: I Vigneri  Vinupetra Etna Rosso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Etna Rosso from volcanic Sicily: dark fruit, mineral edge, food-friendly Nero d'Avola.
description (EN Store): I Vigneri's Vinupetra Etna Rosso DOC showcases the volcanic terroir of Mount Etna through Nero d'Avola, Sicily's signature red varietal. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark plum and black cherry fruit, layered with mineral-driven earth and subtle spice. Balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with grilled meats, Mediterranean herb-based dishes, or earthy mushroom preparations.

name: Shumi  Saperavi Iberiuli Premium
short_description (EN Store): Georgian Saperavi with dark plum, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Shumi's Saperavi Iberiuli Premium showcases the native Georgian varietal's signature dark plum and black cherry character, supported by peppery spice and subtle oak influence. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable yet age-worthy, with emerging earth and leather notes that deepen the palate. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Shumi  Kindzmarauli
short_description (EN Store): Georgian Saperavi with dark fruit, spice, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Shumi Kindzmarauli is a classic Georgian red wine built on Saperavi, the country's signature dark-skinned varietal. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark cherry and blackcurrant fruit, supported by structured tannins and a mineral-driven palate. Spice, earth, and subtle leather notes add complexity and age-worthiness, making this a versatile partner for grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Shumi  Saperavi Dry
short_description (EN Store): Georgian Saperavi with dark cherry, blackcurrant, and peppery spice—a food-friendly, structured red.
description (EN Store): Shumi Saperavi Dry is a single-varietal red from Georgia's indigenous Saperavi grape, a variety prized for its deep color, firm tannin structure, and age-worthiness. The wine displays dominant dark cherry and blackcurrant on the nose, supported by black pepper and subtle spice on the palate. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide backbone and grip, while earthy undertones and mineral finish add complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled lamb, beef stew, and herb-forward Mediterranean dishes.

name: Echo de Lynch Bages AOC Pauillac 2020
short_description (EN Store): 2020 Echo de Lynch Bages: Bages plateau gravel, first vintage in Pei's gravity-flow cellars — ripe power with fresh acidity from a sun-drenched year.
description (EN Store):
Echo de Lynch Bages is the second wine of a Cinquième Cru Classé that the market has long repriced as a Super Second — a gap between official rank and real-world performance that Jean-Michel Cazes's 15-year modernisation programme, begun in 1975, made impossible to ignore. The 2020 carries an additional distinction: it is the first vintage produced in the gravity-flow winery completed that same year, designed by Chien Chung 'Didi' Pei, where parcel-by-parcel fermentation across 80 temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats replaced the previous cellar's constraints.

The fruit originates from the Bages plateau, south-southwest of Pauillac, where deep Garonne gravel — deposited by slow Pyrenean erosion — sits over limestone sub-strata. That gravel profile forces vine roots deep, restricts water retention, and concentrates phenolics; the Gironde estuary moderates temperature swings that would otherwise compromise ripeness in cooler years. In 2020, a sun-ripened growing season delivered powerful tannins matching 2018 and 2019 in density, while retaining freshness and good acidity — an unusual combination for the vintage. Malolactic fermentation was split: one-third in vat, two-thirds in barrel, preserving textural complexity. Aging ran in French oak barriques at 75% new oak for approximately 15–18 months, the Winegrid in-barrel monitoring system — first deployed at Lynch-Bages in 2015, a Bordeaux first — tracking colour and sugar evolution in real time. As the second wine, Echo draws on declassified parcels and younger vines, delivering the house's cassis-tobacco-graphite signature at an earlier drinking window than the grand vin. Drink 2025–2033.

name: Chateau Ormes De Pez Saint Estephe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe with dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco—structured, age-worthy Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Château Ormes de Pez is a respected Cru Bourgeois estate in Saint-Estèphe, producing traditionally styled Bordeaux blends built for cellaring. This 2017 vintage combines Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc, and Petit Verdot into a full-bodied wine marked by blackcurrant and plum fruit, layered with cedar and tobacco complexity. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide structure and aging potential, while secondary oak and tertiary earth notes add depth. Ideal for serious red-meat cuisine and patient collectors.

name: Confidences De Prieure-Linchine 2019 "Le 2nd Vin De Chateau Prieure-Lichine"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Margaux second wine: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco with age-worthy structure and refined tannins.
description (EN Store): Confidences de Prieuré-Lichine is the second wine of Château Prieuré-Lichine, a Margaux estate renowned for elegant, structured Bordeaux. This 2019 blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot delivers a full-bodied expression with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, complemented by pronounced cedar and tobacco notes from careful oak aging. The wine's high tannins and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential, while emerging leather and slate minerality suggest complexity to come. A refined, classically styled Margaux that rewards patience.

name: Monemvasia	 Laloudi
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Greek Agiorgitiko with ripe red fruit, black pepper spice, and mineral-driven finish. Food-friendly Mediterranean red.
description (EN Store): Monemvasia Laloudi is a single-varietal expression of Agiorgitiko, Greece's signature red grape, sourced from the Peloponnese. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannins and a mineral-driven palate. Ripe red cherry and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by black pepper and herbaceous notes on the mid-palate. Earthy undertones and dried-fruit complexity emerge on the finish, reflecting the grape's natural affinity for Mediterranean terroir. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and rustic Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Monemvasia	 Voltes Red
short_description (EN Store): Greek Peloponnese blend of Agiorgitiko and Mavroudi; medium-full red with cherry, plum, and peppery spice.
description (EN Store): Monemvasia Voltes Red is a Peloponnese blend anchored by Agiorgitiko and Mavroudi, two indigenous Greek varieties known for producing food-friendly reds with structure and regional character. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe red and dark fruit on the nose—cherry and plum—complemented by black pepper and herbaceous notes that reflect the Mediterranean terroir. A mineral, earthy undertone emerges on the palate, adding complexity and length. This is a versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, lamb, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Monemvasia	 300 Red
short_description (EN Store): Peloponnese red blend of Agiorgitiko and Mavroudi; medium-full body, ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Monemvasia 300 Red is a Greek red blend from the Peloponnese, combining Agiorgitiko and Mavroudi grapes in a medium-full-bodied expression. The 2009 vintage displays ripe red and dark plum fruit on the nose, with supporting black pepper and subtle oak influence. On the palate, the wine reveals layered complexity: primary cherry and plum give way to secondary spice and herbaceous notes, while tertiary leather, tobacco, and earthy minerality emerge with bottle age. Medium tannins and balanced acidity make this a food-friendly choice for Mediterranean and rustic cuisine.

name: Fontanafredda  Dolcetto Briccotondo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Dolcetto with ripe cherry, plum, and mineral notes. Food-friendly, approachable, and versatile.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Dolcetto Briccotondo DOC is a classic expression of Piedmont's most approachable red varietal. Ripe red cherry and dark plum dominate the nose, supported by subtle spice and earthy minerality. The palate is soft and round, with medium tannins that frame the fruit without demanding extended cellaring. A whisper of leather and tobacco emerges on the finish, adding complexity to an otherwise fruit-forward profile. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with pasta, roasted chicken, or cured meats.

name: Fontanafredda  Barbera Briccotondo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Barbera with ripe cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store):
Fontanafredda's Barbera Briccotondo DOC is a classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal, sourced from the Barolo subregion. The wine opens with bright red cherry and plum fruit, supported by black pepper spice and subtle oak influence. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make it approachable yet structured enough for food pairing. The finish carries earthy undertones and herbaceous notes, characteristic of quality Barbera from this terroir.

This is a versatile table wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes, offering excellent value for everyday enjoyment and special occasions alike.

name: Fontanafredda  Langhe Rosso Eremo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Piedmont blend of Barbera and Nebbiolo with red fruit, spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Langhe Rosso Eremo is a Piedmont red blend uniting Barbera's bright cherry character with Nebbiolo's structural depth. The wine opens with red fruit and dark plum, supported by herbaceous and black pepper notes that reflect the region's cool-climate terroir. Subtle earth and leather undertones emerge on the finish, offering complexity suited to both casual dining and more formal table settings. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food wine across Italian and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Fontanafredda  Barbera D'Alba Raimonda DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Barbera from Piedmont with bright cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Barbera d'Alba Raimonda is a classic expression of Piedmont's signature red varietal. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and dark plum, supported by black pepper spice and subtle earthy minerality. Medium-full body and medium tannins provide structure without heaviness, while bright acidity ensures freshness and food compatibility. A versatile table wine suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and casual entertaining.

name: Fontanafredda  Langhe Nebbiolo Ebbio DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Langhe with cherry, cedar, and herbal complexity. Age-worthy, structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Langhe Nebbiolo Ebbio DOC is a classically structured Piedmont red showcasing the varietal's signature cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide framework for extended cellaring, while balanced acidity ensures freshness. Tar and dried-fruit tertiary complexity emerge with bottle age, making this wine a compelling choice for serious collectors and food-pairing enthusiasts alike.

name: Fontanafredda  Barolo Del Commune di Serralunga D'Alba DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Nebbiolo from Serralunga d'Alba with cherry, licorice, and cedar—age-worthy Barolo with structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Barolo Del Commune di Serralunga d'Alba is a classically structured Nebbiolo from one of Piedmont's most prestigious subregions. The 2019 vintage displays the varietal's signature cherry and licorice aromatics, layered with herbaceous and cedar notes that speak to the terroir's mineral complexity. Full-bodied with high tannins and medium-high acidity, this wine possesses the backbone and age-worthiness expected of serious Barolo, with emerging tar and dried fruit character that will deepen over the next decade.

name: Fontanafredda  Barolo DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Barolo's heartland: structured cherry, cedar, and tar with age-worthy tannin.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's 2020 Barolo DOCG is a classically structured Nebbiolo from Piedmont's most prestigious region. The wine opens with dominant cherry and red fruit character, layered with herbaceous notes that define the primary expression. Cedar and licorice emerge on the mid-palate, supported by the varietal's signature rose undertones. With bottle age, tertiary complexity unfolds: tar, tobacco, and dried fruit develop alongside fine-grained tannins, creating a wine built for the cellar. High acidity and full body anchor a long, mineral finish.

name: Casarena Reservado Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Argentinian Malbec from Luján de Cuyo with dark fruit depth, cocoa spice, and structured tannins—a food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store): Casarena Reservado Malbec is a single-varietal expression from Mendoza's prestigious Luján de Cuyo subregion, where Malbec thrives in high-altitude terroir. The wine displays a rich core of dark plum and black cherry, layered with cocoa and warming spice on the mid-palate. Medium-high tannins provide structure and aging potential, while emerging tertiary notes of leather and tobacco suggest complexity. A versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and cured charcuterie.

name: Casarena Reservado Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Cabernet Sauvignon with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Casarena Reservado Cabernet Sauvignon from Luján de Cuyo, Mendoza, showcases the region's signature dark-fruit intensity and mineral backbone. This single-varietal expression balances ripe blackcurrant and plum with herbaceous undertones and subtle cedar oak influence, delivering medium-full body and approachable tannin structure. Suitable for immediate enjoyment or modest cellaring, the wine pairs naturally with grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Casarena DNA Malbec
short_description (EN Store): 100% Malbec from Luján de Cuyo. Dark fruit, cocoa spice, and structured tannin—a food-friendly Argentine classic.
description (EN Store): Casarena DNA Malbec is a 100% varietal expression from Luján de Cuyo, Mendoza's premier subregion for Malbec. The 2020 vintage delivers the signature dark fruit intensity and structured tannin profile that define quality Argentine Malbec: ripe plum and blackberry on the nose, layered with cocoa and black pepper on the palate. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin make this wine a natural partner for grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb. The secondary cocoa and spice notes also bridge to cured meats and mushroom-forward dishes, offering versatility across the table.

name: Casarena Icono Blend
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Mendoza blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Malbec with blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Casarena Icono Blend is a full-bodied red from Luján de Cuyo in Mendoza, Argentina, blending Cabernet Sauvignon and Malbec to create a wine of considerable structure and depth. The 2020 vintage displays dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, complemented by cedar and oak-derived spice. High tannins and medium-high acidity provide a firm backbone, with tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerging as the wine opens. This is a serious, age-worthy expression suited to substantial dishes and patient cellaring.

name: Penfolds  Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz (750 ml) Limited Relese Lunar New Year Snake Edition
short_description (EN Store): Bin 389's signature: Cabernet/Shiraz aged in barrels that previously held Grange — the 'Baby Grange' in a Year of the Snake limited release.
description (EN Store):
Penfolds Bin 389 earned its 'Baby Grange' reputation through a single deliberate decision: rather than sourcing new oak, the winemaking team matures each vintage in barrels previously used to age Grange (Bin 95) — hogsheads already seasoned with the house's most concentrated Barossa Shiraz. That residual oak character, absorbed over Grange's 18–20-month tenure, transfers a layer of complexity that new wood alone cannot replicate.

The 2021 fruit draws from Penfolds' deep Barossa Valley holdings, anchored by the Kalimna property — 153 ha of ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils that have never been touched by phylloxera, allowing ungrafted vines to reach extraordinary age and root depth. These free-draining soils concentrate the Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz components, delivering mid-palate density without sacrificing structure.

The blend follows Penfolds' multi-regional philosophy: Cabernet Sauvignon and Shiraz co-fermented and blended across South Australian districts, with acclimatised pure yeast cultures and temperature-controlled fermentation — a technique pioneered at Penfolds by Ray Beckwith. The result is a wine that sits architecturally between Grange's power and St Henri's restraint.

This 2021 expression is presented in a Lunar New Year Snake Edition — a limited-release format that does not alter the wine inside, making it a collectible vessel for a production-identical Bin 389. Drink 2025–2035.

name: Di Majo Norante Ramitello Biferno Rosso DOC
short_description (EN Store): Molise red blend of Montepulciano & Aglianico—medium-full body, dark fruit, peppery spice & earthy minerality. Food-friendly classic.
description (EN Store):
Di Majo Norante Ramitello Biferno Rosso DOC is a structured red from Molise, Italy's underrated southern region. This 80% Montepulciano, 20% Aglianico blend delivers medium-full body with balanced tannin and acidity, making it a versatile food wine.

On the nose: dark cherry, plum, and blackberry with black pepper spice. The palate shows ripe red fruit, earthy minerality, and subtle leather notes that suggest modest aging potential. A dry, savory finish with lingering herb and mineral character.

Ideal for grilled meats, braised beef, tomato-based pasta, and mushroom dishes. Serve at 16–18°C.

name: Idi Di Marzo  Vulgaris Montepulciano D'Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure—a versatile Italian red for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Idi di Marzo's Vulgaris Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC is a classically styled red from Italy's Abruzzo region, built on the native Montepulciano grape. The wine opens with dominant red cherry and dark plum fruit, supported by earthy minerality and subtle spice. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make it approachable yet structured enough for hearty dishes. A touch of herbaceous character and emerging tobacco notes add complexity without overshadowing the fruit-forward profile. Ideal for pasta, grilled meats, and rustic Mediterranean fare.

name: Idi Di Marzo Amarne Montepulciano D'Abruzzo DOC - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Montepulciano blend from Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, peppery spice, and earthy minerality—food-friendly Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Idi di Marzo's Amarne Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC is an organic red wine rooted in the Abruzzo region's winemaking tradition. This blend of Montepulciano, Primitivo, and Negroamaro delivers a medium-full body with balanced tannins and a vibrant acidity that frames ripe red cherry and dark plum fruit. Herbaceous notes and black pepper spice add complexity, while earthy undertones and dried fruit character suggest careful aging. A versatile, food-friendly expression suited to Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and tomato-based dishes.

name: Idi Di Marzo Atto Finale - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Abruzzo blend of Montepulciano, Primitivo & Negroamaro—dark fruit, spice & earthy complexity.
description (EN Store): Idi di Marzo's Atto Finale is an organic red blend from Montepulciano d'Abruzzo, combining Montepulciano, Primitivo, and Negroamaro. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark plum and blackberry fruit, supported by herbaceous and spice notes from the blend's southern Italian components. Subtle earth and tobacco undertones emerge on the finish, reflecting the wine's complexity and food-friendly structure. Ideal for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Pajaru Primitivo Solu IGT - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Primitivo from Salento with bold dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice—smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Pajaru's Primitivo Solu IGT is an organic red from Puglia's warm Salento subregion, delivering a bold nose and palate filled with dark fruit, black cherry, and raspberry. Smoke and black pepper notes add complexity and spice, while fully rounded tannins provide structure without harshness. This medium-full-bodied wine is smooth and approachable, making it an excellent match for tomato-based pasta, pizza, grilled meats, and casual comfort food. A versatile, easy-drinking expression of Primitivo that punches above its price point.

name: Pajaru Nero Di Troia Auru IGT - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Nero di Troia from Puglia: dark fruit, smoke & spice with smooth, rounded tannins. Food-friendly Southern Italian red.
description (EN Store): Pajaru's Nero di Troia Auru IGT is an organic red wine from Salento in Puglia, showcasing the bold character of this indigenous Southern Italian varietal. The wine opens with dominant dark fruit and black cherry, supported by notes of smoke and black pepper that add complexity and structure. Medium-full body and balanced medium tannins create a smooth, rounded palate that drinks well across a range of occasions—from casual pizza and pasta nights to grilled meats and braised dishes. The spice-forward profile and organic certification reflect a commitment to natural winemaking in one of Italy's most dynamic wine regions.

name: Pajaru Negroamaro Paritaru IGT - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Negroamaro from Salento with bold dark fruit, pepper spice, and smooth tannins—a food-friendly Puglian red.
description (EN Store): Pajaru's Negroamaro Paritaru IGT is an organic red from Salento, Puglia, showcasing the rustic character and approachability of southern Italian winemaking. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark fruit and red fruit, followed by a palate marked by black pepper spice, subtle smoke, and a touch of earthiness. Medium-full body and smooth, rounded tannins make this wine eminently drinkable across a range of casual and everyday occasions—from pizza and pasta to grilled meats and Mediterranean fare.

name: Pajaru Primitivo Picuraru IGT - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Primitivo from Salento with bold dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice—smooth, food-friendly, and eminently drinkable.
description (EN Store): Pajaru's Primitivo Picuraru IGT is an organic red from Puglia's Salento subregion, delivering the bold, fruit-forward character Primitivo is known for. The wine opens with lashings of dark fruit—cherry and raspberry—supported by subtle smoke and black pepper notes that add complexity without overwhelming the palate. Fully rounded tannins and a smooth finish make this wine approachable and versatile at the table, equally at home with pizza and pasta as with grilled meats and casual comfort fare.

name: Pajaru Meridionale Tre Tenute - Organic
short_description (EN Store): Organic Primitivo from Salento with bold dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice. Smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Pajaru Meridionale Tre Tenute is an organic Primitivo from Salento in Puglia, delivering the warm-climate generosity this southern Italian region is known for. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark cherry and raspberry, followed by a palate filled with ripe red fruit, subtle smoke, and a touch of black pepper spice. Fully rounded tannins and a smooth finish make this wine immediately approachable, yet with enough structure to pair confidently with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, and Mediterranean dishes. An excellent value expression of Primitivo's fruit-forward character.

name: Albert Bichot  Gevrey Chambertin 1er Cru "Petite Chapelle"
short_description (EN Store): Gevrey 1er Cru Petite Chapelle: cold-soaked 6–8 days, Tronçais oak, organic-certified 2018 estate fruit from Côte de Nuits marl-gravel soils.
description (EN Store): When Albéric Bichot committed the entire estate portfolio to organic certification from the 2018 vintage onward, Petite Chapelle became one of the first expressions to carry that shift — meaning this bottle is, by definition, from the inaugural certified harvest. Petite Chapelle sits on the southern flank of Gevrey-Chambertin's Premier Cru belt, where shallow marl over gravel drains rapidly and forces Pinot Noir roots deep, concentrating mineral tension rather than volume. Fruit is handpicked and subjected to a cold soak of 6–8 days before fermentation in thermoregulated conical oak vats, with total maceration of 18–20 days — long enough to extract structural tannin from the gravel-influenced skins without bruising the site's characteristic iron-edged fruit. Aging follows in French oak (Tronçais and Allier forests, light toast, 20–30% new oak) for 12–16 months, a deliberately restrained regime that amplifies climat character rather than layering wood. What distinguishes Bichot structurally is that this wine is vinified at Domaine du Clos Frantin in Nuits-Saint-Georges — the estate physically closest to the Côte de Nuits fruit — rather than at a centralised Beaune facility, preserving micro-terroir fidelity at négociant scale. The 2018 vintage delivered warm, concentrated growing conditions across the Côte de Nuits; expect density and depth with genuine ageing potential through the late 2020s.

name: Albert Bichot  Chambolle-Musigny 1er Cru "les Chabiots"
short_description (EN Store): Chambolle-Musigny 1er Cru from a négociant vinifying at-source; 18–20-day maceration, Tronçais oak, organic-certified from 2018.
description (EN Store): Albéric Bichot made a structural decision that most large Burgundy houses have not: rather than centralising vinification in Beaune, he built six geographically dispersed estate wineries, each processing only the fruit of its own sub-region. The Chambolle-Musigny Les Chabiots passes through Domaine du Clos Frantin in the Côte de Nuits, where marl and gravel soils over Jurassic limestone bedrock deliver the tension that separates Chambolle's Premier Crus from the village tier. Les Chabiots sits on the southern flank of the appellation, where the slope aspect and shallow topsoil concentrate Pinot Noir's aromatic intensity without sacrificing the commune's characteristic finesse. Vinification follows a cold soak of 6–8 days, then 18–20 days total maceration in thermoregulated conical oak vats, followed by 12–16 months in light-toast Tronçais and Allier barrels at 20–30% new oak — calibrated to amplify climat character rather than impose wood. Gravity racking is used throughout to minimise handling. What distinguishes this cuvée within the Bichot portfolio is its organic certification from the 2018 vintage onward, applied across all estate domaine wines — rare discipline for a house operating at this scale. The 2019 vintage delivered ripe, structured Pinot Noir with concentration and depth; the wine rewards 2–3 years of further cellaring but is already showing the floral lift and mineral grip that define Les Chabiots.

name: Château Morin  Saint Estephe
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Estèphe with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins. Food-friendly Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Château Morin's 2015 Saint-Estèphe exemplifies the appellation's signature structure and elegance. A traditional Bordeaux blend dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon, this wine displays ripe blackcurrant and plum on the nose, with supporting cedar and spice notes. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced, medium tannins that frame dark fruit and mineral undertones. Secondary oak aging imparts subtle tobacco and leather complexity, while tertiary development adds earthy sophistication. A food-friendly expression of the Left Bank, suitable for cellaring or near-term enjoyment.

name: Château Citran  Cru Bourgeois Haut Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc Bordeaux blend: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and herbaceous depth. Food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Citran's 2019 Haut-Médoc is a classically proportioned Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (60%), with supporting Merlot and Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and plum fruit, layered with cedar and black pepper from careful oak aging. A herbaceous spine runs through the mid-palate, while tertiary tobacco and earth notes emerge on the finish, suggesting modest bottle age potential. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food wine suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and lamb preparations.

name: La Demoiselle de Sociando Mallet Second wine of Château Sociando Mallet
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Haut-Médoc second wine: Merlot-led Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): La Demoiselle de Sociando Mallet is the second wine of Château Sociando Mallet, a respected Haut-Médoc producer. This 2013 vintage blends 87% Merlot with 13% Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a medium-full-bodied Bordeaux with approachable structure and food-friendly balance. Dark plum and black cherry dominate the primary profile, supported by cedar and subtle oak in the secondary layer. Earthy minerality and graphite notes emerge on the finish, reflecting the terroir of this classic right-bank Bordeaux subregion. A versatile pairing for grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: L”Orme De Rauzan Gassies Haut Medoc
short_description (EN Store): Classic Haut-Médoc with ripe blackcurrant, cedar spice, and structured tannins. Food-friendly Bordeaux for red meat and aged cheese.
description (EN Store): Château Rauzan-Gassies 2015 Haut-Médoc presents the hallmark structure and elegance of the Left Bank. Dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit form the core, supported by cedar and subtle oak spice from élevage. The palate shows medium-full body with balanced tannins and a mineral backbone typical of the appellation. Secondary tobacco and earth notes emerge with air, suggesting modest cellaring potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled beef, lamb, and herb-roasted poultry.

name: Château Du Couvent  Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend with ripe dark fruit, silky tannins, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly Right Bank classic.
description (EN Store): Château Du Couvent's 2020 Pomerol exemplifies the appellation's hallmark softness and depth. Dominated by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon, this medium-full-bodied red opens with dark plum and blackcurrant, underpinned by subtle oak and mineral undertones. The palate reveals supple tannins and a layered structure that evolves toward earthy, tobacco-tinged complexity. A versatile expression suited to both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Bernard Magrez l'Ame de Pape Clement
short_description (EN Store): Pessac-Léognan Bordeaux blend of dark plum, blackcurrant, and cedar—a food-friendly classic with balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Bernard Magrez's l'Ame de Pape Clement is a Pessac-Léognan red blend anchored by Merlot (44%), Cabernet Sauvignon (36%), and Cabernet Franc (20%). The 2019 vintage expresses ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, with supporting cedar and tobacco from oak aging. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while retaining the mineral complexity and dried-fruit undertones typical of Left Bank Bordeaux. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, or aged cheeses.

name: Calice De Faugeres Saint Emilion Grand Cru Second wine of Chateau Faugeres
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion second wine with dark plum, cedar, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly Bordeaux blend.
description (EN Store): Calice de Faugères is the second wine of Château Faugères, a respected Saint-Émilion Grand Cru estate. This 2017 vintage presents a classic Right Bank Bordeaux profile: ripe dark plum and black cherry fruit on the nose, supported by subtle cedar and tobacco notes from careful oak aging. The palate is medium-full bodied with balanced tannins and a mineral, slate-driven finish that speaks to the terroir of Saint-Émilion's limestone plateau. A food-friendly expression that rewards pairing with grilled red meat, braised beef, or herb-crusted lamb.

name: Chateau d'Aiguilhe Castillon Cotes De Bordeaux
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux blend with ripe plum, cedar, and mineral complexity. Food-friendly Right Bank style.
description (EN Store): Château d'Aiguilhe's 2013 Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux exemplifies the refined, food-friendly character of this emerging Right Bank appellation. A classic Bordeaux blend dominated by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon, the wine displays medium-full body and balanced tannin structure. On the nose, ripe red plum and black cherry lead, with secondary cedar and herbaceous notes adding complexity. The palate reveals cassis, subtle oak integration, and a mineral graphite finish that speaks to the region's clay-limestone terroir. Approachable in youth yet structured for moderate cellaring.

name: Relais De La Dominique Saint Emilion Grand Cru Second wine of Chateau La Dominique
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion Merlot with ripe plum, black cherry, and soft chocolate—the second wine of Château La Dominique.
description (EN Store): Relais de la Dominique is the second wine of Château La Dominique, a prestigious Saint-Émilion Grand Cru estate. This 2015 vintage showcases the classic elegance of Right Bank Bordeaux, built on a foundation of 100% Merlot. The wine opens with dominant black cherry and plum aromatics, supported by subtle red fruit and berry complexity. On the palate, ripe stone fruit and dark plum flavors are complemented by soft vanilla and cocoa undertones from careful oak aging. The tannins are well-integrated and approachable, delivering a medium-full body with balanced acidity and a lingering finish. This is a wine of refinement and restraint—ideal for those seeking the character of Saint-Émilion without the premium price of the grand vin.

name: Ballade De La Pointe Pomerol Second wine of Chateau La Pointe
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol second wine: ripe plum, cherry, and cocoa with silky tannins and balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Ballade de la Pointe is the second wine of Château La Pointe, a distinguished Pomerol estate. This 2016 blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc delivers a medium-full-bodied profile anchored by ripe dark plum and black cherry fruit, complemented by subtle cocoa and vanilla notes from careful oak aging. The palate shows juicy berry character with well-integrated tannins and a hint of herbaceous complexity, finishing with elegant structure and balance. A refined expression of Pomerol's signature softness and depth.

name: Pomerol By Clinet
