short_description (EN Store): Pomerol's rare Cab Sauv holdout: Günz gravel & crasse de fer iron subsoil, 28–30-day maceration, unfined/unfiltered — structural precision few Right Bank estates attempt.
description (EN Store):
When Ronan Laborde took stewardship of Clinet in 2004, he made a deliberate choice that runs against the grain of modern Pomerol: rather than replant the estate's Cabernet Sauvignon toward the appellation's dominant Merlot monoculture, he retained it — 12–20% depending on vintage — as the structural and mineral spine of the Grand Vin. That decision is rooted in the terroir itself. Clinet sits at the highest point of the Pomerol plateau (35 metres), where Günz gravel over deep clay with iron-rich crasse de fer subsoil delivers drainage, heat retention, and a graphite-mineral precision that purely clay-bound neighbours cannot replicate.

Vinification proceeds plot by plot across 21 parcels, with maceration of approximately 28–30 days for slow, thorough extraction. Malolactic fermentation is completed in French oak barrel, and the wine ages 16–22 months in approximately 75–80% new French oak. Press wine is kept separate and incorporated at final blending only if additional structure is warranted. The wine is bottled neither fined nor filtered, preserving the iron-mineral imprint of the crasse de fer in the glass. The 2013 vintage — a cooler, structurally precise year on the Right Bank — produced a leaner, more graphite-driven expression of Clinet than the opulent 2009 or 2018, with the Cabernet Sauvignon component lending particular definition. Approachable now with decanting; best drinking window extends through the late 2020s.

name: L'Esprit de Chevalier Pessac Leognan Second wine of Domaine de Chevalier
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pessac-Léognan second wine: Cabernet-Merlot blend with red fruit, cedar, and refined structure.
description (EN Store):
L'Esprit de Chevalier is the second wine of Domaine de Chevalier, one of Pessac-Léognan's most respected estates. This 2020 blend of 65% Cabernet Sauvignon and 35% Merlot delivers the hallmark elegance and food-friendliness of the appellation in a more approachable format than the grand vin.

On the nose, red currant and plum lead, with hints of cassis and subtle cedar. The palate shows medium-full body with balanced, refined tannins and good acidity. Secondary notes of herb and graphite minerality add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. This is a wine built for the table—versatile with roasted meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Les Hauts de Lynch-Moussas Second wine of Chateau Lynch-Moussas
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux second wine: dark fruit, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Food-friendly medium-full body.
description (EN Store):
Les Hauts de Lynch-Moussas is the second wine of Château Lynch-Moussas, a Haut-Médoc estate with a heritage of balanced, age-worthy Bordeaux blends. This 2018 vintage combines 76% Cabernet Sauvignon with 24% Merlot, delivering a medium-full-bodied wine with medium tannin structure.

On the nose and palate, dark cherry and blackcurrant dominate, supported by cedar and subtle spice from oak aging. With time in glass, tertiary notes of tobacco and leather emerge, reflecting the wine's capacity for graceful evolution. The medium acidity and integrated tannins make this an approachable yet serious food wine, suitable for cellaring 5–10 years or enjoying now with hearty fare.

name: Les Fiefs De Lagrange Saint-Julien Second wine of Chateau Lagrange
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux second wine: blackcurrant, cedar, structured tannins. Classic Saint-Julien food pairing.
description (EN Store):
Les Fiefs de Lagrange is the second wine of Château Lagrange, a prestigious Saint-Julien estate. This 2013 vintage blends 61% Cabernet Sauvignon, 30% Merlot, and 9% Petit Verdot into a medium-full-bodied red with classic Bordeaux structure.


On the nose, blackcurrant and black cherry dominate, supported by subtle cedar and oak from careful winemaking. The palate shows ripe dark fruit, balanced acidity, and medium tannins that frame the wine without overwhelming. Emerging tertiary notes of tobacco and earth suggest graceful aging potential.


A food-friendly expression of Saint-Julien terroir, ideal for grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes.

name: Duluc De Branaire Ducru Saint-Julien Second wine of Chateau Branaire-Ducru
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Julien second wine: Cabernet-Merlot blend with red fruit, cedar, and refined tannin structure.
description (EN Store): Duluc de Branaire Ducru is the second wine of Château Branaire-Ducru, a respected Saint-Julien estate. This 2019 blend of 52.5% Cabernet Sauvignon, 44% Merlot, and minor Petit Verdot and Cabernet Franc delivers a medium-full-bodied profile with balanced acidity and refined tannins. The wine opens with red currant and plum, supported by subtle cedar and graphite minerality, with a herbaceous undertone typical of Left Bank Bordeaux. Approachable yet structured, it offers excellent food versatility and represents strong value for a classified-growth second label.

name: Sarget De Gruaud Larose Saint-Julien Second wine of Chateau Gruaud Larose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Julien second wine with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring.
description (EN Store): Sarget de Gruaud Larose is the second wine of Château Gruaud Larose, a prestigious Saint-Julien estate. This 2016 Bordeaux blend showcases the classic structure and aging potential of the appellation. Ripe blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by cedary oak and subtle tobacco notes that emerge with air. Full-bodied and high in tannin, the wine displays the mineral backbone and earthy undertones characteristic of Left Bank Bordeaux. Designed for medium-term cellaring, it rewards patience with evolving complexity.

name: Chateau Cos Labory  Saint Estephe 5Ème Cru Classé 2021
short_description (EN Store): Left Bank Bordeaux classic: structured Cabernet-led blend with dark fruit, cedar, and mineral grip. Elegant Saint-Estèphe from a 5ème Cru estate.
description (EN Store): Château Cos Labory's 2021 Saint-Estèphe represents the Left Bank's signature style: a Cabernet Sauvignon–dominant blend (60%) anchored by Merlot (33%) and Petit Verdot (7%), delivering medium-full body with refined tannin structure. The wine opens with dark fruit—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar and black pepper, evolving toward tobacco and leather on the mid-palate. Minerality and graphite notes underscore the terroir of this 5ème Cru Classé estate, offering both immediate approachability and cellaring potential. A food-friendly classic suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau Pibran Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac classic: dark plum, cocoa, and integrated tannins. Elegant Left Bank structure with soft, approachable texture.
description (EN Store): Château Pibran's 2016 Pauillac exemplifies the refined power of the Left Bank. A Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend, this wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry, supported by cocoa and cedar notes that speak to careful oak aging. The palate is medium-full and velvety, with well-integrated tannins that promise graceful evolution. Tertiary tobacco and leather notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit. A wine of balance and pedigree, suited to serious cellaring or immediate enjoyment with substantial fare.

name: Château Montviel  Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Right Bank Pomerol: ripe dark fruit, silky tannins, elegant structure. Merlot-led blend showing classic Bordeaux depth.
description (EN Store): Château Montviel's 2016 Pomerol exemplifies the appellation's signature elegance. The 80% Merlot base delivers ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, supported by 20% Cabernet Franc that adds structure and herbal complexity. Medium-full body with refined tannins and balanced acidity creates a wine that is approachable now yet capable of graceful aging. Cedar and subtle tobacco notes emerge on the mid-palate, while earthy minerality provides textural depth. A food-friendly Right Bank classic suited to fine dining and special occasions.

name: Chateau Lagrezette Paragon Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied 2013 Malbec from Cahors with ripe plum, violet, and dark chocolate—structured for premium red meat.
description (EN Store): Château Lagrezette's Paragon Malbec is a full-bodied expression of the Cahors terroir, built on a foundation of ripe dark fruit and mineral structure. The 2013 vintage displays dominant blackberry and plum character on the nose, complemented by violet florality and cocoa notes from French oak aging. Medium-high tannins provide grip and aging potential, while subtle earth and spice notes add complexity. This is a wine built for the table—particularly suited to grilled red meat, braised beef, and game preparations where its richness and structure can shine.

name: Chateau Talbot  Saint Julien 4Ème Cru Classé
short_description (EN Store): Classic Saint-Julien 4ème Cru with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—age-worthy Bordeaux elegance.
description (EN Store): Château Talbot's 2021 Saint-Julien expresses the hallmark structure and refinement of a fourth-growth Bordeaux. A Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend, it opens with ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar and graphite minerality. The palate is full-bodied and tightly wound, with high tannins that promise graceful evolution over the next 15–20 years. Secondary notes of tobacco leaf and black pepper emerge mid-palate, while tertiary leather and earth tones suggest the wine's age-worthiness. A classic Left Bank expression built for the cellar and the table.

name: Chateau Leoville Poyferre Saint-Julien Grand Cru Classé 2017
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Julien Bordeaux blend with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Age-worthy Grand Cru Classé.
description (EN Store): Château Léoville Poyferré's 2017 Saint-Julien exemplifies the elegance and structure of Left Bank Bordeaux. A Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot blend, this Grand Cru Classé displays a layered profile: ripe blackcurrant and dark plum on the nose, supported by cedar and graphite minerality. The palate reveals firm, well-integrated tannins and a backbone of acidity that promises graceful evolution over two decades. Tertiary notes of tobacco leaf and leather emerge with air, suggesting complexity to come. A wine of classical proportions, suited to contemplative cellaring or pairing with substantial cuisine.

name: Vigneti del Salento  Zolla Primitivo Di Manduria Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Primitivo Riserva from Manduria with dark fruit, smoke, and peppery spice—a bold, food-friendly Southern Italian red.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Salento's Zolla Primitivo di Manduria Riserva is a full-bodied expression of Puglia's signature varietal, showcasing the warm-climate intensity and ripe fruit character that define the region. The wine opens with a bold nose of dark cherry and plum, followed by layers of smoke and black pepper that add complexity and structure. On the palate, lashings of red and dark fruit are balanced by medium-high tannins and a smooth, rounded finish. This is a wine built for the table—equally at home with grilled meats, tomato-based pasta, or pizza. The spice and smoke notes suggest affinity with charred preparations and cured meats, while the overall drinkability makes it an approachable choice for everyday enjoyment.

name: Fantini Montepulciano D'Abruzzo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe dark fruit, peppery spice, and earthy minerality—a food-friendly Italian classic.
description (EN Store): Fantini's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a medium-full-bodied red wine that showcases the signature character of this Adriatic region. The wine opens with ripe red and dark plum fruit, complemented by subtle black pepper and earthy undertones that speak to the limestone-rich terroir of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo. A mineral backbone and hints of dried herbs provide complexity and structure, making this wine an excellent match for Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic pasta dishes.

name: Fantini Casale Vecchio Montepulciano D'Abruzzo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, herbaceous notes, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Fantini's Casale Vecchio Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a classic expression of Abruzzo's signature red varietal. Medium-full bodied with balanced tannins, it opens with ripe red cherry and dark plum, followed by herbaceous and black pepper notes that add complexity. The wine's earthy undertones and dried fruit character develop on the palate, making it an excellent match for grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-based pasta. A food-friendly Italian red at an accessible price point.

name: Fantini Don Camillo Sangiovese & Cabernet Sauvignon (Gold Collection)
short_description (EN Store): Sangiovese-Cabernet blend from Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, herbaceous spice, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): A well-balanced Sangiovese and Cabernet Sauvignon blend from Italy's Abruzzo region, this wine delivers approachable red-fruit character with layers of dark plum and blackcurrant. The palate shows herbaceous notes and black pepper spice, grounded by medium tannins and a clean finish. Designed for everyday enjoyment with Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and rustic Mediterranean fare.

name: Fantini Edizione Cinque Autoctioni Vino Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Abruzzo blend of five native grapes: Montepulciano-led with Sangiovese, Primitivo, Negroamaro & Malvasia Nera. Medium-full, food-friendly red.
description (EN Store): Fantini Edizione Cinque Autoctioni is a five-grape Abruzzo red anchored by Montepulciano, the region's signature variety, and enriched by Sangiovese, Primitivo, Negroamaro, and Malvasia Nera. This proprietary blend showcases the diversity of southern Italian terroir, balancing ripe red and dark fruit with herbaceous minerality and subtle spice. Medium-full bodied with moderate tannin, it drinks with immediate approachability yet carries enough structure for food pairing versatility—from pasta and pizza to grilled meats and Mediterranean fare.

name: Fantini Three Dreamers Rosso Vino Ottenuto Da Uve Appassite
short_description (EN Store): Abruzzo appassite blend with ripe red fruit, earthy spice, and food-friendly medium-full structure.
description (EN Store): Fantini's Three Dreamers Rosso showcases the traditional appassite (dried-grape) winemaking of Abruzzo, blending native varieties to deliver a wine of immediate appeal and layered complexity. Ripe red and dark plum fruit dominates the palate, supported by earthy undertones and subtle spice that speak to the region's terroir. The medium-full body and balanced tannins make this a versatile companion to Italian classics—from tomato-based pasta and grilled meats to aged cheeses and cured meats. A wine that rewards both casual enjoyment and thoughtful pairing.

name: Maison Jaffelin  Chorey Les Beaune Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with ripe cherry, plum, and earthy minerality. Food-friendly, balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Maison Jaffelin's Chorey-lès-Beaune Rouge is a classic expression of Burgundian Pinot Noir, sourced from the Beaune subregion. The 2022 vintage displays a medium-full body with silky tannins and balanced acidity, opening with dominant red cherry and plum fruit on the nose. Secondary oak and earthy undertones provide subtle complexity, while tertiary minerality and hints of leather emerge on the palate, characteristic of fine Burgundy terroir. This wine is versatile at table, pairing elegantly with roasted poultry, mushroom-based dishes, and traditional French comfort fare.

name: Maison Jaffelin  Bourgogne Hautes-Cotes de Beaune Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Pinot Noir with ripe red fruit, earthy minerality, and food-friendly structure from Hautes-Côtes de Beaune.
description (EN Store): Maison Jaffelin's Bourgogne Hautes-Côtes de Beaune Rouge is a classically styled Pinot Noir from Burgundy's secondary plateau appellation. The 2022 vintage expresses the varietal's signature red cherry and plum character, supported by subtle oak integration and mineral undertones characteristic of the Côte de Beaune terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make this wine versatile at table, equally at home with roasted poultry, earthy mushroom dishes, or lighter braised preparations. A food-friendly expression of Burgundian winemaking tradition.

name: Maison Jaffelin  Morgon Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Gamay from Morgon: bright cherry, earthy minerality, silky tannins. Food-friendly Beaujolais classic.
description (EN Store): Maison Jaffelin's Morgon Rouge exemplifies the finesse of Beaujolais Gamay. This medium-bodied red opens with vibrant red cherry and raspberry, underpinned by earthy minerality characteristic of the granite-rich Morgon terroir. Silky, refined tannins and bright acidity create a wine that is both approachable and age-worthy, with subtle spice and stone fruit notes adding complexity. A versatile food companion suited to casual dining and elegant tables alike.

name: Chateau Fombrion  Cuvee La Griveliere
short_description (EN Store): Bordeaux blend of Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon with ripe dark fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Fombrion Cuvée La Grivelière is a Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon, delivering a medium-full-bodied wine with balanced acidity and approachable tannin. The palate opens with ripe black cherry and dark plum, supported by subtle cedar and herbaceous notes that speak to classic Bordeaux winemaking. A versatile table wine suited to grilled meats, braised beef, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Chateau La Renaissance Pomerol 2017
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc with dark fruit, earthy complexity, and refined structure.
description (EN Store): Château La Renaissance's 2017 Pomerol showcases the classic elegance of Bordeaux's right bank. A blend of 85% Merlot and 15% Cabernet Franc, this wine displays ripe dark plum and blackcurrant on the nose, with subtle oak integration and earthy undertones that develop on the palate. Medium-full body and balanced tannins make it approachable in youth while retaining aging potential. The wine's structure and complexity suit a range of classic pairings, from grilled meats to mushroom-forward dishes.

name: Chateau Bellevue St.Emillion Grand Cru Classe 2017
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion Merlot with ripe dark fruit, soft tannins, and classic Bordeaux structure. Food-friendly and approachable.
description (EN Store): Château Bellevue's 2017 Saint-Émilion Grand Cru Classé is a pure expression of Merlot from one of Bordeaux's most prestigious appellations. The wine displays a medium-full body with refined tannins and balanced acidity, offering ripe plum and black cherry on the nose with subtle oak integration. On the palate, dark fruit flavors meld with earthy undertones and a whisper of cedar, creating a harmonious, food-friendly profile. The soft tannin structure and elegant finish make this wine approachable in its youth while retaining the aging potential characteristic of classified Saint-Émilion estates.

name: Chateau Lafleur Du Roy Pomerol
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pomerol blend of Merlot, Cabernet Franc & Sauvignon. Dark fruit, balanced tannins, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Lafleur Du Roy represents the classic Pomerol style: a Merlot-dominant blend (85%) anchored by Cabernet Franc (10%) and Cabernet Sauvignon (5%). The 2018 vintage delivers a medium-full-bodied red with ripe dark fruit character, soft tannin structure, and herbaceous undertones typical of Right Bank Bordeaux. Approachable yet layered, this wine balances immediate fruit appeal with subtle earthy complexity, making it a versatile table wine for both casual dining and more formal occasions.

name: Chateau Laujac  Cru Bourgeois (Médoc)
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Cru Bourgeois with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco—structured for cellaring and classic red meat.
description (EN Store): Château Laujac's 2020 Cru Bourgeois from Médoc presents a classic Left Bank Bordeaux profile. The blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot delivers a full-bodied wine with prominent blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, layered with cedar, tobacco, and black pepper spice. The high tannin structure and medium-high acidity provide excellent aging potential, while secondary oak influence adds subtle leather and graphite complexity. This is a wine built for the table—pair with grilled red meat, lamb, or slow-braised beef dishes.

name: Duchesse De Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac Bordeaux blend of ripe plum, cherry, and cocoa with silky, integrated tannins.
description (EN Store): A classic Pauillac expression blending Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Cabernet Franc. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry aromatics, supported by subtle berry complexity and hints of vanilla oak. On the palate, juicy red and dark fruit flavors meld with cocoa undertones and soft, well-integrated tannins that provide structure without harshness. A touch of herbaceous character adds freshness and complexity. Medium-Full bodied with balanced acidity, this wine delivers approachable elegance suited to both near-term enjoyment and modest cellaring.

name: Tavernello Vino Rosso d’Italia
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Italian red blend with bright cherry, savoury earth, and food-friendly acidity—excellent everyday drinking.
description (EN Store): Tavernello's Vino Rosso d'Italia is a well-balanced Romagna blend anchored by Sangiovese, with supporting Montepulciano and Nerello Mascalese. The wine opens with vibrant sour cherry and red fruit on the nose, followed by herbaceous and earthy undertones. Medium-Full body and high acidity create a dry, refreshing palate that pairs naturally with Italian cuisine, grilled meats, and cured charcuterie. A reliable, unpretentious red that delivers character and versatility at an accessible price point.

name: Cantine Petrosino Petros Syrah
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Syrah with dark fruit, pepper spice, and earthy complexity—food-friendly and elegant.
description (EN Store): Cantine Petrosino's Petros Syrah is a medium-full-bodied red from Sicily that showcases the versatility of Syrah in southern Italian terroir. The wine opens with dark plum and blackberry fruit, layered with black pepper and spice that give it immediate appeal. Smoke and herbaceous notes add textural interest, while underlying earth and leather suggest modest aging potential. A food-friendly expression suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Casal Bordino Collezione Bordino Montepulciano d'Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Montepulciano d'Abruzzo with ripe red fruit, earthy spice, and food-friendly tannins—a versatile Italian red for everyday enjoyment.
description (EN Store):
Casal Bordino's Collezione Bordino Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is a classic expression of Abruzzo's signature red varietal. The wine opens with ripe red fruit and dark plum aromas, supported by subtle earth and spice notes that reflect the region's terroir. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced tannins create a smooth, approachable drinking experience without sacrificing complexity. The finish carries hints of tobacco and leather, adding depth to this food-friendly red.

This wine excels alongside tomato-based pasta, grilled meats, and rustic Italian fare, making it an excellent choice for both casual meals and more structured dining.

name: Betto Meremma Toscana
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Bordeaux blend of ripe plum, berry, and soft tannins—elegant, food-friendly, and approachable.
description (EN Store): A Tuscan interpretation of the Bordeaux blend, Betto Meremma combines Merlot, Cabernet Sauvignon, and Petit Verdot into a medium-full-bodied red with immediate appeal. The nose opens with dark plum and a medley of red and dark berries, supported by subtle vanilla and herbaceous undertones. On the palate, juicy berry fruit and cranberry notes dominate, with well-integrated tannins that deliver a soft, rounded finish. The 2020 vintage shows the hallmark balance of Tuscan reds—ripe fruit, moderate structure, and enough complexity for food pairing without demanding extended cellaring.

name: Chateau La Croix Meunier Saint -Emilion Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Saint-Émilion blend of Merlot and Cabernet Franc with ripe red fruit, herbaceous notes, and balanced structure.
description (EN Store): Château La Croix Meunier's 2019 Saint-Émilion Grand Cru is a classic Bordeaux blend anchored by Merlot with supporting Cabernet Franc. The wine displays a medium-full body with approachable tannins, offering primary notes of red plum and black cherry complemented by herbaceous and cedar undertones. The balanced acidity and moderate tannin structure make this wine food-friendly across a range of preparations, from grilled meats to braised dishes and tomato-based sauces. Suitable for near-term enjoyment or modest cellaring.

name: Chateau Cazauviel Margaux
short_description (EN Store): Margaux classic: ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity. Age-worthy Bordeaux blend with structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Château Cazauviel's 2019 Margaux exemplifies the Left Bank Bordeaux tradition. A harmonious blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, and Petit Verdot, this wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by elegant cedar and oak spice. The palate reveals fine-grained tannins and medium-high acidity, with tertiary tobacco and leather notes emerging as the wine evolves. Structured for cellaring, it rewards patient collectors with deepening complexity over the next 15–20 years.

name: Domaine Boyer de Bar Pinot Noir - Les Rives de l'Estang
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Languedoc Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, silky tannins, and earthy complexity—versatile for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Domaine Boyer de Bar's Les Rives de l'Estang is a classically styled Pinot Noir from Languedoc-Roussillon, showcasing the varietal's signature elegance and food-friendly character. The wine opens with vibrant red cherry and strawberry aromatics, supported by subtle earthy undertones and a whisper of spice. Medium body and refined tannin structure make this wine approachable young while retaining enough complexity for short-term cellaring. The bright acidity and mineral backbone provide freshness and balance, making it an ideal companion to roasted poultry, mushroom-forward dishes, and light pasta preparations.

name: Domaine Boyer de Bar Cabernet Sauvignon - Combe Croze
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Languedoc Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, cedar complexity, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Domaine Boyer de Bar's Cabernet Sauvignon from Combe Croze presents a classic expression of the Languedoc terroir. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the primary aromatics, supported by pronounced cedar and oak from careful winemaking. The wine's full body and high tannin structure demand substantial food pairings—grilled red meat, braised beef, or lamb are ideal companions. Tertiary tobacco and earth notes emerge with air, adding complexity and age-worthiness to this serious, well-crafted red.

name: Antichello Valpolicella Ripasso DOC Superiore
short_description (EN Store): Valpolicella Ripasso with dark fruit depth, balanced oak, and food-friendly structure—excellent value for red meat and cheese.
description (EN Store): Antichello Valpolicella Ripasso DOC Superiore is a classically structured Veneto red built on the traditional ripasso method, where dried-grape concentration amplifies the primary dark-fruit character. The blend of Corvina, Rondinella, and Corvinone delivers ripe black cherry and plum on the nose, with subtle oak integration and a hint of dried-fruit complexity. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this wine an ideal companion to braised meats, aged cheeses, and tomato-based pasta dishes. The wine's earthy undertone and restrained oak suggest food-first winemaking—a hallmark of quality Valpolicella Ripasso.

name: Chateau de la Graveirette Bon ju Red
short_description (EN Store): Rhône blend of plum, berry, and soft tannins—approachable yet structured for red meat and braised dishes.
description (EN Store): A Rhône South blend anchored by Merlot with supporting Mourvèdre, Marselan, and Grenache, this wine delivers a medium-full body with integrated tannins and medium acidity. The primary character centers on dark plum and a medley of red and dark berries, with subtle cranberry notes adding brightness. Vanilla and herbaceous undertones from winemaking provide secondary complexity, while the soft tannin structure ensures early approachability without sacrificing food-pairing versatility. Ideal for grilled red meat, braised beef, roasted poultry, and tomato-based preparations.

name: Chateau La fon Du Berger Pauillac
short_description (EN Store): Classic Pauillac blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with dark fruit, cedar, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): A traditional Bordeaux red from Pauillac, this 2017 vintage blends Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot to deliver a medium-full bodied wine with balanced structure. Primary aromas of blackcurrant and plum lead to secondary notes of cedar and tobacco, with emerging tertiary leather and mineral complexity. Medium tannins provide food-friendly grip without overwhelming the palate, making this an approachable yet age-worthy expression of the Left Bank style.

name: Domaine Pierre-André Dumas Les Pierreuses Brouilly
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Brouilly Gamay with bright red fruit, floral notes, and food-friendly structure from Burgundy's premier cru vineyard.
description (EN Store): Domaine Pierre-André Dumas' Les Pierreuses is a classic expression of Brouilly, one of Burgundy's ten Beaujolais crus. Built on Gamay's signature red cherry and raspberry fruit, this wine balances bright acidity with silky tannins, offering immediate approachability without sacrificing complexity. Subtle floral and spice notes emerge on the mid-palate, while a mineral earthiness grounds the finish. Ideal for everyday enjoyment or as a food-friendly companion to poultry, pasta, and charcuterie.

name: Domaine Pierre-André Dumas Alchimie Cote de Brouilly
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Gamay from Côte de Brouilly with bright cherry, raspberry, and mineral notes—food-friendly and versatile.
description (EN Store): Domaine Pierre-André Dumas's Alchimie Côte de Brouilly is a refined expression of Gamay from one of Burgundy's most prestigious cru Beaujolais terroirs. The 2017 vintage showcases the varietal's signature elegance: bright red cherry and raspberry on the nose, underpinned by earthy minerality and subtle spice. Medium body and silky tannins make this wine approachable yet structured, with enough acidity to refresh the palate and pair across a wide range of cuisines. A versatile table wine suited to casual dining and food-forward occasions.

name: Chateau Fleur la Mothe Medoc Cru Bourgeois
short_description (EN Store): Médoc Cru Bourgeois: elegant Merlot-Cabernet blend with ripe plum, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Fleur la Mothe's 2016 Médoc Cru Bourgeois is a classically proportioned Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine displays a medium-full body with balanced tannin and moderate acidity, offering ripe dark-fruit character—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar and subtle herbaceous notes. Secondary oak influence adds gentle structure without overwhelming the fruit. This is a food-friendly expression of the Médoc terroir, built for pairing with grilled meats, braised beef, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Chateau Lynch Bages  AOC Pauillac 2020
short_description (EN Store): Bages plateau deep-gravel Pauillac, first vintage in Didi Pei's gravity-flow cellars — 75% new French oak, parcel-by-parcel fermentation across 200 sub-parcels.
description (EN Store):
The 2020 vintage marks a literal new chapter: it is the first wine vinified in the gravity-flow winery designed by Chien Chung 'Didi' Pei, completed that same year — a deliberate architectural investment by the Cazes family to eliminate pump-handling at every stage of production. That decision is inseparable from what's in the glass.

The vines sit on the Bages plateau, south-southwest of Pauillac town, where deep Garonne gravel — deposited over millennia by Pyrenean erosion — drains freely over a limestone sub-strata. The Gironde estuary acts as a thermal buffer, moderating the oceanic climate and enabling the late-harvest ripeness that has defined the house since Jean-Michel Cazes's modernisation programme from 1975.

Fermentation proceeds parcel-by-parcel across 200 sub-parcels in 80 temperature-controlled stainless-steel vats, with malolactic split one-third in vat, two-thirds in barrel. Aging runs 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new oak, monitored via the Winegrid in-barrel system — a real-time alcohol, sugar, colour, and temperature tracker Lynch-Bages pioneered as the first Bordeaux estate to deploy it from 2015.

A Fifth Growth that consistently trades at Second Growth prices, the 2020 delivers sun-ripened concentration with notably fresh acidity and tannin density matching the 2018 and 2019 in structure. Drink from 2027 through 2045.

name: Opus One  Vintage 2021
short_description (EN Store): 93% Cabernet Sauvignon from Oakville's gravelly alluvial bench — 2021's drought-concentrated fruit shaped by 100% new French oak and gravity-flow vinification.
description (EN Store):
When Baron Philippe de Rothschild and Robert Mondavi shook hands in 1979, their explicit ambition was to impose Bordeaux Left Bank discipline on Napa Valley fruit — a transgression that still defines Opus One today. The 2021 vintage was the second-driest in estate history, with delayed budbreak on April 7 pushing the growing season into cooler autumn temperatures, compressing berry size and intensifying concentration without sacrificing the freshness that earlier-than-average picking windows are designed to protect.

The four estate parcels — anchored by the historic To Kalon Q Block — sit on an alluvial fan of gravelly clay loam washed down from the Mayacamas Mountains. Shallow gravel over clay restricts vine vigour; afternoon airflow drawn from San Pablo Bay creates a diurnal swing that locks in acidity even in drought years. The 2021 blend is 93% Cabernet Sauvignon, 4% Cabernet Franc, 2% Petit Verdot, 0.5% Merlot, 0.5% Malbec — the most Cabernet-dominant expression in recent estate history. Each parcel ferments separately in its own stainless-steel tank using native yeasts isolated from each vineyard site, with all movement of must and wine by gravity only. Aging runs 18–20 months in 100% new French oak in the underground Grand Chai, followed by an additional 18 months of bottle age before release. Fined with fresh egg whites; filtration status undisclosed.

No peer in Oakville routinely commits to 100% new French oak across the entire blend while simultaneously targeting Pauillac-like structure over Napa opulence. The 2021's drought-driven concentration and plush texture suggest drinking from 2029 through 2050+.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  1er Grand Cru Classe 2019
short_description (EN Store): The only 1855 First Growth outside the Médoc: Günzian gravel, urban heat, unfiltered — 2019's silky tannins and mineral freshness drink from 2027.
description (EN Store): When Samuel Pepys recorded drinking 'Ho Bryan' at the Royall Oak Tavern in 1663, he was describing wine from a property that had already been producing documented grand vin for over a century — and one that would be classified in 1855 as a special exception, the sole non-Médoc estate in the entire First Growth tier. That singular status is not honorary; it is geological. Haut-Brion sits on two ancient gravel croupes in Pessac, just 5 km from central Bordeaux, where rare Günzian gravel — Pleistocene-era quartz pebbles found nowhere else on the Left Bank — drains aggressively while retaining daytime heat, and the surrounding urban fabric creates a warmer microclimate that consistently delivers among the earliest harvests in Bordeaux, with more reliable phenolic ripeness than any Médoc peer. Fermentation occurs in Haut-Brion's proprietary double-skinned stainless steel vats (the estate introduced stainless steel to Bordeaux in 1961), followed by 14–18 days maceration with gentle remontage using selected Davis 522 yeast. Blending is completed unusually early — by November or December of the harvest year, before oak influence begins — then the wine ages 18–24 months in French oak from the estate's own cooperage, new-oak percentage calibrated to vintage weight. Bottled unfiltered. The 2019 growing season delivered a cool summer with ideal, gradual ripening: the result is a wine of superb richness-to-freshness equilibrium, silky tannins, and subtle Günzian minerality, with the higher Merlot proportion (~45%) giving characteristic suppleness absent from Médoc First Growths. Drink 2027–2055.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  1er Grand Cru Classe 2020
short_description (EN Store): The sole 1855 First Growth outside the Médoc: Günzian gravel, urban heat, unfiltered — 2020 rated 96 pts Decanter.
description (EN Store):
When Jean de Pontac constructed the château at Pessac in 1549, he was building on ground that no other Left Bank estate can replicate: Günzian gravel — ancient Pleistocene-era quartz pebbles deposited over clay — the only true expression of this soil type on the Left Bank. The surrounding urban fabric of Pessac acts as a heat sink, advancing ripening by days and making Haut-Brion's harvest among the earliest in Bordeaux each year.

Fermentation takes place in proprietary double-skinned stainless steel vats (Haut-Brion pioneered stainless steel in Bordeaux in 1961), with maceration of 14–18 days using gentle pump-overs and selected Davis 522 yeast. The blend — approximately 45% Merlot, 44% Cabernet Sauvignon, 10% Cabernet Franc — is assembled unusually early, before oak influence begins, typically by November or December of the harvest year. Aging runs 18–24 months in French oak from the estate's own cooperage, one of only three on-site cooperages in Bordeaux, with new oak percentage calibrated to vintage weight. The wine is bottled unfiltered.

What no peer does: Haut-Brion carries dual classification — the 1855 Médoc list and the 1959 Graves Classification — as a singular exception granted on merit alone. The 2020, rated 96 points by Decanter and described as 'concentrated, powerful and precise,' is built for a long arc: drink 2030–2055.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  Le Clarence de Haut-Brion 2015
short_description (EN Store): Haut-Brion's second wine from Pessac's Günzian gravel croupes — early-blended, unfiltered, 2015's fruit-forward finesse at a fraction of the grand vin.
description (EN Store): Le Clarence de Haut-Brion takes its name from Clarence Dillon, the American financier who purchased the estate in 1935 and rescued it from neglect — a lineage that grounds this second wine directly in the grand vin's winemaking infrastructure, not a separate operation. The source is the same Günzian gravel croupes of Pessac — ancient Pleistocene-era quartz pebbles over clay subsoil, rising to 27 m above the Bordeaux norm, surrounded by urban buildings that trap heat and push harvest dates earlier than virtually any other Left Bank estate. That warmth and drainage combination concentrates Merlot-dominant fruit while preserving aromatic lift. Fermentation takes place in Haut-Brion's proprietary double-skinned stainless steel vats (the estate introduced stainless steel to Bordeaux in 1961), with maceration of approximately 14–18 days and selected yeast. Blending is completed unusually early — by November or December of the harvest year, before oak influence begins — a deliberate sequencing that prioritises fruit clarity over wood integration. Aging follows in French oak barrique, new oak percentage calibrated to vintage weight, then bottled unfiltered. The 2015 vintage delivered fruit-forward texture and smooth tannin structure, approachable earlier than the more austere 2016, yet carrying genuine aging potential. As the only second wine produced from the sole First Growth outside the Médoc — and the only château classified in both the 1855 and 1959 systems — Le Clarence offers access to a terroir with no true peer on the Left Bank.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  Le Clarence de Haut-Brion 2020
short_description (EN Store): Haut-Brion's second wine from Pessac's Günzian gravel croupes — hand-harvested, unfiltered, aged in French oak up to 24 months. 2020: Decanter 96 pts.
description (EN Store):
Le Clarence de Haut-Brion takes its name from Clarence Dillon, the American financier who purchased the estate in 1935 and set in motion the modern era of what is arguably Bordeaux's oldest continuously operating grand cru — a property first documented under the name 'Aubrion' in 1521. As the second wine of the only First Growth outside the Médoc, Le Clarence draws from the same rare Günzian gravel soils: ancient Pleistocene-era quartz pebbles over clay, rising to 27 m elevation across two distinct croupes in Pessac. The surrounding urban fabric creates a warmer microclimate that enables earlier, more reliable ripening than any other Left Bank estate — a structural advantage that shows in the wine's characteristic savouriness and textural suppleness.

Grapes are harvested by hand, fermented in Haut-Brion's proprietary double-skinned stainless steel vats (the estate introduced stainless steel to Bordeaux in 1961), with maceration of 14–18 days and gentle pump-overs. Blending is completed unusually early — by November or December of the harvest year, before oak influence begins — then aged 18–24 months in French oak barrels from the estate's own on-site cooperage, one of only three in Bordeaux. The wine is bottled unfiltered. The higher Merlot proportion relative to Médoc peers delivers a rounded, silky texture that distinguishes the Haut-Brion house style even at second-wine level. The 2020 vintage earned 96 points from Decanter, described as 'concentrated, powerful and precise,' with a drinking window of 2030–2055.

name: Chateau Haut-Brion  Le Clarence de Haut-Brion 2019
short_description (EN Store): Second label of Pessac's only 1855 First Growth; Günzian gravel terroir, unfiltered, 2019's cool-ripened silky structure at accessible entry.
description (EN Store): Le Clarence de Haut-Brion takes its name from Clarence Dillon, the American financier who rescued the estate in 1935 — and the second label carries the full weight of a house that pioneered stainless steel fermentation in Bordeaux as early as 1961, decades before peers followed. That institutional boldness shapes how this wine is made: grapes from the estate's Günzian gravel croupes in Pessac — ancient Pleistocene-era quartz pebbles over clay, at elevations reaching 27 m — are hand-harvested, sorted, and fermented in Haut-Brion's proprietary double-skinned stainless steel vats using selected Davis 522 yeast, with maceration of 14–18 days and gentle remontage. The surrounding urban fabric of Pessac creates a measurably warmer microclimate, enabling earlier, more reliable ripening than anywhere else on the Left Bank. Blending is completed unusually early — by November or December of the harvest year, before oak influence begins, preserving the terroir's native savouriness. Aging follows in French oak barrels from the estate's own on-site cooperage, one of only three in Bordeaux, with new-oak percentage calibrated to vintage weight. The wine is bottled unfiltered. What sets Le Clarence apart within the second-label category is its direct access to the same Günzian terroir and cooperage infrastructure as the grand vin — no peer second label operates its own barrel-making facility. The 2019 vintage, shaped by a cool summer and ideal ripening conditions, delivers superb balance of richness and freshness, with silky tannins and subtle minerality; drinking window 2027–2055.

name: Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion  Grand Cru Classe 2017
short_description (EN Store): Graves Grand Cru Classé: Merlot-led Bordeaux blend with dark fruit depth, cedar complexity, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): Chateau La Mission Haut-Brion 2004 is a Graves Grand Cru Classé red wine anchored by Merlot (56%), with Cabernet Sauvignon (40%) and Cabernet Franc (4%) providing structure and aromatic complexity. This Left Bank Bordeaux blend exhibits the region's hallmark dark fruit intensity—blackcurrant and plum—layered with cedar, tobacco, and graphite minerality characteristic of mature Graves terroir. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins frame a wine built for cellaring and food pairing, with secondary oak influence and tertiary leather notes emerging as the wine evolves. Ideal for serious collectors and those seeking classic Bordeaux architecture.

name: Echo de Lynch Bages AOC Pauillac 2018
short_description (EN Store): Fifth Growth Pauillac that trades at Second Growth prices — Bages plateau gravel, 75% new French oak, ripe house style built for 30+ years.
description (EN Store): When Jean-Michel Cazes launched a 15-year modernisation programme in 1975, the deliberate target was full phenolic ripeness — Lynch-Bages was famously among the last estates to harvest in Pauillac, a choice that defines every bottle. The Bages plateau, south-southwest of Pauillac town, sits on deep Garonne gravel over limestone sub-strata, the product of slow Pyrenean erosion; free drainage forces roots deep while the Gironde estuary moderates temperature extremes, amplifying the concentration that late harvesting delivers. Vinification proceeds parcel-by-parcel across 80 stainless-steel vats, with malolactic fermentation split — one-third in vat, two-thirds in barrel — before 15–18 months in French oak barriques at 75% new oak. From 2015, Lynch-Bages deployed the Winegrid in-barrel monitoring system — the first Bordeaux estate to do so — tracking alcohol, sugar, colour, and temperature in real time, a technological commitment with no parallel among its Pauillac peers. Despite its official Cinquième Cru Classé rank, Liv-Ex market data consistently places Lynch-Bages at Second Growth performance levels. The 2018 hot, dry growing season aligned precisely with the house's ripe-harvest philosophy: concentrated blackcurrant and dark berry, cushioned tannins, and the structural density to reward patience from 2026 through the late 2040s.

name: La Chapelle  de la Mission Haut-Brion 2019
short_description (EN Store): Graves Bordeaux blend of dark plum, blackcurrant, and cedar—a food-friendly classic with medium-full structure.
description (EN Store): La Chapelle de la Mission Haut-Brion 2019 is a Graves Bordeaux red blend anchored by Merlot (50%), Cabernet Sauvignon (40%), and Cabernet Franc (10%). The wine opens with dominant dark plum and blackcurrant fruit, supported by secondary cedar and tobacco notes that reflect careful oak aging. A medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this a versatile table wine suited to grilled red meat, lamb, and braised beef dishes. Tertiary minerality and subtle leather add complexity on the finish.

name: Chateau Haut-Bages Monpelou Pauillac (Cru Bourgeois Superieur)
short_description (EN Store): Pauillac Cru Bourgeois Supérieur: Cabernet-led Bordeaux blend with blackcurrant depth, cedar complexity, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Haut-Bages Monpelou is a Cru Bourgeois Supérieur estate in Pauillac, crafted from a classic Bordeaux blend dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon (68%), with supporting Merlot (27%) and Cabernet Franc (5%). The 2016 vintage expresses ripe blackcurrant and plum on the nose, with emerging cedar and black pepper notes that reflect both oak aging and the wine's Pauillac terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine approachable in youth while retaining aging potential. Tobacco and earth undertones develop on the palate, offering complexity suitable for grilled red meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Chateau La Gasquerie AOP Bordeaux Rouge
short_description (EN Store): Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux blend with dark cherry, plum, and cedar—food-friendly structure for grilled meats and braises.
description (EN Store): Château La Gasquerie's 2022 Bordeaux Rouge from Castillon Côtes de Bordeaux presents a classic right-bank blend profile. Dark cherry and plum dominate the entry, supported by cassis depth and subtle herbaceous notes. Cedar and tobacco emerge mid-palate, with integrated oak providing structure without dominance. The finish carries graphite minerality and dried herb complexity, typical of the appellation's slate-rich terroir. Medium-full body and balanced tannin make this a versatile food wine.

name: Domaine Thibault Liger-Belair  Nuits Saint Georges 1er Cru ""les Saint Georges""
short_description (EN Store): Les Saint-Georges 2021: clay-limestone with dense white pebbles, vines planted 1944, infusion philosophy over extraction — the Grand Cru Nuits never received.
description (EN Store):
When Thibault Liger-Belair reclaimed the family's négociant legacy in 2001, he became the first vigneron in nine generations of a lineage that once owned La Tâche before selling it to DRC in 1929 — and he chose to make his name not on Grand Cru paper, but on a Premier Cru that critics have long argued deserves promotion. Les Saint-Georges sits at the southern tip of Nuits-Saint-Georges on clay-limestone soils threaded with dense white pebbles (chaille), a geological signature that drives the site's characteristic mineral tension. Vines planted in 1944 — in a climat documented since the year 1000 — deliver low yields and concentrated, architecturally precise fruit.

In the cellar, Thibault's deliberate break from Nuits-Saint-Georges' historically muscular archetype is codified in technique: triple sorting (at vine, before and after destemmer), native yeast fermentation in open-top vessels, and a philosophy of gentle infusion over extraction — minimal pumpovers, minimal pigeage. Whole-cluster inclusion is calibrated per vintage. Aging runs 14–18 months in lightly charred (5–7 minute) barrique, with oak species and grain selected post-fermentation and dried a minimum of three years for silkier tannin integration; new oak is capped at 50%. The wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered.

The special feature here is institutional: Les Saint-Georges is the centrepiece of an active reclassification campaign (ongoing since 2007) to elevate the climat to Grand Cru — a promotion the wine's structure and site history make a compelling case for. No vintage-specific critic scores are confirmed for 2021, but the vintage's naturally moderate yields and freshness suit Thibault's precision-first approach; drink from 2026 through 2035+.

name: Hopes End  Barrel Selection Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Cabernet with dark fruit depth, oak complexity, and food-friendly structure for premium dining.
description (EN Store): Hopes End Barrel Selection Cabernet Sauvignon from Barossa Valley presents a medium-full bodied red wine with ripe blackberry and plum at its core, complemented by vanilla and oak from careful barrel aging. Black pepper and smoke notes add complexity and structure, while chocolate undertones provide richness. This wine demonstrates the classic Australian Cabernet profile—fruit-forward yet sophisticated—making it an excellent choice for fine dining occasions and substantial meat dishes.

name: Chateau Kirwan 2021
short_description (EN Store): Margaux Bordeaux blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc & Petit Verdot. Classic structure with dark fruit, cedar, and elegant tannin.
description (EN Store): Château Kirwan is a Margaux-classified growth producing a traditional Bordeaux blend anchored by Cabernet Sauvignon (73%), with supporting Merlot (13%), Cabernet Franc (8%), and Petit Verdot (6%). The 2021 vintage expresses the region's characteristic dark fruit profile—blackcurrant and black cherry—layered with cedar and herbaceous undertones. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure make this wine food-friendly across red meat, poultry, and aged cheese pairings. A classic expression of Left Bank Margaux terroir.

name: Chateau Grand Sorillon  Bordeaux Superior
short_description (EN Store): Balanced Bordeaux blend of Cabernet Franc and Merlot with dark fruit, cedar, and food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Château Grand Sorillon Bordeaux Supérieur is a classically structured red blend combining Cabernet Franc and Merlot. The wine displays a harmonious balance of dark plum and blackcurrant fruit on the palate, complemented by subtle cedar and black pepper notes. Medium-full body and moderate tannin make this wine approachable now while retaining cellaring potential. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and herb-forward preparations.

name: Chateau Faugeres  Saint Emilion Grand Cru 2020
short_description (EN Store): Merlot-led Saint-Émilion Grand Cru with dark plum, cedar, and silky tannins. Classic Bordeaux elegance.
description (EN Store): Château Faugères Saint-Émilion Grand Cru 2020 is a classically structured Bordeaux blend anchored by 85% Merlot, with supporting Cabernet Franc and Cabernet Sauvignon. The wine opens with dominant dark plum and black cherry fruit, layered with secondary cedar and tobacco complexity from careful oak aging. Medium-full body and balanced tannins deliver food-friendly structure without heaviness, while tertiary earthy minerality adds depth and age-worthiness. A refined expression of Right Bank Bordeaux terroir.

name: Masseto  Toscana IGT 2020
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Bolgheri's Pliocene blue-clay hill — 2020 marks only the second vintage to incorporate a trace of Cabernet Franc, vinified across three sub-plots in gravity-flow concrete.
description (EN Store):
In 2008, Masseto became the first Italian wine distributed through the Place de Bordeaux — a commercial declaration that this seven-hectare Bolgheri hillside answers to Pomerol, not Chianti. The 2020 is only the second vintage to quietly transgress the estate's own 100% Merlot identity: a small, undisclosed percentage of Cabernet Franc enters the blend for the first time since 2019, a deliberate structural decision whose full logic the estate has yet to articulate publicly.

The Masseto hill sits on former Pliocene clay quarry terrain — dense blue-grey clay that functions as a natural cistern, releasing stored moisture through dry Tyrrhenian summers while hardening to restrict root penetration and concentrate the vine's output. Three sub-plots are vinified separately: Masseto Alto (110 m elevation; clay, sand, pebbles — linearity and structure), Masseto Centrale (densest Pliocene clay — opulence and tannin mass), and Masseto Junior (70 m; youngest vines — breadth and softness). Marine breezes from the coast 10 km west moderate ripening and preserve acidity despite the clay's heat retention.

Hand-harvested in 15 kg crates with multiple passes, double-sorted, then fermented in 65 hL tulip-shaped concrete tanks at 25–30°C for 21–28 days — gravity-flow throughout the underground winery opened in 2019. 100% new French oak barriques carry malolactic conversion and the first 12 months of aging; the blended wine returns to barrel for a further 12 months, then rests a minimum 12 months in bottle before release. The 2020 scores 97 pts aggregate; drinking window opens 2027.

name: Masseto  Toscana IGT 2012
short_description (EN Store): 100% Merlot from Bolgheri's Pliocene blue-clay hill — three sub-plots vinified separately, 24 months in 100% new French oak. Italy's answer to Pétrus.
description (EN Store):
When Marchese Lodovico Antinori planted Merlot on the Masseto hill in 1984, he was defying every orthodoxy of Tuscan viticulture — no Sangiovese, no Cabernet Sauvignon, just a single variety on a former clay quarry that most growers would have dismissed as too heavy. That transgression became Italy's most consequential winemaking decision of the twentieth century.

The 6.6-hectare hillside sits within Bolgheri, roughly 10 km from the Tyrrhenian Sea. Its Pliocene blue-grey clay acts as a natural cistern, releasing stored moisture slowly through dry summers while hardening to limit root penetration — stressing the vine into concentration. Three sub-plots are kept distinct: Masseto Alto (110 m elevation; clay, sand, pebbles) delivers structure and linearity; Masseto Centrale (densest clay) provides the opulent, tannic core; Masseto Junior (70 m; youngest vines) contributes breadth. Marine breezes and amplified late-afternoon Tyrrhenian light moderate ripening, preserving acidity at natural alcohols that can reach 15%+.

Hand-harvested in 15 kg crates over multiple passes, each sub-plot and each harvest pass is fermented separately in temperature-controlled concrete tanks at 25–30°C for 21–28 days, with small portions fermented directly in barrel. The wine then ages 24 months total in 100% new French oak barriques — the first 12 months for malolactic conversion, the second 12 post-assemblage — followed by a minimum 12 months in bottle before release. Bottled without chill-filtration.

The 2012 vintage marks the estate's first year of certified organic farming, adding a layer of historical significance to this already collector-grade expression. No vintage notes or critic scores for 2012 appear in the estate's published record, so the wine should be assessed on its own terms: a Bolgheri growing season shaped by the discipline of the new organic regime and the characteristic coastal moderation of the appellation.

name: Riporta  Nero D'Avola Sicilia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Nero d'Avola with dark plum, spice, and earthy complexity—a food-friendly red for Mediterranean tables.
description (EN Store): Riporta's Nero d'Avola from Sicily delivers a medium-full-bodied red anchored by dark plum and blackberry fruit. Secondary spice and earth notes add textural interest, while tertiary tobacco and leather suggest modest aging potential. A versatile, food-friendly expression of this southern Italian varietal, suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and tomato-forward cuisine.

name: Riporta  Primitivo Di Manduria
short_description (EN Store): Bold Primitivo from Manduria with dark fruit, smoke, and pepper—smooth, full-bodied, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Riporta's Primitivo di Manduria is a full-bodied red wine from Puglia's sun-drenched Manduria region, delivering a bold nose followed by a palate rich in dark fruit, cherry, and raspberry. Smoke and black pepper notes add complexity and spice, while fully rounded tannins and smooth texture make this wine eminently drinkable and versatile at the table. A classic expression of southern Italian Primitivo—lively, approachable, and built for everyday enjoyment with pizza, pasta, and grilled fare.

name: Concha Y Toro  Terrunyo Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Chilean Cabernet with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and structured tannins—a classic expression of Maule Valley terroir.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Terrunyo Cabernet Sauvignon from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a full-bodied red wine anchored by ripe blackcurrant and dark plum fruit. Cedar and oak frame the primary flavors, while high tannins provide structure and aging potential. Secondary notes of black pepper and vanilla complement the core profile, with tertiary tobacco and leather complexity emerging on the finish. This wine suits grilled red meats, lamb, and slow-braised beef dishes, as well as aged hard cheeses.

name: Riporta  Sangiovese
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Sangiovese with bright cherry, savoury earth, and mineral grip—food-friendly and structured.
description (EN Store): A Sicilian expression of Sangiovese, this wine delivers the varietal's signature red cherry and herbaceous character with a savoury mineral backbone. High acidity and medium-full body frame tart red fruit and earthy undertones, while subtle tobacco and leather notes suggest complexity. Approachable yet structured, it pairs naturally with tomato-based dishes, grilled meats, and aged cheeses.

name: Riporta  Zinfandel
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Zinfandel with ripe dark fruit, black pepper spice, and food-friendly tannins—ideal for grilled meats and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Riporta's Zinfandel from Sicily delivers a medium-full-bodied red wine with characteristic dark plum and red berry fruit on the nose. Black pepper and subtle spice notes add complexity, while the wine's approachable tannin structure and balanced acidity make it a versatile table wine. Ideal for grilled red meat, barbecue, and hearty pasta dishes.

name: Cueva del Viento  Misma Luna
short_description (EN Store): Garnacha Tintorera from Spain's Canary Islands—dark plum, spice, and mineral depth in a food-friendly medium-full red.
description (EN Store): Cueva del Viento's Misma Luna is a single-varietal expression of Garnacha Tintorera sourced from the Canary Islands. The wine displays a medium-full body with ripe dark plum and red fruit on the nose, supported by black pepper spice and subtle licorice notes. On the palate, moderate tannins and balanced acidity frame earthy minerality and dried fruit complexity, making this a versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Cueva del Viento  Monastrell
short_description (EN Store): Canary Islands Monastrell with dark cherry, plum, and peppery spice—food-friendly and mineral-driven.
description (EN Store): Cueva del Viento's Monastrell from Spain's Canary Islands delivers a medium-full-bodied red with ripe dark plum and black cherry at its core, supported by black pepper spice and herbaceous notes. The palate shows good structure with medium tannins and acidity, finishing with earthy minerality that reflects the volcanic terroir. A versatile food wine suited to grilled meats, braised dishes, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Borga  Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Veneto Cabernet with blackcurrant intensity, cedar complexity, and structured tannins.
description (EN Store): A classically structured Cabernet Sauvignon from Veneto, this wine displays the hallmark dark fruit intensity and cedary oak complexity characteristic of the region's serious red wines. Blackcurrant and dark plum dominate the palate, supported by firm, well-integrated tannins and a mineral backbone. Secondary oak and black pepper notes add refinement, while subtle tertiary tobacco and leather suggest aging potential. The full body and medium-high acidity make this a food-friendly choice for grilled meats, lamb, and aged cheeses.

name: Tornatore  Etna Rosso
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sicilian Nerello Mascalese from Etna's volcanic slopes—bright cherry, mineral precision, food-friendly structure.
description (EN Store): Tornatore's Etna Rosso showcases the distinctive character of Nerello Mascalese, Sicily's noble red varietal grown on the volcanic slopes of Mount Etna. This medium-full-bodied wine balances bright red cherry and stone fruit with volcanic minerality and subtle black pepper spice. The silky tannin structure and medium-high acidity make it remarkably food-friendly, bridging Mediterranean and Italian cuisine with ease. Dried herb and earthy undertones emerge on the finish, reflecting the wine's terroir-driven complexity.

name: Tornatore  Etna Rosso Trimarchisa
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Etna Rosso from volcanic slopes; Nerello Mascalese with mineral precision, red fruit, and peppery spice.
description (EN Store): Tornatore's Etna Rosso Trimarchisa is a single-varietal expression of Nerello Mascalese from Sicily's renowned volcanic terroir. The wine displays the characteristic mineral-driven profile of high-altitude Etna production, with bright red cherry and black pepper on the nose, layered with dried herb and subtle smoke. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure without heaviness, making this a food-friendly yet serious red. Volcanic slate and licorice notes emerge on the finish, reflecting the distinctive geology of the region.

name: Tornatore  Etna Rosso Riserva
short_description (EN Store): Etna Riserva from volcanic slopes: elegant Nerello blend with mineral depth, dark fruit, and structured tannin.
description (EN Store): Tornatore's Etna Rosso Riserva is a structured red wine from Sicily's volcanic Mount Etna region, blending Nerello Mascalese and Nerello Cappuccio. The 2017 vintage expresses the terroir's mineral character alongside ripe dark fruit, black pepper spice, and subtle smoke. Medium-full body and medium-high tannin provide food-friendly structure suitable for grilled meats, braised dishes, and herb-forward Mediterranean cuisine. The wine's complexity develops through layers of cherry, plum, earth, and licorice, reflecting both the fruit's ripeness and the volcanic soils' influence.

name: Chateau Margaux  2021
short_description (EN Store): 25-ha Quaternary gravel plateau, on-site cooperage, 100% new French oak — the only First Growth whose name is its appellation.
description (EN Store): When Pierre de Lestonnac converted the La Mothe de Margaux grain fields to vine between 1572 and 1582, he was exploiting a geological accident: a raised gravel mound sitting above the otherwise flat Médoc plain, its Quaternary Garonne gravel draining so freely over limestone-clay marl that vines are forced into deep root competition, suppressing vigour and concentrating aromatic intensity. That 25-hectare plateau remains the heart of the Grand Vin today. The gravelly marl, drained further by the Lestonnat stream and tempered by the Gironde estuary's maritime influence, produces Cabernet Sauvignon of extraordinary finesse rather than brute weight — a structural signature no amount of winemaking can replicate elsewhere in the Médoc. In the cellar, fermentation proceeds parcel-by-parcel across nearly 100 vats — some as small as 25 hL — in a combination of oak and stainless steel, with malolactic fermentation completed in vat for free-run juice and in barrel for press wine. The Grand Vin then ages 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, much of it coopered on-site at the estate's own cooperage — a rarity among the five First Growths that gives the team direct control over toast levels and grain selection. Only roughly one-third of the harvest qualifies for the Grand Vin; the rest is declassified. The 2021 vintage is not represented in the estate's own published vintage notes, but the growing season was characterised across the Left Bank by a cool, late summer that rewarded patience and rigorous selection — precisely the conditions that favour Margaux's thin-soiled, late-ripening Cabernet Sauvignon parcels. Certified organic since 2017, with select parcels under biodynamic trials, the Grand Vin carries its terroir credentials from soil to bottle unfined and unfiltered.

name: Chateau Margaux  2020
short_description (EN Store): 2020 Grand Vin: 89% Cabernet Sauvignon from organic Quaternary gravel over limestone-marl, aged 18–24 months in 100% new French oak coopered on-site.
description (EN Store): Pierre de Lestonnac's 1572 decision to convert La Mothe de Margaux from grain to vine was the founding act of the only First Growth whose name is inseparable from its appellation — a distinction that has shaped every winemaking decision since. The estate's 25-hectare plateau parcel sits on deep Quaternary Garonne gravel over a Tertiary limestone and clay-marl terrace, the gravel mounds rising just enough above the flat Médoc plain to shed water with precision; the Lestonnat stream completes the drainage. Thin, nutrient-poor soils restrain vine vigour and concentrate flavour into small, thick-skinned berries — the structural basis for the Grand Vin's characteristic tannin finesse rather than brute extraction. The 2020 blend reads 89% Cabernet Sauvignon, 8% Merlot, 2% Petit Verdot, 1% Cabernet Franc — the highest Cabernet proportion in recent estate history, harvested at reduced yield due to lower natural fertility. Hand-sorted fruit ferments parcel-by-parcel across nearly 100 vats as small as 25 hL; malolactic fermentation for free-run wine occurs in vat, press wine in barrel. Aging runs 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, a significant share coopered at the estate's own on-site cooperage — unique among the five First Growths and giving the team direct control over toast profile and wood grain. The Grand Vin has carried organic certification since 2017. 2020 completes a third consecutive exceptional year (2018–2019–2020), delivering perfect tannin density alongside the estate's signature aromatic precision; drink 2030–2055+.

name: Chateau Margaux  2001
short_description (EN Store): 2001 Grand Vin from Margaux's 25-ha Quaternary gravel plateau — 18–24 months in 100% new estate-coopered French oak; one-third selection rate.
description (EN Store): When André Mentzelopoulos acquired Château Margaux in 1977 for roughly 72 million francs, the estate's defining asset was already ancient: a 25-hectare plateau of deep Quaternary Garonne gravel laid over a Tertiary limestone-clay-marl terrace — the same 'La Mothe' gravel mounds that drain so aggressively they force Cabernet Sauvignon roots deep enough to produce concentration without weight. The Lestonnat stream and the moderating influence of the Gironde estuary complete a microclimate that consistently delivers aromatic precision over brute power. The 2001 Grand Vin is built on that foundation: a Cabernet Sauvignon-dominant blend (typically 75–89% CS, completed by Merlot, Petit Verdot, and Cabernet Franc) drawn from hand-harvested, hand-sorted fruit, fermented parcel-by-parcel across vats as small as 25 hL, with malolactic fermentation in vat for free-run wine. It then aged 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, a significant share of those barrels coopered on-site at the estate's own cooperage — a practice unique among the five First Growths, giving the team direct control over toast level and grain selection. Only roughly one-third of the harvest qualified for the Grand Vin label. The 2001 Left Bank growing season delivered a cool, late-ripening year that rewarded patience: expect a wine of linear structure and restrained fruit density, now entering a window of secondary complexity, with a drinking horizon extending well into the 2030s.

name: Chateau Margaux  2018
short_description (EN Store): 2018 Grand Vin from Margaux's 25-ha Quaternary gravel plateau; 100% new estate-coopered French oak; high alcohol met by equally high acidity — earlier access than 2016 with serious longevity.
description (EN Store):
Château Margaux is the only First Growth whose name is identical to its appellation — a distinction earned at Napoleon III's 1855 Classification, where it was the sole estate awarded a perfect 20/20. That singular identity is rooted in a 25-hectare plateau of deep Quaternary Garonne gravel over a Tertiary limestone-and-clay-marl terrace, the drainage so efficient that vine vigour is structurally suppressed, concentrating aromatic intensity rather than mass. The Lestonnat stream and the Landes forest to the west moderate the maritime microclimate, while select Cabernet Sauvignon parcels exceeding 80 years of age contribute the skeletal architecture of the Grand Vin.

Vinification proceeds parcel by parcel across nearly 100 vats — some as small as 25 hL — allowing micro-terroir precision before the final assemblage. Malolactic fermentation for free-run wine occurs in vat; press wine completes MLF in barrel. The wine then ages 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, a significant proportion of it coopered on-site at the estate's own cooperage — a rarity among the five First Growths that gives the team direct control over toast level and grain selection. The Grand Vin is bottled unfined and unfiltered at the château.

The 2018 vintage is characterised by high alcohol held in tension by equally high acidity — a combination the estate describes as among the best it has ever produced. More approachable in its early window than the structured 2016, it nonetheless carries the density and acid backbone for extended cellaring. Rigorous parcel selection — only roughly one-third of the harvest enters the Grand Vin — is reflected in a blend dominated by Cabernet Sauvignon from the estate's certified-organic (since 2017) vineyards.

name: Chateau Margaux  2010
short_description (EN Store): 2010 Grand Vin from Margaux's 25-ha Quaternary gravel plateau — 20/20 Decanter, built for 2030–2060+.
description (EN Store):
When Decanter awarded the 2010 Château Margaux a perfect 20/20 en primeur, the consensus was unambiguous: this is a wine of structure and precision, not flattery. Where the 2009 seduced with opulence, the 2010 withheld — dense, tannic, and architecturally severe in youth, demanding patience measured in decades.

The fruit originates from the estate's 25-hectare plateau of deep Quaternary Garonne gravel over a Tertiary limestone and clay-marl terrace — among the Haut-Médoc's most exceptional drainage profiles. The Lestonnat stream assists man-made drainage, keeping vine stress calibrated; the result is the restrained vigour and concentrated berry that defines Margaux's signature finesse over Pauillac-style power.

Hand-harvested and hand-sorted, the 2010 was fermented parcel-by-parcel across nearly 100 vats — some as small as 25 hL — allowing micro-vinification precision. Aged 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, a significant share of those barrels coopered at the estate's own on-site cooperage, a rarity among First Growths that gives the team direct control over toast profile and grain selection. Malolactic fermentation for free-run wine occurred in vat; press wine completed MLF in barrel.

Only roughly one-third of the harvest entered the Grand Vin — the remainder redirected to Pavillon Rouge or declassified — a selection rate that makes the 2010's density all the more remarkable given the vintage's naturally austere tannic structure.

Drink 2030–2060+.

name: Chateau Margaux  2000
short_description (EN Store): From Margaux's 25-ha Quaternary gravel plateau, the 2000 Grand Vin is aged 18–24 months in 100% new French oak from the estate's own on-site cooperage.
description (EN Store): When André Mentzelopoulos acquired Château Margaux in 1977 for roughly 72 million francs, the estate had fallen into neglect — his immediate investment in parcel-by-parcel vinification infrastructure is precisely why the 2000 Grand Vin exists in its current form: nearly 100 individual vats, some as small as 25 hL, allowing micro-vinification of each plot before the final assemblage. The vineyard's crown is a 25-hectare plateau of deep Quaternary Garonne gravel laid over a limestone and clay-marl Tertiary terrace — among the most freely draining outcrops in the Haut-Médoc — where the gravel mounds known as La Mothe rise above the flat Médoc plain, concentrating heat and stressing roots into the limestone below. Fermentation proceeds in both oak and stainless steel vats; free-run wine completes malolactic in vat while press wine undergoes MLF in barrel. The Grand Vin then ages 18–24 months in 100% new French oak, a significant share of it coopered on-site at the estate's own cooperage — a distinction no other First Growth maintains — giving the team direct control over toast levels and stave seasoning. Only roughly one-third of the total harvest is selected for the Grand Vin, the remainder redirected to Pavillon Rouge or declassified entirely. The 2000 vintage in Margaux delivered a warm, dry growing season with exceptional Cabernet Sauvignon ripeness, producing a wine of deep colour, firm but polished tannin structure, and the aromatic precision that defines this commune over its Pauillac peers. Drinking window: 2010–2040.

name: Altos de Rioja  Altos R Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Bold Rioja Reserva with ripe plum, leather, and vanilla oak—a structured, age-worthy Tempranillo with long finish.
description (EN Store): Altos de Rioja's Reserva is a classically styled Tempranillo from Rioja Alavesa, built on a foundation of deep red and dark plum fruit with pronounced acidity and medium tannin structure. Oak aging imparts vanilla and subtle cedar notes, while leather and dried herb complexity emerge on the palate. The wine's bold character and extended finish reflect traditional Rioja winemaking, making it a versatile partner for grilled meats, braised dishes, and aged cheeses.

name: E. Guigal  Cote-Rotie La Landonne
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Côte-Rôtie Syrah with dark fruit, pepper spice, and mineral complexity—a benchmark Northern Rhône expression.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's La Landonne represents the pinnacle of Côte-Rôtie craftsmanship. This 100% Syrah showcases the terroir's volcanic slate and granite soils through a layered profile: dark plum and black pepper on entry, evolving through smoky oak and leather in the mid-palate, finishing with mineral precision and subtle tar notes. Medium-full body and balanced tannin structure reward both immediate enjoyment and cellaring potential.

name: E. Guigal  Cote Rotie La Mouline
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône North blend of Syrah and Viognier with dark fruit, pepper spice, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Côte Rôtie La Mouline is a benchmark expression of the northern Rhône's most prestigious appellation. This Syrah-dominant blend with Viognier showcases the region's signature elegance: ripe blackberry and dark plum fruit balanced by peppery spice, smoke, and mineral undertones. The Viognier component adds subtle floral aromatics and textural refinement. Full-bodied with medium-high tannins and acidity, this wine demands food pairing and rewards cellaring.

name: E. Guigal  Cote Rotie La Turque
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Côte-Rôtie with dark fruit intensity, peppery spice, and elegant floral lift from Viognier.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Côte-Rôtie La Turque is a benchmark expression of the northern Rhône's most prestigious appellation. Syrah dominates with blackberry, dark plum, and black pepper character, while a small proportion of Viognier adds floral complexity and textural elegance. The wine displays full body and medium-high tannins, with secondary smoke and leather notes that deepen on the palate. Tertiary spice and licorice emerge with air, suggesting age-worthiness and complexity. A wine of power and refinement, suited to serious red meat preparations and game.

name: Fly By  Cabernet Sauvignon
