The transgression that still defines the house: Cono Sur was the first Chilean winery to seal quality wine under screwcap in 2002, when cork was considered non-negotiable for any serious bottling. The Bicicleta Pinot Noir is the accessible expression of Chile's most Pinot-obsessed producer — drink within 1–2 years of vintage for peak primary fruit.

name: Louis Latour Pinot Noir Valmoissine (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Var-sourced Pinot Noir from Louis Latour's southern négociant range — flash-pasteurised, unfined, fermented in open wooden vats with 14-day maceration.
description (EN Store):
Louis Latour's reach extends well beyond the Côte d'Or: the Valmoissine Pinot Noir is sourced from the Var département in southern France, a deliberate southward expansion by the house to produce Pinot Noir in a warmer, sun-drenched terroir far removed from the limestone hillsides of Aloxe-Corton. The Var's sandy, alluvial soils and Mediterranean-influenced microclimate yield a Pinot Noir with more immediate fruit density and softer structural tension than a village Burgundy — a conscious trade-off the house made to offer an accessible, terroir-distinct expression of the variety.

Winemaking follows the same red-wine protocol applied across the Louis Latour range: 100% hand-harvested, fully destemmed Pinot Noir, fermented in large open wooden vats with manual pigeage over approximately 14 days, with gravity-only transport throughout the cuverie. Post-fermentation, the wine undergoes the house's openly acknowledged and category-transgressive practice of flash pasteurisation at approximately 72°C for 2–3 seconds before bottling — eliminating the need for fining agents entirely, as the wine falls clear naturally over 2–3 months. No other major Burgundy-rooted négociant applies this technique uniformly across its red range. Oak aging uses 15–35% new French oak from the in-house Louis Latour cooperage, staves air-dried 24–36 months. The 375 ml format makes this the sharpest entry point into the Louis Latour red winemaking philosophy.

name: Chateau de la Vieille Tour Bordeaux Supérieur AOP & Roses
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux Supérieur blend of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot with ripe blackcurrant, cedar, and tobacco complexity.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux Supérieur from Château de la Vieille Tour, this 2022 vintage showcases the traditional marriage of Cabernet Sauvignon and Merlot. The wine opens with dominant blackcurrant and dark plum fruit, supported by cedary oak and subtle tobacco notes that speak to careful winemaking and aging potential. Medium-full body and medium-high tannins provide structure and grip, while secondary leather and herbaceous notes add complexity and age-worthiness. Ideal for cellaring or enjoying now with hearty fare.

name: Chateau Lafleur Pomerol 2020
short_description (EN Store): 4.58ha atop Pomerol's iron-crasse plateau; ~50% pre-1956 Bouchet genetics, unfined/unfiltered, ~1,000 cases/yr. 2020 rated among the estate's finest ever.
description (EN Store):
When André Robin selected Bouchet cuttings by massale in the 1930s and then saved them from the 1956 frost by cutting frozen vines to ground level rather than uprooting them, he preserved a biotype of Cabernet Franc that winemaker Omri Ram asserts exists at only three addresses on Earth: Lafleur, Cheval Blanc, and Ausone. That genetic singularity is the structural spine of every bottle made here.

The 4.58-hectare single-block sits at the northern crest of the Pomerol plateau — the appellation's highest point — on a mosaic of gravel-over-clay and sandy-gravel parcels underlaid by an iron-rich crasse de fer sublayer. That ferric clay drives the wine's signature mineral tension, pulling it away from the plush, Merlot-fat opulence of neighbouring Pétrus toward something coiled and iron-edged.

The 2020 grand vin is built on an approximately 50% Merlot / 50% Bouchet blend, hand-harvested parcel by parcel, double-sorted, and fermented in small traditional concrete vats with ~3 weeks maceration. Malolactic completes in barrel; élevage runs 18 months in French oak barriques at ~50% new, racked every three months. Bottled unfined and unfiltered, gravity-only throughout.

Lafleur is the only Pomerol estate carrying genuine pre-1956 Bouchet genetics — a structural and aromatic fingerprint no peer can replicate — and production is capped at roughly 1,000 cases, with no grand vin released in sub-standard years.

The 2020 vintage is described as among the finest in the estate's history: dense, creamy, and very concentrated with remarkable length. Drinking window: 2030–2060+.

name: Kings of Prohibition  Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Australian Syrah with ripe blackberry, pepper spice, and smoky depth—built for grilled red meat and BBQ.
description (EN Store): Kings of Prohibition Shiraz is a full-bodied Australian red wine showcasing the classic power and spice of Syrah from South Eastern Australia. Ripe blackberry and dark plum dominate the primary profile, supported by assertive black pepper and smoke on the palate. Secondary oak and spice notes add complexity, while tertiary leather and tobacco hints suggest structure and aging potential. Medium-high tannins provide grip and food-friendliness with grilled meats, lamb, and smoky barbecue.

name: La Puerta Alta Malbec
short_description (EN Store): Full-bodied Mendoza Malbec with ripe blackberry, plum, and cocoa. Smooth, structured, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): La Puerta Alta Malbec is a medium-full bodied red from Mendoza's renowned wine region, showcasing the depth and elegance characteristic of Argentine Malbec. Aromas of blackberry, plum, and violet lead to a palate marked by cocoa, earth, and subtle spice. Aged in French oak, the wine displays smooth tannin structure and balanced acidity, making it an approachable yet serious expression suitable for both casual enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Penfolds Bin 389 Cabernet Shiraz Lunar New Year Horse Edition (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Bin 389's defining signature: aged in barrels previously used for Grange, delivering Kalimna-sourced Cabernet-Shiraz depth at a fraction of the flagship's price.
description (EN Store):
Bin 389 carries a nickname earned through winemaking logic, not marketing: 'Baby Grange' — because the Cabernet Sauvignon/Shiraz blend is matured in the same American oak hogsheads that previously held Grange, absorbing residual extract and spice from Australia's most celebrated wine. This is not a romantic flourish; it is a deliberate resource decision that structurally links the two wines across every vintage.

The fruit draws primarily from Kalimna in the Barossa Valley, Penfolds' 290-hectare estate planted on ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils that have remained phylloxera-free — conditions that allow old-vine root systems to reach extraordinary depth, concentrating flavour without irrigation stress. The warm Barossa floor climate builds phenolic ripeness, while the multi-regional blending philosophy allows winemakers to calibrate structure vintage by vintage.

Fermentation employs acclimatised pure yeast cultures in temperature-controlled stainless steel, a technique pioneered at Penfolds by Ray Beckwith using pH meters — a scientific intervention that predates most of the wine world's adoption of fermentation control. Post-fermentation, the wine enters second-fill American oak hogsheads (larger than Bordeaux barriques), where Grange's previous occupancy has softened the wood's grip while preserving its vanilla and cedar character.

What no peer replicates: the cascading-barrel system that ties Bin 389's identity directly to Grange's production calendar, making it structurally unique within Australian wine. The 2023 vintage is the first release in the Lunar New Year Horse Edition packaging, though the wine inside follows the unbroken house recipe.

name: Penfolds St Henri Shiraz Gift Box Lunar New Year Horse Edition (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): St Henri's defining rule: zero new oak. Aged in large old vats, the 2021 Barossa Shiraz delivers pure fruit unmediated by wood tannin.
description (EN Store):
While Max Schubert was building Grange on new American oak hogsheads in the 1950s, St Henri took the opposite path — large, seasoned old oak vats that contribute no wood tannin and no vanilla extract, leaving Barossa Shiraz to speak entirely through fruit and site. That deliberate refusal of new oak remains St Henri's defining transgression within the Penfolds portfolio to this day.

The 2021 vintage draws on Barossa Valley fruit grown in the region's characteristically ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils — phylloxera-free ground that forces vines to root deep, concentrating flavour while moderating vigour. The warm continental climate of the valley floor delivers the mid-palate generosity the house style demands, while the old-vat regime preserves a freshness that new-oak programs would suppress.

Vinification follows Penfolds' acclimatised pure yeast culture protocol in temperature-controlled fermenters. Post-fermentation, the wine enters large old oak vats — no percentage of new wood, no barrique influence — allowing extended maturation without the structural intervention that defines Grange or Bin 707. The result is a Shiraz whose tannin architecture comes entirely from grape skin contact, not cooperage.

St Henri is the only wine in the Penfolds red portfolio that categorically excludes new oak at every stage, making it the house's purest expression of Barossa Shiraz fruit. The 2021 Lunar New Year Horse Edition presents the wine in gift packaging; drinking window 2024–2035.

name: Albert Bichot Bourgogne Pinot Noir “Origines”
short_description (EN Store): Regional Bourgogne Pinot Noir vinified at Beaune's renovated central winery — cold-soaked 6–8 days, aged ~20% new Tronçais oak, bright 2023 red-fruit purity.
description (EN Store):
Bernard Bichot began trading wine in Monthélie in 1831, but the structural decision that defines this house today came nearly two centuries later: rather than centralising all production in Beaune, sixth-generation Albéric Bichot deliberately maintains six geographically dispersed estate wineries, each vinifying only the wines of its own sub-region. The Origines Bourgogne is produced at the Beaune facility — renovated in 2008 — applying the same non-interventionist protocol used across the entire portfolio to a regional-AOC Pinot Noir.

Sourced from négociant partner growers across Burgundy's core sub-regions, the fruit undergoes a cold soak of 6–8 days before fermentation in thermoregulated conical oak vats and/or stainless steel, with 18–20 days total maceration. Aging runs in French oak — predominantly Tronçais and Allier forest barrels, light toast, approximately 20% new oak, the remainder in vat — calibrated to amplify fruit character rather than impose wood structure. Gravity racking is used throughout to minimise handling.

What separates this entry-level cuvée from most négociant Bourgogne is the house's insistence on applying identical vinification logic — cold soak, thermoregulated fermentation, light-toast Tronçais — at every appellation tier, rather than reserving technique for Grand Cru labels. The warm 2023 vintage delivered excellent sanitary conditions and a fresh, aromatic profile; reds show bright red-fruit purity and are approachable now through 2027.

name: Albert Bichot Gevrey-Chambertin
short_description (EN Store): Négociant Gevrey-Chambertin from Domaine du Clos Frantin's marl-gravel Côte de Nuits soils; 2022 ripe fruit, supple tannins, medium-term structure.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot's structural transgression in Burgundy is architectural: rather than centralising vinification in Beaune, the house operates six geographically dispersed estate wineries, each vinifying only its own sub-region's fruit. For Gevrey-Chambertin, that means Domaine du Clos Frantin — a ~7.5 ha estate in the Côte de Nuits whose marl-and-gravel soils over Jurassic limestone bedrock deliver the appellation's characteristic iron-edged depth and firm skeletal structure. The 2022 growing season — hot and dry, with an early harvest — pushed Pinot Noir to generous ripeness, softening the village's typically austere tannin frame while preserving the site's mineral backbone. Vinification follows a cold soak of 6–8 days, then fermentation in thermoregulated conical oak vats with 18–20 days total maceration; aging runs 12–16 months in light-toast Tronçais and Allier barrels at 20–30% new oak for village-level reds, calibrated to amplify terroir rather than impose wood. Gravity racking throughout minimises handling. What sets this apart at village level is the Clos Frantin address itself — the same estate cellar that handles Chambertin and Richebourg fruit, applying identical non-interventionist discipline to the village wine. The 2022 is approachable now, with the structure to develop over five to eight years.

name: Albert Bichot Nuit-Saint-Georges
short_description (EN Store): Côte de Nuits village Pinot Noir from Domaine du Clos Frantin's marl-and-gravel soils, vinified at source with 18–20 days maceration and light-toast Tronçais oak.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot's structural decision to vinify every wine at the estate closest to its source — rather than trucking fruit to a central Beaune facility — means this Nuits-Saint-Georges is made at Domaine du Clos Frantin, the house's 7.5-hectare Côte de Nuits holding whose marl-and-gravel soils sit among parcels that extend up to Grand Cru level (Chambertin, Richebourg, Clos de Vougeot). That marl-over-gravel profile delivers the characteristic Nuits tension: iron-edged density from the clay fraction, drainage-driven concentration from the gravel. Vinification follows a cold soak of 6–8 days before fermentation in thermoregulated conical oak vats, with 18–20 days total maceration to extract structure without astringency. Aging runs 12–16 months in light-toast Tronçais and Allier barrels at 20–30% new oak — calibrated to amplify terroir rather than impose a house signature, a deliberate counter-position to the heavier oak regimes common among large Burgundy négociants. What distinguishes this cuvée within the Bichot portfolio is its direct link to Clos Frantin's Grand Cru-adjacent parcels: the same winemaking team, the same cellar, the same gravity-racking protocol used for Richebourg handles this village wine. The 2022 vintage — hot, dry summer, early harvest — delivers ripe, supple tannins and generous fruit with enough structure for 5–8 years of development.

name: Albert Bichot Pommard
short_description (EN Store): Domaine du Pavillon's 3.5 ha Pommard monopole, cold-soaked 6–8 days, aged in light-toast Tronçais oak — 2022's heat delivers supple tannins and early generosity.
description (EN Store):
Albert Bichot's Domaine du Pavillon holds a 3.5 ha monopole in Pommard — Clos des Ursulines — a structural rarity in a village where climat fragmentation is the norm. Rather than centralise vinification in Beaune, Bichot vinifies this wine at the estate closest to the vines, a deliberate house-wide policy that preserves the micro-terroir distinctions that a central winery would flatten.

Pommard's soils here are iron-rich clay-limestone with a shallow topsoil over compacted marl, producing Pinot Noir of notable density and grip compared to the lighter gravel-over-limestone of neighbouring Volnay. The village's lower elevation and eastward aspect concentrate phenolic ripeness, particularly in a hot year.

Harvest was handpicked, followed by a cold soak of 6–8 days and total maceration of 18–20 days in thermoregulated conical oak vats; this cuvée incorporates up to 30% whole-bunch, adding structural grip and aromatic lift. Aging runs 12–16 months in light-toast Tronçais and Allier barrels at village-level new-oak proportions (20–30%), calibrated to amplify terroir rather than impose wood character.

The whole-bunch component is the transgression here — unusual for a village-level Pommard and more commonly reserved for Premier Cru cuvées in the Côte de Beaune.

The 2022 vintage — hot, dry, early harvest — delivers ripe, generous fruit with supple tannins; approachable now but structured for 5–10 years of cellaring.

name: Chateau Mouton Rothschild  2017 (Premiers Crus)
short_description (EN Store): Plateau de Mouton's deep Günzian gravel, 44 oak vats, 100% new French oak — the 2017 delivers Pauillac's most hedonistic First Growth in a cooler, precise vintage frame.
description (EN Store):
Baron Philippe de Rothschild's 1924 decision to château-bottle his own wine — then a radical act in a négociant-dominated Bordeaux — is the founding logic of everything Mouton does: total control from vine to label. That philosophy reaches its physical apex in the gravity-fed vat room inaugurated in 2012–2013, housing 44 oak fermentation vats of varying sizes alongside 20 stainless-steel tanks, enabling parcel-by-parcel vinification without pump stress on the fruit.

The Plateau de Mouton rises to 27 m above sea level in northern Pauillac, its soils a column of deep Günzian gravel descending over 5 m onto clay-limestone and iron-rich marl — a profile that drains aggressively, forces roots deep, and retains heat through cool nights, concentrating Cabernet Sauvignon to a degree unmatched on the Left Bank. The 2017 vintage, shaped by a late-April frost followed by a warm, dry summer, produced a leaner structural frame than the flanking 2016 and 2018, yet Mouton's gravel croupe buffered the frost damage better than lower-lying estates.

Aging runs 19–22 months in 100% new French oak barrels (medium toast), first in the 100-metre Grand Chai designed by Charles Siclis in 1926, then transferred to second cellars. The wine is fined with egg white before château bottling. Mouton remains one of the last Médoc estates to use oak as its primary fermentation vessel — a deliberate retention of tradition inside an otherwise modernised facility.

The 2017 is the category's outlier: a First Growth built for earlier access than its peers, with the estate's signature black-fruit density and cedar-graphite spine present but in a more approachable register. Drink 2024–2045.

name: One By Penfolds Australia Syrah Grenache Mourvedre
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' Southern Rhône-inspired Bin 138 blend — Shiraz, Grenache, Mourvèdre from Barossa's ancient phylloxera-free sandy loam soils.
description (EN Store): Penfolds has bottled a Southern Rhône-inspired blend since the Bin 138 program, a deliberate transgression from the house's Shiraz-dominant orthodoxy — here, Grenache and Mourvèdre share the frame alongside Shiraz, drawing on Barossa Valley fruit grown in ancient, deep alluvial and sandy loam soils that have never required replanting due to phylloxera-free status. Those soils — low-vigour, heat-retaining, and exceptionally old — push vines toward concentrated, low-yield fruit with a distinctly earthy, ironstone-tinged character that separates Barossa GSM from any Châteauneuf-du-Pape counterpart. Winemaking follows Penfolds' multi-vineyard blending philosophy, with fruit selected across Barossa sub-districts and matured in previously-used oak — the same seasoned barrels that cycle through the broader Penfolds red program — preserving fruit transparency rather than imposing new-wood structure. No single-terroir constraint applies: the blend proportions shift vintage to vintage as Chief Winemaker Peter Gago's team chases fruit quality over formula. What distinguishes this expression within the Penfolds lineup is the Grenache-driven mid-palate generosity and lower structural tension compared to the Shiraz-anchored Bins — a rare moment where the house prioritises plushness over ageability architecture.

name: One By Penfolds Australia Shiraz
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry into California Shiraz — house blending philosophy applied to Australian-born variety on New World soil, ฿999.
description (EN Store):
Penfolds was founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide — a house whose entire identity is built on the conviction that winemaker intelligence, not fixed geography, determines quality. One By Penfolds Australia Shiraz extends that philosophy beyond South Australia into California, applying the same multi-source blending discipline that built Grange to fruit sourced outside the Australian heartland.

The brand library confirms Penfolds operates across Barossa Valley's ancient deep alluvial and sandy loam soils, McLaren Vale's Mediterranean-moderated fruit, and now California — each site contributing to a house style defined by mid-palate richness and structured generosity rather than single-terroir purity. This is a deliberate transgression of the appellation-first orthodoxy dominant in both Australian and Californian fine wine.

Winemaking follows Penfolds' red wine template: temperature-controlled fermentation using acclimatised pure yeast cultures, a technique pioneered by Ray Beckwith using pH meters — a scientific intervention that predates modern winemaking norms by decades. Oak maturation aligns with house practice, prioritising fruit integration over raw wood impact.

The special feature here is categorical: this is Penfolds asserting that Shiraz is a portable variety whose character is shaped by winemaker continuity — only four Chief Winemakers in nearly 180 years — rather than by any single patch of ground.

name: One By Penfolds Australia Cabernet Sauvignon
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' multi-regional blending philosophy applied to Cabernet Sauvignon — house consistency over single-terroir, built for structure and ageability.
description (EN Store):
Penfolds was founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide — a medical man who planted vines for fortified wine prescribed to patients. Nearly 180 years later, the house remains defined by a single deliberate transgression against fine-wine orthodoxy: fruit quality and winemaker expertise over fixed-plot terroir expression, a philosophy that has produced some of the longest-lived red wines outside Bordeaux.

The One By Penfolds Cabernet Sauvignon draws on Australia's multi-regional fruit sourcing tradition — the same blending logic that underpins Bin 707 and Grange — prioritising mid-palate richness and structural consistency across vintages rather than the vintage variation inherent to single-site bottlings.

Winemaking follows the Penfolds red-wine template: temperature-controlled fermentation using acclimatised pure yeast cultures (a technique Ray Beckwith pioneered at the house using pH meters), with oak maturation providing the structural backbone. The house's four-Chief-Winemaker lineage across 180 years enforces a stylistic DNA passed down like a formal apprenticeship.

What distinguishes this expression within the Penfolds range is its entry-level positioning within a house whose blending philosophy was built for bottles costing multiples of this price — the same multi-regional sourcing logic, applied accessibly.

Drink on release through the near term for fruit-forward approachability.

name: Chateau Ducru Beaucaillou Madame de Beaucaillou Haut -Medoc 2020
short_description (EN Store): 2020 Madame de Beaucaillou from Ducru's Haut-Médoc parcels — 81% Cabernet, 300th anniversary vintage, chiselled slate-and-pencil-lead precision at entry price.
description (EN Store): The 2020 vintage marks Ducru-Beaucaillou's 300th anniversary, and production was cut 25% below 2019 levels — a deliberate contraction that concentrates the estate's already severe selection philosophy into this second-label expression. Madame de Beaucaillou draws from the 16-hectare Haut-Médoc parcels in Cussac, where the same Günz-period gravel beds — quartz pebbles, lydian jasper, and flint deposited by the ancient Garonne roughly two million years ago over Oligocene mud — deliver the drainage and heat retention that define Left Bank structure. The estuary's four daily tidal movements moderate summer heat and deflect hail, preserving the freshness that gives the 2020 its chiselled, precise character at 13.5% ABV. The 81% Cabernet Sauvignon / 19% Merlot blend was vinified parcel-by-parcel in stainless steel, with long maceration and gentle extraction, before aging in French oak barriques — a regime that imparts structure without overwhelming the site's natural slate and graphite signature. What sets this expression apart is its access point into a house that restricts Grand Vin output to just 6,000–8,000 cases annually, with only 27% of total vineyard production selected upward — meaning Madame de Beaucaillou carries the same terroir logic and winemaking discipline, redirected. The 2020 drinks with succulence now but the architecture supports cellaring through 2035+.

name: Santa Julia Cuy Malbec Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): Zero-sulfur Malbec from Maipú's gravel soils — indigenous yeast, whole-cluster ferment, unfined and unfiltered by Argentina's largest organic estate.
description (EN Store): José Alberto Zuccardi named this brand after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, but the more transgressive act came decades later: launching a Natural line that strips Malbec — Argentina's most commercially packaged grape — of sulfur additions, fining agents, and filtration entirely. Fruit originates from Maipú, Mendoza, on well-drained gravel and sandy soils at approximately 650 m a.s.l., where the arid continental climate delivers high diurnal temperature swings that preserve acidity and aromatic precision rather than jammy concentration. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous ambient yeasts, incorporating whole-cluster fruit to build structural grip and a tea-like tannic texture distinct from the polished, extracted style that dominates Mendoza at this price point. The wine is bottled unfined, unfiltered, and with zero added sulfites — a commitment that extends across the Natural line and is underpinned by Familia Zuccardi's ~400 ha of certified-organic vineyards, the largest such holding in Argentina. What sets this expression apart categorically: it applies natural-wine protocols to a mainstream New World variety at a sub-฿1,000 price, a combination almost without peer in the Southern Hemisphere. Primary plum and blackberry fruit carries a silky, tea-inflected texture; drink through 2030 for peak freshness.

name: Santa Julia  Cuy Clarete Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): Clarete-style natural red from Mendoza's largest certified-organic estate — indigenous yeast, no sulfur additions, unfined and unfiltered.
description (EN Store): José Alberto Zuccardi named this brand after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, but the winemaking philosophy behind the Natural line is anything but sentimental: zero additions, ambient fermentation, and a deliberate rejection of Mendoza's dominant extracted-and-oaked template. The Cuy Clarete sits within that Natural line as a pale red — a style almost absent from Argentine wine culture — drawing on Familia Zuccardi's certified-organic holdings in Maipú, where well-drained gravel and sandy soils at approximately 650 m a.s.l. deliver fruit with natural freshness rather than concentration. Warm days and cool nights under Mendoza's arid continental climate preserve acidity that heavier extraction would otherwise bury. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts only, and the wine is bottled unfined, unfiltered, with no added sulfites — consistent with the Natural line's NSA philosophy. The clarete approach — brief skin contact on red varieties — produces a translucent, low-tannin red closer in weight to a structured rosé than a conventional Malbec. What sets this apart categorically: no other producer at this price point in Argentina holds Fair for Life certification alongside organic status, meaning the farming ethics behind the fruit are third-party verified. Drink young for maximum aromatic lift and textural freshness.

name: Santa Julia  Cuy Cabernet Sauvignon Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): Natural-line Cab from Mendoza's largest certified-organic estate — indigenous yeasts, zero sulfur additions, unfined and unfiltered.
description (EN Store): José Alberto Zuccardi named this label after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, but the decision that defines the Natural line came decades later: a deliberate break from Mendoza's heavily extracted, sulfur-propped mainstream in favour of ambient-yeast fermentation and zero additions — a transgression at any price point, let alone under ฿1,000. The Cabernet Sauvignon fruit is drawn from Familia Zuccardi's certified-organic holdings in Mendoza, where well-drained gravel and sandy soils force deep root development and restrict vigour, concentrating fruit character without irrigation-driven dilution. The arid continental climate — warm days, cold nights, minimal rainfall — preserves natural acidity that most Mendoza reds sacrifice to ripeness. Fermentation proceeds on indigenous yeasts with no sulfur additions, consistent with the Natural line's no-additions philosophy; the wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered, preserving microbial texture and site-specific aromatic fidelity. What sets this expression apart within its category is the combination of certified-organic viticulture at scale (Argentina's largest such holding) with a genuinely interventionist-free cellar protocol — a pairing almost nonexistent at this price tier in the New World. Drink on the younger side to capture the primary fruit and the tea-like, lightly grippy tannin structure characteristic of the Natural line's Mendoza reds.

name: Billecart Salmon  Le Rose
short_description (EN Store): Old-vine Valofroy Pinot Noir from Mareuil-sur-Aÿ anchors this 40% Meunier-led rosé, built on Billecart's 1958 double cold-settling technique.
description (EN Store):
When Billecart-Salmon relaunched their Brut Rosé in the 1970s, rosé Champagne was broadly dismissed as a frivolous category — the house's insistence on a Chardonnay-dominant, pale-hued style helped rewrite that perception entirely. The still red component — roughly 8–10% Pinot Noir from the old-vine Valofroy vineyard in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, on the chalk-dominant soils south of the Montagne de Reims — is vinified separately and blended in, delivering structural precision rather than colour saturation. The chalk subsoil drives the wine's taut, saline backbone and preserves the aromatic lift that defines the house style.

The blend runs 40% Pinot Meunier (Vallée de la Marne), 30% Chardonnay, 30% Pinot Noir, with primary fermentation conducted parcel-by-parcel in 40-hl thermoregulated stainless tanks. Billecart's signature double cold-settling of must followed by low-temperature fermentation at ~13°C over 3–4 weeks — a technique borrowed from brewing by Jean Roland-Billecart in 1958 — strips the must of phenolics before fermentation begins, producing the house's characteristic aromatic purity. Lees aging runs a minimum of three years in 17th-century chalk tunnels. Dosage sits in Extra Brut territory, consistent with the house's range-wide transition. What no peer replicates is the combination of a named single-vineyard red-wine addition, Meunier dominance, and that brewing-derived cold-settling protocol in a single non-vintage rosé at this price point.

name: 7 Cascine  Prosecco Extra Dry Spumante
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Extra Dry with bright citrus, green apple, and delicate brioche notes—ideal aperitif sparkle.
description (EN Store):
7 Cascine Prosecco Extra Dry Spumante is a classic Veneto sparkler showcasing the signature freshness and elegance of the Prosecco region. This non-vintage cuvée delivers bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with supporting brioche and honeysuckle complexity from the traditional méthode. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and a clean, refreshing finish.

A versatile aperitif wine, it pairs beautifully with oysters, fresh seafood, soft cheeses, and light hors d'oeuvres. The high acidity and delicate fruit profile make it an excellent choice for casual entertaining or as an elegant welcome drink.

name: Deutz  Brut Classic
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Champagne blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir & Pinot Meunier with crisp citrus, brioche & mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Deutz Brut Classic is a non-vintage Champagne blend anchored by Chardonnay with supporting Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier. The wine displays the hallmark profile of quality Champagne: bright lemon and citrus zest on the nose, with green apple and almond notes emerging on the palate. Secondary winemaking character—brioche, toast, and subtle honey—adds depth and elegance. Fine minerality and a dry finish make this a versatile aperitif and food-friendly sparkling wine suitable for oysters, shellfish, soft cheeses, and light canapés.

name: Deutz  Brut Rose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Brut Rosé with brioche complexity and vibrant red fruit—ideal for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): Deutz Brut Rosé is a refined non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The wine displays a pale salmon hue and opens with brioche and toasted almond aromatics, followed by bright lemon zest and strawberry on the palate. High acidity and fine bubbles create a crisp, elegant mouthfeel with mineral undertones and a clean, persistent finish. A versatile aperitif wine suited to oysters, shellfish, soft cheeses, and refined canapés.

name: Pol Roger  Rich Demi-Sec
short_description (EN Store): Elegant demi-sec Champagne with brioche, apple, and honey notes—ideal for celebrations and dessert pairings.
description (EN Store): Pol Roger's Rich Demi-Sec is a balanced Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot Meunier, crafted in the house's signature style. The demi-sec dosage (residual sugar) softens the wine's natural acidity while preserving the complexity of the blend. On the nose, brioche and toasted hazelnut aromas mingle with citrus zest and delicate floral notes. The palate reveals ripe stone fruit, honey, and a creamy mouthfeel, finishing with gentle effervescence and a touch of vanilla. This wine bridges aperitif and dessert service, making it versatile for celebrations, cheese courses, and lighter sweet preparations.

name: Freixenet  Cordon Negro Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Spanish Cava with crisp apple, citrus, and brioche—a versatile aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Freixenet Cordon Negro Brut is a benchmark Spanish Cava from Penedès, blending Macabeo, Xarel·lo, and Parellada in a traditional méthode champenoise style. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, followed by delicate brioche and almond notes from extended aging on the lees. Fine, persistent bubbles and crisp acidity define the palate, with a dry finish and subtle minerality. A food-friendly sparkling wine equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, cheese, and Mediterranean tapas.

name: Freixenet  Vintage Reserva Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Catalan Cava with green apple, citrus, and brioche complexity. Elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Freixenet Vintage Reserva Brut is a traditional-method sparkling wine from Penedès, Catalunya, crafted from a balanced blend of Macabeo, Xarel·lo, and Parellada grapes. The wine displays a pale golden hue with fine, persistent bubbles and a clean, inviting nose of green apple, citrus zest, and ripe pear.

On the palate, crisp acidity drives the primary fruit character, while subtle brioche and toast notes from extended aging add complexity and elegance. The finish is dry and mineral-tinged, with a lingering citrus persistence. This is a versatile sparkling wine suited to aperitif service, seafood dishes, and celebratory occasions.

name: Opera Prima  Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store): Opera Prima Brut is a Spanish sparkling wine from La Mancha, crafted in the traditional sparkling style. The wine displays a pale golden hue with fine, persistent bubbles. On the nose, green apple and citrus dominate, complemented by subtle brioche and yeast-derived complexity. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with high acidity that provides structure and a clean, dry finish. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to aperitifs, celebrations, and light cuisine.

name: Opera Prima  Gold Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Spanish Moscato sparkler with honeysuckle florals, stone fruit, and brioche—ideal for aperitifs and light desserts.
description (EN Store): Opera Prima Gold Moscato is a non-vintage sparkling wine from La Mancha, Spain, crafted from 100% Muscat grapes. This light-bodied sparkler showcases the varietal's signature floral and stone-fruit character, with honeysuckle and peach leading the nose, supported by citrus zest and a subtle brioche complexity from the winemaking process. Fine bubbles and high acidity create a refreshing, elegant finish ideal for aperitifs, fresh cheeses, and fruit-based desserts.

name: Opera Prima  Pink Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Spanish pink sparkling with floral Muscat, stone fruit, and fine bubbles—perfect for celebrations and light appetizers.
description (EN Store): Opera Prima Pink Moscato is a lightly sparkling wine from La Mancha, Spain, built on the aromatic foundation of Muscat with a touch of Tempranillo. The wine opens with pronounced floral and honeysuckle notes characteristic of Muscat, followed by stone-fruit and citrus-zest aromatics. On the palate, fine bubbles carry peach and green-apple flavors, with a brioche undertone from the sparkling production. High acidity and light body make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for fresh cheeses, oysters, and fruit-based desserts.

name: Schramsberg  Blanc de Blancs
short_description (EN Store): California Chardonnay-Pinot Noir sparkling with brioche complexity, citrus brightness, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store):
Schramsberg's Blanc de Blancs represents a refined expression of California sparkling winemaking, built on a foundation of Chardonnay with Pinot Noir support. The wine displays a medium-full body with restrained acidity characteristic of bottle-fermented méthode champenoise production, delivering sustained mousse and elegant structure.

On the nose, green apple and citrus zest lead, supported by toasted brioche and hazelnut notes from extended aging on the lees. The palate shows stone fruit and butter complexity, with a mineral backbone that provides definition and length. The wine's balance between fruit-forward character and winemaking-derived richness makes it versatile across both celebratory occasions and food-forward dining.

name: Zonin  Prosecco Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with bright citrus and brioche notes. Perfect aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Zonin Prosecco Brut is a classic expression of Italy's most celebrated sparkling wine. Crafted from 100% Prosecco grapes in the Veneto region, this non-vintage cuvée delivers a refreshing profile of green apple, citrus zest, and delicate pear on the nose. The palate is lively and elegant, with fine bubbles carrying subtle brioche and honey notes, balanced by crisp acidity and a clean, mineral finish. An approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine ideal for celebrations or casual entertaining.

name: Bisol & Figli  Jeio Cuvee Rose Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco Rosé with red fruit, strawberry, and crisp citrus. Fine bubbles, dry finish. Versatile aperitif.
description (EN Store): Bisol & Figli's Jeio Cuvée Rosé Brut is a refined sparkling wine from Veneto's Prosecco region, crafted from Merlot and Pinot Noir. The wine opens with vibrant red fruit and strawberry aromatics, complemented by crisp citrus notes and a delicate brioche undertone from the traditional méthode. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a light body and high acidity, creating an elegant, refreshing profile ideal for aperitifs, seafood, and soft cheeses. The dry finish makes it a versatile companion to both casual gatherings and refined dining.

name: Bisol & Figli  Jeio Prosecco Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with green apple and brioche notes. Perfect aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Bisol & Figli's Jeio Prosecco Extra Dry is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted entirely from Prosecco grapes. This non-vintage expression showcases the classic profile of the category: bright acidity, delicate bubbles, and a refreshing palate. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, followed by subtle brioche and honeysuckle aromatics. On the palate, stone fruit and minerality provide structure and elegance, making this wine equally at home as an aperitif or paired with oysters, fresh seafood, and soft cheeses.

name: Champagne Ernest Rapeneau  Brut
short_description (EN Store): Classic Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Meunier & Chardonnay with crisp apple, brioche & minerality.
description (EN Store): Champagne Ernest Rapeneau Brut is a non-vintage Champagne blend combining 45% Pinot Noir, 35% Meunier, and 20% Chardonnay—a traditional composition that balances structure, richness, and elegance. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, followed by supporting notes of brioche and toasted hazelnut from the traditional méthode champenoise aging. A mineral backbone and fine acidity define the finish, making this a versatile aperitif and food-friendly sparkling wine suitable for celebrations and casual gatherings alike.

name: Bollinger  Special Cuvee
short_description (EN Store): Prestige Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Chardonnay & Meunier with brioche richness, citrus minerality & elegant complexity.
description (EN Store):
Bollinger Special Cuvée is a non-vintage Champagne blend anchored by 60% Pinot Noir, with 25% Chardonnay and 15% Meunier, crafted in the traditional méthode champenoise. The wine displays a medium-full body with vibrant high acidity and fine effervescence characteristic of premium Champagne.

On the nose, red fruit and citrus notes emerge first, followed by pronounced brioche and toasted bread aromas from extended aging on lees. Hazelnut and subtle honey notes add complexity. The palate is elegant and structured, with minerality and gentle spice providing depth and a persistent, refined finish.

This is a versatile aperitif wine and food-friendly Champagne suited to shellfish, oysters, soft cheeses, and charcuterie.

name: Fantinel  Prosecco Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with bright citrus and brioche notes. Perfect aperitif with fine bubbles and refreshing acidity.
description (EN Store): Fantinel Prosecco Extra Dry is a classic expression of Veneto's signature sparkling wine, crafted from 100% Prosecco grapes. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest aromas, complemented by delicate brioche and floral notes that emerge on the nose. On the palate, fine bubbles deliver a light, refreshing mouthfeel with bright stone fruit and subtle honey undertones, balanced by crisp acidity. The Extra Dry classification allows residual sweetness to enhance the wine's approachability without cloying. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, soft cheeses, and light appetizers.

name: Nino Franco  Prosecco Spumante Superiore "Brut" DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco Superiore with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Dry, refreshing, and versatile.
description (EN Store): Nino Franco's Prosecco Spumante Superiore Brut exemplifies the refined character of Veneto's premier sparkling wine. Crafted from 100% Glera grapes, this NV expression delivers a bright, mineral-driven profile with dominant green apple and citrus on the nose, supported by subtle brioche complexity from the secondary fermentation. The palate is crisp and dry, with fine bubbles and a clean, zesty finish. A versatile aperitif and food-friendly sparkling wine suitable for celebrations and casual gatherings alike.

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Treviso Prosecco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Ideal aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Corte Giara by Allegrini represents the benchmark quality of Prosecco DOC from the Treviso region. This sparkling wine delivers a bright, refreshing profile anchored by crisp green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with supporting pear and subtle brioche complexity from the traditional méthode Charmat production. Fine, persistent bubbles and vibrant acidity define the palate, finishing clean and dry. A versatile aperitif that excels alongside oysters, shellfish, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Billecart Salmon  Le Reserve (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Chalk-cellar NV averaging ~50 months on lees; double cold-settling at 13°C locks in Meunier-led freshness at 3 g/L Extra Brut.
description (EN Store): In 1958, Jean Roland-Billecart borrowed a technique from the brewing industry — double cold-settling of must followed by fermentation held at ~13°C for 3–4 weeks (against an industry norm of 2–3 days) — and made it the house's permanent technical signature. That single decision explains why Le Réserve tastes the way it does. Sourced from chalk-dominant soils across ~300 hectares spanning 40 crus within 20 km of Épernay — Montagne de Reims for Pinot Noir, Côte des Blancs for Chardonnay, Vallée de la Marne and Coteaux Sud d'Épernay for Pinot Meunier — the chalk subsoil drives the wine's taut mineral tension and preserves acidity through warm growing seasons. The blend runs ~40% Pinot Meunier, ~30% Chardonnay, ~30% Pinot Noir, with over 50% reserve wines drawn from up to 15 back-vintages, vinified parcel-by-parcel in 150 individual 40-hl thermoregulated stainless-steel tanks. Dosage is set at 3 g/L Extra Brut — a range-wide shift that strips away any residual-sugar cushion and forces the wine to stand on its lees-derived texture alone. What is unusual here: Le Réserve currently averages ~50 months on lees in 17th-century chalk tunnels, nearly double the legal NV minimum, giving a half-bottle format wine a structural depth that most full-format NV Champagnes never reach. The 375 ml format accelerates secondary development slightly, making this an immediately expressive, high-tension pour.

name: Villa Sandi  Prosecco Spumante Brut Millesimato Valdobbiadene
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif from Valdobbiadene.
description (EN Store): Villa Sandi's Prosecco Spumante Brut Millesimato from Valdobbiadene showcases the classic profile of Glera-based sparkling wine: bright green apple and citrus on the nose, with supporting pear and mineral notes. The palate is dry and refreshing, with fine bubbles and a subtle brioche undertone from traditional méthode. A versatile aperitif wine that pairs beautifully with oysters, fresh shellfish, and light appetizers.

name: Pol Roger  Brut Reserve (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne blend of balanced fruit, brioche complexity, and mineral precision—ideal for celebration and fine dining.
description (EN Store): Pol Roger Brut Reserve is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Pinot Noir (33%), Chardonnay (34%), and Pinot Meunier (33%), representing the house's signature style of elegance and consistency. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, supported by a mineral backbone characteristic of Champagne's chalk terroir. On the palate, secondary notes of brioche and toasted hazelnut emerge from the extended aging on lees, adding richness and complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural freshness. The high acidity and fine bubbles create a crisp, refined mouthfeel suitable for both aperitif service and food pairing.

name: Villa Sandi  Prosecco Spumante "Il Fresco" (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—ideal aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Villa Sandi's Il Fresco Prosecco Spumante exemplifies the refreshing character of Veneto's signature sparkling wine. Glera grapes deliver a bright primary profile of green apple and citrus zest, supported by delicate brioche notes from the traditional méthode. Fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity make this 375 ml format perfect for intimate gatherings or solo enjoyment. Dry on the finish, it pairs beautifully with oysters, fresh cheeses, and light appetizers.

name: Villa Sandi  Prosecco Spumante "Il Fresco"
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Villa Sandi's Il Fresco is a dry Prosecco Spumante from Veneto's heartland, crafted from Glera grapes. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, followed by delicate pear notes and a whisper of brioche on the palate. Fine, persistent bubbles and bracing acidity define its character—a refreshing, food-friendly sparkler ideal for aperitifs, raw seafood, and light appetizers.

name: Fleur De France  Blanc de Blanc Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chardonnay sparkling from Languedoc with green apple, citrus, and toasted brioche—elegant aperitif.
description (EN Store): Fleur de France Blanc de Blanc Brut is a non-vintage Chardonnay sparkling wine from the Languedoc-Roussillon region, crafted in the traditional méthode champenoise style. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by a mineral backbone and subtle pear notes. On the palate, toasted brioche and butter emerge from the secondary fermentation and aging, complemented by gentle oak influence. The fine mousse and medium body deliver a refreshing, elegant profile ideal for aperitifs, oysters, and delicate seafood preparations.

name: 7 Cascine  Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco Superiore with crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche—ideal aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): 7 Cascine Conegliano Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's premier Prosecco region. This 100% Prosecco expression showcases the classic profile of the Superiore designation: bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with delicate floral and stone-fruit undertones. The palate reveals fine, persistent bubbles and a mineral backbone, complemented by subtle brioche and honey notes from traditional production methods. Light-bodied with vibrant acidity, it delivers elegance and freshness—a versatile choice for aperitifs, seafood, and celebratory moments.

name: Moet & Chandon  Ice Imperial (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Champagne engineered for ice: Moët's deliberate high-dosage, Pinot-driven NV built to be poured over a large cube — a category transgression from Épernay.
description (EN Store): When Benoît Gouez, Chef de Cave since 2005, set out to create a Champagne designed explicitly for service over ice, he broke one of the category's foundational rules: sparkling wine and dilution are incompatible. Ice Impérial is formulated with a significantly elevated dosage — far above the ~7 g/L of Brut Impérial — so that melting ice becomes a calibrated ingredient rather than a flaw, progressively opening the wine's texture as it chills. The fruit base draws on Moët's 1,190-hectare estate across Champagne's five sub-regions, with chalky limestone soils delivering the structural backbone that keeps the wine from collapsing under dilution. Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier dominate the assemblage, contributing the red-fruit density and body needed to sustain the high residual sugar without cloying. Fermentation proceeds in thermoregulated stainless steel — the house pioneered this in Champagne — preserving primary fruit clarity. The wine undergoes full malolactic fermentation, adding the creamy mid-palate weight that reads as richness rather than sweetness when cold. What no peer does: Ice Impérial is the only Champagne AOC expression from a major house explicitly dosed and blended for on-ice service, with packaging (wide-mouth bottle, oversized label) engineered for outdoor, poolside, and festival contexts. Drink upon release; not built for cellaring.

name: Prego Nero  Prosecco Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Ideal aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Prego Nero Prosecco Brut is a classic expression of Veneto's signature sparkling wine, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle pear and stone fruit notes. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refined mouthfeel, while a whisper of brioche adds complexity without overwhelming the wine's essential freshness. The high acidity and light body make this an exceptionally versatile aperitif and food-friendly sparkler.

Best served well-chilled as an aperitif, with oysters, or alongside Asian cuisine where its crispness complements spice and umami-rich flavors.

name: Cinzano  Prosecco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco DOC with green apple, citrus, and brioche—ideal for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): Cinzano Prosecco DOC is a classic Italian sparkling wine from the Veneto region, crafted from Glera grapes. This non-vintage expression delivers bright, refreshing character with dominant green apple and citrus notes on the nose, complemented by subtle brioche undertones from the traditional méthode. Fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity define the palate, making it an excellent aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh seafood. The dry finish and elegant profile reflect the hallmark style of Prosecco DOC.

name: Deor  Gold Collection Vino Spumante Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Venetian Extra Dry sparkling with crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Deor's Gold Collection Vino Spumante Extra Dry showcases the indigenous Garganega of Veneto alongside Moscato, delivering a refined sparkling wine with fine, persistent bubbles. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, supported by delicate floral notes and a whisper of brioche from the traditional méthode. On the palate, high acidity and a light body create an elegant, refreshing profile ideal for aperitif service or pairing with oysters, soft cheeses, and Asian cuisine. The dry finish and minerality make this a versatile choice for celebrations and casual entertaining.

name: Pommery  Royal Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Champagne blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir & Pinot Meunier with bright citrus, brioche & mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Pommery Royal Brut is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. This classic cuvée delivers bright lemon and green apple on the nose, with supporting brioche and almond notes from traditional méthode champenoise aging. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and a clean, refreshing finish. Versatile and food-friendly, it suits aperitifs, oysters, and celebratory occasions.

name: Fantini  Gran Cuvee Bianco
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Italian sparkling wine from Abruzzo with crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche notes—ideal for seafood and celebrations.
description (EN Store): Fantini's Gran Cuvée Bianco is a refined sparkling wine from Abruzzo's Montepulciano region, crafted from the indigenous Cococciola grape. This NV expression delivers a bright, mineral-driven profile with dominant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, complemented by delicate brioche and stone fruit undertones. Fine bubbles and high acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly character suited to raw seafood, fresh cheese, and elegant aperitif moments. A versatile Italian sparkler offering excellent value at this price point.

name: Don Luciano  Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—ideal for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): Don Luciano Brut is a refreshing Spanish sparkling wine from La Mancha, crafted in the traditional méthode champenoise style. Expect a vibrant palate of green apple and citrus zest, complemented by delicate brioche and stone fruit notes from extended aging on the lees. Fine, persistent bubbles and bright acidity make this an excellent aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Villa Sandi  Blanc De Blancs Spumante Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Glera sparkler from Veneto with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store): Villa Sandi's Blanc de Blancs Spumante Brut is a refined expression of Glera from Italy's Veneto region. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle pear and stone fruit notes. A delicate brioche character from the traditional méthode adds complexity without overwhelming the fresh, mineral-driven palate. Fine bubbles and bracing acidity define the finish, making this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and light appetizers.

name: Gosset Champagne "Grand Reserve Brut"
short_description (EN Store): Gosset Grand Reserve Brut: elegant Champagne blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir with crisp citrus, brioche complexity, and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Gosset Grand Reserve Brut is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, representing the house's signature style of refined elegance. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by a palate marked by brioche richness and toasted hazelnut complexity. A mineral backbone and subtle honey notes provide depth, while the high acidity and fine mousse deliver a crisp, refreshing finish ideal for aperitif service or pairing with oysters and soft cheeses.

name: Bottega  Spumante Millesimato
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Glera with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle from Veneto.
description (EN Store): Bottega Spumante Millesimato is a dry sparkling wine from Italy's Prosecco region, crafted entirely from Glera grapes. The wine presents a light body with vibrant acidity and fine bubbles characteristic of quality Prosecco production. On the nose, green apple and citrus zest dominate, supported by subtle brioche and toast notes from the winemaking process. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with a dry finish that invites another sip. This is an ideal aperitif wine or pairing for delicate seafood, fresh cheeses, and light appetizers.

name: Bottega  Prosecco DOC Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, dry Prosecco with green apple and citrus—ideal for celebrations and seafood.

Fine, persistent bubbles and a clean, mineral finish make it an elegant aperitif or celebratory pour. name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple and citrus, fine bubbles, and brioche complexity. Elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Bottega Prosecco Gold Brut is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, supported by delicate pear notes and a whisper of brioche from the winemaking process. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a light, elegant mouthfeel with crisp acidity and a dry finish. This is a versatile aperitif wine, equally at home before dinner with canapés or paired with oysters, sushi, and soft cheeses. The wine's freshness and complexity make it an excellent choice for celebrations and refined entertaining.

name: Bottega  Spumante Rose Gold Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir sparkling from Prosecco country—fine bubbles, red berries, brioche, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Bottega's Spumante Rosé Gold Brut showcases the versatility of Pinot Noir in the Prosecco heartland of Veneto. This non-vintage sparkling wine delivers a delicate balance of red berry fruit and stone fruit character on the nose, complemented by brioche and toasted bread notes from the traditional méthode. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles that persist elegantly through a dry, refreshing finish. The high acidity and light body make it an ideal aperitif and food-friendly companion to shellfish, oysters, and soft cheeses.

name: Bottega  Diamond Brut
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir sparkling from Lombardy with crisp apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.

Primary aromas of green apple and citrus zest lead to secondary notes of brioche and stone fruit, underpinned by subtle minerality. The high acidity and elegant structure make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and delicate cheeses.

name: Krug  Grande Cuvee (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Krug's iconic non-vintage Champagne: brioche-rich, mineral-driven, and elegantly complex.
