This is a sophisticated aperitif and food-friendly Champagne suited to celebration and refined dining.

name: Krug  Rose (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Rosé with red fruit, brioche, and fine mousse—a versatile aperitif of refined complexity.
description (EN Store): Krug Rosé is a non-vintage Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Pinot Meunier, and Chardonnay, crafted in the house's signature style. The wine displays a delicate balance of red fruit and raspberry on the nose, complemented by brioche and citrus notes from traditional Champagne winemaking. High acidity and a fine, persistent mousse define the palate, delivering freshness and elegance. The dry profile and structured complexity make it equally suited to aperitif service or pairing with refined cuisine.

name: Veuve Clicquot  Brut Yellow Label (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 50–60 crus, 30 months on chalk-cellar lees — built on the widow who invented the riddling table in 1816.
description (EN Store): In 1816, a 27-year-old widow named Barbe-Nicole Clicquot Ponsardin drilled holes into a kitchen table and invented the pupitre — the riddling board that made mass-produced, clear Champagne possible. That same drive toward structural precision defines the Yellow Label today. Sourced from 50–60 crus across 12 Grand Cru villages — Bouzy, Ambonnay, Verzenay, Aÿ among them — grown on shallow chalk (craie) hillside soils that force vine roots deep, delivering natural drainage, high sun exposure, and the taut mineral backbone that anchors the house's generous fruit. The blend runs Pinot Noir-dominant (~50–55%), with Chardonnay (28–33%) and Pinot Meunier (15–20%); 25–50% reserve wines spanning up to 30 vintages are folded in for consistency and depth. Alcoholic fermentation in stainless steel is followed by full malolactic conversion, softening the chalk-driven acidity into a rounder texture, while a small proportion of base wines see large oak foudres (a practice introduced in 2008) for added complexity. The wine ages a minimum of 30 months on lees — double the AOC requirement — in 24 km of UNESCO-listed chalk crayères beneath Reims, before disgorgement at 9 g/L dosage. What sets this half-bottle apart is unchanged: the same assemblage philosophy as the full format, compressed into a format that accelerates development slightly, making it the most immediately expressive entry point into the house's Pinot-led architecture.

name: Veuve Clicquot  Brut Yellow Label (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 50–60 crus, 30 months on chalk-cellar lees — built on the widow who invented riddling in 1816.
description (EN Store):
In 1816, a 27-year-old widow named Barbe-Nicole Clicquot Ponsardin drilled holes into a kitchen table and invented the riddling pupitre — the mechanical clarification technique that made modern Champagne production possible at scale. That same problem-solving instinct defines Yellow Label today: rather than chasing delicacy, Cellar Master Didier Mariotti assembles 700–800 individual base wine samples from 50–60 crus, blending 25–50% reserve wines held up to 30 years to lock in house consistency across every disgorgement.

The fruit draws from 12 Grand Cru villages — Bouzy, Ambonnay, Verzy, Verzenay, Avize, Le Mesnil-sur-Oger among them — grown on shallow chalk (craie) hillside soils that force deep root penetration and deliver the structural tension that anchors the blend's generous fruit. Alcoholic fermentation runs 8–10 days in stainless steel; full malolactic fermentation is carried out across the range; since 2008, a proportion of base wines pass through large oak foudres for textural complexity. The wine then ages a minimum of 30 months on lees — double the AOC floor — in 24 km of UNESCO-listed chalk crayères beneath Reims.

The blend runs ~50–55% Pinot Noir, 28–33% Chardonnay, 15–20% Meunier, dosage set at 9 g/L. What's unusual: at this price tier, no non-vintage Champagne draws from a comparable breadth of Grand Cru and Premier Cru sources while maintaining this lees-aging commitment. Toasted brioche, chalk-dusted red apple, and white stone fruit resolve into a long, saline finish driven by the craie subsoil signature.

name: Veuve Clicquot  Cuvee Rose
short_description (EN Store): Assemblage-method rosé from 12 Grand Cru villages — 12% still red wine addition, a technique Barbe-Nicole Clicquot invented in 1818.
description (EN Store):
In 1818, Barbe-Nicole Clicquot Ponsardin — a 27-year-old widow with no legal right to run a business — produced the world's first blended rosé Champagne, establishing the assemblage method that defines this cuvée today. The Rosé NV is built on fruit from 12 of 17 Grand Cru villages across the Montagne de Reims, Côte des Blancs, and Vallée de la Marne, grown on shallow chalk (craie) hillside soils that force root systems deep, concentrate extract, and deliver the structural backbone the house's Pinot Noir-dominant philosophy demands.

The blend runs 44–48% Pinot Noir, 25–29% Chardonnay, 13–18% Meunier, with a critical 12% addition of still red wine — not saignée — for colour and red-fruit depth. Alcoholic fermentation proceeds in stainless steel over 8–10 days, followed by full malolactic fermentation to integrate acidity. The wine ages a minimum of 30 months on lees in the house's 24 km of UNESCO-listed chalk crayères beneath Reims, nearly double the AOC minimum, before disgorgement at 9 g/L dosage.

The assemblage rosé method — rather than skin-contact saignée — gives Cellar Master Didier Mariotti precise control over colour intensity and tannin contribution, producing a fuller-bodied rosé than most saignée-based peers at this price tier. Reserve wines drawn from up to 50–60 crus, held between 1 and 30 years, anchor the non-vintage consistency across every release.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Founded 1743, Épernay: 200+ crus blended across chalky limestone Champagne, 24 months on lees — double the AOC minimum.
description (EN Store): When Claude Moët established his house on the Avenue de Champagne in 1743, the ambition was scale without compromise — a philosophy that drove Moët to pioneer stainless steel thermoregulated fermentation and systematic malolactic fermentation before either became industry standard. The Brut Impérial NV is the direct expression of that industrial-scientific rigour applied to non-vintage Champagne. Fruit is drawn from across 234 of Champagne's 319 crus — spanning chalky limestone soils of the Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — with own-estate vineyards covering approximately 1,190 hectares, 50% of which hold Grand Cru status. The chalk subsoil drives tension and preserves acidity through the growing season. A tasting committee of 10 oenologists evaluates 822+ base wines before Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez finalises the assemblage: Pinot Noir 30–40%, Pinot Meunier 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30%, anchored by 20–30% reserve wines and dosed at approximately 7 g/L. The wine then ages a minimum of 24 months on lees in 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay — 9 months beyond the AOC non-vintage minimum. What no peer at this production volume (28–30 million bottles/year) replicates is the laboratory-grade blending discipline: the same house character, vintage to vintage, at a scale that makes it simultaneously the world's largest and one of its most technically consistent Champagne operations.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Founded 1743, Épernay: 200+ crus, 24 months on chalk-cellar lees — NV Champagne engineered by a 10-person tasting committee at 28M-bottle scale.
description (EN Store):
When Claude Moët established his house on the Avenue de Champagne in 1743, he built what would become the largest single vineyard estate in Champagne — approximately 1,190 hectares of chalky limestone soils spanning all five sub-regions: Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube. That chalk subsoil, consistent across 234 of Champagne's 319 crus, drives the house's signature tension between fruit generosity and mineral backbone.

Brut Impérial NV is assembled from 200+ crus — Pinot Noir 30–40%, Pinot Meunier 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30% — anchored by 20–30% reserve wines to sustain house character across harvests. Fermentation occurs in thermoregulated stainless steel tanks with systematic malolactic fermentation, a process Moët pioneered in the region. The wine ages a minimum of 24 months on lees in 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay — well beyond the AOC's 15-month NV minimum — before disgorgement at ~7 g/L dosage and 12% ABV.

The special feature here is industrial-scale scientific precision: Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez leads a committee of 10 oenologists evaluating 822+ base wines multiple times annually — a blending apparatus no small house can replicate, and the reason this NV holds genuine consistency at 28–30 million bottles per year.

name: Moet & Chandon  Grand Vintage 2015
short_description (EN Store): 2015 drought-forged Extra Brut: Pinot Noir 44%, 7+ years on chalk-cellar lees, 5 g/L dosage — nervy tension beneath brioche richness. WS 94.
description (EN Store): When Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez — in post since 2005 — confronted the extreme drought of 2015, he leaned into the vintage's intensity rather than correcting it: the result is an Extra Brut at just 5 g/L dosage, the lowest sugar level in the Grand Vintage range, letting the year's concentrated fruit carry the wine unmasked. The fruit draws from Moët's ~1,190-hectare estate across Champagne's five sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — all rooted in chalky limestone soils whose water-retention properties were critical in a year when drought stress compressed berry size and amplified phenolic concentration. The assemblage locks in Pinot Noir at 44%, Chardonnay 32%, Pinot Meunier 24%, fermented in thermoregulated stainless steel with systematic malolactic fermentation, then aged a minimum of 6–7 years on lees in Moët's 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay — the most extensive in the region — before disgorgement. What distinguishes this expression within the Grand Vintage lineage is the drought-driven structural tension: the low dosage exposes a nervy, almost austere spine of acidity beneath almond, brioche, and white peach, a profile closer to Blanc de Blancs austerity than the house's characteristically seductive fruit-forward register. Wine Spectator 94, Vinous 93; at peak now, can hold to 2030.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial (Magnum) (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Moët's 28km chalk cellars, 200+ cru blend, 24-month lees aging — the NV Brut Impérial in magnum: slower secondary fermentation, extended plateau, amplified texture.
description (EN Store): When Claude Moët established his house on the Avenue de Champagne in 1743, the founding logic was scale without compromise — a philosophy that forced Moët to pioneer stainless steel fermentation and systematic malolactic conversion decades before peers adopted either. The Brut Impérial NV is that philosophy's purest expression. Chalky limestone soils drawn from all five Champagne sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — contribute the house's signature mineral backbone, while 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay hold constant humidity and temperature through a minimum 24 months on lees, nine months beyond the AOC non-vintage floor. Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez and a ten-person oenologist committee evaluate 822+ base wines to assemble a blend of 200+ crus: typically Pinot Noir 30–40%, Pinot Meunier 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30%, anchored by 20–30% reserve wines and finished at ~7 g/L dosage. The magnum format is the special feature: slower CO₂ integration during secondary fermentation produces finer, more persistent mousse and a measurably longer plateau of freshness than the standard 75 cl. At 28–30 million bottles per year, no house in Champagne operates at this volume while maintaining a tasting committee of this depth — making the Brut Impérial simultaneously the category's most democratic and most laboratory-rigorous non-vintage.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial (Jeroboam) (3 L)
short_description (EN Store): Jeroboam of Moët's 200+-cru NV blend: 24 months on chalk-cellar lees, ~7 g/L dosage, Pinot-led fruit anchored by 20–30% reserve wines.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1743 by Claude Moët, the house built its reputation on a counterintuitive proposition: that scale and technical precision are not opposites. Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez (in post since 2005) convenes a tasting committee of ten oenologists to evaluate 822+ base wines before assembling the Brut Impérial NV — a deliberate, laboratory-driven act of authorship, not industrial convenience.

Fruit is drawn from across all five Champagne sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — on chalky limestone soils that drive the wine's characteristic tension and fine-bead effervescence. The estate's 1,190 hectares span 234 of Champagne's 319 crus, with 50% Grand Cru and 25% Premier Cru holdings.

The blend runs Pinot Noir 30–40%, Pinot Meunier 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30%, with 20–30% reserve wines anchoring house consistency. Fermentation is in thermoregulated stainless steel; malolactic fermentation is systematic — a technique Moët pioneered in the region. Lees ageing runs a minimum 24 months in the house's 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay, well above the AOC's 15-month NV floor, at ~7 g/L dosage.

The Jeroboam format (3 L) slows secondary fermentation and lees contact relative to standard bottles, producing a measurably creamier mousse and extended aromatic development — brioche and pastry giving way, with time, to coffee, chocolate, and undergrowth.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial Methuselah (6 L)
short_description (EN Store): Brut Impérial NV in Methuselah: 28 km of chalk cellars, 200+ crus, 24-month lees aging — scaled to 6 litres of house-defining vivacity.
description (EN Store): When Claude Moët established his maison in 1743, the ambition was always volume without compromise — a philosophy that today manifests in a tasting committee of 10 oenologists evaluating 822+ base wines before Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez assembles the final blend. That scientific rigour is what makes the Brut Impérial NV credible at scale. The fruit draws from 200+ crus across all five Champagne sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — all rooted in chalky limestone soils that drive the house's signature bright, slightly creamy vivacity. Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier each contribute 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30%, anchored by 20–30% reserve wines for cross-vintage consistency. Fermentation is in thermoregulated stainless steel — a technique Moët pioneered in Champagne — followed by systematic malolactic fermentation and a minimum 24 months on lees in 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay, nearly double the AOC minimum. Dosage sits at approximately 7 g/L. The Methuselah format (6 litres, equivalent to eight standard bottles) slows secondary fermentation and lees contact proportionally, producing a measurably slower-evolving mousse and extended autolytic development — brioche and pastry now, coffee and truffle with further cellaring — that the standard 75 cl format cannot replicate at the same pace.

name: Moet & Chandon  Rose Imperial (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Rosé Impérial NV: Pinot Noir-led blend across 200+ Champagne crus, chalk-cellar aged beyond AOC minimums, built for vivid red-fruit immediacy.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1743 by Claude Moët on the Avenue de Champagne in Épernay, the house built its reputation on scale without sacrificing technical rigour — a tension that defines every cuvée, including this Rosé Impérial. The chalky limestone soils threading through all five sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — deliver the taut mineral backbone that prevents the wine's fruit-forward character from tipping into sweetness. Pinot Noir anchors the blend at 30–40%, supported by 30–40% Pinot Meunier and 20–30% Chardonnay, drawn from across 200+ crus including parcels rated Grand Cru and Premier Cru. Reserve wines comprising 20–30% of the assemblage are evaluated by a tasting committee of ten oenologists working through 822+ base wines annually under Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez. Fermentation in thermoregulated stainless steel — a method Moët pioneered in Champagne — preserves primary red-fruit character, while lees ageing in the house's 28 km of chalk cellars exceeds the AOC non-vintage minimum of 15 months. The rosé's particular distinction within the Moët range is its deliberate construction for immediate aromatic vivacity rather than the autolytic complexity the house pursues in its Grand Vintage tier — a conscious stylistic inversion. Dosage sits at approximately 7 g/L; 12% ABV.

name: Dom Perignon  Rose Vintage with Gift Box
short_description (EN Store): Rosé from nine Grand/Premier Cru sites on deep Champagne chalk — exclusively vintage-declared, zero Pinot Meunier, extended lees ageing under radical reductive discipline.
description (EN Store): Dom Pérignon has never produced a non-vintage wine in its entire history — every bottle carries a year, and if that year falls short, nothing is released. That absolute standard is the founding logic of the world's first prestige cuvée, commercially launched in 1936 and named for Dom Pierre Pérignon, Benedictine cellarmaster at Abbaye d'Hautvillers from 1668. The Rosé Vintage draws Pinot Noir from Aÿ, Bouzy, Mailly-Champagne, and Verzenay, and Chardonnay from Chouilly, Cramant, Avize, and Le Mesnil-sur-Oger — all Grand or Premier Cru — rooted in deep chalk and limestone that functions as a moisture reservoir, sustaining vine hydration through stress periods and driving the house's signature mineral tension. Vinification is entirely in stainless steel, with malolactic fermentation conducted and oxygen contact minimised at every stage — a reductive philosophy that suppresses oxidative development and locks in the smoky, chalk-driven aromatic profile. Chef de Cave Vincent Chaperon deploys two distinct yeast strains for primary fermentation and two further strains for the in-bottle prise de mousse, treating yeast selection as the primary lever for long-term ageing capacity. No oak is used at any point. The Plénitude system — releasing the same undisgorged vintage at three separate maturation windows (P1 ~8–9 yrs, P2 ~15 yrs, P3 ~30–40 yrs) — has no true parallel among grandes marques. Dosage sits at approximately 4.5–5 g/L, preserving the site's natural acidity rather than masking it.

name: Dom Perignon  (luminated label)
short_description (EN Store): 2008 'miracle vintage' from nine Grand/Premier Cru crus — reductive lees aging 8+ yrs on chalk; Robert Parker 96+pts, best DP since 1996.
description (EN Store): Vincent Chaperon called 2008 a 'miracle' — a punishing growing season that, against all odds, yielded a wine of ferocious tension and chalky austerity now considered the finest Dom Pérignon since 1996. That paradox is the story of this bottle. Grapes are drawn from a fixed core of nine crus anchored in deep chalk and limestone: Chardonnay from Chouilly, Cramant, Avize, and Le Mesnil-sur-Oger on the Côte des Blancs; Pinot Noir from Aÿ, Bouzy, Mailly-Champagne, Verzenay, and the south-facing Premier Cru slopes of Hautvillers above the Marne valley. Chalk functions as a moisture reservoir, sustaining vine hydration through stress — and in 2008, that stress is written directly into the wine's bone-dry, mineral-driven architecture. Whole-cluster Coquard pressing delivers low-tannin juice; Chardonnay and Pinot Noir are vinified separately in stainless steel under an intensely reductive protocol that eliminates oxygen contact at every stage. Two distinct yeast strains drive primary fermentation; two further strains execute the in-bottle prise de mousse, with lees contact extending a minimum of eight to nine years in chalk cellars — no oak at any point. Dom Pérignon is the only major Champagne house that produces exclusively vintage wine, releasing nothing in years that fail its standard. The 2008 earned 96+ points from Robert Parker and is structured for a drinking window of 2020–2040+, rewarding patience with a profile of vibrant citrus, chalky minerality, and smoky reduction that few Champagne vintages of the decade can match.

name: Nua  Vino Spumante Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Piedmont sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Nua Vino Spumante Brut is a light-bodied sparkling wine from Piedmont, crafted from local grapes and bottled as a non-vintage cuvée. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus aromatics, supported by subtle brioche and stone fruit notes from the traditional sparkling winemaking process. High acidity and fine bubbles deliver a crisp, refreshing finish ideal for aperitif service or pairing with oysters, seafood, and soft cheeses.

name: Nua  Prosecco Vino Spumante Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—an elegant, dry sparkling wine from Veneto.
description (EN Store): Nua Prosecco Vino Spumante Extra Dry is a vibrant sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from the Glera grape. This Extra Dry expression delivers a refreshing profile dominated by green apple and citrus notes, with subtle pear undertones and a whisper of brioche complexity from the secondary fermentation. Fine, persistent bubbles and crisp acidity define the palate, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses. The wine's dry character and elegant structure offer excellent versatility for casual entertaining and light cuisine.

name: Follador  Torri di Credazzo Cru Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Extra Brut Prosecco Superiore with crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—a refined aperitif.
description (EN Store): Torri di Credazzo Cru Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Brut represents the pinnacle of traditional Prosecco craftsmanship from the Follador house. This Extra Brut expression showcases the precision and elegance of the Valdobbiadene terroir, with a light, effervescent character defined by green apple, citrus zest, and delicate stone fruit on the nose. The palate reveals subtle brioche complexity from the winemaking process, balanced by vibrant acidity and a mineral finish that lingers gracefully. The fine bubbles and dry profile make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, fresh seafood, and light appetizers.

name: Follador  Prosecco Runier treviso Extra Dry Silver DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Prosecco Extra Dry with crisp green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—a versatile aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Follador's Prosecco Runier Treviso Extra Dry Silver DOC is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. This non-vintage expression showcases the hallmark freshness and elegance of quality Prosecco: bright green apple and citrus on the nose, supported by delicate pear and subtle brioche complexity. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles that dance across the tongue and a dry finish that invites another sip. A versatile aperitif and natural partner to oysters, shellfish, and light appetizers.

name: Follador  Prosecco Farder treviso Extra Dry Gold DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Extra Dry with fine bubbles and elegant dry finish.
description (EN Store): Follador's Prosecco Farder Treviso Extra Dry Gold showcases the classic elegance of Glera from the Prosecco heartland. Bright green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, complemented by subtle pear and floral hints. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with fine bubbles that dance across the tongue, finishing dry with a whisper of brioche complexity. This is a versatile aperitif wine that excels alongside oysters, fresh seafood, and delicate appetizers.

name: Follador  Laelia Rosé Millesimato DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco Rosé with crisp green apple, citrus, and strawberry. Fine bubbles, dry finish. Perfect aperitif.
description (EN Store):
Follador's Laelia Rosé Millesimato represents the refined tradition of Prosecco sparkling wine from Veneto. Crafted from 90% Glera with 10% Pinot Nero, this dry rosé exhibits the hallmark characteristics of quality Prosecco: a delicate pale-pink hue, fine persistent bubbles, and an elegant aromatic profile.

On the nose, crisp green apple and citrus zest dominate, with subtle strawberry notes adding complexity. The palate is light and refreshing, with high acidity that cleanses and invigorates. A whisper of brioche from the production process provides textural depth without overwhelming the wine's natural fruit-forward character.

This is a versatile aperitif wine, equally at home as an opening course or paired with light seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Pitars  Cuvée Stella
short_description (EN Store): Friuli Prosecco sparkler with crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store): Pitars Cuvée Stella is a classic Prosecco from Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Italy's premier sparkling wine region. This non-vintage expression showcases the varietal's signature brightness: vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with delicate pear and floral undertones. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with fine bubbles that carry subtle brioche and honey notes from the winemaking process. A touch of minerality adds complexity without overwhelming the wine's inherent elegance. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with light seafood, fresh cheeses, and Mediterranean appetizers.

name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Ideal for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): Bottega Prosecco Gold Brut is a classic Italian sparkling wine from Veneto, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. This non-vintage expression delivers bright green apple and citrus on the nose, with supporting brioche and toast complexity from the secondary fermentation. Fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity define the palate, finishing dry and refreshing. The 1.5 L format makes it ideal for entertaining and celebrations.

name: Breganze  Prosecco Spumante Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Spumante with green apple, citrus, and brioche—a refreshing Italian sparkling for seafood and celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Breganze Prosecco Spumante Extra Dry is a classic expression of Veneto's signature sparkling wine, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, followed by subtle pear and a whisper of brioche on the palate. Fine, persistent bubbles and crisp acidity define the mouthfeel, leading to a clean, dry finish.

This Extra Dry style balances fruit-forward character with the elegance expected of quality Prosecco, making it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, oysters, and light appetizers. The restrained sweetness and high acidity ensure versatility across casual gatherings and more refined occasions.

name: Breganze  Rosa di Sera Spumante Rosato Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco rosato with crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche—perfect aperitif sparkle.
description (EN Store):
Breganze Rosa di Sera Spumante Rosato Extra Dry is a refined sparkling rosé from Veneto's Prosecco region, crafted from Glera and Marzemina grapes. This Extra Dry expression delivers a pale salmon hue with a persistent, fine bead of bubbles.

On the nose, green apple and citrus zest lead, supported by delicate brioche and floral aromatics. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with red berry notes and a dry, refreshing finish. The high acidity and light body make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, soft cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Diamante  Moscato Spumante Dolce
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Moscato Spumante with lychee, peach, and honeyed florals. Light, effervescent, and naturally sweet—ideal for dessert or celebration.
description (EN Store):
Diamante Moscato Spumante Dolce from Breganze showcases the classic charm of Veneto's Moscato grape in sparkling form. The wine opens with pronounced lychee and peach aromatics, layered with delicate floral notes and honeyed sweetness. Fine bubbles deliver a light, refreshing mouthfeel, while subtle brioche undertones add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

This naturally sweet sparkling wine excels as an apéritif, dessert companion, or celebration wine. Its aromatic profile and gentle acidity make it versatile with fruit tarts, creamy pastries, and soft cheeses, while the residual sugar and high acidity also complement spicy Asian cuisine.

name: Champagne Ruinart Blanc de Blancs
short_description (EN Store): 100% Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs from Champagne's historic Ruinart house—elegant, mineral-driven sparkling with brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Ruinart's Blanc de Blancs is a prestige Champagne composed entirely of Chardonnay, reflecting the house's commitment to finesse and elegance. The wine opens with brioche and toast aromatics layered over citrus and green apple fruit, delivering a refined entry on the palate. Minerality and subtle hazelnut notes emerge mid-palate, underpinned by the wine's characteristic high acidity and fine mousse. A Blanc de Blancs designation signals 100% Chardonnay production, emphasizing purity and aging potential. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with oysters, shellfish, and delicate cheeses.

name: Freixenet  Reserva Real Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish cava with green apple, citrus, and brioche complexity. Elegant, dry, and versatile for celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Freixenet Reserva Real Brut is a distinguished cava from Penedès, Catalunya, crafted from a traditional blend of Macabeo, Xarello, and Parellada grapes. This non-vintage sparkling wine delivers a bright, refreshing profile anchored by crisp green apple and citrus on the nose, with supporting notes of brioche and subtle toast from extended aging on the lees.

The palate is dry and elegant, with fine, persistent bubbles and a mineral edge that adds complexity. A versatile aperitif wine suitable for celebrations, seafood courses, and cheese boards, Freixenet Reserva Real Brut represents reliable quality and approachable sophistication in the cava category.

name: Laurent Perrier  Cuvee Rose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Rosé with red apple, brioche, and citrus—a refined aperitif with fine mousse and versatile food pairing.
description (EN Store): Laurent Perrier Cuvée Rosé is a distinguished non-vintage Champagne blending Pinot Noir, Chardonnay, and Pinot Meunier. The wine opens with vibrant red apple and brioche aromatics, followed by citrus and subtle strawberry notes on the palate. Fine, persistent mousse and bright acidity define its elegant structure, while minerality and delicate floral undertones add complexity. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with oysters, shellfish, soft cheeses, and refined small plates.

name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut Jeroboam (3 L)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco with crisp apple, citrus, and brioche—ideal for celebrations and aperitifs.
description (EN Store): Bottega Prosecco Gold Brut represents the classic expression of Glera from Veneto's prestigious Prosecco region. This non-vintage sparkling wine delivers a vibrant, refreshing profile anchored by green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with supporting brioche and honey notes from the traditional méthode Charmat production. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and bright acidity that make it an excellent aperitif or pairing for seafood, soft cheeses, and light appetizers. The 3 L Jeroboam format suits celebrations and entertaining.

name: Armand de Brignac  Brut Gold
short_description (EN Store): Three equal-vintage assemblage from 30m-deep Montagne de Reims chalk cellars; dosage liqueur oak-aged one year before addition — ~60,000 bottles annually.
description (EN Store):
The Cattier family has worked Premier Cru hillsides around Chigny-les-Roses since 1763, but it was a deliberate structural transgression — insisting that every release blend exactly three distinct harvest years at equal weight, rather than the industry-standard dominant base year topped with reserve wines — that defined Armand de Brignac when Jean-Jacques Cattier launched the brand in 2006. That decision is not cosmetic; it forces each assemblage to stand as a considered, equal-parts argument across three growing seasons.

Fruit originates from 33 hectares of Premier Cru chalk-and-clay hillside vineyards across Rilly-la-Montagne, Chigny-les-Roses, and Ludes on the Montagne de Reims — cool northern aspect that slows ripening and sharpens acidity in the Pinot Noir — supplemented by Grand Cru Chardonnay from Avize, Cramant, and Oger on the Côte des Blancs. The Brut Gold blend runs 40% Pinot Noir, 40% Chardonnay, 20% Pinot Meunier.

Grapes are pressed in traditional vertical Coquard presses, with only the first tête de cuvée fraction selected. Base wines are vinified in concrete tanks to build texture. The cuvée then ages a minimum three years on lees in a dedicated gated section of the Cattier cellars — descending over 30 metres underground, among the deepest chalk galleries in Champagne — before traditional hand riddling over approximately one month. The defining technical signature: the dosage liqueur itself is aged one full year in new French oak barrels before addition at disgorgement at 9 g/L, layering toasted brioche and vanilla complexity that no standard NV dosage protocol delivers. Annual production is capped at roughly 60,000 bottles, each hand-metallised and hand-labelled with French pewter.

name: Moet & Chandon  Brut Imperial (200 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Founded 1743, Épernay: 200+ crus blended across chalky limestone Champagne, 24 months on lees — fruit-forward house style in a single-serve 200 ml format.
description (EN Store): When Claude Moët established his house on the Avenue de Champagne in 1743, he built it on a principle of scale without compromise — a philosophy that today manifests in a tasting committee of 10 oenologists evaluating 822+ base wines annually before Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez finalises the Brut Impérial assemblage. The vineyards span all five Champagne sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — on chalky limestone soils that drive the house's signature mineral tension and fine, persistent mousse. The blend draws on 200+ crus, typically Pinot Noir 30–40%, Pinot Meunier 30–40%, Chardonnay 20–30%, anchored by 20–30% reserve wines for cross-vintage consistency. Fermentation is in thermoregulated stainless steel — a technique Moët pioneered in Champagne — with systematic malolactic fermentation delivering the house's deliberately creamy, fruit-forward contrast to austere, yeast-driven peers. Lees ageing runs a minimum 24 months in 28 km of chalk cellars beneath Épernay, well above the AOC's 15-month NV minimum, at a dosage of approximately 7 g/L. The 200 ml format delivers the identical assemblage and ageing protocol as the full bottle — an unusual commitment to technical parity at single-serve scale.

name: Mionetto  Prosecco Treviso Brut DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with green apple and citrus notes. Perfect aperitif with fine bubbles and refreshing acidity.
description (EN Store):
Mionetto Prosecco Treviso Brut DOC is a classic expression of Italy's most celebrated sparkling wine. Crafted from 100% Glera grapes in the Prosecco region of Veneto, this non-vintage cuvée delivers bright, refreshing character with fine, persistent bubbles.

The nose opens with crisp green apple and citrus zest, followed by subtle pear and floral hints. On the palate, lively acidity frames delicate stone fruit and a whisper of brioche complexity from the traditional production method. The finish is clean and dry, with a mineral edge that invites another sip.

An ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, seafood, and light appetizers, this Prosecco offers excellent value and versatility for casual entertaining.

name: Don Luciano  Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Spanish Moscato sparkling with lychee, peach, and floral honey—elegant aperitif or dessert companion.
description (EN Store): Don Luciano Moscato is a light-bodied Spanish sparkling wine from La Mancha, crafted from the aromatic Moscato grape. On the nose, dominant lychee and peach aromas mingle with delicate floral notes and honeyed sweetness, characteristic of the variety's expressive profile. The palate is crisp and refreshing, driven by high acidity and fine bubbles that cleanse the mouth, with supporting brioche and citrus-zest complexity from the sparkling production. A versatile aperitif or dessert wine suited to warm-weather entertaining.

name: Bottega  Moscato Manzoni Rosé
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Moscato rosé from Veneto with lychee, peach, and floral honey—perfect for Thai cuisine and celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Bottega's Moscato Manzoni Rosé is a light, effervescent sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from the aromatic Moscato grape. The wine opens with dominant lychee and peach aromas, supported by delicate floral and honey notes that define its primary character. Fine bubbles and a touch of brioche complexity on the palate create an elegant, off-dry profile ideal for warm-weather entertaining.

This non-vintage release balances natural fruit sweetness with crisp acidity, making it versatile across appetizers, Asian cuisine, and light desserts. The wine's approachable style and regional pedigree from Conegliano Valdobbiadene position it as an accessible celebration wine for diverse palates.

name: Bodegas Codorniu Raventos  Delapierre Tradicion Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Catalan Cava with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle.
description (EN Store):
Bodegas Codorniu Raventos crafts this non-vintage Cava from the classic Catalan blend of Macabeo, Parellada, and Xarel-lo. Bright green apple and citrus dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle brioche and toasted notes from traditional bottle aging. Fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity deliver a dry, refreshing finish with mineral undertones—a hallmark of quality Cava production.

Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, oysters, and light appetizers.

name: Bodegas Codorniu Raventos  Codorniu Clasico
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Catalan sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—a versatile aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Codorniu Clásico is a non-vintage Cava from the Penedès region of Catalunya, crafted from the traditional Spanish blend of Macabeo, Xarel·lo, and Parellada. This sparkling wine delivers bright primary notes of green apple and citrus zest, complemented by subtle brioche and almond complexity from its time on the lees. The fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and Spanish tapas. A reliable, food-friendly sparkling wine that balances freshness with subtle depth.

name: Bodegas Codorniu Raventos  Cava Codorniu Anna Blanc de Blancs Reserva Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Catalan Cava with Chardonnay-led complexity, fine bubbles, and citrus-brioche character.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Codorniu Raventos' Anna Blanc de Blancs Reserva Brut is a refined Catalan sparkling wine built on a foundation of Chardonnay (70%), complemented by Parellada, Xarel·lo, and Macabeo. The blend showcases the traditional elegance of white-grape Cavas, delivering bright citrus and green apple on the nose with supporting brioche and almond notes from extended aging. Fine, persistent bubbles and high acidity define the palate, while a mineral finish reflects the terroir of Catalunya. Ideal as an aperitif or alongside seafood and fresh cheeses.

name: Laurent Perrier  La Cuvée (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant non-vintage Champagne blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir & Pinot Meunier with bright citrus, brioche & mineral complexity.
description (EN Store):
Laurent Perrier La Cuvée is a benchmark non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The wine opens with vibrant primary notes of lemon and green apple, followed by a refined secondary character of brioche and toasted almond from traditional méthode champenoise aging. A subtle mineral undertone adds complexity and elegance to the finish.

High acidity and fine bubbles make this an exceptionally versatile aperitif and food wine, equally at home as an opening course or paired with seafood, soft cheeses, and light canapés.

name: Laurent Perrier  Brut Millesime 2015
short_description (EN Store): Elegant 2015 vintage Champagne: 50% Chardonnay, 50% Pinot Noir. Crisp green apple, brioche complexity, mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Laurent Perrier Brut Millésimé 2015 is a balanced Champagne blend of equal parts Chardonnay and Pinot Noir, representing the house's signature style of precision and refinement. The 2015 vintage delivers bright primary notes of green apple and citrus zest, complemented by secondary brioche and toast complexity from extended aging on the lees. A mineral backbone and subtle hazelnut undertones provide structure and elegance. High acidity and fine bubbles create a crisp, food-friendly profile ideal for aperitif service or pairing with oysters, shellfish, and refined cheese courses.

name: Laurent Perrier  La Cuvée (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Classic Champagne blend of elegance and finesse—crisp citrus, brioche, and mineral complexity in a half-bottle format.
description (EN Store):
Laurent Perrier La Cuvée is a non-vintage Champagne blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier, crafted to deliver consistent house style across vintages. The 375 ml format offers a refined aperitif or intimate celebration.

On the nose, bright lemon and green apple lead, supported by toasted brioche and almond notes. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with high acidity and fine bubbles creating an elegant mouthfeel. Chalk and citrus zest linger on the finish, characteristic of quality Champagne terroir.

name: Champagne Agrapart  Grand Cru Venus
short_description (EN Store): Zero-dosage Blanc de Blancs from La Fosse aux Pourceaux, Avize — 1959-planted vines, old demi-muid oak, second fermentation under cork.
description (EN Store): Vénus is Agrapart's most uncompromising statement: zero dosage (Brut Nature), sourced exclusively from La Fosse aux Pourceaux, a single parcel of 0.3–0.6 hectares in Avize planted in 1959 on mixed chalk-and-clay soils — the geological middle ground between the pure belemnite chalk of Minéral and the heavier clay-over-limestone of Avizoise, just metres apart on the same east-facing Côte des Blancs slope. That chalk-clay mix delivers both the saline tension of the Côte's bedrock and a textural density that makes zero dosage viable where pure-chalk parcels would feel austere. 100% Chardonnay, hand-harvested and vinified parcel-by-parcel in old 600 L demi-muids with indigenous yeasts; full malolactic fermentation; no new oak at any stage. Crucially, Vénus undergoes second fermentation under cork (agrafé) rather than crown cap — a deliberate choice that introduces micro-oxidation during the secondary fermentation itself, adding complexity unavailable to most Champagnes. Unfined and unfiltered, with a minimum of 4+ years on lees in chalk cellars at constant 10 °C. The 2009 vintage — a warm, generous year in Champagne — brings ripe stone-fruit density to the cuvée's structural frame, softening the zero-dosage austerity while the old-vine concentration sustains the finish. Drink 2024–2038.

name: Champagne Agrapart  Premier Cru 7 Crus
short_description (EN Store): Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs from 7 Côte des Blancs parcels; wild-yeast fermented, 36–48 months on lees, Extra Brut 5 g/L dosage.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1894 by Arthur Agrapart — decades before the modern grower-Champagne movement existed — this Avize-based domaine has estate-bottled under its own label continuously since, a lineage that underpins the 7 Crus NV's philosophy: blend across Grand Cru villages not for house consistency, but to map the geological range of the Côte des Blancs in a single glass. The cuvée draws Chardonnay from Avize, Cramant, Oger, and Oiry, all Grand Cru, where east-facing chalk slopes deliver natural drainage, heat retention, and the saline, iodic tension that defines the appellation's finest expressions.

Base wines are fermented in thermoregulated stainless steel at ~22 °C, then transferred to old 600 L oak demi-muids for six months' élevage — adding textural integration without imparting wood character. Indigenous yeasts drive fermentation throughout; full malolactic conversion softens the chalk-driven acidity without erasing it. The wine spends a minimum 36–48 months on lees before disgorgement, and is released unfined and unfiltered at Extra Brut, 5 g/L dosage. Reserve wines are held 50/50 in old cask and stainless steel, preserving both oxidative complexity and primary fruit.

What separates 7 Crus from most multi-village NV Champagnes is the deliberate refusal of dosage as a textural corrective — the 5 g/L ceiling forces the chalk minerality and lees-derived creaminess to do the structural work alone. As an NV, drinking window is immediate through 4–6 years from disgorgement.

name: Champagne Agrapart  Grand Cru Terriors
short_description (EN Store): 100% Chardonnay from four Côte des Blancs Grand Crus, blended across two base vintages with 40% reserve wines; Extra Brut, 5 g/L dosage, unfined and unfiltered.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1894 by Arthur Agrapart — predating the modern grower-Champagne movement by nearly a century — this Avize-based Récoltant-Manipulant has always bottled under its own label, a radical act of terroir declaration long before the term entered Champagne's vocabulary. Terroirs NV is the house's entry-point argument: 100% Chardonnay sourced from all four Grand Cru villages in the Côte des Blancs — Avize, Cramant, Oger, and Oiry — each sitting on east-facing chalk slopes where belemnite subsoil drains freely and retains warmth, compressing fruit and amplifying saline, mineral tension over richness. The blend draws from two base vintages with approximately 40% reserve wines held in equal parts old cask and stainless steel, building textural complexity without obscuring geological character. Base wines ferment in thermoregulated stainless steel at ~22 °C before six months' élevage in old oak; indigenous yeasts throughout; full malolactic fermentation completed on all cuvées. Lees aging runs a minimum of 36–48 months; the wine is released unfined and unfiltered at Extra Brut, 5 g/L dosage. Where most NV Champagnes blend for house consistency, Terroirs deliberately exposes the geological contrast between four distinct Grand Cru chalk expressions in a single assemblage. Expect chalk-driven salinity, citrus pith, and white-flower tension; drink now through 2030+.

name: Champagne Agrapart  Grand Cru Mineral
short_description (EN Store): Pure belemnite chalk from Les Champboutons (Avize) & Les Bionnes (Cramant); 6 years on lees, 3 g/L dosage, unfined and unfiltered.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1894 by Arthur Agrapart — decades before the modern grower-Champagne movement existed — this Avize domaine has always bottled under its own label, a quiet act of defiance that shaped how the Côte des Blancs is understood today. Minéral draws exclusively from two pure belemnite-chalk parcels: Les Champboutons in Avize and Les Bionnes in Cramant, both Grand Cru, on east-facing slopes where the chalk subsoil drains freely and retains heat, driving the wine's signature saline tension over any sense of richness. Vinification is entirely in old 600 L oak demi-muids with indigenous yeasts, full malolactic fermentation, and no chaptalization; the wine then ages a minimum of 6 years on lees in chalk cellars at constant 10 °C before disgorgement. Bottled unfined and unfiltered at Extra Brut, 3 g/L dosage — low enough to leave chalk-driven texture fully exposed. What separates Minéral from every neighbouring Grand Cru Blanc de Blancs is the deliberate choice to isolate pure-chalk geology from the clay-over-limestone parcels just 600 metres away, making geological contrast, not blending, the winemaking argument. The 2013 vintage, a cool year demanding precision over generosity, rewards patience: the wine is in its drinking window now but structured for another decade of development.

name: Champagne Agrapart  Grand Cru Avizoise
short_description (EN Store): Clay-over-limestone Avize parcels, 600 L demi-muids, 3 g/L dosage — Agrapart's textural counterpoint to pure-chalk Minéral.
description (EN Store):
Where Agrapart's Minéral draws from pure belemnite chalk, the Avizoise is built on a deliberate geological detour: the Les Robards and La Voie d'Épernay parcels in Avize, where clay sits over limestone rather than open chalk — a shift of roughly 600 metres that produces measurably broader texture and a rounder mid-palate weight absent from the house's other single-terroir cuvées.

Those clay-over-limestone soils slow drainage and moderate heat retention differently from the chalk parcels, yielding Chardonnay with more flesh and less saline austerity, while the east-facing Côte des Blancs slope preserves the acidity that defines the appellation.

Vinification follows the house's uncompromising parcel protocol: hand-harvest, indigenous yeast fermentation in old 600 L oak demi-muids (no new wood), full malolactic fermentation, and a minimum six years on lees in chalk cellars at constant 10 °C. Second fermentation proceeds under cork (agrafé), not crown cap — a labour-intensive choice that produces finer, more integrated bubbles. Dosage is held at Extra Brut, 3 g/L; the wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered.

The 2011 vintage — not among the library's documented benchmark years — demands patience; the structural density of the clay-influenced terroir and the extended lees aging suggest this is still in its formative drinking window, best approached from 2024 onward.

name: Champagne Charles Collin  Brut Blanc De Noir
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Blanc de Noir Champagne with brioche complexity and red fruit freshness; versatile aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Champagne Charles Collin's Brut Blanc de Noir is a refined expression of Pinot Noir in sparkling form. Crafted entirely from black grapes, this wine delivers a delicate balance of red fruit character and toasty complexity from extended aging on the lees. The nose reveals brioche, hazelnut, and subtle citrus zest, while the palate shows bright red apple, minerality, and a clean, persistent finish. High acidity and fine bubbles make this an exceptionally versatile aperitif and an excellent pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Moet & Chandon  Rose Imperial (200 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Moët's Rosé Impériale in 200 ml — chalky Épernay cellars, Pinot-led NV blend, fruit-forward house style in a single-serve format.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1743 by Claude Moët, the house built its reputation on scientific precision at scale — a tasting committee of ten oenologists evaluates over 822 base wines annually before Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez finalises each assemblage. Rosé Impériale draws on that same discipline: a Pinot Noir-dominant NV blend sourced across 200+ crus spanning Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube, with 20–30% reserve wines anchoring house consistency vintage to vintage. Beneath Épernay, 28 km of chalk cellars — the most extensive in Champagne — maintain constant humidity and temperature during lees ageing that exceeds the AOC minimum, building the seductive, slightly creamy palate that defines Moët's deliberate contrast to austere, yeast-driven house styles. Fermentation takes place in thermoregulated stainless steel, with systematic malolactic fermentation — a technique Moët pioneered in the region — softening acidity and amplifying the red-fruit vivacity characteristic of the rosé expression. The 200 ml format is the category transgression here: the full NV assemblage philosophy compressed into a single-serve bottle, making Grand Cru and Premier Cru fruit accessible without the commitment of a full bottle. As an NV, it is built for immediate drinking, delivering the house's signature fruit-forward brightness in its freshest window.

name: Freixenet  ICE
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish Cava with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle from Penedès.
description (EN Store): Freixenet ICE is a refreshing Spanish Cava from the Penedès region of Catalunya, crafted from a traditional blend of Macabeo, Xarel.lo, and Parellada with a touch of Chardonnay. The wine delivers bright primary notes of green apple and citrus zest, complemented by subtle brioche and toast from the aging process. Fine, persistent bubbles and crisp acidity define the palate, making this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and light appetizers. The dry finish and elegant structure reflect the classic Cava style.

name: Freixenet  Cordon Rosado Brut (200 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Spanish rosado sparkling with red fruit, strawberry, and crisp citrus—fine bubbles and dry finish.
description (EN Store): Freixenet Cordon Rosado Brut is a dry Spanish sparkling wine from Penedès, crafted from Trepat and Garnacha grapes. The wine opens with vibrant red fruit and strawberry aromatics, complemented by crisp citrus notes and a delicate brioche undertone from the traditional méthode champenoise production. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refreshing palate with bright acidity and a clean, dry finish. At 200 ml, this is an ideal aperitif or celebration pour.

name: Taittinger  Prestige Rose
short_description (EN Store): Chalk-cellar rosé built on 55% Montagne de Reims Pinot Noir plus 15% still red — Taittinger's Chardonnay-house logic inverted in a single cuvée.
description (EN Store): Pierre Taittinger acquired the house in 1932 atop a network of 4th-century Gallo-Roman crayères beneath the former Abbey of Saint-Nicaise — chalk tunnels that now age every bottle at constant temperature and humidity, well beyond appellation minimums. The Prestige Rosé is the one cuvée where Taittinger's signature Chardonnay dominance deliberately yields: ~55% Pinot Noir, ~30% Chardonnay, ~15% still red Pinot Noir from Montagne de Reims and Les Riceys, the latter vinified as a still wine and blended in to anchor colour and red-fruit depth rather than relying on skin contact. Chalk-rich soils across the estate's 288 hectares deliver the high natural acidity and mineral tension that prevent the still-red addition from tipping the wine toward heaviness. Fermentation is in temperature-controlled stainless steel to preserve primary fruit; only first-pressing juice is used across the range. Lees aging in the crayères runs a minimum of 3–4 years — more than double the legal floor — building fine, persistent mousse and restrained autolytic complexity. The antioxidant protocol at disgorgement — ascorbic acid in the liqueur d'expédition combined with CO₂ jetting — allows reduced SO₂ while locking in freshness. Drink now through medium term.

name: Taittinger  Prestige Brut
short_description (EN Store): Chardonnay-forward NV from chalk crayères beneath Saint-Nicaise; 3–4 years on lees, first-pressing juice only, ascorbic-acid disgorgement.
description (EN Store): When Pierre Taittinger acquired the former Abbey of Saint-Nicaise in Reims in 1932, he inherited not just a house but 4th-century Gallo-Roman chalk crayères — the same tunnels where the Prestige Brut NV rests for a minimum of 3–4 years on lees, more than double the appellation's 15-month floor. That decision to over-age the entry cuvée is the clearest expression of the house's philosophy. The chalk-rich soils of Taittinger's 288 hectares across 34 crus — spanning Côte des Blancs, Montagne de Reims, and Marne Valley — deliver exceptional drainage and sustained natural acidity, the geological backbone of the house's signature minerality. The blend runs approximately 40% Chardonnay, 35% Pinot Noir, 25% Pinot Meunier drawn from 35+ crus, with ~50% estate fruit; only first-pressing juice (cuvée) enters the vat. Fermentation is in temperature-controlled stainless steel to lock primary aromatics, with no new oak in the main range. At disgorgement, a proprietary combination of ascorbic acid in the liqueur d'expédition plus CO₂ jetting replaces a portion of SO₂ — an unusual antioxidant protocol that preserves freshness without the oxidative edge common in NV Champagne. In a category dominated by Pinot Noir–led blends, a 40% Chardonnay floor in the non-vintage tier is a structural statement, not a garnish. Drink now through 3–5 years; the extended lees aging means the wine arrives with autolytic development already integrated.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Royal Reserve Brut
short_description (EN Store): Mareuil-sur-Aÿ chalk slopes, 36-month lees aging, and a perpetual solera reserve threading back to 1946 — transparency-first NV Champagne.
description (EN Store): In 1996, Charles Philipponnat did something no Champagne house had done before: he printed the base vintage year, dosage level, and disgorgement date on every back label — turning a category built on blending mystique into an exercise in radical transparency. That decision is inseparable from the wine itself. Sourced from Grand and Premier Cru chalk-and-limestone slopes in Aÿ, Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, Avenay, and Mutigny at approximately 170 m ASL, the pure chalk subsoil drains aggressively, stressing vines into producing concentrated, mineral-driven fruit with the structural tension that defines the house style. The blend runs approximately 65% Pinot Noir, 30% Chardonnay, 5% Pinot Meunier, fermented in a combination of stainless steel and neutral oak, with partial malolactic fermentation preserving acidity while building texture. Reserve wines (25–34% of the blend) are held in a perpetual solera of used oak casks, refreshed annually under a rule-of-thirds rotation, and the solera itself has been in continuous use since 1946 — meaning every bottle carries a thread of nearly eight decades of harvests. Minimum 36 months on lees at 12°C — more than double the NV legal requirement — builds the brioche depth and creamy mousse that distinguish this from category peers. For an entry-level NV, the solera architecture is structurally closer to a prestige cuvée than to any standard reserve-wine blend.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Cuvee Brut (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 110+ crus across chalk-bedrock Champagne; MLF blocked, 24+ months on lees, 8 g/L dosage — 92 pts Wine Spectator.
description (EN Store):
When Émilien Boutillat took the cellar master role at Piper-Heidsieck in 2018 — at 31, the youngest to lead a major Champagne house — he doubled down on a house philosophy that already ran counter to prestige-house orthodoxy: block malolactic fermentation (in part or fully) to preserve raw acidity and fruit purity, rather than chase the creamy autolytic weight that defines many grandes maisons. The result is a Brut NV that reads as deliberately taut and energetic rather than opulent.

Fruit is sourced from 110+ crus across Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, and Côte des Bar, all rooted in Champagne's classic chalk subsoil — a geology that drives the chalky minerality and fine, persistent mousse that anchor the house style. Each cru and each variety is vinified separately in stainless steel (200+ individual vats, inaugurated 1995), preserving site-level typicity before assemblage by blind tasting.

The blend runs approximately 50–55% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Meunier, 15–20% Chardonnay, with a minimum 25% reserve wines ensuring cross-lot consistency. Lees aging: minimum 24 months; dosage circa 8 g/L. No oak at any stage. The special feature here is structural: where most NV Bruts use full MLF to soften acidity, Piper's partial block keeps the wine on a tighter, more citrus-driven frame — a deliberate transgression of category convention. Wine Spectator awarded 92 points (Nov 2022), citing black cherry, toast point, white peach purée, and salted almond on a mouthwatering, spiced finish. Drink on release.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Cuvee Brut (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 110+ crus on Champagne chalk; MLF blocked, 24+ months on lees, 8 g/L dosage. WS 92 pts.
description (EN Store): When chef de cave Émilien Boutillat took the helm at Piper-Heidsieck in 2018 — at 31, the youngest to lead a major Champagne house — he doubled down on a deliberate house transgression: blocking malolactic fermentation to deny the creamy, bready softness that defines many grandes maisons, preserving instead the raw acidity and fruit purity of the chalk. That chalk is the constant beneath every lot — the classic Champagne subsoil running under Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, and Côte des Bar — delivering the mineral backbone that anchors the mousse. Fruit is drawn from 100+ crus across 110+ grower partners, each cru and each variety vinified separately in one of 200+ individual stainless-steel tanks to lock in site-specific typicity before assemblage. The blend runs approximately 50–55% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Meunier, 15–20% Chardonnay, with a minimum 25% reserve wines ensuring cross-vintage consistency; secondary fermentation proceeds in-bottle via méthode champenoise, followed by minimum 24 months on lees and disgorgement at 8 g/L dosage, 12% ABV. No oak touches the wine at any stage. What sets this NV apart from Pinot-dominant peers is the combination of partial MLF block and restrained dosage — a structural choice that pushes the style toward tension and fruit definition rather than autolytic richness. Wine Spectator awarded the current disgorgement 92 points (Nov 2022), citing black cherry, toast point, white peach purée, and salted almond on a creamy, mouthwatering finish; drink on release.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Cuvee Brut (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 110+ crus on Champagne chalk; MLF partially blocked, 24+ months on lees, 8 g/L dosage. Wine Spectator 92 pts.
description (EN Store): When chef de cave Émilien Boutillat took the helm in 2018 at age 31 — the youngest to lead a major Champagne house — he doubled down on a deliberate technical position: partial blocking of malolactic fermentation combined with lower dosage (8 g/L), a direct rejection of the creamy, autolytic softness that defines many grandes maisons. The result is a house style built on tension rather than comfort. Fruit is sourced from 110+ crus across Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, and Côte des Bar, all sitting above the classic Champagne chalk subsoil — a porous, mineral-rich bedrock that regulates vine hydration and drives the chalky, salivating finish that anchors every disgorgement. Each cru and each variety is vinified separately in stainless-steel tanks (200+ individual vats), preserving site typicity before blind-tasting-led assemblage. The blend runs approximately 50–55% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Meunier, 15–20% Chardonnay, with a minimum 25% reserve wines ensuring cross-vintage consistency. Minimum 24 months on lees builds structure without sacrificing the house's signature fruit energy. What no peer does at this scale: Piper-Heidsieck became the first B Corp-certified winery in Champagne in 2022, embedding sustainability into production decisions at every level. Wine Spectator 92 pts (Nov 2022): black cherry, toast point, white peach purée, salted almond — drink on release. This 1.5 L magnum format, the same size that won Supreme World Champion at the 2025 CSWWC, offers superior lees contact uniformity and slower oxidative evolution than standard 750 mL.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Cuvee Brut (6 L)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 110+ crus across Champagne's chalk belt; MLF partially blocked by Boutillat to lock in fruit drive over autolytic richness. 6 L format.
description (EN Store):
When Émilien Boutillat was appointed chef de cave in 2018 at age 31 — the youngest to lead a major Champagne house — he codified a deliberate departure: partial blocking of malolactic fermentation to preserve acidity and fruit purity over the creamy, biscuity register that defines many grandes maisons. The Cuvée Brut NV is the clearest expression of that philosophy at scale.

Fruit is drawn from 110+ crus spanning Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, and Côte des Bar, all rooted in Champagne's classic chalk subsoil — the same geology that drives the house's signature chalky minerality and tight, persistent mousse. No single estate site dominates; the house operates as a precision multi-cru blender from its 200+ individual stainless-steel vats, each cru and variety vinified separately.

The blend runs approximately 50–55% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Meunier, 15–20% Chardonnay, with a minimum 25% reserve wines anchoring house-style consistency across disgorgement lots. Minimum 24 months on lees via traditional méthode champenoise; dosage approximately 8 g/L. No oak at any stage — all stainless steel through primary fermentation.

The 6-litre Methuselah format slows secondary fermentation and lees contact relative to bottle surface area, producing finer, more persistent bubbles and extending the drinking window well beyond the standard 75 cl release.

Wine Spectator 92 pts (Nov 2022): black cherry, toast point, white peach purée, salted almond, creamy and mouthwatering finish.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Rare Rose Millesime 2008 (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Piper-Heidsieck's prestige rosé from the structured 2008 vintage — Pinot Noir-dominant, partial MLF blocked, chalk-driven minerality from 110+ crus.
description (EN Store): Florens-Louis Heidsieck founded his house in 1785, but it was chef de cave philosophy — not heritage — that shaped Rare Rosé Millésime 2008: a deliberate refusal of full malolactic conversion, preserving the raw acidity that makes 2008 one of Champagne's most architecturally precise vintages. 2008 delivered a cool, late-ripening season across Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, and Côte des Blancs, with chalk subsoils drawing tension and salinity into the fruit rather than softness. The house's 200+ individual stainless-steel vats allow each of the 110+ sourced crus to be vinified separately before assemblage — a multi-cru blending discipline that no single-estate producer can replicate at this scale. The blend is Pinot Noir-dominant, consistent with the house's 50–55% Pinot Noir signature, with malolactic fermentation blocked in part to lock in citrus-edged acidity and fruit purity; no oak touches the wine at any stage. What distinguishes this expression within the prestige rosé category is that structural tension — not autolytic richness — is the design objective, a direct inversion of the yeast-forward style favoured by most grandes maisons at this price tier. Extended lees aging on the 2008 base has compressed the mousse to a fine, persistent bead while the chalk minerality remains the dominant structural force through the finish.

name: Villa Conchi  Brut Seleccion
short_description (EN Store): Catalan sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Villa Conchi Brut Seleccion is a traditional-method sparkling wine from Catalunya, blending indigenous Spanish varieties—Xarel·lo, Parellada, and Macabeo—with Chardonnay for structure and complexity. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest, followed by toasted brioche and subtle almond notes from extended aging on lees. A mineral backbone and fine, persistent bubbles define the palate, finishing dry and refreshing. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with oysters, seafood, and soft cheeses.

name: Domaine Champalou  Vouvray Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Vouvray Brut from Chenin Blanc: crisp green apple, citrus, and brioche with fine bubbles and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Domaine Champalou's Vouvray Brut is a classic Loire Valley sparkling wine crafted from 100% Chenin Blanc. This non-vintage cuvée showcases the versatility and elegance of the region's signature grape, delivering bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose with supporting brioche and honey notes from the traditional méthode champenoise. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and a clean, refreshing finish that lingers with subtle stone fruit and floral complexity. An ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses.

name: G.H. Mumm  Cordon Rouge Brut (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant Grand Cru blend from 100–120 crus; dosed at 6–9 g/L — measurably drier than most Brut Grandes Marques.
description (EN Store): When Mumm installed a press house directly in its Verzenay Grand Cru vineyard in 1840 — decades before the practice became standard — it was making a structural argument: proximity to the vine matters more than convenience. That philosophy still governs Cordon Rouge today. 160 of the house's ~218 ha are classified Grand Cru, spread across eight villages including Aÿ, Bouzy, Ambonnay, Verzy, Verzenay, Mailly, Avize, and Cramant, all on the chalk-and-limestone soils of the Montagne de Reims and Côte des Blancs — chalk that drains freely yet retains moisture, forcing deep root systems and delivering the taut mineral tension that defines the house style. The assemblage draws from 100–120 individual crus, blended as Pinot Noir 45%, Chardonnay 30%, Pinot Meunier 25%, with 25–30% reserve wine integrated at assemblage for depth and consistency. Each cru and variety is fermented separately in stainless steel at 18–20°C with natural yeasts; full malolactic fermentation is house policy; a portion of reserve wine is aged in oak vats before final blending. Lees aging runs approximately 2.5 years in chalk cellars beneath Reims — well above the 15-month NV minimum. The defining transgression: dosage is held at 6–9 g/L, significantly below the 12 g/L Brut category norm, a deliberate refusal to let sugar soften what the Pinot Noir-dominant terroir has already structured. The result is one of the drier Brut expressions among major Grandes Marques — taut, golden-fruited, and chalk-driven, drinking well on release and through the near term.

name: Perrier-Jouet  Grand Brut Champagne (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant non-vintage Champagne with bright citrus, brioche complexity, and refined minerality—a versatile aperitif.
description (EN Store): Perrier-Jouet Grand Brut is a benchmark non-vintage Champagne balancing Pinot Noir's structure (40%), Chardonnay's finesse (20%), and Pinot Meunier's roundness (40%). On the nose, green apple and citrus zest lead, followed by toasted brioche and subtle hazelnut from the traditional méthode champenoise aging. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and a dry finish that lingers with honey and white flower notes. A refined, food-friendly aperitif and celebratory wine.

name: Jacob's Creek  Sparkling Moscato Rose (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Australian sparkling rosé with strawberry, raspberry, and brioche—perfect for celebrations and light appetizers.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Sparkling Moscato Rosé is a light, festive sparkling wine from Australia's Barossa Valley. Dominated by fresh strawberry and raspberry on the nose, with supporting citrus and floral notes, it opens with fine bubbles and a crisp, refreshing palate. Brioche and honey undertones from the sparkling production add subtle complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. The high acidity and low tannin structure make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for light appetizers, fresh cheeses, and delicate seafood.

name: Jacob's Creek  Sparkling Chardonnay Pinot Noir (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Sparkling Chardonnay Pinot Noir is a light-bodied, high-acidity sparkling wine from Australia's Barossa Valley. The blend showcases bright primary notes of green apple and citrus zest, complemented by subtle brioche and toast from traditional sparkling winemaking. Fine, persistent bubbles and a dry finish make this an approachable yet refined choice for celebrations or casual entertaining.

name: Jacob's Creek  Sparkling Reserve Chardonnay Pinot Noir (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian sparkling with crisp citrus, red fruit, and brioche complexity. Versatile aperitif or seafood companion.

The high acidity and light body make this an exceptionally versatile aperitif, equally at home with oysters, soft cheeses, or Asian cuisine.

name: Eugenio Collavini  Il Grigio Royal Rose
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Italian sparkling rosé with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.

The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle pear notes and fine, persistent bubbles. A whisper of brioche from the traditional méthode adds complexity without overwhelming the wine's crisp, mineral-driven character. Light-bodied and high in acidity, it delivers a clean, refreshing finish ideal for aperitif service or pairing with delicate seafood and fresh cheeses.

name: Eugenio Collavini  Ribolla Gialla Spumante
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Ribolla Gialla spumante from Friuli with bright green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle.

name: Monsoon Valley  Brut Prestige Sparkling Wine
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Thai sparkling wine with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Elegant aperitif for seafood and Asian cuisine.

Monsoon Valley Brut Prestige is a dry sparkling wine crafted from 100% Colombard grapes in Thailand's Hua Hin region. The wine displays a pale golden hue with fine, persistent bubbles and a clean, inviting nose of green apple, citrus zest, and pear. On the palate, bright acidity and delicate fruit flavors are complemented by subtle brioche and toast notes from the traditional sparkling winemaking process. The finish is crisp and refreshing, with a mineral edge that lingers pleasantly.

This light-bodied sparkling wine is versatile at the table: serve as an aperitif with olives and canapés, pair with oysters and raw seafood, or enjoy alongside Thai cuisine where its acidity and citrus character enhance spicy and sour flavors. A distinctive expression of Thai winemaking.

name: Lanson Champagne  Black Label Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Champagne with crisp citrus, brioche, and mineral complexity—a versatile aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Lanson Black Label Brut is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles and a dry finish that lingers with citrus zest and subtle honey. A refined, food-friendly Champagne suited to celebrations and elegant entertaining.

name: Nicolas Feuillatte  Brut Reserve
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne blend with brioche richness, citrus brightness, and mineral precision—a versatile celebration wine.
description (EN Store): Nicolas Feuillatte Brut Reserve is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The wine displays the hallmark characteristics of quality Champagne: a fine mousse, bright acidity, and layered complexity. On the nose, brioche and toasted almond notes emerge alongside fresh lemon and green apple, underpinned by chalky minerality. name: Nicolas Feuillatte  Brut Rosé
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Brut Rosé with strawberry, lemon, and brioche—a refined aperitif and seafood companion.

name: Yellow Tail  Bubbles
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—ideal for oysters and apéritifs.

name: Yellow Tail  Pink Bubbles
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with apple, citrus, and soft red fruit. Refreshing bubbles for celebrations and casual entertaining.

Yellow Tail Pink Bubbles is a light, approachable sparkling wine from Australia's Riverina region, blending white and red grape varieties to achieve its signature soft pink hue. name: Marta Cava  Reserva Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Catalan Cava with crisp apple, citrus, and brioche—a versatile sparkling for celebrations and seafood.

name: Angove  Long Row Cuvee Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian sparkling with bright citrus, brioche complexity, and mineral precision—a refined Champagne-style cuvée.

name: Angove  Chalk Hill Blue Bubbles
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—organic, elegant, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Angove's Chalk Hill Blue Bubbles is a light, refreshing sparkling wine from South Australia's Riverland region, crafted by the country's largest certified organic winemaker. name: Bottega  Prosecco Gold Brut (200 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Ideal aperitif in a convenient 200 ml format.
description (EN Store): Bottega Prosecco Gold Brut is a classic Glera-based sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted in the traditional Prosecco style. short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco blend with crisp apple, citrus, and brioche—refined bubbles for celebration and seafood.

The palate is crisp and elegant, with stone fruit undertones and a mineral finish that lingers pleasantly. name: Zardetto  Prosecco Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
