name: Louis Roederer  Cristal Rose 2013
short_description (EN Store): Saignée rosé from 45 biodynamic Grand Cru plots; 55% Pinot Noir, 45% Chardonnay, 6 years on lees — zero reserve wines used.
description (EN Store): When Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon converted all 45 Cristal Grand Cru plots to biodynamic viticulture from the 2012 vintage onward, the 2013 became only the second fully biodynamic Cristal Rosé ever released — a deliberate repositioning of a prestige cuvée away from négociant blending logic and toward single-estate terroir accountability. The 80 hectares of Cristal vines span seven Grand Cru communes — Verzenay, Verzy, Beaumont-sur-Vesle, Aÿ, Avize, Mesnil-sur-Oger, and Cramant — where shallow chalk topsoil of roughly 20 cm over pure chalk bedrock forces vine roots deep, producing fruit of high natural tension and mineral salinity. The blend runs 55% Pinot Noir to 45% Chardonnay, with colour extracted via the saignée method rather than red-wine addition, preserving structural precision. No reserve wines enter the blend — an absolute rule for Cristal — and malolactic fermentation is partially blocked to sustain aging architecture. Six years on lees before disgorgement, dosage held to 7–8 g/L. What separates Cristal Rosé from every peer at this price point is the combination of 100% estate Grand Cru fruit, full biodynamic certification, and the refusal to use reserve wines — a grower-estate discipline inside a grande maison framework. The 2013 vintage in Champagne delivered cool-season acidity and taut structure; this expression rewards cellaring well into the 2030s.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Devil's Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—an elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Casillero del Diablo's Devil's Brut is a dry sparkling wine from Chile's Maipo Valley, crafted in the traditional Champagne style. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with subtle honey and red fruit undertones that evolve through the finish. name: Champagne Philipponnat Royal Reserve Rose
short_description (EN Store): Mareuil-sur-Aÿ Pinot Noir house; perpetual solera since 1946 threads eight decades of harvest into every NV bottle. 36-month lees aging, 8 g/L dosage.
description (EN Store):
When Philipponnat printed the base vintage year, dosage level, and disgorgement date on its back labels in 1996 — years before transparency became a Champagne-industry talking point — it was signalling something structural: this house makes decisions it wants buyers to interrogate. The Royal Reserve Rosé is built on the same architecture as the Brut, anchored by a perpetual solera reserve in used oak casks, refreshed annually since 1946 under a rule-of-thirds rotation, meaning every bottle carries a continuous thread of nearly eight decades of Mareuil-sur-Aÿ harvests — a feature almost never found at this tier.

Vineyards sit on south- and southeast-facing pure chalk and limestone slopes at approximately 170 m ASL across Grand and Premier Cru parcels in Aÿ, Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, Avenay, and Mutigny. The chalk delivers both razor drainage and the savoury mineral tension that defines Philipponnat's house signature. The blend is Pinot Noir-dominant (~65%), with Chardonnay (~30%) and a trace of Meunier, fermented from first-press cuvée juice only — no tailles — in a combination of stainless steel and neutral oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation to preserve acidity while building texture. Minimum 36 months on lees — more than double the NV legal floor — at a constant 12°C in 1.5 km of underground chalk galleries. The reserve component (25–34% of the blend) aged in oak via solera adds a vinous depth and patina that no standard reserve-wine protocol can replicate.

name: Champagne Philipponnat  Grand Blanc Extra Brut
short_description (EN Store): Blanc de Blancs from Philipponnat's chalk slopes at Mareuil-sur-Aÿ — but the SKU name 'Grand Blanc Extra Brut 2015' has no match in the validated brand library.
description (EN Store): EDITORIAL NOTE FOR WINE-NOW TEAM: The brand library provided covers Philipponnat Royal Reserve NV Brut — a Pinot Noir-dominant non-vintage blend. The product listed (SKU WSP2548DJ) is named Grand Blanc Extra Brut 2015, a vintage Blanc de Blancs-style cuvée at a distinct price point (฿5,900). Action required: Please supply a brand library entry specific to the Grand Blanc cuvée (vineyard sourcing, Chardonnay village sources, dosage g/L, lees aging duration, oak use, disgorgement date, and any critic scores) so a curation-grade description can be written to the required standard.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Blanc De Noirs Extra Brut  2018
short_description (EN Store): Blanc de Noirs Extra Brut from chalk slopes above Mareuil-sur-Aÿ — 2018 harvest, 69% Pinot Noir, perpetual solera reserve threading back decades.
description (EN Store):
Philipponnat's family connection to Aÿ vines dates to 1522, making the house's Pinot Noir obsession less a stylistic choice than a five-century inheritance. This Blanc de Noirs Extra Brut takes that conviction to its logical extreme: a single-varietal expression drawn from the house's south- and southeast-facing chalk and limestone slopes at roughly 170 m ASL above Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, where pure chalk subsoil delivers both relentless drainage and the mineral tension that defines the house signature.

The 2018 base is built on 69% Pinot Noir with the balance drawn from Chardonnay and Pinot Meunier, vinified from first-press cuvée juice only — no tailles — in a combination of stainless steel and neutral oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation preserving structural acidity while adding textural weight. A 26% reserve-wine component, maintained in used oak barrels via a perpetual solera refreshed annually under the 'rule of thirds,' threads continuity across decades into every bottle. Lees aging runs a minimum of 36 months at a constant 12°C cellar temperature — more than double the legal NV minimum.

What separates this from virtually every peer at the price is the Extra Brut dosage discipline applied to a Blanc de Noirs chassis: the low sugar addition forces the chalk minerality and red-fruit structure of the Pinot Noir to carry the wine entirely on their own terms. The 2018 base shows ripe peach, white flowers, rounded brioche, and pithy citrus — approachable in youth yet built for further development.

name: Champagne Philipponnat  Clos des Goisses Extra Brut 2013
short_description (EN Store): Clos des Goisses 2013: Philipponnat's steep south-facing chalk monopole, Extra Brut, aged deep in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ's 18th-century galleries.
description (EN Store): The Clos des Goisses is Philipponnat's singular obsession — a 5.5-hectare walled monopole on the steepest south-facing chalk slope in all of Champagne, dropping sharply toward the Marne canal at Mareuil-sur-Aÿ. That extreme aspect, combined with pure chalk subsoil, generates a thermal surplus of roughly 1–2°C above the regional average, pushing phenolic ripeness to levels almost incompatible with the Champagne AOC's cool-climate identity. The 2013 vintage, notoriously cool and late, is where this site's heat-banking capacity becomes decisive: where lesser parcels struggled, Clos des Goisses delivered structured, high-acid fruit with genuine depth. Vinification is conducted with first-press cuvée juice only, fermented in a combination of stainless steel and oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation retained to preserve the site's electric acidity while adding textural weight. Extended lees aging in bottle — well beyond NV minimums — builds the autolytic complexity that defines this cuvée's mid-palate. What no peer replicates: Clos des Goisses is a true monopole Extra Brut from a single steep-slope site, produced by a house whose family viticultural roots in Aÿ trace to 1522 — making this arguably Champagne's longest-tenured single-site obsession. The 2013 drinking window rewards patience; its structural tension and chalk-driven salinity are only now beginning to resolve into the vinous, broad-shouldered character that defines Philipponnat's house signature.

name: Champagne Gremillet  Selection Brut
short_description (EN Store): Côte des Bar Champagne with 70% Pinot Noir and 30% Chardonnay; crisp, elegant, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Champagne Gremillet Selection Brut is a non-vintage Champagne from the Côte des Bar subregion, blending 70% Pinot Noir with 30% Chardonnay. This composition delivers a balanced profile: the Pinot Noir contributes red fruit structure and body, while Chardonnay adds finesse and mineral complexity. Expect a crisp, refreshing palate with green apple and citrus zest on entry, supported by brioche and toast from traditional méthode champenoise aging. The wine's high acidity and fine bubbles make it an ideal aperitif and versatile food companion.

name: Champagne Gremillet  Blanc de Blancs Brut
short_description (EN Store): Blanc de Blancs Brut from Côte des Bar: 100% Chardonnay with crisp citrus, minerality, and brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Champagne Gremillet's Blanc de Blancs Brut is a pure expression of Chardonnay from the Côte des Bar subregion. This non-vintage cuvée showcases the elegance and precision characteristic of single-varietal Champagne, with vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, supported by subtle minerality and stone fruit. The palate reveals brioche and hazelnut notes from traditional méthode champenoise aging, finishing with crisp acidity and fine bubbles. A versatile aperitif and food-friendly Champagne suited to oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Champagne Ernest Rapeneau  Brut Magnum (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Classic Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Meunier & Chardonnay with brioche complexity and mineral freshness. Magnum format ideal for celebration.
description (EN Store): Champagne Ernest Rapeneau Brut Magnum presents a balanced expression of the traditional Champagne blend: 45% Pinot Noir provides red-fruit structure and body, 35% Meunier contributes roundness and floral character, and 20% Chardonnay delivers acidity and mineral precision. The wine displays classic brioche and toast notes from extended aging on lees, complemented by citrus zest and subtle hazelnut complexity. The magnum format (1.5 L) ensures slower oxidation and extended aging potential, making it suitable for both immediate enjoyment and cellaring.

name: Sartori di Verona  “Erfo” Prosecco Spumante Brut DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with green apple and citrus—perfect aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Sartori di Verona's Erfo Prosecco Spumante Brut exemplifies the refreshing character of Veneto's signature sparkling wine. The palate is clean and dry, with fine bubbles and a whisper of brioche complexity from the traditional méthode Charmat production. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with oysters, light seafood, and fresh cheeses.

name: Riunite  Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Italian sparkling Muscat with lychee, peach, and honeyed florals—perfect for desserts and spicy Asian fare.
description (EN Store): Riunite Moscato is a lightly sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from the aromatic Muscat grape. The palate is light-bodied with crisp, high acidity and fine bubbles that refresh the mouth. Subtle brioche and citrus zest from the winemaking process add complexity without overwhelming the wine's primary charm. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to casual occasions, dessert courses, and pairing with spicy or aromatic cuisines.

name: Palazzo Grimani by Allegrini  Prosecco DOC Millesimato Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant Prosecco with green apple and citrus; fine bubbles and dry finish. Perfect aperitif or seafood companion.

Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a light, refreshing body with crisp acidity and a bone-dry finish. This is a sophisticated aperitif wine that shines alongside oysters, shellfish, and Asian cuisine.

name: Luc Belaire  Rare Rose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Provence rosé sparkling with red berries, citrus, and brioche—versatile aperitif with fine bubbles and dry finish.

The wine displays a pale salmon hue with fine, persistent bubbles characteristic of quality sparkling production. This is an approachable, celebratory sparkling suitable for aperitifs, seafood, and light cheese courses.

name: Champagne Duval Leroy  Fleur de Premier Cru

name: Champagne Ruinart Rose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Rosé with red berry finesse, brioche complexity, and refined minerality—a celebratory aperitif.
description (EN Store): Ruinart Champagne Rosé is a non-vintage prestige cuvée blending 45% Chardonnay and 55% Pinot Noir. The composition delivers a delicate balance of elegance and structure: Chardonnay contributes citrus brightness and mineral precision, while Pinot Noir imparts red fruit character and body. On the nose, expect red berries, floral notes, and toasted brioche complexity. The palate is refined and dry, with fine bubbles, creamy texture, and a persistent finish marked by citrus zest and subtle minerality. This is a sophisticated aperitif wine suited to celebration and refined entertaining.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Riviera
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant NV from 110+ crus across Champagne's chalk belt — partial MLF block and ~8 g/L dosage keep the structure taut and fruit-forward.
description (EN Store): When Émilien Boutillat took the cellar at Piper-Heidsieck in 2018 — at 31, the youngest chef de cave of any major maison — he doubled down on a deliberate technical position: partial blocking of malolactic fermentation to preserve acidity and fruit purity over the yeast-autolytic richness that defines many prestige neighbours. That decision is the architectural spine of every Piper NV. Fruit is sourced from 110+ crus across Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, and Côte des Bar, all rooted in Champagne's classic chalk subsoil — a geology that drives the chalky minerality and fine, persistent mousse the house treats as non-negotiable. Each cru and each variety is vinified separately in stainless steel (200+ individual vats, inaugurated 1995), preserving typicity before a blind-tasting assemblage process. The blend runs approximately 50–55% Pinot Noir, 30% Pinot Meunier, 15–20% Chardonnay, with a minimum 25% reserve wines anchoring house-style consistency. Lees aging runs a minimum 24 months in bottle via traditional méthode champenoise, with dosage cited at approximately 8 g/L — Brut, but on the leaner end of the category. What sets this apart from peers at the same price tier is the explicit refusal of full MLF: where Moët or Veuve Clicquot lean into creamy roundness, Piper stays angular, chalk-driven, and kinetic. Wine Spectator awarded the current Boutillat-era NV 92 points (Nov 2022), noting black cherry, toast point, white peach purée, and salted almond on a spiced, mouthwatering finish — drink on release.

name: Allart & Fils  Brut Reserve
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Champagne blend of Pinot Noir, Meunier & Chardonnay with brioche complexity and vibrant citrus acidity.
description (EN Store): Allart & Fils Brut Reserve is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a balanced blend of 40% Pinot Noir, 40% Pinot Meunier, and 30% Chardonnay sourced from Montagne de Reims. This traditional composition delivers a medium-bodied wine with bright acidity and layered complexity. On the nose, brioche and toasted notes emerge from extended aging on lees, complemented by green apple and citrus aromatics. The palate reveals a harmonious interplay of red fruit, minerality, and subtle honey notes, finishing with crisp acidity and a persistent mousse. A versatile aperitif and food-friendly Champagne suitable for celebration and refined dining.

name: Allart & Fils  Brut Blanc de Blancs
short_description (EN Store): 100% Chardonnay Blanc de Blancs from Montagne de Reims—crisp, mineral-driven Champagne with citrus and brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Allart & Fils Brut Blanc de Blancs is a pure expression of Chardonnay from the Montagne de Reims, one of Champagne's most prestigious terroirs. This non-vintage cuvée showcases the elegance and precision characteristic of Blanc de Blancs—a style built entirely from white grapes. Expect a vibrant palate driven by green apple and citrus zest, underpinned by fine minerality and a delicate floral character. Winemaking imparts subtle brioche and toast notes that add depth without overwhelming the wine's natural freshness. The high acidity and fine bubbles make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for refined seafood and cheese courses.

name: Dom Perignon  Rose (Luminated Label)
short_description (EN Store): Dom Pérignon Rosé 2009 — reductive lees aging on Pinot Noir-dominant chalk terroir; exclusively vintage-declared, never blended across years.
description (EN Store): Dom Pérignon has never produced a non-vintage wine in its history — every bottle carries a year, and if the harvest falls short, nothing is released. The 2009 Rosé emerges from that uncompromising standard, built on a fixed core of Grand Cru and Premier Cru plots: Pinot Noir from Aÿ, Bouzy, Mailly-Champagne, and Verzenay, Chardonnay from Chouilly, Cramant, Avize, and Le Mesnil-sur-Oger. The deep chalk and limestone subsoils across these crus act as a moisture reservoir, sustaining vine hydration through stress periods and driving the house's signature mineral tension into the fruit. Whole-cluster Coquard pressing minimises skin contact before separate stainless-steel vinification of each variety; malolactic fermentation is conducted, and the wine undergoes extended lees aging undisgorged in chalk cellars — a minimum of eight to nine years for the P1 release — with zero oak contact at any stage. Two distinct yeast strains drive primary fermentation; two further strains manage the in-bottle prise de mousse, a four-yeast architecture Chef de Cave Vincent Chaperon identifies as central to long-term aging capacity. What separates Dom Pérignon from every other grande marque is structural: the same vintage can be re-released as P2 (15+ years on lees) or P3 (30–40 years), meaning the 2009 Rosé is not a finished statement but the first of potentially three distinct expressions of a single harvest.

name: Cinzano  Asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Muscat sparkler from Piedmont with lychee, peach, and honeyed florals—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store):
Cinzano Asti DOCG is a lightly sparkling wine crafted from Muscat grapes in Piedmont's renowned Asti region. The wine opens with pronounced lychee and peach aromatics, complemented by delicate floral and honey notes that define the Muscat varietal's character. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refined mouthfeel, while crisp acidity balances the wine's natural sweetness, creating an approachable yet sophisticated profile.

Ideal as an aperitif or paired with Asian cuisine, fresh cheeses, and fruit-based desserts, this sparkling wine showcases the classic elegance of Asti production.

name: Cinzano  Rose Edition
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pinot Noir sparkling from Piedmont—crisp, fruity, and refreshingly dry for summer entertaining.
description (EN Store): Cinzano Rosé Edition is a light-bodied sparkling wine crafted from Pinot Noir in Italy's Piedmont region. On the palate, it delivers bright red fruit and raspberry character with a crisp, dry finish and silky texture. The high acidity and delicate structure make it an ideal aperitif and versatile companion to seafood, fresh cheese, and refined small plates. A summer-friendly sparkler with understated elegance.

name: Domaine Cold River  Cuvee Tradition Brut (Black Label)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche complexity. Elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Domaine Cold River's Cuvée Tradition Brut (Black Label) is a refined Australian sparkling wine crafted for versatility and food-friendly elegance. The wine opens with vibrant primary notes of green apple and citrus zest, supported by a fine, persistent mousse characteristic of quality sparkling production. Secondary brioche and toast notes add textural complexity and subtle richness on the palate, while underlying minerality provides definition and length. High acidity and light body make this an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for oysters, shellfish, and delicate Asian cuisine.

name: Champagne Albert Lebrun  Blanc de Noir Extra Brut
short_description (EN Store): 100% Pinot Meunier Extra Brut from Champagne Albert Lebrun—elegant, mineral-driven sparkle with brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Champagne Albert Lebrun's Blanc de Noir Extra Brut showcases the finesse of Pinot Meunier, Champagne's most underrated dark grape. name: Champagne Duval Leroy Rose Prestige

name: Botter  Prosecco DOC Rose

name: Tenuta Amadio  Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Superiore with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Elegant aperitif with fine bubbles and refreshing acidity.

short_description (EN Store): Elegant Thai sparkling rosé with red fruit, strawberry, and brioche—crisp, dry, and food-friendly.

name: Granmonte  Crémant Méthode Traditionnelle Extra Brut

name: Bottega  Prosecco Rose DOC Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Italian Prosecco Rosé with strawberry, citrus, and brioche notes. Dry, refreshing, perfect for celebrations and aperitifs.

name: Angove  Organic Cuvee Brut
short_description (EN Store): Organic Chardonnay sparkling from South Australia's pioneering sustainable producer—crisp, elegant, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Angove's Organic Cuvée Brut represents the winery's commitment to certified organic viticulture and premium sparkling winemaking. name: Aromo Chardonnay Brut
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Chardonnay sparkling with bright citrus, brioche complexity, and mineral precision—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Aromo's Chardonnay Brut from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a vibrant sparkling wine built on green apple and citrus zest primary notes. Oak aging contributes brioche, butter, and subtle vanilla complexity, while fine acidity and minerality provide structure and food-friendly versatility. Medium-Full body and persistent mousse make this an approachable yet sophisticated choice for celebrations, seafood courses, or cheese boards.

name: Notte Rossa  Vino Spumante Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Italian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif from Puglia's Salento region.
description (EN Store):
Notte Rossa Vino Spumante Extra Dry is a light, refreshing sparkling wine from Puglia's Salento region, crafted from white grapes native to the area. The Extra Dry classification signals a touch of residual sweetness balanced by vibrant acidity, making it an ideal aperitif or celebratory pour.

On the nose, green apple and citrus zest dominate, with subtle brioche and floral undertones. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with fine bubbles that cleanse and refresh. Stone fruit notes emerge mid-palate, adding complexity without heaviness. The finish is clean and dry, with lingering citrus acidity.

This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to casual entertaining, seafood, and light appetizers.

name: Notte Rossa  Vino Spumante Extra Dry Rose
short_description (EN Store): Dry rosé spumante from Puglia with vibrant red berries, citrus, and fine bubbles—elegant aperitif sparkle.
description (EN Store): Notte Rossa Vino Spumante Extra Dry Rosé is a lively sparkling wine from Puglia's Salento region, crafted from red grapes native to southern Italy. The wine opens with bright strawberry and raspberry aromatics, complemented by delicate citrus zest and a whisper of brioche from the traditional méthode. On the palate, fine bubbles deliver crisp acidity and a dry finish, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for light seafood, fresh cheeses, and Mediterranean small plates. The wine's approachable character and food-friendly profile reflect the warm, fruit-forward character typical of Puglian reds, here transformed into an elegant, refreshing sparkle.

name: Penfolds  Champagne Rose Thienot x Penfolds
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' rule-breaking cross-hemisphere Champagne: Australian blending philosophy meets Thiénot's Marne fruit, zero-dosage transparency.
description (EN Store):
When Penfolds — a house built on the radical premise that winemaker intelligence outranks single-terroir orthodoxy — turned its attention to Champagne, the collaboration with Maison Thiénot was a logical transgression. Founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, the brand has spent nearly 180 years proving that deliberate multi-source blending, not appellation purity, is the engine of quality. That same philosophy now crosses hemispheres.

Thiénot draws from Marne Valley vineyards where chalk-and-clay subsoils drive the tense, saline backbone that anchors this rosé's structure. The assemblage reflects Penfolds' house discipline: fruit selection prioritised for mid-palate weight and textural generosity rather than lean Champagne austerity.

Vinification follows Champagne méthode traditionnelle with secondary fermentation in bottle. The rosé character is achieved through Thiénot's Pinot Noir-dominant base, and the collaboration's stated intent is to surface Penfolds' signature fruit richness within the Champagne framework — a deliberate counterpoint to the house's typical reductive, yeast-driven style.

What is categorically unusual here: an Australian producer with zero AOC Champagne heritage co-authoring a Champagne rosé, applying a multi-regional blending logic to a region that legislates terroir fidelity. The result is a Champagne that reads through a Southern Hemisphere lens — broader, fruit-forward, and architecturally generous by Marne standards.

name: V8+  Prosecco Brut Millesimato DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and brioche—a refreshing Italian sparkling for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): V8+ Prosecco Brut Millesimato DOC is a dry, vibrant sparkling wine from Veneto's Prosecco region, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by subtle pear notes. On the palate, fine bubbles deliver crisp acidity and a clean, refreshing character, with delicate brioche undertones from the traditional méthode Charmat production. The dry finish makes it an ideal aperitif and pairs beautifully with oysters, shellfish, fresh cheeses, and light seafood preparations.

name: V8+  Prosecco Brut Millesimato DOC (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle from Veneto.
description (EN Store): V8+ Prosecco Brut Millesimato DOC is a vibrant sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from 100% Glera grapes. This 1.5 L format delivers bright, refreshing character with dominant green apple and citrus notes on the nose, complemented by subtle pear and stone fruit. Fine bubbles and a crisp, dry finish make it an ideal aperitif or celebration wine. The secondary brioche and honey notes add gentle complexity without overshadowing the wine's essential freshness and elegance.

name: V8+  Prosecco Brut Rose Lele DOC
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Prosecco Rosé with strawberry and citrus notes, fine bubbles, and crisp acidity—ideal for aperitifs and seafood.
description (EN Store): V8+ Prosecco Brut Rosé is a light-bodied sparkling wine from Veneto's Prosecco DOC region, crafted from Glera and Pinot Nero grapes. The wine opens with vibrant strawberry and citrus aromatics, supported by subtle brioche and floral undertones from the traditional Charmat method. On the palate, high acidity and fine bubbles deliver a crisp, refreshing finish with delicate minerality. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to casual entertaining and elegant aperitifs.

name: Charles Du Lac  Mousseux Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux sparkling with bright citrus, green apple, and brioche—an elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store):
Charles Du Lac Mousseux Brut is a non-vintage sparkling wine from Bordeaux, France, crafted in the traditional méthode champenoise style. The wine displays a pale golden hue with persistent fine bubbles and a delicate mousse.

Tasting Profile: Bright citrus and green apple dominate the nose, complemented by subtle stone fruit and minerality. On the palate, crisp acidity is balanced by secondary notes of brioche and toast from extended aging on the lees, creating a refined, elegant mouthfeel with a clean, lingering finish.

Serving & Pairing: Best served well-chilled as an aperitif or paired with oysters, shellfish, soft cheeses, and light canapés. The high acidity and fine bubbles make it an ideal companion to sushi and raw seafood.

name: Expresion  Premium Sparkling Brut

name: Expresion  Premium Sparkling Pink Rose
short_description (EN Store): Spanish sparkling rosé with floral and tropical notes, fine bubbles, and bright acidity—ideal for celebrations and seafood.

name: Pol Roger  Brut Reserve Limied Editon Gift Box 2023 (750 ml)

name: Contadi Castaldi  Franciacorta Brut

name: Contadi Castaldi  Franciacorta Brut Rose

name: Contadi Castaldi  Franciacorta Brut Race Ducati

name: Elmfield  Sparkling Cuvee

name: Elmfield  Sparkling Rose

description (EN Store): Elmfield's Sparkling Rosé from South Eastern Australia blends Shiraz and Petit Verdot into a medium-full-bodied sparkling wine with medium-high acidity and tannin. name: Alpaca  Sparkling Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—an elegant aperitif for seafood and celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Alpaca Sparkling Brut is a non-vintage Chilean sparkling wine from the Central Valley, crafted in the traditional dry style. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus notes, supported by subtle brioche and toast complexity from the secondary fermentation. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refreshing mouthfeel and clean, dry finish.

This is an approachable sparkling wine suited to aperitif service, oyster bars, and casual celebrations. The high acidity and light body make it a versatile partner for seafood, fresh cheese, and light appetizers.

name: Moet & Chandon  Ice Imperial Rose (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Champagne's first ice-format rosé: engineered for over-ice service, high-dosage, Pinot-dominant, from Moët's 1,190-ha chalk-limestone estate.
description (EN Store): When Chef de Cave Benoît Gouez set out to engineer a Champagne that would not merely survive ice dilution but improve under it, the result was Ice Impérial Rosé — a deliberate inversion of every rule governing how Champagne is served. Founded in 1743, Moët & Chandon draws on chalky limestone soils across all five Champagne sub-regions — Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, Côte des Blancs, Sézanne, and the Aube — where shallow chalk retains cold and reflects light, driving the vivid red-fruit character that anchors this cuvée. Pinot Noir and Pinot Meunier dominate the blend, sourced from across Moët's 1,190-hectare estate and contracted growers spanning 234 crus; fermentation proceeds in thermoregulated stainless steel with systematic malolactic fermentation, preserving primary fruit intensity. Dosage is set significantly higher than the Brut Impérial NV's ~7 g/L — calibrated specifically so that melting ice water brings the sweetness into balance rather than stripping it. The special feature here is structural: this is one of very few Champagnes formulated with a serving temperature of 0°C and ice contact as a design parameter, not an afterthought. As a non-vintage assemblage built for immediate, fruit-forward pleasure, it carries no drinking window — it is made to be opened now, over a large ice cube, in a wide-bowled glass.

name: Mare Santo  Rose
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Slovenian Pinot Noir sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif wine.
description (EN Store): Mare Santo's Rosé Sparkling from Goriška Brda showcases 100% Pinot Noir in a light, refreshing format. Bright green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by delicate brioche and mineral undertones from the traditional sparkling winemaking process. The fine bubble structure and high acidity deliver a clean, crisp finish ideal for seafood, cheese, and celebratory occasions.

name: Richland  Brut Cuvee Pinot Noir Sparkling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling Pinot Noir with bright red fruit, raspberry, and silky texture—ideal for seafood and celebrations.
description (EN Store): Richland's Brut Cuvée Pinot Noir is a light-bodied sparkling wine from Australia's Riverina region, crafted from Pinot Noir grapes. The wine opens with vibrant red fruit and raspberry aromatics, delivering a fresh, crisp palate with silky tannins and pronounced acidity. Earthy undertones add subtle complexity to the bright fruit-forward profile, making this an elegant choice for warm-weather entertaining and food-friendly occasions.

name: Allart & Fils  Rose
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Rosé from Montagne de Reims with bright red berries, citrus, and brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Allart & Fils Rosé is a non-vintage Champagne from the prestigious Montagne de Reims subregion, crafted in the traditional méthode champenoise style. This wine displays the hallmark elegance of the appellation: a delicate pale-salmon hue, fine bubbles, and a refined aromatic profile. On the nose, red berries and citrus zest lead, followed by subtle brioche and floral undertones. The palate is crisp and dry, with bright acidity and mineral notes that linger through a clean, refreshing finish. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood and soft cheeses.

name: Piper-Heidsieck  Rare Millesime 2008 (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Piper-Heidsieck's prestige cuvée from the celebrated 2008 vintage — chalk-driven, Pinot Noir-dominant, with partial MLF blocking preserving razor acidity.
description (EN Store): Florens-Louis Heidsieck founded this Reims house in 1785, and the Rare Millésime represents the ceiling of that lineage — a vintage-declared prestige cuvée released only in exceptional years, with 2008 standing among the most structurally complete harvests of the modern Champagne era. Sourced from grand and premier cru chalk-subsoil sites across Montagne de Reims, Côte des Blancs, and Vallée de la Marne, the deep Cretaceous chalk here drives the piercing mineral tension that defines the house's anti-autolytic philosophy. Chef de cave technique is uncompromising: each cru vinified separately in stainless steel, with malolactic fermentation blocked (in part or fully) to lock in primary fruit purity and the high natural acidity that 2008 delivered in abundance. No oak contact at any stage. Extended lees aging — well beyond the NV minimum — builds textural complexity without sacrificing the house's signature fruit-forward energy. What separates Rare from the prestige-cuvée consensus is deliberate restraint: where peers lean into yeast-autolytic richness and high dosage, Piper-Heidsieck holds the line on lower dosage and partial MLF suppression, producing a vintage Champagne that reads more like a precision instrument than a statement of opulence. The 2008 vintage's naturally elevated acidity and fine phenolic structure make this a candidate for continued cellaring, with the chalk minerality and Pinot Noir backbone expected to integrate further through the late 2020s.

name: Follador  El Brolo Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco DOC Brut with vibrant green apple and citrus, fine bubbles, and elegant brioche complexity.
description (EN Store): Follador's El Brolo Prosecco DOC Treviso Brut is a classic expression of the Veneto's flagship sparkling wine. name: Follador  Ruiol Castei Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore Millesimato DOCG Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Superiore with vibrant green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.

name: Martinotti  Prosecco Spumante Brut

name: Champagne  Alain Navarre Cuvée Tradition Brut
short_description (EN Store): Classic Champagne Brut with bright citrus, brioche, and mineral complexity. Elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store):

name: Champagne Vollereaux  Rose De Saigee Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Rosé with brioche complexity, citrus brightness, and red fruit finesse—ideal for celebration and seafood.

On the nose, brioche and toasted almond notes mingle with bright lemon and citrus zest, underpinned by mineral complexity. The palate is crisp and refined, with high acidity balancing strawberry and red fruit nuances. A dry finish with persistent bubbles and subtle toast complexity makes this an excellent aperitif and versatile food companion.

A chalky, mineral undertone rounds the profile, lending the wine structure and a dry, lingering finish. This is a versatile aperitif wine suited to oysters, shellfish, and delicate canapés, or as an elegant standalone celebration.

name: Champagne Colin  Parallele Vertus Premier Cru Extra Brut Blanc de Blancs

name: Champagne Colin  Extravertu Vertus Premier Cru Extra Dry Blanc de Blancs

Expect bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, supported by toasted brioche and hazelnut from the traditional méthode champenoise aging. name: Champagne H.Blin  Brut Tradition
short_description (EN Store): Elegant NV Champagne with Pinot Meunier backbone; crisp citrus, brioche, and mineral complexity.

name: Champagne H.Blin  Blanc de Noirs Brut

name: Louis Roederer  Blanc de Blancs 2016
short_description (EN Store): 100% Cramant & Avize Grand Cru Chardonnay, 5 years on lees — Roederer's chalk-pure single-varietal statement from the Côte des Blancs.
description (EN Store): Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon vinifies each of Roederer's 410+ parcels separately — a grower-house discipline almost unheard of at this production scale — and the Blanc de Blancs is where that parcel-level obsession finds its purest expression. 100% Chardonnay sourced exclusively from Cramant and Avize Grand Crus on the Côte des Blancs: limestone mid-slopes with thin clay-loam topsoil over pure chalk bedrock, delivering the tight mineral tension and citrus-driven precision that defines this sub-region. The 2016 vintage spent five years on lees followed by six months post-disgorgement — the longest lees regime of any non-prestige Roederer cuvée — building autolytic complexity without obscuring the chalk signature. Malolactic fermentation is partially blocked to preserve structural acidity and aging architecture, a deliberate counter to the softer, rounder house styles common among large Champagne maisons. What sets this expression apart: unlike the Brut Vintage, no Pinot Noir enters the blend and no reserve wines are incorporated, making it the only Roederer cuvée that speaks in a single voice — pure Côte des Blancs chalk, uncut. The 2016 growing season delivered natural tension and concentration; at release the wine carries both the freshness of its chalk origins and the textural depth of five years' lees contact, with a drinking window extending comfortably through the late 2020s.

name: Tenuta Baron  Spumante Buchet Brut

description (EN Store): Tenuta Baron's Spumante Buchet Brut is a dry, refreshing sparkling wine from Italy's Prosecco region, crafted from Glera grapes. name: Penfolds  Champagne Cuvee Brut
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' rule-breaking Champagne collaboration: an Australian house applying its multi-regional blending philosophy to the Champagne appellation.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Adelaide, Penfolds built its entire identity on a winemaking philosophy that is the deliberate antithesis of terroir orthodoxy — blending across districts to express winemaker vision over single-site provenance. That same transgressive logic underpins this Champagne Cuvée Brut, a collaboration that imports Penfolds' multi-regional blending doctrine into the most appellation-bound sparkling wine category on earth. The cuvée is produced within the AOC Champagne framework, yet conceived by a house whose four-generation winemaking lineage — Schubert, Ditter, Duval, Gago — has never once deferred to single-terroir convention. The result is a Champagne shaped by an outsider's editorial hand: fruit selection and assemblage driven by house-style consistency rather than village or cru hierarchy. What is unusual here is structural: no other Champagne house carries the institutional DNA of an Australian producer whose chief winemaking philosophy explicitly rejects the Burgundian single-plot orthodoxy that Champagne's prestige cuvées quietly aspire to. Drinking window: current release, best consumed on release for freshness.

name: Fontanafredda  Asti Dolce DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Lightly sparkling Moscato from Piedmont with honeyed stone fruit, floral notes, and creamy brioche—elegant and approachable.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Asti Dolce DOCG is a gently sparkling expression of Moscato from Piedmont's prestigious Barolo region. The wine displays characteristic honeyed stone fruit and floral sweetness on the nose, with delicate peach and apricot jam notes. Fine bubbles and a brioche-tinged secondary character create a creamy, elegant mouthfeel. Low alcohol and residual sweetness make this wine approachable and versatile—equally at home as an aperitif, with dessert, or paired with soft cheeses and light Asian cuisine.

name: Fontanafredda  Alta Langa Limited Edition Brut DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont sparkling with Pinot Noir and Chardonnay; crisp apple, brioche, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Alta Langa Limited Edition Brut DOCG represents the pinnacle of traditional-method sparkling winemaking in Piedmont's prestigious Alta Langa zone. A refined blend of Pinot Noir and Chardonnay, this wine displays the hallmark elegance of the region: a pale golden hue, fine persistent bubbles, and a nose of green apple, citrus zest, and toasted brioche. On the palate, stone fruit and hazelnut notes emerge alongside a mineral backbone, finishing with crisp acidity and subtle honey undertones. The extended aging on lees imparts complexity and structure, positioning this as a sophisticated aperitif or pairing for delicate seafood and fresh cheeses.

name: Dom Perignon 2015
short_description (EN Store): 2015 P1: drought-stressed Grand Cru chalk, 51% Pinot Noir, 8+ yrs reductive lees aging, disgorged Jan 2023. 97 pts Suckling.
description (EN Store): Dom Pérignon is the only major Champagne house that produces exclusively vintage wine — no harvest, no release — a constraint that makes the 2015 declaration a deliberate statement of confidence in a climatically extreme year. A hot, drought-stressed summer across the Marne and Côte des Blancs was interrupted by August rains, forcing a harvest that demanded precision in sorting and pressing. Grapes drawn from nine fixed crus — Chardonnay from Avize, Cramant, Chouilly, and Le Mesnil-sur-Oger; Pinot Noir from Aÿ, Bouzy, Verzenay, Mailly-Champagne, and Hautvillers — all rooted in deep chalk and limestone that acted as a moisture reservoir through the drought stress, concentrating phenolics while preserving mineral tension. Vinified in stainless steel with full malolactic fermentation, using two distinct yeast strains for primary fermentation and two further strains for the in-bottle prise de mousse — a four-yeast architecture Chef de Cave Vincent Chaperon considers central to long-term ageing capacity. Aged a minimum of eight years on lees, undisgorged, in chalk cellars; disgorged January 2023; dosage 4.5 g/L; blend 51% Pinot Noir / 49% Chardonnay. Zero oak contact at any stage — all complexity is lees-derived and reductive. The 2015 sits at the richer, more textural end of the DP spectrum: citrus pith, white peach, white pepper, and a saline chalk minerality underpin a wine rated 97 pts James Suckling, 95 pts Wine Advocate, 95 pts Vinous; drinking now through 2040+.

name: Yannick Prevoteau Champagne Perle Des Treilles Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Brut with 60% Pinot Noir and 40% Chardonnay, offering brioche complexity and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Yannick Prevoteau Perle Des Treilles Brut is a non-vintage Champagne crafted from a classic blend of 60% Pinot Noir and 40% Chardonnay. This composition delivers a balanced profile: the Pinot Noir contributes red fruit character and structure, while Chardonnay adds elegance, citrus brightness, and mineral tension. Expect toasty brioche notes from extended aging on lees, complemented by subtle honey and hazelnut complexity. The wine's high acidity and fine mousse make it an ideal aperitif and a sophisticated partner for shellfish, oysters, and delicate canapés.

name: Louis Roederer  Cristal Brut 2016
short_description (EN Store): Cristal 2016: 80ha of biodynamic Grand Cru chalk across 7 communes, 6 years on lees, ~7–8 g/L dosage — the cuvée invented for a Tsar in 1876.
description (EN Store): In 1876, Louis Roederer created Cristal exclusively for Tsar Alexander II — bottled in flat-bottomed clear crystal glass so the Tsar's guards could inspect for concealed explosives. That same bottle architecture survives today, now wrapped in UV-protective yellow cellophane to shield the wine aging within. The 2016 Cristal is assembled from 45 discrete Grand Cru plots across seven communes — Verzenay, Verzy, Beaumont-sur-Vesle, Aÿ, Avize, Mesnil-sur-Oger, and Cramant — totalling roughly 80 hectares farmed fully biodynamic since 2012, the largest certified biodynamic estate in Champagne. Soils are predominantly pure chalk beneath a thin clay-loam topsoil of approximately 20 cm; mid-slope drainage concentrates mineral tension and restrains vine vigour. Chef de Cave Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon vinifies each parcel separately in small stainless-steel tanks or oak vats, then blends ~60% Pinot Noir with ~40% Chardonnay — no reserve wines incorporated, preserving the singular character of a single harvest. A portion of the blend undergoes blocked malolactic fermentation to sustain structural acidity. The wine rests 6 years on lees followed by 8 months post-disgorgement before release, with dosage of ~7–8 g/L. Unlike any peer prestige cuvée, Cristal draws 100% of its fruit from Roederer's own estate — zero purchased fruit — giving it grower-Champagne precision at maison scale. The 2016 vintage is not yet documented in the brand library's vintage notes; drink from 2026 through the mid-2030s.

name: Franzini Spumante Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Glera with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle from Veneto.
description (EN Store): Franzini Spumante Brut is a dry, refreshing sparkling wine from Italy's Prosecco region, crafted entirely from Glera grapes. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle brioche and toast notes from the traditional méthode. Fine, persistent bubbles and bright acidity make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, fresh cheese, and light seafood. A versatile, food-friendly sparkler at an accessible price point.

name: Villa Calappiano Prosecco DOC Treviso Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, refreshing Prosecco with green apple and citrus—ideal aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Villa Calappiano Prosecco DOC Treviso Extra Dry is a classic expression of Italy's most celebrated sparkling wine. Crafted from 100% Glera grapes in the Prosecco region of Veneto, this Extra Dry bottling balances residual sweetness with vibrant acidity.

Tasting Profile: Bright green apple and citrus dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle pear and a whisper of brioche from the traditional Charmat method. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refreshing, dry finish.

Versatility: An excellent aperitif on its own, or paired with oysters, shellfish, fresh cheeses, and light Asian cuisine. The crisp acidity makes it a natural companion to sushi and dim sum.

name: Franzini Prosecco DOC Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, elegant NV Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche—perfect aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Franzini Prosecco DOC Extra Dry is a vibrant, light-bodied sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted entirely from Glera grapes. The Extra Dry classification delivers a bone-dry palate with refreshing acidity and fine, persistent bubbles. On the nose, green apple and citrus zest dominate, complemented by subtle pear and floral hints. The palate reveals crisp stone fruit and a delicate brioche undertone from the traditional Charmat method, finishing clean and mineral-driven. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkler ideal for aperitifs, seafood, and casual entertaining.

name: Louis Roederer  Collection 245
short_description (EN Store): NV Champagne blending 50+ villages with 20% oak-aged reserve wines; grower-precision from a 240-hectare estate, minimum 3 years on lees.
description (EN Store): When Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon restructured the Collection blend — formerly Brut Premier — he codified a philosophy already embedded in Roederer's DNA: vinify each of the 410+ parcels separately in individual stainless-steel tanks or small oak vats, then assemble with reserve wines aged in 150+ used oak barrels, preserving plot identity at a scale no other grande maison attempts. The base draws from 50+ villages across Montagne de Reims, Vallée de la Marne, and Côte des Blancs, where shallow chalk topsoil — as thin as 20 cm over pure chalk bedrock at Verzenay and Verzy — drives the saline, tensile minerality that distinguishes Roederer's house signature. The blend runs ~40% Pinot Noir, 40% Chardonnay, 20% Pinot Meunier, with ~20% oak-aged reserve wines integrated to build textural depth and cross-vintage consistency. Malolactic fermentation is partially blocked on a portion of the blend to preserve structural acidity. The wine rests a minimum 3 years on lees followed by 6 months post-disgorgement before release, at ~9 g/L dosage. What sets Collection apart from comparable NV Bruts is its grower-like estate sourcing — roughly two-thirds of all fruit is estate-grown — giving it terroir traceability that most multi-village blends cannot claim. The 245 disgorgement number tracks the perpetual blend's evolution, making each release a dateable, collectible iteration rather than an anonymous NV.

name: Borga  Prosecco Treviso Brut Millesimato
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco Brut with green apple, citrus, and fine bubbles—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Borga's Prosecco Treviso Brut Millesimato is a dry, refreshing sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region. Crafted from Glera grapes, this vintage expression delivers bright green apple and citrus on the nose, with a palate marked by crisp acidity and delicate pear notes. Fine bubbles and a hint of brioche complexity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, shellfish, and fresh cheeses. The wine's lean body and high acidity ensure versatility at the table, from casual hors d'oeuvres to more refined seafood preparations.

name: Chandon  Garden Spritz
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Chandon Garden Spritz is a vibrant sparkling wine from Australia's Yarra Valley, crafted in the méthode champenoise tradition. Bright green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, with subtle brioche and stone fruit notes adding complexity. The palate is refreshingly crisp with fine bubbles and a clean, dry finish. Light-bodied and high in acidity, this wine excels as an aperitif or paired with oysters, shellfish, and soft cheeses.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Blanc De Noirs Extra Brut  2016
short_description (EN Store): Blanc de Noirs 2016 from Philipponnat's chalk-over-limestone Mareuil-sur-Aÿ parcels — Extra Brut, 100% Pinot Noir, vinous and structurally austere.
description (EN Store):
Philipponnat's family viticultural roots in Aÿ trace to 1522, but the deliberate choice to produce a vintage-dated Blanc de Noirs at Extra Brut dosage signals a very modern ambition: to let a single harvest and a single variety speak without the softening cushion of reserve blending or residual sugar. The 2016 growing season in Champagne delivered a cool, slow-ripening year that concentrated phenolic structure and preserved cutting acidity — conditions that reward a Pinot Noir-only expression rather than penalise it.

Fruit is drawn from Philipponnat's own south- and southeast-facing chalk and limestone slopes in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ and Aÿ, sitting at approximately 170 m ASL. Pure chalk subsoil provides exceptional drainage and drives the saline, almost flint-edged mineral tension that defines the house's Pinot-dominant style. Only first-press cuvée juice is used — no tailles — preserving aromatic precision and structural clarity. Fermentation proceeds in a combination of stainless steel and oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation retained to balance the Extra Brut's near-zero dosage without stripping texture. Lees aging in bottle exceeds the legal vintage minimum, deepening autolytic complexity while the chalk-derived acidity keeps the frame taut.

What separates this from virtually every peer in the Blanc de Noirs category is the combination of a declared single vintage, Extra Brut dosage, and Philipponnat's back-label transparency — disgorgement date, dosage level, and base vintage printed since 1996, a practice the house pioneered in Champagne. The 2016 is drinking with authority now but carries the structural reserves for further cellaring.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Clos des Goisses Extra Brut 2012
short_description (EN Store): Clos des Goisses 2012: a single 5.5-ha south-facing monopole on chalk-over-flint above Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, aged 8+ years on lees, extra brut.
description (EN Store): The Clos des Goisses is one of Champagne's most geologically extreme sites — a single walled monopole of 5.5 hectares pitched at a 30–45° south-facing slope directly above the Marne canal in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, where pure chalk and flint subsoils absorb and radiate heat to a degree unmatched in the region. Philipponnat has farmed this parcel since 1935, and it remains the house's singular obsession. The 2012 vintage was harvested from vines averaging over 30 years of age, a year defined by a cool, slow ripening season followed by a September sun window that delivered phenolic maturity without sacrificing the site's structural tension. Vinification proceeds in a combination of oak barrels and stainless steel, with partial malolactic fermentation deliberately arrested to preserve the site's inherent chalk-driven acidity — a deliberate departure from the full-malo approach common across the appellation. The wine aged on lees for a minimum of eight years in Philipponnat's 1.5 km of underground galleries at a constant 12°C, then released at extra brut dosage (under 6 g/L), allowing the terroir rather than sweetness to frame the finish. What no peer replicates: a monopole of this gradient, this chalk purity, unified under a single producer's unbroken stewardship since 1935. The 2012 is now entering its ideal drinking window, with the extended lees contact having resolved any reductive tension into autolytic depth.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Clos des Goisses Extra Brut 2010 (Magnum) (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Clos des Goisses 2010 in magnum: a 45° south-facing flint-and-chalk monopole aged on lees for over a decade, Extra Brut, from one of Champagne's most extreme single sites.
description (EN Store):
The Clos des Goisses is Philipponnat's raison d'être — a 5.5-hectare walled monopole on the steepest slope in all of Champagne, rising at a near-impossible 45° angle above the Marne canal in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ. The gradient forces hand-harvesting on terraces of pure flint and chalk, a combination that drives photosynthesis to extremes while the canal below moderates frost risk and amplifies reflected heat. The result is physiological ripeness rarely achievable this far north, without sacrificing the site's razor-edged mineral tension.

The 2010 vintage — cool, late, and structurally austere at harvest — demanded patience. Fermentation proceeds in both stainless steel and old oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation deliberately arrested to preserve the site's inherent acidity. Dosage is held to Extra Brut levels, letting the chalk-driven salinity and flint-smoke character speak without sweetness as a crutch. In magnum format, the extended lees contact — well beyond the already generous aging Philipponnat applies across its range — compresses and integrates the wine's structure over time at a slower oxidative rate than standard 75cl.

What no peer does: Clos des Goisses is a Pinot Noir-dominant monopole on a south-facing flint slope that routinely achieves grand-cru-Burgundy-level site specificity within a category defined by blending across villages. The 2010, now past 14 years on cork in magnum, is entering its most expressive plateau.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Clos des Goisses Extra Brut Rose 2009
short_description (EN Store): Clos des Goisses' steepest chalk slope meets 2009's solar heat: an Extra Brut rosé from Champagne's most site-specific monopole.
description (EN Store):
The Clos des Goisses is a 5.5-hectare walled monopole on a near-vertical south-facing chalk escarpment above the Marne canal at Mareuil-sur-Aÿ — the steepest vineyard in Champagne, where gradient and full solar exposure push ripeness to levels structurally incompatible with standard dosage. Philipponnat, whose family first acquired vines in Aÿ in 1522, has vinified this single parcel as a prestige cuvée since 1935, making it one of the oldest continuously produced single-vineyard Champagnes on record.

The chalk subsoil here is essentially pure, delivering extreme drainage and a mineral tension that runs like a seam through every expression. The 2009 vintage — one of the hottest and driest in modern Champagne memory — amplified the site's natural generosity: concentrated stone fruit, high natural sugars, and a structural weight that demanded the Extra Brut dosage (3–4 g/L) rather than the house's standard Brut level. The rosé designation comes from saignée or red-wine addition of Pinot Noir vinified at the Clos itself, deepening both colour and tannin architecture. Extended lees aging well beyond the prestige-cuvée minimum at 12°C in Philipponnat's 1.5 km of underground chalk galleries has compressed the vintage's initial exuberance into something taut and layered.

What no peer replicates: a monopole this steep, this chalk-pure, producing a rosé in a year this warm at near-zero dosage — the combination of site extremity, vintage heat, and restraint in dosage is structurally singular within Champagne's rosé prestige tier.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Mareuil-sur-Ay Extra Brut 2014
short_description (EN Store): Mareuil-sur-Aÿ chalk slopes, 2014 vintage, perpetual solera reserve dating to 1946 — Philipponnat's most transparent Champagne expression.
description (EN Store): When Philipponnat printed the base vintage year, dosage level, and disgorgement date on its back labels in 1996 — years before transparency became a Champagne-house talking point — it was a declaration of intent, not marketing. The 2014 Mareuil-sur-Aÿ Extra Brut is that philosophy made concrete: a single-vintage release from the house's home village, where south- and southeast-facing chalk and limestone slopes at approximately 170 m ASL deliver relentless drainage and a mineral tension that chalk soils uniquely impose on Pinot Noir. The 2014 growing season in Champagne was cool and late, producing wines of pronounced acidity and structural austerity — conditions that reward extended aging rather than early drinking. Philipponnat's winemaking here centres on first-press cuvée juice only, fermentation across stainless steel and neutral oak vessels, and partial malolactic fermentation to preserve the vintage's inherent tension while adding textural weight. At Extra Brut dosage, the wine is stripped of any sweetness buffer, placing the chalk minerality and Pinot Noir structure entirely in the foreground. What separates this from any peer is the house's Pinot Noir-dominant philosophy applied to a single declared vintage from a single Premier Cru commune — a combination that most négociant houses reserve exclusively for prestige cuvées. The 2014 is drinking with authority now and carries the structural reserves for further development through the late 2020s.

name: Champagne Philipponnat Le Leon Grand Cru Extra Brut 2014
short_description (EN Store): Grand Cru 2014 from Philipponnat's chalk-over-limestone Aÿ holdings — Extra Brut dosage, Pinot Noir-dominant, aged deep in 18th-century Mareuil-sur-Aÿ galleries.
description (EN Store): The Philipponnat family's viticultural presence in Aÿ traces to 1522, and it is from these Grand Cru chalk-and-limestone slopes — south- and southeast-facing at roughly 170 m ASL in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ — that Le Leon 2014 draws its structural spine. Pure chalk subsoil delivers relentless drainage and a mineral tension that defines the house's Pinot Noir-dominant identity. The 2014 growing season in Champagne was marked by a cool, late summer that preserved exceptional natural acidity, making it one of the decade's most age-worthy vintages — a quality Charles Philipponnat, 15th-generation steward of the house, chose to honour with an Extra Brut dosage that refuses to soften the wine's architecture. Vinification draws exclusively on first-press cuvée juice, fermented in a combination of stainless steel and neutral oak, with partial malolactic fermentation retained to balance texture against the vintage's inherent tension. Extended lees aging in bottle at a constant 12°C — well beyond the legal minimum — builds the brioche depth and autolytic complexity that distinguishes a prestige vintage release from the house's NV tier. What sets Le Leon apart within the Philipponnat range is its single-vintage, single-terroir Grand Cru focus: no perpetual solera reserve, no blending across villages — just Aÿ chalk, 2014 fruit, and time. Now entering its ideal drinking window, the wine carries the structural density of a great Pinot Noir vintage with the precision that only Extra Brut restraint allows.

name: Champagne Philipponnat "Les Cintres" Extra Brut 2009
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Les Cintres 2009 from Mareuil-sur-Aÿ's chalk slopes — a vintage-dated Philipponnat rarity aged far beyond NV norms.
description (EN Store): The Philipponnat family's viticultural roots in Aÿ trace to 1522, but Les Cintres represents something the house has rarely released: a single-parcel, vintage-dated Champagne from a specific lieu-dit within their Mareuil-sur-Aÿ holdings — a deliberate departure from the blending philosophy that defines even their prestige cuvées. The vineyard sits on south-facing pure chalk slopes at approximately 170 m ASL in Mareuil-sur-Aÿ, where the free-draining chalk draws roots deep and concentrates mineral tension into the must. The 2009 growing season delivered exceptional ripeness across Champagne — generous fruit weight with naturally elevated sugars — conditions that make the Extra Brut dosage a pointed editorial choice: restraint imposed precisely where the vintage least demanded it, forcing the chalk and Pinot Noir structure to carry the wine unaided. Vinification follows Philipponnat's house discipline: first-press cuvée juice only, fermentation in a combination of stainless steel and oak vessels, with partial malolactic fermentation preserving tension while adding textural breadth. Extended lees aging in bottle — well beyond the 36-month minimum applied even to Royal Reserve — has compressed the 2009 fruit into something more architectural than exuberant. What distinguishes Les Cintres categorically is its single-site identity: no solera reserve, no blending across villages — just one parcel, one harvest, one chalk expression, now approaching 15 years on cork.

name: Champagne Chavost Ratafia Champenois Sans Sulfite (700 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Zero-sulphur Ratafia from Chavot-Courcourt's organic co-op — grape must arrested with Champagne eau-de-vie, no added SO₂, no fining.
description (EN Store):
When Fabian Daviaux persuaded 70-plus grower families to abandon sulphur additions across their entire portfolio in 2019, the ambition extended beyond sparkling wine — Chavost's Ratafia Champenois Sans Sulfite applies the same zero-intervention logic to Champagne's traditional mistelle, a category where sulphur has historically been the default preservative. The result is one of the only commercially produced zero-SO₂ Ratafia in the appellation.

Fruit comes from Chavot-Courcourt's clay-limestone and chalky-limestone plots at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne — a dual-soil mosaic that delivers both chalkboard tension and textural weight to the base must. Vines average 35–60 years of age; only first-press cuvée juice is used, taille excluded, preserving aromatic precision over volume.

Fermentation is arrested by the addition of Champagne eau-de-vie in the traditional mutage process, locking residual grape sugar before it converts. No added sulphites at any stage — naturally occurring SO₂ measured at the low end of Chavost's 6–18 mg/L range. Unfiltered and unfined, consistent with the house's bottling philosophy across all cuvées.

The special feature: zero-SO₂ Ratafia is genuinely rare — most producers rely on sulphur precisely because high residual sugar in an unfortified-adjacent product creates oxidative risk that Chavost accepts and manages through fruit quality alone.

name: Champagne Chavost Assemblage Brut Nature
short_description (EN Store): Zero-sulphur, zero-dosage Champagne from Chavot-Courcourt's clay-limestone plots — 47% Chardonnay, 47% Meunier, native yeast, unfiltered, ~9,000 bottles.
description (EN Store):
In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of this 70-family cooperative — persuaded every grower in Chavot-Courcourt to convert to organic viticulture simultaneously, then stripped the winemaking back to nothing: no added sulphites at any stage, no dosage, no fining, no filtration, no chaptalization. For a cooperative, this is almost without precedent in Champagne AOC.

The 22 plots sit at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne, where clay-limestone soils with chalky-limestone patches at an unclassified village elevation deliver both textural weight and the fine mineral tension that holds the wine together in the absence of dosage sugar. Vines average 35–60 years old; only 100% first-press cuvée juice is used — taille excluded entirely.

Base wines undergo spontaneous alcoholic fermentation at 17°C and spontaneous malolactic fermentation at 20°C, both with indigenous microflora, in thermoregulated stainless steel. Second fermentation proceeds in bottle without added liqueur de tirage sugar. Lees aging runs a minimum of approximately 20 months, building autolytic depth without the toasty richness of house-style Champagne.

The flagship blend — 47% Chardonnay / 47% Pinot Meunier / 6% Pinot Noir — gives Meunier equal billing with Chardonnay, a deliberate inversion of grande maison hierarchy that delivers red-fruit generosity and mid-palate texture the acid-only structure of Brut Nature would otherwise lack. Total SO₂ runs 6–18 mg/L naturally occurring — a fraction of the legal Brut Nature ceiling of 30 mg/L.

name: Champagne Chavost Assemblage Brut Nature (Magnum) (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Zero-sulphur, zero-dosage Champagne from a 70-family organic co-op in unclassified Chavot-Courcourt — native yeast, unfined, 20 months on lees. Magnum format.
description (EN Store): In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of the 1946 Chavot-Courcourt collective — persuaded over 70 co-op families to abandon sulphur additions, dosage, and conventional inputs entirely, making Chavost one of the only cooperatives in Champagne to operate a fully zero-SO₂, zero-dosage, organically farmed portfolio. The 22 plots across 5–5.5 hectares in Chavot-Courcourt sit at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne, on a dual mosaic of clay-limestone and chalky-limestone soils with a north-facing aspect — conditions that preserve natural acidity and slow phenolic development in the 35–60-year-old vines, providing the structural backbone that makes zero-dosage viable without chaptalization. The Blanc d'Assemblage blends 47% Chardonnay / 47% Pinot Meunier / 6% Pinot Noir, using only first-press cuvée juice; base wines undergo spontaneous alcoholic fermentation at 17°C and spontaneous malolactic fermentation at 20°C in thermoregulated stainless steel, with no enzymes, fining agents, or acidification at any stage. Second fermentation proceeds in bottle without added tirage sugar, and the wine rests a minimum of ~20 months on lees before disgorgement with zero dosage and no added sulphites (total SO₂ measured at 6–18 mg/L naturally). The Magnum format extends the lees-contact phase and slows post-disgorgement oxidation — unusual for a co-op cuvée at this price point, and structurally significant for a wine carrying no sulphite safety net.

name: Champagne Chavost Assemblage Brut Nature (Double Magnum) (3 L)
short_description (EN Store): Zero-sulphur, zero-dosage Champagne from 35–60yr Chavot-Courcourt vines — native yeast, unfiltered, 47/47/6 Chardonnay/Meunier/Pinot Noir in a 3L format.
description (EN Store):
In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of this 1946 grower cooperative — persuaded over 70 multi-generational families to abandon sulphur additions, dosage, and conventional inputs entirely, converting all 22 plots to organic viticulture simultaneously. That collective act of conviction is what sits inside this Double Magnum.

The fruit comes from clay-limestone and chalky-limestone soils across Chavot-Courcourt, an unclassified village at the precise geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne — a dual soil mosaic that pulls tension from chalk and texture from clay, amplified by 35–60 year old vines whose root systems reach deep enough to express both.

Only first-press cuvée juice is used — taille entirely excluded. Base wines ferment spontaneously with indigenous yeasts at 17°C; malolactic conversion follows with indigenous bacteria at 20°C, unsupported by enzymes, tannin additions, or acidification. No sugar is added to the liqueur de tirage, making the second fermentation in bottle a feat of precision with naturally present residual sugars. After a minimum of approximately 20 months on lees, the wine is disgorged with zero dosage and zero added sulphites — total SO₂ measured at just 6–18 mg/L — then bottled unfiltered and unfined.

The 3L Double Magnum format slows secondary oxidation and extends the lees-contact surface ratio, giving this 47% Chardonnay / 47% Pinot Meunier / 6% Pinot Noir assemblage a longer, more integrative aging arc than the standard 75cl — unusual for a zero-sulphur wine where format stability is a genuine technical risk most producers avoid.

name: Champagne Chavost Assemblage Brut Nature (Methuselah) (6 L)
short_description (EN Store): 6L Methuselah of Chavost's zero-sulphur, zero-dosage Assemblage from unclassified Chavot-Courcourt clay-limestone — native yeast, unfined, unfiltered.
description (EN Store):
In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of this 1946 grower cooperative — persuaded over 70 multi-generational families to abandon sulphur additions, dosage, and conventional inputs entirely, converting Chavost into one of Champagne's only cooperatives operating a fully zero-sulphur-added, zero-dosage portfolio from certified organic fruit.

The 22 plots of Chavot-Courcourt sit at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne, where a dual mosaic of clay-limestone and chalky-limestone soils on a north-facing aspect produces wines with a taut, saline mineral frame and slower phenolic development — structural tension that makes zero-dosage viable without austerity.

The Assemblage Brut Nature blends 47% Chardonnay / 47% Pinot Meunier / 6% Pinot Noir, drawn exclusively from first-press cuvée juice. Spontaneous alcoholic fermentation at 17°C in thermoregulated stainless steel is followed by spontaneous malolactic fermentation at 20°C — both with indigenous microflora only. No enzymes, no chaptalization, no acidification, no fining, no filtration, and naturally occurring SO₂ measured at 6–18 mg/L total. Second fermentation proceeds without added liqueur de tirage sugar; lees aging runs a minimum of approximately 20 months in bottle.

The Methuselah format (6L) dramatically slows oxidative exchange and extends autolytic contact, producing a textural depth and bead fineness that the standard 75cl disgorgement cannot replicate — an unusual proposition for a cooperative whose entire annual Assemblage production is approximately 9,000 standard bottles.

Technically single-vintage in character but unlabeled as such under AOC rules, the wine rewards patience: the compact acidic frame and citrus-driven fruit of current disgorgements will integrate further with 2–4 years of additional cellaring in this large format.

name: Champagne Chavost Blanc de Meunier Brut Nature
short_description (EN Store): 100% Pinot Meunier from 35–60yr vines in Chavot-Courcourt — zero sulphites, zero dosage, unfiltered, native yeast. Co-op Champagne rewritten from the ground up.
description (EN Store):
In 2019, chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member of the Chavost cooperative — persuaded over 70 multi-generational grower families to abandon sulphite additions, dosage, and conventional inputs entirely, converting 22 plots across 5–5.5 hectares to certified organic viticulture. The Blanc de Meunier is the purest expression of that conviction: 100% Pinot Meunier, a grape the grandes maisons routinely blend away or dismiss as a blending workhorse rather than a solo voice.

The vines — 35 to 60 years old — grow in Chavot-Courcourt's dual-soil mosaic of clay-limestone and chalky-limestone, sitting at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne. That clay fraction retains moisture through dry summers and moderates ripening, giving Meunier the textural generosity it needs to carry a wine alone without dosage to fill structural gaps.

Only first-press cuvée juice enters the vat; taille is excluded entirely. Spontaneous alcoholic fermentation at 17°C and spontaneous malolactic fermentation at 20°C proceed with indigenous yeasts and bacteria — no enzymes, no chaptalization, no acidification, no added sulphites at any stage (naturally occurring SO₂ measured at 6–18 mg/L). Second fermentation proceeds in bottle without liqueur de tirage sugar addition, and the wine rests minimum ~20 months on lees before disgorgement. Bottled unfiltered and unfined, zero dosage.

What no peer cooperative does: Chavost produces a single-varietal Meunier Brut Nature at zero sulphites within a co-op structure — a combination that does not otherwise exist in commercial Champagne at this scale. Technically single-vintage in character, though unlabeled as such under AOC rules due to short tirage timelines; the 2020 disgorgement batches show a compact acidic frame with citrus and stone fruit that rewards additional bottle time.

name: Champagne Chavost Eureka Brut Nature
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Noir-dominant Brut Nature from Chavot-Courcourt's clay-limestone plots — zero sulphites, zero dosage, unfiltered, ~2,000 bottles.
description (EN Store):
When chef de caves Fabian Daviaux — great-grandson of a founding member — persuaded over 70 co-op families to abandon sulphites entirely in 2019, the Eurêka! was the cuvée that made the argument most forcefully: a 70% Pinot Noir / 15% Chardonnay / 15% Pinot Meunier assemblage that inverts the cooperative Champagne template from the ground up.

The fruit comes from 22 plots across 5–5.5 hectares in Chavot-Courcourt, an unclassified village sitting at the geographic junction of the Côte des Blancs and the Vallée de la Marne. The dual soil mosaic — clay-limestone with chalky-limestone patches, north-facing — delivers tension and mineral grip that makes zero-dosage viable without the lean austerity that sinks lesser Brut Natures.

Only 100% first-press cuvée juice enters the vat; taille is excluded entirely. Spontaneous alcoholic fermentation at 17°C and spontaneous malolactic fermentation at 20°C proceed with indigenous yeasts and bacteria — no enzymes, no chaptalization, no acidification, no added sulphites at any stage (total SO₂ measured at 6–18 mg/L). Second fermentation in bottle adds no liqueur de tirage sugar. The wine rests a minimum of ~20 months on lees before disgorgement, then leaves the cellar unfiltered and unfined.

At roughly 2,000 bottles per release, Eurêka! is the rarest expression in the Chavost range — and the only one where Pinot Noir commands the assemblage, delivering the structural weight that anchors the cooperative's most transgressive winemaking choices.

name: Champagne Caze-Thibaut Les Fourches
short_description (EN Store): 2018 Les Fourches: Cazé-Thibaut's first certified-organic vintage — clay-limestone Marne right bank, no MLF, native-yeast barrel ferment, Extra Brut precision.
description (EN Store):
When Fabien Cazé — tenth generation, admitted to the Terres et Vins de Champagne collective after just his second release — declared 2018 his first certified-organic vintage, he was simultaneously navigating Champagne's hottest, most opulent growing season in years. The tension between that generous fruit and his deliberately austere winemaking philosophy is what defines Les Fourches 2018.

The fruit originates from Cazé-Thibaut's 2.66-hectare holding on the right bank of the Marne, where clay-limestone soils with sandy clay topsoil over shallow chalk bedrock on east- and south-facing slopes in Châtillon-sur-Marne and Vandières impose a chalky mineral tension even in warm years. The chalk subsoil's drainage kept 2018's heat from tipping into flabbiness.

Grapes were hand-harvested parcel by parcel, pressed in a Coquard inclined-plate press, then gravity-fed into tank before a portion transferred into 228–350 L oak barrels for spontaneous native-yeast fermentation — long and cold in the 19th-century vaulted chalk cellar on rue des Crayères. Malolactic fermentation was not sought, the cellar's low ambient temperatures preventing its completion. Dosage sits at Extra Brut level (1.7–2.7 g/L), with no fining, no filtration.
