Fruit is hand-harvested from 40-year-average-age vines and pressed in a traditional Coquard; primary fermentation proceeds in stainless steel with indigenous pied-de-cuve yeasts, full malolactic completed in tank, and parcels vinified separately before blending. Reserve wines rest in used demi-muids sourced from Domaine Olivier Lamy in Saint-Aubin, held in a perpetual reserve system initiated in 2011. The wine ages sur lattes for a minimum of 30–36 months, is bottled unfiltered and unfined, and receives an Extra Brut dosage of just 3 g/L using rectified grape must rather than cane sugar. The 2021 vintage is the first under the fully reformed, single-vineyard-oriented range — expect the Kimmeridgian mineral spine to be particularly pronounced, with the low-SO2 protocol lending an unusually vivid, unmediated fruit character. Drink now through 2028.

name: Champagne Pierre Gerbais La Loge
short_description (EN Store): Single-parcel Côte des Bar Pinot Noir from Kimmeridgian marl, zero SO2 in vinification, 30+ months on lees, Extra Brut 3 g/L.
description (EN Store): In 2021, Aurélien Gerbais dismantled his entire cuvée structure and rebuilt it around single-vineyard bottlings — a deliberate break from the blending orthodoxy that governs most Champagne houses. La Loge is one of those parcel-specific expressions: 100% Pinot Noir from Celles-sur-Ource, where soils are Kimmeridgian marl and limestone studded with fossilised ammonites — the same geological band that defines Chablis Premier Cru — sitting at the confluence of the Seine, Arce, Laigne, and Ource river valleys. That marine-fossil subsoil drives a saline, chalky tension into the fruit that no blending can replicate. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous pied-de-cuve yeasts in stainless steel, full malolactic completed in tank, and zero SO2 added during vinification or ageing — only a minimal adjustment at bottling (total SO2 ~25–30 mg/L). Reserve wines rest in used demi-muids from Olivier Lamy (Saint-Aubin) within a perpetual reserve system initiated in 2011. The wine ages sur lattes for a minimum of 30–36 months, then rests at least six months post-disgorgement before release. Dosage is fixed at Extra Brut 3 g/L using rectified grape must rather than cane sugar, bottled unfiltered and unfined. What no peer does: zero-SO2 vinification on a single-parcel Aube Pinot Noir aged in Burgundian demi-muids — a transparency protocol that exposes terroir with almost uncomfortable directness. Drink now through 5+ years from disgorgement date.

name: Champagne Pierre Gerbais Les Grandes Cotes
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard Côte des Bar Pinot Noir from Kimmeridgian marl; zero SO2 vinification, 30+ months on lees, Extra Brut 3 g/L.
description (EN Store): In 2021, Aurélien Gerbais dismantled his multi-cuvée range and rebuilt it entirely around single-vineyard bottlings — a structural decision that placed each parcel's Kimmeridgian identity at the centre of every wine. Les Grandes Côtes is 100% Pinot Noir from Celles-sur-Ource, where soils of Kimmeridgian marl and limestone — dense with fossilised ammonites, geologically akin to Chablis — sit above bands of Portlandian clay-limestone, delivering both tensile mineral drive and textural depth. Hand-harvested fruit is pressed in a traditional Coquard press, with cold débourbage at 12°C before primary fermentation in stainless steel using indigenous pied-de-cuve yeasts; full malolactic fermentation is completed in tank. Zero SO2 is added during vinification or ageing — only a minimal adjustment at bottling (total SO2 ~25–30 mg/L). The wine ages sur lattes for a minimum of 30–36 months, then rests at least six months post-disgorgement before release. Dosage is fixed at Extra Brut 3 g/L using rectified grape must rather than cane sugar, preserving site purity. The zero-SO2 vinification protocol for a grower Champagne at this price point is genuinely rare — a transparency commitment that most Aube peers have not matched. Drink now through 4–5 years from disgorgement date.

name: Champagne Pierre Gerbais Beauregard
short_description (EN Store): Single-vineyard 100% Pinot Noir saignée rosé from Kimmeridgian marl in Celles-sur-Ource; zero SO2 during vinification, Extra Brut 3 g/L dosage.
description (EN Store): In 2021, Aurélien Gerbais restructured the entire Pierre Gerbais range around a single radical commitment: every cuvée would be a single-vineyard bottling, and no sulphur dioxide would be added at any stage before bottling — a transparency standard almost unheard of in Champagne AOC. Beauregard is the estate's rosé expression, produced as 100% Pinot Noir via saignée from the Beauregard parcel in Celles-sur-Ource, Côte des Bar. The vineyard sits on Kimmeridgian marl and limestone — the same geological formation that defines Chablis Premier Cru — interlaced with Portlandian clay-limestone and fossilised ammonites, at the confluence of the Seine, Arce, Laigne, and Ource river valleys. This cool, moisture-moderating microclimate preserves tension and cut in the Pinot Noir fruit. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous pied-de-cuve yeasts in stainless steel, full malolactic completed in tank, and the wine ages sur lattes for a minimum of 30–36 months before disgorgement. Total SO2 at bottling is held to ~25–30 mg/L, with dosage fixed at Extra Brut 3 g/L using rectified grape must (MCR) rather than cane sugar — a deliberate choice to keep the liqueur d'expédition within the wine's own flavour register. Bottled unfiltered and unfined. The saignée method on Kimmeridgian-grown Pinot Noir delivers a rosé of genuine structural weight rather than delicacy, making Beauregard an outlier among Aube grower rosés.

name: Po’ Bolle Selezione Premium
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Italian sparkling with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Po' Bolle Selezione Premium is a refined Italian sparkling wine from Piedmont, crafted to deliver brightness and finesse. Crisp green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by subtle brioche and toasted notes that hint at careful production. The palate is clean and dry, with fine bubbles that persist elegantly, making this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, fresh seafood, and delicate cheeses.

name: Bottignolo Funé Valdobbiadene Prosecco D.O.C.G. Extra Brut
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Extra Brut Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Bottignolo's Funé Valdobbiadene Prosecco D.O.C.G. Extra Brut is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's premier Prosecco region. Crafted from Glera grapes, this Extra Brut expression showcases the varietal's signature crispness with dominant green apple and citrus notes, supported by subtle brioche complexity from traditional winemaking. The wine's high acidity and fine bubble structure deliver a clean, mineral-driven finish ideal for aperitif service or pairing with delicate seafood and oysters. A versatile choice for celebrations and refined entertaining.

name: Le Morette Trentaseimesi Metodo Classico
short_description (EN Store): Lugana Turbiana from glacial-moraine clay, 36 months on lees via Metodo Classico — Le Morette's sparkling expression of a grape they helped genetically define.
description (EN Store):
Le Morette's clonal research on Turbiana — conducted in collaboration with the University of Milan and the Lugana Consortium — resulted in three newly certified clones, making this family estate a scientific co-author of the entire appellation's future. The Trentaseimesi ("thirty-six months") draws on that proprietary vine knowledge, grown on the compact, chalky marly clay of glacial-moraine origin that defines the southern shore of Lake Garda, where clay content exceeds 40% in the best parcels. That dense, mineral-laden subsoil restricts vine vigour and concentrates the saline, textural character that distinguishes Lugana Turbiana from any other Italian white grape.

Produced by Metodo Classico — secondary fermentation in bottle — and aged on lees for a full 36 months, the wine builds autolytic complexity (brioche, dried citrus peel, chalk dust) without erasing the site's salinity. The estate's position on a narrow strip between Lake Garda and the UNESCO-listed Frassino Lake creates a mild microclimate with meaningful diurnal range, preserving the acidity that sustains such extended lees contact.

What is genuinely unusual here: Turbiana is almost never subjected to this length of Metodo Classico aging — the grape's high-clay terroir and natural structural tension make it a rare candidate for a 36-month tirage, a format more associated with Franciacorta or Champagne than with Lugana DOC.

name: Louis Roederer  Rose Vintage 2017
short_description (EN Store): Saignée rosé from 100% estate Grand/Premier Cru fruit; ~20% oak-aged without MLF locks in chalk-driven tension across a generous 2017 frame.
description (EN Store): Chef de Cave Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon made a deliberate structural choice for the Rosé Vintage: blocking malolactic fermentation on roughly 20% of the blend — the oak-aged portion — to preserve the tensile acidity that chalk soils demand and that dosage alone cannot manufacture. That decision is the backbone of this wine. The fruit comes entirely from Roederer's own 240 hectares of Grand and Premier Cru parcels across Montagne de Reims and Vallée de la Marne — shallow chalk topsoil of ~20 cm over pure chalk bedrock at Verzenay and Verzy, mid-slope positions ensuring drainage and slow, even ripening. The blend is ~70% Pinot Noir, 30% Chardonnay, with colour extracted via the saignée method rather than red-wine addition, giving structural grip alongside the rosé hue. Vinification proceeds parcel-by-parcel in small stainless-steel tanks or oak vats (1–2 hectares per vessel); the wine then ages an average of 4 years on lees plus 6 months post-disgorgement. The special feature here is the combination of saignée extraction with partial non-MLF oak aging — a technique more common in Blanc de Blancs than in rosé — producing a Champagne rosé with genuine tannin texture and chalk-mineral length. The 2017 vintage, while not formally documented in Roederer's own vintage notes, follows a season of frost-reduced yields and concentrated fruit; the wine is approachable now but structured for further development through the late 2020s.

name: Champagne Andre Clouet Grand Reserve Grand Cru Brut
short_description (EN Store): Grand Cru Champagne from Montagne de Reims: brioche-forward blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir & Meunier with vibrant citrus and mineral precision.
description (EN Store):
Champagne André Clouet Grand Réserve Grand Cru Brut is a non-vintage Champagne from the prestigious Montagne de Reims subregion, crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The wine displays a refined profile anchored by bright lemon and green apple primary notes, complemented by toasted brioche and almond complexity from extended aging on the lees. A chalky minerality and subtle hazelnut undertone provide structure and elegance on the finish.


This Grand Cru expression exemplifies the house's commitment to consistency and quality across vintages, offering versatility across the table—from aperitif service through seafood, soft cheese, and delicate canapés.

name: Champagne Andre Clouet Un Jour De 1911 Grand Cru Classé Brut
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Grand Cru Champagne with brioche richness, citrus precision, and mineral complexity—a refined aperitif.
description (EN Store): Champagne André Clouet's Un Jour De 1911 Grand Cru Classé Brut is a prestige cuvée from the storied Montagne de Reims, crafted from a balanced blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier. The wine opens with vibrant lemon and green apple, followed by a rich secondary layer of brioche and toasted almond that speaks to careful aging on the lees. Mineral complexity and subtle hazelnut notes emerge on the finish, creating a wine of both immediate pleasure and contemplative depth. This is a sophisticated aperitif and a natural companion to oysters, shellfish, and delicate canapés.

name: Montelvini Prosecco Doc Rose Treviso Brut (Serenitatis Collection)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Prosecco Rosé from Treviso with crisp green apple, strawberry, and brioche—perfect aperitif sparkle.
description (EN Store): Montelvini's Prosecco Docg Rosé from the Treviso subregion delivers the hallmark elegance of Glera-based sparkling wine with a delicate pink hue. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, followed by subtle strawberry and floral notes. On the palate, fine bubbles carry bright acidity and a dry finish, with supporting brioche and mineral complexity. A versatile aperitif that pairs beautifully with oysters, soft cheeses, and light seafood, or as an accompaniment to Asian cuisine where its acidity cuts through spice and richness.

name: Montelvini Prosecco Doc Treviso Extra Dry (Serenitatis Collection)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Ideal for oysters, sushi, and Thai cuisine.
description (EN Store): Montelvini Prosecco Doc Treviso Extra Dry showcases the classic profile of Glera-based sparkling wine from the Veneto region. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle pear and stone fruit notes. Fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refined mouthfeel, while a touch of brioche complexity from the production process adds elegance without heaviness. The Extra Dry classification ensures a balanced sweetness level that complements rather than dominates. This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine suited to casual celebration and refined aperitif service.

name: Montelvini Asolo Prosecco Superiore Docg Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Glera sparkler from Asolo with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Montelvini's Asolo Prosecco Superiore DOCG Extra Dry showcases the classic elegance of Veneto's premier sparkling designation. Crafted from Glera grapes in the prestigious Asolo subregion, this wine delivers a bright, refreshing profile anchored by green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with supporting pear and subtle floral notes. The palate is crisp and lively, with fine bubbles and a dry finish complemented by delicate brioche undertones from the traditional production method. A versatile aperitif that pairs beautifully with oysters, shellfish, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Diamante  Prosecco Spumante Extra Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Prosecco with green apple, citrus, and brioche—elegant aperitif sparkle from Veneto.
description (EN Store): Diamante Prosecco Spumante Extra Dry is a vibrant sparkling wine from Breganze in Veneto, crafted from Glera grapes. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by subtle pear notes. On the palate, fine bubbles deliver a refreshing, dry character with supporting brioche complexity and a clean, mineral finish. This Extra Dry expression balances fruit-forward appeal with the elegance expected of quality Prosecco, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for light seafood and fresh cheeses.

name: Enjoy It Non Alcoholic Sparkling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp German sparkling with Riesling & Chardonnay; bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral edge. Alcohol-free refreshment.
description (EN Store): A non-alcoholic sparkling wine from Germany's Rheinhessen region, blending Riesling and Chardonnay to deliver a light, refreshing profile. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by delicate stone fruit and mineral notes that echo the region's slate-rich terroir. High acidity and fine carbonation create a crisp, clean finish ideal for aperitifs, seafood, or occasions requiring an alcohol-free alternative without compromise on complexity.

name: Perrier-Jouet  Blason Rose (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Champagne Blend with bright citrus, red berries, and toasted brioche—a refined aperitif for celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Perrier-Jouet Blason Rosé is a non-vintage Champagne blend of Chardonnay, Pinot Noir, and Pinot Meunier, crafted in the house's signature elegant style. The wine opens with vibrant citrus and red fruit aromatics—strawberry and raspberry—layered with brioche and toasted almond complexity from extended aging on the lees. A mineral, chalky backbone provides structure and refinement, while the wine's crisp acidity and fine bubble mousse deliver a clean, refreshing finish.

This is a versatile aperitif wine suited to celebrations, seafood, and delicate canapés, or as an elegant standalone sipper.

name: Zonin Prosecco Rose Brut
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Prosecco Rosé with strawberry, citrus, and brioche notes. Crisp, dry, and perfect for celebrations.
description (EN Store):
Zonin Prosecco Rosé Brut is a refined sparkling wine from Italy's Veneto region, crafted from Glera grapes. The wine displays a pale salmon hue with fine, persistent bubbles characteristic of quality Prosecco production.

On the nose, strawberry and citrus blossom aromas lead, complemented by subtle green apple and floral notes. The palate is crisp and dry, with bright acidity balancing delicate stone fruit and a hint of brioche complexity from the traditional méthode. The finish is clean and mineral-driven, making this an excellent aperitif or pairing wine for light cuisine.

name: Edenvale Pink Moscato (Non-Alcoholic Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Delicate non-alcoholic sparkling Muscat with floral and lychee notes, perfect for aperitifs and light desserts.
description (EN Store): Edenvale's Pink Moscato is a non-alcoholic sparkling wine crafted from Muscat grapes, delivering a light, refreshing profile ideal for those seeking alcohol-free celebration. The wine opens with pronounced floral and lychee aromatics, supported by stone fruit and citrus notes that define its primary character. Fine bubbles and a brioche-honey secondary layer add complexity and elegance, while crisp acidity ensures a clean, food-friendly finish. Versatile for aperitifs, cheese boards, and fruit-forward desserts.

name: Fratelli Ponte Spumante Brut 68
short_description (EN Store): Glera-based Piedmontese Brut from the Ponte family's Gorzano cellar — tank-method fizz built for varietal purity over prestige posturing.
description (EN Store):
When Giuseppe Ponte established the Gorzano cellar in San Damiano d'Asti in 1968, the family's philosophy was already fixed: prioritise varietal authenticity and accessible pricing without compromising appellation integrity. The Spumante Brut 68 — its name anchoring it to that founding year — is a 100% Glera produced under the Prosecco DOC framework, vinified in temperature-controlled stainless steel to lock in the grape's inherent white-peach and acacia aromatics rather than layering on secondary fermentation complexity.

San Damiano d'Asti sits between Asti and Alba on the chalky soils that define this corridor of Piedmont, a terrain that lends a flinty undercurrent to what might otherwise read as purely fruit-driven. The estate's deliberate avoidance of oak at any stage — consistent across their entire white and sparkling range — keeps the palate lean and the mousse precise. Fermentation in stainless steel preserves CO₂ integration at low temperatures, producing a fine, persistent bead without the bready autolytic character of traditional-method wines.

What distinguishes this expression within its category is its Piedmontese provenance: Glera grown and vinified in Asti Province rather than the Treviso heartland, giving the wine a structural dryness that sits at the firmer end of the Brut spectrum. As a non-vintage release, it is built for immediate consumption and consistent house style across harvests.

name: Masottina Virage Rose Brut Spumante
short_description (EN Store): Glera-Pinot Noir Charmat rosé from Masottina's gravity-fed Ogliano winery — no pumps, no oak, morainic-soil precision in a brut frame.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1946 by Epifanio Dal Bianco, Masottina built its reputation on a single engineering conviction: that Glera's aromatic delicacy is destroyed by pump-induced oxidation. Adriano Dal Bianco — second-generation CEO and head winemaker — designed the gravity-feed vinification system at Terre di Ogliano so that must travels from reception through soft pressing to fermentation tanks entirely by gravity, a protocol far more common in premium still-wine estates than in Charmat-method sparkling production.

The Virage Rosé Brut draws on Masottina's Veneto holdings, blending Glera with Pinot Noir per DOC Rosé regulations. Primary fermentation runs in stainless steel at controlled temperature (15–18°C), preserving the grape's native aromatic lift. Secondary fermentation (prise de mousse) is conducted in pressurised autoclaves at 13–14°C — the Charmat/Martinotti method — producing the fine, persistent perlage that is a house signature. No oak is used at any stage. Residual sugar sits within the Brut threshold (≤12 g/l), keeping the palate dry and focused.

What sets this expression apart within the DOC Rosé category is the gravity-feed infrastructure behind it: a Charmat producer engineering its must-handling with the oxidation-aversion of a Grand Cru still-wine cellar. The NV character follows the house's fruit-precise, low-sweetness profile — red berry, white peach, and the mineral edge that Masottina's morainic-soil sourcing consistently delivers. Drink on release through 2–3 years.

name: Masottina RDO Prosecco Extra Dry Millesimato DOCG
short_description (EN Store): 100% Glera from Ogliano's glacial-morainic soils, gravity-fed to autoclave — mineral density rare in Extra Dry Prosecco.
description (EN Store): When Adriano Dal Bianco engineered a gravity-feed vinification system at Terre di Ogliano — moving must from reception through soft pressing to fermentation tanks without a single pump — he applied a technique borrowed from premium still-wine estates to a Charmat-method house, a deliberate transgression of category convention. The R.D.O. designation anchors this Millesimato to Rive di Ogliano, the only sub-zone in the entire Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG with glacially-derived morainic soils: free-draining, mineral-rich deposits left by the Piave glacier, sitting within a natural amphitheatre where cold Alpine air descends nightly through the Fadalto valley, driving diurnal swings that sharpen aromatic intensity and preserve acidity. 100% Glera, white-vinified at 15–18°C in stainless steel, undergoes secondary fermentation in pressurised autoclaves at 13–14°C for approximately three months — triple the duration of Masottina's standard DOC Treviso line — followed by a further month of post-autoclave bottle rest. Residual sugar sits in the Extra Dry bracket (12–17 g/l), yet the morainic mineral backbone keeps the palate from reading sweet. The Millesimato single-vintage declaration is the special feature: where most Prosecco DOCG is blended across years, this bottling commits to a single harvest, making terroir expression — not consistency blending — the explicit goal. Drink within two to three years of harvest for peak aromatic lift and mousse finesse.

name: Masottina Calmaggiore Prosecco Brut DOC
short_description (EN Store): 100% Glera from Treviso plain calcareous-clay soils; gravity-fed Charmat at 13–14°C preserves aromatic precision over sweetness.
description (EN Store): When Adriano Dal Bianco engineered a gravity-fed must-transfer system at the Terre di Ogliano winery — eliminating every pump between reception, soft press, and fermentation tank — he applied a technique borrowed from premium still-wine estates to a Charmat-method house. That decision defines Masottina's house philosophy across every tier. The Calmaggiore Prosecco Brut DOC draws its fruit from the calcareous-clay soils of alluvial and morainic origin on the Treviso plain, where the flatter terrain and heavier subsoil yield a rounder, more immediately expressive Glera than the mineral-edged Rive di Ogliano crus higher in the hills. 100% Glera, white-vinified at 15–18°C in stainless steel, then refermented in pressurised autoclave at 13–14°C for approximately one month — the controlled cold environment locking in green apple, white pear, and acacia-blossom aromatics. Residual sugar sits at or below 12 g/l, keeping the finish dry and the perlage fine rather than frothy. What separates this entry-tier expression from category peers is the pump-free oxidation control applied even at DOC level — a production standard more commonly reserved for DOCG flagships. Drink young, within 12–18 months of disgorgement, to capture peak aromatic lift.

name: Masottina Contradagranda Prosecco Superiore Brut DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Three-subzone DOCG Brut blending Ogliano's glacial-morainic depth with Solighetto florals and Rolle acidity — gravity-fed, pump-free Charmat from a 1946 family estate.
description (EN Store): When Epifanio Dal Bianco founded Masottina in 1946, the hills above Ogliano were farmed by families who had never heard of a gravity-feed winery — that engineering decision came from his son Adriano, who designed the Terre di Ogliano facility so that must travels from reception through soft pressing to fermentation tanks without a single pump stroke, eliminating oxidation at every transfer point. ContradaGranda is the house's DOCG multi-subzone blend, drawing fruit from three distinct communes: Ogliano for complexity, Solighetto for floral lift, and Rolle for structural acidity. Ogliano's soils are glacial-morainic — the only such geology in the entire Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG — delivering free-draining fertility and a natural water reserve that sustains vines through dry spells, while nightly Alpine air descending through the Fadalto valley drives diurnal swings that sharpen aromatic intensity. Secondary fermentation runs approximately one month in pressurised stainless-steel autoclaves at 13–14°C, followed by roughly one month of post-autoclave bottle rest; no oak touches the wine at any stage. Residual sugar sits at or below 12 g/l Brut threshold, consistent with the house philosophy of fruit precision over sweetness. The special feature here is the deliberate cross-subzone architecture: where most DOCG Prosecco draws from a single hillside, ContradaGranda is engineered as a composite terroir, using Rolle's limestone-driven acidity as a structural counterweight to Ogliano's richer morainic fruit.

name: Masottina Virage Cuvee Brut Spumante
short_description (EN Store): Gravity-fed Glera from Ogliano's glacial-morainic soils — Adriano Dal Bianco's pump-free system preserves aromatic precision in this Brut Spumante.
description (EN Store): When Adriano Dal Bianco engineered a gravity-feed vinification system at Terre di Ogliano — moving must from reception through soft pressing to fermentation tanks without a single pump — he applied a technique more common to grand cru still-wine estates to a Charmat-method Prosecco house. That decision defines the Virage Cuvée Brut. The fruit originates from Masottina's Rive di Ogliano holdings in the southeast of the Conegliano Valdobbiadene DOCG, where glacially-derived morainic soils — the only such geology in the entire appellation — deliver free-draining, mineral-rich ground with a natural water reserve. Cold Alpine air descending nightly through the Fadalto valley creates diurnal swings that sharpen aromatic intensity in the 100% Glera fruit. Primary fermentation proceeds in stainless steel at 15–18°C; secondary prise de mousse is conducted in pressurised autoclaves at 13–14°C, preserving the fine, persistent perlage the house prizes. Residual sugar is held at or below 12 g/l, sitting firmly in Brut territory and resisting the category's drift toward sweetness. What separates this from the Prosecco mainstream is the pump-free oxidation control — a structural commitment that keeps the Glera's aromatic profile intact from vine to bottle. As a non-vintage blend, it is built for freshness and should be opened within 1–2 years of release.

name: Rive della Chiesa Spumante Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Muscat sparkler from Veneto with lychee, peach, and honeyed florals—elegant aperitif with fine bubbles.
description (EN Store): Rive della Chiesa's Spumante Moscato is a delicate, aromatic sparkling wine crafted from Muscat grapes in Italy's Veneto region. The wine opens with pronounced lychee and peach aromatics, complemented by honeyed floral notes that define its primary character. On the palate, fine bubbles carry bright acidity and subtle brioche complexity, creating an elegant, refreshing profile ideal for aperitif service or pairing with light appetizers and fresh cheeses.

name: Campo Viejo Cava Brut (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish Cava with green apple, citrus, and brioche notes. Elegant aperitif with fine bubbles and dry finish.
description (EN Store):
Campo Viejo Cava Brut is a dry sparkling wine from Spain's Rioja region, crafted from the classic Cava blend of Macabeo, Xarel·lo, and Parellada grapes. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus aromas, followed by subtle brioche and toast complexity from traditional bottle aging. On the palate, crisp acidity and fine, persistent bubbles deliver a refreshing, elegant character with a clean, mineral-edged finish.

This is an approachable, food-friendly sparkling wine ideal for celebrations or casual entertaining. Its bright fruit profile and dry style make it versatile with seafood, cheese, and light appetizers.

name: Jacob's Creek Reserve Sparkling Rose (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Australian sparkling rosé with bright raspberry and red fruit, crisp acidity, and elegant minerality—perfect for celebrations and seafood.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Reserve Sparkling Rosé is a lively, fruit-forward sparkling wine from Australia's Barossa Valley, crafted from Pinot Noir and Chardonnay. On the nose and palate, bright raspberry and red fruit dominate, supported by delicate floral and brioche notes from the traditional méthode. The wine's high acidity and light body deliver a refreshing, crisp finish with subtle minerality, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, fresh seafood, and soft cheeses. Its approachable character and vibrant fruit profile suit warm-weather entertaining and casual celebrations.

name: Louis Roederer  Blanc de Blancs 2017
short_description (EN Store): 100% Cramant & Avize Grand Cru Chardonnay, 5 years on lees — parcel-by-parcel vinification from Champagne's largest biodynamic estate.
description (EN Store): Under Chef de Cave Jean-Baptiste Lécaillon, Louis Roederer made a deliberate structural choice that separates this Blanc de Blancs from virtually every peer at this level: every parcel of fruit is vinified individually in small stainless-steel tanks or oak vats sized to just one or two hectares, preserving the distinct chalk signature of each plot before blending. The fruit comes exclusively from Cramant and Avize — two Grand Cru villages on the Côte des Blancs — where Chardonnay roots into limestone mid-slopes with thin clay-loam topsoil over pure chalk bedrock, a geology that drives the wine's taut, saline mineral tension rather than any richness of soil. Malolactic fermentation is partially blocked to retain structural acidity, and the wine rests on lees for a minimum of five years — a full year longer than Roederer's Brut Vintage — followed by six months post-disgorgement, building autolytic complexity without sacrificing the chalk-driven linearity that defines these two communes. What makes this expression genuinely unusual is that 100% of the fruit is estate-grown from Roederer's own Grand Cru holdings, farmed under organic and biodynamic protocols — a grower-level terroir commitment inside a large Champagne maison. The 2017 vintage in Champagne delivered ripe, generous Chardonnay with good natural acidity after a frost-reduced crop; the extended lees aging has integrated that ripeness into a wine with considerable tension and a drinking window extending well into the late 2020s.

name: Masottina Viaventi Prosecco Rose Brut Millesimato
short_description (EN Store): Glera + Pinot Noir Charmat rosé from Ogliano's glacial-morainic soils, gravity-fed vinification, ~1-month autoclave on selected yeasts.
description (EN Store): When Adriano Dal Bianco engineered a gravity-fed must-transfer system at Terre di Ogliano — eliminating every pump between reception, pressing, and fermentation — he applied a technique borrowed from premium still-wine estates to a Charmat-method sparkling house. That decision defines the Viaventi Rosé's aromatic precision. Glera and Pinot Noir are vinified off-skins at 15–18°C in stainless steel, with secondary fermentation conducted in pressurised autoclaves at 13–14°C for approximately one month — no oak at any stage. The fruit originates from Masottina's estate holdings across the Treviso DOC zone, where calcareous-clay soils of alluvial and morainic origin on the Piave plain deliver a softer textural baseline than the mineral-dense Rive di Ogliano crus. Residual sugar sits within the Brut threshold (≤12 g/l), keeping the Pinot Noir's red-berry character dry and precise rather than confected. What separates this from most Prosecco Rosé DOC is the pump-free oxidation control upstream: the same gravity system that protects the delicate Glera aromatics in the DOCG flagship is applied here, producing a mousse of notably fine, persistent perlage. As a Millesimato, the wine is vintage-dated — an unusual commitment for a DOC Rosé tier — anchoring the fruit character to a single harvest rather than blending across years.

name: De Venoge Gift Box 3 Princes (Blanc De Blancs+ Rose + Blanc De Noir)
short_description (EN Store): Three Princes expressions — 100% Chardonnay, 100% Pinot Noir, and Rosé — each aged 48 months on chalk-cellar lees, dosage ~6 g/L.
description (EN Store):
When Henri-Marc de Venoge created the first illustrated, named Champagne label in 1838 — a year after founding the house — he was signalling that provenance and identity mattered as much as the wine itself. The Princes gift set distils that philosophy into three distinct expressions, each built from a single variety or style, all sharing the same 48-month lees aging in chalk cellars beneath the UNESCO-listed Avenue de Champagne in Épernay.

The Princes Blanc de Blancs draws exclusively on Grand and Premier Cru Chardonnay from Mesnil-sur-Oger and Trépail — chalk-dominant soils that drive vertical, saline minerality and restrained fruit tension. The Princes Blanc de Noirs is built from 100% Pinot Noir sourced across Montagne de Reims Premiers and Grands Crus, with a parcel contribution from Les Riceys in the Aube — an unusual cross-sub-regional blend that adds darker-fruited density to the structure. The Princes Rosé completes the trio. All three are vinified exclusively from first-press cuvée juice (taille excluded), with dosage held at approximately 6 g/L — low enough to keep the chalk-soil mineral signature unobscured. The 48-month lees contact, four months beyond the NV Cordon Bleu minimum, is the technical differentiator within the house's own range, building autolytic complexity without sacrificing the freshness that defines the Princes tier.

name: De Venoge Gift Box Louis XV  (1 Bottle Louis XV + 2 Glasses)
short_description (EN Store): Grand Cru 50/50 Chardonnay–Pinot Noir, no Meunier, low 7 g/L dosage — De Venoge's prestige cuvée in its iconic royal-court decanter bottle.
description (EN Store):
When Henri-Marc de Venoge created the first illustrated, named label in Champagne history in 1838, he was already signalling that this house would operate by different rules. The Louis XV cuvée carries that transgressive instinct forward: no Pinot Meunier appears in the blend — a deliberate departure from the three-variety orthodoxy that defines most prestige cuvées. Instead, the wine is built entirely on 50% Grand Cru Chardonnay and 50% Grand Cru Pinot Noir, sourced from the chalk-dominated subsoils of Mesnil-sur-Oger, Trépail, and Montagne de Reims — terroirs where thin topsoil forces deep root penetration into pure chalk, driving the mineral tension that anchors the wine's structure.

Cellar master Isabelle Tellier vinifies exclusively from first-press cuvée juice, excluding all taille, then ages the wine on lees for an extended period in De Venoge's chalk cellars beneath the UNESCO-listed Avenue de Champagne mansion. Dosage is held at 7 g/L — low enough to keep the wine's Grand Cru architecture exposed. The 2012 vintage, the current release, delivers tangerine, clementine, and cookie-dough complexity with a full-bodied density that reflects the ripeness of the harvest. The decanter-shaped bottle — a direct reference to crystal flasks used by European royal courts — is not decorative theatre but a statement of the house's self-positioning since 1837. Drink 2024–2030.

name: Lungarotti  Pinot Grigio IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Umbrian Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Lungarotti's Pinot Grigio from Torgiano, Umbria, exemplifies the refreshing character of Italy's most celebrated white varietal. This medium-bodied expression balances vibrant acidity with delicate stone fruit and citrus aromatics, delivering a clean, mineral-driven palate that invites food pairing or standalone enjoyment. The wine's bright profile and elegant finish make it an ideal aperitif and a versatile table wine for lighter cuisine.

name: Sunnycliff  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Sunnycliff Chardonnay: Murray Darling elegance with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): Sunnycliff Chardonnay from Australia's Murray Darling region delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The primary character showcases green apple and citrus brightness, supported by ripe stone fruit notes that give the wine approachability and fruit-forward appeal. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut complexity, while underlying minerality adds refinement and food-pairing versatility. This is a food-friendly Chardonnay suited to cream-based dishes, roasted poultry, and shellfish preparations.

name: Zonin  "Classico" Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Veneto Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Zonin's Classico Pinot Grigio from Veneto delivers the varietal's signature freshness and mineral-driven character. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, with subtle stone fruit undertones that emerge on the palate. Medium body and bracing acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or versatile food companion, particularly suited to seafood, light pasta, and fresh preparations. A reliable, well-balanced expression of northern Italian white wine tradition.

name: Zonin  "Ventiterre" Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Veneto Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Zonin's Ventiterre Pinot Grigio exemplifies the refreshing, food-friendly character of northeastern Italian white wine. Grown in the Veneto region, this single-varietal expression delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and subtle floral notes. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with crisp acidity that makes it an ideal aperitif or seafood pairing. Medium body and low tannin ensure approachability across casual and formal settings.

name: Banrock Station  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Australian Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Banrock Station Chardonnay from Australia's Riverland region delivers a balanced, food-friendly white wine with medium-full body and moderate acidity. The primary character showcases green apple and citrus brightness, complemented by stone fruit notes of peach and pear. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and subtle toasted hazelnut complexity, while a mineral undertone adds freshness. This approachable style suits casual dining and everyday entertaining, pairing well with roasted poultry, seafood preparations, and cream-based pasta dishes.

name: Hardys  Varietal Range Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Approachable McLaren Vale Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for everyday dining.
description (EN Store): Hardys' Varietal Range Chardonnay from McLaren Vale delivers a well-balanced expression of Australia's signature white wine style. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus aromatics, followed by ripe stone fruit and peach on the palate. Subtle oak aging contributes vanilla and toasted hazelnut notes, while a clean minerality provides structure and freshness. Medium-full body and moderate acidity make this wine approachable yet food-friendly, ideal for a range of occasions from casual weeknight dinners to more formal entertaining.

name: Hardys  Stamp Chardonnay/Semillon
short_description (EN Store): Chardonnay-Sémillon blend from McLaren Vale with citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): A medium-full bodied white blend from Australia's McLaren Vale, combining Chardonnay's structure with Sémillon's textural elegance. The palate opens with green apple and citrus zest, moving into ripe stone fruit and peach notes. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and subtle toast, while underlying minerality provides freshness and complexity. Balanced acidity and clean finish make this a food-friendly expression suitable for a range of occasions.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Vint Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Oakville Chardonnay with ripe tropical fruit, vanilla oak, and creamy butter—a full-bodied classic for seafood and rich poultry.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi's Private Selection Chardonnay from Oakville showcases the hallmark richness of Napa Valley fruit. Tropical notes and green apple lead the palate, supported by generous vanilla and butter from oak aging. The full body and medium acidity make this wine a versatile partner for cream-based dishes, shellfish, and roasted poultry. A reliable, well-crafted expression of California Chardonnay tradition.

name: Robert Mondavi   Private Selection Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Napa Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus and herbaceous character; ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Private Selection Sauvignon Blanc from Oakville, Napa Valley, delivers the classic profile of the varietal: bright citrus zest and herbaceous notes dominate the palate, supported by green apple and mineral undertones. The high acidity and light body make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, particularly with oysters, grilled fish, and fresh goat cheese. A versatile aperitif and dining companion for warm-weather entertaining.

name: Robert Mondavi   Woodbridge Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Chardonnay with ripe apple, vanilla oak, and tropical fruit—ideal for cream sauces and roasted poultry.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Woodbridge Chardonnay is a fruit-forward California white wine showcasing the ripeness and oak-aging tradition of Napa Valley winemaking. The palate opens with ripe apple and tropical fruit notes, supported by creamy vanilla oak and buttery texture characteristic of New World Chardonnay. Stone fruit and hazelnut undertones add complexity, while medium acidity and medium-full body make this wine versatile at the table. A reliable choice for cream-based pasta, roasted chicken, and soft cheeses.

name: Montes  Alpha Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Casablanca Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived vanilla—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Montes Alpha Chardonnay from Chile's Casablanca Valley showcases the region's cool-climate signature. Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity, this wine opens with green apple and citrus aromatics, evolving to ripe stone fruit on the palate. Oak aging contributes subtle vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut notes, while underlying minerality and restrained spice add complexity. A versatile expression suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based preparations.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Reserva Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—refined and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Reserva Chardonnay from Puente Alto in Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium body and vibrant acidity. The primary character centers on ripe stone fruit and citrus, with supporting notes of green apple that provide freshness and definition. Winemaking influence introduces subtle butter and vanilla oak, balanced by hazelnut undertones, while mineral complexity adds textural depth and elegance. This wine bridges fruit-forward approachability with restrained oak integration, making it versatile across seafood, poultry, and cream-based preparations.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mapu Sauvignon Blanc (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Mapu Sauvignon Blanc from Puente Alto in Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a bright, refreshing white wine built on medium body and medium-high acidity. The primary profile showcases dominant citrus zest and green apple with supporting herbaceous and tropical fruit character, creating an inviting aromatic presence. Secondary minerality and stone fruit notes add subtle complexity without overshadowing the wine's core freshness. Ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and spicy Asian cuisine, this screw-cap bottling offers reliable quality and immediate drinkability at an accessible price point.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Curicó Valley Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's Gato Negro Chardonnay from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. Green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by subtle oak-derived butter and vanilla notes. Stone fruit and hazelnut complexity emerge on the palate, finishing with a clean, mineral edge. A versatile expression suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based preparations.

name: San Pedro  Gato Negro Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's Gato Negro Sauvignon Blanc from Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with bright acidity and aromatic intensity. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and herbaceous character, followed by green apple and tropical fruit notes that evolve on the palate. A mineral backbone and clean finish make this an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for oysters, grilled fish, and fresh cheese.

name: Concha Y Toro  Reservado Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Maipo Valley Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Reservado Chardonnay from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, followed by ripe stone fruit and peach on the palate. Winemaking influence appears as buttery oak and vanilla notes that add texture and complexity without overwhelming the fruit. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine versatile at the table—equally at home with grilled fish, creamy pasta, or roasted poultry.

name: Concha Y Toro  Frontera Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Chardonnay with citrus zest, stone fruit, and subtle oak—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Frontera Chardonnay from Chile's Maipo Valley delivers a clean, approachable expression of the classic white varietal. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by ripe stone fruit and a whisper of tropical character. On the palate, subtle butter and vanilla oak add texture and depth without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. Medium body and crisp finish make this an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Conte Placido  Placido Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Veneto Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Conte Placido's Pinot Grigio from Veneto delivers the classic profile of Italy's most celebrated white varietal. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, with subtle stone fruit and mineral undertones. The palate is clean and refreshing, with medium body and vibrant acidity that lingers on the finish. An approachable, food-friendly white ideal for warm-weather entertaining and coastal cuisine.

name: Carpineto  Dogajolo, Chardonnay, Grechetto, Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan white blend of Chardonnay, Grechetto & Sauvignon Blanc—crisp, mineral-driven, with stone fruit and oak complexity.
description (EN Store): A refined Tuscan white blend uniting three complementary varieties: Chardonnay brings structure and oak-aged richness, Grechetto contributes minerality and herbaceous character, and Sauvignon Blanc adds bright citrus and green apple freshness. The result is a medium-full bodied wine with balanced acidity, offering layers of stone fruit, vanilla oak, and subtle tropical notes. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, creamy pasta, and fresh cheeses.

name: Castello Banfi  Le Rime IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio–Chardonnay blend from Montalcino with citrus, green apple, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Castello Banfi's Le Rime is a refreshing white blend pairing Pinot Grigio's bright acidity and citrus character with Chardonnay's stone-fruit depth and subtle oak influence. Sourced from Montalcino in Tuscany, this medium-bodied wine delivers vibrant green apple and citrus zest on the nose, followed by pear and minerality on the palate. The medium-high acidity and clean finish make it an ideal aperitif or food-friendly companion to seafood, light pasta, and fresh cheeses.

name: Wente Vineyards  Wente Morning Fog Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Bright California Chardonnay with citrus and stone fruit; crisp acidity and subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): Wente Vineyards' Morning Fog Chardonnay is a California classic that balances fruit-forward character with elegant restraint. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, followed by ripe peach and subtle minerality on the palate. Gentle oak aging contributes soft butter and vanilla notes without overwhelming the wine's natural freshness. Medium body and crisp acidity make this a versatile food wine, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Castello Banfi  San Angelo Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Tuscan Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Castello Banfi's San Angelo Pinot Grigio is a classic expression of Italy's most approachable white varietal. Grown in Montalcino's continental climate, this wine delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and subtle floral notes on the palate. Medium body and vibrant acidity make it an ideal aperitif or seafood pairing, offering excellent refreshment without demanding food.

name: Garofoli  PODIUM, Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi Classico
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Verdicchio from Marche: green apple, citrus, and mineral freshness. Elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Garofoli's PODIUM Verdicchio dei Castelli di Jesi Classico is a single-varietal expression of one of Italy's most distinctive white grapes. Verdicchio thrives in the Castelli di Jesi zone of Marche, where limestone-rich soils impart mineral precision and bright acidity.

On the nose and palate, expect green apple, citrus zest, and stone fruit as the dominant character—the hallmark of well-made Verdicchio. A subtle herbaceous undertone and almond nuttiness emerge in the mid-palate, while mineral salinity and lemon notes provide structure and length. The wine's refreshing acidity and medium body make it supremely food-friendly and ideal as an aperitif.

Best served chilled with grilled fish, shellfish, fresh salads, or light pasta dishes.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero del Diablo Reserva Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Reserva Chardonnay from Chile's Cachapoal Valley delivers a refined expression of the varietal. Stone fruit and citrus dominate the nose, supported by subtle oak-derived butter and vanilla notes. The palate is medium-bodied with bright acidity and a clean, mineral finish. This wine balances approachability with complexity, making it an excellent choice for seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero del Diablo Reserva Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—crisp and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Casillero del Diablo Reserva Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Cachapoal Valley delivers a refreshing white wine built on bright citrus zest and green apple aromatics. The primary profile emphasizes herbaceous and tropical fruit character, supported by mineral undertones and subtle stone fruit complexity. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a clean, dry finish ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Gavi Le Marne DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Cortese from Gavi DOCG: citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision. Elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Michele Chiarlo's Gavi Le Marne DOCG is a benchmark expression of Cortese from Piedmont's premier white-wine zone. This single-varietal white delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with underlying stone-fruit minerality and herbaceous nuance. The palate is clean and refreshing, driven by medium-high acidity and a dry, precise finish that lingers with almond and wet-stone complexity.

A versatile aperitif and seafood wine, it excels alongside grilled fish, oysters, and shellfish, while its crisp structure complements Asian cuisine and light vegetable dishes.

name: Domaine Laroche  Chablis Les Chanoines AOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chablis from Domaine Laroche: pure Chardonnay with mineral precision and citrus freshness.
description (EN Store): Domaine Laroche's Chablis Les Chanoines is a benchmark expression of unoaked Chardonnay from Burgundy's northernmost white-wine region. This 100% Chardonnay delivers the mineral-driven character and bright acidity that define classic Chablis: green apple, citrus zest, and wet-stone minerality dominate the palate, with subtle herbaceous and floral undertones. Medium body and crisp finish make it an ideal aperitif and a natural partner for oysters, raw seafood, and delicate fish preparations.

name: Domaine Laroche  Chablis Domaine Saint Martin AOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chablis from Domaine Laroche: pure Chardonnay with mineral precision and citrus freshness.
description (EN Store): Domaine Laroche's Chablis Domaine Saint Martin AOC is a classic expression of unoaked Chardonnay from Burgundy's northernmost white wine region. The wine showcases the mineral-driven character and bright acidity that define Chablis terroir, with green apple, citrus zest, and wet stone notes that reflect the region's limestone-rich soils. A refreshing, food-friendly white with herbaceous undertones and a saline finish.

name: Batasiolo  Gavi Del Comune Di Gavi Granee
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Cortese from Gavi with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes—a refreshing Italian white for seafood and light fare.
description (EN Store): Batasiolo's Gavi del Comune di Gavi Granee is a single-varietal expression of Cortese, the signature white grape of Piedmont's Gavi zone. This wine delivers a refreshing profile anchored by bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and almond undertones. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with herbaceous accents and a clean, lingering finish. Medium body and medium-high acidity make it versatile at table and equally compelling as an aperitif.

name: Pio Cesare  Gavi white DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Cortese from Gavi: citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Pio Cesare's Gavi DOCG is a benchmark expression of Cortese from Piedmont's Gavi subregion. The 2022 vintage delivers a clean, mineral-driven profile with prominent citrus zest and green apple on the nose, followed by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous undertones. Medium body and bracing acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for delicate seafood, raw preparations, and fresh vegetable dishes. The wine's crisp finish and elegant restraint reflect the terroir-focused winemaking tradition of the region.

name: Santa Helena  Varietal Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Santa Helena's Varietal Chardonnay from Chile's Colchagua Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, followed by ripe peach and stone fruit on the palate. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut notes, while a mineral backbone provides freshness and structure. This is an approachable, well-crafted Chardonnay suited to both casual enjoyment and food pairing.

name: Santa Helena  Varietal Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and crisp minerality—ideal for seafood and Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Santa Helena's Sauvignon Blanc from Colchagua Valley delivers a fresh, food-friendly white wine with pronounced citrus and herbaceous character. The wine opens with bright lime and green apple aromatics, supported by subtle tropical fruit and mineral notes that reflect the region's terroir. High acidity and light body make this an excellent aperitif and versatile pairing partner for seafood, salads, and spiced Asian dishes.

name: Vigneti Del Vulture  Pippoli Bianco Basilicata IGT
short_description (EN Store): Greco and Fiano blend from Vulture: bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness with vibrant acidity.
description (EN Store): Vigneti Del Vulture's Pippoli Bianco is a Greco and Fiano blend from the volcanic Vulture region of Basilicata, southern Italy. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green-apple notes, supported by subtle stone-fruit character and a distinctive mineral backbone. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food wine, particularly suited to seafood, light Asian cuisine, and fresh vegetable preparations. The herbaceous undertones suggest careful handling of the fruit, allowing the natural expression of these southern Italian varieties to shine.

name: Vigneti Di Zabu  Chiantari Inzolia Chardonnay Sicilia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian blend of Inzolia & Chardonnay: bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness with subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): A distinctive Sicilian white blend marrying the indigenous Inzolia grape with Chardonnay, this wine delivers a refreshing profile anchored by bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose. Stone fruit and tropical notes emerge on the palate, supported by a mineral backbone and herbaceous undertones characteristic of Sicily's volcanic terroir. Subtle oak and butter notes from winemaking add textural depth without overwhelming the wine's natural freshness. Medium body and crisp acidity make this an exceptionally food-friendly expression, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Little Yering  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Yarra Valley cool-climate Chardonnay, barrel-fermented in French puncheons with wild yeast — entry-tier precision from Australia's oldest vineyard site.
description (EN Store): Yering Station's home estate dates to 1838, making it Victoria's oldest vineyard site — yet the Rathbone family's 1996 relaunch imposed a discipline unusual at any price point: every parcel is assessed blind at an annual allocation tasting before tier assignment, meaning Little Yering earns its designation on merit each vintage rather than by formula. That same philosophy governs the winemaking: wild yeast fermentation and barrel fermentation in French puncheons are applied even at this entry level, a commitment to texture and site expression that most producers reserve for premium tiers. The Yarra Valley's clay-loam soils — the documented source of Chardonnay richness across the estate's five sites — combined with cool maritime influence at elevations ranging to 1,250 m, produce fruit with natural tension and restrained sugar accumulation. Screwcap closure, in place for over two decades, preserves that primary fruit integrity through the drinking window. What sets Little Yering apart within its category is the application of Reserve-level fermentation technique — wild yeast, puncheon barrel work — at a sub-฿1,000 price point, a direct consequence of the estate's blind-tasting allocation system rather than a marketing decision. Drink now through 2–3 years for peak primary fruit expression.

name: Alois Lageder  Muller Thurgao Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Alto Adige Müller-Thurgau with vibrant citrus, green apple, and mineral freshness—an elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Alois Lageder's Müller-Thurgau from the Adige Valley showcases the varietal's signature brightness and aromatic character. Dominant green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose and palate, supported by delicate stone fruit and herbaceous undertones. The wine's medium body and pronounced acidity deliver a crisp, refreshing finish with subtle minerality, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for light seafood, fresh salads, and Asian cuisine.

name: Alois Lageder  Gewurztraminer Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Alto Adige Gewürztraminer with lychee, rose, and spice—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Alois Lageder's Gewürztraminer from the Adige Valley showcases the varietal's signature aromatic profile: prominent lychee and rose blossom on the nose, with supporting notes of peach and honeyed spice. Medium-bodied with bright medium-high acidity, the wine balances richness with refreshing minerality and citrus zest on the finish. A classic expression of Alto Adige's cool-climate white wine tradition, offering versatility across appetizers, Asian cuisine, and delicate cheese courses.

name: Allegrini  Soave Classico DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Garganega-Chardonnay blend from Soave Classico; bright citrus, green apple, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store):
Allegrini's Soave Classico is a refined expression of the Garganega grape, blended with 20% Chardonnay to add complexity and structure. Hailing from the historic Soave region of Veneto, this wine delivers a vibrant, refreshing profile anchored by bright citrus zest and crisp green apple on the nose. The palate reveals delicate stone-fruit notes and a subtle herbaceous character, finishing with mineral precision and a lingering lemon acidity that defines the Classico style.

Medium-bodied and elegant, this wine showcases the purity and terroir-driven character that has made Allegrini a benchmark producer in northeastern Italy. Its versatility makes it an ideal aperitif or food companion for seafood, light pasta, and fresh cheeses.

name: Corte Giara by Allegrini  Pinot Grigio Delle Venezie IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Veneto with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Corte Giara by Allegrini represents the benchmark expression of Pinot Grigio from the Veneto region. This single-varietal white delivers a clean, refreshing profile anchored by vibrant citrus zest, green apple, and subtle stone fruit. The palate is bright and mineral-driven, with a crisp finish that invites another sip. A versatile aperitif or ideal pairing for grilled fish, shellfish, and light Mediterranean fare.

name: Bodegas Salentein  Portillo Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Mendoza Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Salentein's Portillo Sauvignon Blanc from Mendoza showcases the varietal's signature brightness and aromatic intensity. Light-bodied with pronounced high acidity, this wine delivers fresh citrus zest, green apple, and herbaceous character on the nose and palate. Tropical undertones and subtle minerality add complexity, making it an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for oysters, grilled fish, and fresh salads. The wine's crisp profile and clean finish reflect the cool-climate influence of Mendoza's high-altitude vineyards.

name: Errazuriz  Estate Reserva Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Errazuriz Estate Reserva Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Aconcagua Valley delivers a crisp, expressive white wine built on high acidity and light body. The primary character showcases bright citrus zest and herbaceous intensity, with supporting green apple and tropical fruit notes that evolve on the palate. Mineral undertones and grassy nuance add complexity and food-friendliness. A versatile aperitif and seafood companion.

name: Torres  Vina Esmeralda
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Catalan blend of Muscat and Gewürztraminer; floral, stone fruit, and tropical notes with refreshing acidity.
description (EN Store): Torres Viña Esmeralda is a distinctive white blend from Catalunya that marries the exotic floral character of Gewürztraminer (15%) with the honeyed stone-fruit richness of Muscat of Alexandria (85%). The wine opens with pronounced aromatics—jasmine and rose—followed by peach, lychee, and citrus zest on the palate. Medium body and brisk acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food wine, particularly suited to aromatic Asian cuisines, shellfish, and fresh cheese.

name: Lindeman's  Bin 95 Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Hunter Valley Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral edge.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Bin 95 Sauvignon Blanc from Australia's Hunter Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with bright citrus zest and herbaceous character. The wine's high acidity and light body make it an ideal aperitif and seafood companion, with tropical fruit undertones and a clean, mineral finish that reflects the region's terroir.

name: Belleville  Chardonnay Viognier (Fruit wine blend)
short_description (EN Store): Chardonnay-Viognier blend from Rhône Valley with crisp apple, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A Rhône Valley fruit wine blend pairing Chardonnay's structure with Viognier's aromatic generosity. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit undertones. Oak aging introduces vanilla and butter complexity, while medium acidity and medium-full body create a wine suited to both seafood and richer poultry dishes. Minerality emerges on the finish, adding refinement.

name: Louis Latour  Chablis 1er Cru Montmains
short_description (EN Store): Kimmeridgian-clay Montmains Premier Cru from Louis Latour's négociant range — Chablis' most structured left-bank lieu-dit, 2021 vintage.
description (EN Store):
Louis Latour's roots in Burgundy stretch to 1731, but the house's négociant reach extends north to Chablis, where the Montmains Premier Cru occupies the left bank of the Serein river — a lieu-dit prized for its dense Kimmeridgian clay-limestone soils packed with fossilised oyster shells (exogyra virgula), which drive a saline, chalky tension that separates Montmains from the flintier, more open-knit Fourchaume on the opposite bank. The cool continental microclimate and north-northwest-facing slopes slow ripening, preserving the high natural acidity that defines the appellation's structural identity.

As a négociant-éleveur cuvée, Louis Latour sources from established Montmains growers and applies house élevage: 100% Chardonnay, fermented and aged in a combination of stainless steel and neutral oak to preserve primary fruit and mineral precision rather than impose the toasty, new-oak signature reserved for the house's Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru. Malolactic fermentation proceeds to soften the appellation's characteristically aggressive acidity without erasing it.

What distinguishes this expression within the Latour range is its deliberate restraint — where the house's Côte de Beaune whites receive 100% new French oak with no lees-stirring, Montmains is handled to let Chablis terroir speak rather than house style dominate. The 2021 vintage in Chablis delivered naturally high acidity and taut, concentrated fruit after a frost-reduced crop, producing wines of notable tension and longevity; drink 2024–2030.

name: Donnafugata  Anthilia Sicilia
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Grillo with bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—a crisp, food-friendly white.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Anthilia is a single-varietal Grillo from Sicily's sun-drenched vineyards, delivering a refreshing white wine built on vibrant citrus zest and stone fruit aromatics. The palate shows green apple and subtle tropical notes, underpinned by a distinctive minerality and herbaceous character that speaks to the island's volcanic terroir. Medium body and bracing acidity make this an ideal aperitif and versatile seafood companion—equally at home with grilled fish, raw oysters, or spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Frescobaldi  Pomino Bianco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan white with crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral elegance—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Frescobaldi's Pomino Bianco DOC is a refined Tuscan white wine that balances bright citrus and stone fruit character with subtle herbaceous and mineral undertones. Medium-bodied with vibrant acidity, it delivers a clean, refreshing palate ideal for seafood, light pasta, and aperitif occasions. The wine's complexity and food-pairing versatility reflect the Pomino terroir's cool-climate influence in the Chianti Classico region.

name: Frescobaldi  Remole Bianco Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan white with bright citrus and stone fruit; crisp, mineral-driven, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Frescobaldi's Remole Bianco is a Tuscan white wine that delivers refreshing citrus and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and herbaceous notes. Medium-bodied with pronounced acidity, it shows mineral tension on the palate and a clean, dry finish. A versatile food wine suited to seafood, fresh salads, and bright Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Altivo  Classic Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Altivo Classic Chardonnay from Mendoza showcases the varietal's signature balance of fresh acidity and ripe fruit character. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and peach on the palate. Subtle oak aging introduces butter and vanilla notes without overwhelming the wine's natural elegance. A touch of minerality and hazelnut in the finish adds complexity and length. Medium body and crisp acidity make this a versatile pairing wine for seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Inspired Company  Unoaked Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Unoaked Australian Chardonnay with bright citrus and stone fruit; crisp, refreshing, food-friendly.
description (EN Store): An unoaked Chardonnay from South Eastern Australia, this wine emphasises the varietal's natural acidity and fruit-forward character. Bright citrus and green apple dominate the palate, complemented by subtle stone fruit and mineral notes. The absence of oak allows the wine's refreshing quality to shine, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for lighter cuisine—seafood, salads, and delicate preparations.

name: Piccini  Pinot Grigio IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Trentino-Alto Adige with bright citrus and stone fruit—refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): A classic expression of Pinot Grigio from Italy's northern Trentino-Alto Adige region, this wine delivers the varietal's signature freshness and mineral clarity. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, with supporting stone fruit and pear notes that unfold on the palate. Medium body and brisk acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for grilled fish, oysters, and light pasta dishes. The crisp, clean finish reflects the cool-climate terroir of its Alpine origin.

name: Rawson's Retreat  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—a refreshing, food-friendly white.
description (EN Store): Rawson's Retreat Chardonnay from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright stone fruit and citrus on the nose, with a medium body and crisp acidity that keeps the palate lively. Subtle oak and butter notes from winemaking add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. This is an approachable, versatile white wine suited to casual dining and fine-dining occasions alike.

name: Louis Latour  Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): 10.5 ha atop the Hill of Corton; limestone summit Chardonnay fermented in 100% new in-house-made French oak with zero lees-stirring — the appellation Louis Latour's ancestor created.
description (EN Store):
It was a 5th-generation Louis Latour who, after phylloxera devastated the limestone summit of Corton in the 1890s, made the deliberate decision to replant with Chardonnay rather than restore Aligoté — an act of transgression that effectively authored the Corton-Charlemagne AOC as it exists today. The house now holds 10.5 hectares across five named lieux-dits: Le Charlemagne, En Charlemagne, Languettes, Pougets, and Renardes, all on the south and south-east-facing limestone-rich soils at the hill's crown, where thin topsoil over fractured limestone forces deep root penetration and concentrates mineral tension in the must.

Grapes are crushed before pressing, fermentation begins in stainless steel without cold settling, then the must is transferred hot into 228-litre pièces of 100% new French oak — staves air-dried 24–36 months and toasted light-long (45 minutes) at the house's own cooperage — to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation. Critically, no lees-stirring is practised, a deliberate choice that preserves primary limestone minerality rather than building autolytic creaminess. The wine is racked to stainless steel in May–June, settled, and bottled December–February with bentonite fining only.

The result is structurally unlike most Côte de Beaune whites: the full new-oak regime without bâtonnage produces a wine that is simultaneously toasty and taut rather than broad and buttery. The 2018 vintage — named Wine Spectator Top 10 Wine of 2021 and the fifth consecutive fine white Burgundy harvest — delivers outstanding volume-to-freshness balance, approachable young yet built for the medium term.

name: Louis Latour  Pouilly-Fuisse 1er Cru
short_description (EN Store): Pouilly-Fuissé 1er Cru from Louis Latour's négociant range — Chardonnay fermented in French oak, no lees-stirring, 2022 vintage.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1797 by Jean Latour — whose family had tended vines since 1731 — Maison Louis Latour built its white Burgundy identity on a deliberately opulent, oak-driven philosophy that runs counter to the lees-stirring orthodoxy of the Côte de Beaune. That same house signature extends into this Pouilly-Fuissé Premier Cru: 100% Chardonnay, fermented in 228-litre French oak barrels (made at the in-house Louis Latour cooperage from air-dried French oak) with no lees-stirring, allowing the wine to build texture through slow oxidative contact rather than reductive autolysis. Pouilly-Fuissé's Premier Cru vineyards sit on the limestone and clay soils of the Mâconnais, where the combination of Jurassic limestone bedrock and continental warmth produces Chardonnay with more generous stone-fruit weight than the Côte de Beaune, while retained acidity keeps the structure taut. Malolactic fermentation completes in barrel, integrating oak and fruit before the wine is racked to stainless steel and bottled — from the 2022 vintage onward, under Diam 30 closure, a significant shift from cork for the house. The deliberate refusal to stir lees is the defining transgression here: where most Mâconnais producers build creaminess through bâtonnage, Latour achieves richness purely through oak integration and site weight. The 2022 vintage delivered vivid natural acidity alongside plush, ripe fruit — a combination that suits this fuller-bodied, toasty house style particularly well.

name: Pala  Stellato Vermentino Di Sardegna DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sardinian Vermentino with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing coastal white.
description (EN Store): Pala's Stellato Vermentino di Sardegna DOC is a vibrant expression of Sardinia's maritime terroir. This single-varietal white showcases the classic profile of Vermentino: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, complemented by subtle stone fruit and a distinctive mineral, sea-salt character that speaks to the island's coastal influence. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, grilled fish, and Mediterranean fare. The herbaceous undertones add complexity and food-friendliness, while the clean finish invites another sip.

name: Pala  Soprasole Vermentino Sardegna DOC
