short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sardinian Vermentino with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing white for seafood and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store):
Pala Soprasole Vermentino Sardegna DOC is a classic expression of Sardinia's signature white varietal. This single-varietal Vermentino showcases the region's coastal terroir through bright citrus zest, ripe stone fruit, and distinctive mineral salinity. The wine's medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, food-friendly profile ideal for warm-weather entertaining and seafood-centric menus.

The herbaceous undertones and subtle tropical notes add complexity without overwhelming the wine's core freshness. Best served chilled as an aperitif or paired with grilled fish, shellfish, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Cigalus Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Domaine de Cigalus — Bertrand's first biodynamic estate (2002) — blends Chardonnay, Viognier & Sauvignon Blanc, aged in French oak under lunar-calendar discipline.
description (EN Store):
Domaine de Cigalus was the first of Gérard Bertrand's estates to convert to biodynamics, beginning in 2002 — a deliberate proof-of-concept that would eventually scale across all 17 properties to make Bertrand the world's largest Demeter-certified producer. That pioneering commitment started here, in the IGP Aude Hauterive, where the estate's warm Mediterranean climate is tempered by the strong regional winds that reduce disease pressure and allow farming without chemical intervention.

The 2022 vintage across southern France was defined by heat and drought stress, producing low yields and concentrated fruit with elevated natural alcohol — conditions that compress aromatic intensity into the whites while demanding careful cellar management to preserve freshness. Cigalus Blanc is an unconventional assemblage for the Languedoc: Chardonnay, Viognier, and Sauvignon Blanc co-fermented and aged primarily in French oak barrels, with all winemaking decisions — harvest timing, racking, bottling — governed by the biodynamic lunar calendar.

What sets this cuvée apart within the Bertrand portfolio is the deliberate crossing of Burgundian variety logic (Chardonnay, oak aging) with aromatic Rhône character (Viognier) and Loire-style lift (Sauvignon Blanc) under a single IGP designation — a blend no AOP in France would legally permit. The 2022 delivers concentrated stone fruit and waxy floral depth from the Viognier, structured by oak-derived texture, with the Sauvignon Blanc providing cut and length on a warm, full-bodied frame.

name: Prinz Von Hessen  H Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Rheingau Riesling with bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Prinz Von Hessen's Rheingau Riesling exemplifies the region's classical white wine character: a dry to off-dry expression of Germany's signature varietal, marked by vibrant acidity and aromatic complexity. Expect bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with stone fruit and floral undertones emerging on the palate. The wine's minerality and herbaceous notes reflect the terroir of the Rheingau, one of Germany's premier Riesling-producing regions. Medium body and crisp acidity make this a versatile food wine, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

name: Gerard Bertrand  6eme Sens Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Languedoc blanc, vinified parcel-by-parcel across 17 estates — Roussanne, Vermentino, Viognier shaped by Mediterranean wind and limestone.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand built his empire on a deliberate provocation: proving that the world's largest biodynamic wine operation could exist not on a single domaine but across 17 estates spanning the full breadth of Languedoc-Roussillon — every harvest timed to the lunar calendar, every parcel vinified separately before Bertrand himself presides over the final assemblage. The 6ème Sens Blanc draws on that multi-terroir philosophy, pulling Roussanne, Vermentino, and Viognier from sites shaped by warm Mediterranean climate, strong coastal winds that suppress disease pressure, and the limestone and sandy soils that drive the aromatic lift and textural precision characteristic of the appellation. Fermentation takes place in stainless steel and/or French oak barrels following pneumatic pressing, preserving the aromatic integrity of each variety before blending. All viticulture is Demeter-certified biodynamic, with zero synthetic inputs across the estate portfolio — a commitment that began with Domaine de Cigalus in 2002 and now spans the entire group. What sets this cuvée apart within its category is the sheer geographic breadth behind a single blend: no single-estate white at this price point draws on the same diversity of Languedoc sub-terroirs. The current vintage reflects the warm, sun-driven character of southern France, delivering generous aromatic expression with the freshness that biodynamic farming and Mediterranean wind exposure consistently deliver.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturalys Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Sauvignon Blanc from Languedoc, vinified in stainless steel under lunar-calendar timing — aromatic precision at scale.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand built his empire on a deliberate provocation: proving that the world's largest biodynamic wine operation could exist not in Burgundy or Alsace, but across 900+ hectares of Languedoc-Roussillon, every estate Demeter-certified and every harvest decision governed by the lunar calendar. The Naturalys Sauvignon Blanc sits within that philosophy — certified biodynamic, zero chemical inputs in the vineyard, and vinified to let the grape's natural aromatic register speak without interference. Fermentation takes place in stainless steel, preserving the variety's inherent tension and primary fruit character against the warm Mediterranean backdrop. The Languedoc's strong seasonal winds reduce disease pressure naturally, allowing biodynamic farming at a scale most producers would consider impossible, while the region's sun-drenched days and cooler nights at higher-altitude parcels contribute the acidity that keeps the wine focused. No malolactic fermentation is applied, a deliberate choice to retain the wine's natural freshness — consistent with Bertrand's approach across his aromatic white and rosé program. What sets Naturalys apart within the portfolio is its explicit organic certification alongside biodynamic practice, positioned as an accessible entry point into Bertrand's zero-compromise farming philosophy. Drink young to capture the full aromatic lift.

name: Robertson Winery  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp South African Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Robertson Winery's Sauvignon Blanc is a light-bodied white wine from South Africa's Western Cape, crafted in the classic style that emphasizes freshness and aromatic intensity. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and herbaceous notes, followed by green apple and tropical fruit on the palate. A pronounced mineral backbone and grassy undertone provide complexity, while the wine's high acidity ensures a clean, refreshing finish ideal for warm-weather drinking and food pairing.

name: Mascota Vineyards  La Mascota Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' La Mascota Chardonnay is a single-varietal white from Argentina's Mendoza region, crafted in a classically balanced style. The wine opens with vibrant citrus and peach aromatics, supported by green-apple freshness and a mineral undertone. On the palate, subtle butter and vanilla notes—likely from measured oak aging—add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character. Medium body and crisp acidity make this an approachable, food-friendly expression suitable for seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Caliterra  Chardonnay Reserva
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Caliterra's Chardonnay Reserva from Colchagua Valley presents a classically styled white wine balancing ripe stone fruit with bright citrus acidity. The palate shows characteristic Chardonnay richness from oak aging—vanilla and butter notes—tempered by crisp green apple and minerality. Medium body and refreshing acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Two Vines  Chardonnay Columbia Crest
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—ideal for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): Two Vines Chardonnay from California delivers a balanced, food-friendly white wine anchored by ripe peach and green apple on the nose. The palate shows classic Chardonnay character: stone fruit and citrus zest supported by subtle butter and vanilla oak notes, with a refreshing medium-high acidity that keeps the wine lively and elegant. Medium body and low tannin make this an accessible choice for casual dining and entertaining.

name: Laurenz V.  Charming Gruner Veltliner
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Austrian Grüner Veltliner from Kamptal with vibrant citrus, green apple, and mineral character.
description (EN Store): Laurenz V.'s Charming Grüner Veltliner is a classic expression of Austria's signature white varietal from the Kamptal region. This medium-bodied wine showcases the hallmark freshness and mineral precision of the appellation, with bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose leading to a palate of stone fruit and herbaceous notes. The wine's crisp acidity and white pepper spice make it an ideal aperitif or food companion for seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh vegetable dishes.

name: Laurenz V.  Singing Gruner Veltliner
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Austrian Grüner Veltliner with vibrant citrus, green apple, and mineral notes—a refreshing white for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Laurenz V.'s Singing Grüner Veltliner is a classic expression of Austria's signature white varietal from the Kamptal region. This medium-bodied wine showcases the hallmark characteristics of Grüner Veltliner: bright citrus zest, green apple, and stone fruit on the nose, with a crisp, herbaceous palate underpinned by mineral tension. The medium-high acidity and white pepper spice make it an exceptionally food-friendly choice, equally at home with grilled seafood, fresh salads, or as an elegant aperitif.

name: Lindeman's  Premier Selection Semillon / Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Hunter Valley blend of Semillon and Chardonnay with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Premier Selection Semillon/Chardonnay is a classic Hunter Valley white blend that balances the textural elegance of Semillon with the fruit-forward appeal of Chardonnay. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and peach notes. A herbaceous undertone and mineral backbone provide complexity and food-friendliness. Medium body and crisp acidity make this an ideal aperitif or versatile table wine for lighter fare.

name: Agricola Querciabella  Batar Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Tuscan white with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing acidity and herbaceous minerality.
description (EN Store): A vibrant white from Tuscany's Chianti Classico region, this wine showcases the region's capacity for elegant, food-friendly whites. Bright citrus and green apple dominate the palate, supported by subtle stone fruit and a pronounced mineral backbone. The herbaceous undertones and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood and light Mediterranean fare.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Vaudon  Chablis
short_description (EN Store): Chablis from Drouhin's 18th-century Moulin de Vaudon estate (~40 ha): indigenous-yeast fermentation, restrained oak, 2022's heat balanced by naturally low pH.
description (EN Store): Joseph Drouhin's Chablis operation is anchored at the Moulin de Vaudon, an 18th-century watermill estate commanding roughly 40 hectares in the Chablis appellation — a deliberate geographic commitment that separates this cuvée from négociant blends assembled at arm's length. The Kimmeridgian limestone and clay soils of Chablis, cooler and more northerly than the Côte de Beaune, drive the piercing, saline mineral tension that defines the appellation's identity. The 2022 growing season was hot and dry across Burgundy, yet Chablis' continental latitude and the appellation's characteristically low-pH soils preserved acidity, yielding whites that carry phenolic ripeness without sacrificing the chalky backbone the terroir demands. In the cellar, 100% Chardonnay undergoes slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions; alcoholic and malolactic fermentation proceed in French high-forest oak barrels with indigenous yeasts, followed by light bâtonnage during élevage and 10–25% new oak (seasoned approximately three years before coopering). What sets Drouhin apart in Chablis is the same biodynamic farming discipline — horse-ploughed soils, cover crops, ECOCERT-certified since 2009 — applied across all 100 hectares of the Burgundy estate, including these northern holdings. The 2022 delivers ripe stone fruit framed by chalk-driven tension; drink now through 2028.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Vaudon  Chablis "Reserve De Vaudon"
short_description (EN Store): Chablis from Drouhin's 18th-century Moulin de Vaudon estate: 40 ha of Kimmeridgian terroir, indigenous-yeast fermentation, restrained oak — 2021's frost-driven precision.
description (EN Store):
Joseph Drouhin's Chablis holdings are anchored at the Moulin de Vaudon, an 18th-century watermill estate commanding roughly 40 hectares in the appellation — a scale that allows parcel-level selection while maintaining the house's négociant-domaine discipline. The Réserve de Vaudon draws from Kimmeridgian limestone and clay soils, the fossilised oyster-shell bedrock that gives Chablis its signature saline, chalky tension and distinguishes it from any other Chardonnay terroir in Burgundy.

Vinification follows Drouhin's white-wine protocol: slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions, then alcoholic and malolactic fermentation in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts, with light bâtonnage during élevage. New oak is kept deliberately restrained — 10–25% new wood depending on appellation level — so the barrel acts as a vessel for texture rather than flavour. The estate is fully ECOCERT-certified organic and biodynamic, with horse ploughing and cover crops between rows, a commitment unusual for a 40-hectare Chablis holding.

2021 brought severe frost and mildew pressure across Chablis; strict sorting and small yields concentrated the surviving fruit into wines of marked freshness and mineral precision. Drink from 2024 through 2029.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Puligny Montrachet
short_description (EN Store): 2021 Puligny-Montrachet from Drouhin's biodynamic Côte de Beaune holdings — small-yield frost year, indigenous-yeast barrel ferment, 10–25% new French oak.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full biodynamic conversion across its Burgundy holdings — certified by ECOCERT for the 2009 vintage — an extraordinary undertaking for a 100-hectare négociant-domaine hybrid. Puligny-Montrachet sits on the limestone-rich, clay-limestone soils of the Côte de Beaune's southern slope, where shallow topsoil over fractured limestone bedrock forces vine roots deep, concentrating mineral tension in the juice. The 2021 growing season brought severe frost pressure and mildew, demanding strict sorting; the resulting yields were small, but the fruit that survived carries the vintage's hallmark precision and cut. Vinification follows slow pneumatic pressing, with free-run juice fermented in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts; malolactic fermentation completes in barrel alongside light bâtonnage, with 10–25% new oak (seasoned approximately three years before coopering) ensuring the wood serves the terroir rather than dominating it. What distinguishes Drouhin's approach is the deliberate restraint on new oak at village level — a house-wide philosophy of finesse over extraction, overseen by winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin, that keeps the limestone-driven mineral character of Puligny at the fore. The 2021 vintage delivers the freshness and precision that define the year; drink from 2025 through 2032.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Corton Charlemagne Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru from Drouhin's biodynamic estate: indigenous-yeast barrel fermentation, restrained oak, and 2022's heat-forged low-pH tension.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full organic and biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate — earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage — a commitment almost without precedent for a négociant-domaine of this scale. Corton-Charlemagne sits on the upper slopes of the Corton hill, where thin, iron-rich limestone and marl soils over hard rock force vine roots deep, concentrating mineral tension and suppressing excessive fruit weight. Vinification follows slow pneumatic pressing, with free-run juice separated from press fractions before alcoholic and malolactic fermentation in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts; light bâtonnage during élevage integrates lees texture without obscuring site character. Oak is seasoned approximately three years before coopering, and new-oak exposure is deliberately restrained — Drouhin's house philosophy prioritises terroir transparency over barrel imprint, an unusual discipline at Grand Cru level where many producers lean into new wood. The 2022 growing season was hot and dry, producing phenolically ripe fruit, yet whites held relatively low pH despite the heat — delivering the structural tension that defines Corton-Charlemagne's limestone-driven identity. Drink from 2026 through 2036+.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Macon Villages
short_description (EN Store): Mâconnais Chardonnay from Drouhin's organically farmed Burgundy holdings — slow-pressed, barrel-fermented with indigenous yeasts, lees-aged for textural depth.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin made a defining commitment when Philippe Drouhin began converting all 100 hectares to organic and biodynamic farming in 1990 — a scale almost without precedent among Burgundy's négociant-domaines, earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage. The Mâcon-Villages draws from the southernmost arc of Drouhin's Burgundy holdings, where Chardonnay grows on the limestone-clay soils characteristic of the Mâconnais plateau, delivering naturally moderate yields and a cooler, mineral-inflected ripeness distinct from the richer Côte de Beaune. Vinification follows the full Drouhin white-wine protocol: slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions, alcoholic and malolactic fermentation in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts, light bâtonnage during élevage, and 10–25% new oak seasoned approximately three years before coopering. What sets this cuvée apart within its category is that Drouhin applies the same barrel-fermentation and indigenous-yeast regime used for its Premier Cru whites all the way down to this village-level Mâcon — a deliberate house choice that prioritises textural complexity over the tank-fresh simplicity most producers default to at this appellation tier. The current vintage drinks with the generous, precise character Drouhin's winemaker Véronique Boss-Drouhin achieved across the range — approachable now, with enough lees-derived structure to reward 2–3 years of cellaring.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Pouilly Fuisse
short_description (EN Store): Mâconnais Chardonnay from Drouhin's biodynamic négociant portfolio — barrel-fermented with indigenous yeasts, restrained oak, and 2022's heat-tempered precision.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate — an extraordinary undertaking for a négociant-domaine hybrid of this scale, earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage. Pouilly-Fuissé sits in the southern Mâconnais, where Chardonnay roots into limestone-clay soils beneath the dramatic rock faces of Solutré and Vergisson; the elevation and calcareous bedrock drive the appellation's characteristic tension between stone-fruit richness and chalky mineral grip. Vinification follows Drouhin's white-wine signature: slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions, then alcoholic and malolactic fermentation conducted in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts, with light bâtonnage during élevage and 10–25% new oak seasoned approximately three years before coopering. What distinguishes this cuvée within the Drouhin range is its position at the southern frontier of the house's Burgundy portfolio — Pouilly-Fuissé is the most southerly and structurally generous appellation Drouhin works with, yet the house philosophy of finesse over power keeps the oak integration restrained where peers lean into new wood. The 2022 vintage delivered phenolically ripe fruit with relatively low pH despite the hot, dry growing season; whites show balance and early accessibility with the structure to develop over 3–6 years.

name: Nederburg  Lyric Sauvignon blanc Chenin Blanc Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant South African blend of Sauvignon Blanc, Chenin Blanc & Chardonnay with crisp citrus and stone fruit.
description (EN Store): A refreshing white blend from Paarl, Western Cape, combining Sauvignon Blanc (48%), Chenin Blanc (46%), and Chardonnay (6%) into a harmonious expression of South African terroir. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous notes. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a crisp, food-friendly profile ideal for seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations. The mineral backbone and tropical undertones add complexity without overwhelming the palate.

name: Nederburg  Winemaster's Reserve Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant South African Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak complexity—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Nederburg's Winemaster's Reserve Chardonnay from Paarl showcases the refined character of South African viticulture. This single-varietal expression balances ripe stone fruit and citrus notes with measured oak influence, delivering a medium-bodied white of considerable versatility. Subtle butter and brioche notes from winemaking complement the wine's natural minerality, while a crisp, medium-high acidity ensures freshness and food compatibility across a broad range of cuisines and preparations.

name: Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite)  Aussieres  Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Languedoc Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and refined oak—a versatile white for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): Domaines Barons de Rothschild's Aussieres Blanc is a classically styled Chardonnay from Languedoc-Roussillon, balancing primary stone fruit and green apple character with measured oak influence. The wine displays medium-full body and medium acidity, offering approachability without sacrificing complexity. Toasted oak and butter notes from winemaking provide structure, while subtle minerality adds refinement on the finish. This is a food-friendly white suited to shellfish, grilled fish, cream-based pasta, and roasted poultry.

name: E.Guigal  Cotes du Rhone Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône white blend: stone fruit, floral, minerality. Refreshing, food-friendly, versatile.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Côtes du Rhône Blanc is a classically proportioned white blend anchored by Viognier (70%), with supporting roles from Roussane, Marsanne, Clairette, and Bourboulenc. The wine delivers a harmonious profile of stone fruit (peach and apricot), delicate floral aromatics, and mineral undertones, balanced by crisp acidity and subtle white pepper spice. Medium-bodied and refreshing, it exemplifies the elegance and food-friendliness of northern Rhône white blends, making it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, light pasta, and Mediterranean fare.

name: E.Guigal  Saint Joseph Blanc AOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône white from Guigal: stone fruit, citrus, and mineral complexity. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Saint Joseph Blanc is a refined expression of the northern Rhône's classic white blend. Dominated by Marsanne with a touch of Roussanne, this wine delivers ripe stone fruit and citrus on the nose, supported by subtle hazelnut and honey notes from careful winemaking. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with medium body and bracing acidity that makes it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood and lighter cream sauces. A benchmark example of Rhône white elegance.

name: E.Guigal Condrieu Blanc AOC
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Viognier from Condrieu: stone fruit, citrus blossom, and mineral complexity in a refreshing, elegant white.
description (EN Store): E. Guigal's Condrieu Blanc is a benchmark expression of Viognier from France's most prestigious appellation for the variety. The 2020 vintage showcases the grape's signature aromatic intensity—ripe apricot and peach stone fruit layered with citrus blossom and honeyed minerality. Medium body and crisp medium-high acidity deliver freshness and precision, while subtle oak integration adds textural depth without overwhelming the wine's delicate floral character. A versatile pairing for seafood, light poultry, and spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Pighin  Pinot Grigio Grave Del Friuli DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Friuli Pinot Grigio with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—a refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Pighin's Pinot Grigio from Grave del Friuli showcases the classic profile of northeastern Italy's signature white: bright citrus and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone-fruit notes of pear and peach. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with medium body and bracing acidity that defines the region's terroir. A versatile, food-friendly expression ideal for aperitifs, seafood, and light summer fare.

name: Castello Colle Massari  Melacce Montecucco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Tuscan Vermentino with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing white for seafood and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Castello Colle Massari's Melacce Montecucco DOC is a single-varietal Vermentino from Tuscany's coastal Montecucco region. This white wine showcases the classic profile of the variety: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle stone-fruit and tropical undertones. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with a saline finish characteristic of Mediterranean whites. Medium body and medium-high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for grilled fish, shellfish, and fresh Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Marques de Caceres  Verdejo
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Verdejo from Rioja Alta with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing white wine for seafood and light dishes.
description (EN Store):
Marqués de Cáceres Verdejo is a vibrant single-varietal white from Spain's prestigious Rioja Alta subregion. This wine showcases the classic profile of Verdejo: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and herbaceous undertones. The palate is clean and refreshing, driven by medium-high acidity that carries the wine through to a crisp, mineral-tinged finish. Medium body and low tannin make it an approachable, food-friendly choice.

Ideal for grilled white fish, shellfish, fresh oysters, and Mediterranean-inspired tapas. The acidity also makes it an excellent match for spicy Asian cuisine, particularly Thai dishes with sour and heat elements.

name: Clarendelle  Blanc Inspired by Haut-Brion AOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux white blend of Semillon and Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and herbaceous character.
description (EN Store): Clarendelle Blanc is a classic Bordeaux white blend from Entre-Deux-Mers, combining 76% Semillon with 24% Sauvignon Blanc. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by stone fruit and herbaceous notes that reflect the Sauvignon Blanc component. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, mineral-driven palate ideal for seafood, light pasta, and Asian cuisine. A versatile aperitif and food wine.

name: Bottega  Pinot Grigio delle Venezie IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Veneto with bright citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Bottega's Pinot Grigio delle Venezie IGT is a classic expression of northeastern Italy's most celebrated white varietal. Sourced from the Conegliano Valdobbiadene subregion of Veneto, this wine delivers the hallmark freshness and mineral precision expected of the appellation.

On the nose, bright citrus and green apple dominate, with subtle stone fruit undertones and a whisper of herbaceous character. The palate is clean and linear, driven by crisp acidity and a lean mineral structure that lingers through the finish. The wine's light body and refreshing profile make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for delicate seafood, raw preparations, and fresh vegetable-forward dishes.

name: Cristo Di Campobello  Bianco IGP
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian white blend of Chardonnay, Grillo, Insolia & Catarratto with green apple, citrus & subtle oak.
description (EN Store): Cristo Di Campobello Bianco IGP is a Sicilian white blend that marries the structure of Chardonnay with the regional character of Grillo, Insolia, and Catarratto. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by stone fruit and tropical undertones that reflect Sicily's sun-drenched terroir. Oak aging adds subtle honey and butter notes, while a mineral backbone provides freshness and complexity. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at table—equally at home with seafood, cream-based pasta, or as an aperitif.

name: Marchesi Di Barolo  Gavi di Gavi DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Cortese from Piedmont: citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness. Elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Marchesi di Barolo's Gavi di Gavi DOCG is a refined expression of Cortese from Piedmont's prestigious Gavi zone. This single-varietal white showcases the grape's signature citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with stone fruit and subtle minerality on the palate. Medium body and bracing acidity deliver a refreshing, food-friendly profile ideal for aperitifs, seafood, and light Mediterranean fare. A benchmark example of northern Italian white wine elegance.

name: Cordero Di Montezemolo  Langhe Arneis DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Arneis with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—a refreshing white for seafood and light cuisine.
description (EN Store): Cordero Di Montezemolo Langhe Arneis DOC is a refined expression of Piedmont's signature white varietal. Arneis, meaning 'little rascal' in Piedmontese dialect, delivers a vibrant profile of citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone-fruit notes and subtle almond character. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with medium body and bracing acidity that defines the varietal's refreshing character. A wine of elegance and food-friendliness, ideal for seafood, light pasta, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Monchiero Carbone  Roero Arneis Cecu d la Biunda DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Nebbiolo from Roero with cherry, herbaceous notes, and mineral complexity. Medium-bodied with vibrant acidity.
description (EN Store): Monchiero Carbone's Roero Arneis Cecu d la Biunda DOCG showcases the distinctive character of Nebbiolo from Piedmont's Roero subregion. The 2022 vintage presents a medium-bodied white with bright cherry and herbaceous primary notes, complemented by cedar and licorice secondary characteristics. Medium-high acidity and tannin structure provide freshness and grip, while developing minerality suggests age-worthiness. A refined expression suited to food-forward occasions and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Cristo Di Campobello  Laluci DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Grillo with vibrant citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing white for seafood and warm-climate cuisine.
description (EN Store): Cristo Di Campobello's Laluci DOC is a classic expression of Grillo, Sicily's signature white varietal. The wine displays a bright, refreshing profile with dominant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, complemented by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous notes. A distinctive saline minerality runs through the palate, characteristic of Sicily's coastal terroir, with crisp acidity that cleanses and invites another sip. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with Mediterranean seafood, grilled fish, and spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Edi Simcic  Triton Lex
short_description (EN Store): Slovenian white blend of Sauvignon Blanc, Chardonnay & Rebula with vibrant citrus, herbaceous depth & mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Edi Simcic's Triton Lex is a three-varietal white blend from Goriška Brda, Slovenia's premier white wine region. The marriage of Sauvignon Blanc's citrus and herbaceous character, Chardonnay's stone fruit and subtle richness, and Rebula's mineral backbone creates a wine of layered complexity and food-friendly versatility. Bright acidity and clean aromatics define the profile, making this an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, salads, and lighter Mediterranean fare.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Curicó Valley Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Reserva Chardonnay from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: ripe stone fruit and citrus on the nose, with a refreshing green-apple note. Medium body and bright acidity frame a palate enriched by restrained oak aging—butter and vanilla notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit. A whisper of minerality and hazelnut in the finish suggests careful winemaking and terroir influence. Versatile at table, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Echeverria  Reserva Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Echeverria's Reserva Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Curicó Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose and palate, supported by herbaceous and tropical undertones. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a refreshing, clean finish with subtle minerality. A versatile white wine suited to seafood, salads, and lighter cuisine.

name: Feudo Maccari  Grillo IGT

name: Cantina Terlano  Pinot Grigio DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Terlano with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—a refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.

A medium-bodied white wine with pronounced medium-high acidity, it delivers bright citrus zest, green apple, and stone fruit on the nose and palate, underpinned by subtle minerality and herbaceous undertones. name: Cantina Terlano  Gewurztraminer DOC

name: Cantina Terlano  Quarz Sauvignon Blanc DOC

name: Cantina Terlano  Winkl Sauvignon Blanc DOC

name: Framingham  Sauvignon blanc

name: Cannonball  California Chardonnay

name: Carta Vieja  Chardonnay (375 ml)

name: Carta Vieja  Chardonnay

description (EN Store): Carta Vieja's Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Loncomilla Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright, refreshing, and food-friendly. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and herbaceous character, supported by green apple and tropical fruit notes that evolve on the palate. name: Gallician  Prestige Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Rhône white blend of Grenache Blanc, Clairette & Marsanne—stone fruit, citrus, herbaceous minerality. Elegant & refreshing.
description (EN Store):
Gallician Prestige Blanc is a classic Rhône Valley white blend combining Grenache Blanc (50%), Clairette (30%), and Marsanne (20%). This composition yields a wine of medium body with bright, medium-high acidity that defines its refreshing character.

On the nose and palate, stone fruit and citrus zest dominate, supported by subtle herbaceous and mineral notes that speak to the terroir. Floral undertones and a whisper of almond complexity round out the profile, creating a wine suited to both aperitif service and food pairing across seafood, grilled vegetables, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Laurent Miquel  Chardonnay Viognier
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc blend of Chardonnay & Viognier: stone fruit, citrus, and floral elegance with crisp minerality.
description (EN Store): A refined Languedoc white blending 65% Chardonnay with 35% Viognier, this wine marries the structure and citrus-driven freshness of Chardonnay with Viognier's distinctive floral aromatics and stone-fruit generosity. The medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, food-friendly profile with notes of peach, green apple, and subtle butter complexity. Minerality and a clean finish make this an approachable yet sophisticated choice for seafood, creamy pasta, and soft cheeses.

name: Salisbury 1848  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Murray Darling Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived vanilla—a versatile, food-friendly white.
description (EN Store): Salisbury's 1848 Chardonnay from Australia's Murray Darling region delivers a medium-full-bodied white wine with bright acidity and restrained oak influence. The primary character centers on green apple and citrus, supported by stone fruit notes that suggest ripe but balanced fruit. Oak-derived vanilla and subtle butter notes provide textural richness without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. A whisper of toasted hazelnut and minerality adds complexity, making this a versatile food wine suited to both lighter seafood preparations and richer cream-based dishes.

name: Salisbury 1848  Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Salisbury's Pinot Grigio from Murray Darling delivers the classic profile of this versatile white: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with subtle stone fruit undertones. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly wine ideal for warm-weather entertaining. The clean, mineral finish makes it an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for light seafood, salads, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Crane Lake  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Lodi with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Crane Lake's Sauvignon Blanc from California's Lodi region delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright, zesty, and approachable. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and herbaceous aromatics, followed by green apple and tropical fruit on the palate. A mineral backbone and clean acidity provide structure and refreshment, making this an ideal choice for warm-weather sipping or pairing with light cuisine.

name: Coastal Ridge  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): California Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Coastal Ridge Chardonnay represents a well-balanced expression of California's signature white varietal. Medium-full in body with restrained acidity, this wine opens with green apple and citrus aromatics, followed by stone fruit and subtle oak-derived vanilla and butter notes. The palate shows good structure and a hint of hazelnut complexity, finishing with toasted brioche undertones. A versatile food wine suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based preparations.

name: Bogle  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): California Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile, food-friendly Central Coast classic.
description (EN Store): Bogle Chardonnay from California's Central Coast delivers a well-balanced expression of the varietal, marked by ripe green apple and citrus on the nose, with supporting notes of vanilla oak and butter from careful winemaking. The medium-full body and moderate acidity make it an approachable yet structured wine, suitable for both casual enjoyment and food pairing. Subtle stone fruit and toasted hazelnut notes add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward character.

name: Cakebread  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Carneros estate Chardonnay, barrel-fermented in French oak with sur lie lees-stirring — built for the table, not the trophy shelf.
description (EN Store): When Jack and Dolores Cakebread founded their Rutherford estate in 1973, they embedded a resident chef and a culinary workshop into the winery by 1985 — a deliberate structural decision that made food compatibility a winemaking parameter, not an afterthought. That philosophy drives every lot decision on this Chardonnay. The fruit draws primarily from Tinsley Ranch and Cuttings Wharf Road in Carneros, where shallow haire loam over well-drained subsoils and persistent Pacific fog keep canopy temperatures low, slowing ripening and locking in the natural acidity that defines the house style. Barrel fermentation in French oak, followed by sur lie aging with hand-stirring of lees, builds textural weight without surrendering that Carneros tension — the lees contact rounds the mid-palate while the cool-site acidity keeps the line taut. Malolactic fermentation is decided lot-by-lot, giving the winemaking team a precision lever to calibrate richness against freshness across the blend. What sets this expression apart within Napa Chardonnay is the deliberate restraint on oak influence: at a 200,000-case house, the style has remained unchanged across only four winemakers in 50+ years, resisting the trend toward high-extraction, heavily toasted bottlings. The 2020 vintage was shaped by wildfire pressure across Napa Valley; Cakebread's estate sourcing and selective harvesting practices were the primary mitigation tool, and the resulting wine skews toward freshness and precision over weight.

name: Caymus  Conundrum White
short_description (EN Store): Refreshing California white blend with citrus and stone fruit; crisp acidity and versatile food-friendly profile.
description (EN Store): Caymus Conundrum White is a California white blend from Napa Valley, crafted to deliver approachable refreshment and food versatility. The wine opens with bright citrus and stone-fruit aromatics, supported by tropical undertones and a clean, mineral finish. Medium body and medium-high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for a wide range of cuisines—from grilled seafood and shellfish to Asian dishes and light pasta preparations.

name: Chateau Montelena  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and elegant oak—a classic expression of California's premier white wine region.
description (EN Store): Château Montelena's 2020 Chardonnay represents the benchmark style of Napa Valley's most celebrated white wine. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus aromatics, layered with ripe stone fruit that speaks to the region's warm growing season. On the palate, medium-full body and restrained acidity create a seamless texture, while judicious oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut complexity without overwhelming the fruit. Minerality and subtle hazelnut notes emerge on the finish, suggesting careful winemaking and potential for graceful aging. A versatile food wine suited to fine dining and special occasions.

name: Duckhorn  Decoy Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Ripe tropical and stone fruit with creamy vanilla oak—a full-bodied Napa Chardonnay for shellfish and rich poultry.
description (EN Store): Duckhorn's Decoy Chardonnay is a full-bodied expression of Napa Valley fruit, built on a foundation of ripe tropical and stone fruit character. Vanilla oak and butter from barrel aging create a creamy, approachable palate, while subtle brioche and hazelnut notes add complexity. Medium acidity and low tannin make this wine food-friendly and elegant, ideal for lobster, crab, cream-based risotto, and roasted chicken.

name: Far Niente  Chardonnay

Green apple and citrus notes lead the palate, supported by rich butter and vanilla from oak aging, with subtle hazelnut complexity emerging on the finish. name: Francis Coppola  Director's Cut Chardonnay

name: Grgich Hills  Chardonnay

name: Signus  Crisp White (Sauvignon Blanc) (Fruit wine blend)

name: Torres  Pazo Das Bruxas Rias Baixas DO

description (EN Store): Torres' Pazo Das Bruxas represents the signature style of Rías Baixas: a vibrant, mineral-driven Albariño with bright citrus and white peach on the nose, followed by a saline, flinty palate. name: Vesevo  Greco di Tufo DOCG

Ideal as an aperitif, with light desserts, or alongside Asian cuisine where its natural sweetness and acidity balance spice and heat.

name: Los Vascos  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Casablanca Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Los Vascos Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Casablanca Valley delivers the classic profile of the varietal: bright citrus zest and herbaceous character on the nose, followed by green apple and tropical fruit on the palate. High acidity and mineral structure make this wine an ideal aperitif and food companion, particularly with seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine. name: Joseph Drouhin  Laforet Chardonny de Bourgogne
short_description (EN Store): Bourgogne AOC Chardonnay from Drouhin's organically farmed Burgundy holdings — barrel-fermented with indigenous yeasts, 2021's frost-reduced yields delivering precision over volume.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth-generation Drouhin family, Maison Joseph Drouhin completed a full organic and biodynamic conversion across its 100-hectare Burgundy estate — earning ECOCERT certification for the 2009 vintage — a commitment almost without precedent for a négociant-domaine of this scale. The Laforêt Chardonnay draws from Drouhin's Burgundy AOC-level holdings across the Côte d'Or and Mâconnais, where clay-limestone soils and the house's horse-ploughed, cover-cropped viticulture prioritise root depth and mineral uptake over yield. Vinification follows the full Drouhin white-wine protocol: slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions, then alcoholic and malolactic fermentation conducted in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts, with light bâtonnage during élevage and approximately 10–15% new oak — a regime that builds texture without masking site character. What distinguishes Laforêt within the regional Bourgogne tier is that it receives identical winemaking treatment to Drouhin's Premier Cru whites: no shortcuts in vessel or yeast selection simply because the appellation is entry-level. The 2021 vintage, shaped by severe spring frost and mildew pressure that drastically reduced yields, produced wines of uncommon freshness and precision — lower volume forcing strict sorting and concentrating what remained. Drink now through 2026.

name: Anakena  Birdman Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish—ideal for seafood and Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Birdman Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Rapel Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright, refreshing, and food-friendly. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and herbaceous character, followed by green apple and tropical fruit notes that define the primary profile. High acidity and light body make this an excellent aperitif and versatile pairing partner for seafood, salads, and spicy cuisine.

name: Viu Manent  Estate Collection Reserva Sauvignon Blanc

name: Oxford Landing  Sauvignon Blanc

Oxford Landing Sauvignon Blanc from Australia's Riverland region delivers a bright, refreshing white wine built on high acidity and light body. name: Duckhorn  North Coast Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Central Coast Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous minerality, and tropical fruit—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Duckhorn's 2023 North Coast Sauvignon Blanc from Central Coast delivers the classic profile of the varietal: bright citrus zest, herbaceous undertones, and mineral-driven complexity. Light-bodied with piercing acidity, this wine showcases green apple, lime, and subtle tropical fruit on the palate, finishing clean and refreshing. A versatile white suited to oysters, grilled fish, fresh salads, and spicy Asian cuisine.

name: Framingham  Classic Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Marlborough Riesling with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—ideal for spicy Asian cuisine and seafood.
description (EN Store): Framingham's Classic Riesling from Marlborough's Wairau Valley showcases the region's signature precision and brightness. A 100% Riesling expression, this wine delivers vibrant citrus zest and stone fruit on the nose, with green apple and subtle floral notes adding complexity. A versatile white suited to spicy Asian dishes, raw seafood, and light grilled preparations.

name: Joseph Drouhin  Bourgogne Aligote
short_description (EN Store): 100% Aligoté from Drouhin's organically farmed Burgundy holdings — fermented with indigenous yeasts, aged in lightly oaked barrels by Véronique Boss-Drouhin.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1880 and now stewarded by the fourth generation, Maison Joseph Drouhin made a deliberate choice that sets it apart from most large Burgundy négociants: the entire portfolio is restricted to Pinot Noir and Chardonnay — and, at the regional level, Aligoté. This Bourgogne Aligoté is the house's sole concession to Burgundy's other white grape, and it is treated with the same low-intervention rigour applied across the range. Drouhin's Burgundy AOC parcels sit on the clay-limestone soils characteristic of the Côte d'Or's lower slopes, where the grape's naturally high acidity is preserved by the cooler mesoclimates and the biodynamic farming — horse-ploughed, cover-cropped — that has been ECOCERT-certified since the 2009 vintage. Vinification follows slow pneumatic pressing with free-run juice separated from press fractions; alcoholic and malolactic fermentation proceed in French high-forest oak barrels using indigenous yeasts, with light bâtonnage during élevage and 10–25% new oak depending on appellation level. What is unusual here is the application of a Grand Cru–calibre winemaking protocol — native yeast barrel fermentation, lees contact, tasting-led élevage decisions — to an appellation where most producers opt for tank-fermented, early-bottled simplicity. The current vintage delivers Aligoté's signature chalky tension and citrus-pith drive, softened fractionally by lees texture, and is best consumed within three to four years of harvest.

name: Kendall Jackson  Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Approachable California Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—a versatile table wine for everyday elegance.
description (EN Store): Kendall Jackson Vintner's Reserve Chardonnay is a benchmark California white wine that balances fruit-forward character with restrained oak influence. The nose opens with ripe peach and pear, complemented by citrus zest and a whisper of vanilla from careful oak aging. On the palate, medium body and bright acidity deliver stone fruit and green apple flavors, finishing with a soft buttery texture and toasted hazelnut notes. This wine exemplifies the approachable, food-friendly style that has made Kendall Jackson a staple in American wine culture.

name: Kendall Jackson  Grand Reserve Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Kendall Jackson's Grand Reserve Chardonnay from Sonoma County showcases the varietal's classic California expression. Ripe peach and green apple lead the nose, supported by toasted oak and subtle vanilla from careful barrel aging. The palate is medium-bodied with bright acidity and a creamy mouthfeel, finishing with hazelnut and citrus zest. A versatile, well-balanced wine suited to both casual dining and more refined table settings.

name: Montes Winemaker's Choice  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Casablanca Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, herbaceous snap, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Montes Winemaker's Choice Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Casablanca Valley delivers the region's signature brightness and freshness. Crisp citrus zest and herbaceous notes dominate the palate, supported by green apple and subtle tropical fruit character. name: Montes Winemaker's Choice  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Casablanca Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-aged complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Montes Winemaker's Choice Chardonnay from Chile's Casablanca Valley showcases the region's cool-climate signature: bright green apple and citrus aromatics balanced by ripe stone fruit.
