name: Domaine Huet  Le Clos Du Bourg Moelleux1Er Trie
short_description (EN Store): Vouvray moelleux: honeyed Chenin Blanc with stone fruit, minerality, and elegant sweetness.
description (EN Store): A Moelleux-classified Vouvray from the Loire Valley's premier Chenin Blanc producer. This 2006 vintage showcases the noble-rot-influenced sweetness and complexity characteristic of Domaine Huet's Le Clos du Bourg vineyard. Expect honeyed stone fruit, quince, and apricot preserve on the nose, with a floral, mineral backbone that prevents cloying sweetness. The palate reveals layers of dried apricot, citrus zest, and wet-stone minerality, balanced by crisp acidity. A wine of elegant restraint and aging potential.

name: Brotte  Grand Vallon Cotes Du Rhone Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Rhône white with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—versatile aperitif and food wine.
description (EN Store): Brotte's Grand Vallon Côtes du Rhône Blanc is a classic southern Rhône white blend, crafted from traditional varieties including Grenache Blanc, Clairette, and Bourboulenc. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and white peach, supported by herbaceous minerality and a whisper of sea salt on the finish. Medium-bodied with crisp acidity, it balances freshness with subtle complexity, making it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, Mediterranean fare, and spiced Asian cuisine.

name: Pieropan  Soave Classico Calvarino Doc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Garganega from Soave's Calvarino cru: crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision in a classic northeastern Italian white.
description (EN Store):
Pieropan's Soave Classico Calvarino is a benchmark expression of Garganega from the Veneto's most prestigious white-wine region. The 2019 vintage delivers a refined profile: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, followed by subtle stone-fruit depth and a distinctive almond-tinged minerality on the palate. Medium body and vibrant acidity create a wine of elegance and food-friendliness, with a clean, lingering finish that speaks to the volcanic soils of the Calvarino cru.

A versatile aperitif or table wine suited to seafood, fresh pasta, and aged cheeses.

name: Pieropan  Soave Classico La Rocca Doc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Garganega from Soave's heart: citrus-driven, mineral-edged white with refreshing acidity and subtle stone-fruit depth.
description (EN Store): Pieropan's Soave Classico La Rocca is a benchmark expression of Garganega from the Veneto's most prestigious white-wine zone. The 2019 vintage delivers a medium-bodied white with pronounced citrus and green-apple aromatics, underpinned by mineral tension and a whisper of almond. The palate is crisp and linear, with lemon zest and pear notes that evolve toward subtle herbaceous complexity. Medium-high acidity and low phenolics make this a food-friendly aperitif and table wine, equally at home with grilled fish, fresh shellfish, or creamy risotto.

name: Pio Cesare  L'Altro Langhe Chardonnay DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Chardonnay with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—refined and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Pio Cesare's L'Altro Langhe Chardonnay is a refined expression of Chardonnay from Piedmont's prestigious Langhe region. This 100% varietal white showcases the classic tension between ripe stone fruit and bright citrus acidity that defines quality Chardonnay in northern Italy. On the palate, green apple and lemon zest lead, supported by subtle hazelnut and butter notes from careful winemaking. The wine's medium body and mineral backbone make it an exceptionally versatile food companion—equally at home with seafood, creamy pasta, or fresh cheese.

name: Pio Cesare  Piodilei Langhe Chardonnay DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Chardonnay with citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—refined and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Pio Cesare's Piodilei Langhe Chardonnay is a refined expression of northern Italian white winemaking. The 2022 vintage showcases the classic Chardonnay profile: bright citrus and green apple on the nose, complemented by stone fruit and a whisper of hazelnut from careful oak aging. Medium body and crisp acidity create a balanced, elegant palate with subtle butter and minerality that lingers. This is a versatile food wine, equally at home with seafood, poultry, or creamy risotto.

name: Domaine Philippe Colin  Chevalier-Montrachet Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Vines from 1955 & 1978 on shallow Puligny limestone; fermentation starts in steel, finishes in French oak — Grand Cru precision without opulence.
description (EN Store):
When Philippe Colin separated from Domaine Michel Colin-Déléger in 2004, he inherited a sliver of Chevalier-Montrachet planted in two distinct moments — 1955 and 1978 — vines old enough to have witnessed the appellation's modern reputation being written. The 2021 vintage carries additional weight: it is the first in which Philippe formally began transferring the domaine to son Simon, making this bottling a document of generational inflection.

Chevalier-Montrachet sits above Le Montrachet on steeply inclined, shallow limestone and calcareous clay in Puligny-Montrachet. The thin topsoil forces roots deep into fractured bedrock, restricting yields and concentrating mineral tension rather than fruit weight — the structural opposite of the richer, clay-dominant soils lower on the slope.

Vinification follows Philippe's deliberate counter-style: manual harvest, direct pneumatic pressing, and alcoholic fermentation initiated in thermoregulated stainless steel at low temperature to lock in Chardonnay's floral register before the must is transferred to French oak barrels (228 L, 450 L, and 500 L from five cooperages) to complete fermentation and undergo malolactic. Lees aging runs 12–18 months with periodic stirring, followed by a further 3–6 months rest in stainless steel before light filtration and bottling under the domaine's signature technical wax capsule — applied to all whites since 2011.

The steel-start/barrel-finish hybrid is the rule-break: most Grand Cru Burgundy goes directly to wood, but Colin's method preserves aromatic lift that the site's austere limestone would otherwise suppress. Critics aggregated on Wine-Searcher score the 2021 Chevalier-Montrachet at 93/100 — a vintage of bracing acidity and structural tension that rewards cellaring well into the 2030s.

name: Albert Bichot  Domaine Long-Depaquit  Chablis
short_description (EN Store): Domaine Long-Depaquit's 65 ha of Kimmeridgian limestone-clay in Chablis, vinified 100% stainless steel, delivering the appellation's sharpest mineral signature.
description (EN Store):
Domaine Long-Depaquit — Albert Bichot's 65-hectare Chablis estate, certified organic from 2018 — sits entirely on Kimmeridgian limestone and clay, the ancient seabed geology that gives Chablis its distinctive oyster-shell salinity and taut, chalky tension. The 2022 growing season brought a hot, dry summer and early harvest, producing Chardonnay of unusual aromatic generosity and full-bodied weight while the Kimmeridgian subsoil preserved the structural acidity that defines the appellation.

At village level, Long-Depaquit ferments and ages in 100% stainless steel — no oak contact whatsoever — a deliberate choice that strips away any textural softening and forces the wine to speak entirely through its terroir. This is the same estate that holds La Moutonne, the 2.35 ha quasi-monopole spanning Vaudésir and Preuses Grand Crus that was excluded from the 1938 Grand Cru classification, making Long-Depaquit one of Chablis's most historically anomalous landholders. Vinification follows the house's non-interventionist protocol: handpicked fruit, gravity racking, minimal handling throughout.

The 2022 vintage delivers ripe, generous stone fruit and white blossom underpinned by the estate's signature flint-and-chalk minerality — approachable now but structured for three to five years of further development.

name: Bernard Magrez  Oustric Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Languedoc Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Bernard Magrez's Sauvignon Blanc from Languedoc-Roussillon delivers a refreshing white wine built on bright citrus zest and green apple, underpinned by herbaceous and grassy notes. The medium body and medium-high acidity create a clean, food-friendly profile with tropical undertones and mineral tension. A versatile aperitif and dining companion for oysters, grilled fish, goat cheese, and Asian cuisine.

name: Chateau Reynon- Blanc - Cadillac
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux white blend of Sauvignon Blanc and Sémillon; citrus-driven with herbaceous minerality.
description (EN Store): Château Reynon's Blanc from Cadillac showcases the classic Bordeaux white blend formula: 89% Sauvignon Blanc paired with 11% Sémillon. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, underpinned by herbaceous and grassy notes that define the primary character. A mineral backbone and subtle stone fruit in the secondary layer add depth and texture. Medium body and brisk acidity make this an ideal aperitif and food-friendly companion to seafood, Asian cuisine, and lighter fare.

name: Domaine Champalou  Vouvray "Le Portail"
short_description (EN Store): Vouvray Chenin Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—elegant Loire Valley expression.
description (EN Store): Domaine Champalou's Le Portail is a classic Vouvray Chenin Blanc from the 2021 vintage, showcasing the Loire Valley's signature mineral-driven white-wine character. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, transitioning to ripe stone fruit and honeyed notes on the mid-palate. A distinctive wet-stone minerality and subtle floral undertones provide complexity and food-friendly acidity. This is a versatile expression suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and lighter cuisine.

name: Nik Weis  Urban Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Mosel Riesling with bright peach, apricot, and mineral slate—crisp, off-dry, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Nik Weis's Urban Riesling from the Saar subregion of Mosel showcases the classic profile of German Riesling: a light-bodied white with vibrant acidity and mineral precision. The wine opens with primary notes of peach and apricot, complemented by lime and green apple on the palate. A distinctive slate minerality runs through the finish, characteristic of Saar terroir, with subtle floral and honey undertones adding complexity. The off-dry character balances the wine's crisp acidity, making it versatile at table and equally rewarding as an apéritif.

name: Sato  Central Otago Pinot Gris "L'atypique"  (Orange wine)
short_description (EN Store): Central Otago Pinot Grigio skin-contact orange wine with stone fruit, honey, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): Sato's L'atypique is a skin-contact orange wine crafted from Pinot Grigio in Central Otago's Bannockburn subregion. Extended contact with grape skins imparts a distinctive amber hue and layered aromatic profile, moving beyond the varietal's typical white-wine expression. The wine balances primary stone fruit and honey notes with secondary spice and herbal complexity, while tertiary oxidative development adds minerality and subtle tannin structure. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a food-friendly profile suited to both delicate seafood and aromatic Asian cuisine.

name: Sato  Central Otago Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Central Otago Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Sato's 2023 Central Otago Riesling from Bannockburn showcases the region's signature mineral-driven character and bright acidity. Stone fruit and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by subtle floral and green apple notes. The palate is crisp and linear, with wet stone minerality and a clean, refreshing finish. This is a classic New Zealand Riesling built for food pairing and aperitif enjoyment.

name: St.Paul's  Chardonnay DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Trentino Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—refined and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): St.Paul's Chardonnay DOC from Trentino-Alto Adige showcases the classic elegance of northern Italian Chardonnay. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by ripe stone fruit and a whisper of minerality. On the palate, restrained oak and butter notes add complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity and medium body. The finish is clean and refreshing, with lingering citrus and a subtle hazelnut undertone. A versatile white that bridges casual aperitif moments and refined table service.

name: St.Paul's  Pinot Grigio Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Alto Adige Pinot Grigio with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—ideal for seafood and light appetizers.
description (EN Store): St.Paul's Pinot Grigio from the Adige Valley in Alto Adige showcases the classic elegance of northern Italy's premier white wine region. This medium-bodied expression balances vibrant citrus and green apple with subtle stone-fruit undertones, underpinned by a refreshing mineral spine. The medium-high acidity delivers a clean, crisp finish that makes it an ideal aperitif or companion to seafood, fresh salads, and light antipasti.

name: St.Paul's  Gewurztraminer Justina DOC
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Gewurztraminer from Trentino with lychee, rose, and spice—a versatile aperitif and Asian cuisine companion.
description (EN Store): St.Paul's Gewurztraminer Justina DOC represents the classic expression of this aromatic white varietal from Italy's Trentino-Alto Adige region. The wine opens with pronounced floral and lychee aromatics, supported by honeyed stone fruit and white pepper spice on the palate. Medium body and brisk acidity deliver refreshment and food-friendly versatility, making it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with aromatic Asian cuisines, soft cheeses, and spiced dishes.

name: Delas  Cotes Du Rhone Saint Esprit Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Rhône white blend of Grenache Blanc, Clairette, Bourboulenc & Viognier—stone fruit, citrus & floral minerality.
description (EN Store): Delas's Côtes du Rhône Saint Esprit Blanc is a refined expression of southern Rhône white winemaking, blending Grenache Blanc, Clairette, Bourboulenc, and Viognier into a harmonious, food-friendly white. The wine opens with stone fruit and citrus aromatics, supported by subtle floral and herbaceous notes that speak to the region's terroir. Medium body and bright acidity create a refreshing palate, with minerality and a crisp finish that invites pairing with seafood, Asian cuisine, and Mediterranean fare. A versatile aperitif and table wine for warm-weather entertaining.

name: Silverlake  S Series Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Crisp South Australian Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Silverlake S Series Chardonnay is a single-varietal Sauvignon Blanc from South Australia, crafted to deliver bright, refreshing character. The wine opens with dominant citrus zest and green apple, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that define its primary profile. Stone fruit and tropical notes emerge on the mid-palate, complemented by subtle minerality that adds complexity and structure. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with raw seafood, grilled fish, or fresh salads. The mineral finish provides a clean, lingering conclusion.

name: Silverlake  S Series Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp South Australian Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and refreshing minerality.
description (EN Store): Silverlake's S Series Sauvignon Blanc is a classic expression of the varietal from South Australia's cool-climate regions. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and grassy undertones that speak to the wine's freshness. On the palate, medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a clean, refreshing profile with stone fruit and subtle tropical notes adding complexity. A whisper of minerality rounds out the finish, making this wine an ideal aperitif or food companion for seafood, salads, and lighter fare.

name: Nautilus Estate  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant grapefruit, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Nautilus Estate's Sauvignon Blanc from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by citrus zest and green apple notes that lend freshness and definition. A mineral backbone provides structure and length, making this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Tenuta Lunelli  Pietragrande
short_description (EN Store): Trentino white blend of Chardonnay, Sauvignon, and Pinot Blanc—crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Tenuta Lunelli's Pietragrande is a carefully composed white blend from Trentino-Alto Adige, combining 80% Chardonnay with Sauvignon Blanc, Incrocio Manzoni, and Pinot Blanc. The blend marries the richness and structure of Chardonnay with the aromatic lift and herbaceous character of Sauvignon, creating a wine of medium body and bracing acidity. Expect dominant citrus zest and stone-fruit notes on the nose, supported by subtle oak and butter from winemaking, with a mineral, crisp finish. Ideal for seafood, fresh pasta, and lighter Mediterranean fare.

name: Kaiken  Terroir Series Torrontes
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Argentine Torrontés with citrus, stone fruit, and floral notes. Crisp, refreshing, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Kaiken's Terroir Series Torrontés showcases the distinctive character of Mendoza's signature white varietal. This single-varietal expression delivers bright citrus zest and stone-fruit aromatics on the nose, with supporting floral and herbaceous notes that evolve on the palate. The wine's medium body and pronounced acidity create a refreshing, mineral-driven finish ideal for warm-weather drinking and versatile food pairing.

name: Cono Sur  Tocornal Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Entry-tier Sauvignon Blanc from Cono Sur's Colchagua Valley estate, cold-fermented in stainless steel under screwcap — a closure the house pioneered in Chile in 2002.
description (EN Store): Cono Sur was the first Chilean winery to adopt screwcap closures for aromatic whites in 2002, a deliberate break from convention at a time when cork was still considered non-negotiable for any wine with export ambitions. The Tocornal Sauvignon Blanc sits within that same philosophy: preserve freshness through technical precision rather than tradition. Sourced from Colchagua Valley, where the home winery at Chimbarongo anchors Cono Sur's 1,246-hectare estate network, the fruit benefits from the valley's warm days and cool nights driven by Andean altitude influence, which slows ripening and retains natural acidity. Vinification is carried out in temperature-controlled stainless steel tanks — the winery holds 12 million litres of capacity — with no oak contact, preserving the variety's primary aromatic profile intact. Bottled under screwcap, the wine is sealed at peak freshness, consistent with the house's long-standing position that aromatic whites are better served by inert closure than by cork variability. Within a portfolio dominated by Pinot Noir ambition, this Sauvignon Blanc represents Cono Sur's volume-tier white program, built for immediate accessibility. Drink young, within 1–2 years of vintage, to capture primary fruit character at its most vivid.

name: Cono Sur  Bicicleta Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Cool-coastal Sauvignon Blanc from Cono Sur's Colchagua-anchored multi-valley program — screwcap-sealed since 2002, fermented in temperature-controlled stainless steel.
description (EN Store): When Cono Sur adopted screwcaps for aromatic whites in 2002, it was the first Chilean winery to do so — a deliberate rejection of cork convention driven by the conviction that freshness, not tradition, was the correct closure philosophy for high-acid whites. The Bicicleta Sauvignon Blanc sits within that same logic: a wine built around preservation of primary aromatics above all else. Sourced from cool-climate coastal sites across Cono Sur's multi-valley network — including Campo Lindo and El Centinela, where Humboldt Current breezes and foggy mornings slow ripening on granitic and sandy soils — the fruit arrives with natural tension and green-herb precision that warmer inland blocks cannot replicate. Vinification is entirely in temperature-controlled stainless steel, with no oak contact, preserving the site-driven citrus and herbaceous character without textural interference. No malolactic conversion is indicated, keeping acidity structural and linear. The special feature here is scale without compromise: Cono Sur's 314 hectares of certified organic vineyards and drip-irrigation discipline underpin even the entry tier, meaning the Bicicleta benefits from the same agronomic rigour applied to the house's icon wines. Drink young, within 1–2 years of vintage, for maximum aromatic lift.

name: Buronga Hill Estate  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant South Eastern Australian Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, green apple, and herbaceous notes—crisp and refreshing.
description (EN Store):
Buronga Hill's Sauvignon Blanc is a classic expression of South Eastern Australian white wine craftsmanship. This 100% Sauvignon Blanc delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting herbaceous and tropical notes that evolve on the palate. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile with subtle minerality and a clean, crisp finish.

Ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and vibrant Asian cuisine, this wine showcases the versatility and approachability that define quality Australian Sauvignon Blanc.

name: Bodega Volcanes de Chile  Reserva Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Central Valley Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, green apple, and herbaceous minerality—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Bodega Volcanes de Chile's Reserva Sauvignon Blanc is a classic expression of Chile's Central Valley terroir, delivering bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose with supporting herbaceous and tropical notes. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with medium body and bracing acidity that makes it an ideal companion to seafood, fresh cheeses, and spice-forward Asian cuisine. A versatile, well-balanced white wine suited to casual dining and aperitif service.

name: Fattoria Le Pupille  Poggio Argentato Toscana IGT
short_description (EN Store): Sauvignon-led Tuscan white with citrus snap, stone fruit, and herbaceous minerality—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Poggio Argentato is a Maremma white blend anchored by Sauvignon Blanc (60%), with supporting roles from Petit Manseng, Traminer, and Semillon. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, underpinned by stone fruit and subtle tropical notes. A herbaceous minerality runs through the mid-palate, lending complexity and freshness. Medium body and crisp acidity make this a versatile aperitif and seafood companion, equally at home with raw oysters, grilled fish, or bright Mediterranean salads.

name: Black Oak  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): California Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Black Oak Chardonnay from Sonoma, California, delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, complemented by subtle oak-derived vanilla and butter notes. On the palate, stone fruit and hazelnut flavors emerge, with a toasted oak finish that adds complexity without overwhelming the fruit. This wine's versatility makes it an excellent choice for a range of occasions, from casual weeknight dinners to more refined entertaining.

name: Layer Cake  Virgin Chardonnay - Monterey
short_description (EN Store): Monterey Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—versatile, food-friendly California white.
description (EN Store): Layer Cake Virgin Chardonnay from Monterey's Central Coast delivers a classically styled California white with approachable charm. Bright citrus and green apple lead the nose, followed by ripe stone fruit and a whisper of vanilla oak from measured barrel aging. The palate is clean and refreshing, with medium body and crisp acidity that cuts through rich dishes without overwhelming delicate flavors. A touch of minerality on the finish adds complexity. Ideal for seafood, creamy pasta, roasted poultry, and soft cheeses.

name: Starmont  Carneros Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Carneros Chardonnay with bright citrus, orchard fruit, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): Starmont's 2017 Carneros Chardonnay exemplifies the region's cool-climate elegance. Green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and a whisper of minerality. On the palate, buttery oak and vanilla provide structure without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity, while hazelnut and toast notes add complexity. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this a food-friendly expression suited to shellfish, roasted poultry, and cream-based preparations.

name: Starmont  Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Napa Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Starmont's 2022 Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend delivers a classic expression of the varietal's signature brightness. Aromas of citrus zest, fresh grass, and green apple lead to a palate marked by crisp acidity and mineral tension. The Semillon component adds subtle tropical fruit and stone fruit complexity, softening the wine's edges while maintaining its refreshing character. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, fresh cheese, and herbaceous cuisine.

name: Aldridge  'Twynham' Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant South Australian Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak complexity. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Aldridge's Twynham Chardonnay is a classically styled white wine from South Australia, showcasing the region's ability to produce balanced, food-friendly expressions of the varietal. The wine displays a harmonious blend of stone fruit and citrus on the nose, with supporting notes of butter and subtle oak influence from winemaking. Medium body and crisp acidity make it versatile at table, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes. A reliable choice for those seeking approachable elegance without pretension.

name: Corte Viola  Pinot Grigio IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Veneto Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Corte Viola's Pinot Grigio from Veneto delivers the classic profile of Italy's most celebrated white varietal. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by delicate stone fruit and subtle floral notes. On the palate, medium body and vibrant acidity create a refreshing, mineral-driven experience with a clean, lingering finish. This is an approachable, food-friendly white suited to casual dining and elegant aperitifs alike.

name: Bodegas Muga  Blanco Rioja DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Rioja Blanco with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing medium-bodied white from Spain's premier region.
description (EN Store): Bodegas Muga's Blanco Rioja DOC is a refined white blend anchored by Viura with supporting Garnacha Blanca and Malvasía de Rioja. The wine delivers bright citrus zest and stone-fruit character on the nose, with a crisp, refreshing palate driven by medium-high acidity. Herbaceous undertones and subtle minerality add complexity without overwhelming the fruit-forward profile. Medium body and clean finish make this an excellent aperitif or food-friendly companion to seafood, light pasta, and Spanish tapas.

name: Domaine Thibault Liger-Belair  Bourgogne Aligote "Clos des Perrieres la Combe"
short_description (EN Store): Biodynamic Aligoté from a named Clos in Nuits-Saint-Georges — native yeast, unfined, from a family that once owned La Tâche.
description (EN Store):
Thibault Liger-Belair is the first vigneron in nine generations of a family that operated exclusively as négociants — the same lineage that once held La Tâche Grand Cru before selling it to DRC in 1929. When he founded the domaine in 2001, he turned that trading heritage into a grower's obsession with individual parcels, including this single-site Aligoté from the Clos des Perrières la Combe, a named enclosure within the limestone-and-chaille (limestone-flint) soils of the Nuits-Saint-Georges terroir. The chaille subsoil — a dense mix of fractured limestone and flint — imparts a stony, saline tension to Aligoté that the grape rarely achieves in the Côte Chalonnaise benchmarks of the appellation.

Viticulture is fully biodynamic, with soils plowed by horse to preserve structure and microbial life. In the cellar, hand-harvested fruit undergoes triple sorting before native yeast fermentation in open-top vessels. Aging follows the domaine's standard protocol for village-level whites: older oak barrels, minimal intervention, no fining, no filtration, with sulfites sourced from a natural Polish mine rather than synthetic inputs.

What is categorically unusual here: Aligoté is almost never given single-clos treatment at this level of biodynamic rigour — this is Pinot Noir-grade site specificity applied to Burgundy's workhorse white variety. The warm 2020 vintage was navigated by Thibault with deliberate restraint to preserve freshness and energy rather than allow over-extraction, yielding a wine of taut mineral drive rather than weight.

name: F.X. Pichler  Riesling Burgstall
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Austrian Riesling from Wachau with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): F.X. Pichler's Riesling Burgstall represents the hallmark precision of Austria's Wachau region. This medium-bodied white showcases vibrant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with stone fruit and subtle floral undertones emerging on the palate. Medium-high acidity and mineral-driven structure deliver a refreshing, elegant finish that invites food pairing across seafood, Asian cuisine, and delicate vegetable dishes.

name: Monsoon Valley Signature White
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Thai Chenin Blanc with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—ideal for spiced cuisine and seafood.
description (EN Store): Monsoon Valley Signature White is a 100% Chenin Blanc from Thailand's Hua Hin region, crafted to complement the country's vibrant culinary traditions. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by stone fruit and subtle tropical notes that reflect the terroir's warm climate. A delicate honey and floral secondary character adds complexity, while mineral undertones provide structure and a crisp, refreshing finish. Medium body and medium-high acidity make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, particularly with Thai spiced dishes, grilled seafood, and light Asian preparations.

name: Jacob's Creek  Classic Chardonnay (375 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Classic Chardonnay from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a balanced expression of the varietal. Green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by ripe stone fruit on the palate. Oak aging contributes subtle vanilla and buttered notes without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. Medium-full body and restrained tannin make this a versatile table wine suited to cream-based dishes, poultry, and seafood preparations.

name: Jacob's Creek  Classic Chardonnay (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for fine dining and everyday occasions.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Classic Chardonnay from Australia's Barossa Valley delivers a well-balanced white wine with approachable fruit-forward character. The wine opens with green apple and citrus aromatics, transitioning to ripe stone fruit and peach on the palate. Subtle oak influence introduces vanilla and toasted hazelnut notes, while a creamy mouthfeel and medium acidity make it food-friendly across a range of cuisines and occasions. A reliable choice for both casual entertaining and fine dining settings.

name: Jacob's Creek  Reserve Chardonnay (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Adelaide Hills Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Reserve Chardonnay from Adelaide Hills showcases the region's cool-climate character through bright citrus and green apple aromatics layered with ripe stone fruit. name: Eugenio Collavini  Collio Broy DOC

A mineral backbone and whisper of tropical character add complexity without overwhelming the wine's refreshing, food-friendly nature. The finish is crisp and dry, making this an ideal aperitif or pairing wine for seafood, salads, and lighter Mediterranean fare.

name: Elena Walch  Chardonnay Castel Ringberg

name: Penfolds  Bin 311 Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Cool-climate Chardonnay from Adelaide Hills & beyond, whole-bunch pressed, French oak-aged — Penfolds' anti-Grange: terroir-led, not blender-led.
description (EN Store):
Bin 311 occupies a deliberate counterpoint within the Penfolds portfolio: where the house built its reputation on multi-regional blending that subordinates place to winemaker vision, Bin 311 is constructed to let cool-climate fruit speak — sourced from high-altitude sites including Adelaide Hills, where elevation and diurnal temperature swing preserve natural acidity and tighten phenolic development. The Adelaide Hills component grows on well-drained soils at altitude, producing Chardonnay with lower sugar accumulation and higher tartaric retention than Barossa floor fruit.

Fruit is whole-bunch pressed — the same entry point used for Yattarna (Bin 144) — separating clean juice from phenolic extraction before fermentation. Maturation proceeds in French oak rather than the American hogsheads that define Penfolds' red program, a deliberate choice that keeps the oak influence finer-grained and the fruit profile primary. Lees contact during barrel aging builds texture without obscuring the site-driven acidity.

What distinguishes Bin 311 categorically is its position as Penfolds' proof-of-concept for cool-climate white winemaking: the same whole-bunch pressing protocol as the flagship Yattarna, at a fraction of the price, making the house's white winemaking philosophy directly accessible. The 2017 vintage in South Australia delivered moderate temperatures and even ripening, producing Chardonnay with focused stone fruit and persistent mineral length.

name: Penfolds  Max's Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Adelaide Hills Chardonnay named for Max Schubert, the architect of Grange — whole-bunch pressed, cool-climate fruit at altitude.
description (EN Store): Max Schubert, the winemaker who built Penfolds' reputation on Shiraz and American oak, lends his name to a Chardonnay that sits at the opposite end of the house's stylistic spectrum — a deliberate nod to the cool-climate whites his legacy made possible. Adelaide Hills, the high-altitude spine of the Mount Lofty Ranges east of Adelaide, supplies the fruit: elevated sites deliver a naturally long, cool growing season that preserves acidity and restrains phenolic ripeness, producing Chardonnay with tension rather than weight. Penfolds specifies whole-bunch pressing of hand-picked fruit — the same entry point used for the flagship Yattarna (Bin 144) — minimising phenolic extraction before fermentation and setting the structural foundation for a leaner, site-expressive style. The winemaking approach prioritises freshness over oak saturation, a meaningful departure from the house's signature American-hogshead richness that defines the red portfolio. What distinguishes Max's within the Penfolds white hierarchy is its single-region Adelaide Hills identity: where Yattarna draws on Tasmania, Henty, and Tumbarumba to build complexity through multi-regional blending, Max's Chardonnay commits to one cool-climate address, making it the most terroir-legible white in the lineup. Current vintage; drink on release through 3–5 years for primary fruit clarity.

name: Penfolds  Koonunga Hill Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Penfolds' entry-level Chardonnay draws on South Australia's cool-climate fruit, vinified in the house's multi-regional blending tradition since 1844.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1844 by Dr. Christopher Rawson Penfold at Magill Estate, Penfolds built its reputation on a deliberate counter-orthodoxy: winemaker expertise over single-terroir purity. Koonunga Hill Chardonnay is the clearest expression of that philosophy at the accessible tier — fruit sourced across South Australia's cool-climate zones, assembled for consistency of house character rather than site declaration.

South Australia's cool-climate regions — Adelaide Hills at altitude, and broader multi-district fruit — deliver Chardonnay with natural acidity retention and stone-fruit concentration rather than tropical weight. The absence of phylloxera in Barossa's ancient sandy loam soils underpins vine health across the broader network that feeds this blend.

Whole-bunch pressing is confirmed Penfolds practice for their Chardonnay tier, preserving phenolic freshness. The wine follows the house's multi-regional blending protocol, prioritising fruit quality and palate consistency over vintage-to-vintage variation — the same philosophy that governs Grange, applied here at entry level.

What distinguishes Koonunga Hill within its price tier is precisely this institutional blending discipline: a nearly 180-year winemaking lineage — only four Chief Winemakers — enforces a stylistic DNA that most sub-฿1,000 Chardonnays simply cannot replicate.

Drink on release through 2–3 years for primary fruit expression.

name: The Old Town  South African Dry White (Fruit wine blend)

description (EN Store): The Old Town South African Dry White is a fruit wine blend from Western Cape, crafted for everyday enjoyment. name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Casablanca Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and toasted oak complexity—elegant Chilean expression from a prestigious Bordeaux house.
description (EN Store): Baron Philippe de Rothschild's Escudo Rojo Gran Reserva Chardonnay from Chile's Casablanca Valley showcases the cool-climate potential of this prestigious region. Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity, the wine opens with green apple and citrus zest, supported by subtle stone fruit notes. Oak aging contributes toasted vanilla and butter complexity, while a mineral backbone provides structure and elegance. The finish is clean and persistent, reflecting careful winemaking and quality fruit selection.

name: Yalumba  Y Series Viognier
short_description (EN Store): Australia's Viognier pioneer — planted Eden Valley in 1980 when the variety was virtually unknown outside France. Cool-fermented, aromatic, site-driven.
description (EN Store):
Yalumba planted Viognier in Eden Valley in 1980, when the variety had almost no presence outside the northern Rhône — a deliberate bet on altitude and aromatic potential that predated Australia's broader Viognier wave by two decades. That pioneering commitment makes the Y Series the entry point into a house that has shaped the entire category on this continent.

Eden Valley's shallow, rocky, well-drained soils at 400–550 m elevation force vine roots deep while moderating ripening temperatures, preserving the lifted floral aromatics and natural acidity that distinguish the region's Viognier from warmer, lower-altitude fruit. The diurnal range at these elevations is the structural engine behind the wine's freshness.

Fermentation is conducted in stainless steel under cool, controlled conditions, a deliberate choice to lock in primary aromatic compounds — apricot kernel, white peach, dried ginger — rather than allow them to volatilise. All Yalumba wines have been 100% vegan since the 2012 vintage, with no animal-derived fining agents used across the range. The Y Series draws on Yalumba's Nursery-sourced clonal material, the same research infrastructure that underpins the flagship Virgilius.

What sets this expression apart is its direct lineage to Australia's most serious Viognier program: the same Eden Valley elevation and clonal knowledge that informs the Virgilius is channelled here at an accessible tier — a genuine house-style statement, not a commodity varietal.

The current vintage is best consumed within 2–3 years of release to capture peak aromatic intensity.

name: Damilano  Langhe Arneis DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Piedmont white with citrus zest, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—ideal for seafood and light pasta.
description (EN Store): Damilano's Langhe Arneis is a refreshing white wine from Piedmont's prestigious Langhe region, crafted entirely from the Arneis grape. This varietal expresses bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone-fruit and pear notes that unfold on the palate. A herbaceous undertone and subtle minerality provide complexity and food-friendly structure, while crisp acidity and a clean finish make this wine an excellent aperitif or companion to seafood, light pasta, and fresh vegetable dishes.

name: Mastroberardino  Radici Fiano di Avellino DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Fiano from Irpinia: citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity in a refreshing, food-friendly white.
description (EN Store):
Mastroberardino's Radici Fiano di Avellino DOCG represents the classical expression of Fiano from Irpinia, Campania's premier white wine region. This single-varietal white displays a medium body with bright, medium-high acidity that defines the variety's signature profile.

On the nose and palate, expect citrus zest and stone fruit as the dominant primary character, supported by subtle herbaceous and floral notes. A distinctive mineral backbone and saline finish reflect the volcanic terroir of the Irpinia hills, adding complexity and food-friendliness. The wine's structure and balance make it equally suited to aperitif service or pairing with Mediterranean seafood and vegetable-forward cuisine.

name: Monteverro  Vermentino IGT
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Tuscan Vermentino with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing white for seafood and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Monteverro's Vermentino IGT from Maremma delivers a vibrant, refreshing white wine rooted in Tuscany's coastal terroir. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and stone fruit aromatics, supported by a clean, mineral-driven palate with subtle sea salt notes. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a lively mouthfeel ideal for warm-weather drinking and food pairing. The herbaceous undertones and crisp finish reflect the varietal's affinity for Mediterranean climates and its traditional role as a food-friendly aperitif wine.

name: Monteverro  Chardonnay IGT
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A 100% Chardonnay from Monteverro in Tuscany's Maremma region, this 2018 vintage delivers classic white wine elegance. Stone fruit and citrus dominate the nose, with green apple and peach on the palate, supported by buttery oak and hazelnut complexity. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a refreshing, mineral finish ideal for seafood, cream-based dishes, and poultry.

name: Concha Y Toro  Casillero Del Diablo Reserva Privada Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish—ideal for seafood and Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): A refreshing single-varietal Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Maipo Valley, this wine delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and a hint of tropical fruit. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with lively acidity that cuts through rich dishes and complements delicate seafood. A versatile aperitif and food wine for warm-weather entertaining.

name: Talamonti  Trebi Trebbiano D'Abruzzo DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Trebbiano d'Abruzzo with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral notes—a refreshing Italian white for seafood and light fare.
description (EN Store): Talamonti's Trebbiano d'Abruzzo is a classic expression of this native Italian varietal, sourced from the Abruzzo region. The wine displays a clean, refreshing profile with dominant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, complemented by subtle stone-fruit notes and a mineral backbone. Medium body and brisk acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food wine, particularly suited to grilled seafood, fresh salads, and light pasta dishes. The herbaceous undertones and crisp finish reflect the terroir of Montepulciano d'Abruzzo, delivering excellent value for everyday enjoyment.

name: Talamonti  Trabocchetto Pecorino IGP Colline Poscaresi
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Abruzzo Pecorino with citrus, green apple, and mineral salinity—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Talamonti's Trabocchetto Pecorino IGP Colline Poscaresi is a vibrant white from Abruzzo's Montepulciano d'Abruzzo subregion. This 100% Pecorino delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with underlying stone fruit and a distinctive mineral backbone. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing wine for seafood, light pasta, and fresh salads. The herbaceous undertones and subtle salinity reflect the terroir of the Colline Poscaresi, offering excellent value for everyday enjoyment.

name: Cuvaison  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived richness. Elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Cuvaison's 2017 Chardonnay from Napa Valley showcases the classic California style: ripe stone fruit (peach and apricot) balanced by bright citrus acidity and subtle minerality. Oak aging contributes layers of butter, vanilla, and toast that add complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural fruit character. Medium-full body and refreshing acidity make this a versatile table wine suited to both casual dining and more refined preparations.

name: Concha Y Toro  Marques de Casa Concha Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chilean Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy pasta.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Marqués de Casa Concha Chardonnay from Chile's Rapel Valley delivers a refined expression of the varietal. The wine opens with green apple and citrus aromatics, supported by ripe stone fruit on the palate. Oak aging contributes vanilla and toasted hazelnut notes, while a mineral backbone provides freshness and structure. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this wine a versatile companion to seafood, roasted poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Famille Perrin  Réserve Côtes-du-Rhône Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Rhône white with citrus zest, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Famille Perrin's Réserve Côtes-du-Rhône Blanc is a refined white blend from the southern Rhône Valley, crafted in the classic Châteauneuf-du-Pape tradition. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, supported by subtle stone-fruit undertones and herbaceous minerality. The palate is crisp and refreshing, with medium body and pronounced acidity that carries the wine through to a saline, almond-tinged finish. This is an elegant, food-friendly white suited to grilled seafood, Mediterranean cuisine, and aperitif service.

name: Dalrymple  The Cave Block Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): South Australian Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and refined oak integration—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Dalrymple's The Cave Block Chardonnay is a medium-full bodied white wine from South Australia's premium regions, showcasing the varietal's classic balance of fruit and structure. The 2021 vintage displays prominent stone fruit and citrus character on the nose, complemented by subtle oak influence that adds vanilla and buttery complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. A minerally backbone and toasted hazelnut notes emerge on the palate, suggesting careful oak handling and quality fruit selection. This is a versatile food wine suited to both casual entertaining and more refined table settings.

name: De Stefani  Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Veneto Pinot Grigio with bright citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store):
De Stefani's Pinot Grigio from Veneto exemplifies the classic profile of Italy's most celebrated white varietal. Bright citrus and green apple dominate the nose, with supporting pear and herbaceous undertones. On the palate, the wine delivers crisp acidity balanced by subtle minerality, creating a clean, refreshing finish ideal for warm-weather drinking and versatile food pairing.

This light-bodied white showcases the varietal's natural elegance without heavy oak influence, allowing the fruit character to shine. The wine's structure makes it equally suited to casual aperitif service or as a complement to seafood, salads, and Asian cuisine.

name: Alta Mora  Etna Bianco
short_description (EN Store): Carricante from Etna's volcanic slopes: bright citrus, mineral precision, and saline finish.
description (EN Store): Alta Mora Etna Bianco is a single-varietal expression of Carricante, Sicily's signature white grape, grown on the mineral-rich volcanic soils of Mount Etna. The 2022 vintage delivers a crisp, refreshing profile dominated by citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and peach notes. The palate is marked by pronounced minerality and a distinctive saline finish—hallmarks of Etna's terroir—layered with subtle herbaceous complexity. Medium body and bright acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, grilled fish, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Maxville  Sauvingon Blanc Napa Valley
short_description (EN Store): Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous character, and mineral finish—crisp and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A classic Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc showcasing the varietal's signature brightness and aromatic complexity. name: Sutil  Reserve Chardonnay

name: Sutil  Reserve Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with zesty citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—crisp and food-friendly.

name: La Belle Angele  Chardonnay

name: Arnaldo Caprai  Umbria Chardonnay IGT
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Umbrian Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus brightness, and subtle oak complexity.

name: Fantinel  "Borgo Tesis” Pinot Grigio DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Friuli Pinot Grigio with vibrant citrus, green apple, and mineral notes—refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.

name: Conti Zecca  Donna Marzia Vermentino Bianco

name: Anselmi  San Vincenzo IGT

The finish is clean and crisp, making this an excellent aperitif or food-friendly companion to seafood, light pasta, and fresh cheeses.

name: Anselmi  Capitel Croce IGT
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Garganega from Soave: crisp citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Anselmi's Capitel Croce represents the refined expression of Garganega from Veneto's prestigious Soave region. The wine's medium body and bracing acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food-friendly companion to seafood, light pasta, and fresh cheeses. name: Sterling  Vintner S Collection Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Calistoga Chardonnay with bright citrus and stone fruit, balanced oak and minerality. Versatile California classic.
description (EN Store): Sterling Vineyard's Vintner S Collection Chardonnay from Calistoga showcases the region's warm-climate fruit character with a refined, food-friendly profile. Bright citrus and stone-fruit aromatics dominate the nose, supported by subtle oak-derived butter and vanilla notes. name: 19 Crimes  Hard Chard (Chardonnay)
short_description (EN Store): Barossa Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, buttered oak, and crisp citrus—a versatile food-friendly white.
description (EN Store): 19 Crimes Hard Chard is a medium-full bodied Chardonnay from Australia's Barossa Valley, showcasing the region's warm-climate fruit character. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus aromatics, followed by a palate of ripe stone fruit balanced by creamy butter and toasted oak. Secondary vanilla and hazelnut notes add complexity, while a subtle minerality on the finish provides freshness and structure. This is a food-friendly expression suited to rich poultry dishes, shellfish preparations, and cream-based sauces.

name: Royal Tokaji  Furmint Dry Vineyard Selection (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Furmint from Tokaj with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—elegant dry white for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Royal Tokaji's Furmint Dry Vineyard Selection showcases the signature character of Hungary's most celebrated white grape. Bright citrus zest and stone-fruit aromatics dominate the nose, supported by herbaceous and mineral undertones that speak to the volcanic terroir of Tokaj. On the palate, medium body and vibrant acidity deliver refreshing green-apple and peach notes, with a clean, mineral finish. This dry expression balances fruit-forward appeal with the complexity and structure that Furmint is prized for, making it a versatile companion to seafood, fresh salads, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Santiago Ruiz  Albarino
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Iberian field blend of Albariño, Godello & co. Citrus, stone fruit, coastal minerality. Refreshing aperitif & seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Santiago Ruiz's 2024 Albariño-led field blend showcases the mineral-driven character of northwestern Spain's Atlantic-influenced vineyards. A harmonious mix of five indigenous varieties—Albariño, Loureiro, Godello, Treixadura, and Caíño Blanco—delivers bright citrus zest and green-apple acidity balanced by subtle stone-fruit depth and saline minerality. The herbaceous undertone and crisp finish make this wine an ideal aperitif and a versatile partner to grilled fish, shellfish, and Asian cuisine.

name: Botter   Pinot Grigio delle Venezie (Organic Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp organic Pinot Grigio from Veneto with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Botter's Pinot Grigio delle Venezie is a classic expression of the Veneto region's signature white varietal, crafted under organic principles. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple aromatics, followed by subtle stone fruit and floral undertones. On the palate, medium body and vibrant acidity deliver a clean, refreshing profile with mineral precision and a crisp finish. This is an approachable, food-friendly white suited to aperitif service, seafood preparations, and light seasonal cuisine.

name: La Soufrandiere  Pouilly-Fuisse Climat "En Chatenay"
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pouilly-Fuissé from a respected Burgundy producer: citrus, stone fruit, and hazelnut with mineral precision.
description (EN Store): La Soufrandiere's Pouilly-Fuissé Climat "En Chatenay" is a classically structured white Burgundy showcasing the mineral and fruit-driven character of Chardonnay from this prestigious appellation. The 2022 vintage displays bright lemon and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle hazelnut and stone fruit notes. Medium-Full body and crisp acidity provide excellent food compatibility, while restrained oak integration allows the terroir expression to shine. A refined expression of Pouilly-Fuissé's limestone-rich soils.

name: La Soufrandiere  Pouilly-Vinzelles  “la Soufrandiere”
short_description (EN Store): Burgundy Chardonnay with bright citrus, stone fruit, and hazelnut—elegant and mineral-driven.
description (EN Store): La Soufrandiere's Pouilly-Vinzelles showcases the classic Burgundian Chardonnay profile: bright lemon and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle hazelnut and stone fruit on the palate. Medium-Full body and crisp acidity provide structure and freshness, while restrained oak integration adds refinement without dominance. Minerality and a whisper of butter suggest careful winemaking and terroir expression. This is a food-friendly white suited to seafood, cream sauces, and aged cheeses.

name: La Soufrandiere  Pouilly-Vinzelles  Climat “les Quarts”
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Chardonnay with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—a refined expression of Pouilly-Vinzelles terroir.
description (EN Store): La Soufrandiere's Pouilly-Vinzelles Climat "les Quarts" is a single-vineyard Chardonnay from one of Burgundy's most prestigious white-wine appellations. The 2022 vintage showcases the region's hallmark balance of ripe stone fruit and crisp acidity, with lemon zest and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle hazelnut and mineral undertones. Medium-full body and restrained oak integration reflect careful winemaking that prioritizes the expression of terroir over extraction. This is a wine of elegance and food-friendliness, suitable for both aperitif enjoyment and pairing with refined cuisine.

name: Domaine Leflaive  Batard Montrachet Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Bâtard-Montrachet Grand Cru from biodynamic Leflaive: native-yeast fermentation, ~25% new French oak, 18-month élevage on calcareous clay — saline, stony 2022.
description (EN Store): When Anne-Claude Leflaive completed the estate's full biodynamic conversion by 1997 — then considered radical across the Côte d'Or — her stated aim was not philosophy but precision: to make the soil speak without interference. Bâtard-Montrachet is where that ambition is most audible. The Grand Cru sits on argilo-calcaire soils, the calcareous clay matrix delivering both the weight that distinguishes Bâtard from the more aerially tense Chevalier above it and the limestone-driven tension that separates it from richer, flatter sites. Horse-ploughing preserves soil structure and mycorrhizal integrity across Leflaive's Grand Cru parcels — a labour-intensive commitment that directly influences root depth and mineral uptake. Vinification begins with manual harvest, parcel-by-parcel sorting, and long pneumatic pressing, followed by 24-hour static settling before the must is racked directly into French oak — Vosges and Allier — for alcoholic fermentation on indigenous yeasts only. New oak reaches approximately 25% at Grand Cru level, with 12 months in barrel and a further 6 months in stainless steel before spring bottling with minimal intervention — effectively near-unfiltered. What is unusual here: Leflaive deliberately withholds the richness Bâtard's clay could easily produce, using the stainless-steel second winter to sharpen rather than soften. The 2022 vintage — sunny and warm — delivers ripe stone fruit and white flower alongside saline and flint, more approachable in youth than cooler years yet carrying the structural architecture for a decade-plus of development.

name: Domaine Leflaive  Bienvenues Batard Montrachet Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Bienvenues-Bâtard-Montrachet 2019: biodynamic Grand Cru from calcareous clay mid-slopes, native-yeast fermented, mineral-driven and compact — needs a decade.
description (EN Store): When Anne-Claude Leflaive completed the domaine's full biodynamic conversion by 1997 — considered radical in the Côte de Beaune at the time — the declared aim was not organic virtue-signalling but amplified site-specificity: each climat speaking more precisely for itself. Bienvenues-Bâtard-Montrachet is the smallest of Leflaive's four Grand Cru holdings (part of 4.8 ha total), sitting on argilo-calcaire soils with a higher clay fraction than the upper limestone-dominant slopes, generating a wine of density and textural grip rather than pure aerial lift. Harvest is manual with parcel-by-parcel sorting; must undergoes 24-hour débourbage before racking directly into French oak (Vosges and Allier) for alcoholic fermentation with indigenous yeasts only. Élevage runs 12 months in cask — up to ~25% new oak at Grand Cru level — followed by 6 months in stainless steel tank before spring bottling with minimal intervention, effectively near-unfiltered. Light bâtonnage bridges alcoholic and malolactic fermentation, preserving lees texture without obscuring the site's mineral signature. What distinguishes this expression categorically: it is one of only four Grand Cru cuvées from a domaine that treats biodynamics as a precision instrument, not a marketing posture — horse-ploughed soils, homeopathic fining, and zero appellation blending. The 2019 vintage delivered dense extract and bold acidity in a compact, mineral-driven frame; Grand Cru examples from this year demand patience — best approached from 2029 onward.

name: Mascota Vineyards  Unánime Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Mendoza Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and oak-derived vanilla—a medium-full white for elegant seafood and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Mascota Vineyards' Unánime Chardonnay from Mendoza showcases the classic elegance of Argentina's premier white-wine region. The 2017 vintage displays a harmonious balance of ripe stone fruit and bright citrus character, enriched by subtle oak aging that introduces vanilla and hazelnut complexity. Medium-full body and restrained acidity create a wine of refinement suitable for both aperitif enjoyment and food pairing versatility. The minerality and subtle butter notes suggest careful winemaking that respects the varietal's natural expression while adding textural depth.

name: Messenez Family  Chateau M Sauvignon Blanc - Semillon
short_description (EN Store): Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend from Chile's Rapel Valley: crisp citrus, herbaceous notes, and stone fruit with refreshing acidity.
description (EN Store): A Bordeaux-style white blend from Chile's Rapel Valley, this Messenez Family offering combines the vibrant herbaceous and citrus character of Sauvignon Blanc with the subtle stone fruit and textural richness of Semillon. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, food-friendly profile with notes of green apple, lime zest, and minerality. The blend's versatility makes it an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, salads, and lighter Asian cuisine.

name: Lucien Crochet  Sancerre Blanc "Les Calcaires"
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Loire Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous minerality, and elegant acidity—a classic expression of Sancerre's chalky terroir.
description (EN Store): Lucien Crochet's Sancerre Blanc 'Les Calcaires' is a quintessential expression of the Loire Valley's most celebrated white wine region. name: Pinical Estate  Chardonnay

name: Victoria Park  Chardonnay

description (EN Store): Victoria Park's Margaret River Chardonnay delivers a classic expression of the region's cool-climate potential. name: Moulin de Gassac  Sauvignon Blanc IGP
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Languedoc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): A refreshing white wine from the Languedoc-Roussillon region, this Sauvignon Blanc delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and a mineral backbone. name: 7 Cascine  Moscato d'Asti DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Delicate Piedmont Moscato with floral aromatics, stone fruit, and gentle sweetness—ideal for dessert or aperitif.
description (EN Store): 7 Cascine Moscato d'Asti DOCG is a lightly sparkling white wine from Piedmont's Asti region, crafted from 100% Moscato grapes.
