Low alcohol and gentle carbonation make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for light desserts and fruit-based preparations.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Macon Village (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Burgundy Chardonnay with bright lemon, green apple, and hazelnut; medium-full body, crisp acidity.
description (EN Store): A classic Burgundy Chardonnay from the Mâcon Village appellation, this wine showcases the region's signature balance of fruit-forward character and mineral precision. Bright lemon and green apple dominate the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit and a whisper of hazelnut from careful oak aging. The palate is medium-full and refreshing, with crisp acidity that carries the citrus notes through to a clean, buttery finish. Versatile at table and approachable on release.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Chardonnay  (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chardonnay from Beaujolais with apple, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Georges Duboeuf's Chardonnay from Vin de France d'Oc delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, complemented by subtle oak-derived butter and vanilla notes. On the palate, stone fruit and hazelnut flavors emerge, supported by a mineral backbone that adds complexity and food-friendliness. This is an approachable yet refined expression suited to a broad range of table settings—from casual weeknight dinners to more formal entertaining.

name: Georges Duboeuf  Sauvignon Blanc (VDF D'OC)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Beaujolais with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): A light-bodied, high-acidity Sauvignon Blanc from the Beaujolais region, this wine showcases the classic profile of the varietal: bright citrus zest, herbaceous character, and a clean mineral backbone. Green apple and grass notes provide freshness on the palate, while subtle tropical fruit undertones add complexity. The wine's crisp structure and lively acidity make it an excellent aperitif and a versatile food companion, particularly suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and lighter cuisine.

name: The Long Little Dog  Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Chardonnay-Colombard blend with ripe stone fruit, citrus zest, and toasted oak—a versatile, food-friendly white.
description (EN Store): The Long Little Dog Blanc is a Languedoc-Roussillon white blend anchored by Chardonnay and Colombard, delivering a medium-full frame with balanced acidity. The wine opens with green apple and citrus zest, supported by ripe stone fruit notes. Oak aging introduces subtle butter and toast, while minerality and hazelnut complexity emerge on the finish. A well-crafted, approachable expression suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based cuisine.

name: 19 Crimes  Sauvignon Block
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Sauvignon Blanc from Barossa Valley with crisp citrus, tropical notes, and refreshing minerality.
description (EN Store): A lively single-varietal Sauvignon Blanc from Australia's Barossa Valley, this wine showcases the classic aromatic profile of the grape with bright citrus zest, green apple, and tropical fruit character. The palate is fresh and mineral-driven, with herbaceous undertones and a clean, crisp finish supported by medium-high acidity. Approachable and food-friendly, it delivers excellent value for everyday enjoyment and special occasions alike.

name: Baron Philippe de Rothschild  Mouton Cadet Reserve Sauternes
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sauternes blend of honeyed apricot and citrus, balanced sweetness with minerality.
description (EN Store): A refined Sauternes from the prestigious Bordeaux house, this blend of Sauvignon Blanc, Sémillon, and Muscadelle showcases the classic botrytis-influenced character of the region. Honeyed apricot and stone fruit dominate the palate, complemented by bright citrus zest and subtle floral minerality. The wine balances natural sweetness with crisp acidity, delivering elegance and complexity suitable for dessert service or pairing with rich cheeses and traditional pâtés.

name: Lindeman's  Gentleman Collection Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Hunter Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Lindeman's Gentleman Collection Chardonnay from Australia's Hunter Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The primary character showcases green apple and citrus brightness alongside stone fruit notes, while secondary oak influence introduces vanilla, butter, and toast complexity. A touch of minerality rounds the palate, making this wine versatile at the table and approachable on its own.

name: Louis Latour  Chablis Chanfleure
short_description (EN Store): Chablis négociant cuvée from the house that pioneered Corton-Charlemagne; 100% Chardonnay, Kimmeridgian limestone terroir, stainless-steel vinification.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1797 by Jean Latour — whose family had tended Burgundian vines since 1731 — Louis Latour built its white wine identity on limestone-driven Chardonnay, a philosophy that extends from the Grand Cru summit of Corton all the way north to Chablis. Chanfleure is sourced from the négociant arm of the house, drawing on Chablis's characteristic Kimmeridgian limestone and clay soils — the same ancient seabed geology that gives the appellation its signature saline, oyster-shell tension. The cool continental climate of the Yonne valley, with its sharp diurnal swings, preserves the naturally high acidity that defines the style. Vinification follows a reductive, stainless-steel-only approach — no oak contact — preserving the site's mineral precision and green-fruit clarity without the textural weight Louis Latour deliberately builds into its Côte de Beaune whites through new-oak barrel fermentation. Malolactic fermentation is standard for the appellation, softening the edge of Chablis's inherent tartness while retaining structural backbone. What distinguishes Chanfleure within the Louis Latour range is its deliberate stylistic inversion: where the house's signature Corton-Charlemagne is built on 100% new French oak and no lees-stirring for maximum richness, this Chablis is the house's purest expression of unoaked, terroir-transparent Chardonnay. The 2024 vintage in Chablis followed a challenging but ultimately rewarding growing season; drinking window 2025–2028.

name: Louis Latour  Bourgogne Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Entry-level Burgundy Chardonnay from the house that invented Corton-Charlemagne — fermented in French oak, no lees-stirring, Diam 30 sealed from 2022.
description (EN Store): When the 5th-generation Louis Latour replanted phylloxera-devastated hillsides on the limestone summit of Corton with Chardonnay after 1891, he effectively authored the Corton-Charlemagne appellation — the same house philosophy of oak-driven, textural white Burgundy flows downward into this Bourgogne Chardonnay. Sourced across the Côte d'Or as a négociant-éleveur expression, the wine draws on the house's 225-year-old understanding of Burgundian limestone and clay-gravel soils, where chalky subsoils drive the characteristic tension that prevents richness from becoming flabby. Fermentation begins in stainless steel without settling, then the must is transferred hot into 228-litre French oak barrels — made at the Louis Latour in-house cooperage from air-dried French oak — to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation. No lees-stirring is practised, a deliberate house signature that preserves primary fruit clarity rather than building creamy autolytic weight. The 2022 vintage marks the first year bottled under Diam 30 closure, a house-wide shift away from natural cork. What distinguishes this entry-level cuvée within its category is that it receives the identical vinification protocol as the Grand Cru whites — the same vessel, the same no-stirring discipline — simply without the limestone-summit terroir of Corton to amplify it. The 2022 growing season delivered vivid natural acidity alongside plush fruit weight, producing a Bourgogne Blanc with more structural tension than the warm 2018 or 2019 benchmarks.

name: Dominio de Punctum  Finca Fabian Verdejo
short_description (EN Store): Verdejo from La Mancha: crisp citrus and green apple with mineral precision. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Dominio de Punctum's Finca Fabian Verdejo is a single-varietal white from Spain's La Mancha region, showcasing the classic profile of this indigenous grape. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle herbaceous notes and a distinctive mineral backbone. The wine's medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, food-friendly character ideal for aperitifs, grilled seafood, and Mediterranean small plates.

name: Judith Beck  Bambule Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Austrian Chardonnay from Neusiedlersee with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Judith Beck's 2019 Bambule Chardonnay is a single-varietal white from Austria's Neusiedlersee region in Burgenland. The wine displays a classic Chardonnay profile: ripe stone fruit and green apple on the nose, supported by citrus zest and a mineral undertone. On the palate, butter and vanilla oak provide structure and richness, balanced by medium-high acidity that keeps the wine fresh and lively. The finish carries subtle hazelnut complexity, suggesting careful oak handling and winemaking precision. This is a versatile food wine suited to seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Albert Bichot  Domaine Long-Depaquit Chablis Grand Cru "Moutonne" Monopole
short_description (EN Store): 2.35 ha quasi-monopole spanning Vaudésir & Preuses — excluded from 1938 Grand Cru classification, yet undeniably Grand Cru in every technical sense.
description (EN Store): La Moutonne exists in a bureaucratic paradox: a 2.35-hectare site straddling two recognised Grand Cru lieux-dits — Vaudésir and Preuses — that was deliberately excluded from the 1938 Grand Cru classification, leaving Domaine Long-Depaquit to market it as a quasi-monopole outside the official hierarchy while the wine itself performs at the apex of Chablis. Domaine Long-Depaquit, the ~65-hectare Chablis estate within the Albert Bichot portfolio, sits on Kimmeridgian limestone-clay soils — the ancient Jurassic seabed that defines Chablis' most mineral expressions — and La Moutonne's position spanning two Grand Cru slopes concentrates both the chalky tension of Preuses and the broader, more textured energy of Vaudésir into a single wine. At Grand Cru level, Long-Depaquit ferments in used oak barrels with aging running 11–16 months, a regime calibrated to amplify Kimmeridgian character rather than impose wood. The house's non-interventionist philosophy — gravity racking throughout, vinification at the Chablis estate rather than centralised in Beaune — keeps the wine structurally honest. What no peer replicates: the monopole status across two Grand Cru classifications simultaneously, a legal anomaly that has no parallel in Burgundy. The cool, frost-pressured 2021 vintage delivered precisely the conditions La Moutonne rewards: high natural acidity, linear minerality, and compressed fruit that will integrate over 8–15 years from harvest.

name: Domaine De L'Herre  Sauvignon Blanc Cotes De Gascogne
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Côtes de Gascogne with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Domaine De L'Herre's Sauvignon Blanc from Côtes de Gascogne delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous and grassy undertones that define the primary character. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with high acidity that provides freshness and food-friendliness. A light-bodied white wine ideal for warm-weather drinking and versatile table pairings.

name: Anakena  Nuna Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and crisp minerality—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Anakena's Nuna Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Cachapoal Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright citrus zest and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by green apple and tropical fruit notes. High acidity and light body make this wine refreshing and food-friendly, with subtle minerality adding complexity. A versatile white suited to oysters, grilled fish, fresh salads, and spicy Asian dishes.

name: Quinta Da Fonte Souto  Florão White
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Portuguese white from Douro with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing acidity ideal for seafood and Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Quinta Da Fonte Souto's Florão White is a medium-bodied Portuguese white wine from the Douro region, characterized by bright citrus and stone fruit aromatics with a refreshing, medium-high acidity. The wine displays herbaceous undertones and mineral complexity on the palate, finishing clean and crisp. A versatile table wine suited to lighter fare and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Cabriz  Cohleita Dao Branco
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Portuguese white from Dão with vibrant citrus, stone fruit, and mineral character. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Cabriz Colheita Dão Branco is a refreshing white wine from Portugal's esteemed Dão region, crafted to showcase the bright, mineral-driven character typical of the appellation. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple notes, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous undertones that speak to the region's cool-climate terroir. Medium body and crisp acidity make this an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, light pasta, and fresh cuisine.

name: Concha Y Toro  Reservado Sauvignon Blanc Pedro Jimenez
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc–Pedro Jiménez blend from Maipo Valley; citrus-driven with herbaceous minerality.
description (EN Store): A refreshing white blend from Concha y Toro's Maipo Valley portfolio, combining Sauvignon Blanc's signature citrus and herbaceous character with Pedro Jiménez's stone-fruit complexity. Medium body and bright acidity deliver a clean, mineral-edged finish ideal for seafood, fresh salads, and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Penfolds  Yattarna Bin 144 Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Australia's 'White Grange' — 100% Chardonnay sourced across Tasmania, Henty, Adelaide Hills & Tumbarumba, whole-bunch pressed and multi-regionally blended.
description (EN Store): Penfolds named Yattarna its 'White Grange' not as marketing shorthand but as a structural declaration: the same multi-regional blending philosophy that built Grange's reputation for winemaker intellect over single-terroir orthodoxy is applied here to 100% Chardonnay. The name itself derives from an Aboriginal word for 'little by little' — a nod to the painstaking, vintage-by-vintage calibration of fruit sources. Fruit is drawn from four cool-climate regions: Tasmania, Henty (western Victoria), Adelaide Hills, and Tumbarumba — each contributing distinct tension: Tasmanian and Tumbarumba parcels deliver high-altitude acidity and linear citrus drive, while Adelaide Hills adds textural weight and stone-fruit generosity. Whole-bunch pressing of hand-picked fruit is the winemaking entry point, preserving phenolic delicacy before barrel fermentation and extended lees aging in French oak — a deliberate departure from the American hogshead regime that defines Penfolds' red program. The special feature here is categorical transgression: Penfolds, a house whose identity is Shiraz and American oak, produces its prestige white entirely in French oak across a cool-climate, multi-state canvas no single Australian appellation could replicate. The 2019 vintage — a hot, drought-affected year delivering compressed, low-yield fruit — earned 97 pts Decanter and 98 pts Wine Spectator; Chief Winemaker Peter Gago designates it a 'sleeper vintage.' Drink 2025–2038.

name: Noble Hill  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Stellenbosch Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Noble Hill's Sauvignon Blanc from Stellenbosch delivers a refreshing white wine experience anchored by bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose. The palate reveals herbaceous minerality and subtle stone fruit, with a clean, crisp finish driven by medium-high acidity. This is a versatile food-friendly white suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Domaine De Rochebin  Vieilles Vignes Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Mâconnais Chardonnay with citrus freshness, stone fruit, and subtle oak complexity—a refined white for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): Domaine De Rochebin's Vieilles Vignes Chardonnay from Mâconnais exemplifies the region's signature balance of fruit-forward character and mineral precision. This single-varietal expression showcases the classic Burgundian profile: bright citrus and green apple on the nose, layered with ripe stone fruit and a whisper of hazelnut from careful oak aging. The medium body and crisp acidity provide excellent food versatility, while subtle butter and vanilla notes add textural depth without overwhelming the wine's natural elegance.

name: La Cour Des Dames  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Languedoc Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): La Cour Des Dames Sauvignon Blanc is a light-bodied white wine from Languedoc-Roussillon, France, showcasing the classic profile of the varietal. Bright citrus zest and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by green apple and grassy undertones that speak to the wine's freshness and mineral-driven structure. The high acidity provides excellent food-pairing versatility, making it an ideal companion for oysters, grilled fish, fresh goat cheese, and vibrant Asian cuisine. A versatile aperitif wine that delivers crisp, clean drinking pleasure.

name: Notte Rossa  Vermentino  Salento IGP
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Vermentino from Salento with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing white for seafood and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Notte Rossa's Vermentino from Salento in Puglia delivers the classic profile of this Mediterranean white: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, followed by stone-fruit and tropical undertones. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with a distinctive sea-salt character and herbaceous finish that speaks to the region's coastal terroir. Medium body and crisp acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for grilled fish, shellfish, and light Mediterranean dishes.

name: 9 Lives Reserve Inspiring Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision—versatile aperitif and food wine.
description (EN Store): San Pedro's 9 Lives Reserve Inspiring Riesling showcases the bright, mineral-driven character of Curicó Valley fruit. This light-bodied white opens with stone fruit and citrus zest, supported by a clean, flinty minerality and subtle floral notes. High acidity and a dry finish make it an exceptionally food-friendly expression, equally at home as an aperitif or paired with delicate seafood, spicy Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

name: Naudin Père & Fils  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Burgundy Chardonnay from Pouilly-Fuissé with bright citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): A classic Pouilly-Fuissé Chardonnay from the respected Naudin Père & Fils estate, this wine showcases the mineral-driven character and elegant restraint typical of Burgundy's premier white terroirs. Bright lemon and green apple dominate the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit and a whisper of hazelnut complexity. On the palate, medium-high acidity provides structure and freshness, while carefully integrated oak and butter notes add depth without overwhelming the fruit. The medium-full body carries the wine through rich dishes yet maintains the finesse expected of fine Burgundy. A versatile food wine suited to cream-based preparations, shellfish, and aged cheeses.

name: Michele Chiarlo  Roero Arneis Le Madri DOCG
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Piedmont Arneis with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Michele Chiarlo's Roero Arneis Le Madri is a refined expression of Piedmont's signature white varietal. Arneis, meaning 'little rascal' in Piedmontese dialect, delivers a vibrant profile: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, followed by delicate stone-fruit notes and subtle almond character. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with herbaceous undertones and a clean, persistent finish. Medium body and bracing acidity make this wine exceptionally versatile at table—equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, light pasta, and fresh cheeses.

name: Granmonte  Spring Chenin Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Thai Chenin Blanc with green apple, citrus, and floral notes—refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Granmonte's Spring Chenin Blanc from Khao Yai's Asoke Valley presents a vibrant white wine built on the classic Chenin Blanc profile of bright acidity and mineral tension. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by delicate stone fruit and floral undertones. The palate is clean and refreshing, with a honeyed mid-palate that adds subtle complexity without obscuring the wine's essential crispness. Medium body and medium-high acidity make this an ideal aperitif or food-friendly companion to Thai cuisine, seafood, and light Mediterranean fare.

name: Granmonte  Verdelho
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Thai Verdelho with citrus zest, stone fruit, and tropical minerality—ideal for spicy Asian cuisine and fresh seafood.
description (EN Store): Granmonte Verdelho from Khao Yai's Asoke Valley presents a refreshing white wine built on bright citrus zest and stone-fruit character. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a clean, food-friendly profile suited to warm-climate viticulture. Tropical undertones and subtle herbaceous notes add complexity without heaviness, making this wine a natural companion to Thai spice, Vietnamese fresh rolls, and grilled or raw seafood preparations.

name: Granmonte  Viognier
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Thai Viognier with stone fruit, floral notes, and refreshing acidity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Granmonte's Viognier from Khao Yai's Asoke Valley showcases the classic elegance of this northern Rhône varietal adapted to Thailand's terroir. The wine opens with ripe stone fruit and apricot, layered with honeyed florals and subtle white pepper spice. Medium body and bright acidity make it a versatile companion to seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations. A refined expression of Viognier's aromatic potential in Southeast Asia.

name: Granmonte  Gradient Verdelho
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Thai-grown Verdelho with citrus zest and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Granmonte's Gradient Verdelho is a single-varietal white wine from Thailand's Khao Yai region, crafted to deliver bright acidity and aromatic freshness. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and tropical undertones that reflect the warm climate terroir. A herbaceous minerality on the palate adds complexity and food-friendliness, making this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for spiced Asian cuisine, grilled seafood, and fresh preparations.

name: Granmonte  The Orient Viognier
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Thai Viognier with stone fruit, floral notes, and refreshing acidity—elegant aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Granmonte The Orient Viognier is a 100% Viognier white wine from Thailand's Khao Yai region, crafted in the Asoke Valley. This medium-bodied expression showcases the varietal's signature aromatic profile: ripe stone fruit and apricot on the nose, supported by floral and honeyed secondary notes. Medium-high acidity provides freshness and food-friendliness, making it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, Thai cuisine, and light Mediterranean dishes.

name: Colina  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Chardonnay with citrus, stone fruit, and subtle oak—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A medium-bodied Chilean Chardonnay offering bright citrus and green apple on the nose, complemented by stone fruit and minerality on the palate. Subtle oak aging contributes notes of butter and vanilla, adding complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural freshness. The medium-high acidity provides excellent structure and versatility at the table, making this an approachable yet sophisticated choice for everyday enjoyment or casual entertaining.

name: Baron de Lestac Bordeaux Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Bordeaux with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): Baron de Lestac Bordeaux Blanc is a classic expression of Sauvignon Blanc from France's prestigious Bordeaux region. This light-bodied white wine showcases the varietal's signature herbaceous character and bright citrus profile, with notes of lime, grapefruit, and green apple. A pronounced mineral backbone and high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or food-friendly companion to seafood, fresh cheeses, and Mediterranean cuisine.

name: Angove  Chalk Hill Blue Semillon Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend from organic South Australian vineyards; citrus-driven with herbaceous minerality.
description (EN Store): Angove's Chalk Hill Blue Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon represents the winery's commitment to organic viticulture in the Riverland region. This medium-bodied white delivers vibrant citrus zest and green-apple acidity on the nose, with herbaceous undertones that reflect the cool-climate influence. On the palate, stone-fruit and tropical notes emerge, balanced by crisp minerality and a clean, refreshing finish. The blend's structure—driven by Sauvignon Blanc's acidity and Semillon's textural weight—makes it an ideal aperitif or food wine for seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh salads.

name: Angove  Anchorage Semillon  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc–Semillon blend from organic South Australian vineyards; citrus-driven with herbaceous freshness.
description (EN Store): Angove's Anchorage Semillon Sauvignon Blanc is a vibrant white blend from the Riverland region, crafted by Australia's largest certified organic winemaker. The marriage of Sauvignon Blanc's herbaceous snap and Semillon's stone-fruit richness creates a refreshing, food-friendly expression with pronounced citrus zest and green-apple acidity. Medium body and mineral undertones make this an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, fresh salads, and spicy Asian cuisine.

name: Aromo  Reserva Privada Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish. Ideal for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Aromo Reserva Privada Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a classic expression of the varietal with bright acidity and clean, expressive aromatics. The wine opens with prominent citrus zest and herbaceous character, followed by green apple and tropical fruit notes that reflect the region's cool-climate influence. A mineral backbone and subtle stone fruit complexity round out the palate, creating a refreshing and food-friendly profile suitable for a wide range of occasions.

name: Aromo  Reserva Privada Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Maule Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived vanilla—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Aromo's Reserva Privada Chardonnay from Chile's Maule Valley presents a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and restrained acidity. The palate opens with green apple and citrus notes, supported by ripe stone fruit character. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and subtle toasted hazelnut complexity, while underlying minerality adds freshness and structure. This is a versatile, well-balanced Chardonnay suited to both aperitif service and food pairing across seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Aromo  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Maule Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Aromo's Chardonnay from Chile's Maule Valley delivers a classically styled white wine with medium-full body and balanced acidity. The nose opens with green apple and citrus zest, followed by ripe peach and stone fruit on the palate. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and toasted hazelnut notes, while a mineral backbone provides freshness and structure. The wine finishes clean with lingering citrus and subtle spice, making it an approachable yet sophisticated choice for a range of occasions.

name: Domaine de la Motte  Chablis
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chablis with citrus, green apple, and mineral precision—a classic expression of unoaked Chardonnay.
description (EN Store): Domaine de la Motte's Chablis exemplifies the region's signature style: a pure, mineral-driven white built on Chardonnay's natural acidity and restraint. Lemon and green apple dominate the nose, with subtle hazelnut and stone-fruit undertones. The palate is clean and focused, with a distinctive wet-stone minerality that defines the terroir. Minimal oak allows the fruit and mineral character to shine, making this an ideal aperitif or pairing for oysters, grilled fish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Robert Mondavi  Woodbridge Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Napa Valley Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Robert Mondavi Woodbridge Pinot Grigio is a clean, food-friendly white wine from Napa Valley's Oakville region. The wine displays bright citrus and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and pear notes on the palate. Medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a refreshing, crisp finish ideal for warm-weather sipping or pairing with lighter cuisine.

name: Barton&Guestier  Partager Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux white with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing acidity for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Barton&Guestier's Partager Blanc is a medium-bodied white wine from Bordeaux, France, crafted in the classic dry white tradition. Bright citrus and stone fruit dominate the palate, supported by herbaceous notes and mineral undertones that reflect the region's terroir. The wine's medium-high acidity delivers a refreshing, food-friendly character ideal for seafood, fresh salads, and lighter fare.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Art de Vivre Languedoc AOP (Clairette)
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-certified biodynamic Clairette from Languedoc AOP — lunar-calendar harvest, pneumatic press, stainless ferment from the world's largest biodynamic estate network.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand formally built his wine company in 1992 on a foundation laid at Château de Villemajou in Corbières — but the defining commitment came in 2002, when Domaine de Cigalus became the first of what would grow into 17 Demeter-certified biodynamic estates, a scale of conversion unmatched by any producer globally. Art de Vivre draws on Clairette grown across Languedoc AOP's warm Mediterranean basin, where limestone and garrigue-covered hillsides concentrate the grape's naturally low-acid, broad-textured character while strong regional winds reduce disease pressure and eliminate the need for chemical intervention. Vinification follows pneumatic pressing to protect aromatic delicacy, with fermentation conducted in stainless steel to preserve primary fruit and varietal lift — every step timed to the biodynamic lunar calendar that governs all winemaking decisions across the Bertrand group. Clairette is the rare Languedoc white variety that resists malolactic conversion naturally, delivering textural weight without the need for oak or lees manipulation, which makes its appearance here — rather than the house's more common Roussanne-Vermentino-Viognier blends — the distinguishing feature of this cuvée. The current vintage reflects the warm Mediterranean growing season typical of the appellation, with early-drinking appeal and peak expression within 1–2 years of release.

name: Reserve St Martin  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and buttery oak—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Reserve St Martin's Chardonnay from Pays d'Oc delivers a classically styled white wine balancing bright primary fruit with measured oak influence. Green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit notes. On the palate, buttery richness and vanilla oak provide structure and warmth, while underlying minerality adds complexity and length. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at table—equally at home with shellfish, roasted poultry, or cream-based dishes.

name: Satellite  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant grapefruit, herbaceous notes, and mineral edge.
description (EN Store): A classic expression of Marlborough Sauvignon Blanc, this wine showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic intensity. Grapefruit and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by citrus zest and green apple notes that lend freshness and vibrancy. Subtle minerality and grassy undertones add complexity and food-friendly structure. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, fresh cheese, and light Asian cuisine.

name: Domaine de Pellehaut  Harmonie De Gascogne Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Southwest French blend of Chardonnay, Ugni Blanc, Colombard & Manseng—citrus, stone fruit & herbaceous minerality.
description (EN Store):
Domaine de Pellehaut's Harmonie de Gascogne Blanc is a carefully balanced white blend from Southwest France, uniting four complementary varieties into a refreshing, food-friendly expression. The Chardonnay provides structure and subtle oak undertones; Ugni Blanc and Colombard contribute bright acidity and citrus character; Manseng adds stone-fruit depth and a touch of aromatic complexity.


On the palate, expect vibrant citrus zest, green apple, and peach, with a mineral backbone and herbaceous notes that speak to the terroir. The medium body and crisp finish make this an ideal aperitif or pairing wine for seafood, salads, and lighter Mediterranean fare. A versatile, well-crafted bottle that rewards both casual sipping and thoughtful food pairing.

name: Albert Bichot  Domaine Long-Depaquit  Chablis 1er Cru "Fourchaume"
short_description (EN Store): Domaine Long-Depaquit's Fourchaume: Kimmeridgian limestone-clay soils, barrel-fermented Premier Cru Chablis, organic from 2018.
description (EN Store): When Albéric Bichot committed Domaine Long-Depaquit's entire estate to organic viticulture from the 2018 vintage onward, it was a structural declaration — not a marketing gesture — that the 65-hectare Chablis domaine's Kimmeridgian limestone-and-clay soils would be managed without synthetic inputs, a rare commitment at this scale in the appellation. Fourchaume sits on the right bank of the Serein river, its south-southwest-facing slopes of Kimméridgien marl and oyster-fossil limestone delivering the saline, chalky tension that separates Premier Cru Chablis from any other Chardonnay address in Burgundy. The fossil-rich subsoil drives a pronounced mineral salinity and keeps natural acidity high even in warmer years. Vinification follows Long-Depaquit's Premier Cru protocol: barrel fermentation in used French oak, then 11–16 months of aging in a mix of stainless steel and used oak, preserving the site's stony character without imposing wood. New-oak percentage is deliberately kept low — the house philosophy explicitly scales oak to amplify climat character, not to add flavour. What distinguishes this expression is that it is vinified at the Chablis estate itself, not transported to Beaune — Bichot's deliberate policy of estate-proximate vinification means the cold-morning microclimate of the Serein valley is captured in the glass. The 2019 vintage in Chablis delivered generous, sun-ripened fruit with the structural acidity to age; Fourchaume at this stage shows drive and concentration, with a drinking window extending comfortably through 2028–2030.

name: Louis Jadot  Chablis 1er Cru Fourchaume
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Chablis 1er Cru with crisp citrus, green apple, and mineral complexity—a benchmark expression of unoaked Chardonnay.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Chablis 1er Cru Fourchaume is a benchmark expression of Burgundy's most prestigious unoaked Chardonnay terroir. The 2022 vintage showcases the signature mineral-driven profile of Fourchaume's limestone-rich soils, with vibrant lemon and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle hazelnut and stone-fruit undertones. Medium-Full body and crisp acidity frame a wine of classical elegance—neither heavy nor austere—that rewards both immediate enjoyment and careful cellaring. The wet-stone minerality and citrus zest linger on the finish, speaking to the vineyard's geological character.

name: La Chablisienne  Bourgogne Aligote
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Burgundy Chardonnay with green apple, lemon, and mineral notes—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): La Chablisienne's Bourgogne Aligote is a refined expression of Burgundy's classic white wine tradition. This unoaked or lightly oaked Chardonnay showcases bright green apple and lemon zest on the nose, with a mineral-driven palate that reflects the limestone terroir of the Chablis region. Subtle hazelnut and stone fruit notes add complexity without overwhelming the wine's natural elegance. Medium body and crisp acidity make it supremely versatile at the table—equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, fresh cheese, and light vegetable dishes.

name: La Chablisienne  Chablis 1er Cru Fourchaume
short_description (EN Store): Chablis 1er Cru from La Chablisienne: mineral-driven Chardonnay with citrus, apple, and subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): La Chablisienne's Chablis 1er Cru Fourchaume is a classically structured white wine from Burgundy's premier terroir. The 2022 vintage expresses the signature mineral precision of unoaked and lightly oaked Chardonnay, with dominant lemon and green apple on the nose, supported by hazelnut and subtle oak influence. Medium-Full body and bracing acidity define the palate, while wet stone and citrus zest notes evolve on the finish, revealing the limestone-rich character of the Fourchaume climat. A versatile food wine suited to seafood, cream sauces, and aged cheeses.

name: La Chablisienne  Chablis 1er Cru Montmains

name: la Chablisienne  Chateau Grenouille Chablis
short_description (EN Store): Unoaked Chablis from La Chablisienne: crisp lemon, green apple, hazelnut minerality. Château Grenouille 2020.

The wine's medium-full body and crisp acidity make it versatile at table—equally at home with raw oysters, grilled white fish, or creamy risotto.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Naturae Orange
short_description (EN Store): No-sulfite Languedoc orange wine: 10–15-day skin contact on Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc & Viognier, then amphora-aged — biodynamic, zero inputs.
description (EN Store):
Gérard Bertrand built his Naturae range around a single constraint: zero added sulfites, certified organic, and every decision timed to the biodynamic lunar calendar — a discipline applied across all 17 Demeter-certified estates in Languedoc-Roussillon. Naturae Orange draws from the house's warm Mediterranean terroir, where strong coastal winds reduce disease pressure and allow grapes to reach full phenolic maturity without chemical intervention — the prerequisite for a stable no-sulfite wine.

The blend — Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc, Viognier, Marsanne, Mauzac, Muscat, and Clairette — undergoes whole-bunch or semi-carbonic skin maceration for 10–15 days, treating white varieties with the same extraction logic as a red wine. Post-maceration, the wine moves first to oak vat for 2–3 weeks, then into terracotta amphora for ongoing micro-oxygenation and tannin integration — a vessel choice that softens grip without adding oak character.

What separates this from most entry-level orange wines is the amphora finishing stage: where peers typically rack to stainless steel, Bertrand uses unglazed terracotta to allow slow, even oxygen exchange, building textural complexity without the reductive edge common in no-sulfite skin-contact wines. The result is a structured, amber-hued white with phenolic grip, dried-apricot depth, and the saline mineral thread characteristic of sun-drenched Languedoc fruit. Drink now through 2–3 years from vintage.

name: Gerard Bertrand  Orange Gold
short_description (EN Store): Demeter-biodynamic orange wine from Languedoc — skin-macerated 10–15 days, zero sulfites, amphora-aged for tannin integration.
description (EN Store): Gérard Bertrand built his orange wine program as a deliberate provocation: the world's largest Demeter-certified biodynamic producer applying red-wine vinification logic to white and aromatic Languedoc varieties — no added sulfites, no compromise. The blend draws from across the Languedoc-Roussillon estate network, assembling Chardonnay, Grenache Blanc, Viognier, Marsanne, Mauzac, Muscat, and Clairette — varieties whose warm Mediterranean soils and strong coastal winds (reducing disease pressure and concentrating phenolics) make extended skin contact viable without chemical intervention. Whole-bunch or semi-carbonic maceration runs 10–15 days with skins and seeds in contact, extracting tannin, colour, and aromatic compounds in the manner of a red vinification. The wine then moves through oak vat aging of 2–3 weeks followed by terracotta amphora aging, the porous clay delivering regular micro-oxygenation that integrates grippy seed tannins without adding oak character. What no peer at this scale does: zero-sulfite orange wine produced under full Demeter biodynamic certification, every decision — harvest date to bottling — governed by the lunar calendar. The current vintage delivers the textural density of skin contact tempered by Languedoc aromatic generosity; drink now through 3–4 years for peak phenolic integration.

name: Batonis Kari  Tsinandali White Dry
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Georgian white blend of Rkatsitli and green grapes—citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness.
description (EN Store): Batonis Kari Tsinandali is a traditional Georgian white wine from the Kakheti region, blending 80% Rkatsitli with 20% green grapes. This field blend showcases the characteristic freshness and mineral-driven profile of the Tsinandali terroir, with vibrant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous notes. The medium body and brisk acidity make it an ideal aperitif and food-friendly companion to seafood, Asian cuisine, and light Mediterranean fare.

Bone-dry and elegant, it serves as an ideal aperitif and pairs beautifully with seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh vegetable dishes.

name: Merk  Riesling Kabinett Dry
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Pfalz Riesling with bright stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision. Dry, refreshing, food-friendly.

name: Ulrich Langguth  Riesling Quality Off Dry

name: Nik Weis  Goldtröpfchen Riesling VDP.GRAND CRU (GROSSES GEWÄCHS)

name: Nik Weis  Goldtröpfchen Riesling Spätlese
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Mosel Riesling Spätlese with ripe stone fruit, slate minerality, and crisp acidity—a classic expression of Saar terroir.

A versatile food wine suited to both casual aperitif service and thoughtful pairing with seafood, Asian cuisine, and delicate cheeses.

name: Nik Weis  Mosel Dry Riesling VDP.ESTATE WINES (GUTSWEINE)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Mosel Riesling with stone fruit, citrus zest, and mineral slate—elegant dry expression from the Saar.

name: Richland  Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Australian Muscat with floral and stone fruit charm—refreshing, off-dry, and food-friendly.

name: Belena  Moscato
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Australian Muscat with floral, lychee, and stone-fruit notes. Refreshing, off-dry, and food-friendly.

A touch of honey and tropical character adds complexity, while medium-high acidity provides freshness and balance. Ideal as an apéritif or paired with Asian cuisine, fruit desserts, and soft cheeses.

name: Belena  Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Australian Pinot Grigio with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.

Medium body and vibrant acidity make this wine an ideal aperitif or pairing for grilled fish, oysters, and fresh salads. name: Abbotts & Delaunay  Sauvignon Pay D'Oc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Languedoc Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, green apple, and herbaceous minerality—refreshing and food-friendly.

On the palate, stone fruit and tropical hints provide depth without heaviness, while crisp acidity and a clean finish make this an ideal aperitif or food companion. A versatile, well-balanced white suited to seafood, fresh cheese, and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Louis Jadot  Mâcon-Villages Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Chardonnay with bright citrus, green apple, and hazelnut—a versatile white for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Mâcon-Villages Blanc is a classic expression of unoaked to lightly oaked Burgundy Chardonnay, showcasing the region's mineral-driven white wine tradition. name: Louis Jadot  Coteaux Bourguignons Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Burgundy Chardonnay with citrus, apple, and hazelnut—versatile white for seafood, cream dishes, and poultry.
description (EN Store): Louis Jadot's Coteaux Bourguignons Blanc is a refined expression of Chardonnay from Burgundy's classic terroir. A versatile white suited to grilled fish, shellfish preparations, cream-based pasta, and roasted poultry.

name: Agua Santa  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with citrus, green apple, and herbaceous notes—refreshing and mineral-driven.

A versatile expression suited to seafood, fresh cheeses, and warm-weather entertaining.

name: Massolino  Langhe DOC Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Piedmont Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, buttery oak, and mineral precision—a refined white for seafood and cream dishes.

name: William Fevre  Saint-Bris

name: Mr.Riggs  'Watervale' Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp McLaren Vale Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision. High acidity and elegant restraint.

name: Messenez Family  Chateau M Grand Vin Villes Vignes Chardonnay

name: Costera  White (Chardonnay) (Fruit Wine Blend) (Screw Cap)
short_description (EN Store): Chilean Chardonnay with stone fruit, citrus, and buttery oak—versatile white for seafood, poultry, and cream sauces.

name: Bosquet des Papes Chateauneuf-du-Pape Tradition Blanc

name: Chateau Maris  Viognier Grenache (Organic)
short_description (EN Store): Organic Viognier-Grenache blend from Languedoc with stone fruit, citrus, and floral notes. Refreshing and versatile.
description (EN Store): A Languedoc white blend pairing Viognier's signature stone fruit and floral character with Grenache Blanc's mineral backbone and herbaceous complexity. name: Domaine De La Cornasse  Chablis

name: La Valentina  Trebbiano d’Abruzzo DOC

name: Luigi Bosca  De Sangre White Blend

name: Adulation  Chardonnay

Refreshing and food-friendly, it exemplifies the elegant, restrained style that defines quality Chablis.

name: Domaine du Chardonnay  Chablis ler Cru Montmain
short_description (EN Store): Chablis Premier Cru from Montmain: crisp Chardonnay with mineral precision, citrus brightness, and subtle oak complexity.
description (EN Store): Domaine du Chardonnay's Chablis Premier Cru Montmain is a benchmark expression of unoaked and lightly oaked Chardonnay from Burgundy's northernmost white-wine region. The 2022 vintage showcases the terroir's signature mineral-driven profile: bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with a palate of lemon, stone fruit, and wet-stone minerality. Subtle secondary notes of butter and hazelnut suggest restrained oak aging, adding textural depth without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity and freshness. The finish is clean and persistent, with a saline quality characteristic of Chablis Premier Cru sites.

name: Domaine du Chardonnay  Chablis Les Lys
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, mineral-driven Chablis showcasing pure Chardonnay with citrus and stone fruit on a refreshing, elegant frame.
description (EN Store): Domaine du Chardonnay's Chablis Les Lys is a classic expression of unoaked Chardonnay from Burgundy's northernmost white wine region. The 2022 vintage delivers bright citrus and green apple on the nose, with a distinctive mineral backbone characteristic of Chablis terroir. Medium body and vibrant acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile that showcases the purity of the grape without oak influence. Subtle stone fruit and floral notes emerge on the palate, finishing with a clean, saline minerality that defines the appellation's signature style.

name: Louis Latour  Macon-Lugny Les Genievres
short_description (EN Store): Mâcon-Lugny négociant Chardonnay from Louis Latour, bottled under Diam 30 since 2022 — Burgundy's oldest family house, founded 1797.
description (EN Store): Jean Latour's family had been growing vines since 1731, but it was the house's négociant reach — spanning every major Burgundy appellation — that made Louis Latour the largest single Grand Cru owner in the Côte d'Or. Mâcon-Lugny Les Génièvres sits at the southern end of that network, drawing fruit from the limestone-clay soils of the Lugny plateau in the Mâconnais, where the continental climate and moderate elevation produce Chardonnay with naturally higher acidity and leaner fruit weight than the Côte de Beaune. Vinification follows the house's white Burgundy protocol: grapes are crushed before pressing, fermentation initiated in stainless steel, then the wine is racked and settled before bottling in December–February — a reductive, freshness-preserving approach that contrasts sharply with the 100% new-oak, barrel-fermented treatment reserved for Corton-Charlemagne. From the 2022 vintage onward, the wine is sealed under Diam 30 closure, a house-wide shift that eliminates cork taint risk across the entire range. What distinguishes this expression within its Mâconnais peer group is its provenance within a négociant system that air-dries its own French oak staves for up to 36 months in-house — discipline that filters down in philosophy even where oak is absent. No vintage-specific critic scores are available for this cuvée in the brand library.

name: Louis Latour  Genevrieres Meursault 1er Cru
short_description (EN Store): Genevrières 1er Cru: Meursault's deepest clay-limestone pocket, fermented and aged in French oak with no lees-stirring — Louis Latour's textural, opulent white Burgundy signature.
description (EN Store):
A family whose vine ownership on the Côte d'Or traces to 1731 brings a particular conviction to Meursault: Louis Latour's whites are fermented without settling, transferred hot into 228-litre French oak pièces to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation, then aged with no lees-stirring — a deliberate departure from the bâtonnage-heavy style that defines most Meursault producers. The result is texture built from oak integration rather than from stirred lees, giving the wine a denser, more resinous weight than its neighbours.

Les Genevrières sits on Meursault's mid-slope, where clay content in the limestone bedrock is at its most pronounced — the heaviest soils in the appellation's Premier Cru band. That clay retains moisture through dry seasons and slows ripening, producing Chardonnay with deeper concentration and a broader aromatic register than the lighter, more mineral Perrières directly above. Fermentation begins in stainless steel before transfer hot to barrel, preserving primary fruit while the oak regime — barrels coopered in-house from French oak air-dried 24–36 months — adds structure without dominating.

The 2018 vintage delivered outstanding balance of volume and freshness across the Côte de Beaune, the fifth consecutive fine white Burgundy harvest, and was approachable young without sacrificing mid-term development. What separates this expression categorically: Louis Latour applies the same no-lees-stirring protocol here that it uses for Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru — an unusually austere, oak-primary approach for a Premier Cru Meursault that most houses reserve only for their highest tier.

name: Louis Latour  Puligny-Montrachet
short_description (EN Store): Négociant Puligny-Montrachet from Burgundy's most storied white-wine house — fermented in French oak with no lees-stirring, 2022 vintage.
description (EN Store): When the 5th-generation Louis Latour replanted phylloxera-devastated hillsides with Chardonnay after 1891, he was making a bet on limestone that would define Côte de Beaune white winemaking for the next century — the same limestone-driven philosophy that runs through every white in the Latour portfolio, including this Puligny-Montrachet. Puligny's soils are characteristically shallow, free-draining limestone and clay over hard Bathonian bedrock, producing Chardonnay of taut mineral tension and slow phenolic development — conditions that reward the house's deliberately non-interventionist white-wine approach. Grapes are crushed before pressing, fermentation initiated in stainless steel without settling, then transferred hot into 228-litre French oak pièces — made in-house at the Louis Latour cooperage from oak air-dried 24–36 months — to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation. No lees-stirring is practised, a deliberate counter-move to the bâtonnage-heavy style common across the Côte de Beaune, producing texture from oak integration rather than autolytic richness. The wine is racked to stainless steel post-harvest, settled naturally, and bottled December–February; from 2022, sealed under Diam 30 closure — the first vintage to carry this change. The 2022 growing season delivered vivid natural acidity alongside generous fruit concentration, making it one of the more structured recent Puligny expressions from this house.

name: Louis Latour  Puligny-Montrachet Chalumeaux 1er
short_description (EN Store): Puligny 1er Cru Les Chalumeaux 2020: Latour's négociant-éleveur craft on a cool, tight vintage — fermented and finished in French oak, no lees-stirring.
description (EN Store): Louis Latour's roots in Puligny-Montrachet run through the same négociant-éleveur philosophy that drove the 5th-generation Louis Latour to replant phylloxera-devastated hillsides with Chardonnay after 1891 — a house that has shaped Côte de Beaune white Burgundy for over two centuries now in its 11th generation. Les Chalumeaux sits on the eastern flank of Puligny-Montrachet, where shallow, well-drained limestone-and-clay soils over Bathonian bedrock concentrate tension and mineral precision rather than volume. The 2020 growing season delivered cool nights and intense sunlight, producing grapes with bold structure, steeliness, and concentrated spice — conditions that reward patience over early drinking. Vinification follows the house's white Burgundy signature: grapes crushed before pressing, fermentation initiated in stainless steel without settling, then transferred hot into 228-litre French oak pièces — made in-house at the Louis Latour cooperage from oak air-dried 24–36 months — to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation; no lees-stirring at any stage, a deliberate choice that preserves primary fruit definition over creamy autolytic texture. What distinguishes this house approach from most Côte de Beaune peers is precisely that absence of bâtonnage combined with in-house barrel production, yielding a structured, oak-integrated style rather than a lees-driven one. The 2020 vintage across Louis Latour's Côte de Beaune whites is characterised by intensity and tight architecture — drink 2025–2032.

name: Louis Latour  Chevalier-Montrachet Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Chevalier-Montrachet 'Les Demoiselles' (0.51 ha): 100% new in-house oak, no lees-stirring — a racy, almondy 2017 from Burgundy's most storied white Grand Cru.
description (EN Store): Louis Latour's holding in Chevalier-Montrachet is a single parcel of just 0.51 hectares, named 'Les Demoiselles' — one of the most precisely bounded plots on the Côte de Beaune, sitting above Puligny-Montrachet on a steep, east-facing escarpment where thin, free-draining limestone soils over fractured rock force Chardonnay roots deep and keep yields brutally low. That skeletal terroir is the engine behind the wine's structural tension. In the cellar, Louis Latour applies the same uncompromising regime used for Corton-Charlemagne: grapes are crushed before pressing, fermentation begins in stainless steel without settling, then the must is transferred hot into 100% new, in-house-made French oak pièces (228 L) to complete both alcoholic and malolactic fermentation — with no lees-stirring at any stage. The barrels are built at the Louis Latour cooperage from French oak air-dried for up to 36 months, toasted light-long (45 minutes), giving a precise, integrated oak signature rather than a dominant one. What sets this expression apart within the Grand Cru tier is that refusal to stir: where most white Burgundy producers use bâtonnage to build texture and buffer acidity, Latour lets the wine find its own line — the result in 2017 is notably racy and fine, with a lightly almondy character and a polished, taut frame. The 2017 vintage on the Côte de Beaune delivered a lighter, more tensile style than the opulent 2016 or 2018; Jancis Robinson scored it 17/20, with a drinking window of 2023–2032.

name: Chavin Zero Non-Alcoholic Chardonnay (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Languedoc Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and toasted oak—a versatile white for seafood and cream sauces.
description (EN Store): This Languedoc Chardonnay delivers a classic expression of the varietal, balancing bright green apple and citrus aromatics with the warmth of toasted oak and subtle butter notes. Medium-Full in body with restrained acidity, it shows stone fruit depth and a mineral finish that speaks to the region's terroir. Ideal for grilled fish, shellfish preparations, and cream-based pasta dishes.

name: Xige Estate  Jade Dove White (Welschriesling, Ugni Blanc)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Ningxia white blend of Welschriesling & Ugni Blanc—citrus, stone fruit, mineral finish. Refreshing & food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Xige Estate's Jade Dove White is a carefully balanced blend of Welschriesling and Ugni Blanc from Ningxia's Qingtongxia subregion. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by delicate stone fruit and subtle floral aromatics. Medium body and crisp, medium-high acidity create a refreshing palate that finishes with mineral precision. This is a versatile white suited to seafood, Asian cuisine, and casual entertaining.

name: Donnafugata  D&G Isolano Erna Bianco DOC
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Grillo with bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—crisp and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Donnafugata's Isolano Erna Bianco is a single-varietal Grillo from Sicily's DOC region, showcasing the characteristic brightness and minerality of this Mediterranean white. The 2021 vintage displays dominant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit and tropical undertones. description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Classic Sauvignon Blanc is a light-bodied white wine from Australia's Barossa Valley, delivering bright acidity and fresh aromatics. description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Roero Arneis Pradalupo DOCG is a refined expression of Piedmont's signature white varietal. description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Gavi del Comune di Gavi DOCG is a single-varietal expression of Cortese, the signature white grape of Piedmont's Gavi zone. name: Fontanafredda  Derthona Timorasso Colli Tortonesi DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Piedmont white from rare Timorasso grape—citrus, stone fruit, mineral complexity. Elegant aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Fontanafredda's Derthona Timorasso represents a distinctive expression of Piedmont's lesser-known white varieties. Timorasso, cultivated in the Colli Tortonesi hills, yields a wine of medium body and pronounced acidity, marked by bright citrus zest and stone-fruit character on the nose. This is a food-friendly white suited to grilled seafood, Mediterranean preparations, and fresh vegetable dishes.

name: Casarena Estate Torrontes

description (EN Store): Casarena Estate Torrontes is a vibrant white wine from Mendoza's Luján de Cuyo subregion, showcasing the distinctive character of Argentina's signature white varietal. The wine opens with pronounced citrus zest and peach, supported by delicate floral aromatics and a mineral backbone. On the palate, green apple and herbaceous notes provide freshness and complexity, while medium-high acidity delivers a clean, crisp finish. This is a versatile, food-friendly expression ideal for warm-weather drinking and pairing with Asian cuisine, seafood, and fresh preparations.

name: Domaines Albert Bichot  Secret de Famille Bourgogne Cote d'Or Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): 2021 Côte d'Or Chardonnay from Bichot's Beaune négociant house — cool-vintage precision, 80% stainless/20% used oak, linear minerality.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot's family presence in Burgundy stretches to 1350, yet the structural decision that defines this wine is modern and deliberate: regional Bourgogne is vinified at the Beaune central winery using the same gravity-racking, non-interventionist protocol applied to the house's Grand Cru estates — a refusal to treat entry-level appellations as an afterthought. The Bourgogne Côte d'Or AOC draws fruit from partner growers across the limestone-and-clay corridor of the Côte de Beaune, where shallow topsoils over Jurassic limestone bedrock concentrate Chardonnay's tension and restrict vine vigour. Fermentation is split: approximately 80% stainless steel to preserve primary fruit and acidity, 20% in used French oak barrels (Tronçais and Allier, light toast) to add textural weight without imposing wood character. Aging runs 11–16 months, with the stainless component retaining the cool vintage's naturally high acidity intact. The 2021 growing season — marked by spring frost, disease pressure, and sharply reduced yields — produced Chardonnay of exceptional linear precision across Burgundy, and this cuvée reflects that: taut, mineral-driven, with the kind of structural clarity that over-ripe years cannot replicate. Drink 2024–2028.

name: Domaines Albert Bichot  Saint-Veran
short_description (EN Store): 2021 Saint-Véran Chardonnay from Bichot's Mâconnais sourcing — cool-vintage precision, linear minerality, stainless-led vinification.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot's structural commitment to decentralised vinification — each wine made at the estate closest to its source — extends to its Mâconnais sourcing, where the 2021 Saint-Véran is handled with the same terroir-fidelity logic applied across all six of the house's estate wineries. Saint-Véran sits on Jurassic limestone and clay soils in the southern Mâconnais, where the calcareous subsoil drives the chalky, taut character that separates it from the richer, more voluptuous Pouilly-Fuissé to its north. The 2021 growing season — marked by spring frost, disease pressure, and sharply reduced yields — compressed the fruit and amplified the appellation's natural acidity, producing a Chardonnay of linear minerality and high acidity rather than the broader, fruit-forward profile of warmer years. Vinification follows Bichot's regional-white protocol: predominantly stainless steel fermentation and aging, with a proportion of used oak (approximately 20%) to add textural weight without obscuring the limestone-driven freshness. No new oak is deployed at this appellation level, preserving the site's cool-climate precision. What distinguishes this expression is its vintage-driven austerity — 2021 Saint-Véran behaves closer to a Chablis in structure than to its Mâconnais peers, a consequence of the season rather than a stylistic choice. Drink 2024–2027 for maximum tension; the acidity will soften marginally with another year of bottle age.

name: Domaines Albert Bichot  Pouilly Fuisse
short_description (EN Store): Négociant Chardonnay from Pouilly-Fuissé, 2022's hot harvest delivering full-bodied, aromatic whites with medium-term structure — vinified at the estate closest to source.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot's structural commitment to decentralised vinification — each of its six estate wineries processes only the wines of its own sub-region — means this Pouilly-Fuissé is handled at the facility geographically closest to the Mâconnais source, not consolidated into a Beaune production line as most large négociants would do. Pouilly-Fuissé sits on a complex mosaic of Triassic limestone and clay soils beneath the dramatic rock faces of Vergisson and Solutré, elevations reaching 400 metres, where diurnal temperature swings preserve aromatic tension even in warm years. The 2022 growing season delivered an early, hot harvest; fermentation in stainless steel tanks with a proportion of used oak barrels captures the vintage's generous, aromatic fruit while the used-oak component adds textural weight without imposing new-wood character. Aging runs 11–16 months in a split of stainless steel (dominant) and used French oak, consistent with Bichot's regional white protocol of approximately 80% steel to 20% used barrel. What distinguishes this expression is Bichot's deliberate refusal to centralise: the micro-terroir fidelity of a Mâconnais Chardonnay is preserved through proximity of vinification, not just sourcing. The 2022 vintage character — ripe, full-bodied, and aromatic — is approachable now but carries sufficient limestone-driven structure for three to five years of further development.

name: Fantini  Pinot Grigio Terre Siciliane
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Abruzzo with citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing and food-friendly.

name: Fantini Calalenta Pecorino Terre di Chieti IGT

name: Maison Jaffelin  Saint-Veran Blanc

name: Maison Jaffelin  Chablis

name: Joseph Mellot  Sancere La Chatellenie

name: Joseph Mellot  Pouilly Fume Le Tronsec

The off-dry profile adds subtle sweetness that complements both delicate seafood and bold flavors without overwhelming the palate.

name: Karthäuserhof  Bruno Riesling Dry
short_description (EN Store): Dry Mosel Riesling with vibrant citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Karthäuserhof's Bruno Riesling Dry represents the classic dry expression of Germany's most versatile white varietal. From the Mosel region, this 2021 vintage delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone-fruit notes of peach and lime. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with a clean, refreshing finish characteristic of quality dry Riesling. Medium body and medium-high acidity make this wine exceptionally food-friendly, particularly suited to seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations.

name: Karthäuserhof Bruno Riesling Kabinett Feinherb
short_description (EN Store): Mosel Riesling Kabinett with bright citrus, stone fruit, and floral notes. Off-dry, refreshing, and versatile with Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Karthäuserhof's 2021 Bruno Riesling Kabinett Feinherb is a classic Mosel expression showcasing the region's signature elegance. The wine opens with vibrant green apple and citrus zest, supported by delicate stone-fruit and floral aromatics. On the palate, medium body and crisp acidity deliver refreshment, while subtle honey and minerality add complexity. The off-dry Feinherb style (semi-dry) balances residual sweetness with acidity, making it exceptionally food-friendly and approachable as an aperitif or paired with Asian cuisine, seafood, and light salads.

name: Tavernello Vino Bianco d’Italia Amabile
short_description (EN Store): Approachable Italian white blend with stone fruit, citrus, and subtle honey—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Tavernello's Vino Bianco d'Italia Amabile is a lightly sweet, medium-bodied white blend from Emilia-Romagna, crafted from Malvasia Bianca, Moscatel, and Trebbiano. The wine opens with stone fruit and citrus aromatics, supported by delicate floral and honey notes. Medium-high acidity provides freshness and balance, making it an approachable, versatile table wine suited to casual dining and warm-weather enjoyment.

name: Ruppertsber Riesling Dry Pfalz
short_description (EN Store): Dry Riesling from Pfalz with vibrant citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Crisp, refreshing, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): A classic dry Riesling from Germany's Pfalz region, this wine showcases the varietal's signature balance of fruit-forward character and mineral precision. Aromas of citrus zest, green apple, and white peach lead to a palate marked by crisp acidity and subtle stone fruit flavors. The dry style emphasizes freshness and versatility, making it an excellent choice for aperitifs, light seafood, and spicy Asian cuisine.

name: Santa Sofia Pinot Grigio Grada DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Veneto with bright citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Santa Sofia Pinot Grigio Grada DOC is a classic expression of northeastern Italy's most celebrated white varietal. Sourced from the Veneto region, this wine delivers the hallmark freshness and mineral precision expected of the style. Bright citrus zest and green apple dominate the nose, with supporting stone fruit notes that carry through to a clean, dry palate. The medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or versatile food wine, particularly suited to seafood, fresh salads, and light pasta dishes.

name: Voga Italia  Pinot Grigio
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Pinot Grigio from Veneto with bright citrus, green apple, and mineral notes. Refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Voga Italia's Pinot Grigio from Italy's Veneto region delivers the classic profile of this beloved white varietal: a light-bodied wine with vibrant medium-high acidity and a clean, refreshing character. On the nose, citrus and green apple dominate, supported by subtle herbaceous and mineral notes. The palate is crisp and dry, with stone fruit undertones and a mineral finish that lingers pleasantly. This is an approachable, food-friendly white ideal for warm-weather drinking and versatile table service.

name: Concha Y Toro  Terrunyo Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Sauvignon Blanc from Chile's Maule Valley—zesty citrus and herbaceous notes with bright minerality.
description (EN Store): Concha y Toro's Terrunyo Sauvignon Blanc is a vibrant expression of Chile's Maule Valley terroir. Bright citrus zest and herbaceous character dominate the palate, supported by green apple and subtle tropical notes. The wine's high acidity and mineral backbone make it an ideal aperitif and food companion, particularly with oysters, grilled fish, and fresh cheeses.

name: Tornatore  Etna Bianco
short_description (EN Store): Etna Bianco from Carricante: mineral-driven Sicilian white with citrus zest, green apple, and saline complexity.
description (EN Store): Tornatore's Etna Bianco is a single-varietal expression of Carricante, the signature white grape of Mount Etna's volcanic slopes. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, underpinned by pronounced minerality and subtle herbaceous notes that reflect the wine's terroir. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an elegant aperitif and a versatile food companion, particularly suited to seafood, Mediterranean dishes, and lighter preparations.

name: Tornatore  Etna Bianco Pietrarizzo
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Sicilian white from Etna's volcanic slopes: citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision in a refreshing, food-friendly frame.
description (EN Store):
Tornatore Etna Bianco Pietrarizzo is a single-varietal expression of Carricante, the signature white grape of Mount Etna's northern slopes. Grown on the volcanic soils of the Pietrarizzo cru, this wine captures the mineral intensity and aromatic precision characteristic of high-altitude Sicilian viticulture.


On the nose, citrus zest and green apple lead, followed by subtle stone-fruit notes and a distinctive saline minerality that reflects the wine's volcanic provenance. The palate is crisp and focused, with medium body and bracing acidity that drives a clean, refreshing finish. Floral undertones add elegance without obscuring the wine's core identity.


A versatile food wine suited to seafood, Mediterranean cuisine, and aperitif service.

name: Fly By  Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): California Chardonnay with bright citrus, apple, and oak-driven complexity—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Fly By Chardonnay represents California's approachable take on this classic white varietal. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus aromatics, supported by medium acidity that keeps the palate fresh and food-friendly. Oak influence introduces subtle vanilla and butter notes, while the medium-full body provides enough weight for richer preparations without overwhelming delicate seafood. Stone fruit and hazelnut undertones add complexity, making this a versatile choice for both casual dining and more refined pairings.

name: District Series Russian River Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Sonoma Chardonnay with ripe apple, citrus, and oak-driven complexity—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Precision's District Series Russian River Chardonnay showcases the classic elegance of Sonoma's premier white wine region. Ripe green apple and bright citrus dominate the palate, complemented by creamy oak influence and subtle vanilla notes. Medium-Full body and balanced acidity make this wine versatile at the table, equally at home with shellfish, roasted poultry, or cream-based pasta dishes. A well-crafted expression of California Chardonnay tradition.

name: Corryton Burge  South Australia Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Eden Valley Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived richness. Elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Corryton Burge's South Australia Chardonnay from Eden Valley showcases the region's cool-climate character through bright green apple and citrus aromatics balanced by creamy oak influence. The medium-full body and restrained acidity create a wine of elegance and versatility, with secondary notes of butter and vanilla adding textural depth. Minerality and subtle hazelnut in the finish suggest careful winemaking and aging potential. A refined expression suited to premium dining and special occasions.

name: Dr Loosen Erdener Treppchen Riesling Kabinett
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Mosel Riesling Kabinett with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral precision—refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Dr Loosen's Erdener Treppchen Riesling Kabinett is a classic expression of the Mosel's terroir, showcasing the region's signature balance of fruit, acidity, and minerality. Light-bodied and crisp, this wine opens with green apple and citrus zest, followed by delicate stone fruit and floral notes. The palate is clean and refreshing, with a subtle honey undertone and mineral finish that lingers gracefully. Kabinett classification signals a naturally sweet entry point—ideal for those seeking elegance without heaviness.

name: Wairau River  Summer Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Marlborough Riesling with citrus, stone fruit, and mineral freshness—perfect for seafood and spicy Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Wairau River's Summer Riesling from Marlborough showcases the region's signature bright acidity and aromatic character. This single-varietal expression delivers vibrant citrus zest and stone fruit on the nose, with green apple and subtle floral notes adding complexity. On the palate, medium body and medium-high acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile with a clean mineral finish. Ideal as an aperitif or paired with seafood, Asian cuisine, and spiced dishes.

name: Maison Leroy Meursault 2000
short_description (EN Store): Lalou Bize-Leroy's blind-selection Meursault, bottled unfined and unfiltered, released only at her personal judgment of peak readiness — 25 years on.
description (EN Store):
Founded in 1868 by François Leroy in Auxey-Duresses — a village sharing its southern boundary with Meursault itself — Maison Leroy operates on a principle that inverts négociant convention: Lalou Bize-Leroy tastes hundreds of wines blind en primeur each year, buying only the parcels that meet her threshold, then holds them in her Auxey-Duresses cellars until she alone judges them ready for release. The 2000 vintage reaches the market not on a commercial schedule but on Lalou's terms.

Meursault's limestone-rich soils — Bathonian and Oxfordian marl interlaced with clay — retain moisture through dry summers while forcing Chardonnay roots deep for mineral extraction. The village's east-facing slopes moderate afternoon heat, preserving the tension that defines the appellation.

Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts in wooden vats, temperatures permitted to climb toward 33°C for full extraction. Élevage follows in François Frères and Cadus barrels, the wine resting on its lees through racking cycles before an unfined, unfiltered bottling preserves every textural nuance the parcel delivered. Sorting at harvest matches picker-to-sorter ratios, ensuring only physiologically complete fruit enters the vat.

What separates this from any other village Meursault is the cellar-release philosophy: a 2000 vintage offered in its third decade is not a curiosity but a deliberate statement — Lalou's conviction that the wine is only now expressing what she purchased.
