The 2000 Côte de Beaune growing season delivered generous ripeness tempered by a cool August, producing whites of weight and freshness simultaneously. At 25 years, expect a wine in its secondary plateau: lanolin, toasted hazelnut, and beeswax layered over a still-present saline spine. Drink through 2032.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Puligny-Montrachet 1er Cru "Les Pucelles" (Magnum) (1.5 L)
short_description (EN Store): Les Pucelles 2021 in magnum: Pierre-Vincent Girardin's 456-litre barrel regime and zero bâtonnage deliver electric Puligny tension, bottled unfined on the lunar calendar.
description (EN Store):
Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian family — and immediately broke from the richer, oak-forward Meursault tradition by fermenting whites in custom 456-litre barrels (double the Burgundy-standard 228-litre pièce), a deliberate engineering choice to halve wood-to-wine contact and keep terroir signal dominant over cooperage influence.

Les Pucelles sits on the southern flank of Puligny-Montrachet's premier cru belt, its Jurassic marly limestone soils at roughly 260 m elevation producing Chardonnay of pronounced salinity and tight mineral architecture — conditions that reward Girardin's low-interventionist hand far more than a richer, bâtonnage-driven style would allow.

Indigenous yeast fermentation proceeds in those oversized François Frères / Seguin-Moreau barrels at 30% new oak, with no bâtonnage and minimal sulphur additions; 12 months on fine lees in barrel are followed by 6 months in stainless-steel tank before bottling. The wine is released unfined and unfiltered, with bottling dates timed to the lunar calendar.

The magnum format is the special feature: the larger volume-to-surface ratio slows oxidative exchange through the cork, preserving the crunchy, electric acidity that defines this cuvée for a measurably longer arc than a standard 750 ml.

The 2021 vintage — ravaged by spring frost across Burgundy, yields severely curtailed — produced whites of exceptional concentration and nerve; the reduced crop load intensified the site's characteristic tension. Drink 2026–2038.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Meursault 1er Cru "Les Charmes"
short_description (EN Store): Meursault Les Charmes 1er Cru from a 13th-generation prodigy: 456-litre barrels, zero bâtonnage, unfined — electric mineral purity over Côte de Beaune richness.
description (EN Store): Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian winemaking family — and immediately broke from Meursault's butter-and-oak tradition by fermenting his whites in custom 456-litre barrels (double the Burgundy-standard 228-litre pièce), a deliberate engineering choice to halve the wood-to-wine contact ratio and keep terroir signal clean. Les Charmes sits on the mid-slope of Meursault at approximately 260 m elevation, on Jurassic marls and marly limestones with east-to-south exposure — soils that deliver structural tension and a saline, chalky mineral thread rather than the fat, glyceric texture the appellation's reputation often implies. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts in those oversized François Frères / Seguin-Moreau barrels; no bâtonnage is performed, preserving the wine's angular freshness; 30% new oak is used at village level and above, followed by 6 months in stainless steel before bottling unfined and unfiltered on the lunar calendar. The refusal of bâtonnage in a premier cru Meursault — where lees-stirring is near-universal practice — is the defining transgression: where peers build creaminess, Girardin builds tension. The 2022 vintage in Burgundy delivered generous ripeness tempered by a cool August, producing whites with vivid acidity and mid-weight concentration — a strong fit for Girardin's precision-first house style; drink 2025–2032.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Meursault 1er Cru "Les Perrieres"
short_description (EN Store): Meursault's most electric 1er Cru from a 13th-gen prodigy: 456L barrels, zero bâtonnage, unfined — Les Perrières stripped to pure stone.
description (EN Store): Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian winemaking family — and immediately broke with the richer, more oak-driven Meursault tradition his own lineage helped define. Les Perrières sits on the mid-slope of Meursault at approximately 260 m elevation, its name derived from the ancient limestone quarries beneath: Jurassic marly limestone that drains sharply, stresses the vine into concentration, and drives the cru's signature flinty, almost saline tension rather than the buttery weight of lower-slope Meursault. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts in custom 456-litre barrels — double the volume of Burgundy's standard 228-litre pièce — a deliberate engineering choice that halves the wood-to-wine surface ratio and keeps oak influence subordinate to terroir. No bâtonnage is performed, preserving the wine's vertical, mineral line rather than building textural richness; 12 months in barrel at 30% new oak are followed by 6 months in stainless steel before bottling unfined and unfiltered on the lunar calendar. The special feature: in a village historically celebrated for opulence, Girardin produces what is arguably Meursault's most architecturally austere premier cru — closer in spirit to Chablis Grand Cru than to a Roulot Charmes. The 2022 vintage in Burgundy delivered generous but well-framed fruit with natural acidity intact, lending Les Perrières its characteristic taut, quarry-stone core alongside riper stone-fruit weight.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Corton-Charlemagne Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Pierre-Vincent Girardin's Corton-Charlemagne fermented in custom 456-litre barrels — half the wood contact of Burgundy's standard pièce — for crystalline grand cru tension.
description (EN Store): Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian winemaking family — and immediately broke with the richer, oak-driven Meursault tradition by commissioning custom 456-litre barrels (François Frères / Seguin-Moreau), double the volume of the region's standard 228-litre pièce, to halve wood-to-wine contact on his whites. Corton-Charlemagne sits at the hill's upper slope on the communes of Aloxe-Corton and Pernand-Vergelesses, where thin, iron-rich limestone and marl soils over hard Jurassic bedrock — combined with a southwest-facing aspect — generate the appellation's signature tension between stony austerity and latent power. Girardin ferments with indigenous yeasts only, maintains a cold cellar throughout, and applies zero bâtonnage, preserving the site's mineral salinity rather than building textural weight. Aging runs 12 months in the 456-litre barrels at 30% new oak, followed by 6 months in stainless-steel tank before bottling unfined and unfiltered on the lunar calendar. The special feature: no peer in Meursault or Corton routinely combines oversized-barrel fermentation, no lees-stirring, and lunar-timed bottling at this grand cru level — the result is a Corton-Charlemagne built on electric acidity and saline precision rather than the toasty generosity the hill can easily produce. The 2022 vintage in Burgundy delivered excellent natural balance after a dry, warm summer with well-timed September rains; expect this wine to reward 5–12 years of cellaring.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Batard-Montrachet Grand Cru
short_description (EN Store): Bâtard-Montrachet Grand Cru from a 13th-generation prodigy: 456-litre barrels, zero bâtonnage, unfined — electric mineral purity over Meursault-tradition richness.
description (EN Store):
Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian winemaking family — and immediately broke with the oak-rich Meursault tradition his lineage helped define. His Bâtard-Montrachet sits on the limestone-and-marl grand cru strip straddling Puligny and Chassagne, where Jurassic marly limestone over compacted clay delivers the appellation's signature tension between fat and stone. The mid-slope east-facing aspect concentrates morning sun while preserving cool nights, driving phenolic ripeness without sacrificing acidity.

The technical signature is deliberate and measurable: fermentation in custom 456-litre barrels (François Frères / Seguin-Moreau) rather than Burgundy's standard 228-litre pièce halves the wood-to-wine surface ratio, suppressing oak extraction while retaining the micro-oxygenation that builds texture. No bâtonnage, minimal sulphur, and 30% new oak keep the wine taut rather than creamy. Twelve months in barrel are followed by six months in stainless steel before unfined, unfiltered bottling timed to the lunar calendar.

What no peer in this appellation does at this price tier: a producer under 30 sourcing a contracted Bâtard parcel and deliberately engineering less wood contact than Leflaive or Colin-Morey — prioritising saline, crystalline drive over the buttered-stone opulence the climat is famous for. The 2022 vintage in Burgundy delivered generous ripeness with preserved freshness after a warm, dry summer moderated by well-timed September rain — expect Bâtard's inherent breadth amplified by PVG's precision, drinking well from 2026 through 2035+.

name: Domaine Vincent Dancer Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru ''La Romanée"
short_description (EN Store): Alsatian engineer turned Chassagne pioneer: La Romanée 1er Cru, wild-yeast fermented in neutral oak, unfined, 2022 radiant vintage.
description (EN Store): Vincent Dancer arrived in Chassagne-Montrachet in 1996 not as a vigneron's heir but as an Alsatian-born engineer — and promptly became the village's first producer to achieve organic certification (2012), rewriting what Chassagne white could be in the process. La Romanée sits within the Premier Cru belt on the limestone-and-marl argilo-calcaire soils of the southeast-facing slope, where the clay fraction retains just enough moisture to sustain vines through dry summers while the fractured limestone drives the saline, stony tension that defines the cuvée. Yields are held to 15–25 hl/ha by horse-and-hand cultivation. Whole-cluster pneumatic pressing feeds fermentation with indigenous yeasts in barrel — no temperature control, no sulfur during fermentation — a process that may continue slowly for up to a year post-harvest. Aging runs 12–18 months in a mix of 228 L, 350 L, and 600 L neutral barrels (zero new oak purchased since 2019), with no bâtonnage, no fining, and no filtration. The wine is bottled under Diam 30 closure, adopted from the 2022 vintage onward. What no peer in the village does: Dancer refuses all new wood entirely, letting the argilo-calcaire terroir speak without aromatic interference from toasted oak. The 2022 vintage is described as radiant across the domaine's whites — floral, stony, and complex — with the structural cut to develop over a decade.

name: Domaine Vincent Dancer Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Morgeot - Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Alsatian engineer turned Chassagne pioneer: Morgeot 1er Cru fermented on wild yeasts in neutral oak, unfined, unfiltered — 2022 radiant vintage.
description (EN Store): Vincent Dancer arrived in Chassagne-Montrachet in 1996 with an engineering background and no winemaking lineage — and became the village's first organic-certified producer (2012), dismantling a local tradition of richer, oak-driven whites through sheer non-interventionism. Morgeot sits on the southeast-facing limestone-and-marl argilo-calcaire soils of Chassagne's lower Premier Cru slope; the clay fraction retains just enough moisture to buffer the warm 2022 season, while the underlying limestone drives the cuvée's characteristic saline tension and cut. Whole-cluster pneumatic pressing feeds indigenous-yeast fermentation in a mix of 228 L, 350 L, and 600 L neutral barrels — no new oak purchased since 2019 — with fermentation allowed to continue slowly for up to a year post-harvest and zero bâtonnage, preserving a discreetly reductive, crystalline texture rather than any creamy mid-palate weight. The wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered under Diam 30 closure, a shift adopted from the 2022 vintage onward. What separates this from virtually every Morgeot peer is the combination of horse-and-hand cultivation at 15–25 hl/ha yields and the complete absence of sulfur during fermentation — site transparency over winemaker signature. The 2022 vintage is described as radiant across the domaine; expect a taut, stony expression with considerable aging runway through the late 2030s.

name: Domaine Vincent Dancer Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Tête du Clos - Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Alsatian engineer turned Chassagne pioneer: 100% Chardonnay from limestone-marl Premier Cru, wild-yeast fermented in neutral oak, unfined and unfiltered under Diam 30.
description (EN Store): Vincent Dancer arrived in Chassagne-Montrachet in 1996 with an engineering background and no winemaking lineage — and proceeded to become the village's first organically certified producer (2012), dismantling a local tradition of richer, oak-driven whites from the inside. The Tête du Clos Premier Cru sits on the argilo-calcaire soils characteristic of Chassagne's upper slope: limestone and marl with a southeast aspect that delivers steady morning sun and natural drainage, concentrating flavour at the domaine's target yields of 15–25 hl/ha. Fermentation is triggered by indigenous yeasts only, conducted in a mix of 228 L, 350 L, and 600 L neutral barrels — no new oak has been purchased since 2019 — with no temperature control and no sulfur additions during fermentation; the process can extend slowly for up to a year post-harvest. The wine sees 12–18 months of barrel aging with zero bâtonnage, then is bottled unfined and unfiltered under Diam 30 closure, adopted from the 2022 vintage onward. What separates Dancer from virtually every Chassagne peer is the complete absence of winemaker imprint: no new wood, no lees-stirring, no fining — site transparency as an absolute discipline rather than a marketing claim. The 2022 vintage is described as radiant across the domaine's whites; expect a taut, saline, reductively edged Chardonnay that will reward three to eight years of cellaring.

name: Domaine Vincent Dancer Meursault 1er Cru Perrieres
short_description (EN Store): Les Perrières 2022: ultra-stony altered marl, wild-yeast fermentation lasting up to a year, zero new oak — Dancer's most mineral-charged Premier Cru.
description (EN Store): Vincent Dancer — an Alsatian-born engineer with no winemaking lineage — became Chassagne-Montrachet's first organically certified producer in 2012, then systematically dismantled every convention the village was built on. Day-to-day stewardship passed to his son Théo in 2021, who carried the 2022 vintage through harvest. Les Perrières sits on highly stony, altered marl soils within Meursault Premier Cru, a geology that strips away any tendency toward richness and redirects energy into vertical, saline mineral tension. Horse-and-hand cultivation at target yields of 15–25 hl/ha concentrates that site character further. Whole-cluster pneumatic pressing feeds indigenous-yeast fermentation in a mix of 228 L, 350 L, and 600 L neutral barrels — no new oak has entered the cellar since 2019 — with no temperature control and no sulfur during fermentation; the ferment may continue slowly for up to a year post-harvest. No bâtonnage, no fining, no filtration, bottled under Diam 30 closure from the 2022 vintage onward. What separates this from any peer Premier Cru is the combination of near-zero winemaker imprint and a discreetly reductive signature that amplifies the stony, saline character of the Perrières marl rather than softening it with wood or lees work. The 2022 vintage is described as radiant, with Perrières among the finest of the year — floral, stony, and complex — with a drinking window that rewards patience.

name: Domaine Hubert Lamy Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Chaumees
short_description (EN Store): Chassagne 1er Cru fermented with indigenous yeasts in 300–600 L demi-muids, 24-month lees aging, zero new oak in this warm 2020.
description (EN Store):
When Olivier Lamy systematically replaced Burgundy's standard 228 L barriques with 300–600 L demi-muids across his cellar, it was a calculated response to premature oxidation — and the move has since reshaped how the Côte de Beaune thinks about white-wine élevage. Les Chaumées sits within Chassagne-Montrachet's Premier Cru mosaic, where shallow clay-limestone soils over hard limestone bedrock at roughly 250–300 m elevation deliver the structural tension that separates this village's whites from the rounder expressions lower on the slope.

Hand-harvested fruit is pneumatically pressed, lightly settled overnight, then fermented directly in barrel with ambient indigenous yeasts over approximately 90 days — a deliberately slow, cool-cellar fermentation that builds aromatic depth before malolactic fermentation completes in wood. Élevage runs 24 months on lees in mostly used demi-muids, followed by a further 6 months in tank; bâtonnage is restrained. In 2020, a warm, low-yield, high-luminosity year, Olivier cancelled most new barrel orders — new oak is effectively zero for this cuvée — to avoid overwhelming the ripe, concentrated fruit.

The result is a Chassagne Premier Cru that carries the domaine's chiselled limestone signature into a fuller, more textured register than its Saint-Aubin siblings. The 2020 structure and concentration suggest a drinking window from 2025 through 2033+.

name: Le Puy Marie-Cecile
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux white blend of Sauvignon Blanc & Sémillon with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision.
description (EN Store): Le Puy Marie-Cécile is a refined white blend from Côtes de Francs, Bordeaux, combining Sauvignon Blanc and Sémillon in a classic regional style. The 2023 vintage expresses the bright, zesty character typical of this terroir: vibrant citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and a distinctive mineral spine. The high acidity and light body deliver crisp, clean palate impressions ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and aperitif service. A food-friendly expression of Bordeaux white winemaking.

name: Domaine Jean-Pierre Guyon Morey-Saint-Denis "la Bidaude" - Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Côte de Nuits Chardonnay from La Bidaude, Morey-Saint-Denis — whole-cluster philosophy, indigenous yeasts, unfiltered; a white from red-wine country.
description (EN Store): When Jean-Pierre Guyon expanded his domaine through deliberate parcel acquisition from 1987 onward, he applied the same low-intervention logic he uses for Pinot Noir to his white vinifications — a decision that makes La Bidaude one of the few Chardonnays in Morey-Saint-Denis produced under a whole-cluster, indigenous-yeast regime. La Bidaude sits within Morey-Saint-Denis, a village whose limestone-rich soils sit between the schist-influenced terroirs of Gevrey-Chambertin and the clay-limestone of Chambolle-Musigny, lending the wine a structural tension between mineral grip and textural weight unusual for village-level Côte de Nuits white. Guyon ferments with indigenous yeasts only, in temperature-controlled stainless steel, then moves the wine through 10–14 months in French oak (one-year-old barrels at village tier), followed by a second élevage of 3–4 months in stainless steel before bottling unfined and unfiltered with minimal SO₂, timed to the biodynamic lunar calendar. The special feature here is categorical: Guyon is an ECOCERT-certified organic Côte de Nuits domaine applying a red-wine fermentation philosophy — whole-cluster, natural yeast, long infusion — to a white, producing a Chardonnay with the textural density and site-specificity that most Morey-Saint-Denis whites never attempt. The 2021 vintage, low-yielding and challenging across Burgundy, was cited by Berry Bros. & Rudd as a benchmark for the domaine's low-intervention approach; expect concentrated, tightly wound fruit with the structure to develop through 2028–2030.

name: Domaine Jean-Pierre Guyon Nuits-Saint-Georges "les Argillas" - Pinot Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Pinot Blanc from Nuits-Saint-Georges' marl-limestone soils — unfiltered, indigenous-yeast, biodynamic-bottled white from a Côte de Nuits red-wine estate.
description (EN Store):
Jean-Pierre Guyon built his domaine's reputation entirely on Pinot Noir, which makes this Nuits-Saint-Georges white — vinified from Pinot Blanc on the marl-limestone soils of Les Argillats — a deliberate transgression from a producer who has never followed category convention. The marl-limestone subsoil of Nuits-Saint-Georges imparts a chalky, saline tension that distinguishes this site from the clay-limestone dominant across Guyon's Côte de Nuits red parcels, and that mineral backbone drives the wine's structure in the absence of tannin.

Winemaking follows the same near-natural philosophy applied across the entire domaine: indigenous yeast fermentation, no sulphur added during alcoholic fermentation, and élevage in one-year-old French oak barrels (village-tier protocol), followed by a secondary rest in stainless steel before bottling. The wine is unfiltered and unfined, with minimal SO₂ at bottling only, timed to the biodynamic lunar calendar — a discipline Guyon applies from Bourgogne Régionale through Grand Cru without exception.

What is genuinely unusual here: Pinot Blanc is confirmed in Guyon's range only as a blending component (~30%) in Coteaux Bourguignons Blanc; a varietal Pinot Blanc from a Nuits-Saint-Georges lieu-dit is an outlier expression with no direct peer at this address. The 2021 vintage — low-yielding and challenging across Burgundy — was cited by Berry Bros. & Rudd as a benchmark for Guyon's low-intervention style, with the reduced crop concentrating the site's mineral character. Drink 2024–2029.

name: Domaine Sylvain Pataille Marsannay "le Chapitre" - Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Le Chapitre's iron-rich white clay — Chenôve's steepest, warmest slope — fermented with ambient yeast, aged in older 350L barrels. 2021 precision.
description (EN Store): Le Chapitre carries a 16th–17th-century reputation that once placed it alongside Burgundy's grands crus — a claim Sylvain Pataille, trained oenologist and the defining voice of Marsannay since 1999, takes seriously enough to bottle it alone. The site sits on the steepest slope in Chenôve, outside the Marsannay AOC boundary, on white clay dense with iron — a soil profile that drives both textural weight and a saline, ferrous tension in the finished wine. The warmest exposition in Pataille's portfolio accelerates phenolic ripeness while the clay's water retention moderates drought stress, producing Chardonnay of unusual density for the northern Côte de Nuits. Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeast only, preceded by long, slow pressing with a vertical press and extended skin contact before the juice sees oak. Aging runs 12–24 months in older 350L barrels at a low new-oak percentage, preserving site character over wood imprint; no fining is applied, though the wine is lightly filtered at bottling with minimal SO₂. What sets Le Chapitre apart categorically is its appellation status: this is Bourgogne Blanc, not Marsannay, because the parcel falls outside the 1987 AOC boundary — a grand-cru-pedigreed terroir bottled under Burgundy's most modest village label. The cool, frost-affected 2021 vintage delivered lower yields and heightened mineral precision; the wine will reward 4–8 years of cellaring.

name: Domaine Sylvain Pataille Marsannay "La Charme aux Prêtres"
short_description (EN Store): La Charme aux Prêtres: white clay, iron-rich Marsannay soil — Chardonnay fermented on ambient yeast, aged in older 350L barrels with minimal SO₂.
description (EN Store):
When Sylvain Pataille — a trained oenologist who consults for roughly 15–20 Burgundy domaines — founded his estate in 1999 on entirely leased land, he identified La Charme aux Prêtres as one of Marsannay's most compelling white-wine sites: white clay with a pronounced iron content, a soil profile that delivers both textural weight and a saline, ferrous edge that distinguishes it from the limestone-dominant lieux-dits nearby. The iron-rich clay retains moisture through dry spells while imposing a natural tension on the vine, producing Chardonnay of density without heaviness.

Vinification follows indigenous yeast fermentation after a long, slow pressing with a vertical press and extended skin contact before pressing — a technique that builds phenolic grip and aromatic complexity before the juice even sees oak. Aging proceeds in older 350L barrels at a low new-oak percentage, preserving site character over wood influence; no sulphur is added during fermentation, with minimal SO₂ introduced only at bottling. The wine is filtered but not fined. In 2021 — a cool, frost- and mildew-pressured vintage that cut yields sharply — Marsannay's inherent freshness and mineral precision are fully expressed, producing a wine of taut acidity and site-specific tension. Drink 2025–2032.

name: Domaine Pierre Girardin Puligny-Montrachet
short_description (EN Store): 13th-gen Girardin, Puligny-Montrachet 2022: 456L barrels, no bâtonnage, unfined — electric minerality over richness.
description (EN Store): Pierre-Vincent Girardin launched his domaine in 2016 at roughly 19 years old — the 13th generation of a Burgundian family — and immediately broke from the richer, more oak-driven Meursault tradition by deploying custom 456-litre barrels (double the Burgundy-standard 228-litre pièce) to halve wood-to-wine contact on his whites. Puligny-Montrachet sits on the Côte de Beaune mid-slope at approximately 260 m elevation, its east-to-south-facing parcels rooted in Jurassic marly limestone — a calcium-dense, well-drained substrate that drives the appellation's signature tension and chalky salinity rather than textural weight. Fruit is hand-harvested, carefully sorted, and fermented with indigenous yeasts in those oversized François Frères/Seguin-Moreau barrels; no bâtonnage is performed and sulphur additions are kept minimal, preserving the juice's crystalline freshness. After 12 months in barrel (30% new oak), the wine rests a further 6 months in stainless steel before bottling unfined and unfiltered, timed to the lunar calendar. The complete absence of lees-stirring is the defining departure here — where most Puligny producers use bâtonnage to build mid-palate weight, Girardin sacrifices texture for site-specific precision. The 2022 Burgundy vintage delivered generous ripeness with retained acidity, giving this cuvée both presence and the electric, saline drive that defines the domaine's house style.

name: Simon Colin Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Chaumees
short_description (EN Store): Les Chaumées sits at Chassagne's cool upper border with Saint-Aubin — chalky-mineral soils, ~25% new oak, native-yeast ferment, third-generation Colin breaking from his father's richer style.
description (EN Store): Simon Colin launched his micro-négociant 'laboratory' in 2017 at age 24 — before inheriting 9.3 hectares of family vines in 2021 — training first at Domaine Étienne Sauzet in Puligny and completing a harvest in Oregon, a deliberately cross-regional education rare in a village where most producers never leave. Les Chaumées Premier Cru occupies the upper, cooler fringe of Chassagne-Montrachet where the appellation borders Saint-Aubin, on chalky-mineral soils that drive the cuvée's taut, saline character and pronounced acidity rather than the rounder, clay-inflected weight found lower on the slope at Les Vergers. Fermentation is conducted in barrel with indigenous yeasts, followed by malolactic fermentation in vessel; aging runs approximately 12 months in a mix of 228L barrique, 350L demi-muid, and concrete egg — with ~25% new French oak for this Premier Cru, then 3–4 months in tank before bottling under natural cork. The deliberate restraint on new wood — a conscious departure from the more opulent, oak-forward style of his father Philippe Colin's generation — keeps the chalky terroir signal unobscured. In 2022, a warm and generous vintage, Les Chaumées delivers ripe citrus, honeycomb, and hazelnut with integrated oak and the structural density to develop through 2033 and beyond.

name: Simon Colin Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Chaumees - Clos St-Abdon
short_description (EN Store): Les Chaumées 1er Cru: upper-slope chalky-mineral terroir, ~25% new oak, native-yeast ferment — Simon Colin's generational break from Chassagne convention.
description (EN Store): Simon Colin launched his micro-négociant label in 2017 at age 24 — before inheriting 9.3 hectares of family vines in 2021 — specifically to build a style distinct from his father Philippe's generation: less oak, more terroir, more acid tension. That deliberate rupture is most legible in Les Chaumées. Les Chaumées 1er Cru sits at the cooler upper border of Chassagne-Montrachet where it meets Saint-Aubin, on chalky, stony limestone soils that drive the saline, mineral register absent from the richer mid-slope parcels. The elevated position and chalky substrate compress ripeness and amplify the site's linear character. Fermentation is conducted in barrel using indigenous yeasts; aging runs approximately 12 months in a mix of 228L barrique, 350L demi-muid, and concrete egg — with ~25% new French oak for this Premier Cru, the remainder in older wood, followed by 3–4 months in tank before bottling on natural cork. Only fine lees are retained; SO₂ additions are minimal. The concrete egg component — unusual for a Premier Cru Chassagne — preserves primary fruit and amplifies textural precision without the reductive risk of full barrel aging. In the warm 2022 vintage, Les Chaumées delivers ripe citrus, honeycomb, and hazelnut with the structural grip to develop through 2033+; the site's cooler aspect tempers the vintage's generosity into something more architecturally precise than many 2022 Chassagnes.

name: Simon Colin Chassagne-Montrachet 1er Cru Les Vergers
short_description (EN Store): Third-generation Chassagne heir breaks from his father's oak-forward house style: Les Vergers 2022 fermented on native yeasts in predominantly used wood for taut, site-driven Chardonnay.
description (EN Store): Simon Colin launched his micro-négociant label in 2017 at age 24 — before inheriting 9.3 hectares of family vines in 2021 — as a deliberate act of stylistic separation from his father Philippe Colin's generation, training instead at Domaine Étienne Sauzet in Puligny and completing a harvest in Oregon before returning to Chassagne. Les Vergers sits mid-slope in Chassagne-Montrachet's Premier Cru belt, where the chalky brown limestone of the upper slope gives way to slightly heavier clay content, lending the wine more textural weight than the cooler, more mineral Les Chaumées above it. Fermentation and aging proceed in a mix of 228L barrique and 350L demi-muids using indigenous yeasts, with predominantly used/older wood and approximately 12 months in barrel followed by 3–4 months in tank before bottling on natural cork — a regime that keeps fruit primary and terroir unobscured. The 2022 vintage's warmth and generosity are channelled through Simon's restraint-first philosophy: where peers in Chassagne lean into new oak and richness, he deliberately suppresses both, producing a mid-slope Premier Cru with citrus, honeycomb, and hazelnut character anchored by the clay-limestone's structural grip. Drinking well now through 2033+.

name: Domaine Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Clos du Meix
short_description (EN Store): Monopole Premier Cru from shallow clay-limestone over bedrock; 90-day ambient fermentation in 300–600 L demi-muids, 2021's cool tension fully intact.
description (EN Store):
Clos du Meix is Domaine Hubert Lamy's monopole — a sole-ownership Premier Cru that Olivier Lamy vinifies and bottles as a parcel-precise portrait of Saint-Aubin's geology, unblended with any other climat. The Lamy family's viticultural roots in Saint-Aubin trace to 1640, but it is Olivier who redrew the domaine's technical identity, replacing standard 228 L barriques with 300–600 L demi-muids (mostly 1–5 years old, 0–15% new wood) — a deliberate anti-premox and anti-oak-dominance strategy he pioneered before it became a Côte de Beaune convention.

The vineyard sits on shallow clay-limestone soils, 20–40 cm deep over hard limestone bedrock, at roughly 230–350 m elevation in the Côte de Beaune. That thin soil cap forces root systems deep into fractured rock, delivering the chiselled, site-specific limestone minerality that separates Saint-Aubin from its richer southern neighbours.

Fermentation proceeds with indigenous yeasts over approximately 90 days in a temperature-controlled white-wine cellar, followed by malolactic fermentation in barrel and 24 months of lees aging, then a further 6 months in tank before bottling under DIAM cork. Bâtonnage is restrained or absent. SO₂ additions are minimal, applied only after the first year of aging.

As a monopole, Clos du Meix offers something no négociant can replicate: a single-owner, single-vintage, single-site reading of this exact limestone pocket — uncut by blending decisions. The 2021 vintage, shaped by frost pressure and cool growing conditions, delivers the appellation's most classically proportioned profile: bright, taut acidity and precise minerality over a restrained, textural frame. Drink from 2025 through 2032+.

name: Domaine Hubert Lamy Saint-Aubin 1er Cru Les Frionnes
short_description (EN Store): Les Frionnes, southeast-facing limestone, vines from 1935 — 90-day ferment in demi-muids, 24-month lees aging, 2021 tension at its sharpest.
description (EN Store):
When Olivier Lamy systematically retired the domaine's standard 228 L barriques in favour of 300–600 L demi-muids, he was solving a specific problem: premature oxidation and oak dominance that obscured the chiselled limestone character Saint-Aubin's shallow soils produce. Les Frionnes sits northeast of the village on a southeast-facing slope, its 20–40 cm of clay-limestone over hard limestone bedrock — at roughly 230–350 m elevation — delivering the cool, tense raw material that makes this strategy legible in the glass.

Vines planted in 1935, 1960, and 1985 give three generations of root depth into that bedrock. Hand-harvested Chardonnay is pneumatically pressed, lightly settled overnight, then fermented with indigenous yeasts over approximately 90 days at reduced cellar temperatures — a deliberate prolongation that builds aromatic complexity before malolactic fermentation completes in barrel. The wine then ages 24 months on lees in mostly used demi-muids (0–15% new oak), followed by a further 6 months in tank before bottling under DIAM cork with minimal sulphur.

What distinguishes Les Frionnes within the range is the multi-generational vine mosaic: the 1935 plantings predate the domaine itself by nearly four decades, anchoring a structural depth that younger-vine Saint-Aubin cannot replicate. The cool, frost-pressured 2021 vintage — lower yields, bright acidity, precise minerality — is the most classically proportioned expression of this climat in recent memory; drink from 2025 through 2033.

name: Castelli The Sum Riesling
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Great Southern Riesling with stone fruit, citrus, and mineral precision—ideal for Asian cuisine and seafood.
description (EN Store): Castelli Estate's Riesling from Australia's Great Southern region delivers a classic expression of the varietal: bright stone fruit and citrus aromatics with a mineral backbone. The wine's high acidity and light body make it exceptionally food-friendly, particularly with spiced Asian dishes, fresh seafood, and herbaceous preparations. Expect green apple, lime zest, and subtle floral notes on the palate, with a clean, refreshing finish.

name: Pictor Sauvignon Blanche
short_description (EN Store): Vibrant Chilean Sauvignon Blanc with citrus zest, green apple, and herbaceous minerality—crisp and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Pictor Sauvignon Blanche is a refreshing Central Valley white showcasing the varietal's signature brightness. Expect lively citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous undertones and subtle stone fruit on the palate. The wine's medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a clean, mineral finish ideal for seafood, fresh cheeses, and warm-weather dining.

name: La Rue De L'Amour Premium Selected Blanche
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Bordeaux white blend of Sauvignon Blanc and Sémillon with vibrant citrus and herbaceous character.
description (EN Store): A classic Bordeaux white blend pairing Sauvignon Blanc's signature herbaceous and citrus-driven profile with Sémillon's stone fruit richness and textural depth. Light-bodied with bracing acidity, this wine delivers bright lime and green apple on the nose, followed by minerality and subtle floral notes on the palate. The interplay of crisp acidity and delicate fruit makes it an ideal aperitif or companion to seafood, fresh cheeses, and vibrant Asian cuisine.

name: Viña Valoria Blanco Rioja D.O.
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Viura from Rioja with bright citrus and green apple; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Viña Valoria Blanco is a single-varietal Viura from Spain's Rioja region, delivering the classic profile of this indigenous white: bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and mineral notes. Medium body and medium-high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for grilled white fish, shellfish, and fresh salads. The herbaceous undertone adds complexity without heaviness, positioning this wine as a versatile, food-friendly choice for warm-weather entertaining and casual dining.

name: Askaneli Tsinandali
short_description (EN Store): Georgian Tsinandali: crisp white with citrus, stone fruit & herbaceous minerality. Refreshing aperitif & seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Askaneli Tsinandali is a classic Georgian white wine showcasing the indigenous Tsinandali grape variety. This medium-bodied expression delivers bright citrus and green apple on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and herbaceous notes that reflect the wine's cool-climate terroir. The medium-high acidity provides a refreshing, mineral-driven finish ideal for aperitif service or pairing with delicate seafood and Asian cuisine.

name: Askaneli Rkatsiteli White
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Georgian white with citrus, stone fruit & mineral edge. Versatile aperitif & seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Askaneli Rkatsiteli is a classic expression of Georgia's signature white varietal, showcasing the grape's natural affinity for bright acidity and mineral-driven character. The wine opens with citrus zest and green apple, followed by stone-fruit notes of peach and apricot. A subtle herbaceous undertone and saline minerality provide complexity and food-friendly structure. Medium-bodied with crisp acidity, this white delivers refreshment without heaviness, making it equally at home as an aperitif or paired with seafood, Asian cuisine, and light vegetable dishes.

name: Le Morette Mandolara Lugana D.O.C.
short_description (EN Store): 100% Turbiana from glacial-moraine clay above Lake Garda's southern shore — fermented cold in stainless steel by a family that breeds the grape's own certified clones.
description (EN Store): Le Morette's clonal research on Turbiana — conducted in collaboration with the University of Milan and the Lugana Consortium — yielded three newly certified clones now used across the entire DOC. The Mandolara is where that genetic mastery meets its most direct expression. Grown on compact, chalky marly clay of glacial-moraine origin with clay content exceeding 40% on the estate's parcels in San Benedetto di Lugana and Desenzano del Garda, the vines sit on a narrow strip between Lake Garda's southern shore and the UNESCO-listed Frassino Lake — a microclimate defined by the surrounding Alpine foothills moderating diurnal swings and the lake's thermal mass extending the growing season. That high-clay, mineral-laden substrate drives pronounced saline tension and textural weight into the fruit. Winemaking is deliberately reductive: soft crush, 100% stainless steel fermentation at 16°C, zero skin contact, minimum one month in bottle at 12.5% ABV — no oak at any stage. The special feature here is the estate's dual identity as both nursery and winery: Le Morette controls Turbiana's clonal future while simultaneously bottling its most aromatic, unoaked expression. Vineyards are farmed without synthetic chemicals to protect the tufted-duck habitat at Frassino Lake, making biodiversity a structural production constraint rather than a label claim. Drink now through three to four years for peak aromatic lift.

name: Le Morette Benedictus Lugana D.O.C.
short_description (EN Store): Old-vine Turbiana from glacial-clay Lugana: 24-hr cold maceration + partial tonneaux aging lifts structure far beyond the appellation's typical register.
description (EN Store): Le Morette's clonal research on Turbiana — conducted in collaboration with the University of Milan and the Lugana Consortium — yielded three newly certified clones, and it is from the estate's oldest parcels, vines exceeding 45 years, that Benedictus is drawn. Those vines root into compact, chalky marly clay of glacial-moraine origin with clay content above 40%, a soil type that restricts yield, concentrates mineral salts, and builds the structural backbone that separates Benedictus from lighter Lugana expressions. The narrow strip between Lake Garda's southern shore and the UNESCO-listed Frassino Lake generates a mild microclimate with meaningful diurnal swing — cool nights preserving aromatic tension against warm, lake-moderated days. Vinification begins with 24 hours of cold skin maceration, extracting phenolic grip and textural weight before fermentation proceeds in stainless steel at 16–18°C; a portion then moves to used French tonneaux for partial fermentation and aging, with new-oak proportion deliberately reduced over successive vintages. Minimum three months in bottle before release at 13.5% ABV. What no peer in the appellation replicates is the combination of estate-selected old-vine clonal material and a maceration-plus-oak protocol applied to a grape most producers handle exclusively in steel. Turbiana at this site ages well — the high-clay, mineral-dense soils build wines that reward three to seven years of cellaring.

name: Le Morette Serai Bianco Veronese I.G.T.
short_description (EN Store): 100% Turbiana from glacial-moraine clay on Lake Garda's southern shore — stainless-only, 16°C ferment, zero oak.
description (EN Store):
Le Morette's clonal research on Turbiana — conducted in collaboration with the University of Milan and the Lugana Consortium — yielded three newly certified clones, making this family estate a scientific architect of the entire Lugana DOC's genetic future. That depth of varietal knowledge flows directly into the Serai Bianco Veronese I.G.T., a 100% Turbiana bottled under the broader Veronese designation.

The vines grow on compact, chalky, marly clay of glacial-moraine origin on the narrow strip between Lake Garda's southern shore and the UNESCO-listed Frassino Lake nature reserve. Clay content exceeds 40% in the estate's best parcels; the lake's surrounding mountains generate a pronounced diurnal temperature range that preserves aromatic tension in the grape.

Vinification is entirely in stainless steel, temperature-controlled at 16°C, with no skin contact and no oak at any stage — a deliberate choice to keep Turbiana's saline, mineral-driven character unmediated. Minimum one month of bottle aging before release; ABV sits at 12.5%, preserving freshness over weight.

What sets this apart: the estate's synthetic-chemical-free viticulture is structurally tied to protecting the tufted-duck habitat at Frassino Lake — biodiversity management is baked into the production model. Drink in its youth for maximum aromatic lift, though the high-clay mineral base gives it more mid-term structure than its entry-level positioning suggests.

name: Reguta Müller Thurgau Tre Venezie I.G.P.
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Müller-Thurgau from Friuli with green apple, citrus, and mineral freshness—ideal for seafood and light cuisine.
description (EN Store): Reguta's Müller-Thurgau from Tre Venezie I.G.P. is a refreshing white wine rooted in the cool-climate terroir of northeastern Italy. The varietal expresses bright green apple and citrus zest on the nose, with supporting stone fruit and herbaceous notes that evolve on the palate. A crisp, mineral finish and medium-high acidity make this wine an excellent aperitif and a natural partner for grilled fish, oysters, and fresh salads.

name: Reguta Ribolla Gialla Venezia Giulia I.G.P.
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Friuli white with vibrant citrus and green apple; mineral-driven refreshment for seafood and fresh cuisine.
description (EN Store): Reguta's Ribolla Gialla from Venezia Giulia delivers the signature brightness and mineral precision of northeastern Italy's flagship white varietal. Dominant citrus zest and green apple aromatics frame a medium-bodied palate with lively acidity, supported by subtle herbaceous and mineral undertones. The wine's clean, refreshing profile makes it an ideal aperitif and a versatile partner for seafood, raw preparations, and light vegetable-forward dishes.

name: Vasu Vasu Pinot Grigio Colline Pescaresi I.G.P.
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Abruzzo Pinot Grigio with vibrant citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Vasu Vasu's Pinot Grigio from Abruzzo's Colline Pescaresi region delivers the varietal's signature brightness: green apple and citrus zest dominate the nose and palate, supported by subtle pear and stone fruit notes. Medium body and lively acidity create a refreshing, food-friendly profile ideal for warm-weather drinking. Minerality adds textural interest without heaviness, making this an excellent aperitif or pairing for grilled fish, oysters, and light salads.

name: Cantine Paolini Sicilien Chardonnay Sicilia D.O.C.
short_description (EN Store): Sicilian Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and oak-derived vanilla—elegant and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Cantine Paolini's Sicilian Chardonnay expresses the warmth and minerality of the Mediterranean terroir through a medium-full frame. Green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and subtle floral notes. Oak aging contributes vanilla, butter, and hazelnut complexity, while the palate reveals good acidity and a clean, mineral finish. A versatile white suited to both aperitif service and pairing with seafood, poultry, and cream-based dishes.

name: Chateau d'Yquem  "Y" Bordeaux Blanc 2017
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Bordeaux white blend of Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, stone fruit, and mineral complexity.
description (EN Store): A refined white wine from Bordeaux's prestigious Pomerol region, this blend of Sémillon and Sauvignon Blanc showcases the classic interplay of these complementary varieties. The 2017 vintage delivers bright citrus zest and stone fruit on the nose, underpinned by herbaceous and grassy notes characteristic of Sauvignon Blanc. The palate reveals medium-full body with medium-high acidity, offering tropical undertones and subtle honey notes from the Sémillon component. Mineral complexity and a whisper of petrol suggest age-worthiness and depth. Ideal for sophisticated seafood preparations, creamy pasta dishes, and soft cheeses.

name: J. Lohr Riverstone Barrel Aged Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): 100% Arroyo Seco Chardonnay barrel-fermented and aged ~9 months sur lie on alluvial riverstones; 2022 screwcap debut.
description (EN Store):
Jerry Lohr planted Arroyo Seco in 1972 — eleven years before the AVA existed — staking a claim on a stretch of Monterey County floor where the Arroyo Seco River deposited layers of alluvial cobbles and 'Greenfield potato' stones that force vine roots to compress within 1–5 feet of the surface. That shallow, heat-radiating gravel bed, swept daily by cold Monterey Bay fog and wind, is the physical engine behind Riverstone's name and its structure.


The 2022 is 100% Chardonnay from those estate blocks, night-harvested to preserve aromatic integrity, then pressed on-site and transferred directly to barrel — barrel-fermented rather than tank-fermented, a choice that builds textural integration from the first day of fermentation. Malolactic fermentation is employed for roundness, and the wine rests sur lie for approximately 9 months in oak, accumulating lees-derived creaminess without losing the site's inherent cool-climate tension.


The 2022 vintage marks the introduction of a screwcap closure — an unusual move for a barrel-fermented California Chardonnay at this tier, prioritising reductive freshness and closure consistency over cork tradition. The result is a wine where stony Arroyo Seco minerality and preserved acidity read clearly against the oak-derived texture, with the 2022 warm-season fruit weight providing immediate accessibility and a drinking window extending comfortably through 2027.

name: Santa Helena Sweet White
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Chilean white with citrus and stone fruit; refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): Santa Helena's Sweet White from Colchagua Valley delivers a refreshing, approachable profile anchored by bright citrus and stone-fruit aromatics. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for light seafood, Asian cuisine, and fresh preparations. The wine's clean, fruit-forward character emphasizes drinkability and versatility across casual dining and entertaining occasions.

name: Maison Jaffelin  Meursault
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Meursault Chardonnay with citrus precision, buttery oak, and mineral complexity—a benchmark white Burgundy.
description (EN Store): Maison Jaffelin's 2022 Meursault is a classically structured white Burgundy built on Chardonnay's signature balance of acidity and richness. Lemon and green apple dominate the nose, supported by subtle stone fruit and a whisper of hazelnut. On the palate, buttery oak and toasted notes provide mid-palate weight, while crisp acidity and mineral tension sustain a clean, focused finish. This is a wine of restraint and elegance—neither over-oaked nor austere—suited to fine dining and serious food pairing.

name: Argiolas Iselis Nasco Bianco Di Cagliari
short_description (EN Store): Sardinian Nasco with bright citrus, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—a refreshing coastal white.
description (EN Store): Argiolas' Iselis Nasco di Cagliari is a single-varietal white from Sardinia's warm, maritime terroir. Nasco is a native Sardinian grape prized for its aromatic intensity and natural salinity. This wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green-apple freshness, supported by subtle stone-fruit undertones. The palate is crisp and mineral-driven, with a distinctive saline edge that reflects the island's coastal influence. A light herbaceous note and faint almond character emerge on the finish, adding complexity without weight. Medium body and bracing acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood and Mediterranean fare.

name: Enjoy It Non Alcoholic Sauvinon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp German Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral precision—refreshing aperitif or seafood companion.
description (EN Store): A light-bodied, high-acidity Sauvignon Blanc from Germany's Rheinhessen region, this non-alcoholic expression captures the varietal's signature herbaceous and citrus-driven character. Bright green apple, lime zest, and grassy minerality dominate the palate, delivering the crisp, refreshing profile Sauvignon Blanc is prized for. The absence of alcohol does not diminish the wine's structural integrity or food-pairing versatility. Ideal as an aperitif, with oysters, fresh salads, or any occasion calling for a clean, vibrant white without the alcohol content.

name: Banfi La Pettegola Vermentino
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Tuscan Vermentino with citrus zest, stone fruit, and mineral salinity—ideal for seafood and Mediterranean fare.
description (EN Store): Banfi's La Pettegola Vermentino is a refreshing white wine from Montalcino, Tuscany, showcasing the classic profile of this Mediterranean varietal. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple notes, supported by subtle stone fruit character and a distinctive mineral salinity that reflects the region's terroir. Medium body and crisp acidity make it an excellent aperitif or pairing partner for grilled fish, shellfish, and light Mediterranean dishes. The herbaceous undertones add complexity without overwhelming the wine's clean, food-friendly structure.

name: Edenvale Chardonnay (Non-Alcoholic Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Chardonnay with crisp apple, citrus, and subtle oak—alcohol-free sophistication.
description (EN Store): Edenvale's 2023 Chardonnay delivers the hallmark elegance of the varietal in a non-alcoholic format. Green apple and citrus dominate the nose, supported by stone fruit and a whisper of vanilla oak. On the palate, medium-full body and balanced acidity create a structured, food-friendly profile with notes of butter, minerality, and toasted oak. A versatile white suitable for aperitifs, seafood, and cream-based dishes.

name: Edenvale Sauvignon Blanc (Non-Alcoholic Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Crisp, herbaceous Sauvignon Blanc from South Australia with vibrant citrus and minerality—alcohol-free refreshment.
description (EN Store): Edenvale's 2023 Sauvignon Blanc delivers the signature brightness of the varietal in a non-alcoholic format. Expect a light-bodied wine with pronounced citrus zest, herbaceous notes, and green apple character, underpinned by mineral tension and a clean, refreshing finish. The high acidity makes it an ideal aperitif or pairing partner for seafood, fresh cheeses, and Asian cuisine.

name: Pierre Chavin  Le Petit Etoile Chardonnay (Non-Alcoholic Wine)
short_description (EN Store): Elegant non-alcoholic Chardonnay from Languedoc with apple, citrus, and subtle oak—a sophisticated alcohol-free alternative.
description (EN Store): Pierre Chavin's Le Petit Etoile Chardonnay is a non-alcoholic white wine crafted from classic Chardonnay grapes in the Languedoc-Roussillon region of France. This 2022 vintage delivers the hallmark character of Chardonnay—crisp green apple and citrus on the nose—complemented by subtle oak influence and buttery undertones that suggest careful winemaking. Medium-full bodied with balanced acidity, it offers a sophisticated alternative for those seeking the complexity of wine without alcohol content.

name: Golden Paws Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Australian Chardonnay with ripe stone fruit, citrus, and subtle oak—versatile for seafood, poultry, and creamy dishes.
description (EN Store): Golden Paws Chardonnay from South Eastern Australia delivers a well-balanced expression of the classic varietal. The wine opens with bright green apple and citrus aromatics, supported by ripe stone fruit notes that carry through the palate. Subtle oak influence adds layers of vanilla and toasted hazelnut, while a mineral backbone provides freshness and structure. Medium-Full body and moderate acidity make this a versatile food-friendly wine suitable for a range of occasions, from casual weeknight dinners to more refined entertaining.

name: Alto Los Romeros  Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Colchagua Sauvignon Blanc from a multi-valley estate reaching 900 m — coastal cool tension at an entry price rarely seen in Chilean whites.
description (EN Store): Viña Luis Felipe Edwards built its reputation on a deliberate multi-valley sourcing strategy, and the Alto Los Romeros Sauvignon Blanc is a direct expression of that philosophy — drawing on cool-influenced sites across Colchagua and potentially the Leyda Valley, where estate blocks at El Canelo and Atalaya sit just 2–9 km from the Pacific. Leyda's marine-origin loam over sandstone-quartz and alluvial clay-gravel-granite soils impose a structural tension and persistent acidity that warm-valley Chilean Sauvignon Blanc rarely achieves at this price tier. At the Puquillay Alto estate in Colchagua, granitic and volcanic rock substrates at elevations up to 900 m asl further sharpen the fruit profile, slowing ripening and preserving aromatic lift. Winemaking at the Puquillay facility employs pneumatic pressing and temperature-controlled fermentation — the cold-side discipline that locks in primary aromatics in a variety this volatile. Oak is not part of the equation at this varietal tier, keeping the wine's expression entirely site-driven. What separates this from the Chilean Sauvignon Blanc crowd is the genuine altitude and coastal proximity behind it — structural conditions more commonly associated with Casablanca or San Antonio, deployed here at a sub-฿1,000 price point. The 2023 vintage from Central Chile benefited from moderate conditions following the drought years of 2021–22, with recovered vine balance supporting brighter natural acidity.

name: Alto Los Romeros Reserva Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): High-altitude Colchagua Chardonnay from LFE's multi-valley estate network — cool-site tension at an entry price rarely seen in Chilean whites.
description (EN Store):
When Luis Felipe Edwards Sr. acquired the Puquillay Alto estate in 1976, the horseshoe-shaped micro-valley sheltered by the San Fernando ridge — with vineyards climbing to 900 m asl — was already planted with vines dating to the 1930s. That elevation is the structural argument for this Chardonnay: at altitude, diurnal swings are wide, ripening is slow, and the granitic-volcanic soils of Puquillay Alto drain freely while retaining just enough mineral charge to keep the fruit taut. The result is a Colchagua Chardonnay that reads cooler than its valley address suggests.

Winemaking at the Puquillay facility employs pneumatic pressing and temperature-controlled fermentation — the technical baseline for preserving aromatic lift in a warm-climate white. Oak regime at the Reserva tier is not disclosed by the producer; the wine's profile points toward restrained or neutral wood influence, keeping primary fruit and site character in the foreground. Low-yield viticulture is a stated house philosophy across all LFE tiers, concentrating juice without sacrificing freshness.

What separates this from the bulk of Chilean Chardonnay at this price is the deliberate sourcing logic: Alto Los Romeros draws from genuinely high-altitude and coastal-cool sites rather than the warm valley floors that define most value-tier Chilean whites, delivering structural acidity that most peers at ฿900 simply cannot replicate. Drink now through 2–3 years from vintage.

name: Apollonio Laicale Salento Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Salento Chardonnay from Apollonio's calcareous-clay estate near Lecce — stainless-fermented, sea-breeze-cooled, from Italy's oldest southern winery.
description (EN Store): Founded in 1870 and now holding Italy's rare Impresa Storica d'Italia designation, Apollonio built its reputation on Salento's indigenous reds — which makes the Laicale Chardonnay a deliberate departure: the house that ages one million litres per year in French oak here strips back to pure terroir expression. The vineyards sit within the Valle della Cupa near Monteroni di Lecce, roughly 5 km from Lecce at approximately 100 m elevation, on calcareous soils mixed with clay — the classic Salento karst geology that drives tension and saline lift into white varieties. The microclimate is shaped by simultaneous Adriatic and Ionian sea breezes, which moderate the intense Mediterranean sun and deliver natural acidity and mineral character that the continental interior cannot replicate. Vinification follows controlled-temperature fermentation in stainless steel, preserving primary aromatics and the site's inherent freshness — a pointed contrast to the oak-dominant philosophy applied across the rest of the portfolio. What distinguishes Laicale within the Apollonio range is precisely this restraint: Chardonnay grown on iron-rich calcareous clay in a dual-sea-breeze corridor, handled without wood, producing a textural yet mineral-driven white from a house whose identity is otherwise defined by oak and extraction. Drink young to capture the site's salinity and citrus-driven energy.

name: Frateli ponte roero arneis docg
short_description (EN Store): 100% Arneis from calcareous Canale d'Alba & Castellinaldo soils, harvested early September, lees-aged 3 months in steel — zero oak, pure varietal.
description (EN Store):
Fratelli Ponte built their Roero Arneis program around a deliberate refusal: no oak, at any stage. Where many Piedmontese producers reach for barrique to add texture to Arneis's naturally low-acid frame, the Ponte family vinifies entirely in temperature-controlled stainless steel and rests the wine on fine lees for three months — letting the grape's inherent white-peach and almond character speak without wood interference.

The fruit is sourced from Canale d'Alba and Castellinaldo, two communes on the Roero's right bank of the Tanaro where calcareous and chalky soils — lighter and sandier than the clay-heavy Langhe across the river — give Arneis its characteristic floral lift and a saline, mineral thread that heavier soils suppress. Early September harvest locks in aromatics before the variety's tendency toward phenolic ripeness takes over.

The DOCG Roero Arneis designation requires 100% Arneis, and Fratelli Ponte meets it with single-variety fruit from both communes blended for consistency. No oak contact at any point is the defining technical signature — unusual even within the appellation, where partial barrel fermentation is permitted and practiced. The result is a wine whose texture comes entirely from lees contact rather than wood, preserving the grape's delicate bitter-almond finish that oak would mask. Drink within 2–3 years of vintage for peak aromatic freshness.

name: Fratelli Ponte Moscato D'Asti Fiore Di Loto DOCG
short_description (EN Store): 100% Moscato Bianco from chalky Asti soils; tank-fermented to preserve aromatic lift in this gently sparkling DOCG from San Damiano d'Asti.
description (EN Store):
Fratelli Ponte's roots in San Damiano d'Asti — the family's Frazione Gorzano cellar established by Giuseppe Ponte in 1968, positioned between Asti and Alba on the chalky soils that define this corridor of Piedmont — give the Fiore Di Loto its structural backbone. Chalky, calcium-rich terrain in the Asti province drains freely, stressing the Moscato Bianco vine into producing intensely aromatic, low-yield fruit with pronounced floral concentration and natural acidity that prevents the sweetness from becoming cloying.

Vinification follows the Asti method: a single, interrupted fermentation in temperature-controlled stainless steel, halted before dryness to retain both residual sugar and the CO₂ produced during fermentation — no secondary refermentation, no dosage. The result sits at the low-alcohol end of the DOCG spectrum, preserving the grape's primary peach-blossom and apricot character without oxidative interference. 100% Moscato Bianco, no blending component.

What distinguishes this expression within the Moscato d'Asti DOCG is Fratelli Ponte's deliberate house philosophy of varietal authenticity over commercial sweetness amplification — the same restraint applied to their Barbera and Arneis is carried through to this frizzante format. Drink young, within 12–18 months of vintage, to capture the aromatic volatility at its peak.

name: Fratelli Fiorellino White Blend
short_description (EN Store): Piedmont white blend from Fratelli Ponte's San Damiano d'Asti estate — stainless-vinified for varietal purity, third-generation family farming across 35 ha of chalky Asti soils.
description (EN Store): Founded in the post-WWII era by Carlo Ponte and anchored since 1968 at Frazione Gorzano, Fratelli Ponte has built its house philosophy around a deliberate rejection of oak-driven winemaking — a stance that shapes every white in the portfolio as much as the reds. The Fiorellino White Blend draws from the estate's chalky soils in San Damiano d'Asti, situated in the corridor between Asti and Alba where calcareous subsoils drive tension and aromatic lift rather than weight. Vinification is entirely in temperature-controlled stainless steel, preserving primary fruit and varietal character without any wood influence — a conscious house decision to prioritise freshness at accessible price points without sacrificing appellation integrity. No oak contact, no malolactic softening documented; the stainless regime is the same philosophy applied to the estate's Roero Arneis, where fine-lees aging of three months in tank adds textural breadth without compromising aromatics. What distinguishes this expression within the Fratelli Ponte range is its blended construction — unusual in a portfolio otherwise built on single-variety DOC/DOCG bottlings — suggesting a deliberate house cuvée designed for early drinking and fruit-forward accessibility. Drink young to capture the chalky-soil brightness that San Damiano d'Asti's calcareous terrain imparts.

name: Montecillo Albarino
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish Albariño with citrus, stone fruit, and coastal minerality. Refreshing and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Montecillo's 2023 Albariño is a vibrant expression of Spain's Atlantic-influenced white wine tradition. The wine opens with bright citrus zest and green-apple aromatics, followed by stone-fruit notes of peach and apricot. A distinctive saline minerality runs through the palate, reflecting the maritime terroir of the region. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a crisp, refreshing mouthfeel with a clean, lingering finish. This is a versatile food wine suited to grilled seafood, shellfish, and Mediterranean-inspired dishes.

name: Bodegas Volver  Triga Chardonnay
short_description (EN Store): Chardonnay from 660 m limestone soils near Pinoso, Medio Vinalopó — Alicante's high-altitude answer to the variety's overripe Mediterranean trap.
description (EN Store): When Rafael Cañizares and Jorge Ordóñez founded Bodegas Volver in 2004, their stated mission was rescuing overlooked Spanish terroir — and the Triga Chardonnay applies that logic to a white grape in a region where Chardonnay is rarely taken seriously. The fruit is sourced from the Medio Vinalopó area near Pinoso, at 660 m elevation on poor, shallow limestone soils with minimal organic matter — conditions that force the vine to struggle, suppressing yields to a controlled 6,000 kg/ha and concentrating flavour without sacrificing freshness. The altitude moderates the fierce Alicante heat, extending the ripening window and preserving the natural acidity that lower-lying Mediterranean Chardonnay routinely loses. The wine carries the Alicante Singular sub-designation within DO Alicante, a classification reserved for wines of demonstrable site specificity. Winemaking details for this specific label are not fully disclosed in primary sources, though the house philosophy centres on controlled fermentation temperatures to preserve aromatic precision. The 2022 growing season brought scarce rainfall and high temperatures during final ripening, with the vineyard's altitude acting as a natural brake — delivering slow, even ripening with fruit concentration and retained acidity. Drink now through 2026.

name: Bodegas Volver Paso a Paso Verdejo
short_description (EN Store): La Mancha Verdejo from Bodegas Volver — the house that repositioned Spain's bulk-wine heartland as terroir-driven territory.
description (EN Store): Rafael Cañizares co-founded Bodegas Volver in 2004 with the explicit mission of rescuing near-abandoned indigenous Spanish varieties from DO La Mancha — a region historically dismissed as bulk-wine country — and proving that its extreme continental climate could produce wines of genuine site character. Paso a Paso Verdejo is sourced from La Mancha's high plateau, where poor sandy soils over clay subsoil and limestone patches, combined with a harsh continental climate averaging just 242 mm of annual rainfall, force vines to dig deep and produce concentrated, tightly structured fruit. Dry-farmed and organically managed, the vineyards experience large diurnal temperature swings during ripening that preserve natural acidity — the defining structural feature of this bottling. Fermentation is carried out at controlled low temperature to lock in Verdejo's characteristic aromatic lift, consistent with the house's minimal-intervention philosophy across its white range. What sets this expression apart within the Paso a Paso line is its deployment of Verdejo — a variety more associated with DO Rueda's gravelly riverbanks — in the limestone-clay soils of La Mancha, producing a leaner, more mineral-driven profile than the lush, herbaceous benchmark style of the north. Drink young to capture the tension between La Mancha's sun-driven fruit density and the altitude-preserved acidity.

name: Neleman Organic Vineyards Hey You White
short_description (EN Store): Organic Spanish white with bright citrus and stone fruit; crisp, refreshing, and food-friendly.
description (EN Store): Neleman Organic Vineyards' Hey You White is a vibrant, organically farmed white wine from Valencia, Spain. The wine opens with dominant citrus and green-apple aromatics, supported by subtle stone-fruit notes that give it immediate freshness and appeal. Medium body and medium-high acidity create a clean, refreshing palate structure ideal for warm-weather drinking and versatile food pairing. Herbaceous undertones and mineral texture add complexity without heaviness, making this an excellent choice for seafood, fresh salads, and spicy Asian cuisine.

name: La Bastarda Bianco di Toscana
short_description (EN Store): Tuscan white blend of Trebbiano and Chardonnay; crisp citrus, green apple, and mineral notes with refreshing acidity.
description (EN Store): La Bastarda Bianco di Toscana is a Tuscan white blend combining Trebbiano and Chardonnay to create a refreshing, food-friendly wine. The nose opens with bright citrus zest and green apple, supported by subtle stone fruit and herbaceous undertones. On the palate, medium body and medium-high acidity deliver a crisp, mineral-driven finish that lingers cleanly. This is a versatile table wine suited to seafood, light pasta dishes, and Mediterranean fare.

name: Finca Fella Cala Rey Verdejo Sauvignon Blanc
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Spanish white blend of Verdejo and Sauvignon Blanc with vibrant citrus, herbaceous notes, and mineral finish.
description (EN Store): A refreshing white wine from Spain's Almansa region, this Verdejo and Sauvignon Blanc blend delivers bright citrus zest and green apple on the nose, with herbaceous undertones that speak to the Sauvignon Blanc component. The palate is clean and mineral-driven, with supporting stone fruit and a crisp, dry finish. Medium body and medium-high acidity make it an ideal aperitif or pairing for seafood, salads, and light Mediterranean fare.

name: Elena Walch Chardonnay Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Elegant Alto Adige Chardonnay with crisp apple, citrus, and subtle oak—refined minerality and stone fruit complexity.
description (EN Store): Elena Walch's Alto Adige Chardonnay exemplifies the precision and elegance of northern Italian white winemaking. Grown in the cool Adige Valley, this single-varietal expression balances vibrant primary fruit—green apple and citrus—with measured oak influence that adds butter and toast without overwhelming the wine's natural acidity. Stone fruit and minerality emerge on the mid-palate, while hazelnut and subtle complexity suggest careful winemaking and potential for short-term aging. Medium-Full body and restrained tannin make this a versatile food wine, equally at home with shellfish, roasted poultry, or cream-based pasta.

name: Elena Walch Beyond the Clouds Bianco Alto Adige DOC
short_description (EN Store): Crisp Alto Adige white with citrus zest, green apple, and alpine minerality. Refreshing aperitif and seafood companion.
description (EN Store):
Elena Walch Beyond the Clouds Bianco Alto Adige DOC is a refreshing white wine from Italy's mountainous Alto Adige region, crafted to showcase the cool-climate character of the Adige Valley. The wine opens with vibrant citrus zest and green apple, supported by delicate stone fruit notes and a distinctive minerality that reflects the alpine terroir. A herbaceous undertone adds complexity and freshness to the palate.

Medium-bodied with bright acidity, this wine is ideal as an aperitif or paired with grilled fish, shellfish, and light pasta dishes. The crisp finish and clean profile make it a versatile choice for warm-weather entertaining and Mediterranean-inspired cuisine.

name: Albert Bichot Chablis
short_description (EN Store): Domaine Long-Depaquit Kimmeridgian limestone Chablis, 100% stainless steel, 2023 floral-mineral vintage — pure Chardonnay without a trace of oak.
description (EN Store):
Albert Bichot's Chablis is vinified at Domaine Long-Depaquit, the house's ~65-hectare Chablis estate, where soils are Kimmeridgian limestone and clay — the same 155-million-year-old seabed geology that defines the appellation's saline, oyster-shell character. Rather than centralising production in Beaune, Bichot made a deliberate structural choice to vinify every sub-regional wine at the estate closest to its source, meaning this Chablis never leaves its own terroir until bottling.

At village level, the wine is fermented and aged 100% in stainless steel, with no oak contact at any stage — a clean break from the barrel-influenced whites produced at Bichot's Côte de Beaune estates. This vessel choice is intentional: it preserves the cool-climate tension and chalky mineral drive that Kimmeridgian soils impart, rather than softening them with wood. Handpicked Chardonnay is processed with gravity racking throughout to minimise oxidative handling.

What distinguishes this expression within the Bichot portfolio is its position as the entry point to a Chablis range that extends all the way to the quasi-monopole La Moutonne — a 2.35 ha Grand Cru anomaly spanning Vaudésir and Preuses, excluded from the 1938 classification. The 2023 vintage delivered warm growing conditions with good sanitary health, producing a Chablis with a notably floral and mineral profile and fresh aromatic lift — drinking well now with a short-to-medium window ahead.

name: Albert Bichot Domaine Long-Depaquit Chablis 1Er Cru “Les Lys”
short_description (EN Store): Kimmeridgian limestone-clay Premier Cru from Domaine Long-Depaquit's Les Lys, barrel-fermented and aged on lees — 2023 floral-mineral Chablis.
description (EN Store): Domaine Long-Depaquit, Albert Bichot's 65-hectare Chablis estate, sits on the ancient seabed soils that define the appellation's identity: Kimmeridgian limestone and clay studded with fossilised oyster shells, the geological signature that drives Chablis's saline, chalky tension. Les Lys is a Premier Cru climat on the Left Bank, where the cool continental microclimate and north-facing aspect slow ripening and concentrate mineral extract rather than sugar accumulation. Bichot's structural decision to vinify Chablis at Domaine Long-Depaquit itself — rather than centralising production in Beaune — means the wine never leaves its sub-regional context during élevage. Premier Cru whites at this level are barrel-fermented in used French oak (Tronçais and Allier, light toast) with a portion in stainless steel, then aged 11–16 months, preserving the site's mineral architecture while adding textural weight. The entire estate has been farmed organically since the 2018 vintage, a commitment that distinguishes Domaine Long-Depaquit from the majority of Chablis's large-scale operators. The 2023 vintage delivered warm conditions with excellent sanitary health, producing a Chablis with pronounced floral lift and clean mineral definition — drink from 2025 through 2030.

name: Albert Bichot Domaine Long-Depaquit Chablis Grand Cru “Bougros”
short_description (EN Store): Bougros Grand Cru on Kimmeridgian limestone-clay; barrel-fermented Chardonnay from Domaine Long-Depaquit's 10 ha of certified-organic Grand Cru holdings.
description (EN Store): Domaine Long-Depaquit — the ~65-hectare Chablis estate at the heart of Albert Bichot's six-winery structure — holds 10 hectares of Grand Cru, farmed organically since the 2018 vintage. Bougros occupies the westernmost and steepest flank of the Grand Cru slope, where Kimmeridgian limestone-clay soils (the same Jurassic seabed strata that define Chablis' saline, oyster-shell character) sit at higher elevation than the central lieux-dits, generating a more austere, tension-driven expression than Valmur or Les Clos. Grapes are handpicked, then barrel-fermented in used French oak — the house deliberately scales new-oak percentage to appellation weight, keeping Grand Cru Chablis in predominantly used wood to let Kimmeridgian minerality speak rather than vanish under toast. Aging runs 11–16 months with gravity racking throughout, and the estate's non-interventionist philosophy means no corrective additions to mask the site's natural austerity. What sets this apart: Long-Depaquit is one of the very few Chablis Grand Cru proprietors to vinify entirely on-site in Chablis rather than trucking fruit to a centralised Beaune facility — a structural decision that preserves the cold-cellar micro-oxidation specific to the Serein valley. The 2022 vintage's hot, dry summer produced full-bodied, aromatic whites with early approachability, yet the Bougros site's elevation and clay retention provide enough structure for medium-term development through 2030–2034.

name: Albert Bichot Bourgogne Chardonnay “Origines”
short_description (EN Store): Regional Bourgogne Chardonnay vinified at Beaune's renovated central winery — 80% stainless steel, 20% used oak — from the ripe, aromatic 2022 harvest.
description (EN Store): Albéric Bichot's structural decision to vinify every wine at the estate closest to its source — rather than centralising production — applies even at the regional level: this Bourgogne Chardonnay is handled at the Beaune winery, renovated in 2008, using the same non-interventionist philosophy that governs the house's Grand Cru programme. Fruit is sourced from long-term partner growers across Burgundy's major sub-regions, giving the blend breadth that a single-village wine cannot achieve. The 2022 growing season delivered a hot, dry summer and early harvest, producing full-bodied, aromatic whites with natural richness balanced by the appellation's inherent acidity.
