Vinification follows a predominantly stainless steel regime (approximately 80%), with the remaining 20% in used French oak barrels — Tronçais and Allier, light toast — a deliberate calibration that adds textural weight without masking fruit character. No new oak enters the equation at this level, a conscious choice that separates Bichot's regional whites from négociant peers who routinely deploy new wood on entry-level Chardonnay. The result is a wine that drinks generously now, with the 2022 vintage's ripe stone-fruit profile already integrated; a short-term window of two to four years captures it at its most expressive.

name: Albert Bichot Macon Villages
short_description (EN Store): Mâcon-Villages 2022: Bichot's négociant Chardonnay from a hot, early harvest — fermented predominantly in stainless steel, full-bodied and aromatic.
description (EN Store): Albert Bichot has operated as a négociant-éleveur since Bernard Bichot founded the trading house in Monthélie in 1831, and the Maison's structural commitment — vinifying every wine at the estate closest to its source — means this Mâcon-Villages is handled at the facility nearest the Mâconnais, not centralised in Beaune. That logistical discipline is the same philosophy applied to Grand Cru; it simply scales down to village level. The Mâconnais sits on Jurassic limestone and clay soils, the same broad Kimmeridgian-influenced geology that runs south from Chablis, delivering the chalky tension that keeps southern Burgundy Chardonnay from tipping into flabbiness. Vinification follows Bichot's regional-white protocol: predominantly stainless steel fermentation (approximately 80%), with the remainder in used oak barrels, preserving primary fruit and site-driven texture without imposing wood. The 2022 vintage brought a hot, dry summer and early harvest, producing Chardonnay with generous, ripe fruit and a fuller body than the leaner 2021, yet retaining structural acidity. What distinguishes this expression within the négociant tier is Bichot's sourcing from long-term partner growers rather than spot-market fruit, giving consistency of site across vintages. Drink now through 2026 while the aromatic generosity of the vintage is at its peak.

name: Albert Bichot Domaine Du Pavillon Meursault
short_description (EN Store): Domaine du Pavillon Meursault 2022: Côte de Beaune Chardonnay, barrel-fermented with ~25% new oak, from a certified-organic estate. Hot vintage, full-bodied, drink now through 2030.
description (EN Store):
Albéric Bichot made a structural decision that most large Burgundy négociants have never replicated: rather than centralising vinification in Beaune, he built six geographically dispersed estate wineries, each vinifying only its own sub-region's fruit. The Meursault in this bottle was made at Domaine du Pavillon, the house's 17-hectare Côte de Beaune estate, which also holds Corton-Charlemagne and the Pommard monopole Clos des Ursulines — a deliberate choice to keep terroir fidelity intact at scale.

Meursault's soils here are the classic Côte de Beaune profile: limestone-rich with clay and marl subsoil, moderately elevated on the mid-slope, delivering the textural weight and latent richness that defines the appellation. The 2022 growing season — hot and dry with an early harvest — pushed Chardonnay to full physiological ripeness, producing a wine with generous aromatic intensity and a broad, enveloping palate weight.

Vinification follows Domaine du Pavillon's white-wine protocol: barrel fermentation in a mix of used and new French oak (approximately 25% new oak, Tronçais and Allier, light toast), with 12–15 months of aging on lees before bottling. The estate has been certified organic since the 2018 vintage — unusual for a house operating at this production scale across six estates. The 2022's ripe, full-bodied character is approachable now but carries sufficient structure for medium-term cellaring through 2030.

name: Jacob's Creek Moscato White (750 ml)
short_description (EN Store): Aromatic Australian Moscato with lychee, peach, and floral notes—light, refreshing, and perfect for Asian cuisine.
description (EN Store): Jacob's Creek Moscato from Barossa Valley delivers a classic expression of Muscat's aromatic character. Lychee and peach dominate the nose, supported by delicate floral and honey notes that give the wine its signature charm. On the palate, bright citrus and stone fruit flavors emerge, balanced by crisp acidity and a light, refreshing body. This wine is an ideal aperitif or pairing for Thai and Asian cuisines, where its sweetness and aromatic profile complement spicy and sour elements. Also pairs beautifully with soft cheeses and fruit-based desserts.

name: Santa Julia  Cuy White Natural Wine
short_description (EN Store): Ambient-yeast white from Maipú's gravel-sandy soils — unfined, unsulfured, from Argentina's largest certified-organic vineyard holding.
description (EN Store): José Alberto Zuccardi named this brand after his daughter Julia in the 1980s, but the Natural line represents the family's most deliberate break from Mendoza convention: zero additions, zero sulfur, zero filtration at a price point where most producers still rely on corrective winemaking. The Cuy White draws fruit from Familia Zuccardi's estate at Maipú — approximately 650 m elevation, arid continental climate with extreme diurnal swing — where well-drained gravel over sandy soils limit vine vigour and concentrate aromatic intensity while preserving the acidity that defines this line. Fermentation proceeds on ambient/indigenous yeasts with no sulfur additions at any stage; the wine is bottled unfined and unfiltered, preserving every textural and aromatic compound the fruit delivers. What sets the Cuy White apart within the Natural range is its white-wine expression of a philosophy more commonly applied to skin-contact or red cuvées — a genuinely rare format in Mendoza's natural-wine output. Argentina's largest certified-organic vineyard holding (~400 ha, first certified 2001) and 'Fair for Life' certification (2013) underpin every bottle. Drink on release for maximum freshness and aromatic lift.
